summary: "Code Folding does not support newest PHP features"
description: """There may not ever be a fix for issue APPSTUD-8131/APPSTUD-7371, as the code folding is _prepended_ on currently existing *correct* code.    However, there is an annoying bug that I know has a *very* easy fix: Adding support for the most recent PHP additions, viewable [here|https://wiki.php.net/RFC#implemented]; most notably, [Short Array syntax|https://wiki.php.net/rfc/shortsyntaxforarrays] and [Function array dereferencing|https://wiki.php.net/rfc/functionarraydereferencing].    Apologies, I don't know how to link to an existing issue, so I will leave it up to the moderators to examine APPSTUD-8131 and APPSTUD-7371, as these were the two issues that were most closely related to what I am experiencing.    Duplication steps:    Start with code that looks something like this:  {code:title=Working & Foldable Code}  class Example {      private function output($params = array()) {          return array('example'=>array('err1'=>'ERROR!'));      }      public function bar() {          return $this->output(array('good'=>'times'));      }  }  $foo = new Example();  $errors = $foo->bar();  echo $errors['example']['err1'];    This code _also_ runs just fine, outputs """"ERROR!"""" to the page, but as you'll notice immediately, is no longer foldable.    Apologies for not testing this in the latest release; I'm behind a webFilter at work and am currently trying to get the RCP update URL unblocked. As soon as I am able to test this issue in 3.6.0, I will update this ticket."""

summary: "Code Folding does not support latest PHP version changes"
description: """There may not ever be a fix for issues [APPSTUD-8131|https://jira.appcelerator.org/browse/APSTUD-8131] and [APPSTUD-7371|https://jira.appcelerator.org/browse/APSTUD-7371], as the code folding is _prepended_ on currently existing *correct* code.    However, there is an annoying bug that I know has a *very* easy fix: Adding support for the most recent PHP additions (viewable [here|https://wiki.php.net/RFC#implemented]); most notably, [Short Array syntax|https://wiki.php.net/rfc/shortsyntaxforarrays] and [Function array dereferencing|https://wiki.php.net/rfc/functionarraydereferencing].    See [APPSTUD-8131|https://jira.appcelerator.org/browse/APSTUD-8131] and [APPSTUD-7371|https://jira.appcelerator.org/browse/APSTUD-7371] for open issues most closely related to what I am experiencing.    Duplication steps:    Start with code that looks something like this:  {code:title=Working & Foldable Code}  class Example {      private function output($params = array()) {          return array('example'=>array('err1'=>'ERROR!'));      }      public function bar() {          return $this->output(array('good'=>'times'));      }  }  $foo = new Example();  $errors = $foo->bar();  echo $errors['example']['err1'];    This code _also_ runs just fine, outputs """"ERROR!"""" to the page, but as you'll notice immediately, is no longer foldable due to all the errors reported in <USER>    _Note: Apologies for not testing this in the latest release; I'm behind a webFilter at work and am currently trying to get the RCP update URL unblocked. As soon as I am able to test this issue in 3.6.0, I will update this ticket._"""

summary: "Problems debugging scripts that use the names print_array, print_hash or print_string "
description: """Hi,    I've found a strange behavior while using the variables view in <USER>Studio 3 (build: 3.6.0.201407100658) as well as RubyMine 6.3.3 (Build #RM-135.1104) during debugging.    What I have done:  I have a small script for demo  #################################  #!/usr/bin/env ruby    def print_array_    puts 'now in print_array'  end    def print_hash_    puts 'now in print_hash'  end    def print_string_    puts 'now in print_string'  end    sample_ary = [1,2]  sample_str = 'sample string'  sample_hsh = {'ary' => [1,1],                'str' => 'sample'}    puts 'Breakpoint 1'            puts 'Breakpoint 2'            puts 'Breakpoint 3'            #################################    The last tree lines were break points in the debugger.      This script works well with the method names given in the script and the content of variables was displayed in the variables view when the first breakpoint was reached (see files *working_fine.png).    After renaming the method's names by removing the last underscore, the behavior during debugging becomes strange. Using the name print_array (without trailing underscore) and trying to see the content of the sample_ary at the first breakpoint, aptana and rubymine skipped the breakpoint and the variable view changed to the screen seen in the files *print_array.png. The behavior was similar when print_hash_ was renamed to print_hash and I tried to see the content of sample_hsh.    When renaming print_string_ to print_string RubyMine gave the same result. In Apatana the picture was a little different (see <USER>- print_string.png).    I'm unable to determine the exact reason for this behavior. Is it ruby-1.9.3-p547, ruby-debug-ide 0.4.22, ruby-debug-base19x 0.11.30.pre15, <USER>Studio or RubyMine? Maybe print_array is a reserved name for the use of ruby-debug-ide, which uses the name for an internal method.    Please keep me informed about your opinion on this topic.    Best regards  Bertram      """

summary: "pydev debugging hang gunicorn"
description: """Running Gunicorn from the pydev debugger cause a hang on the sleep method in the arbitrer.py.              ready = select.select([self.PIPE[0]], [], [], 1.0)  cause the next code:              if not ready[0]:                  return  to return always keeping the code in a loop.    The workaround to this issue is to fall back to the old mechanism:  include this in the python code:    import pydevd  pydevd.patch_django_autoreload()  pydevd.settrace(suspend=False)    and run the pydevd debugger server    In this way everything works as expected and the critical part is passed. summary: "Crash on ""Refresh Running Browser"""
description: """The command """"Refresh Running Browser(s)"""" (Commands->HTML) causes <USER>Studio to crash.    I have long had a command of my own (defined in a ruble) mapped to the key combination CTRL-SHIFT-R. Originally (numerous releases ago), pressing the key combination invoked my command immediately. At some point afterwards, a pop-up menu began appearing when I pressed CTRL-SHIFT-R, listing my command first and the """"Refresh Running Browser(s)"""" command second. I couldn't figure out how to remove the """"Refresh Running Browser(s)"""" command, but I developed a muscle memory to quickly hit the """"1"""" key to invoke the first item in the pop-up menu. No big deal.    But with one of the recent updates, the """"Refresh Running Browser(s)"""" command somehow gained precedence over my own command, meaning it began to show up in position #1 on the pop-up menu. My muscle memory to hit the """"1"""" key means that I keep invoking the """"Refresh Running Browser(s)"""" command, and every time I do it crashes <USER>Studio.     APSTUD-3205 implies that """"Refresh Running Browser(s)"""" should be a Mac-only command, which might explain why it crashes my Windows version.    In any case,     # The """"Refresh Running Browser(s)"""" command is not hidden on the Windows platform, as implied by APTSTUD-3205, and  # You probably don't want it to crash <USER>Studio anyway    I can't find a way (documented or otherwise) to remove the key-mapping between CTRL-SHIFT-R and """"Refresh Running Browser(s)."""" If the root problem is intractable, but there is a way for me to hide this command in my own installation, I'd sure appreciate hearing about it.    Thanks!"""

summary: "Repository branch and Repository tag task fails to run"
description: """h3. Summary    When we use *VCS branching* and *VCS tagging* tasks and upgrade Bamboo to 6.7 and later, the plans having these tasks (renamed to *Repository Branch* and *Repository Tag* will fail with the error {{svn: E155007: 'build-dir/PROJ-PLAN-JOB' is not a working copy}}  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Define a Subversion repository in a plan.   # Create 2 jobs - one with *Source code checkout* task and *Repository tagging* and other job with just *Repository tagging*   # Run this plan    h3. Expected Results    The plan should introduce 2 tags and both the jobs must get successfully executed/built.  h3. Actual Results    The plan fails with the below exception in the build log file:    h3. Workaround    For build plans introduce a Source code checkout task in every job wherever we have *Repository Tag* or *Repository Branch* is defined.    For deployment plans add a Source code checkout task to every environment, a script task that uses a native SVN client to check out the correct revision used in the build plan in the same directory as the source code task and then the Repository tag task.   (The reason for the extra script task with a checkout is the fact that the source code checkout task always checks out the latest changes which may differ from the revision that was used in the build plan.)"""

summary: "The repository selected has incomplete data. Please reload the page and try again"
description: """h3. Summary  When we define a new linked repository (globally or in plan level), we get the below error   _An unexpected error has occurred. The repository selected has incomplete data. Please reload the page and try again_. We will also see 2 *New repository* in the repository list section when we see this error.   !image-2019-04-15-14-52-54-025.png|thumbnail!    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to any random plan and navigate to  *Plan configuration -> Repositories ->  Add repository -> _Select_ Bitbucket server*  # Double click (not a single click) the Bitbucket Server repository icon.  # Select the respective repository.  # Change the branch from the default one  # Hit 'Save repository'    h3. Expected Results  A new repository is created as per the definition.    h3. Actual Results  * We see an error under Repository section. No repositories are listed when we click on the dropdown.  * We also see two *New repository* like the above screenshot.     h3. Workaround  When we define a new repository, we should just click on the respective repository type only once.    h3. Suggested solution  Allow only one request to be processed and prevent multiple clicks from triggering multiple requests."""

summary: "Pull request in Bitbucket server shows builds results from 2 PR branch plans"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If PR declined and similar PR is created, PR in Bitbucket will shows build results from two different PR branch plans    h3. Environment  Bitbucket Server 6.2.0    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Set Plan Configuration to """"Create plan branch"""" when pull request is created  # Create a PR in Bitbucket Server  A branch plan (e.g. """"test"""") is created automatically in Bamboo  # Decline the PR in Bitbucket Server  The branch plan """"test"""" is disabled in Bamboo  # Create a new PR for the same branch in Bitbucket Server  A new branch plan """"test 1"""" is created in Bamboo  # Check the related build results in the first PR (declined) second PR (new) in Bitbucket Server    h3. Expected Results  * The first decline PR will show the build result from Bamboo branch plan """"test""""  * The second PR will show the build results from Bamboo branch plan """"test 1""""    h3. Actual Results  * The first decline PR show the build result from Bamboo branch plan """"test"""" and """"test 1""""  * The second PR show the build results from Bamboo branch plan """"test"""" and """"test 1""""    h3. Notes  * PR in Bitbucket server should only shows the build results from its linked PR branch plan in Bamboo  * Related to https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/BAM-20043    h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Source Code Checkout Task will not work as expected if using SVN behind Apache proxy"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Source Code Checkout task is not working as expected if the SVN Server is available on both http and https behind an Apache proxy.  h3. Environment   * SVN Server   * Apache Proxy using the following proxy rules:  {code:xml}  <VirtualHost *:80>      ServerName svn.someserver.com        RewriteEngine On      RewriteCond %{HTTPS} !=on      RewriteRule ^/?(.*) https://svn.someserver.com/$1 [R=301,L]  </VirtualHost>    <VirtualHost *:443>      ServerName svn.someserver.com      SSLEngine On      SSLCertificateFile /opt/ssl/server.crt      SSLCertificateKeyFile /opt/ssl/server.key      SSLProxyEngine on      <Location />              ProxyPass http://svn-server:18080/              ProxyPassReverse http://svn-server:18080/      </Location>  </VirtualHost>  {code}    h3. Expected Results    The repositories will be cloned respecting the branch path defined at the task configuration.  h3. Actual Results    If you try to clone the repository using the http address, the Source Code Checkout task will ignore the branch path and clone the entire repository.    i.e:  h6. Over HTTPS:    h6. Over HTTP    h3. Workaround   * Use only the HTTPS address while using the Source Code Checkout task.    Or   * Clone the repository using a script task instead of using the Source Code Checkout task."""

summary: "Quarantine test stop plan branch trigger and branch creation for subsequent plans that use the same repository"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a test is quarantined, plan branch fails to trigger when it's created and logs an error ({color:#de350b}Error 1{color}). Also if an attempt is made to create a plan branch manually, it creates the plan branch but an error modal is produced ({color:#de350b}Error 2{color}).    It will also stop plan branches being created for some other plans that use the same repository, but it is order dependant. Hitting the exception for one plan will stop the iterative creation process from looping through all plans.    h4. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan branch: _when a new branch in the repository is created_   # Create a test that fails   # Quarantine the failed test   # Create a branch in the git repository    h3. Expected Results    A plan branch should be created in Bamboo and build triggered.  All plans that have automatic branch detection on and use the repository should have the branch created.    h3. Actual Results    The plan branch is created, but the build is not triggered. Log *atlassian-bamboo.log* shows;    {color:#de350b}*Error 1*{color}     *{color:#de350b}Error 2{color}*      Plan branches are not created for any remaining plans with automatic branch creation on that were not checked before hitting this exception.    h3. Workaround    From the Plan summary, navigate to the Quarantined tests tab, and click the Resume button to resume the test"""

summary: "Build failing on ""Non required shared artifact"""
description: """h4. Steps to reproduce    # Create a build with 2 stages, 1 of them a final stage.  # In the first stage, add N jobs that generate an artifact.  # Each job should generate an artifact:  ## Make them search for {{**/*.log}} or something.  ## Untick """"required"""".  # Create a task in the job that generates a """"log"""" file (let's call it """"job-with-no-log"""").  # Update one of the jobs to not output any log files at all (so that the artifact will fail to generate).  # Create a job in the final stage that will collect all of the log files from all of the artifacts and concatenate them together.  ## You should use artifact subscriptions for this.  # Run the build.    h4. Expected results    The final stage's task will execute successfully when one of the configured shared artifacts is absent.    h4. Actual results        h4. My notes    * The wording of the error message in the final stage's build logs is confusing. If the artifact was """"non required"""", why did the build fail?   * The """"Required"""" checkbox for artifacts states that """"Build fails if the artifact cannot be published."""" However, the job that generates the artifact actually does fail? That's a mismatch of expectations :(    Bamboo seems to be torn between making builds fail loudly vs. allowing builds to continue when some data is absent. My expectation was that by unticking """"required"""" on an artifact definition, any subsequent subscriptions to that artifact would work (as in, silently ignore the non-existence of it). It seems the Bamboo team's thinking does not match mine. I can appreciate that the intention is probably that builds fail if """"unexpected"""" things happen, but this artifact is definitely optional as far as my build is concerned; it can cope just fine without it and I would expect the build to pass accordingly.     I'd be interested in workarounds, but I'd also be keen to see the behaviour (or at least the wording) become less confusing. """

summary: "Bamboo uses /bin/sh or cmd.exe as default interpreter with YAML specs"

Issue Summary    By default, Bamboo should use [shell interpreter |https://docs.atlassian.com/bamboo-specs-docs/6.9.1/specs.html?yaml#task-script]for YAML, however, this is not the case  h3. Steps to Reproduce  {code}  version: 2  ......    tasks:            - script: |          #!/bin/bash          Hello Bamboo  {code}  h3. Expected Result    The interpreter will be chosen based on the shebang line of the script. shell (default)  h3. Actual Results    Interpreter is /bin/sh or cmd.exe  h3. Workaround    Add shell as interpreter value  {code:java}  task:  ......    - script:       interpreter: shell       scripts:       - '#!/bin/bash'       - 'ls  ${pwd}'{code}"""

summary: "Build behavior is inconsistent when re-running failed/incomplete jobs with manual final stages"
description: """h3. Summary    Build behavior is inconsistent when re-running failed/incomplete jobs with manual final stages  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan with one normal stage (Stage 1) and one manual final stage (Stage 2).   # Have at least one job in each stage. This job needs to fail (the reason is not important).   # Run build.   # Stage 1 will finish with a failed Job. Manually run Stage 2.   # Stage 2 will finish with a failed job.   # Click Run -> Rerun failed/incomplete jobs only.   # Stage 1 will finish. Its job will fail.    h3. Expected Results   # The user should be able to rerun the final stage.    h3. Actual Results    Rerunning the final stage is not possible.  h3. Notes    Our documentation states that """"A manual stage can only be triggered if the previous stage has completed successfully."""" ([reference|https://confluence.atlassian.com/bamboo/using-stages-in-a-plan-289276862.html#Usingstagesinaplan-manual]). Nevertheless, the bahavior is inconsistent and the user should be able to run the final stage regardless.  h3. Workaround     Run a new build."""

summary: "Renamed user not retaining permissions if logged in before next sync"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If a renamed user logs in before the next synchronisation, embedded Crowd will rename the user. However, it does not update Bamboo permission entries in the same way that a directory synchronisation does.     Since the user is renamed from a Crowd perspective, the next sync won't detect a rename and permissions won't be updated at that point either.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Connect Bamboo to Active Directory (AD) and sync  # Add a specific AD user to have plan permissions  # Rename the user in AD  # Login as that user before the next syncronization    h3. Expected Results  Permissions are preserved upon rename.    h3. Actual Results  Permissions aren't preserved.    h3. Workaround  # Rename the user back to the pre-renamed username and perform a directory synchronisation at *User directories >> Synchronise*  # Rename the user back to the desired target username and perform a directory synchronisation at *User directories >> Synchronise* before the user logs in for the first time after the rename"""

summary: "Bamboo's Report ""Number of Tests"" feature shows wrong number on test failure"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  h3. Environment    This issue occurs for all Bamooo versions and apparently on all operating systems.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Setup a Bamboo build plan   # Use e.g. a Maven builder task with e.g. 10 test cases   # Make at least one (or more) of these test cases fail    h3. Expected Results    Independent of any test case failing or not, as the feature's description is """"How many tests does your build have?"""" we should see the real number of existing test case and thus 10.  h3. Actual Results    However, we see 0 as a result since this build is considered as test-less. The following  KB is explaining the situation: [""""Number of Tests"""" report is blank|https://confluence.atlassian.com/bamkb/number-of-tests-report-is-blank-935564988.html], so at least it's clear why this is happening.  h3. Notes    Even being explained by the above KB, this Report's outcome is wrong.     A suggestion would be to run all test cases and Bamboo internall count separately tests failing, tests passing and total number of tests (basically sum of the first two ones) and display all 3 numbers in one graph (e.g. with different colors) or as of 3 parallel graphs.  h3. Workaround    The currently only known workaround is the one mentioned in the KB, namely have the build pass all test cases."""

summary: "Unable to secure remote agents via automatic keystore management"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  It is not possible to secure the remote agents to connect to the Bamboo Server (using SSL) through the automatic keystore management feature.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure Bamboo to use SSL in Broker URL and Broker Client URL ([Securing your remote agents|https://confluence.atlassian.com/bamboo/securing-your-remote-agents-289277197.html])  # Restart Bamboo  # Install remote agent    h3. Expected Results  Remote agent installed and able to connect to Bamboo server successfully without problem    h3. Actual Results  Remote agent fail to connect to the SSL Broker URL  The below exception is thrown in Bamboo server atlassian-bamboo.log file:      h3. Notes  The same settings works fine in Bamboo 5.14.4.1    h3. Workaround    h6. Option 1  # Edit {{$BAMBOO_HOME/bamboo.cfg.xml}}  # Add {{socket.verifyHostName=false}} to {{bamboo.jms.broker.client.uri}}. Example below:  {code}      <property name=""""bamboo.jms.broker.client.uri"""">failover:(ssl://bamboo:54663?wireFormat.maxInactivityDuration=300000&socket.verifyHostName=false)?initialReconnectDelay=15000&maxReconnectAttempts=10</property>  {code}  # Restart Bamboo  # Restart agents    h6. Option 2  # Add the following setting into {{<remote-agent-home>/conf/wrapper.conf}}    # Restart remote agent"""

summary: "Cannot edit deployment project details with EDIT permission granted"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Cannot edit deployment project details with EDIT permission granted.  h3. Environment       * Bamboo 6.7.3 or 6.9.0   * Internal user directory    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a deployment project and grant a UserA EDIT permission (no GLOBAL create permission)   # Login with UserA    # Attempt to edit the deployment project details ( ... > Edit Project Details)    h3. Expected Results    Allowing to edit the deployment project name and the build plan to which is linked.  h3. Actual Results    !edit-deployment-name.png!       h3. Workaround    Grant the user a global Create permission."""

summary: "Upgrade Bamboo Embedded crowd"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When an LDAP group is the same as a group in Bamboo internal directory, incremental synchronization will be broken.    The bug CWD-5308 affects Bamboo Embedded crowd 3.2.6         h3. Workaround    Use distinct unique group names for Bamboo internal group and the LDAP directory."""

summary: "Information about a test failing on master branch is not shown"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan with a failing test on mater branch   # Create another plan branch from the master and run it    h3. Expected Results    Information that the test is failing on the master branch is visible  h3. Actual Results    No information about the test failing on master branch is shown  h3. Workaround    No workaround available"""

summary: "planKey parameter doesn't work on actions inheriting from BuildResultsActions"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Passing planKey parameter to an action fails with an exception:  Source: https://community.developer.atlassian.com/t/bamboo-application-is-not-working-in-bamboo6-9-any-more/29733    h3. Workaround    Use buildKey parameter"""

summary: "YAML specs unable to assign to Docker Runner for deployment environments"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When defining Deployment environments in YAML specs, declaring a docker image per documentation, does not change the """"Deploy Release IN"""" settings for the environment.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create YAML specs per documentation    # Commit and push changes       h3. Expected Results    Deployment Environment configured to run in Docker rather than in Agent    h3. Actual Results  Deployment Environment remains in """"Agent""""     !image-2019-07-29-14-40-36-208.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround     Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Plan-scoped repo not found when pushing Bamboo Specs to sandbox-bamboo"
description: """h3. Symptoms    When pushing a Bamboo Spec to sandbox-bamboo, an exception is thrown about a repository not being found, despite the fact that it's a plan-scoped repository that definitely exists in the plan being pushed. The error occurs regardless of what characters exist in the repo name (e.g. we also tried """"bob"""", which gave the same error).    h3. Steps to Reproduce  *Update*: detail conditions to have this problem:  - define branch detection with default trigger  - in the default trigger configuration  reference plan repository that does not yet exist in the plan  - add the repository to the plan  Since branch detection configuration is imported before the repository, it is not find when importing the default trigger.     # Check out the branch [https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/build-specs/branch/BSP-448-create-plans-for-shindig-branches]   # Create and run an IDEA run configuration with these settings:    !Screen Shot 2019-07-29 at 1.45.22 pm.png|width=447,height=210,thumbnail!  h3. Expected results    Plan is deployed to target Bamboo instance without error.  h3. Actual results    The exception below is thrown:    h3. Notes    [https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/build-specs/branch/BSP-448-create-plans-for-shindig-branches]  h3. Workaround    Do the update in 2 steps: in the first create all the required repositories, in the 2nd add branch detection configuration"""

summary: "Incorrect export of Docker task to Java Specs"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Docker task configured by UI incorrectly exported to Java Specs  # misses detach container flag and adds linkToDetachedContainers when it was not configured at UI  # incorrect Docker Pull Registry option when Custom registry is selected    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create Docker run task  # Check Detach checkbox and provide container name  # Create another Docker pull task  # Select the option """"Custom registry"""" and provide the repository  # Export plan to Java Specs    h3. Expected Results    h3. Actual Results    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "When attempting to start an elastic instance, Bamboo does not try another image configuration after failure"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a build could potentially be built on multiple Elastic Images; if there is an error starting the first image chosen, Bamboo does not fail over to one of the other image configurations.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    (for purposes of my testing Default VPC is not available)    # Create 3 separate Elastic Image Configurations  ## Image1 - M5 Large  ##* configured to correctly start, in my testing assign the correct VPC subnet  ## Image2 - T2 small  ##* assign to incorrect VPC  ## Image3 - T2 medium  ##* assign to incorrect/default VPC  # Add the same Capability to each image configuration    # edit your test build to add the capability above as a requirement.  # you now see '3 possible elastic agents'  # Start the build  h3. Expected Results    Build should start and run on Image1    h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file:      This error repeats at each interval which Bamboo detects builds in the queue, and attempts to start a new elastic instance.    h3. Notes    Bamboo is defaulting to the cheaper(smaller) elastic instance, and not failing over to any of the other valid image configurations.    h3. Workaround    use agent dedication, rather than capabilities."""

summary: "Expensive messages processing can't be parallelized"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Adding       {code:java}  -Dbamboo.max.concurrent.expensive.messages=2{code}       to the JVM arguments does not increase the number of BuildTailMessageProcessingThread-expensive threads from 1 to more.  h3. Environment   * Bamboo 6.7.2  h3.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Bump Bamboo heap to 4GB   # Single plan, one job, one script task to generate a large log:    {code:java}  yes """"HelloWorldHelloWorldHelloWorldHelloWorldHelloWorldHelloWorldHelloWorldHelloWorldHelloWorldHelloWorldHelloWorldHelloWorld"""" | head -n 2000000{code}   # Expensive regex set on the job:    {code:java}  (.*,\([^abc]*\):.*){code}   # Run on remote agents only   # Concurrent build limit set to 4   # Run 4 concurrent builds of the same plan   # Take a thread dump when the plan is finished.    h3. Expected Results    Two BuildTailMessageProcessingThread-expensive parsing the build logs.  h3. Actual Results    Only one BuildTailMessageProcessingThread-expensive parsing the build logs.    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Plan branch deleted under multi repository scenario when branch is deleted on non-default repository"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a plan branch is pointed at multiple repositories, if the branch configured on the second repository is deleted – the entire plan branch is marked for deletion even if the branch that the default repository is pointed to isn't yet deleted.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a single plan with two different repositories in Bitbucket Server   ** *Plan Configuration >> Branches*   *** Create plan branch: When new branch in repository is created   *** Delete plan branch: After branch was deleted from repository 0 days   ** *Plan Configuration >> Triggers*   *** Replace trigger with *Repository Polling* with a 5 second interval. This poll will detect the removal of the branch eventually.   # Manually create a new plan branch from a branch in repository one at *Plan Configuration >> Branches >> Create plan branch* E.g. develop   # Visit the new plan branch configuration and override the branch on the *second* repository on the *Repositories tab* to one that you will delete.   # Push a delete to the branch you just configured on the second repository:  {code:java}  git push --delete origin delbranch  git branch -d delbranch   {code}    Now observe in Bamboo as it fails to find {{delbranch}} in the refs and marks the entire plan branch for deletion, even though its main repository points at *develop* and that is the name of the plan branch.  h3. Expected Results     The expected behaviour is not yet defined. The plan branch should likely be disabled since a dependant branch has been removed but deleting the entire branch plan is not desirable unless both branches no longer exist (or if the branch on the default repository is removed). A disabled reason outlining why should be visible somewhere for plan administrators.    h3. Actual Results    Plan branch is marked for deletion and eventually removed.    Log snippet from atlassian-bamboo.log file:    h3. Workaround    No workaround aside from disabling *Delete plan branch* at *Plan Configuration >> Branches* for the entire plan or on each important branch individually at *Branch Configuration >> Branch details tab >> Uncheck: Clean up plan branch automatically*"""

summary: "Internal server error (Plan is not a DB object) when disabling job from branch plan build result"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Internal server error (Plan is not a DB object) when disabling job from branch plan build result.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Visit a branch plan build result  # Expand left-hand side bar, navigate to a job  # Actions >> Disable job    h3. Expected Results  Master Job disables    h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file and presented as an Internal Server Error in the UI:        h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Branch status screen NPE if the Bamboo user e-mail is undefined"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When accessing the branch status screen the information fails to load because of a NullPointerException.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Login to Bamboo as a user that does not have an e-mail defined. This can happen for example when users are imported from an external LDAP server.   # Visit the Branch status screen.    h3. Expected Results    The branch status screen displays the information correctly.    h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the Bamboo server log file:      h3. Workaround    There is no workaround.  """

summary: "SCP Task will fail if ssh host has ed25519"
description: """h3. Summary    SCP Task will fail if ssh host has ed25519  h3. Environment   - Bamboo or agent connecting via ssh to a host with ed25519 present    h3. Steps to Reproduce   - Create a key pair using rsa.   - Use a SCP task to connect to the host.   - Run plan/deployment.    h3. Expected Results    Connection successful  h3. Actual Results    Bamboo will fail to create the connection  h3. Notes    The problem is related to BouncyCastle and ed25519, the error will appear even though the user doesn't use ed25519.  h3. Workaround         Use a script task instead with the *scp* command in it."""

summary: "Some of the Bamboo icons are in low resolution"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Some of the icons in Bamboo are in low resolution and some are not; thereby giving an imbalance in the overall experience with a mixture of high and low resolution icons.  This difference is visible in high resolution (retina display) monitors. leads to negatively influence for business people.    h3. Expected Results  All icons are of same resolution.    h3. Actual Results  Some icons look blurry. For instance, look at the below screenshot.   !Screenshot 2019-08-09 at 14.33.59.png|thumbnail!         """

summary: "Cloning a plan from any project results in Plan already exists error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When we clone a plan, we get a page that says """"Plan creation failed. Plan ABC-XYZ already exists."""". However, it is created in the background already.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Click on Clone -> Clone plan -> choose a random plan and enter new plan details -> Click 'Clone'.    h3. Expected Results    A new plan with the same contents of the original plan is created successfully without error.  h3. Actual Results    We get an error """"Plan creation failed. Plan ABC-XYZ already exists.""""    The below exception is thrown in the catalina.out file:    h3. Workaround    Although the application complains that the plan creation is failed, the plan is actually cloned in the background and you can access without issues."""

summary: "Creating a linked repository via Bamboo Specs with incorrect project key casing causes remote events from Bitbucket to be ignored for that repository"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Creating a linked repository via Bamboo Specs with incorrect project key or repository slug casing causes remote events from Bitbucket to be ignored for that repository.    projectKey value must be uppercase to be valid  repositorySlug value must be lowercase to be valid    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Push a Bitbucket Server linked repository via Specs to Bamboo with a lowercase project key and uppercase repository slug. E.g.    # Create a plan using that linked repository  # Push a commit to Bitbucket for that repository    h3. Expected Results  Plan triggers    h3. Actual Results  Plan doesn't trigger as projectKey and repository slug on inbound remote event doesn't match what's stored against the repository.    h3. Notes  # Manual run works fine  # Viewing the Repository setting through Bamboo Administration does not show any error  # The problem will happen to plan repository as well    h3. Workaround  Re-save the repository via the UI to persist correct casing or  Fix the casing in the Bamboo Spec and repush:  * .projectKey value must be uppercase  * .repositorySlug value must be lowercase"""

summary: "Audit logs have the disable-enable actions shown in the wrong order"
description: """h3. Summary  Audit logs have the disable-enable actions shown in the wrong order.    h3. Environment  Tested in Bamboo 6.9    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Navigate to *Administration - Audit Log*.  # Disable audit logging.  # Wait at least one minute to have a different timestamp.  # Enable audit logging.    h3. Expected Results  We expect to see two entries in the audit logs. One disabling it. One enabling it.    h3. Actual Results  Both entries are there, but in the wrong order.   It should be      but it shows    (this is read bottom-up)    h3. Notes  For the action of re-enabling audit logs, the timestamp is wrong. In my example, it should show 13:22 instead.    h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "XSRF tokens are accumulating in Firefox"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Using Bamboo in Firefox leads to a 'missing XSRF token' error. On inspection, I find a large number of {{atl.xsrf.token}} cookies, suggesting that I'm hitting a 50 cookie limit.    The cookies differ by Path; it looks like two distinct values, with a separate copy of that cookie for around twenty different paths. That means I'm not sending them on every request, but that Bamboo can't set a new one.    h3. Environment    * Current Firefox  * Bamboo 6.10.0-m134      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Use Bamboo, browsing to a number of different pages   # Note that the number of cookies set accumulates   # Note that a limit is hit, where Firefox will not pass back the cookie Bamboo is expecting.    h3. Workaround  Use Firefox's Storage Inspector to delete cookies."""

summary: "Job with dependent artifact can hang during branch creation if triggered before artifact exists"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Job with dependent artifact can hang during initial branch build if triggered before artifact exists.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create producer build plan   ## Link it to a repository.   ## Create plan branch: When new branch in repository is created   ## Trigger >> Bitbucket Server repository triggered   ## One stage with job that creates artifact A and a script task with {{sleep 30;}} (to delay the first build from completion)   ## Share artifact A   # Create Consumer build plan   ## Link it to a repository.   ## Create plan branch: When new branch in repository is created   ## Trigger >> Bitbucket Server repository triggered (repository polling untested but assumed to be the same)   ## 2 stages   ## First stage is a placeholder and can do anything.   ## 2nd stage has job with artifact download task to download artifact A   # Push a new branch    h3. Expected Results    Consumer plan job that downloads the artifact fails as dependent artifact is not found.  h3. Actual Results    Job hangs indefinitely.    The below exception is *correctly* thrown in the {{atlassian-bamboo.log}} file and is displayed against the build result:    h3. Notes    The job never gets dispatched, so while this doesn't consume an agent the impact is it will use up a concurrent build slot for that plan and appear to be building while viewing the result.  h3. Workaround    The build can be stopped manually by *Viewing the build result >> Actions >> Stop Build*.    To avoid the problem: Remove the commit trigger (*Plan Configuration >> Triggers*) from the child plan and instead use a Bamboo plan dependency to trigger the child plan:   * [Setting up plan build dependencies|https://confluence.atlassian.com/bamboo/setting-up-plan-build-dependencies-289276887.html]"""

summary: "Possible to create duplicate project keys with concurrent Bamboo Specs pushes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Possible to create duplicate project keys with concurrent Bamboo Specs pushes.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create two Bamboo Specs that reference the same project but create different plans  # Ensure the project does not already exist (so that it is created)  # Push both specs concurrently e.g.  {code}  #!/bin/bash  cd ~/Documents/bamboo-specs/bamboo-specs/ && mvn -Ppublish-specs &  cd ~/Documents/bamboo-specs-2/bamboo-specs && mvn -Ppublish-specs &  {code}  # Attempt to browse the project in the UI at *Projects >> Project*    (!) There may be some variance in reproducibility as this is a race condition.    h3. Expected Results  Only one project is created.    h3. Actual Results  Two projects are created with the same key in {{PROJECTS}} table:    ||project_id||created_date||updated_date||project_key||title||description||marked_for_deletion||oid||  |218759169|2019-09-04 11:58:32|2019-09-04 11:58:32|PKEY|TITLE| |false|3225703233104165889|  |218759170|2019-09-04 11:58:32|2019-09-04 11:58:32|PKEY|TITLE| |false|3225703233104165890|      The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log and UI when viewing the project:      h3. Workaround  Introduce a minor sleep (2 - 4 seconds) between Bamboo Specs pushes."""

summary: "Obfuscate ""Authors"" list on Reports tab for non-admins"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    {{[http://Bamboourl/authors/viewAuthors.action|http://bamboourl/authors/viewAuthors.action]}}    This page shows a list of all authors whom have commited to repositories linked to Bamboo. All non-admins can view this information. As a lot of commit 'authors' are listed as email addresses, this can lead to privacy concerns.  h3. Suggested Solution    Obfuscate author details for non-admins who vew the {{viewAuthors.action}} page.  h3. Workaround   # Open the file *<BambooInstall>/atlassian-bamboo/author/viewAuthors.ftl* in an editor (note the file name is viewAuthor*s*.ftl, not viewAuthor.ftl)   # You will find the following content:       # Remove all lines starting from {{<table id=""""authorTable"""" class=""""aui tablesorter"""">}} up to and including {{</script>}}   # After that, the contents of the file should be       # Save the file and restart Bamboo"""

summary: "Specs status hang if checkout fail due to LFS"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Specs status will hang in """"Scanning in progress"""" if LFS is enabled in the repository but not installed in Bamboo    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Enable LFS support in the Bitbucket Server repository  # Do not install or uninstall Git LFS in Bamboo server  # Run Specs for the repository    h3. Expected Results  Specs will fail to run because unable to checkout without LFS    h3. Actual Results  * The Bitbucket Server repository in Bamboo show the Specs status """"Scanning in progress"""" without stopping.  * The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file:      h3.Notes  * The same status hang problem will happen if checkout fail due to other problem as well  * Specs status should change to fail if checkout fail    h3. Workaround  # Recreate a new repository in Bamboo to remove the hang Specs status  # Install Git LFS in the machine hosting Bamboo so that it will not happen again"""

summary: "S3 bucket for Elastic agent sync is empty on Bamboo 6.4.2"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  S3 bucket for Elastic agent sync is empty on Bamboo 6.4.2. This affects some but not all regions.     (!) Not all regions have been tested.    Affected:  * US West 1 (N. Cali)  * AP South-East 2 (Sydney)    Unaffected:  * US East 1 (N. Virginia)    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Launch an Elastic Agent on Bamboo 6.4.2 using US West 1 (N. Cali).    h3. Expected Results  The agent loads successfully.    h3. Actual Results  The agent hangs on pending indefinitely and never connects    The below is observed in the {{/home/bamboo/bamboo-elastic-agent.out}}      h3. Workaround  The list of regions below are unaffected, so if possible within your AWS infrastructure you could utilize one of them:  * US East 1 (N. Virginia)"""

summary: "Adding Docker capability in Windows agent using \ in the path shows ""This entry cannot contain the character \"" error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Adding Docker capability (*Bamboo cog icon > Agent > add capability*) using Windows path like C:\Program Files\Docker shows the following error:    h3. Suggested Solution    Bamboo should accept character \ in the path for Docker capability.  h3. Environment   * Bamboo agent running Windows    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access *Bamboo cog icon > Agent > add capability*   # Select Docker capability and put:      h3. Expected Results    Bamboo should add Docker capability when using \ in the path.  h3. Actual Results    The message below is shown on Agent capability page:    !image-2019-09-16-12-11-59-798.png|width=937,height=206!  h3. Workaround    Add the Docker agent capability using [bamboo-capabilities.properties|https://confluence.atlassian.com/bamboo/configuring-remote-agent-capabilities-using-bamboo-capabilities-properties-289276849.html] file:  """

summary: "Unable to Start Stock Elastic Images"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Attempting to spin up an elastic agent from one of the stock AMIs results in a permissions error    h3. Environment    Bamboo 6.10.2    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure an Elastic Image to use one of the new stock images from Bamboo 6.10.2   # attempt to start an Elastic Agent using this instance      h3. Expected Results    Elastic Instance and Agent both start.    h3. Actual Results     !image-2019-09-17-11-36-06-547.png|thumbnail!     The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file:      h3. Workaround  There once was a workaround here, but it's no longer necessary since the issue is fixed so we've removed it prevent any confusion."""

summary: "YAML specs deployment trigger does not work without plan"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    It's not possible to scan YAML specs when configuring a deployment trigger for build success on branches if the branch plan does not exist in bamboo yet.    Environment    Bamboo 6.9.1 with YAML specs    h3. Steps to Reproduce    Create YAML specs for deployments and set a trigger to set off on build success of a branch plan.  Make sure you don't have the branch plan created.    h3.Expected Results    Just a warning message that the branch plan does not exist and the deployment is configured without the trigger.    h3. Actual Results    An error message:        h3. Workaround    There is no workaround reported yet.   """

summary: "Build log is showing inaccurate information when replacing an invalid Working subdirectory path to another (yet invalid) value at script tasks"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Build log is showing inaccurate information once we change an invalid """"Working subdirectory"""" value for the script task to another (yet invalid) value.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new plan   # Add a Script task to it with an invalid path at the """"Working subdirectory"""" field ({{Path/from/step2}})   # Run the plan   # Change the """"Working subdirectory"""" content to a different nonexistent path ({{Path/from/step4}})   # Run the build again    h3. Expected Results    It's expected the Job to fail in both cases because we are pointing the """"Working subdirectory"""" to a non-existent path and we should see the correct non-existent path at the build logs  h3. Actual Results    Both builds will fail but in the build logs for the second build (Step 5) we'll still see the error referencing the path set on step 2:    But if you look at the agent logs, you'll see that the task is failing with the correct directory ({{Path/from/step4}}):    (i) If you set a valid subdirectory the build will pass   (i) It does not matter how many times you change the """"Working subdirectory"""" value. if is a non-existent path, the logs will still show the first one.  h3. Workaround    Restarting Bamboo server will adjust the build log information"""

summary: "tests tab displays only the first 1000 test result entries"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a test report contains more than 1000 test results, Bamboo doesn't list them all when we navigate to 'tests' tab in build result summary.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a test report xml file with more than 1000 test results in a build   # Add a JUnit parser task to parse this test report.   # Run this build    h3. Expected Results    All the test results in the Build result summary page are listed (preferably paginated showing only the first few and leave the rest in next subsequent pages).  h3. Actual Results    Only the first 1000 test results are listed, but the Tests tab clearly shows there are more test passed/failed.  h3. Workaround    There is no workaround to display the remaining tests for now."""

summary: "Build report chart does not show 0 when selecting more than 1 build"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When the user selects more than one build making a report chart of successful builds, 0% results do not show on the chart.  h3. Environment    Bamboo 6.9.2   Tested for """"percentage of successful builds"""" and """"build activity"""".  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Choose two plans that have days that all builds failed and some days with success.   Go to reports and create a chart selecting both plans, group by day and the date filter desired.  h3. Expected Results    A chart that varies from 0% to 100%  h3. Actual Results    There are now 0% results when having more than one plan selected.   !Screen Shot 2019-09-19 at 17.16.51.png|width=236,height=194!   !Screen Shot 2019-09-19 at 17.16.35.png|width=236,height=194!    !Screen Shot 2019-09-19 at 17.17.12.png|width=236,height=194!  h3. Notes    The same can be done with build activity when there is no activity in a day.  h3. Workaround    For a single plan chart, there is no issue, although this does not solve the problem. For actually showing all the plans together there is no known workaround."""

summary: "Export file has inconsistent data due to potential race condition"
description: """h3. Summary  Export file has inconsistent data due to potential race condition      h3. Steps to Reproduce  - Run a manual or schedule backup. What's important is to have the server paused.  - Perform any kind of operations (excluding builds) that can modify data in the DB at any level.    h3. Expected Results  Those operations won't be allowed since the server is paused.     h3. Actual Results  Operations are allowed.     h3. Notes  - These scenarios can create inconsistencies in the export file.   - When performing an import, these problem will raise.   - If this happens, please open a ticket with the Bamboo support team to address that particular issue.    h3. Workaround  Block all kind of write operations while the server is paused."""

summary: "Docker runner: removing bamboo.tmp.directory volume breaks job tasks"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Users can deliberately remove volumes that are necessary for the correct operation of a job tasks. If they are needed they should not be removable.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a job with docker runner   # Create a script task with a simple command   # Remove the *bamboo.tmp.directory* volume    h3. Actual Results    The script task will not work due to the fact that the docker runner will not find it.      h3. Workaround  Go to *Job configuration >> Docker >> Volumes* and make sure you have the following volumes configured:  ||Host directory||Container directory||  |${bamboo.tmp.directory}|${bamboo.tmp.directory}|  |${bamboo.working.directory}|${bamboo.working.directory}|    """

summary: "Remote Mercurial trigger not working with .hg/hgrc [hook] setting ""changegroup.update"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary  h3. Environment    Bamboo version 6.7.2 running and Mercurial version 5.1 (as of hg --version from command-line) being installed on Linux / MacOS.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Stand up a local Mercurial repository with source file(s) and connect it to e.g. TortopiseHg being used as Mercurial server.   # Add this Mercurial repository to a Bamboo build plan, either as a Linked repository or plan internally.   # Configure this build plan's """"Remote trigger"""" to use the Mercurial repository and prepare it according to [Repository triggers the build when changes are committed|https://confluence.atlassian.com/bamboo/repository-triggers-the-build-when-changes-are-committed-289276902.html] by copying the SVN _postCommitBuildTrigger.sh_ script into your local repository folder and there edit _.hg/hgrc_ adding       # Now modify at least one of the repository files and then _hg add_ plus _hg commit_ the change.    h3. Expected Results    The build should automatically be triggered.  h3. Actual Results    Build is not triggered. No error appears in the logs.  h3. Workaround    Change the _.hg/hgrc_ setting to this:    and a _hg commit_ immediately kicks off a build for the plan key pointed to."""

Workaround    Add the following lines at the end of the {{<installation directory>/atlassian-bamboo/WEB-INF/classes/log4j.properties}} file and restart your Bamboo:  """

summary: "Incorrect link under column ""Test"" in ""Quarantined tests"" tab for failing build plan test(s)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  h3. Environment    Bamboo 6.10.2, no more needed.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Run a build plan containing at least one failing test (precondition to allow quarantining).   # Quarantine a failed test of the above plan as of _Dashboard >> Build >> All build plans >> #buildresult in column """"Build""""_. On this page scroll down to the failing tests and aside each one find _Cog >> Quarantine_ (last selectable option).   # Navigate to the Build dashboard of this plan and there click the most right tab """"Quarantines tests"""".   # Select the link under the """"Test"""" column for the quarantined test. This link is broken and thus results in a 404 - """"Page not found"""".Expected Results    h3. Expected Results    The link is correct as in elder Bamboo versions. Here an example of a correct one:    h3. Actual Results    The link is broken. Reusing the above example it shows:    Comparing the wrong with the correct link, it's apparent that within the _PROJ-PLAN-JOB-BUILD_ part between """"browse/"""" and """"/test"""", for the broken link _JOBx_ is missing.  h3. Workaround    There is currently no workaround known, other than manually inserting JOBx into the link so to reach this failing plan's """"Test case result"""" page. Once another approach is applicable, we'll add it here."""

summary: "Missing dependencies for repository-stored-specs on Bamboo 6.10.3 "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Bamboo specs dependencies are missing from [Maven repository|    [https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/com/atlassian/bamboo/bamboo-specs-parent/]]:  {code:xml}      <parent>          <groupId>com.atlassian.bamboo</groupId>          <artifactId>bamboo-specs-parent</artifactId>          <version>6.10.3</version>          <relativePath/>      </parent>  {code}    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install bamboo 6.10.3   # Scan a repository-stored-specs-enabled linked repository    h3. Expected Results  The specs code will be compiled and the changes will be applied to Bamboo  h3. Actual Results  The scan fails and the following error is thrown in Bamboo server logs:        h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior."""

summary: "No agent found when setting e.g. ""JDK 11"" as an agent specific capability matching ""JDK 11"" as a plan's job requirement"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  h3. Environment    Bamboo 6.2.8, JDK 11 being installed on Bamboo host.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add capability """"JDK 11"""" specific to an agent as described in this KB: [Defining a new JDK capability|https://confluence.atlassian.com/bamboo/defining-a-new-jdk-capability-289277157.html]   # Navigate to a build plan, there into a job and on tab """"Requirements"""" select capability """"JDK 11"""" with attribute """"exists"""" and press the """"Add"""" button.    h3. Expected Results    Behind """"Can build on"""" on this job's Requirements page should appear the agent possessing capability """"JDK 11"""".  h3. Actual Results    What appears on this page is """"No agent available"""".  h3. Workaround    *Dedicating* the project / build / job that shall use capability """"JDK 11"""" on page Agent Summary > Agent via tab """"Dedicate agent"""" overcomes this bug.    Besides since in Bamboo version 6.7.2 this bug is not present, a *Bamboo upgrade to 6.7.2 or higher* should also bypass it."""

summary: "Creating new release fails if deployment id is bigger than 2,147,483,647"
description: """h3. Steps to reproduce:   # Create new deployment project that will have id larger than 2,147,483,647   # Go to deployment page and click 'Create release'    h3. Expected Results    New release configuration page is shown  h3. Actual Results    Action fails with an error: {{Can not create release, no deployment project with id 0 could be found}}  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Branch deletion / expiry can run on branches that override the default repository which will usually end up with it being removed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Branch deletion / expiry can run on plan branches that override the default repository. If the default repository on the master doesn't contain the branch (which is common), it will end up with the plan branch being deleted. It's dangerous and can lead to unexpected branch deletion. It's not obvious from a user perspective that this will occur due to how branch detection works in Bamboo.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan pointing at repository 1   # Manually create a plan branch   # Override the plan branch repository to point to repository 2 and a specific branch. Ensure this branch does not exist in repository 1.   # Enable branch deletion, 0 days at *Plan Configuration >> Branches*    h3. Expected Results    The plan branch is not disabled and eventually deleted, as the branch does exist on the overridden repository.  h3. Actual Results    The plan branch is disabled and eventually deleted even though it exists on the overridden repository.  h3. Workaround    Don't use plan branch deletion / expiry on plans where plan branches will override the default repository when the default repository does not contain the same branches as the override."""

summary: "Scp Task is slow due to high number of disk operations"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  ScpTask calls File.getCanonicalPath() a lot of times. (O(n^2) complexity)    h3. Environment  Any.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an artifact with big number of file   # Use an Scp task to copy it    h3. Expected Results  Task completes in reasonable time (should be linear to number of files and total size of them)  h3. Actual Results  Performance degrades very fast with increasing number of files.    h3. Notes    (Optional - If Necessary)  h3. Workaround    Tar and/or zip your artifact prior to transfering it."""

summary: "Setting a plan as disabled prevents specs of publishing its configuration"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The *enable plan* configuration should not prevent a plan to be published. This is not a coherent/intuitive behavior. Enabling a plan and publishing it are different things which will create confusion for users.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a specs plan configuration in Bitbucket with the plan configuration disabled:         # Configure a spec linked repository in Bamboo with the expected permissions   # Run the specs scan     h3. Expected Results   # The plan is created   # The plan has the master branch disabled    h3. Actual Results   # The plan is not published    h3. Note    This behavior is not coherent. If we publish a specs plan and later set the plan as disabled through specs it will be still available in Bamboo. In other words, disabling a plan should not prevent it to be published.  h3. Workaround   # Create the plan enabled   # Disabled the master branch through specs"""

summary: "Repository cannot be added to a build plan by a user without global ""Admin"" permission"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  h3. Environment    Bamboo 6.10.2 / 6.10.3, no further prerequisites required for reproduction.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create project """"Test Project"""" and therein a simple build plan """"Test Plan"""" minimally containing task Source Code Checkout. Other than this, set up a Linked repository in Bamboo for a Bitbucket Server Git repository which the below Bamboo user has full access to.   # Create a new user """"testuser"""" and provide no global permissions to this user*.*   # Navigate to _Projects >> """"Test Project"""" >> press button """"Project settings""""_ in the right upper corner and set """"Project permissions"""" as well as """"Plan permissions"""" visible on the left-hand side, as such:   ** Project permissions: """"Create plan"""" (or """"Admin"""" - same effect)   ** Plan permissions: """"Admin""""   # Now navigate to plan """"Test Plan"""", open its configuration page and there tab """"Repositories""""   # Pressed button """"Add repository"""". The above and previously created Linked repository shows up.   # Add this Linked repository to plan """"Test Plan"""".    h3. Expected Results    See the repository being added as a plan repository and thereafter to appear in the """"Repositories"""" tab (also see Notes).  h3. Actual Results    What we see instead is that this process never terminates (unless the """"Cancel"""" button is pressed) and the below exception is trown and shows up in Bamboo's Server log (_atlassian-bamboo.log_):    h3. Notes    For Bamboo 6.9.2 we see the following behavior:   * Operation terminates quickly and with success   * """"(/) *Repository created successfully*"""" being displayed on the """"Repositories"""" tab   * No error messages in the Bamboo Server log    Apparently this issue is related to global permission and apparently a user needs global """"Admin"""" permission for the failing Bamboo versions to add a Linked repository to a build plan.  h3. Workaround    The only work-around found so far, for the faulty Bamboo versions, is that a non-Admin Bamboo user asks a global Bamboo Admin user to add a repository to a certain build plan. Any further action sequence bypassing this bug will be denoted here, once avaialble."""

summary: "High CPU and hung builds when using complex label regex"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When complex label regex is used, it will cause  * high CPU usage  * all other builds hung in Bamboo server (even though remote agents have completed the builds)  * builds that have completed on an agent earlier appear to still be running (because their result is being processed by the server, or waiting to be processed)    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a build with complex label regex setting at {{Plan Configuration >> Stages & jobs >> <jobName> >> Miscellaneous/Other}}    # Configure a Script task with the following command:    # Create multiple remote agents  # Disable local agent  # Enable Concurrent builds  # Run the same plan for a multiple times to perform concurrent builds    h3. Expected Results  All builds will finish without problem    h3. Actual Results  * High CPU usage in Bamboo server when the first plan finish running  * Some builds hung for certain time even though the remote agent logs shows that it has completed  * Some builds fail due to remote agent disconnected even though the remote agent logs shows that it has completed and sent the result to Bamboo server  * Thread dump shows that """"BuildTailMessageProcessingThread-expensive:pool"""" is processing label search, causing it unable to process other remote agent communication messages:      h3. Notes  * If large build log (~160MB) is produced, the label search might take a few days to complete causing all other builds to hung and fail (Bamboo server have to restarted to stop this process)  * The fix in BAM-20570 may produce more than one thread processing the remote agent message but it will still produce high CPU and the same problem will still happen if multiple build produce large build logs at the same time  * It would be great if one of the following is used to prevent it:  ** timeout on label search so that it will not cause other builds to hung (user may not know that label search has timeout without checking Bamboo log)  ** label search will only process the first XX lines (the label setting can have a message that it will only process the first XX lines)  ** detect complex regex or [initial|https://www.regular-expressions.info/catastrophic.html] """".*"""" (if it is possible) and prevent user from configuring it  ** perform label search in the remote agent side    h3. Workaround  Do not use complex regex in label setting"""

summary: "Secure password variables"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Encrypted password variables can be viewed as plain text with the option of *Show*. If for some reason an unintended guest has access to the SPECS managed git repository, such encrypted string can be copied as a variable value in the unwanted guest instance, and decoded    h3. Expected Results  The option Show should be removed to keep encrypted password secure.   A possibility of reusing encrypted variables in different projects should be eliminated  """

description: """h3. Issue Summary    According to logs Bamboo was not able to remove repository cache folder and after reporting process for remove branch list was finished. It might be coincidence but it looks like git process for branch detection prevented Bamboo from cache folder removal  h3. Steps to Reproduce    It's hard to reproduce and happened several times at customer environment.   h3. Expected Results    Executed by Bamboo processes are synchronized by folder and don't conflict  h3. Actual Results    Cache folder kept in broken state  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

Workaround  Single click in Save"""

description: """h3. Issue Summary    At some conditions Bamboo is unable to clear inconsistent git cache and perform change detection  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # none   It seems that this happens if the change detection and the branch detection run at the same time.  h3. Expected Results    When the git cache is inconsistent should be deleted and recreated.  h3. Actual Results    The detention of the cache fails:    h3. Workaround    Manually clean the git cache."""

summary: "Bamboo queue fail to work properly due to duplicates"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Bamboo queue fail to work properly due to duplicates    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Steps may vary depending on each instance    h3. Expected Results  No duplicates entries in the queue, since keys should always be unique    h3. Actual Results  The following error is displayed in the server logs:      h3. Workaround  Upgrade to a version of Bamboo higher or equal to 6.10.0."""

summary: "Opening stop build in a new tab for a queued build will cause an XSRF failure and inconsistent build state"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Opening stop build in a new tab for a queued build will cause an XSRF failure and inconsistent build state, the build will not be shown as complete until the OrphanedBuildMonitor cleans it after 720 seconds. If this is performed on a *re-run* build, there will be a NullPointerException too and OrphanedBuildMonitor cannot clean it and Bamboo needs to be restarted or the build is infinitely in a queued state.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Run a plan and allow it to complete  # Disable all agents so build waits in queue  # Re-run the same build  # In Chrome > Right Click Stop Build > Open in new tab   # {{XSRF: A mutative operation was attempted}} error because opening stop build in a new tab as the browser performs a {{GET}} instead of {{POST}}.   # Now try to stop the build normally (without opening it a in a new tab)  # NullPointerException    h3. Expected Results  Shouldn't be able to get into this state. Perhaps {{stopPlan.action}} should be completely protected (in-memory caches and stopping the build) from unsupported HTTP methods like {{GET}} since it performs mutative actions.    h3. Actual Results  Upon opening {{Stop Build}} in a new tab (even for non re-runs) a {{GET}} request cannot mutate the build state from {{QUEUED}} -> {{NotBuilt}}:      Upon trying to stop this build normally with a {{POST}} and when {{OrphanedBuildMonitor}} attempts to clean it due to the state mismatch  The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file and UI:        h3. Workaround  For a build that was re-run, once the XSRF error is encountered -- Bamboo will need to be restarted to clear up the build result, otherwise it will appear as queued indefinitely because the orphaned build monitor encounters a NullPointerException for re-runs affected by this.    If the build was a first time build and the XSRF error is encountered, after 720 seconds the Bamboo orphaned build monitor will successfully clear up the build result."""

summary: "Links generated for GitHub Enterprise Repositories point to github.com"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Links generated for GitHub Enterprise Repositories point to github.com    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Link GHE repo   # Click links from application    h3. summary: "Local agent doesn't hang-up SSH session after network interruption"
