Issue Summary    When an SSH task is running on a local agent, if there's a network event which causes the SSH connection to fail, hanging the build, Bamboo fails to properly close the connection.  Leaving the build in an 'unknown' state    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # configure plan using local agent to SSH into a remote server   # run build   # while build is running force network disconnect between Bamboo server, and target server.    h3. Expected Results    Bamboo closes the SSH session    h3. Actual Results    log entries included below    h3. Workaround    Stop and restart the Bamboo application to reset the Local Agent status"""

summary: "Master or branch plan Bitbucket Server triggered stop working "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Bitbucket Server trigger stops working for a particular master or branch plan randomly. Restarting Bamboo will make it work again.  h3. Steps to Diagnose   # Enable debug logging:       # atlassian-bamboo.log file shows that Bamboo receive Bitbucket Server trigger but did not start queue polling job even though the previous build have finished and it does not receive any other trigger:      h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Bamboo always performs a full fetch when building new commits"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Bamboo always performs a full repository fetch when building new commits even when *Fetch whole repository* is unchecked.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Set up a plan with a Git repository and checkout task  # Push a new commit  # Run the build    h3. Expected Results  Bamboo fetches only the branch specified on the repository    h3. Actual Results  Bamboo always performs a full repository fetch, even though the revision exists on the configured branch. The below is observed in the build log :      Checkout times are increased and extra load is placed on repository hosts.    h3. Note  This is normal behaviour if building with a custom revision and that revision doesn't exist on the branch configured on the repository. It may explain why you continue to see this message if running a fixed version of Bamboo with certain builds.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Unable to use Perforce repository in branch plan"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Using Perforce repository in branch plan will make it unable to run with NullPointerException    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a plan with Perforce repository  # Create a branch plan  # Run the branch plan    h3. Expected Results  The branch plan will run successfully    h3. Actual Results  The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file:      h3. Notes  * Even though P4 does not have branch support in Bamboo, Bamboo will still perform the same checkout as the master plan - works fine in Bamboo 6.9.2 or older  * Caused by the changes in https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/BAM-12984  * After restarting Bamboo server, the branch plan that fail will works fine    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Incorrect deployment trigger setting in View as Java Specs"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  After selecting the option """"Use a custom plan branch"""" and save the """"After successful build plan"""" trigger with a branch, editing the trigger to """"Use the main plan branch"""" does not change the Java Specs in View as Java Specs    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a """"After successful build plan"""" trigger for a deployment environment  # Configure the trigger to """"Use a custom plan branch"""" with non-default branch  # Save the setting  # Edit the same trigger to change it to """"Use the main plan branch""""  # Click on """"View as Java Specs""""    h3. Expected Results  The trigger setting in the Java Specs does not refer to the branch plan    h3. Actual Results  The Java Specs shows the branch plan:      h3.Notes  After cloning environment, trigger setting changes are not reflected in View as Java Specs    h3. Workaround  Delete the trigger and recreate it again"""

summary: "SPECS Export missing SSH and SCP port method"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a plan with SCP Task or SSH task is exported as SPECS, neither port is exported    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan   # Add SCP or SSH task   # In the advanced option of the task config, add port number  # Navigate to Plan Configuration -> Actions->View plan as Java Specs     h3. Expected Results   The SSH task or SCP task configuration should contain the defined port method  h3. Actual Results  No port information is exported    h3. Workaround    Manually add the port methods"""

summary: "Bamboo Java Specs fails if the linked repository assigned to the plan is deleted and created back with the same name"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Bamboo Specs, both executing from the IDE and using RSS will fail if the linked used by the plan is deleted from Bamboo and created back with the same name.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan via specs using a linked repository   # Delete the linked repository   # Recreate the liked repository with the same name   # Run the specs again or commit a change to the specs-enabled repository    h3. Expected Results  The plan will be updated with the new repository ID  h3. Actual Results  The plan will not be published and the below exception is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file:    h3. Workaround  h5. 1. If using repository-stored Specs (RSS)   * Delete the plan from Bamboo UI before committing the new changes to the repository    h5. 2. If running specs from the IDE/Maven.   * Delete the repository at plan's repository settings before executing the specs code again."""

summary: "Unable to authenticate Bitbucket Cloud Wiki repository with username and password"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Using username and password and """"Git"""" type repository could not authenticate with Bitbucket Cloud Wiki repository    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Navigate to {{Administration >> Build Resources >> Linked repositories}}  # Click on """"Add repository""""  # Select """"Git"""" repository  # Use the Bitbucket Cloud Wiki repository, for example https://bitbucket.org/user/repo.git/wiki  # Select the option """"Username and password""""  # Fill in the credential (or with Bitbucket Cloud App password)  # Test the connection    h3. Expected Results  Connection test successful    h3. Actual Results  Connection test fail with the below exception is thrown in the atlasssian-bamboo.log file:      h3. Workaround  Use SSH URL with SSH key instead of HTTPS"""

summary: "Some special characters prevent artifacts from being downloaded from the UI"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If there is a single artifact matching the copy patterns and contains % or ; in the file name, Bamboo will fail to download and will show a """"Page not found"""" error  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set a script task with the following command:     # Create an artifact with the following copy pattern {{**/*.war}}   # Run a build   # On the build results go to artifacts and try to download    h3. Expected Results    The artifact downloads.  h3. Actual Results    !image-2020-01-15-17-21-39-441.png!  h3. Workaround    Set a script task to create an artifact matching the same copy pattern. If there is more than one artifact the following screen will be shown and the artifact file will be downloadable:   !image-2020-01-15-17-28-31-454.png!"""

summary: "Tests cases are duplicated after run with a customized revision"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Test cases are duplicated after a build is ran on a customized revision    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Build will run multiple times and tests are entered once and updated in the *test_case* table as expected  # Run a customized build with a different commit, tests are updated in the *test_case* table as expected  # Run the plan once again, tests are updated in the *test_case* table as expected  # Run a customized build with a different commit, tests are updated in the *test_case* table as expected  # Run the plan once again, tests are {color:red}*duplicated*{color}  h3. Expected Results  The *test_case* should only be entered once per plan and be updated with additional build runs  h3. Actual Results  The test cases are duplicated which affects the ability to track consistently    h3. Workaround    Upgrade to Bamboo 6.8.1 - This doesn't fix retroactively """

summary: "Bamboo artifact extraction does not release file descriptors on large zip files"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When Bamboo compresses large numbers of files for artifact transfer, it does not release the file descriptors so memory (disk space) is still used. This can quickly lead to {{java.io.IOException: No space left on device}}  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a build using S3 Artifact Handler   # Create a task that generates over 100 large files into a subdirectory   # Define an Artifact definition with copy pattern {{**.*}} in the subdirectory   # Create a job that requires this artifact   # Run both jobs multiple times (the error will eventually be seen here)    h3. Expected Results    Builds all complete as expected  h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo-agent.log file:    h3. Workaround    Restart the Remote agent"""

summary: "Clean working directory can report success despite being unable to remove all files"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Clean working directory (task, source-code checkout and post-build) can report success despite being unable to remove all files.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Set locale to non-UTF8:    # Set JVM to UTF-8    # Add script task:    # Enable Cleanup working directory after completion at *Job > Miscellaneous*    h3. Expected Results  Clean working directory should report a failure to clean the directory completely.    h3. Actual Results      h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Bamboo is unable to clean filenames with unicode characters unless OS locale is set to UTF8 "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  A regression since Bamboo 6.9.0 means that Bamboo is unable to remove files during clean working directory with Unicode characters in the filename unless the {{LC_CTYPE}} is configured to a UTF8 charset.     This can also cause repository stored specs scans to hang. The first scan will work since the checkout directory is empty however all the rest will fail and cause a """"hanging"""" scan that never completes.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Set locale to non-UTF8:  {code}  localectl set-locale LANG=C  {code}  # Set JVM to UTF-8  {code}  -Dfile.encoding=utf-8 -Dsun.jnu.encoding=utf-8  {code}  # Add script task:  {code}  touch fileö.txt  {code}  # Enable Cleanup working directory after completion at *Job > Miscellaneous*    h3. Expected Results  Clean working directory should remove the file.    Repository Stored Specs scans should succeed.    h3. Actual Results  File is not removed.    Still reports success:  {code}  simple 01-Jan-2020 01:01:01 Successfully removed working directory at '/home/bamboo-home/xml-data/build-dir/TEST-BAMBOO-JOB1'  {code}  * BAM-20784    RSS scans hang and the below is shown in the {{atlassian-bamboo.log}}:      h3. Workaround  # Set locale to a UTF-8 variant:    #*  Or just the {{LC_CTYPE}} if preferred:    # Restart Bamboo    NOTE: This can also occur if the environment variable doesn't include the variable *LANG=en_US.UTF-8*. In this case, the fix will be adding it or fix if it's incorrect.   """

summary: "Add option to encode UTF-8 BOM for MsBuild RSP file generation"
description: """Microsoft compilers and build tools expect some input files (such as the RSP file) to be encoded as UTF-8 BOM. Bamboo currently generates RSP files for the MsBuild task which are encoded UTF-8 no BOM.    Add UTF-8 BOM encoding option in the task configuration UI such that the user can select to generate a UTF-8 BOM RSP file which is more portable.    *Workaround*   Use a script task instead of MsBuild task. The script should: a) create the RSP file with the correct encoding, then b) call MSBuild and pass the resulting file as an argument.    Note: Make sure to set the correct code page for the shell from the script with the command:     before creating the RSP file."""

summary: "In a multi-repository scenario Bamboo still publishes a change detection required event when a remote event is received for a repository that is disabled on the trigger"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In a multi-repository scenario with Bitbucket Repository Triggered, Bamboo still publishes a change detection required event when a remote event is received for a repository that is disabled on the trigger. This causes an unnecessary change detection to be performed on the other triggering repositories, compounded significantly when there are large number of branch plans and can flood the plan exec queue.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a plan with two repositories pointed at master and two plan branches  # Each branch will point at their respective branch for the primary repository and master for the secondary  # Configure the trigger so that its only enabled for the primary repository.  # Push a change to master on the secondary repository    h3. Expected Results  The remote event is ignored and no change detection is performed, as the repository the event is received for is not a triggering repository.    h3. Actual Results  Bamboo publishes 6 change detection events; two for the master plan and each plan branch even though the secondary repository isn't a triggering repository. The primary repository is checked with a {{ls-remote}} even though the change happened on the secondary repository that was disabled for triggering:  Example:      h3. Workaround  Convert the repository that you don't want to trigger off, to a plain *Git* type repository instead of *Bitbucket Server*."""

summary: "Telemetry FTL NullPointerException"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When accessing telemetry links for any plans under Wallboard -> All plans, the ftl scripts throw the following error:    caused by a NULL buildsToList.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access """"All plans"""" telemetry directly without going via a Project (when being logged out).    h3. Expected Results    No error and show only the wallboard entries that has anonymous user read access.  h3. Actual Results    The below error is thrown in the log file:    h3. Workaround    Add the below lines and restart Bamboo  {noformat:title=telemetry.ftl}  <div id=""""wallboard"""">  [#if filteredBuilds??] <!-- this Line is new -->  [#assign buildsToList= filteredBuilds] {noformat}   """

summary: "Hung builds on remote agents are unable to stop spawned processes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Remote agents are unable to force stop spawned processes.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a build to executed on a remote agent   # The build process hangs, takes longer than expected, or becomes otherwise unresponsive   # There is currently no method for the agent to stop or force stop the process    h3. Expected Results    It's expected that a remote agent should be able to kill a hung process.    h3. Actual Results    The agent is unable to stop the spawned process because it has no parameters to determine if the process has hung.    h3. Workaround    The current workaround is to manually stop the process using a command such as “kill -9” or configuring a [hanging build event| https://confluence.atlassian.com/bamboo/configuring-the-hanging-build-event-289277188.html]."""

summary: "Concurrent builds overwrite test_case last_build_number causing duplication of tests"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Concurrent builds overwrite test_case last_build_number causing duplication of tests  h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Enable concurrent builds;  # Configure a plan that has tests and parsing, plus a script task """"sleep ${bamboo_timing}"""" and set the variable timing to 1; This script task is not connected to the bug, it's just an easy way to simulate shorter and longer builds;  # Run the plan with customized options (#1), overriding the timing variable to something like 30;  # Immediatly after running, run the plan again (#2), generating parallel builds of the same plan;  # The later run (#2) will finish before the earlier run (#1). When the build #1 finishes, it will override the last_build_number on the test cases of this plan;  # Run the plan again;      h3. Expected Results  Test is not duplicated  h3. Actual Results  We have the duplicated test given Bamboo didn't find last_build_number of #2; It will start counting the tests as if it'd never existed.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Upgrading to Embedded Crowd with an LDAP configuration missing port will fail with a confusing error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When upgrading Bamboo to a version with Embedded Crowd (6.6+), if the LDAP configuration in {{<bamboo-home>/xml-data/configuration/atlassian-user.xml}} does not contain a {{<port>}} value, the post-bootstrap upgrade task 60601 will fail with an error:      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Bamboo 6.2-6.5  # Configure an {{atlassian-user.xml}} file with an LDAP directory  # Remove (or comment out) the {{<port>}} value from the {{atlassian-user.xml}}  # Upgrade to Bamboo 6.6-6.10.4    h3. Expected Results  Upgrade will be successful, and new Bamboo version starts    h3. Actual Results  The upgrade tasks fail, and the following exception is thrown in the {{atlassian-bamboo.log}} file:      h3. Workaround    Update {{atlassian-user.xml}} to include the {{<port>...</port>}}."""

summary: "Deployment logs are named with a negative value and cannot be directly accessed via the UI, once DEPLOYMENT_RESULT primary key surpasses 32bit int"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Once primary key surpasses 32bit int deployment logs are named differently and cannot be directly accessed via the UI.    The log file is saved with the signed 32bit deployment result id on disk however the link expects the actual big int filename.    For example:   2012545028-2154037258-2170683537.log becomes 2012545028-2154037258--2124283705.log because the last number, 2170683537 == -2124283705 after the conversion.    When attempting to download the deployment log, you get *Page not found*.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # When deployment_result_id reaches 2,147,483,648 (max int + 1), perform a release  # Attempt to download the log     h3. Expected Results  The log can be downloaded.    h3. Actual Results  The log is visible as a snippet in the deployment result.  When attempting to download the full deployment log, you get *Page not found*.    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file:      h3. Workaround  # Click *Download* log  # When presented with the Page not found, truncate the log file from the URL. Example:  # {{$BAMBOO_BASE_URL/deployment-download/2154037258/build_logs/2012545028-2154037258-2170683537.log}} *becomes*  # {{$BAMBOO_BASE_URL/deployment-download/2154037258/build_logs}}  # You will now be presented with a directory listing of *all* the log files for that environment; choose the one with the matching timestamp of the deployment result."""

summary: "Cannot create a release from createDeploymentVersion.action once DEPLOYMENT_PROJECT primary key surpasses 32bit int"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Once {{DEPLOYMENT_PROJECT.deployment_project_id}} (primary key) surpasses 32bit int, cannot create a release from {{createDeploymentVersion.action}}.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # When deployment_project_id reaches 2,147,483,648 (max int + 1), perform a release  # Attempt to create a release from *Deployment Environment >> ... >> Create Release*  or *Build Result >> Create Release*    h3. Expected Results  Can use the *Create Release* UI without an error.    h3. Actual Results  The UI will show *Can not create release, no deployment project with id 0 could be found* or the user will get *Access Denied*    The below exceptions are thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file:        In case of Access Denied:      h3. Workaround  Create a release from the {{selectVersionForExecute.action}} which can be reached via *Deployment Dashboard or after clicking an Environment >> Deploy Icon (It looks like a Cloud with an arrow)*"""

summary: "Source code checkout task cannot be added or edited on deployment plans, once VCS_LOCATION primary key surpasses 32bit int"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Once VCS_LOCATION primary key surpasses 32bit int, Source code checkout task cannot be added or edited on deployment plans.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # When vcs_location_id reaches 2,147,483,648 (max int + 1), edit a deployment plan  # Attempt to add and save a source-code checkout task (or view an existing one)    h3. Expected Results  Source-code checkout task can be added.    h3. Actual Results  An error is shown in the UI upon hitting save:  *For input string: """"2548170753""""* (where the # is the ID of the repository)    No exception is found in the atlassian-bamboo.log.    h3. Workaround    The only workaround is to modify or add the source-code checkout via Bamboo Specs instead of the UI.    # Export the deployment project as a Bamboo Spec: *Edit Deployment Project >> ... >> View Project as Bamboo Spec*  # Take the exported spec and create a Bamboo Specs project using that code: [Tutorial: Create a simple plan with Bamboo Java Specs|https://confluence.atlassian.com/bamboo/tutorial-create-a-simple-plan-with-bamboo-java-specs-894743911.html]  # Add or modify the Source-code checkout task. Example:    # Push the spec to the server:  """

summary: "When using Docker Runner, Bamboo ignores the container directory for the Docker script"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using Docker Runner, Bamboo ignores the container directory for the Docker script    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a plan with one job.  # (Optional) Create a script task to print information. For example:    # Under """"Docker"""", choose """"Docker container"""". For the image, choose one. For example: """"ubuntu:latest"""".  # Under volumes, change the default working directory mapping. For example, """"Container directory"""" will now be set """"/tmp"""".  # Run the build.    h3. Expected Results  Bamboo will use """"/tmp"""" as the default working directory inside the container. This will also be reflected on the script <temp-path>/runInDockerXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX.sh     h3. Actual Results    When runInDockerXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX.sh is created, it uses the working directory defined in the cfg.xml file (the file name varies depending on which type of agent is being used), ignoring the mapping set before.     This will show in the build log in the following way:      h3. Workaround    Use the default value in the """"Container directory"""" field, which is ${bamboo.working.directory}"""

summary: "NullPointerException when starting Bamboo IM server"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Bamboo shows NullPointerException on the logs when the JVM parameter *atlassian.mail.senddisabled* is set to true and if there are no IM server configured on Bamboo.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add the following JMV parameter on setenv.sh:     # Start Bamboo    h3. Expected Results    Bamboo should show something like this:    h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file:    h3. Workaround    Remove the JVM parameter."""

summary: "Bamboo fails to ignore spaces at the end of the ""bamboo.home"" value in the bamboo-init.properties file"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Bamboo fails to ignore spaces at the end of the """"bamboo.home"""" value in the bamboo-init.properties file.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  In the file bamboo-init.properties add some spaces at the end, for example:      h3. Expected Results  Spaces will be removed or ignored.     h3. Actual Results  Spaces are ignored for all folders but """"jms-store"""". This will creates two home folders:  - One without spaces, with all the content *but* """"jms-store"""".  - One with spaces, with the folder """"jms-store"""" inside.     h3. Workaround    Fix the file bamboo-init.properties by removing the spaces. """

summary: "System error links for deployments point to the wrong URL to browse the result"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  System error links for deployments point to the wrong URL to browse the result    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a deployment with a SCP task  # Point it to a host that will timeout, e.g. 1.1.1.1  # Start the deploy  # Stop the deploy immediately  # Visit *Bamboo Administration >> System errors*  # Click the hyperlink for the deployment ID    h3. Expected Results  You're taken to the deployment result page with the logs and errors.    h3. Actual Results  {code}  An unexpected error has occurred  Could not find the '249298945-249397249' build  {code}    The below warning is shown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file:      h3. Workaround  # Click *View Error Log*   # Take the final number in the hyperlink. E.g. In the link  {quote}  *Build 249298945-249397249 259162116* : Error occurred while executing the build for ...  {quote}  take *259162116* and insert it into the below URL to access the deployment result:  #* {{$BAMBOO_BASE_URL/deploy/viewDeploymentResult.action?deploymentResultId=259162116}}"""

summary: "The documentation link to create  Amazon S3 bucket in Bamboo Artifacts Handler UI is broken"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The documentation link to create Amazon S3 bucket in Bamboo Artifacts Handler UI is broken  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to Bamboo Artifacts Handler UI   # Click on the Learn More link (You can either use the name of an existing Amazon S3 bucket or type a new bucket name, which creates a new bucket in your Amazon infrastructure. [Learn more)|http://docs.atlassian.com/bamboo/docs-0610/Amazon+S3+artifact+storage]    h3. Expected Results    Links to a page with the documentation  h3. Actual Results    Page Not Found  h3. Workaround    None"""

summary: "Windows installer doesn't work with AdoptJDK 8"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Can't install Bamboo from Windows installer if only AdoptJDK is available at environment  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install AdoptJDK 8 at Windows   # Run Bamboo x64 installer    h3. Expected Results    It's possible to install Bamboo  h3. Actual Results    Installer shows error message that it can't find Java. Being provided path to AdoptJDK java.exe installer complains this application is not supported  h3. Workaround    Install Oracle JDK or use zip distributive of Bamboo"""

summary: "Credentials_id range above 2,147,483,647 breaks credentials selectors"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The credentials table keeps shared credential definitions in Bamboo. For credentials_id field the values larger than 2,147,483,647 some functionality would fail.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a shared credentials entry with the ID value larger than 2,147,483,647. (These ID values are typically created by hibernate so one may have to manually adjust the credentials_id attribute value.)   # Create a task that can use shared credentials, for example the SCP task. In the configuration of the task attempt to use the shared credentials selector.    h3. Expected Results    The task configuration succeeds.  h3. Actual Results    A validation error message in the UI similar to: Can't find shared credentials by ID: 2154790914.  h3. Workaround    There is no workaround."""

summary: "Branch plan drop-down doesn't differentiate between enabled/disabled branches"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The branch plan drop-down button does not differentiate between enabled and disabled branches as older Bamboo versions.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Bamboo 7.0.1.   # Access any plan that contains branches and disable any branch.   # Click on the drop-down button in the plan branch.    h3. Expected Results    Bamboo should differentiate between enabled and disabled branches as older Bamboo versions:    (i) Enabled branches are shown in blue and at the top of the list and disabled branches are shown in gray at the bottom of the list.  h3. !image-2020-03-19-15-52-11-412.png|width=518,height=334!    h3. Actual Results    In Bamboo 7.0.1, all the branches are shown in gray color:      !image-2020-03-19-15-51-48-131.png|width=409,height=202!    !image-2020-03-19-15-51-04-538.png|width=415,height=357!  h3. Workaround    Currently, there's no workaround."""

summary: "Group configuration is not working for groups with ""/"" forward slashes"
description: """h3. Summary    Group configuration is not anymore working for groups with """"/"""" forward slashes    Bamboo doesn't allow modifying groups with some special characters like """"/"""", but they can be created in Bamboo, LDAP or JIRA for example.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a group called """"name/name"""" in Bamboo   # Try to navigate to the Bamboo group permissions page.    h3. Expected Results    The permissions can be configured  h3. Actual Results    The Browser will receive a 400 error for the following request:    The following dialog will show up:   !Screen Shot.png|thumbnail!  h3. Note   * This problem is reproduced in Bamboo 7.0.2   * This issue is probably affecting other characters (it is also present for the backslash for example)    h3. Work Around   * To delete a group, first open the group's page and then delete from the top right hand corner.   * To add or remove users, you will need to navigate to the user and add/remove the user to group from there"""

summary: "Empty wallboard / FTL error in telemetry.action"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Wallboard doesn't display correctly  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # go to wallboard    h3. Expected Results    Wallboard displays  h3. Actual Results    Wallboard is empty, ftl error reported in the logs.  h3. Workaround    Go to _<bamboo_installation_dir>/atlassian-bamboo_ folder. Find the file called _telemetry.ftl_ and replace it with the one attached to this ticket.     """

summary: "Upgrading Bamboo results in the error - The data types ntext and varchar are incompatible in the equal to operator."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When Bamboo is upgraded from 6.10.x to 7.0.2 with MSSQL 2012, we get this error  {noformat:title=}  ERROR [localhost-startStop-1] [AbstractUpgradeManager] com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The data types ntext and varchar are incompatible in the equal to operator.      h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Deployment with Docker Container will have NPE at the end"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Running Deployment Project with Docker Container will have NPE at the end    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure a Deployment Project to run with Docker Container (Docker Runner) instead of Agent Environment  # Trigger a deployment    h3. Expected Results  It will run successfully without error    h3. Actual Results  The below exception is thrown in the deployment log file:      h3. Notes  The deployment still finish with status Success even though the NPE happens    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Bamboo fails to download artifacts in plan branches when using Specs branches"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Having Specs Branches enabled, Bamboo will fail to run a build where an Artifact Download task is configured.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Have Bamboo 7.0.2, with Bitbucket integrated to it.   # In Bitbucket, create a repository for the specs code, which will deploy two plans, PlanA and PlanB  # Configure PlanA to create an artifact.  # Configure PlanB to download that artifact, and in the field """"Artifact name"""", select a specific artifact.   # Create a linked repository for the specs repository. Enable Specs on it.   # Make sure PlanA and PlanB have this repository as default repository in the plan.   # Create a branch. Now both PlanA and PlanB will have this branch created as plan branches.   # Run PlanA.  # Run PlanB.    h3. Expected Results  PlanB will run successfully.    h3. Actual Results    PlanB fails to start the run. In the logs we see:      h3. Workaround    If, in the Artifact Download task, you select """"Artifact name -> All artifacts"""", you will be able to bypass this bug."""

summary: "Bamboo fails to create Specs code when ""Build warnings parser"" task is present. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Bamboo fails to create Specs code when """"Build warnings parser"""" task is present.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a plan with at least one repository in it configured.   # Create a task of type """"Build warnings parser"""".  # Explicitly set the repository in the task configuration.   # Click on """"View Plan as Java Specs"""".    h3. Expected Results  Specs code is generated and shown     h3. Actual Results    Bamboo fails to generate code. In the logs we see:      h3. Workaround    By choosing """"Default repository"""" instead of explicitly choosing a repository, Specs code will be generated successfully."""

summary: "RSS branch does not work with slash in Windows"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If Bamboo hosted in Windows and a branch with forward slash in the name is used, RSS branch specs will fail to run    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Bamboo in Windows  # Integrate Bamboo with Bitbucket Server  # Create Bitbucket Server repository in Bamboo  # Enable Specs for the repository  # Confirm that the Specs run successfully to create a Bamboo Plan  # Create a branch name """"feature/testing""""  # Create the branch plan for it  # Check the Specs Status for the branch """"feature/testing""""    h3. Expected Results  It run successfully    h3. Actual Results  The below exception is thrown in the specs log:    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file:      h3. Workaround  Remove bamboo-specs folder in the branch so that Bamboo will not run Specs build on branch plan"""

summary: "shortPlanName contains branch name for deployments"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    According to https://confluence.atlassian.com/bamboo/bamboo-variables-289277087.html bamboo.shortPlanName should contain the name of the plan. In the case of deployment plans this variable contains the branch name (instead of the corresponding build plan name).  h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a script task that only contains echo ${bamboo.shortPlanName} in a deployment plan.  # Create a release from the master branch. The value of the bamboo.shortPlanName is the correct one, the name of the build plan.  # Create a release from another branch. The value of the bamboo.shortPlanName is the name of the branch instead of the name of the build plan.      h3. Expected Results    The variable value is the name of the plan.    h3. Actual Results    The variable value is the name of the branch.    h3. Workaround    There is no workaround."""

summary: "Internal Server Error instead of 404"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Internal Server Error instead of 404  h3. Steps to Reproduce    ie this query: [https://HOSTNAME/build/result/viewBuildResults.action?buildKey=PLUG-PLUGB5D3X|https://ecosystem-bamboo.internal.atlassian.com/build/result/viewBuildResults.action?buildKey=PLUG-PLUGB5D3X]  h3. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Specs repository Link broken "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Specs repository links break for the nth repository and work fine for the first repository.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Bamboo Project   # Create 2 Linked repositories: test, test2   # Enable Bamboo Specs for that repository in the Linked repository configuration   # Go to Project settings page into """"Bamboo Specs repositories"""" tab   # Add the 2 linked repositories   # Refresh the page with and look at the links for repositories    h3. Expected Results    The first repository link works ok and the nth repository works ok.  h3. Actual Results    The first repository link works ok    The nth repository links fail    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no workaround."""

summary: "The Specs repository list shows a deleted repository."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The repository list shows repositories that are already deleted in linked repository.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # For the second repository created, run the following SQL:  {code:sql|title=Postgres}  update VCS_LOCATION   set MARKED_FOR_DELETION = true   where NAME = 'test2';  {code}  {code:sql|title=MySQL}  update VCS_LOCATION   set MARKED_FOR_DELETION = 1   where NAME = 'test2';  {code}    h3. Expected Results    The repository should not appear in the Bamboo specs repositories page.  h3. Actual Results    The repository will appear on:   * *Bamboo Specs repositories* page    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Bamboo logo and some icons are not vertically aligned well to the center"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Bamboo logo and some icons (nav links, help, administration) are not vertically aligned well to the center. The help and administration mouse-over highlights also overshoot the bar which results in a shadowing that extends onto the page.    This is more apparent in Firefox but also affects Chrome.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Open Bamboo  # Mouse-over the help and administration icons.    h3. Expected Results  All nav bar elements are well vertically aligned to the center.    h3. Actual Results   !bamboo-header.png!    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Application Navigator refresh button doesn't work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Application Navigator refresh button doesn't work    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # In Bamboo's Administration section, navigate to """"Application Navigator""""  # Click on the refresh button    h3. Expected Results    The page is refreshed    h3. Actual Results    - Nothing happens.   - The logs will not display any errors.   - Inspecting the page, we will see the error:          h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Username/group case changed breaks permission"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If the permission is granted when username is in lower case, the permission does not work when the username changed to upper case    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a user """"admin"""" in Bamboo internal directory  # Grant Admin permission to the user """"admin"""" in Bamboo Global Permission  # Create a user """"Admin"""" in Crowd internal directory  # Setup Crowd User Directory in Bamboo  # Set Crowd directory to be higher than Bamboo internal directory at Bamboo User Directories setting  # Check the granted permission at Bamboo Global Permissions    h3. Expected Results  The granted permission still works    h3. Actual Results  Bamboo Global Permissions list shows the user """"Admin"""" instead of the lower case """"admin"""" but the granted permissions are missing (the checkbox are not selected)    h3. Note  The same problem will happen if Bamboo internal directory have upper case group name """"Test"""" while Crowd directory have lowercase """"test""""    h3. Workaround  # [Enforce lowercase username/groups|https://confluence.atlassian.com/crowd/enforcing-lower-case-usernames-and-groups-for-an-application-179446857.html] in Crowd server  # Synchronise Crowd directory in Bamboo  Bamboo will only get lowercase usernames/groups from Crowd server now"""

summary: "Bamboo fails to clone from mirror when Branch merging option is enabled in a plan"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When the linked repository is configured to use 'Mirror', Bamboo clones from the master repository instead of mirror repository whenever we have """"Branch merging enabled"""" activated.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a linked repository (Bitbucket server type) and choose a mirror from the dropdown (and Enable Verbose logging under Advanced option).   # Add this repository to a plan and have a Source code checkout task and enable Branch merging in that plan (choosing to create a plan branch when pull request is created).   # Create a new pull request in Bitbucket on that repository.   # A new plan branch is created and run successfully.    h3. Expected Results    The build is expected to clone from a mirror when branch merging is activated.  h3. Actual Results    The build is cloned from master repository.  h3. Workaround    Perform branch merging using a Script task."""

After login, it will have error to access sign up page. User still unable to perform sign up    h3. Workaround 1  Disable Anonymous user Global Permission    h3. Workaround 2  # Shutdown Bamboo  # Edit the file {{<bamboo-install>/atlassian-bamboo/feature/dashboard/welcome-mat/welcome-mat.soy}} to remove the following lines:    # Restart Bamboo  """

summary: "Variable substitution failure not enforced for Repository Tag Task"

Steps to Reproduce   # create a plan   # create a Job with a Repository Tag task with *${bamboo.jira.version}* as the tag   # build the plan with a repository trigger    h3. Actual Results    Job succeeds with the Jira application variables key as the repository Tag    !tagtask.jpg|width=234,height=165!  h3. Workaround    None at the moment"""

summary: "Page refresh non-stop if rerun build after changing stage job"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Rerunning a build result after changing the stage job will cause the build to refresh non-stop  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create two Jobs in the same Stage   # First JobA with Script task """"sleep 10""""   # Second JobB with Script task """"echo hello""""   # Create two local agents so that both Jobs can be performed at the same time   # Run the build   # Create a new Second Stage without any new Job   # Move the second JobB to the Second Stage   # Rerun the build result in Step 5    h3. Expected Results    JobA will run first before starting JobB.  h3. Actual Results   * JobA and JobB start running at the same time   * JobB finishes first even though it is in the Second Stage   * Even though both Jobs have completed, the main build status is still In Progress   * Clicking on JobB in the build result will make the page refreshing non-stop    h3. Workaround    Restart Bamboo"""

summary: "Timed-out plan-branches still visible in Plan-Branch tree"

If I click on the plan branch I get an error message like: """"An unexpected error has occured: Build xyz does not exist"""".     On the main page of the build plan, the deleted plan branches are not visible, even after clicking on """"View all branches"""".    There should be a way to remove the deprecated plan branches from the tree."""

summary: "When creating new environments with YAML, ""default-environment-permissions"" doesn't work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When creating new environments with YAML, """"default-environment-permissions"""" doesn't work    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a deployment project.  # Make it deployable with YAML.   # In the YAML file, create a brand new environment. For example:    # Set permissions for this environment using """"default-environment-permissions"""". For example:    # Deploy the environment and permissions.    h3. Expected Results  Environment will have the permissions set.    h3. Actual Results  Environment doesn't have permissions    h3. summary: "Bamboo doesn't handle command output in encoding different than UTF-8 well"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a command outputs something to stdout in encoding different than UTF-8, Bamboo won't display it correctly in the log page.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a build with a script task   # As a part of the task run command that outputs non-UTF-8 string, with special characters to stdout, for instance java program shown below run with `-Dfile.encoding=windows-1257` java command line switch   # Repeat running Bamboo with the same encoding option: it will still fail.         h3. Expected Results    If Bamboo is run with encoding set to command encoding, logs should be displayed correctly  h3. Actual Results    Log handling is hard coded as UTF-8, but command output will be handled according to file.encoding option resulting in incorrectly displayed logs (??? instead of actual characters)  h3. Workaround    In order to see the stdout output:   * set Bamboo character encoding to match the command's encoding (using `-Dfile.encoding' JVM parameter)   * in the log page, click on the view link (at this point the command output will still be mangled)   * switch the page's character encoding in the browser options to view the command output"""

summary: "Upgrading Bamboo to 7 causes any changes to directories to be reset to default values within a Docker task"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Upgrading Bamboo to 7 causes any changes to directories to be reset to default values within a Docker task. The fields affected are:  * Container working directory  * Host directory   * Container data volume    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Start a Bamboo 6.8.3 instance  # Configure a build and deployment plan with a Docker task, change the type to a Docker run task  # Change the default values for:  #* Container working directory  #* Host directory   #* Container data volume  # Upgrade to Bamboo 7.0.4    h3. Expected Results  Existing configurations for are preserved     h3. Actual Results  Configurations are reset to default:  * Container working directory: /data  * Host directory: ${bamboo.working.directory}  * Container data volume: /data    The below is logged in {{$BAMBOO_HOME/atlassian-bamboo.log}} when upgrading:      h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Gatekeeper merge and push does not trigger Specs build"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Modifying Specs from branch plan and then using Gatekeeper to merge and push into master plan does not trigger Specs build in master plan when there's a merge conflict  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Use the following YAML Specs to create the plan:     # Create a branch, commit and push.   # Change specs on this branch to change the stage name and echo part:     # Commit, but *don't push.*   # Change specs on master, making a change that will cause merge conflict on gatekeeper for the branch (change the same thing):     # Commit and push.   # Bamboo Specs will detect new commit on master and update configuration on master.   # Now push the branch changes from Step 3.   # Bamboo will detect and update configuration changes on branch.   # Gatekeeper will try to merge, merge conflict, build fails.   # Fix the merge conflicts *on the branch* without merging branches, just make sure the changes are enough, for example changing the echo to be same as master branch but change     # Commit and push.   # Bamboo Specs will detect commit on branch and update configuration on branch.   # Gatekeeper will merge branch into master.    h3. Expected Results  After branch plan finish running in Step 14 and push to master branch, master branch will perform Specs build    h3. Actual Results   * Bamboo Specs will detect the new commit on master, but it will use the last commit of the branch to update Specs, it ignores the merge commit from gatekeeper. The consequence is that Bamboo Specs ignore the changes that would update master, leaving the VCS master with a different Bamboo Specs than what is currently applied in the plan.   * From the above Steps to Reproduce, the stage name is not changed in master plan   * The below log message is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file:    xxx = commit in Step 11 from branch plan  yyy = commit in Step 5 from master plan    h3. Workaround  * When merge conflicts happen with Bamboo Specs in the gatekeeper, rerun Specs from the Repository Settings.   * Alternatively, disable Bamboo push-on-success merge and then perform the merge and push manually """

Env 1 is not present in list    Exception in logs      h3. Workaround       Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Update documentation to state that agents do not get automatically upgraded when going from 6.9.x or less to 6.10.x or more"

Issue Summary    Since we updated the wrapper for remote agents on 6.10, the remote agents have to be re-installed when upgrading as the upgrade is not picked up automatically due to the wrapper update.    This information is not reflected on our docs:  * https://confluence.atlassian.com/bamboo/bamboo-upgrade-guide-720411366.html  * https://confluence.atlassian.com/bamboo/bamboo-remote-agent-installation-guide-289276832.html    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Upgrade from 6.9.x or less to 6.10.x or more    h3. Expected Results  Remote agents are upgraded automatically    h3. Actual Results    This is not the case due to the wrapper update. However, our docs still state that this is the case.    h3. Workaround    No workaround"""

Steps to Reproduce   # Connect Bamboo to LDAP repo   # Initiate bulk update of users at LDAP side   # Synchronize users   # See user synchronization started    h3. Expected Results    Builds can be normally build  h3. Actual Results    Builds don't start as some DB tables are locked  h3. Workaround       Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Branch plan created through a pull request is not disabled when the PR is deleted"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Branch plan create through a pull request is not disabled when the PR is deleted.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a plan, with the branch configuration set to """"When pull request is created""""  # Create a repository in Bitbucket server. Link this repository to the plan.   # In Bitbucket, create a branch and then a PR. A plan branch will be created in Bamboo.  # In Bitbucket, delete the PR.    h3. Expected Results  Just like when a branch is deleted, we expect the plan branch to be disabled immediately.     h3. Actual Results    The plan branch remains enabled.     h3. Workaround    The plan branch needs to be disabled or deleted manually."""

summary: "Use of ""Agent-local artifact handler"" for a remote agent build creates error on producer and on consumer side"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    By checking """"Agent-local artifact handler"""" for a build that runs on a remote agent, two instead of one artifacts are created on producer side and the consuming deployment environment fails with error """"Unable to download artifact"""".  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a simple plan that basically produces an artifact (could be as simple as a file created and filled with some text per a Bamboo Script task).   # Create the appropriate artifact definition for this plan with """"Required"""" set.   # Check """"Agent-local artifact handler"""" in the Bamboo UI under _Cog >> Overview >> Artifact handlers_, for shared and non-shared.   # Create a consumer which is a deployment environment, there run the Artifact Download task and select that artifact from the producer plan to copy it in.   # The deployment environemnt has trigger """"After successful build"""" set.   # Either delegate the build to run on a remote agent or set the remote agent's Capabilities and this plan's Requirements to match and thus dynamically force the build to run on the agent.   # Finally run the build plan, either manually or per e.g. commit trigger in case the build plan contains a Source Code Download task.    h3. Expected Results   * The build plan creates one single artifact.   * The deployment environment is able to successfully download the artifact being produced by the build plan.    h3. Actual Results   * The *build plan creates two artifacts*, even if its build working directory is cleaned prior to a new build, of which only the second one is accessible and can be opened. Both artifacts  have the same and a valid file size and seem identical.   * The *deployment environment* with which the artifact is shared *fails* with the log containing the following error message.        h3. Workaround    As long as that plan is running on Bamboo Server or a local agent, everything behaves as expected. So basically two options allow overcoming that error:   # Run this build with the configuration from above on Bamboo Server or on a local agent.   # Stay with the build on a remote agent, but uncheck """"Agent-local artifact handler"""", so basically change the Artifact handler setting back to default."""

Expected Results    Choose multiple subnet IDs and save successfully.  h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the server log file:    h3. summary: "If a test name has a dot in its name (""."" character), Bamboo won't parse test results properly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If a test name has a dot in its name (""""."""" character), Bamboo won't parse test results properly    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a plan that produces test results, with tests in it (for example, maven + JUnit).   # Make sure at least one test name has a dot in it.    h3. Expected Results  Bamboo parses results normally.    h3. Actual Results    Bamboo will split tests with every dot that finds in the name, making tests results inconsistent with what is reported in the build log.     h3. Workaround    Avoid using dots in test names. """

summary: "When changing artifact dependencies on build plan job, changes are not stored"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When changing artifact dependencies on build plan job, changes are not stored. After clicking *Save* to apply the changes and consequently refreshing the page, the changes are reverted to the previous value.    Also, these changes do not appear on the audit logs.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan with two stages, one job on each stage.   # For job one, create 2 artifacts.   # For job two, in the section """"Artifacts -> Artifacts dependencies"""" Create a new artifact dependency to one of the artifacts.   # Click on *Edit*. Change the artifact selected. Click *Save.*   # Refresh page.  # Go to Plan Audit logs check for the dependency change information.    h3. Expected Results    The changes are stored and reflected after refreshing the page.  h3. Actual Results    The changes are not stored, and after refreshing the page we can see that the artifact dependency value still reflects the previous artifact selected.  h3. Workaround    The only possible workaround is to delete the existing artifact dependency and create a new one with the correct value."""

summary: "Specs build fail when creating new Artifact Download task"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Branch plan Specs builds fail when creating new Artifact Download task if there is duplicate branch plan    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create Specs plan with the following YAML:    # Create branch """"test"""" that have the same YAML Specs in Bitbucket Server  It will automatically create branch plan """"test"""" in Bamboo  # Create a duplicate branch plan in Bamboo, two Bamboo branch plan """"test"""" and """"test 1"""" using the same branch """"test"""" in Bitbucket Server  # Modify YAML Specs in branch """"test"""" with the following content:      h3. Expected Results  Specs build for branch """"test"""" will succeed    h3. Actual Results  Specs build for branch """"test"""" fail. The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-bamboo.log file:      h3. Notes  * If no duplicate branch plan, the Specs build will work fine  * If the branch plan named """"test"""" is deleted (branch plan """"test 1"""" still remain), the problem still happen  * If the branch plan named """"test 1"""" is deleted (branch plan """"test"""" still remain), Specs build works fine    h3. Workaround  There is no need to use Artifact Download task. A Shared artifact will be downloaded automatically to the next stage without Artifact Download task."""

Expected Results  Bamboo should identify the cache is corrupted during the initial build, recreate it, and retry to run the build.  h3. Actual Results  The initial build greys out and the below exception is thrown in the atlassian.bamboo.log file:      then, the change detection will also identify the problem:      And finally the cache is deleted:      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Import YAML spec in a repository with bamboo-specs/bamboo.yaml that has:       {code:java}  Default Job:    key: JOB1    docker:      image: ubuntu      volumes:        ${bamboo.working.directory}: ${bamboo.working.directory}        ${bamboo.tmp.directory}: ${bamboo.tmp.directory}      docker-run-arguments:      - --net=host  {code}  2. Run specs scan.  h3. Expected Results    The created plan, namely docker runner config should container extra args --net=host  h3. Actual Results    Docker run args are ignored. The resulting yaml looks like this:    h3. summary: "Rerunning a build after deleting a stage can break result removal and expiry"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Rerunning a build after deleting a stage can break result removal and expiry.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a 3 stage plan  # Run a build  # Delete the 2nd stage  # *Rerun* the build from Step 2 (don't perform a *new* build, if it was build #1, rerun build result #1)  # Attempt to manually delete the result or expire it    h3. Expected Results  The result can be removed or expired.    h3. Actual Results  Result cannot be deleted or expired.    The below exception is thrown in the $BAMBOO_HOME/logs/atlassian-bamboo.log (or UI if deleting a result) file:      You may also see the following:    * This stacktrace has been heavily truncated    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Bamboo is redacting every occurrence of a password variable's value in build logs"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Bamboo is trying to find the value of a secret variable in the logs (e.g. password) and redact every occurrence of it.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Add a plan variable named *bamboo.password* with value *build*.  !Screen Shot 2020-08-24 at 09.05.20.png|thumbnail!  # Create a Script task with the following content:  #* echo """"This is my build!""""  !Screen Shot 2020-08-24 at 09.07.14.png|thumbnail!  # Trigger a new build.  # Check the logs of the build.    h3. Expected Results    # The build should echo the message: This is my build!  # None of the occurrences of the word *build* in the logs should be redacted.    h3. Actual Results    # The build displays the following message:    # Every occurrence of the word build in the logs has been redacted:      This makes it pretty easy to figure out what is the value of the *bamboo.password* variable.    h3. Workaround    Change your password to a word or combination of letters that's not present in the build logs i.e. make sure there's no other occurrences of it in the logs."""

summary: "Incorrect deployment project artifact linked data not fixed during upgrade"

Steps to Reproduce  # Install Bamboo 7.0.2  # Integrate with Bitbucket Server  # Use the following RSS Bamboo YAML Specs:    # Run Specs build in Bamboo to create the build plan and deployment project  # Create a new branch in Bitbucket Server  It will automatically create branch plan in Bamboo. The Specs build in the branch plan incorrectly linked deployment project with the branch plan  # Run master build plan or deployment project  It works fine  # Upgrade Bamboo to version 7.1.1  # Delete the branch plan in Bamboo  # Run the build plan or deployment project    h3. Expected Results  Deployment manage to download the artifact successfully    h3. Actual Results  The below exception is thrown in the deployment log file:      h3. Notes  * The incorrect deployment project linked bug have been fixed in Bamboo 7.1.1 but the problematic data isn't fixed during the upgrade  * If there are 100 repositories with Specs and incorrect linked data, it will be troublesome to rerun Specs builds for all affected repositories    h3. Workaround  # Use the following SQL to look for affected repositories:    # Rerun Specs build for the repository in the main branch again"""

summary: "Changing/Editing branch variable values in Bamboo, ends up changing another variable value"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Changing/Editing branch variable values in Bamboo, ends up changing another variable value  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to {{Plan Configuration >> Variables}}   # Create two variables:  ||Variable Name||Value||  |test1|first|  |test2|second|     # Navigate to {{Plan Branch Configuration >> Variables}}   # Add variable """"test1"""" with value """"first""""   # Click on Edit to the value """"first"""" to change it to be """"newvalue""""   Somehow, Variable Name """"test1"""" is shown as """"test2"""". It seems that it changes to to the first not-overwritten variable.   # Click on Save   Variable Name is shown as """"test1"""" again   # Refresh the page   # Add both variables """"test1"""" and """"test2"""" to override   # Edit the Variable Name or Value for """"test1"""" or """"test2""""   # Try to create new Variable for the branch plan    h3. Expected Results   * Step 5 Variable Name should not automatically changed to """"test2"""" when editing the Variable Value   * Step 7 refreshing the page should show Variable Name """"test1"""" with Variable Value """"newvalue""""   * Step 10 should not be possible to add new variable to branch plan: BAM-17604    h3. Actual Results   * Step 7 refreshing the page shows Variable Name """"test2"""" with Variable Value """"newvalue"""" even though it is supposed to be """"test1""""   * Step 9 after adding all variables to override, editing Variable Value does not cause Variable Name to have drop down list anymore. Variable Name field have become Text field. Variable Name can be edited to become a new variable that does not exist previously   * Step 10 is possible to create new variable for branch plan after adding all variables to override    h3. Workaround    If want to modify Variable Value that have been added to override, delete the Variable from the list of Override first and then use the ADD function to change the Variable Value     """

summary: "Plan branch does not inherit the quarantined tests from master"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Plan branch does not inherit the quarantined tests from master if the master plan already ran the same tests in a job that was deleted.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan with Junit tests;   # Run the tests and make sure that at least one will fail;   # Quarantined the failed tests;   # Delete the job that runs the tests;   # Create a new job to run the same tests;   # Run the plan and quarantine the failed test;   # Create a plan branch.    h3. Expected Results    The plan branch creation succeeds and has the same quarantined tests from master, also the initial build runs.  h3. Actual Results    The plan branch is created; however, the tests are not quarantined and the initial build does not run.   In the atlassian-bamboo.log we can see the following error:    Also, in the test_class table, we can check the deleted job being referenced at the master_job_id column.  h3. Workaround    Currently, there's no workaround for this issue.    h3. Solution    Upgrade Bamboo to 7+ version."""

Expected Results    Bamboo upgrades with no errors.  h3. Actual Results    The upgrade process fails. In the logs we can see:     h3. A workaround will be added here when available    For version 7.1.1: [^atlassian-bamboo-web-7.1.1-varsizepatch.jar]   # Stop Bamboo.   # Download the file:   # Run the following queries:       # Navigate to the folder <bamboo_server_directory>/atlassian-bamboo/WEB-INF/lib/. Back it up.   # Place the new jar in that folder, and change its name to atlassian-bamboo-web-7.1.1.jar, to make it look like the previous one.   # Start Bamboo again."""

summary: "Builds can be queued before active objects import (into a fresh instance) completes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Builds can be queued before active objects import into a fresh instance completes. Impact is mostly unknown. If the build that makes it into the queue relies on an Active Objects entity, it may fail.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a plan with a repository and repository polling, set the interval really low (like 1s)  # Export the instance to XML  # Stop the instance  # Create a new Bamboo instance  # Before importing, commit to the repository so it finds a change  # Progress through wizard and import the XML backup from Step 2    h3. Expected Results  Server transitions to PAUSED and no builds make it into the queue before Active Objects import.    h3. Actual Results  Server transitions to PAUSING (not paused) as the build gets into the queue before the Active Objects import begins.    The below can be observed in the logs:      h3. Workaround  Disable all build triggers before performing the export."""

summary: "First merge attempt of feature brach to master fails on cloned build plan if no previus build on master run"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    No initial build is run on a cloned Bamboo build plan when cloning an existing plan by pressing button """"Save and continue"""" and not button """"Clone"""".    Hence for that new and cloned build plan no build on the linked repository's master branch is run and thus no Git repository cache exists for it.    Apparently Bamboo's change detection is causing an issue here, so that on a first Git merge attempt the cloned plan is not triggered per the set commit trigger.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Clone a plan as dewcribed above, then you need to enable the build plan => No initial build on master (/)   # Create a new Git branch e.g. """"Feature-3"""" => That is creating a Bamboo plan branch with that same name and there triggers a build (/) [<---- per """"When new branch in repository is created"""" event being set in Plan configuration]   # Edit any file on this """"Feature-3"""" branch, commit the change and create a PR for this change => That triggers a build on this """"PR-commit"""" branch (/) [<---- again, per the """"When new branch in repository is created"""" event]   # Merge this change back to feature branch """"Feature-3"""" which makes everything correct on Bitbucket Server (so a successful code merge) and triggers another build for """"Feature-3"""" (/) [<---- now per commit trigger inherited from master plan]   # *Merge """"Feature-3"""" to master* => That should trigger a build on the cloned build plan, on master, but does not (x)   # *Merge again """"Feature-3"""" to master* (after source code edit and PR) => Now it triggers a build on the cloned build plan, on master, on a second and any subsequent attempts (/)    h3. Expected Results    Build running in step 5 already.  h3. Actual Results    No build is triggered in step 5 from above.    After the first """"Feature-3"""" to master merge attempt the below shows up in Bamboo's Server log:    On Bitbucket Server side the merge succeeds and build on master is not triggered in Bamboo. Now on the second (and any further) merge attempt, the following is logged out, basically just as above:    But in this case, the build really occurs on Bamboo side and consequently """"Sending build notification to Stash"""" is going back to Bitbucket Server as re-printed below.    So that proofs that Bitbucket Server is notifying equally (per """"Received pull request opened event""""), for a fist and all subsequent merge attempts from that feature branch to master, but Bamboo Server is behaving differently on first one compared to all further ones.    *The difference seems to be Bamboo's change detection. It does not kick in on the first attempt, but on the second and next ones it does.*  h3. Workaround    Running a first """"dummy build"""" after cloning (or clone so that it runs that first build automatically) and either delete that first build or simply ignore it."""

summary: "Scheduled triggers sometimes collide with the branch expiry"
description: """h3. Summary    Scheduled triggers sometimes collide with the branch expiry process and don't trigger anymore.  h3. Expected results    Bamboo triggers the scheduled trigger.  h3. Actual Results    The trigger thread (QuartzTriggerJob) get stuck if the branch expiry (BranchExpiryJob) is running at the same time.  h3. Workaround    Change the scheduled trigger time to a different time that the branch expiry (default value is at 3 am)."""

summary: "S3 Artifact folders produced on Windows are being published as files"

Steps to Reproduce  # Configure S3 Artifcat at {{Administration >> Plans >> Artifact handlers}}  # Use Windows Remote Agent to generate artifact that have multiple level folders  # Use Linux Remote Agent to perform Artifact Download task    h3. Expected Results  Each folder within the artifact should be a link to the next folder within the tree until the final file is reached. e.g.      h3. Actual Results  The first level within the artifact is a full link to the file directly. e.g.      h3. Note  Old bug BAM-16379    h3. Workaround  Do not use S3 Artifact setting at Administration or configure certain plan to avoid using it at Plan Configuration"""

summary: "After successful deployment trigger no longer works after cloning a deployment project"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  After successful deployment trigger no longer works after cloning a deployment project    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a deployment project and two environments  # Add an """"After successful deployment"""" trigger to the second environment that triggers upon the first completing  # Clone the deployment project  # Run the first environment in the resulting cloned project    h3. Expected Results  The second environment triggers after the first runs    h3. Actual Results  The second environment does not trigger.    h3. Workaround  Visit the trigger configuration at *Deployment Environment >> Triggers* in the cloned project and *Save* it again. It will fix the trigger definition and it will start triggering again."""

After start playing with changing various options on build expiry and then unchecking all checkbox - for some reason dslForPlan and java bamboo specs are still pertaining those configurations as they were selected.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to Plan   # Click 'Other' tab   # Check 'Override global build expiry configuration'   # Enter 5 days, max builds: 5.   # Uncheck 'Override global build expiry configuration'   # Verify dsl / bamboo java specs    h3. Expected Results    No additional buildExpiry configuration is present in java bamboo specs.  h3. Actual Results    The below config is visible in java bamboo specs:  h5. Bamboo specs:    h5. Dsl:    h3. Workaround       Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available         Partial Workaround:   # Tick 'Override global build expiry configuration'   # Tick 'Do not expire anything for this plan'   # Save   # Untick 'Do not expire anything for this plan'   # Untick 'Override global build expiry configuration'   # Save   # It clears somehow configuration but dsl is different from bamboo specs and it should be gone at all.         """

summary: "When editing a variable value that is being overwritten, the display field for a variable name changes."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When editing a variable value that is being overwritten, the display field for a variable name changes.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan with at least 2 variables. Example:   ## Var1 = Value1   ## Var2 = Value2   # Create a plan branch.   # In the plan branch, section """"Variables"""", overwrite the value of Var2.   # Refresh the page.   # For the new entry on screen for Var2, click on the value field.    h3. Expected Results    Nothing will change. User will be able to set a new value.  h3. Actual Results    In the UI, the variable name will change to another value.    Note: Builds for plan branches will be affected by this.  h3. Workaround   - Option 1: delete and re-create the variable.   - Option 2: fix the field for the variable name manually."""

summary: "Bamboo Yaml Specs fails to publish using ""include"" and more than one document in a file"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Bamboo Yaml Specs fails to publish using """"include"""" and more than one document in a file    h3. Steps to Reproduce  (This test was done using Bitbucket server as repository host)  # Prepare Bamboo and Bitbucket server by integrating them with an application link.  # Create in Bitbucket a repository to use for Yaml Specs.  # In the file bamboo-specs/bamboo.yaml, paste:    # In Bamboo, configure this repository as a Linked repository with specs enabled.   # The scan will succeed.   Phase 2:  # Rename bamboo.yaml to newFile.yaml.  # Create bamboo.yaml with the content    # Commit changes.    h3. Expected Results  Same successful publishing as before    h3. Actual Results    Publishing fails with error      h3. Workaround    Split Yaml documents into multiple files"""

summary: "Yaml Specs: Plan branch uses master configuration"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The issue can be reproduced with Docker runner only. Changes to bamboo.yaml in a branch are not used when a build is kicked off in Docker  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a specs Bitbucket repository with run.sh and bamboo-specs/bamboo.yaml   # Add one just one task to the job - ./run.sh, i.e. execute the script (inline)   # Push a new branch to a Bitbucket repo. Rename run.sh to run-branch.sh, update bamboo.yaml to run the updated script (new name)   # Either create a plan branch manually or enable auto plan branch creation    h3. Expected Results    The build should execute run-branch.sh  h3. Actual Results    Execution of run.sh is attempted, i.e. master task is used. The build fails since script task tries to execute run.sh which does not exist in a new branch.    Apparently, when a file like TEST-SPEC0-JOB1-3-ScriptBuildTask-1875714466825985243.sh is generated, it uses the wrong specs even though xml-data/build-dir/TEST-SPEC0-JOB1 has the right bamboo.yaml.  h3. Workaround    None"""

summary: "YAML Specs export: Missing source-plan which results in unknown task type artifact-download when importing"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Yaml generated from a Bamboo plan that has artifact download task (in UI) isn't valid when it's used in a specs repo. artifact-download tasks is missing source-plan attribute.    Specs scan fails with an error: Unknown task type artifact-download  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan with artifact download task (can be all artifacts or a specific one) which download artifacts from same plan   # Export as YAML   # Use yaml in bamboo-specs/bamboo.yaml and create a specs repository with it    {code:java}  ---  version: 2  plan:    project-key: VARS    key: GIT    name: git  stages:  - Default Stage:      manual: false      final: false      jobs:      - Default Job  Default Job:    key: JOB1    tasks:    - checkout:        force-clean-build: 'false'    - script:        interpreter: SHELL        scripts:        - echo """"Hello"""" > hello.md    - artifact-download:        artifacts:        - name: artifact    artifacts:    - name: artifact      pattern: hello.md      shared: true      required: true{code}  h3. Expected Results    Specs scan is successful  h3. Actual Results    h3. Workaround    Add source-plan to artifact-download task since it's missing when YAML export happens."""

Actual Results    Moving plan fails with an error:  _String [<PROJECT KEY>-<JOB KEY>] is not a job key_  !Screenshot 2020-10-15 at 15.15.56.png|width=506,height=161! summary: "Deployment of old releases fail after changing the artifact name."
