Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan that produces an artifact """"Test""""   # Create a deployment project/environment linked to this plan   # Run a build   # Create release-1 and deploy it to the environment   # Run a second build   # Create release-2 and deploy it to the same environment   # Go to the linked plan and change the artifact name to """"Test_change""""   # Go back to the deployment project and try to rollback to release-1    h3. Expected Results    The deployment succeeds  h3. Actual Results    The artifact download task fails and the below error is thrown in the build logs:    h3. Workaround    Rename the artifact back to the original name."""

summary: "Docker Runner: working directory unavailable if a container is run with non root user"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When executing tasks in Docker, a non root container user cannot cd or even ls the mounted working directory:   {code:java}  docker@2c56fb2fda8b:/tmp$ cat runInDocker6969616781478797756.sh   #!/bin/sh  cd '/home/eugene/Downloads/atlassian-bamboo-7.1.1/bamboo-home/xml-data/build-dir/TEST-TES4-JOB1'  umask 000  '/home/eugene/Downloads/atlassian-bamboo-7.1.1/bamboo-home/temp/TEST-TES4-JOB1-47-ScriptBuildTask-1840878499782133846.sh'  exit $?  {code}  As a result, the script above fails with:  {code:java}  /tmp/runInDocker2036597224817806522.sh: 2: cd: can't cd to /home/eugene/Downloads/atlassian-bamboo-7.1.1/bamboo-home/xml-data/build-dir/TEST-TES4-JOB1  /tmp/runInDocker2036597224817806522.sh: 4: /tmp/runInDocker2036597224817806522.sh: /home/eugene/Downloads/atlassian-bamboo-7.1.1/bamboo-home/temp/TEST-TES4-JOB1-44-ScriptBuildTask-1163266806075301163.sh: Permission denied{code}     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a job using instructure/core:xenial   # Add a script task (inline) or any other task   # Run the plan    h3. Expected Results    Plan runs ok  h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:    h3. Workaround    Use images with root user"""

summary: "Queued build will become undefined if branch expiry deletes its branch while queued"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Queued build will display as *undefined You do not have the permission to view this build.* in the *Build Activity* dashboard if branch expiry deletes its branch while queued    (!) Please note, undefined is also a very normal to display in the build queue if you do not have permission to view the plan that the build is queued for. This should never display for Bamboo administrators though, since they have permission to all plans which is generally a sign you are affected by this bug.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Disable your agents so no builds can proceed  # Create a branch plan and configure it to remove deleted branches after 0 days  # Queue a build for it  # Delete that branch in Bitbucket   # Trigger branch expiry:    # Visit build activity dashboard: *Builds > Build Activity*    h3. Expected Results  Build is removed from queue    h3. Actual Results  Build stays in the queue and appears as *undefined You do not have the permission to view this build.*    If an agent becomes available, it will attempt to build it and clear the queue.    h3. Workaround  Restart Bamboo to clear the queue of undefined builds.     If a capable agent ever comes available to build it, it will also disappear but given that it remained in the queue for a long time already, it may be that a capable agent will never become available."""

summary: "query-bar: refresh data when ""Find"" is clicked in the query bar"
description: """There is an issue on github -- https://github.com/mongodb-js/compass/issues/1897 -- asking for a data refresh when """"Find"""" is being clicked in the query bar regardless if the query is changed or not. Seems reasonable! Especially if writes, for example, have been performed since the last time you clicked """"Find"""".    From github:    """"Currently, if you perform a search on a collection, if you press """"Find"""" again the search won't be performed again unless the query has changed.    This doesn't work well when an external system has changed the data and you want to repeatedly query the collection to check for the changes.    When pressing """"Find"""" regardless of whether the search has changed since the last query, a new search should be performed.    Thanks!!"""""""

summary: "compass-crud: initial load when loading large documents runs out of RAM"
description: """Seems like with documents over a particular size, electron runs out of RAM even with allowDiskUse:true flag. Issue originally reported on github – [https://github.com/mongodb-js/compass/issues/1894]    Description from github:   """"*Feature Request*   When I try to connect to mongodb server that have 14MB for each document (like a document that have one binary field) in the collection (around 10 documents is enough to trigger this behavior) the hosted mongodb atlas in *.mongodb.net will report error as matched documents exceed 100MB and they do not fit into the RAM - even if the client passes allowDiskUse:true the hosted server will ignore this.   When this happens Documents tab on it's initial load will show there is no documents and on the bottom it will popup line bar saying loading error from server (I need to then use projection to exclude the document field that is the huge binary data in order to load the set)   As this is fine - e.g. I could alter the query to not load more stuff into the result set it would be beneficial to try to load documents by using id only projection at least to see some documents and have some warning that a projection was used as having No Documents seems like the collection is empty if no one pay attention to the warning loading error that disappear after some time    As for Aggregate tab - this is where it is not usable as the tab will not render any content into itself - e.g. it will be empty so a client of mongodb UI can't use this tab at all - I can't change the filter/aggregate etc    *Detailed Description*   At least fix Aggregate tab to work - e.g. to be able to add aggregation and test it out    *Context*   Have ability to use aggregations even if the documents in mongodb are huge    I'm using Windows version of MongoDB Compass Comunity UI version 1.20.3"""""""

summary: "Sort does not preserve field order"
description: """It looks like Compass does not preserve the order of sort keys.    This manifests the same JavaScript problem as SERVER-11358 / NODE-578 when numeric-like keys are used.    For example, a sort order of:   {'65534':1, '65533':1}    ends up as:   {'65533':1, '65534':1}    An order-preserving data structure should be used.    """

summary: "Data import does not support embedded JSON anymore"
description: """<!--- Please note this repository's Issue Tracker is not being watched.  Issues are instead being tracked in our public Jira project:  https://jira.mongodb.org/projects/COMPASS/summary  -->    <!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->    ## Feature Request  Defect - Data import does not support nested structures anymore as of 1.21.0    ## Detailed Description  <!--- Provide a detailed description of the change or addition you are proposing -->  Since the release of version 1.21.0, when importing data into a collection where the data for a field is a JSON, content for the field is treated as a string by default and none of the selectable field types seem to be for JSON-like objects. This was not an issue on version 1.20.5    Here is a screenshot of the newly released import flow and the different selectable field types, in my case the attribute is an array of strings  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/51335736/80751372-7fd10900-8ade-11ea-944d-97740280a9fa.png)    ## Context  <!--- Why is this change important to you? How would you use it? -->  As it is working right now, importing an exported collection does not generate as output the same content. This is doable through CLI but it's something that was already supported and now it's not anymore    ## Possible Implementation  <!--- Optional: suggest an idea for implementing addition or change -->>     """

summary: "Aggregation pipeline applies sampling even after disabling sample mode"
description: """Context   * config.chunks collections with 811k documents   * Compass Version 1.21.2    Steps:   # Add $group stage with code:      # *Turn off sample mode*   # See that preview output document includes \{ """"_id"""" : """"shard_5"""", """"count"""" : 5 }    Expected:   - The document should have had count 626    Theory   - It seems that turning off sample mode on an existing pipeline doesn't apply directly.    Workaround:   - If I start a *new* pipeline, then turn off sample mode *before* creating any stages it works."""

summary: "Aggregation pipeline applies sample mode with $out"
description: """Context   * config.chunks collections with 811k documents   * Compass Version 1.21.2    Steps:   # Add $group stage with code:      # *Turn off sample mode*   # Add a {{$out}} stage to collection {{chunks_summary}}   # See that output document in {{chunks_summary}} includes \{ """"_id"""" : """"shard_5"""", """"count"""" : 5 }    Expected:   - The document in {{chunks_summary}} should have had count 626.   - I would *never expect the sampling to be applied to the $out* even if _Sample mode_ was enabled    Theory   - It seems that turning off sample mode on an existing pipeline doesn't apply directly.    Workaround:   - If I start a *new* pipeline, then turn off sample mode *before* creating any stages it works."""

summary: "Compass Hangs When Disconnecting From a Connection to a Secondary"

summary: "Compass doesn't export all existing fields in CSV"
description: """The user reports that Compass is not exporting all fields even when he add the fields manually.  I've tested it doing a comparison with mongoexport and confirm that Compass is not showing all fields as you can see in the attached screenshot.  Exported format: CSV  Tested bot exporting all documents and with the query option.    Tested on:   - Version 1.21.2   - Version 1.22.0-beta.2    [Intercom chat|https://app.intercom.com/a/apps/qq7v0gqb/inbox/inbox/3717212/conversations/36687800012576]    [User's cluster|https://cloud.mongodb.com/v2/5ee8e5810e3c401ea98b089e#clusters]    User's explanation:  _For example, let's say some of my documents have about 50 variables. Others have the same 50 variables, plus 30 more. Some website users only fill out 50 of the survey questions. Others fill out all 80. I want to export all 80 fields, but when I use the export function for the entire collection of documents the tool will only find 50 fields! This is causing me to miss a lot of data._    _If I only export the documents for the website users that filled out all 80, then the tool will find all 80. However, if the export includes any of the users who only filled out 50, then the tool will only find 50. When I add the other 30, the tool still only exports those 50 even for the users who filled out all 80. It's maddening._    _Is there a way I can override this and force it to download all my data for the entire collection?_"""

summary: "Page Properties macro with filter ""Including text"" won't correctly filter Strings that are placed outside of the Page Properties Macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Page Properties macro with filter """"Including text"""" won't correctly filter Strings that are placed outside of the Page Properties Macro. Instead, the Page Properties Report will return all pages containing the string anywhere in the page.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create two pages containing a *Page Properties* macro with the *New Editor* and add a label to them (e.g. 'test') - (Page 1, Page 2)   # On one of these pages add a string of text """"TEST FOR FILTER"""" *inside* the Page Properties macro(Page1);   # On the other one add a string of text """"TEST FOR FILTER"""" *outside* the Page Properties macro (Page2);   # Create a new page using the New Editor and add a page properties macro to report on pages using the label 'test'; (Parent Page);   # Edit the Page Properties Report macro -> add a filter """"Including text"""" and report on the string 'TEST FOR FILTER', similar to the screenshot below: (Copy of Parent Page)   !Screen Shot 2020-07-27 at 14.16.49.png|thumbnail!    # Publish the page (Copy of Parent Page) and check the results returned by Page Properties Report.    h3. Expected Results   * Parent Page to report on *Page 1* as it is the only one containing the string 'TEST FOR FILTER' inside the Page Properties macro(/)    h3. Actual Results   * Parent Page reports both *Page 1* and *Page 2*, even though only Page 1 has the 'TEST FOR FILTER' string inside the Page Properties Macro(x)    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available      """

summary: "Not possible to add inline comments for two words close to each other using Smart Terms "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  It is not possible to add inline comments for two words close (highlighted with the smart terms) to each other using Smart Terms add-on for Confluence Cloud.  It works though, if the words highlighted with the terms, are not consecutive and there is words in between them that is not using the smart terms add-on.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install the smart terms add-on for Confluence Cloud    # Install the Smart Terms add-on on the browser extension, required to highlight the word to make it a smart term on the page.  # Highlight one word and add a smart term to it, using the add-on  # Highlight the next word and add a smart term to it  # Highlight any other word and add the term with the smart terms, which is not consecutive to the previous words.  # Highlight the two consecutive words and try to add an inline comment.  # Highlight the other word with more words that aren't using the smart terms, which isn't next to the two consecutive highlighted words and try to add an inline comment    h3. Expected Results  Any of the words highlighted with the smart terms will allow to add inline comments.    h3. Actual Results    The words next to each other, (consecutive only) will not allow you to add inline comments.    h3. Workaround   Disable temporarily the browser extension Smart Terms to add inline comments."""

summary: "Placeholder text is not shown on template preview when the template has a variable"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Using the *Placeholder* text on our templates is an amazing way to let tips and instructions to our users when using that template. To make sure we are using the full potential of the templates, working with template variables improves this experience!  When our templates have Placeholder text and we add a variable, the template preview is not showing the placeholder text for some weird reason.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new template (Go to a space and navigate to *Space Settings > Content Tools > Templates*);  # Add a *Placeholder* text;  # Add a *variable*;  # Save the template;  # Create a new page using this template.    h3. Expected Results  In the template preview, we are able to populate the variable and either see the placeholder text.    h3. Actual Results  We can only see the template variable in the preview.    *Notes*:  * Though the placeholder text is missing in the template preview, when we go ahead with the page creation, the placeholder text is there.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "The wildcard/operator search does not work on content body"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The wildcard/operator search does not work on content body  h3. Steps to Reproduce    When searching in our <USER>spaces on codetables we find some results (253). When using the wildcard * and search for codetable*. We find 254 results.   But when searching for codetab* we find only 1 result.    It seems like wildcard search is not active. Also using ? or ~ doesn't have there expected results.  h3. Expected Results    Both the search patters need to result in the same results.  h3. Actual Results    The wildcard is just being ignored when searching the content of a page. i.e. {{codetable*}} is just a search for {{codetable}}  and {{codetab*}} is a search for {{codetab}} .  So only one result shows for {{codetab*}}   h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Users are not able to move a draft unless they have admin permission on the new Space"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    According to our [documentation|https://support.<USER><USER>cloud/docs/move-copy-and-hide-pages/], moving a page from one Space to another requires permissions to *view* and *delete* pages in the original Space, and permissions to *view* and *add* pages in the destination Space. However, when trying to move a draft (unpublished page), the move dialog won't suggest Spaces that you don't have *admin* permission.    (i) This bug only affects the new editor.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create one Space with your admin user (let's call it *Admin Space*).   # Grant *view pages, add pages, and admin* permissions to a test user (let's call it *Test User*) on it.   # Create another Space with your admin user (let's call it *Not Admin*).   # Grant *view and add pages* permissions to *Test User* on it.   # Create a third Space with your admin user (let's call it *Test Space*).   # Grant *view, add and delete pages* permissions to *Test User* on it.   # While logged as the *Test User*, create a page on *Test Space*.   # While editing the page, click on the three ellipses on the top right > *Move page*.   # On the *Space* field, enter the keyword *Admin*.    h3. Expected Results    As the user has permissions to view and create pages on both *Admin Space* and *Not Admin*, both Spaces should return on the dialog.  h3. Actual Results    Only Spaces in which the user is an admin are found (in this example, only *Admin Space*).  h3. Workaround    The user is able to move the page after publishing it. This bug only affects the ability to move *drafts*. That being said, the user is able to publish the page on the original Space, and then click on the three ellipses on the top right corner > *Move*. In there, all the expected Spaces will be listed."""

summary: "Sticky Toolbar and sticky table headers are not working properly on Edit mode"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Sticky Toolbar and sticky table headers are not working properly on a page on the *Edit* mode, if the same displays the message """"Google Analytics"""" on the bottom of the page.    The message is displayed because there is an add-on installed called Google Analytics, from the market place, and after reaching the bottom of the page where the message is located, the sticky toolbar disappears and the sticky table header goes to the center of the page, instead of keeping the header at the top and sticking to the table only.    The behavior stops happening when the add-on is uninstalled from the instance.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install the Google Analytics add-on for Confluence Cloud   # Create a new page and add some tables there   # While on the *edit* mode, proceed to scroll down the page until you reach the bottom   # Scroll up the page and check the sticky table header   # Check the behavior of the toolbar    h3. Expected Results    The sticky headers should stick on the page and not disappear when scrolling the page.  h3. Actual Results    Toolbar disappears and the sticky table header is on the center of the table/page, instead of being on the top of the table.  h3. Workaround    Uninstalling the add-on Google Analytics stops this behavior to happen on any pages."""

summary: "blog post macro is not showing excerpts"
description: """*Issue Description* :  The blog post macro is not displaying the excerpts of blogs in """"excerpts"""" display mode    *Steps to reproduce the issue*   * Create a blog post with or without excerpt macro.   * Come to a different page or overview page of the space and enter blog post macro.   * In macro config mode on """"Content Type to Display"""" select excerpts.   * Publish the page.    *Expected behavior:*    it should display a short excerpt from each blog post. If the post contains an Excerpt macro, the Blog Posts macro will display the content defined in the Excerpt macro. If the post does not contain an Excerpt macro, the Blog Posts macro will display the first few sentences of the post.    *Actual Behavior* :   It's not showing any extracted text from the blog on overview page. It is acting as if *Title* is selected in """"Content type to display""""     *Entire* works fine :        The *title* also works fine:        *Workaround* :    Select either of them *Entire* or *Title* which is far from ideal workaround."""

summary: "Calendar set with Asia/Tokyo (+0900) timezone doesn't work well with repeatedly events"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The *Team Calendars* add-on is a great feature that helps us keep tracking things on Confluence. The possibility to create a different calendar with a timezone that is apart from our own, as well from our site, is amazing for companies that work with global teams!  Working with weekly events, though, can sometimes present us with an issue identified when the calendar is set with *Asia/Tokyo (+0900)* timezone and the period of the day on your event is on PM time.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new calendar anywhere in your site - make sure to set the timezone to *Asia/Tokyo (+0900)*;  # On this new calendar, create a new event on Friday (everybody loves Friday, that's why);  # Set the following fields like this:  ** *When*: the day you chose but make sure the start and end times are set to PM;  ** *All day event*: uncheck this, so you can edit above info;  ** *Repeat*: Weekly - every Friday;  # Save the event.    h3. Expected Results  The events are created on every Friday.    h3. Actual Results  The first event gets created on Friday, but the following ones on every Thursday.    *Notes*:  * This issue could be affecting more timezones, but we would need to check it further.    h3. Workaround  Edit the first event and:  # Uncheck the *_Edit this instance only_* option;  # Change the start time to AM;  # Save the event;  # Note it changed to all Fridays, edit the event again and change the start time back to PM:  ** Again, don't forget to uncheck *_Edit this instance only_*."""

Actual Results  The excerpt include macro checkbox tick gets unchecked after page refresh and it does not get rendered to excerpt macro page checkbox.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Adding macro input parameters to an app breaks existing macros, throwing ""No macro metadata"" error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    A Confluence Cloud macro which had a single macro input parameter has been updated by adding 2 more macro input parameters (via the Connect descriptor).    For new instances of the macro (i.e. new page, new macro), everything works just fine. But for existing instances of macro (i.e. macro was inserted into page with app version that only had one input parameter), you cannot edit them at all once you've upgraded to the new version of the Connect app (that has more input parameters).    On editing an instance of the macro, “No macro metadata” modal is returned, rather than the proper edit modal.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create Connect macro with a single macro input parameter.   # Insert macro into *Page A.*   # Update Connect macro descriptor with additional input parameters.   # Insure that your Confluence Cloud instance has been updated with latest version of the Connect macro.   # Edit *Page A*, and try to edit the macro settings.    h3. Expected Results    Macro edit modal shows up as expected.  h3. Actual Results    Modal with error windows shows up.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior."""

summary: "Licensed users are not able to search for pages from Spaces in which they can access because Anonymous access is enabled"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    On Confluence Cloud, you can enable Anonymous access, which, would allow everyone to access it. Although this permission works and the users are able to access the Space and its pages, licensed users (logged users) can access pages from the Space, but can't search for them. The search won't include pages from Spaces that the user is only able to access because Anonymous access is enabled.    *(!) Note: This bug only affects licensed users. Anonymous users are able to search for pages.*  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Confluence Space.   # Grant anonymous users permission to view pages.    # Access this Space with a test user, who doesn't have direct permission to view pages in this Space.   # Note that the user is able to access the pages, as expected.   # Now, try to search for a page title from this Space (using the advanced search).    h3. Expected Results    The page should be included in the search results, as the user has access to it.  h3. Actual Results    The page is not returned on the search results.  h3. Workaround    Grant *view all* permission to this user on this Space."""

summary: "Table gets broken in the editing mode if it contains more than a header row"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Playing with tables on Confluence is always a great option to keep things in order. It is nice how we can easily copy and paste table content from a table into another one, which let the experience really great!  Though, we have to take care when copying determined types of content between tables, like the header row from a table to another one, as this makes Confluence reads that the table where we pasted it as if it has two headers.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page and insert a table;  # On the table header, type *1*, *2*, and *3* on each of the header cells;  # Create another table under the current one;  # At the header, insert *A*, *B*, and *C*;  # Add more 20 table rows;  # On the first table, copy its header (select it all until the row gets blue and copy it);  # Paste it on the 10th row of the second table;  # Publish the page;  # Edit it and scroll the second table.    h3. Expected Results  The content from the copied row gets pasted as a regular row, not a header  *OR*  The table supports multiple headers.    h3. Actual Results  The table rows get broken - as much content you have in the table, worse the experience gets.    h3. Workaround  After editing the page, in the issued table, remove and then re-add the *header row*.    (!) This workaround must be applied every time we edit the page, as it is a momentaneously solution for each time you are editing the page.    ----    You can also delete the pasted header row and manually add its content there, so the table will contain a single header and will work fine.   In order to identify which is the duplicated header, go to the affected page in the *view mode* and click over the *ellipses > Page Source* - navigate to the broken table and identify which table row has the text highlighted with a gray background-color - in case the entire row is grey, then it is probably also the second header.  """

summary: "Adding a Notes Panel in the Default Space Content template makes the space overview empty"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  It is awesome how we can customize the default templates that Confluence has - this way, we can always have things according to our own needs!  When we decide to edit the *Default Space Content* template (the one that will create the overview pages for each new space), inserting the *Notes Panel* (the purple one) makes the content get rid when the space is created, creating a space overview with empty content.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Navigate to *Settings > Global Templates and Blueprints*;  # Go down until you find the *Default Space Content* template;  # Edit it;  # Add some very random content, it can be some Lorem Ipsum text;  # Anywhere in the template, place a *Notes Panel* (once again, the purple one);  # Save the template;  # Create a new blank space.    h3. Expected Results  The overview page of the new space has all the content you inserted in the template.    h3. Actual Results  The overview page is empty.      h3. Workaround  Please, try using a different panel macro in the template until we get this fixed."""

summary: "Attachments with the title starting with a blank space won't work in a Space Import"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    On Confluence Cloud, you are able to attach files to a page, even if their names start with a blank space (i.e """" test picture.png""""). The attachment will work properly. However, if you generate an export of this Space, and then imports it on another Instance, the attachment won't work properly.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Attach a file which its title starts with a blank space to a Confluence page.   # Publish the page.   # Export this Space.   # Import it on another Instance. (Or, you can delete it from the current Instance, and then import it).   # Access the page.    h3. Expected Results    The attachment should work, as it was working before being exported.  h3. Actual Results    The attachment is replaced with below error:    !image-2020-08-14-21-27-44-659.png!  h3. Workaround    Depending on the file extension/type, editing its title will work. To do so:   # Access the impacted page.   # Click on the three ellipses on the top right corner > *Attachments ( x )* (in which *x* would be the total number of attached files to it).   # Click on *Properties* for the impacted attachment.   # Edit the attachment name.   # Hit *Save*.    In case it doesn't work, download the attachment, and attach it once more to the page."""

description: """h3. Issue Summary    Existing Connect apps are unable to be updated with macro alias check enabled since the alias will conflict with the previous version macro alias.      Feature flag has been toggled off to the previous state prior to macro alias check  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Enable macro alias check and update existing installed connect app    h3. Expected Results    1. You should be able to update connect app  h3. Actual Results    Error is thrown `Installation failed. New app macro alias conflicts with existing app macro alias.`  h3. Workaround    No Workournd         *Reference:*    https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-69663?error=login_required&error_description=Login+required&state=9e345fa1-9afe-4b18-9107-c712ee7c90b8"""

summary: "Activity Stream Gadget Blank when loading page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The Activity Stream Gadget is blank when added to a Confluence page.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Navigate to a Confluence site that is linked to a Jira instance  # Add Activity stream gadget to a page  # Publish  # Reload the page      h3. Expected Results    The latest activity should be shown    h3. Actual Results    Blank Gadget (See Image)        h3. Workaround    * Going into edit mode then closing out allows the Gadget to load once.   * From the sidebar, navigate to another page and click the title of the affected page again.  * However, refreshing the page causing the Activity stream gadget to go blank again,. """

summary: "Broken Image on mobile App(ios/Android), Legacy/Old Editor"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Broken Image on mobile(ios/Android)    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page in the old editor.   # insert image links from other pages i.e. *search on other pages*.   !image-2020-08-20-11-04-20-534.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  The image on the mobile app must appear.  h3. Actual Results    The error message displays: 'Broken Image'.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Issue statistics macro displays blank in published mode."
description: """h3. Summary   * Issue statistics macro shows blank on every load of the page.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    * Create a page.  * Add Issue statistics macro.  * Load the page multiple times in different tabs or in the same tab.    h3. Expected Behavior  It should load the content/number of issues in the table every time page is loaded.    h3. Actual Behavior  It displays a blank box on loading the page with Issue statistics macro    h3. Note :  We are seeing this issue on Two-dimensional filter statistics macro as well.  Created a separate case: https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-69449    h3. Workaround   Edit the page and close it without making any changes, it will display the issue stats.        """

summary: "Search does not highlight exact string when using double quotes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Search is returning correct results (within pages), however, it highlights incorrect portions of results (even when using double quotes)    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page with a lot of text (e.g. using https://www.lipsum.com/)   # Try to search of a string of text. For example, I search for a long string of text to demonstrate this behavior:    {quote}  """"diam consectetur sodales ultricies. Nulla blandit rutrum leo, in faucibus dui commodo ac. Integer in purus est. Nam faucibus eros vitae tortor""""  {quote}    h3. Expected Results  * Pages with the exact phrase to be returned, with the exact sentence highlighted - especially when using double quotes    h3. Actual Results  * Pages with the exact phrase are returned, however the highlighted text doesn't match perfectly the search parameters - when using double quotes     !Screen Shot 2020-08-20 at 15.49.08.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Clicking on next page link in labelled content page shows System Error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Clicking on next page in labelled content page causes system error to appear on some pages    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Step 1: Create a label and add it to multiple pages   # Step 2: Click on next page link     h3. Expected Results  next page results displayed    h3. Actual Results  error shown    h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available     !labels.png|thumbnail!    !error.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Adding the JIRA Chart macro and using the ""Pie Chart"" option generates an error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When attempting to create Jira Chart macro using the """"pie chart"""" option, the user would receive an error:        From splunk, we can see it affects a few tenants at this time. The error message is:        h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Jira chart macro  # Enter Jira filters in the macro setting input field  # Select Pie chart option      h3. Expected Results  Pie chart is displayed in the macro preview    h3. Actual Results  An error is shown    h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Adding filter beyond three option in page property report macro is not working as expected to narrow down search"
description: """h3. Summary   Adding filter beyond three option in page property report macro is not working as expected to narrow down the search    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Add page property macro on multiple pages with labels.  * On new page add page property report macro and apart from label and space filter add two more filters say *Including text* and with *With Title*    h3. Expected Behavior  * It should narrow down the results as per results.    h3. Actual Behavior  * It ignores the third filter and searches for the fourth option.  * Put title search as a third option and the text search as fourth, then the title search is ignored.  * When I put the text search as a third option and the title search as     fourth, then the text search will be ignored.      """

summary: "Long comments on the comment box, are not allowing you to see the comments when scrolling up the page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When commenting on a page, if the page has too many comments or a few long comments on a page, if you write a long comment/reply int eh comments section, when scrolling up the page, the comment box remains sticky on the page and it is only possible to see the page when the comment section ends, and then the box is scrolled down with it, being possible to scroll the page after it.    This causes a confusion when trying to scroll the page, as you can't see what is being scrolled up and it gives the impression that the page itself is not being scrolled up.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page with some content on it   # Add a long comment on the bottom of the page, around 5 paragraphs with 5 lines each, for example.   # Add another comment on the page  # Try to write a long comment at bottom of the page  # Try to scroll up to see the page content until you reach the top    h3. Expected Results  You're able to see the page content when scrolling up    h3. Actual Results  The page does not show what is being scrolled up, depending on the comment size in the comment box, but, after the comment section ends, it scroll the page as expected.    h3. Workaround    Scroll up until you see the page or use the side scroll bar of the browser."""

summary: "Invite to edit feature is not working in new editor"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Invite to edit feature is not working in the new editor.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Step 1 Create or edit a <USER>page.   # Step 2 Click on invite to edit option.    h3. Expected Results  It should show the dialog to select the users to invite.  Screenshot from old editor:   !image-2020-08-24-15-35-08-277.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  Nothing happens when we click on invite to edit option.   !image-2020-08-24-15-34-47-364.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Confluence Smart links not working on instances <instancename>.jira.com"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When pasting a link for a Confluence page, the link does not convert/render as a smart link card.    It seems to be occurring only with instances using jira.com domain, while on atlassian.net instances domains are working as expected.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Copy a link to an existing published page in Confluence   # Create a new page  # Paste the link copied previously and paste on the page    h3. Expected Results  It should render the page link as a smart link    h3. Actual Results  It does not render as a smart link, only a normal link URL for the page.    h3. Workaround    Click on edit link, then insert a name for the page (any name or the page's name) and hit enter to save changes."""

summary: "Editing the Retrospectives template breaks the default auto-populating info"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The Retrospectives template is awesome to pull up information about our teams and our meetings. It's a cool way to keep tracking things up on our daily activities!  Though this template is awesome the way it is, there is always something we can add that will fit our needs. Apparently, changing the Retrospectives template will make it to stop auto-populating some default information when creating the draft page.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to a space and navigate to *Space Settings > Content Tools > Templates*;  # Find the *Restorspectives* tempalte and edit it;  # Add an additional text somewhere on the page;  # Save it;  # Create a new page and call the *Retrospectives* template.    h3. Expected Results  The template content is called with all the auto-populated info (*Date*, *Team*, and *Participants*).    h3. Actual Results  The auto-populated information is missing    h3. Workaround  You can either reset the template to default or insert the information manually after creating the page."""

summary: "Invite to Edit button no longer working"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  One of the most awesome features Confluence has is the Collaborative Editing option, where we can share a draft from a page we are working on with our team and make dynamic editions on our pages at the same time!  At this moment, when we try to share a draft page with someone from our team by hitting the *Invite to Edit* button, nothing happens.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create or edit a page;  # On the right-top of the screen hit the *Invite to Edit* button.    h3. Expected Results  A dialog box is open and you can insert the users you want to invite.    h3. Actual Results  Nothing happens    *Notes*:  * On the browser console, it is like if the requests were all good and being called correctly after clicking on the button.    h3. Workaround  Copy the draft URL and share it with your team. Just make sure to remove this part of the URL: {{?draftShareId=DRAFT-ID}}"""

summary: "Accessing a link to a restricted attachment results in a browser error (ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a user tries to access a file that is attached on a page in which he/she doesn't have access to, the user faces an error on the browser. Although it is expected that access would be denied, the Confluence UI doesn't load at all, and the user doesn't have any indication that the issue is related to permissions.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Upload an attachment to a page.   # Click on the three ellipses on the top right corner > *Attachments*.   # Copy the *View link*.    # Share this link with a user that doesn't have permission to access this page.    h3. Expected Results    Confluence should display a restricted content message.  h3. Actual Results    A browser error is displayed.     Example from Chrome:    !image-2020-08-24-19-26-59-857.png|width=321,height=209!    Firefox example:    !image-2020-08-24-19-27-24-322.png|width=504,height=196!  h3. Workaround    Granting the user with the desired permissions to view the page in which the file is attached to would fix the issue."""

summary: "Space logo breaks if Space Key uses a specific space key (FO, ITS, RL, DOC, CONF, IN)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The ability to change the logo for a Space that uses specific Space Keys such as *FO*, *ITS*, *DOC*, *CONF, IN* or *RL* is not working properly.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Space using the Space Key '*FO*' or '*fo*'(the Space name doesn't make any difference).   # On the Space, click on *Space Settings*.   # Click on *Edit*, next to the Space Logo.   # Upload your custom logo.    h3. Expected Results    The image should be uploaded and defined as the Space Logo.  h3. Actual Results    The Space logo breaks:    !image-2020-08-24-19-57-00-320.png!   !image-2020-08-24-19-57-10-157.png!    (!) Trying to upload a different image or restoring the default doesn't work.  h3. Workaround    *Option 1* Use another browser, such as Firefox, to access the space and the logo should be displayed correctly.    *Option 2* Create a new space with a different Space Key and move/copy your pages to this new space, then delete the remaining one."""

summary: "Knowledge base does not respect image's alignment from a Confluence Page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When adding an image and wrapping it within a text, the page gets published fine.   But if you use it as a KB on Jira <USER> the image alignment will not be the same as the page.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page and add a text and an image to it   !image-2020-08-25-09-04-17-286.png|thumbnail!   # Wrap (left or right) the image within the text and publish this page  # Link this space as a KB from a <USER>project  # Open this page as a KB on this SD project    h3. Expected Results  The same alignment should be used on both views, the page, and the KB    h3. Actual Results  The alignment is gone:   !image-2020-08-25-09-05-29-510.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Fabric editor does not style nested Jira macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  {quote}For the new page editor a Jira single issue macro that is nested in a rich-text body macro (either staticContentMacro or dynamicContentMacro): the Jira macro renders as a plain link instead of a styled Jira link.    Things work fine for old/original pages. It is only the new/fabric pages where the problem exists.    In this screenshot the first Jira macro is placed directly on the page and renders a styled Jira link. The second Jira macro is nested in one of our macros and renders a plain link:    !plain_Jira_link.png!  {quote}  Originally reported on [the developer community|https://community.developer.<USER>t/new-editor-does-not-style-nested-jira-macro/40760]  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a block macro   # insert a Jira macro in it    h3. Expected Results    Styled Jira Macro  h3. Actual Results    Plain text  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Link macro is being cut off if a child page is created under multiple parent pages and the space has a long name"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If the space has a long name, the overview page will carry the same name with the home after it, and if a page is created under multiple parent pages, it will cause the path page names on the page to be on two lines and causing the link macro to be cut off on the third line in a Confluence Page.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a space with a long name on it, example: Space Name Long enough   # Create a new page and create at least 4 children pages, with also long names on it. example: Testing Bugs and Tools   # Edit the last children page and, add some text to the page  # Insert the link macro (ctrl+k) to the third line    h3. Expected Results  The link macro will show the paste link option.    h3. Actual Results  The paste link option is cut off of the page, not being possible to see it.    h3. Workaround  Paste the link macro on another line, as it only happens on third line of the page."""

summary: "Search with double quotes to look for an exact phrase not working accordingly."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    User not getting any results when searching with double quotes to look for an exact phrase.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page with a lot of text (Test-Only)   # As example Search for (""""Test-Only"""")   # As example Search for (""""Test AND Only"""")    h3. Expected Results    The search result will display the page which contains Test-Only    h3. Actual Results    No result being shown.    h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Acrhived blog link in personal space"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The user is redirected to an old/archived Atlassian link from personal space.        h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a personal space   # Go to the overview page and find for *Check out 5 ways to get the most from your Confluence personal space.* at the bottom of the page.   # Click on the link      h3. Expected Results    User should be seeing the latest blog link    h3. Actual Results    The user is redirected to an archived blog link and some of the links in the blog are broken.    h3. Workaround     Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Content by label macro is not working for Unlicensed users"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The [Content by Label macro|https://support.<USER><USER>cloud/docs/insert-the-content-by-label-macro/] is used to report pages using specific labels. However, this macro is not working for Unlicensed users - they are prompted with an error message.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Confluence Space, and link it as a Knowledge Base for a Jira <USER>project.   # On JSD, make sure that *Any active user* is able to access the Space.   # Create a page on this Space.   # Add a Content by Label macro on it.   # Access this page, as a <USER>customer (Unlicensed user).    h3. Expected Results    The macro should work properly, reporting the pages that have the desired label.  h3. Actual Results    Below error message is displayed on the page:    Example:    !image-2020-08-26-15-48-50-754.png|width=709,height=240!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Trello Board macro with Confluence"
description: """h3. Summary   Trello Board Macro not rendering properly.     h3. Steps to reproduce    * Following [Use Trello and Confluence together|https://support.<USER><USER>cloud/docs/use-trello-and-<USER>together/], the doc I could add the Trello board macro but on the preview or in view mode the Trello board doesn't load.  * Copying and pasting a link directly to the page, prompt to login, clicking on the board on preview mode or view mode takes me to another tab where Trello board is visible in Trello but not in Confluence.  * I could see Trello listed in managed apps.    h3. Expected Behavior   * The board should load post rendering the macro or copying or pasting the Trello link  * Fully interactive Trello boards on Confluence page should appear    h3. Actual Behavior  * Trello board keeps loading on both modes, cached and incognito mode.    h3. Workaround  None. However, clicking on link from <USER>page works fine on another tab.  """

summary: "Attachment files refer to the undefined links in advanced search/live search"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Trying to search the attachments in advanced search or in live search the attachment refers to the undefined links.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Step 1 Go to advanced search and try to search an attachment file name.   !image-2020-08-27-14-12-29-577.png|thumbnail!    !image-2020-08-27-14-12-45-935.png|thumbnail!    # Step 2 click on any suggestion attachment.   !image-2020-08-27-14-13-24-658.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  It should redirect it to the attachment link, on the page where the attachment is linked.    h3. Actual Results  It redirects to the undefined link.     h3. Workaround     Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Editor fails to save pages"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    with the old editor pages, It is not possible to edit and publish as there is an error """" can't save changes"""".  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access a page with the old editor.   # Click on edit option.    h3. Expected Results    The edit should be successful and the operation must be saved.  h3. Actual Results    The error message appears """" *cant save changes*"""". upon checking the browser logs seem like the synchrony is failed.    h3. Workaround    1. Update the page to the new editor.    2. Create a copy of the page and can be used further."""

summary: "TOC issue - headings inside table does not work as expected "
description: """h3. Summary   If the headings put inside the table, it does not work as expected on TOC macro.  So on TOC macro, if you click on the heading i.e inside the table, it does not take to the heading of the page or that part of the page. It stays there or takes it to the top of the page.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  1. Create a page.  2. Add a table to it.  3. Use heading to add a title.  4. Add another title with a heading font on the page.    h3. Expected Behaviour  By clicking on the headings it should take to the part of the page where the heading is mentioned.    h3. Actual Behaviour   It does nothing for the title mentioned inside the table but it works absolutely fine for the title mentioned on the page without a table.    h3. Workaround   Put the heading outside the table.      """

summary: "Moving a page inside the editor throws an error in the UI"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  * When attempting to move a page inside the editor and publish the page afterward, in the new location, the following error is thrown:   !Screen Shot 2020-08-27 at 13.48.58.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a copy of a page in *Space A*  # Inside the editor, click on the *... >> Move page* option  # Select *Space B* and the new parent page  # Click on the *Save* button  # Confirm the breadcrumbs of the page changed to the new location  # Click on the *Publish* button    h3. Expected Results  # The copied page will be published in the new space, under the new parent page.    h3. Actual Results  # The following error message is thrown and nothing happens:   !Screen Shot 2020-08-27 at 13.48.58.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  # Create the copy of the page and publish it, in the same space as the original page  # Once the page is published, click on the *... >> Move* option  # Type in the new space and parent page name  # Click on the *Move* button"""

summary: "Not able to add inline comments to consecutive words if it has character formatting"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In Confluence Cloud, if you add character formatting to consecutive words, it is not possible to add inline comments to the words highlighted.  Even though it is formatting, it states that it is not possible to add inline comments to macros, but there is no macro there.  However, it is possible to add comments on those words individually.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page   # Add some text    # Add character formatting to one word (e.g: italic)  # Add character formatting to another consecutive word (e.g: bold)  # Highlight those consecutive words  # Try to add inline comment    h3. Expected Results  You're able to insert an inline comment on the selection of the consecutive words.    h3. Actual Results  You're not able to insert an inline comment on the consecutive words using the character formatting.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Page Properties Report macro with filter ""Including text"" is not differing blank spaces from -"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    It's possible to use the [Page Properties Report|https://support.<USER><USER>cloud/docs/insert-the-page-properties-report-macro/] macro, filtering by text. However, the macro is not differing blank spaces from - (i.e. macro test from macro-test).    (on) This bug affects both editors.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page with the Page Properties macro on it, with a table inside (let's call it *Page A*).  # Add a string to the page (i.e. *macro-test*).  # Publish the page.  # Add a label to the page (i.e. *test*).  # Create another page with the Page Properties macro on it, with a table inside (let's call it *Page B*).  # Add a string to the page (i.e. *macro test*).  # Add the same label to the page (*test*).  # Now, create a page with the Page Properties Report, and set below filters:  - Label: *test*  - Including text: *""""macro test""""* (with double quotes, which should search for the exact match).    h3. Expected Results  Only *Page B* should be reported on the macro.    (on) Note: macro-test - without double quotes - should be considered as a single word, as it was the case prior to this bug.    h3. Actual Results  Both pages are reported.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Table sorting ignores (/) etc"
description: """I made a big table with some (/) (!) (x) entries then tried to sort it. Confluence doesn't distinguish between empty cells and the symbols so… nothing changes when I sort."""

summary: "Space logo is broken, after updating it with a custom one, and reverting to the default image"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  * Reverting broken logo image to the default space logo won't fix BUG [CONFCLOUD-70683|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-70683]    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a space with one of the Space Keys mentioned on this BUG [CONFCLOUD-70683|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-70683]   # Click on *Space Settings* and update the space logo with a custom one;  # Due to this previous BUG, the logo will be displayed as a broken image.  # Edit the logo again, and this time revert the logo back to the default one again.   !Screen Shot 2020-08-31 at 11.26.54.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  * Logo to be reverted to the default space logo    h3. Actual Results  * Space logo is still displayed as a broken image      h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Renaming the Instance will break smartlinks for image previews from pages on the old editor"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When you click on an image placed on the Legacy editor, its preview will load, and it's possible to copy the preview URL, which is usually according to the structure below:    When placing this link on the new editor, the link is replaced by a smarlink with the page's title. After re-naming the Instance, the smartlink won't display the page's title anymore.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page using the old editor.   # Add an image to the page.   # When accessing the page on view mode, click on the image.   # The image will load. Copy its URL.   # Create a new page on the new editor.   # Paste the preview link on the page, and publish it.   # Note that the link is replaced with a smartlink with the page's title.   # Re-name the Instance.    h3. Expected Results    The smartlink should keep displaying the page's title for users that have access on it. For example:    !image-2020-08-31-13-56-34-749.png!  h3. Actual Results    The raw link is displayed (the page title doesn't load anymore):    !image-2020-09-16-11-48-38-268.png!  h3. Workaround    Updating the image link to the new base URL will fix this issue."""

summary: "Roadmap planner upon publishing shows one week earlier."
description: """h3. Issue Summary   * Roadmap planner upon publishing starts one week earlier.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Create a Page, add the Roadmap Planner with multiple bars in weeks (setting a specific date as start, Aug-17 for example), and publish the page.    h3. Expected Results   * Macro on the page will show the weeks as set when editing it.    h3. Actual Results  * Macro shows one week earlier (Aug-10, following the example above).     h3. Workaround   * Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available     """

summary: "Using links that lead to page edit within Page Properties Report macro results in link being displayed as whole link"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If we use a link that leads to a page in edit mode, Page Properties Report macro will display the link as a regular link instead of the visual option that we have for links in Confluence.    *Link in original page*    *!Screen Shot 2020-09-01 at 15.24.54.png|width=357,height=178!*    _the value for the VALUE 01-1 row is the link that leads to the page in edit mode_    *Link inside Page Properties Report macro*    *!Screen Shot 2020-09-01 at 15.25.17.png|width=354,height=94!*  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page   # Insert the Page Properties macro in that page   # Insert a link that leads to any page in edit mode inside the macro   # Publish the page   # Create another page   # Insert the Page Properties report macro in that page   # Make sure that the Page Properties Report macro is looking for the correct Page Properties macro   # Publish the page    h3. Expected Results    The link is displayed similarly as to what we see in the original page, the shortened version with the page title.  h3. Actual Results    The link is displayed as a full fledged link  h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Copying text from a Panel macro and pasting it in another Panel duplicates the macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If you highlight text from a Panel macro, and paste it on the page, only the text would be placed (not the macro). However, pasting the text inside the panel macro, would create a new Panel with the content.    (i) This bug affects the new editor.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page using the new editor.   # Add a panel, and add some text to it.   # Highlight the text from this panel.   # Add a new panel on the page.   # Paste the text on the new panel.    h3. Expected Results    The text should be pasted inside the macro.  h3. Actual Results    A new panel is created for the text.    !Panel Bug.gif!  h3. Workaround    Paste the text on the editor, and then copy from it, to paste inside the macro."""

summary: "URL converted to smart card showing error for anonymous user"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    URL converted to a smart card showing error for an anonymous user    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Copy any <USER>page URL   #  Paste it and choose display as a card    h3. Expected Results    Anonymous sees the card being displayed.    h3. Actual Results    Anonymous user sees the following    !Capture.PNG|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Display as a link instead of Card,"""

summary: "Description says 'likes' rather than 'like' when it's me and one other person"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Selection of 'likes' over 'like' is incorrect when the likers are me and one other person.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # 'Like' a comment that one other person has liked     # Step 2    h3. Expected Results  {quote}You and XXX like this  {quote}  h3. Actual Results  {quote}You and XXX *likes* this    !Screenshot 2020-09-03 at 9.24.47 am.png!  {quote}  h3. Workaround    This is cosmetic; no workaround required."""

summary: "Exclude Headings feature on Table of Contents macro is not working properly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The [TOC macro|https://support.<USER><USER>cloud/docs/insert-the-table-of-contents-macro/] offers the ability to *exclude headings* from the report. According to the macro's documentation:  {quote}Filter headings to exclude according to specific criteria. You can use wildcard characters.   If the headings you want to exclude are *Overview* and *Summary*, enter *Overview|Summary* in this field.  {quote}  However, this filter is not working properly - It's not looking only for exact matches. For example, using *Test* to exclude this heading, would also exclude any other Heading on the page that has the word *Test* on it, for example """"Test results"""".    Although the macro preview works properly (it excludes only exact matches) while editing the page, once you publish it, note that the TOC is excluding more Headings that it was expected.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page on Confluence, and add these Headings (*Test*, *Some Tests*, *Test Results*, *test*)   # Add the TOC macro to the page.   # On the *Exclude Headings* field, fill *Test*.   # Save the macro.   # Publish the page.    h3. Expected Results    The macro should only exclude *Test* heading from the report, as shown on its preview:    !image-2020-09-02-21-20-16-301.png!  h3. Actual Results    After publishing the page, note that the TOC is also excluding *Tests* and *Some Tests*:    !image-2020-09-02-21-21-19-406.png!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available    h4. Notes    Note that the field is case sensitive, so the *test* heading was kept."""

summary: "Permission to archive own pages is not working as expected"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  According to [our documentation|https://support.<USER><USER>cloud/docs/archive-pages/], users can either have permission to only archive their own pages, or to archive all the pages they have access to.    *To archive any page in the Space*, the user needs the Space permission to *Archive*.  *To archive own pages only*, the user needs the Space permission to *Add pages*.    However, users that only have permission to *View all* pages, are also able to archive their own pages.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Access a Space in which you have permission to create a page, and are an admin.  # Create a page, and publish it.  # Navigate to *Space Settings > Permissions*.  # Click on *Edit permissions*.  # Make sure your user doesn't have permission to *Add pages* anymore.  # Click on *Save all* button.  # Navigate back to the page.  # Click on the three ellipses on the top right corner.    h3. Expected Results    The archive option shouldn't be visible, as the user doesn't have the required permission.    h3. Actual Results  The archive option is visible, and the user is able to archive the page.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Clicking on the Search makes the first character you type be read as a shortcut"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Depending o the case, when you click over the search bar and right after it you start typing fast, the first character you inserted is read as a shortcut.   Pages that have huge content can be easier affected.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to a page that has a lot of content;   # Fast click over the search bar and start typing """"_example_"""".    h3. Expected Results    You type and it is inserted in the search box.  h3. Actual Results    After hitting *E*, the page gets edited instead of your word gets typed in the search bar.    !Fast Clicking.gif|width=443,height=245!    *Notes*:   * While testing it, it doesn't happen 100% of the times;   * If you do this while Confluence is still loading the page, it is easier to face this issue;   * As much content your page has, as easier it is to face it.    h3. Workaround  Instead of clicking in the search bar, you can use the shortcut */* (slash) to search;"""

summary: "Jira Chart returns duplicate status columns when using a query that returns NextGen and regular projects in Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  * Jira Chart macro duplicate status columns when reporting on NextGen and Software projects, even though both projects use the exact same Status.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create two Jira projects, one being NextGen, while the other being a regular software project.   # Populate these two projects with some issues, transitioning them to status """"Done"""", """"In Progress"""" and """"To Do"""". - Both these projects should have the same default statuses;  # Create a page on Confluence,  and add a *Jira Chart* macro > Select the """"Two Dimensional"""" option;  # Add a filter that would return all issues across both projects (In my case I used ' *text ~ """"test""""* ')    h3. Expected Results  * All issues that have the same status to be displayed under the same column.    h3. Actual Results  * Issues with the exact same status are reported on different columns, as can be seen in this next screenshot.   !Screen Shot 2020-09-04 at 11.35.41.png|thumbnail!     (i) This affects both the Legacy and the new editor alike    h3. Workaround  * The only workaround would be to add two separate *Jira Chart* macros, each one reporting just one regular projects, while the other reports on NextGen projects."""

summary: "Spaces List macro not retrieving results when selecting ""Categories"" as Scope of spaces in the new editor"
description: """h3. Summary  In Confluence, the user has the option to use the [Spaces List macro|https://<USER><USER>confcloud/spaces-list-macro-724765290.html] so the same will retrieve a list of spaces based on different options from the drop-down. If the user selects the *Category*, the results will be the same doesn't matter what category is selected in the drop-down list.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page using the new editor and edit the same  # Tap the *Insert* button > *Other Macros* > *Spaces List*  # On the macro menu, make sure to select the """"Category"""" option    h3. Expected Results  Each option should bring results according to the selected option.    h3. Actual Results  The same will display doesn't matter wha category is selected in the drop-down list.    h3. Notes  (Optional - If Necessary)    h3.Workaround  There is no workaround at the moment. """

summary: "Time Spent Aggregate field doesn’t show results in jira filter macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Filters in JIRA and JIRA filters in Confluence behaves differently.Time Spent Aggregate field doesn’t show results in Jira filter macro.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Step 1 Go to Jira filters> search any project   !image-2020-09-07-14-58-42-837.png|thumbnail!    # Step 2 Go to <USER>page and insert JIRA filter macro insert *Time Spent* and *Time Spent Aggregate field*   !image-2020-09-07-15-02-07-517.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  Jira filter macro should show the same results as filters in JIRA.    h3. Actual Results  Jira filter macro is not showing *Time Spent Aggregate field* value in the <USER>page.    Note: Jira filter macro shows *Time Spent Aggregate field* value only if Storie have sub tasks.   !image-2020-09-07-15-05-53-519.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "User with View permission only not able to see space in the space directory"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The user with View permission only not able to see space in the space directory    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a test group and give <USER>product access   # Add a user to the test group (make sure the user only in this 1 particular group)   # Give the test group *view* permission only in any space.   # Then go to space directory    h3. Expected Results    The user will be able to see the space which he has permission to view.    h3. Actual Results    The user sees empty space directory.    h3. Workaround    Provide the users with a direct link to space"""

summary: "Keyboard shortcuts do not work in some keyboard layouts "
description: """h3. Summary   Languages such as Russian or Arabic (different characters by default) conflict with the shortcuts and they do not work.    h3. Suggested Solution  Keep shortcuts bound to the specific key that is being pressed instead of the actual character sent by the keypress.    h3.  Workaround  Change keyboard layout to American English"""

summary: "Error rendering macro 'recently-updated-dashboard' : null after selecting space categories"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a user select the *Space Categories* tab on any *Recently Updated Dashboard* macro, this macro will throw an error, along with any existing and new *Recently Updated Dashboard* macros.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page and add the *Recently updated dashboard* macro;  # Publish the page and the macro will work as expected  # Hold command (mac) or Ctrl (windows) and select the *Space Categories* tab;  # Refresh the page    h3. Expected Results  The macro should continue working    h3. Actual Results  The macro (and every other Recently Updated Dashboard on the instance) will break with the following error:  {code:none}Error rendering macro 'recently-updated-dashboard' : null{code}  | [^CONFCLOUD-70764.mp4] |    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Importing a space that contains pages with restrictions makes it impossible to remove said restrictions on the Free Plan"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a user from a Free site imports a space that contains page with restrictions, removing those restrictions becomes impossible. Confluence throws an error and tells the user to Try Again.    This happens with imports both Confluence Cloud and Server. The user who is importing the space into Confluence Cloud must not have access to the page in the origin Confluence Cloud Site of the space.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a free Confluence site   # Import a Space with restricted pages from another Confluence Site/Instance   # Go into the imported space   # Go into Space Settings   # Go into the Permissions tab   # Select the Restricted Pages tab   # Try to remove the restrictions    h3. Expected Results    Restrictions are completely removed  h3. Actual Results    Restrictions are not removed. This error is thrown:    !Screen Shot 2020-09-09 at 15.47.22.png|width=287,height=137!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Two Dimensional Filter Statistics macro works intermittently when within a panel"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The *Two Dimensional Filter Statistics* macro is a great widget we can use to pull up JIRA data within our beautiful Confluence pages. This feature is awesome to create great reports!  Lately, we have noting that this widget is not behaving well when it is inserted within a Panel macro.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Inert a *Panel* macro;  # Within the Panel, call *{two* and insert the *Two Dimensional Filter Statistics*;  # Configure it with the JIRA filter and fields you want;  # Make sure to save the widget properties and then insert it into the page;  # Publish the page.    h3. Expected Results  The *Two Dimensional Filter Statistics* results load in the page.    h3. Actual Results  A blank frame is placed in the screen.    *Notes*:  * This issue occurs intermittently.     h3. Workaround   # Remove the *Two Dimensional Filter Statistics* macro from the panel;   # Directly in the page, add the *Two Dimensional Filter Statistics* macro;   # Make sure to save the properties and then insert the macro;   # Cut the macro and paste it into the *Panel*;   # Publish the page.  """

summary: "Atlas Grotesk Regular font for pdf shown as box"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    User-uploaded a pdf file with Atlas Grotesk Regular font to the page.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a word file with *Atlas Grotesk Regular* font   # Convert the Word file to pdf  # Upload the pdf fle to a page  # Click on the pdf    h3. Expected Results    User will be able to see the text/font inside the pdf.    h3. Actual Results     !Capture.PNG|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Use pdf macro to read the pdf file."""

summary: "Headings 1 and 2 inside layouts on the Legacy editor are not working properly on Windows"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    On the Legacy editor, if you create a Layout, and insert a Heading 1 or 2 inside of it, the macro will work, but scroll arrows will be displayed when accessing the page on view mode. The text will be cut off as well.    (i) This bug only affects the Legacy editor.    (i) This bug only affects Windows. I wasn't able to reproduce it on macOS.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page using the Legacy editor.   # Add a layout on it, and a Heading (1 or 2) inside of it.   # Publish the page.    h3. Expected Results    The layouts should render properly (screenshot taken from macOS):    !image-2020-09-11-12-55-29-932.png!  h3. Actual Results    The layouts are cut off, and have scroll arrows:    !image-2020-09-11-12-59-05-653.png|width=1038,height=267!  h3. Workaround    (on) This behavior affects Google Chrome and Edge, but it doesn't affect Firefox. So, if possible, load the page on Firefox.    Or, editing the page and adding a new line after the Heading, would fix it."""

summary: "Specifying a label on Livesearch macro does not affect results from both the dropdown search results preview and the See more results for option"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Specifying a label on Livesearch macro does not affect results from both the dropdown search results preview and the See more results for option    When a user sets the search parameters within the macro to be restricted to a specific label, _+the results shown in the preview within the dropdown are incorrect.+_    _+If the user clicks show more the results are also incorrect.+_    However, if the user presses the enter/return key to send the search, the results show on the search page are correct.    In the first video, _+you can see the incorrect behavior in both the preview and the full search page:+_      !Screen-Recording-2020-09-11-at-1.gif|width=393,height=280!    Here, in the second video you can see +_the expected results by pressing the enter/return key:_+      !Screen-Recording-2020-09-11-at-1(1).gif|width=394,height=280!      By looking at the second video, you can see that the label value is only passed through to the search when pressing enter/return.       h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create pages   # Add a label to those pages   # Create a new page   # Place a Livesearch macro in it   # Limit the search scope to content containing the label you created earlier   # Save the macro   # Publish the page   # Search for the pages you created earlier for this test    h3. Expected Results    Only the content with the label will show in the preview and the """"See more results for"""" option  h3. Actual Results    Any content with the search string will show up  h3. Workaround    Instead of relying on the preview or the """"See more results for"""" option, press enter/return to be redirected into the full search page."""

summary: "Contributors Summary is showing 0 results for Comments, Labels, Watches and any other field besides Edits"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The *Contributors Summary* macro is showing 0 results for any field besides *Edits*.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page and add the *Contributors Summary* macro  # Set the space key and select the desired fields (tested with """"edits,comments,labels,watches"""")  # Save the macro and publish the page    h3. Expected Results  The macro should list the respective values for all fields    h3. Actual Results  Only the *Edits* field displays a value different than 0    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "The ""make page full-width"" button can remove tables from complex pages"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In some cases, when a page has a high number of tables and other elements, clicking on the """"make page full-width"""" / """"make page fixed-width"""" button multiple times can remove tables from the page.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page (storage format:  [^CONFCLOUD-70791.html] )   # Publish the page and edit it again   # Click on the """"make page full-width"""" / """"make page fixed-width"""" button to resize the page   # Repeat step 3 until the page automatically scrolls down and some of the tables are removed    h3. Expected Results  The page should keep all of its contents    h3. Actual Results  Some/ all the tables are removed from the editor      h3. Workaround  * When this happens, hitting Ctrl+Z / command+Z a couple of times will restore the draft's contents.   * Alternatively, if the page was published, revert it to a previous version through *•••* -> *Page History*."""

summary: "Nested macro's in the new editor experience result in endless stream of requests to Confluence"
