description: """h3. Issue Summary    Nested macro's in the new editor experience result in endless stream of requests to Confluence  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Put a static macro from a connect app (for example numbered headings) on a page in the new editor experience.   # Next another macro inside, for example the info panel.    h3. Expected Results    The macro should display correctly on the page  h3. Actual Results    Check the network tab in the browser, it shows an endless stream of calls to /convert/storage  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Numbered custom fields break the Filter Results widget"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The *Filter Results* macro is amazing to pull up JIRA information on Confluence pages, making beautiful reports of our projects.  Though, when inserting a new Filter or editing an existing one, if you have a custom numbered field on your JIRA project and would like to add it into the filter, it won't work.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a custom field on JIRA, selecting the Number one;  # Still on JIRA, create a filter from a project that you added the previously created custom field;  # Go to Confluence and create a new page;  # Call the widget with */filter*;  # Chose the filter you just created;  # On the add fields drop-down list, find the numbered custom field you created and add it;  # Save the display options of the widget.    h3. Expected Results  A preview of the widget is loaded.    h3. Actual Results  Nothing happens and you can no longer use this macro - you have to cancel and start it over.    *Notes*:  * As soon as you hit the *Save* option in the display options, some 200 requests are displayed, but with an error similar to this: {{SyntaxError: JSON.parse: unexpected keyword at line 1 column 1 of the JSON data}}  * The issue also happens when a user using  Text Field (multi-line) and Select List (single choice)    h3. Workaround    Users may use Jira issue macro to display the field."""

summary: "If an image is aligned to left or right in a page, the text on the page is wrapped with the image in the include page macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When using the include page macro, if the page to be included contains an image and some text after the image, if the same image is aligned to the left or right, all the text in the page is wrapped, even though it was not used the option to wrap to left or right.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page   # Add any image (.png, .jpg .jpeg)   # Add some text below the image  # Align it to the left or right (the text will stay at left by default)  # Publish the page  # Create a new page  # Insert the include page macro  # Choose the page created previously  # Publish the second page    h3. Expected Results  The page with the include page macro displays the content the same way it is showing up on the first page to be included the content.    h3. Actual Results  The words are wrapped as it was used the wrap alignment.    h3. Workaround  Use the Align Center instead of left or right alignment and the text won't be wrapped."""

summary: "Space Export timer is not acurate"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When exporting a space, specially large ones, the timer does not represent the actual time to complete the whole export.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access a space with complex and large pages, with many pages and child pages.   # Go to Space Settings > Content Tools > Export   # Choose PDF and export the space;    h3. Expected Results  * Timer should represent accurately the time for the process to complete.    h3. Actual Results  * Timer does not represent time to completion acurately, and may present users with a wrong estimate      h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "View all people on People Directory doesn't work as indicated"
description: """h3. *Requirement:*     Change copy to _*""""Search* *people and teams""""*_  h3. Issue Summary    With the recent implementation of the new People Directory, it's no longer possible to list all people on the Confluence People Directory (differing from what's indicated on [this public documentation|https://support.<USER><USER>cloud/docs/search-the-people-directory/]). However, there's still an option to do so, which doesn't work as indicated.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access Confluence.   # Navigate to *People > View all people and teams*.    h3. Expected Results    As the button indicates to display *all people and teams*, all users should be displayed.    !image-2020-09-17-16-08-49-276.png!  h3. Actual Results    Only the people who have most recently collaborated on the same content as you will be displayed.  h3. Workaround    If the user is a site-admin, it's possible to find a list of all Confluence users on the Instance administration."""

summary: "Title query not working as expected - with special characters"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The files/pages/blogs are not being shown in the search results if they contain special characters.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page/attachment with a name that has a special character(like !""""#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^_`{|}~) in it.   # Go to search/advanced search and put the title there.    h3. Expected Results  The file/page name should show up in the results when searched.    h3. Actual Results  The file/page does not show up in the search results.    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Space settings button fails to load for some users"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  For specific users, when accessing any space, the sidebar buttons such as *Overview*, *Blog* and specially *Space Settings* (which can't be disabled) won't load.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Logged-in as the affected user, try to access any space    h3. Expected Results  Every (not disabled) button from the sidebar should load as expected.    h3. Actual Results  The sidebar buttons such as *Overview*, *Blog* and *Space Settings*  won't load    The below exception is thrown in the browser's console:    Note that the space key mentioned is not from that particular space. In fact, no space with that particular key was found on the affected instances.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "AP.content.getContext() returns an empty object in dynamic content macro editors"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    AP.content.getContext() returns an empty object in dynamic content macro editors.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an app with a dynamic content macro defining a custom editor.   # Open the editor and call AP.content.getContext(callback)    h3. Expected Results    The callback is called with a context object similar to:            {code:java}  <USER> {    content: {      id: """"12345678""""      type: """"page""""      version: """"7""""    }    macro: {      hash: """"58e722af-f259-41be-a8df-ddddddd""""      id: """"58e722af-f259-41be-a8df-eeeeeeee""""    }    space: {      id: """"1234567""""      key: """"FOO""""    }  }  {code}     h3. Actual Results    The context object is empty (for an unlicensed app):    h3. Workaround    None"""

summary: "Unclear macro ID semantics"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When developing dynamic content macros, the semantics of macro IDs should be available from the documentation, but is not. For example, does the ID of a macro mutate? The label ID implies not, but experimentation indicates the macro ID mutates after the macro editor is opened, then closed calling saveMacroData.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Search the documentation for clear details about the semantics of macro IDs.    h3. Expected Results    Semantics are found.  h3. Actual Results    Semantics are not found.  h3. Workaround    None"""

summary: "Table cells colors don't show up on the Published page on the Confluence App for Android , only in edit mode."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using the mobile app Confluence for Android, even though the table cells has colors    applied on the same, it doesn't reflect on the published page on the app, but if you click on edit, it shows up the colors as expected.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page   # Insert a table   # Change the color in some cells of that table   # Publish the page    h3. Expected Results    The table cells colors shows up as expected.  h3. Actual Results    There is no table cell colors in the published page when accessing it on the mobile app for Confluence on Android, but it shows up on the edit mode.  h3. Workaround    * Click on the edit button, when accessing the page in the mobile app for Android, the colors will show up there.    * Alternatively, in the settings screen, turn new page viewing experience switch to on (table cell colors are supported in this experience)"""

summary: "Converting a page that has numbered lists with images within a section macro will break the page's structure"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    According to our [documentation for page conversion|https://support.<USER><USER>cloud/docs/convert-pages-to-the-new-editor/], the *Section macr**o* is completely converted to the new editor. However, if your Legacy page has a numbered list with images within this macro, the page will look different on the view mode. On the conversion preview, the images are still correctly aligned, as well as on the page's edit mode. However, on the view mode, the images are wrongly aligned.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page on the Legacy editor.   # Add the section macro on it.   # Place a numbered list within the macro.   # Add images to the numbered list.   # Convert the page.    h3. Expected Results    The page should respect its alignment, as it does on the edit mode:    !image-2020-09-21-12-33-49-457.png!  h3. Actual Results    After converting the page, the list alignment is different on the view mode:    !image-2020-09-21-12-34-43-804.png!  h3. Workaround    Edit the page, copy the numbered list and paste it outside of the section macro (remove the content from the macro)."""

summary: "Images added to the Announcements (Default Welcome Message template) through the include and excerpt macro does not respect the alignment"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  While it is not possible to insert images on templates, if an image is insert on the template Default Welcome Message through the include or excerpt macros, which is used for the announcements in the Confluence Dashboard, it does not respect the alignment and make it default to the left.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page    # Insert an image aligned to center or to the right   # Publish the page   # Make sure that the Dashboard is the site homepage    # Go to the template Default Welcome Message in the Global Templates and Blueprints in Settings or access the <USER>dashboard and click on the edit pencil in *announcements*  # On the template, use the include page and search for the page just create to include  # Save the template  # Access the Confluence Dashboard    h3. Expected Results  The image alignment from the original page will be respected.    h3. Actual Results  The image is always aligned left. Specific to the *announcements* feed.  Different behaviors are seem with the include page in templates/pages.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Export reports on your space's users to excel not working"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    User seeing *Internal Server Error* when exporting excel for users at space Analytic    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Navigate to space whose analytics you want to view.   # Click Analytics in the sidebar.  The Space Analytics appear.  # Click the Users tab.  # Click the ellipses !image-2020-09-22-23-23-00-701.png|thumbnail! on the top right of the page.  # Click Export Excel.    h3. Expected Results    The excel file will be downloaded.    h3. Actual Results    User seeing Internal Server Error message.    h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Title CQL query doesn't behave as expected with all special characters "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Certain special characters don't behave correctly in title CQL searches  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page with a special ascii character in the title    # Try exact match CQL query title =    # Try fuzzy match CQL query title ~    h3. Expected Results    The relevant page with special character is returned   h3. Actual Results    Varied. Exact match searches on pages with double quotes in the title don't work at all. Exact match searches for ` will also return results with ^ in the same position. Other characters like : need to be escaped to be searched on.   h3. Workaround    Most special characters will behave correctly if escaped with \ in the query. """

summary: "All centrally aligned images are left-aligned on word export"
description: """All centrally aligned images are left-aligned on word export    *Steps to reproduce the issue*  # Create a page  # Add an image  # Set the image to center alignment  # Save page  # Export page to Word (image is not aligned properly) """

summary: "Search Results macro does not return correct matches"
description: """h3. Issue Summary   * Search Results macro does not return correct matches. This behavior is apparent especially when looking for macro terms, such as macro's names.    h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Create a page containing a Search Results Macro;   # On Search terms look for a macro, e.g. """"macroName:excerpt-include*""""   # click on the refresh Button    h3. Expected Results   * Either pages containing the macro to be displayed, or an error message in the lines of """"Incorrect Search Parameters"""" to be displayed    h3. Actual Results   * Unrelated pages are shown, similar to the screenshot below:    !Screen Shot 2020-09-23 at 16.18.22.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Pasting an image into an Info Panel actually splits the panel in two"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  * Pasting an image into an Info Panel actually splits the panel in two, and the image is not inserted into it.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add an info panel macro, and insert an image into the page (outside of the macro);  # Copy/cut the image and try to paste within the Info Panel Macro;    h3. Expected Results  * Image to be pasted within the macro;    h3. Actual Results  * Macro is split in two, and the image is pasted in between macros, as can be seen in this next GIF   !Screen Recording 2020-09-23 at 04.58 PM.gif!     h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Link macro is no longer rendering links as smart links"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In the new editing experience, when pasting a link in the page, as google drive for example, renders the same as a smart link, the same was possible using the link macro (ctrl +k), but it seems to no longer be possible.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page   # Paste a Confluence link page (published view) on the page   # Press ctrl+k or /link to use the link macro   # Paste the link for the Confluence page on the link macro   # Press enter      h3. Expected Results  The link for the Confluence page or other links such as google drive, dropbox <USER> will be rendered as Smart links as well.    h3. Actual Results  It is a dumb link instead of a Smart Link.      h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Specific date fields are not listed on Team Calendar's Jira Issue Dates events "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If a specific date field has a limited context on Jira to appear on specific projects only, that field won't be listed as an option on Team Calendar's Jira Issue Dates events if the JQL query used includes any issue from another project.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a *Date Picker* field on Jira, e.g.: """"Test Date Picker Field""""  # Limit its context to a certain project only, let's say project ABC  # On Team Calendars, create a new *Jira Issue Dates* event  # Use a JQL that includes a different project, such as   {code:none}project in (ABC, DEF) and """"Test Date Picker Field"""" is not empty{code}    h3. Expected Results  The """"Test Date Picker Field"""" custom field should be listed.    h3. Actual Results  The field is not listed.    h3. Workaround  Currently, the field is only listed if using the specific project *only*.  Based on that, limiting the JQL to the projects included on the field's context should allow the field to be listed."""

summary: "Exporting Word pages containing links to other pages end up with broken links"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  * When exporting a page that contains links to other Confluence pages to Word, the generated file end up pointing to broken links, instead of links to these pages.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page, give it a name, and paste a link to another Confluence page > Publish the page;   # Click on ••• > Export to word;   # Edit the exported file in Word;    h3. Expected Results   * Link to correctly point to Confluence page :   *{{https://<InstanceName>.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/<SPACEKEY>/pages/<PageID>}}*    h3. Actual Results   * Link currently points to:  *{{http:///wiki/spaces/<SPACEKEY>/pages/<PageID>}}*    h3. Workaround  * Add a link to the page by using /link or {link and the exported file will have the links correctly pointing to Confluence's page."""

summary: "Smart Links not working with Box links if the same don't start with app.box.com on URLs "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In the new editing experience, we're able to use Smart Links with a variety of links, such as google drive, dropbox, figma and box links.    However, even though it works for app.box.com links to be rendered as smart links if the same are pasted from the share copy link, the same don't work if the link is not from the share link on box.com and if the link has the name of the company, such as companyname.box.com/s/filename instead of app.box.com/s/filename, the links doesn't render as smart links.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page   # Paste a link from box.com from the share file link that has the similar link companyname.box.com/s/filename   # With the same URL link, replace the companyname with the """"app"""" word, it should be like app.box.com/s/filename      h3. Expected Results  Both URLs links are valid and render as Smart Links    h3. Actual Results  Only the link as app.box.com/s/filename render as a Smart Link    h3. Workaround  Replace the companyname.box.com to use the app.box.com to render it as a smart link or use the ctrl + k (link macro) to enter the link a name to the file link shared."""

summary: "Clicking twice on a placeholder to select it and type on it, causes the text to be typed backwards"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In the new editing experience, you can create new templates and insert a placeholder on the same, alongside another options.    However, if you click twice on the placeholder inserted on the template, to select it and then edit it, it causes the text to be typed backwards and the placeholder color stays (gray).    It seems to be affecting only user created templates.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new template (it can be global or at a space level)   # Insert some placeholders and texts there   # Save the template   # Create a new page based on the template create  # Click twice on the placeholder to select it  # Start typing       h3. Expected Results  Text replaces the placeholder and it is on the right orientation.    h3. Actual Results  The text is typed backwards and the placeholder isn't replaced with the text, it stays as a placeholder.    h3. Workaround  Click on the placeholder, without selecting and start typing. It should erase the placeholder and the text to be typed normally on the page.  If the bug has already triggered on the placeholder, select and delete it and start typing normally.    """

summary: "Jira issues shows as hyperlink when used with page property report macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira issues show as a hyperlink when used with page property report macro  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page with page property macro.   # Add a table to page property macro > Add a Jira issue in one of the column/rows   # Create another page and add page property report macro, it will show the Jira issue as *Jira issue macro link*.   # Now go back to the page you created with a page property macro and edit it.   # Load the page with a page property report macro, it will now show the Jira issue link as just *hyperlink*    h3. Expected Results    Once you edit the page with page property macro, the Jira issue in page property report macro should still show the issues with Jira issues  h3. Actual Results    It shows as a hyperlink    h3. Workaround   Currently, the only workaround is to edit the page property macro and place the link with /Jira issues macro.    And if this page needs an edit > after publishing again edit the page and place the issue link with Jira issue macro.    Please note this is not an optimal workaround but will work on those cases where a page with page property macro do not need more editing.    page with page property macro   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   page with page property report macro   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     edited the Page with page property macro.   !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!     refreshed the page with page property report macro.   !screenshot-4.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "List Indenting Buggy"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Sometimes can get into mode where lists can't be indented properly  More context: https://atlassian.slack.com/archives/CFLEB3342/p1601321380029300    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # https://hello.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CONF/pages/890266951/Reproduce+Editing+Bug  # DO NOT EDIT THE PAGE  # Copt a section from that page to a new page  # Try indenting / unindenting to reproduce / fix the problem.     h3. Expected Results  Can indent / unindent any item    h3. Actual Results  Cannot indent / unindent specific items      h3. Workaround  Unindent `H`"""

summary: "JIRA issue macro not displaying affected and fix versions"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JIRA issue macro not displaying affected and fix versions. The column will be empty.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create an issue  # Add 2 versions to the project where the issue is created  # Go to the issue and add affected/fix version  # Create a filter for the project to display all the field with affected/fix version  # Add JIRA Issue Macro to the <USER>page with the filter you created, then click on Display options.  # Add affected/fix version under Columns to display  # Save the page      h3. Expected Results    Affected/Fix versions are displayed in the JIRA Issue Macro        h3. Actual Results    The column is empty    h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Renaming a Blueprint is not working as expected"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Although it's not possible to re-name a Blueprint (we have this feature request:https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-30926 to track our customers' interest on it), Confluence allows you to edit it, and set a new title. The new title won't reflect to the UI (neither on the Templates list or the creation dialog), however the title is updated on Template editor UI and on the Audit Log.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access Confluence.   # Navigate to a Space.   # Click on *Space Settings > Content Tools*.   # Edit a Blueprint (for example, *Product roadmap*).   # Set a new title.   # Save the template.    h3. Expected Results    The template editor shouldn't allow you to set a new title.  h3. Actual Results    A new entry will be created on the Template lists, however the title would remain the original one:    !image-2020-09-29-13-11-26-701.png!    The change is also logged on the Audit Log:    !image-2020-09-29-13-12-24-158.png!    When accessing the Blueprint on edit mode, the new title will be used:      !image-2020-09-29-14-29-31-384.png!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Tapping a space name (without a home page) while moving a page redirects the user to the space"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When [moving a page to a different space|https://support.<USER><USER>cloud/docs/move-copy-and-hide-pages/] that does not have a home page, by searching for the space name and then, selecting the suggestion, it will redirect the user to the space in question while the Move dialog is still displayed.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  (i) Make sure to have a space not containing a home page.  # On an existing page, make sure to tap the ellipsis button at the top right and select *Move*  # Select the *Advanced* option  # Type the name of a space that does not have a home page.  # Tap the space name's suggestion    h3. Expected Results  The user should be kept on the same page until the *Move* action is executed    h3. Actual Results  The user is redirected to the space without a home page while the Move dialog is still displayed. A video has been recorded to demonstrate the phenomenon:     [^move_phenomenon.mp4]     h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. Still, you can use the *Move* option as it will properly work."""

summary: "The recently Updated macro and Recently Updated dashboard are not working properly on Instances which its name starts with wiki"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    On Instances in which the base name starts with wiki (*wikitest.atlassian.net*, for example) the Recently Updated Macro and the Recently Updated dashboard are not working properly. The macros load the pages properly, however the link to the pages are broken, with the base URL duplicated, for example:    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an Instance which its name starts with wiki (i.e. *wikisomething*)   # Create a page.   # Add the Recently Updated Macro on it.   # Publish the page.   # Click on a link from the macro (make sure to have updated some pages recently).    h3. Expected Results    The links should work properly.  h3. Actual Results    The links don't work as expected, as the base URL will be duplicated on it.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Slowness in initial page LOAD or RENDER"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Some pages with large elements are slow to load on initial page RENDER. This issue is separate from slowness experience when trying to EDIT the page.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page with lot of large elements such as tables or macros.   # Navigate to the page    h3. Expected Results  Page loads quickly with all elements    h3. Actual Results  Page takes more than 10 seconds to load.     h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira macros and gadgets failing with 504 (gateway timeout) error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using certain complex filters in the JIRA Issue/Filter macro or in Jira gadgets such as the Two Dimensional Filter Statistics macro, it will either fail to display the preview or throw a 504 status error at the UI.    The filter used can either be a complex filter that takes a considerable amount of time to process or it will return a high number of issues.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a complex filter on Jira   # Use this filter on Confluence, in the JIRA Issue/Filter macro or the Two Dimensional Filter Statistics one    h3. Expected Results    The macro should display its preview.  h3. Actual Results   * For the JIRA Issue/Filter macro, the following error is thrown at the UI:  {code:none}  Received the following HTTP error code from the server:504    In both cases, the request takes 10 seconds to fail.    We also noticed the issue is intermittent. For some cases, the filter may not work at the first attempt but trying again might work.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no other workaround besides decreasing the filter's complexity so it can return results quicker."""

summary: "Unable to publish a template if it it contain's broken link"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Users are not able to publish an existing page template if it contains a broken page link.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a test page on <USER>and copy the link   # Go to Global Templates and Blueprints page - https://yoursitename.atlassian.net/wiki/pages/templates2/listglobaltemplates.action  # Click add global page template  # Add a title and paste the link you copied on step 1.  # Save the template.  # Now, delete the test page and purge it from the space thrash.  # Then go back to the global page template and click edit  # After that, click save      h3. Expected Results    The page template will be saved.    h3. Actual Results    User sees  below error when clicking save:      h3. Workaround    Remove the broken link and click save"""

summary: "The syntax highlighting for the language ABAP in code macro is not correct"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The syntax highlighting for the language ABAP in code macro is not correct.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page and add code macro to it.  # select language as ABAP.   # Add the ABAP language to it. and publish the page.    h3. Expected Results   !image-2020-09-30-19-38-23-649.png|thumbnail!   h3. Actual Results   !image-2020-09-30-19-39-20-802.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround     Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Expand macro does not show in add macro option"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When searching for expand macro from View More macro option to view all macros, it does not display the expand macro option .   However, if we use */expand* directly on page, it displays the expand macro.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page and search for expand macro using '*/expand*'   # Now try searching for expand macro from the *Add macro* option.    h3. Expected Results    The expand macro should be listed in *Add macro* option as well.  h3. Actual Results    The macro does not gets listed in Add macro option, and on the other hand if we use */expand*, it does gets displayed.    !image-2020-10-01-00-27-21-593.png|width=210,height=103!          !image-2020-10-01-00-27-47-284.png|width=240,height=147!  h3. Workaround    Use expand macro from /expand option only."""

summary: "Bulk archiving throws up a confirmation dialog and shouldn't"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Bulk archiving throws up a confirmation dialog and shouldn't    Bulk archiving throws up a confirmation dialog and shouldn't  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to Bulk archive focused state   # Select multiple pages   # Click archive button    h3. Expected Results  No confirmation message. Straight into archive action.  h3. Actual Results  Confirmation message is displayed before starting archive action.    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Copy bug: left side space nav menu and page title should say ""Bulk Archive"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary  left side space nav menu should say """"Bulk Archive""""  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # static    h3. Expected Results  """"Bulk Archive""""  h3. Actual Results  """"Bulk Tools""""  The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:      h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "The table gets cut from the right side when a page is word exported"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The table gets cut from the right side when a page is word exported.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page and add a table to it with at least two columns.   # Add long text to at least one of the columns, it should be this long that the text should not comprise in the page and the scroller comes up.   !image-2020-10-02-13-51-32-838.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  As it is in the PDF export:   !image-2020-10-02-13-53-40-002.png|thumbnail!   h3. Actual Results     !image-2020-10-02-13-53-08-097.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Filter result macro doesn't respond to certain fields"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The *Filter Results* macro becomes irresponsive if we select certain fields for example *Start date* , *% Completion* .  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a *Filter results* macro on a page.   # Click edit to add fields in the macro.   # Select field *Start date* from the dropdown list.   # Click again the dropdown list to look for other fields to be added.    h3. Expected Results    The Filter results macro should display other fields to be added.  h3. Actual Results    The Filter results macro becomes irresponsive, and no other field can be selected to add .    !image-2020-10-02-17-38-12-306.png|width=186,height=114!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no workaround available."""

summary: "Page property report not displaying the field value with merge cell"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The page property report macro won't be displaying any content below the merged cell.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page with a page property macro  # Add a table and add an id, make the column as header  # Add 5 row and then merge the 3rd-row cell   !444.PNG|thumbnail!    # Create another page and add page property report macro    h3. Expected Results    The Page property report will display all the field value    h3. Actual Results    The page property report will only display the first 2-row field value.    h3. Workaround    Delete the table and create a new table without merging the cell"""

summary: "Images in Confluence are rendered bigger than original file in Jira Issue"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  * Images on Confluence are being rendered bigger than the original file, when these images are attached on a Jira Issue and reported though /Jira macro    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a test issue and upload an image (e.g. the 800x400 image annexed);   # Create a page in Confluence and add a /Jira macro > paste the link to the issue above;  # On Display Options select only *Key*, *Summary*, *Description*;  # Publish the page > Page should look like this next screenshot:   !Screen Shot 2020-10-05 at 10.51.36.png|thumbnail!   # Right-click on the image > Inspect Element > Check image's dimensions    h3. Expected Results  * Image to have the same dimensions as the original file (in this case 800x400)    h3. Actual Results  * Image is actually larger than the original file (808x400);   !Screen Shot 2020-10-05 at 10.51.02.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Space logo and Space name not showing for anonymous users, even if it is open to public access"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    It is possible to [setup public access|https://support.<USER><USER>cloud/docs/set-up-public-access/] to the entire instance or particular spaces in Confluence.    When the anonymous users access those public spaces, the space logo and the space name turns into a grey bar instead, and the overview page on the left bar is gone, leaving only the page tree visible.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new space    # Create a new page and publish it   # In space Settings, make sure to open it to anonymous user (at least view permissions)   # In the global permission settings, open the instance to be accessible to anonymous users   # Copy the published page or space URL link and open it on incognito/in private window of your browser    h3. Expected Results  You'll be able to see the overview page in the left sidebar and the space logo/space name.    h3. Actual Results  The overview page name in the left side bar isn't there and the space name and logo are greyed out.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira macro can't list issues when there's a Select List custom field listed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using the *JIRA Issue/Filter* macro, setting Table view and selecting a *Select List* custom fields under *Columns to display* will prevent the macro from displaying the results.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a *Select List* custom field on Jira   # Create an issue on Jira and set that field   # In a Confluence page, insert a JIRA Issue/Filter macro and find that issue created on step 2   # Set the macro to *Table* under *Display Options*   # Add the field created on step 1 to the *Columns to display*   # Save the macro and publish the page    h3. Expected Results    The JIRA Issue/Filter macro should display the results  h3. Actual Results    The JIRA Issue/Filter macro will show the following error:  {quote}(!) Refresh didn't work. Try again or click an issue to see it in JIRA.  {quote}  No errors in the browser's console or in the requests.  h3. Workaround    Currently, the only available workaround is to remove the *Select List* custom field from the *Columns to display* in the Jira macro."""

summary: "Users is not in list of suggestion in Auto Complete/Mentions if the ""Full name"" is multibyte language like Japanese."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Users is not in list of suggestion in Auto Complete/Mentions if the """"Full name"""" is multibyte language like Japanese.  It works fine if the """"Public name"""" is multibyte language.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create user like below   #* Full name : 山田太郎   #* Public name : Yamada tarou   # Edit a page    # Input """"@山田""""    h3. Expected Results  The user *is* in the list of the suggestion.    h3. Actual Results  The user *isn't* in the list of the suggestion.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira macro URL links not working according to configured display URL"
description: """*Issue*   JIRA Issue Macro in the Confluence page is using Display URL while the Application URL is unreachable.    *Replication steps*   1.Link Jira server and <USER>cloud.   2.Ensure that the Display URL and the Base URL of Confluence and JIRA is using the proxied URL.   3.Create a page in Confluence and insert a JIRA Issue Macro.    *EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR*   The JIRA macro issue links should redirecting to Display URL: [https://helpdesk-vn.xyz.com\|https://helpdesk-vn.xyz.com/] (this is Jira server URL)    *ACTUAL BEHAVIOUR*   The JIRA issue macro links redirecting to Application URL: [https://jiraz.xyz.com|https://jiraz.xyz.com/]; this is proxy URL) instead of Display URL: [https://helpdesk-vn.xyz.com\|https://helpdesk-vn.xyz.com/] (this is Jira server URL)    *Note*   If we access the single issue link then it works fine."""

summary: "Agile Wallboard Gadget doesn't show the name of the first user of a board using swimlanes based on Assignees"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Adding an Agile Wallboard Gadget in a Confluence page to show a Jira Software board using swimlanes based on Assignee doesn't show the information properly. The name of the first user in the swimlanes doesn't appear, but the tickets of the user show in the columns backlog, in progress, done (for example).    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Jira Software Kanban project;  # Create a Kanban board using swimlanes based on Assignees  # Create a Confluence page;  # Add an Agile Wallboard Gadget to the page;  # Select the board to show in the Gadget;    h3. Expected Results  The Gadget will show the columns of the board (backlog, in progress, done, ...), the users, and their tickets.    h3. Actual Results  The name of the first user in the swimlanes doesn't show in the Gadget and the user's tickets appear inside the columns names.    h3. Workaround  There is no workaround at the moment."""

summary: "Jira Issue shows wrong number of issues and limited to 100"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira Issue/filter on Confluence shows the wrong number of issues at the end of the list and also limited to 100  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new *page*   # Insert a *JIRA Issue/Filter*   # *Search* for more than 100 issues   # Click on *Display options*   # On *Maximum issues* write the number 1000   # Click on the button *Insert*    h3. Expected Results   * At the end of the page, you should see """"Showing 105 out of 105 issues"""" and all of the issues listed, or at least, should create numbered pages to display all issues    h3. Actual Results   * At the end of the page, you see """"Showing 1000 out of 105 issues"""" and a maximum of 100 issues listed    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira issue/filter macro is not showing information from custom URL field created in Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Content of a custom URL field is not showing in the """"Jira issue/filter"""" macro     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Jira Software project;  # Create a custom URL field;  # Add information in this custom field;  # Create a Confluence page;  # Add the """"Jira issue/filter"""" macro in this page;  # Create a query to search project where you added information to the custom URL field created;  # Open the *Display options* of the macro  # Add the custom URL field created in the *Columns to display*    h3. Expected Results  The macro will show the columns set in the search of your macro, and the proper information of those fields.    h3. Actual Results  The custom URL field is not populated.    h3. Workaround  There is no workaround at the moment."""

summary: "Attachments macro will make the date picker to be cut on the legacy editor"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In a page using the legacy editor, if we place an Attachments macro and then insert a date macro, when editing that date macro the date picker will be cut, preventing Saturday to be shown.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page in the legacy editor template  # Add an Attachments macro  # Add a date macro and select any date  # Publish the page and edit it again  # Click on the date macro to open the date picker    h3. Expected Results  The date picker should display correctly  |  !Screen Shot 2020-10-06 at 22.06.27.png|thumbnail!  |    h3. Actual Results  The date picker is cut, preventing Saturday to be displayed  |  !Screen Shot 2020-10-06 at 22.05.15.png|thumbnail!  |    h3. Workaround  Either refresh/ reload the editor or remove the attachments macro."""

summary: "Header row in table do not stick on top of page in page include macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  We we use a table in include page macro the table header do not stick on top of page while scrolling the page.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Step 1 Create a page and insert a table.   !image-2020-10-07-15-46-20-318.png|thumbnail!    # Step 2 Create another page insert a page include macro and select the page, created in first step.   !image-2020-10-07-15-47-16-634.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  Table header should stick on the top of the page while scrolling the page.   !image-2020-10-07-15-48-05-344.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  Table header do not stick on the top of the page while scrolling the page.   !image-2020-10-07-15-48-56-982.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Size of PDF upon export has increased"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    It is possible to [export Confluence pages|https://support.<USER><USER>cloud/docs/export-content-to-word-pdf-html-and-xml/] and spaces to various formats, including PDF.    Recently, it seems that those exports size have increased, even though no more pages or attachments were added to the pages/space to be exported into PDF.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a space   # Add some pages in the space   # Go to space *Settings > content tools*   # Click on *Export*   # Choose the option *export* to *PDF*   # Choose either *Full* or *Custom* export   # Click *Export*    h3. Expected Results  Export to PDF size didn't increased much from one export to another, if the content hasn't changed much too.    h3. Actual Results  The size difference between PDFs export increased a lot.     h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira Issue Macro returns template error when using some Jira filters"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JIRA filters for some tenants are returning error when used inside JIM.    We can fix it using the Governator tool: Refresh Application links data but after a while, the problem appears again.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Jira filter   # Create a JIM with the filter created in step 1    h3. Expected Results   # JIRA macro should show tickets based on Jira filtered results    h3. Actual Results    Error is displayed in JIM asking user to refresh    A query on prod returns the following stack trace:    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available         Potential workaround: Disable using Jira filters and use JQL instead"""

summary: "Create from template doesn´t update correctly when moved"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Create from template macro that doesn´t update correctly when moved from one space to another space. The meeting note will be added to the old space.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   #  Create a page and add create from template macro   # Now move the page to another space  # Click creates from template macro and then publish the template.    h3. Expected Results    The page will be located in the new space.    h3. Actual Results    The page is located in the old space.    h3. Workaround    Once the page is moved to the new space, edit the macro and click save."""

summary: "Unable to search questions using first word"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  if we search the question with just the first word of the question (Finnish word), it will not return the result, it doesn’t show the suggestions for the existing questions.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Step 1 Go to <USER>question addon and search for question using first word, ex:testing   !image-2020-10-08-19-28-00-628.png|thumbnail!       h3. Expected Results  Questions with word """"testing"""" should show in the suggestion list but it shows only create""""testing""""   !image-2020-10-08-19-30-32-849.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  It shows create option.    Note: If we search first word """"testing """" and add space, it shows the results.   !image-2020-10-08-19-32-18-916.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  - Search with first word and space.  - Search by incomplete word, it will show the suggestion for related results."""

summary: "Table of Contents Macro's does not navigate if heading in the table header."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Table of Contents Macro does not go to the expected header destination if heading in the table header.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add Table of Contents Macro on the page   # Below on the page, Add single-column tables and put heading in the table header.   # Publish the page and click on the TOC    h3. Expected Results    The user being skip directly to the information they’re looking for.     h3. Actual Results    Nothing happens when you click on it.    h3. Workaround     Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Android mobile app limits the list of spaces to 40"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If a user has more than 40 starred spaces, the list of spaces won't list all of them, being limited to 40 spaces.   This will make it impossible to access certain spaces.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Star (favourite) 50 spaces  # Access Confluence using Android's app  # Select *Spaces* at the bottom and scroll down trough the list    h3. Expected Results  It should list all starred spaces    h3. Actual Results  Only 40 spaces are shown:  |  !Screenshot_20201008-213513_Confluence.png|thumbnail! |  | !Screenshot_20201008-213519_Confluence.png|thumbnail! |  | !Screenshot_20201008-213527_Confluence.png|thumbnail! |    h3. Workaround  Use the Search to look for the desired page inside of the space you want to access."""

description: """h3. Issue Summary  'Created vs Resolved Chart' gadget not displaying any results    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add 'Created vs Resolved Chart' gadget to the page.   # Publish the page.    h3. Expected Results  The results should appear.    h3. Actual Results  Nothing displayed, just a blank white box.    h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

description: """h3. Issue Summary    'JIRA road map' gadget is not working  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add the 'JIRA road map' gadget to the page.   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!      # Publish the page.    h3. Expected Results    The results should appear.  h3. Actual Results    Nothing displayed, just a blank white box.  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira Issue/Filter showing total issue count is not working properly on edit mode"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    It's possible to add a Jira Issue/Filter macro on a Confluence page, to display results on Jira, based on a JQL query. Instead of displaying the details for the issues, the macro can be used to display total issue counts. However, in case the page has many of them (I was able to replicate on pages with more than 90), they don't work properly when editing the page.    (on) The page works properly on view mode, this issue only happens on edit mode.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Confluence page.   # Add the Jira Issue/Filter macro on it, setting it to display total issue count.   # Copy this macro, and place it on the page 89 times.   # Publish the page.   # Note that all counts will work as expected. Now, access the page on edit mode.    h3. Expected Results    The issue counts should work as they do on view mode.   h3. Actual Results    The macro displays below error message:    !image-2020-10-09-19-32-08-108.png!  h3. Workaround    If you click over the macro error, you'll be able to click on the pencil to edit it. Then, note that the filter loads properly on the macro preview. Clicking on the *Insert* button will fix the macro on the editor. However, this issue may happen again (it's intermittent and the fix is not permanent)."""

summary: "All day events are not notifying users watching the calendar"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Events set to *All day* are not triggering reminders to the users that are following the calendar.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a calendar  # Create a new event of any event type and set it to *All day event*  # Set a reminder for that specific event type  # Make sure you're watching the calendar and enable the reminders for all event types    h3. Expected Results  You should receive a reminder notification related to that event    h3. Actual Results  No reminders are currently being triggered/ sent to *All day events*    h3. Workaround  Currently the only available workaround is to create another event at the beginning of the day without setting it to *All day* in order to trigger the reminder."""

summary: "Jira Calendars on Confluence are throwing an error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    An error is shown in Team Calendars for *Jira Issues Dates* event type  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a project on Jira with at least 5 issues   # Create a custom field using the *Date and Time picker* type and set its value on all of the 5 issues   # Create a Calendar on Confluence   # Add an event using *Jira Issues Dates* and fill the JQL field   # Set the field created on step 2   # Save it and reload the page    h3. Expected Results    It should display the calendar  h3. Actual Results    An error is thrown at the UI:   !Screen Shot 2020-10-12 at 19.42.05.png|width=700!   {quote}Error(s) occurred.    Unable to load events of broken - CALENDAR NAME (f748534b-65d0-4683-8119-c363cda58269): com.atlassian.<USER>extra.calendar3.exception.CalendarException: calendar3.error.jiralink  {quote}    h3. Workaround  It's been noticed that if the field is just a Date one (instead of date and time) it doesn't fail. Based on that, the workaround would be to replace the selected field with a Date only field."""

summary: "Content by label macro display all the lines in an Excerpt macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Content by label macro display all the lines in an Excerpt macro of the returned page, rather than the first line.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page with any text inside an Excerpt macro   # Create a label for the created page   # Create another page and insert the Content by label macro   # On the filter, add the label of the page with the Excerpt macro selecting on the Display excerpts the option simple    h3. Expected Results  According to the doc page [Insert the content by label macro|https://support.<USER><USER>cloud/docs/insert-the-content-by-label-macro/], when using the Display excerpts, *Simple*, it should display only *the first line* of text contained in an Excerpt macro any of the returned pages.    h3. Actual Results  Is showing *all the lines* if the text contained in an Excerpt macro any of the returned pages.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Team Calendars throws an error for some events intermittently"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Team Calendars is eventually throwing an error up on the screen without an apparent reason. This comes and goes, so it happens intermittently.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a calendar;   # Add a bunch of distinct events.    h3. Expected Results    The events load fine.  h3. Actual Results    Some events throw errors at the top of the calendar saying it was a PSQL exception   !image-2020-10-14-08-34-02-131.png!    *Notes*:   * At this moment, we are not able to identify how the issue is reliably reproducible, but we are collecting more information about it and will update this bug request as we go.    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Sprint Burndown Gadget has incorrect field for Sprint"
description: """h3. Summary    Sprint Burndown Gadget shows the label *Board* for the field *Sprint*.  !image-2020-10-13-11-11-44-018.png!  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Confluence page.   # Add a Sprint Burndown Gadget macro.    h3. Expected Results    The macro settings will show the label *Sprint* for the field *Sprint*.  h3. Actual Results    The macro settings show the label *Board* for the field *Sprint*.    *Notes*:   * This was previously reported on this other bug that was closed as *won't fix* - the reason was that we wouldn't pay too much attention to it due to the fact we didn't have too many customers reporting about this issue, but it worth it to give this any attention, as it misleads the end-user in the UI.    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no workaround to apply on this bug."""

summary: "Jira issue/filter macro is not showing information from custom label field created in Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Content of a custom *label* field is not showing in the """"Jira issue/filter"""" macro     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Jira project;  # Create a custom *label* field;  # Add information in this custom field;  # Create a Confluence page;  # Add the """"Jira issue/filter"""" macro in this page;  # Create a query to search project where you added information to the custom *label* field created;  # Open the *Display options* of the macro  # Add the custom *label* field created in the *Columns to display*    h3. Expected Results  The macro will show the columns set in the search of your macro, and the proper information of those fields.    h3. Actual Results  The custom *label* field is not populated.    h3. Workaround  There is no workaround at the moment."""

summary: "Pages can't be displayed nor edited if they have a calendar macro and Team Calendars is not enabled"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If a page has a Calendar macro and Team Calendars doesn't have a valid subscription currently active in the instance, the page will fail to load and be edited.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Activate/ Subscribe to the Team Calendars app  # Add the calendar macro to a page and publish it  # Unsubscribe to Team Calendars  # Refresh the page / try to edit it      h3. Expected Results  The page should still be displayed and be editable    h3. Actual Results  * The page won't load with the following error message:  {quote}(x) We couldn't display your content  Refresh the page and try again. If the problem persists, contact Atlassian Support{quote}  * It can't be edited with the following error message:  {quote} (x) We couldn't load the editor.  Refresh the page and try again. If the problem persists, contact Atlassian Support.{quote}    h3. Workaround  * Re-activate Team Calendars and remove the macro from the page    Or     * Contact us through [https://support.<USER>] mentioning this case so we can remove the macro directly from the page's source code."""

summary: "Trello board is not shown in edit mode"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Trello board is not shown is edit mode  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add Trello board macro to the page     # Add same board again    h3. Expected Results    Both Trello boards are shown  h3. Actual Results    All Trello boards except first are shown as placeholders      !f69afd05-8956-47fc-a178-c91002ffff99.png!    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Updating the title of a page which its link is on a Page Properties macro will reflect on the Page Properties but won't on the Page Properties Report"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    On Confluence, if you paste a link to another page on the editor, this link will be automatically replaced by a SmarLink, which would be a card with the page's title. For example:    !image-2020-10-14-18-52-28-254.png!    This behavior also happens when pasting a link to a page within a table/Page Properties macro. However, if you edit this page and change its title, although the new title will reflect on the Page Properties, the Page Properties Report will still show the old one.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page (for example, *Atlassian Support Test*).   # Create another page (for example, *Testing links within Page Properties*), and then pace a table within a Page Properties macro.    # Add the link to *Atlassian Support Test* on the table. For example:   !image-2020-10-14-18-58-30-382.png|width=558,height=170!   # Add a label to this page.   # Create another page (for example, *Page Properties Report Test*), and place the Page Properties Report macro on it.   # Configure the macro to pull pages with the label used on *Testing links within Page Properties*, and make sure to set the report to pull the link column. For example:   !image-2020-10-14-19-00-12-659.png!   # Edit *Atlassian Support Test*, updating its title.   # Check both pages now:   - *Testing links within Page Properties*   - *Page Properties Report Test*    h3. Expected Results    The updated title should reflect on both pages.  h3. Actual Results    The title is updated on *Testing links within Page Properties*, but not on *Page Properties Report Test:*    !image-2020-10-14-19-03-05-133.png|width=605,height=186!    !image-2020-10-14-19-03-34-838.png|width=586,height=72!  h3. Workaround    Performing a dump update on *Testing links within Page Properties* would fix the issue (there's no need to change anything on the page's content)."""

summary: "iframe macro can't render external PDF files"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When pointing the iframe macro to an external public PDF file, nothing will be rendered    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Get a link to a publicly available PDF file from an external website  # Add the iframe macro to a Confluence page and point it to that PDF file  # Save the macro and publish the page    h3. Expected Results  It should render the PDF file the same way it happens with an usual iframe object    h3. Actual Results  Nothing is rendered and the following errors are logged to the browser's console:  {code:none}batch.js?externals=__local-default__&locale=en-US:1 TypeError: Cannot read property 'appendChild' of null      at HTMLDocument.<anonymous> (batch.js?externals=__local-default__&locale=en-US:889)      at o (VM3113 userscript.html:3)      at Object.add (VM3113 userscript.html:3)      at init.ready (VM3113 userscript.html:3)      at new init (VM3113 userscript.html:3)      at e (VM3113 userscript.html:3)      at batch.js?externals=__local-default__&locale=en-US:889      at batch.js?externals=__local-default__&locale=en-US:891  WRMCB @ batch.js?externals=__local-default__&locale=en-US:1  (anonymous) @ batch.js?externals=__local-default__&locale=en-US:892    batch.js?externals=__local-default__&locale=en-US:1 TypeError: Cannot read property 'add' of undefined      at batch.js?externals=__local-default__&locale=en-US:937      at batch.js?externals=__local-default__&locale=en-US:951{code}    h3. Workaround  Download the PDF file and attach it to the page instead of its link."""

summary: "A Hyperlink does not re-direct a user to the Anchor Heading if it contains a slash (/) in it"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  A hyperlink does not re-direct a user to the Anchor Heading if it contains a slash ( / ) in it    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page, add a heading with a slash in it ( / ). _ex: *Hi / hello*_   # Copy it links and put it in a hyperlink on the same page.  # Publish the page.    h3. Expected Results  When a user clicks on a hyperlink, the user should be redirected to the Anchor heading (with '/').    h3. Actual Results  The user is not redirected to the anchor heading when a user clicks on its respective hyperlink.    h3. Workaround  Remove '/' from the heading."""

summary: "Pasting colored text into Confluence loses original color formatting"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Copying colored text from any source other than Fabric itself and then pasting it into Confluence loses the original chosen color.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page in Confluence   # Copy colored text from another source other than Fabric itself   # Paste it into the recently created Confluence page    h3. Expected Results    The color formatting is kept  h3. Actual Results    The color formatting is lost, even though the answer to this report says that keeping the original color format is expected:    https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-61861         It only works when copying from the Fabric (New) editor itself.         Examples    From Microsoft Word:    !Screen-Recording-2020-10-16-at-1.gif|width=163,height=100!    From Apple Pages    !Screen-Recording-2020-10-16-at-1(1).gif|width=171,height=104!    From HTML Content    !Screen-Recording-2020-10-16-at-1(2).gif|width=199,height=121!    From the Legacy Editor    !Screen-Recording-2020-10-16-at-1(3).gif|width=192,height=117!         Example where it works:    From Fabric:    !Screen-Recording-2020-10-16-at-1(4).gif|width=205,height=125!  h3. Workaround    No workaround. We need to manually set the text color again"""
