Expected Results    The Jira macro repair should be successful    h3. Actual Results      The Jira Macro repair fails for customer.    h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Enabling Confluence Anonymous Access through JSD won't create a record on Confluence Audit Logs"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If your Instance has JSD + Confluence, it's possible to use Confluence as a KB for a JSD project. When configuring JSD, it's possible to make changes to below setting:    *Can customers access and send requests from the help center without logging in?*     Marking this option as *Yes* will enable Anonymous Access on Confluence Global Permissions. JSD will notify the user about this change, however, in case the user accepts, Confluence Global permission will be changed and this change won't be logged on Confluence Audit Logs.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up an Instance with both Jira <USER>and Confluence.   # Make sure that Anonymous access is not enabled on Confluence Global Permissions.   # Create a JSD project, and link it to a Confluence Space.   # Access the JSD settings (<INSTANCE>.atlassian.net/secure/admin/SDConfiguration.jspa).   # <USER>*Can customers access and send requests from the help center without logging in?* as *Yes*.   # Click on *Got it*.   # Access Confluence's Audit Log.    h3. Expected Results    As this permission was changed on Confluence, there should be an entry for it on the Audit Log.  h3. Actual Results    This change is not logged on the Audit Log.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "JSD admins can enable Anonymous access on Confluence even if they are not Confluence administrators"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If your Instance has JSD + Confluence, it's possible to use Confluence as a KB for a JSD project. When configuring JSD, it's possible to make changes to below setting:    *Can customers access and send requests from the help center without logging in?*     Marking this option as *Yes* will enable Anonymous Access on Confluence Global Permissions. However, any JSD admin will be able to change this (even if this user is not an admin on Confluence).  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up an Instance with both Jira <USER>and Confluence.   # Make sure that Anonymous access is not enabled on Confluence Global Permissions.   # Create a JSD project, and link it to a Confluence Space.   # Log on JSD with an account that doesn't have admin access on Confluence.   # Access the JSD settings (<INSTANCE>.atlassian.net/secure/admin/SDConfiguration.jspa).   # <USER>*Can customers access and send requests from the help center without logging in?* as *Yes*.   # Click on *Got it*.    h3. Expected Results    The user shouldn't be able to change this setting, as it would reflect in an app that he/she doesn't administer.  h3. Actual Results    Anonymous access is enabled on Confluence.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Images and macros are being cut-off when a page is exported to PDF"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When erxporting pages to PDF the end result contain cut-off images and macros. It seems that page breaks are not being considered when exporting a page to PDF.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page containing some images (I was able to reproduce with 8 large images);  # Add a Table of content macro, some headings, and some info panels between images;  # Publish the page and export it to PDF    h3. Expected Results  * Content of the page to be correctly segregated on page breaks.    h3. Actual Results  * Content (images/macros) are cut off on the exported PDF files, as can be seen in this screenshot here:   !Screen Shot 2020-10-20 at 12.04.22.png|thumbnail!     reference PDF:  [^ITS-PDFExporttest-201020-1459.pdf]     h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Epic Link is displaying the epic's ID instead of its title on the Jira macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Using the *JIRA Issue/Filter* macro to list issues with the *Epic Link* field, this field is showing the epic's id instead of its title, (it was showing the title before September/29).    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an issue on jira and fill the Epic Link field   # Insert a *JIRA Issue/Filter* macro on a Confluence page and search for that issue   # Under *Display Options* -> *Columns to display* add the *Epic Link* field   # Save the macro and publish the page    h3. Expected Results  In the *Epic Link* column it should display the Epic Name    h3. Actual Results  It shows the Epic's issuekey    !Screen Shot 2020-10-20 at 18.28.44.png|thumbnail!       h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Slow Performance while editing Legacy Editor Page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    This bug tracks any reports of page performance issues on the LEGACY EDITOR. These issues may be caused by a large number of tables with data.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a la page with 10+ tables each with around 10-20 rows   # Notice that the performance of the table was slow while inserting some text to the table.   # Page load times vastly increase    h3. Expected Results    Performance of the table should be stabilized while inserting text on the tables.  h3. Actual Results    Degraded performance while editing page and intermittent inability to save.  h3. Workaround    Try removing elements on the page or splitting up the page and see if that improves the editing speed. If you are able to remove elements and see performance improvements, please share as a comment to help us narrow our focus on key elements that are slowing page performance."""

summary: "Select a different project in JiraRoadmap macro's edit modal doesn't update the macro on the page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Select a different project in JiraRoadmap macro's edit modal and hit save doesn't update the macro on the page.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to a Confluence page that has JiraRoadmap macro embedded.   # Focus on the macro and click on """"edit"""" button, see edit modal shows up.   # Select a different project, click on """"save"""" button and edit modal closes.   # Macro is not updated with the selected project.    h3. Expected Results    Macro is updated with selected project.  h3. Actual Results    Macro should display the roadmap of the selected project.  h3. Workaround    Delete the macro and insert a new JiraRoadmap macro."""

summary: "Search redirecting links are inconsistent in ""live search macro"""
description: """h3. Summary    The *Live Search* macro generated links are not working properly and all ends with *$label.getUrlPath($spaceKey)*.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Insert *Live Search* macro on your page and choose a label that will populate the macro.   # Publish the page.   # Click on any link generated by the  *Live Search* macro.    h2. Troubleshooting performed:   *  I see that upon using the search option in the page I see that the image in search results redirects to a """" *Dead link*"""" and below errors re-logged in our developer tools:      However, If is select on """" *see more results* """" in the search option and select on the same image It redirects me to the correct page and the image attached as referenced in the Image attachments.  h3. Expected Results    You are properly redirected when clicking on a link.  h3. Actual Results    You are redirected to an error page _Oops, you've found a dead link_  h3. Workaround    Use the advanced search to view accurate results."""

summary: "There is no longer the option to change restrictions on the overview page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The option to change restrictions has been removed from actions that can be done on a page, now it is only possible through the lock icon. However, it is not possible to change the restrictions on the *Overview page* because there is no lock icon on it.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new space.   # On the overview page, go to *More (...)*   # There is no option to change page restrictions    h3. Expected Results    There should be a way to change restrictions on the Overview page.  h3. Actual Results    There is no option to change restrictions on the Overview page.  h3. Workaround    In case the Overview pages were restricted before this change was made, the user can remove all restrictions by navigating to *Space Settings > Permissions > Restricted Pages*    Find the Overview page in the list of restricted pages and remove restrictions by clicking on the lock next to each user's name. After that, the pages become open to everyone who is allowed to view the space."""

summary: "Inserting an image on legacy editor inside the panel macro and then aligning the image, does not allow the text to be in the same line "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When you insert an image on the legacy editor, it is possible to add text on the line.  However, if you align the image, to the left for example, the text goes below the image and it is not possible to put the text back to the image's line.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page in the legacy editor   # Add an image   # Add some text on the same line as the image  # Align the image to the left (the text will go below)  # Try to insert the text back on the image's line    h3. Expected Results  It is possible to get the text back to the same image's line    h3. Actual Results  It is not possible to add the text to the same line as the image is after the align      h3. Workaround  Remove the content and add it again, without aligning the image.  The text will should stay on the same line as the image is."""

summary: """Help"" link for Anchor macro in macro browser follows to ""Page not found"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary    """"Help"""" link for Anchor macro in macro browser follows to """"Page not found""""  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open macro browser   # Find """"Anchor"""" macro   # Click on the """"Help"""" link in the top right corner    h3. Expected Results    Link follows you to the corresponding page with info about Anchor macro  h3. Actual Results    Page [https://<USER><USER>display/ConfCloud/Anchors] is open, there is """"Page not found"""" error    !image-2020-10-22-17-00-25-582.png!    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Sorting columns with numbers at the end of a sentence will not work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  After [adding a table in the editor|https://support.<USER><USER>cloud/docs/add-formatting-to-your-page/#Addformattingtoyourpage-tables] and including the same sentence for each row, but, only a different number at the end of each, it will not allow the sort to work.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Edit/create a page in Confluence.  # Add a table on the page.   # Make sure that this table has the same sentence in different rows, but, only a different number at the end of each.  # Publish the page  # Tap the sorting option for the created table.    h3. Expected Results  The sorting should work based on the numbers, as it happens with sentences with numbers at the beginning or only numbers for each row.    h3. Actual Results  The sort does not work, in opposite to numbers only or numbers at the beginning of the sentence, like the gif demonstrating the example below:     !sorting.gif|thumbnail!    (i) This also affects a list of numbers that will have a single letter inside (different letters or the same letter, but, with different numbers), like in the image below:    !single.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround  Add the numbers to the beginning of the sentence instead."""

summary: "Filter Search on Jira macros is not working properly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary   * When you add a Jira Macro, you are able to look for Jira filters, that will retrieve the desired info. Recently, however, due to Confluence not rendering filters' drop-down menu correctly, users are unable to select the desired filter. The drop-down menu seems to be rendered behind the macro insert modal.    This issue currently affects:   * Filter Results   * Created vs. Resolved Chart   * Pie Chart   * Recently Created Chart   * Average Time in Status   * Jira Road Map (Gadget)   * Activity Stream (Gadget)    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page > add one Jira macro (in this case we'll use Pie Chart)   # The Filter box and statistic type box sit on top of each other so you can barely see the input box for the filter (screenshot 1)   !Screen Shot 2020-10-22 at 16.05.17.png|width=699,height=434!   # Click on the very top of the dialogue box (you'd have to be almost pixel perfect) to search for the filter's keywords;    h3. Expected Results   * The drop-down menu to display filter results based on key-words.    h3. Actual Results   * The *Filter* dialogue box is not properly rendered, and when you are able to click on it and add key-words the actual drop-down menu with the filters' results is not shown to the user.   * It seems that the drop-down menu is rendered behind the macro insertion modal.    !Screen Shot 2020-10-22 at 16.33.36.png|width=388,height=234!    (Part of the drop-down being rendered)  h3. Workaround   * If you type a few letters (e.g. """"asd""""), and hit enter on your keyboard the *Filter* dialogue box will be expanded, allowing you to see what you're typing;   * It seems that in some cases adding the first two letters of the filter that you're looking for will generate a drop-down list with possible filters;   * Otherwise, you'd have to type the precise Filter's name in order to search for it, and hit enter twice. (The issue here is that the drop-down menu is not rendered correctly, and the correct option will be selected, but you won't be able to see it)"""

summary: "Filter Results macro: users cannot add a Text Field under Fields to Display"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using the Filter Results macro and accessing Fields to Display, it is not possible to add a text custom field to the list.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Text custom field on JIRA, selecting the Number one;   # Still on JIRA, create a filter from a project that you added the previously created custom field;   # Go to Confluence and create a new page;   # Call the widget with */filter*;   # Chose the filter you just created;   # On the add fields drop-down list, find the text custom field you created and try to add it;    h3. Expected Results    The text field should be added to the list of fields to display.  h3. Actual Results    The field never shows up in the list after pressing *Add*  h3. Workaround    Users may use the Jira issue macro to display the field."""

summary: "Full Space PDF Export causing OutOfMemoryError"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Generating a PDF export of a large space can cause an OutOfMemoryError and causing the JVM to crash.    h3. Environment   * Confluence Server    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to a large space with lots of pages and attachments.   # Generate a full Space PDF export.    h3. Expected Results  The PDF export process completes without issues after some time.    h3. Actual Results  The process starts consuming JVM memory over time, causing initial slowness then ultimately crashing the JVM due to OutOfMemoryError.    h3. Notes  This behavior can be triggered by any user with the ability to generate space PDF exports and can cause outages in Production environments. Server downtime.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available.  A few things can be done to prevent the OOME:  # Increase JVM heap size to allow the PDF export process to complete.  # Break down a large Spaces into smaller Spaces.  # Disable PDF exports altogether: [How to disable PDF Export for Pages or Spaces|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-disable-pdf-export-for-pages-or-spaces-711361415.html]."""

summary: "When coping indented notes from word, outlook, or any text editor the formatting is not being carried over into Confluence."
description: """When coping indented notes from word, outlook, or any text editor the formatting is not being carried over into Confluence.      h3. Issue Summary    When users are taking notes locally in a notepad, word, outlook for example When a bullet """"unindents"""" more than one level, Confluence misses that. Not only does this put that bullet at the wrong level, it also means all later bullets are shifted by one level. Add this up over a long series of meeting notes, and the end result looks very little like the original. I have attached a picture showing the issue. Note the indent level of the last line """"Back to First level"""". In Word, it really is back to the far left, however in Confluence it is actually at the Second level.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a sample word doc, or text with indents   # Create table in any supported browser and paste the content into confluence     h3. Expected Results  Indents should be properly formatted    h3. Actual Results  Indents are shifted over one level      h3. Workaround    NO workaround available seems to happen consistently across all supported browsers.    """

summary: "Using $config.getBaseUrl() and  $config.getDomainName() calls in user macros does not work in 7.x Confluence"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Using $config.getBaseUrl() and   $config.getDomainName() calls in user macros does not work in 7.x Confluence    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create user maco with $config.getBaseUrl()   # Try to click on macro,     h3. Expected Results  Should pull the baseURL     h3. Actual Results  URL will show $config.getBaseUrl() instead of actually picking up the base URL same with getdomain name    h3. Workaround    Use the hardcoded URL for the baseURL instead of the call. """

summary: "Importing space from Cloud fails due to duplicated lower_username"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Importing a space backup of Cloud instance fails violating unique constraint """"unq_lwr_username""""  {code:none}  com.atlassian.confluence.importexport.ImportExportException: Unable to complete import because the data does not match the constraints in the Confluence schema. Cause: PSQLException: ERROR: duplicate key value violates unique constraint """"unq_lwr_username"""" Detail: Key (lower_username)=(<EMAIL>) already exists.  {code}    This is replicable when   * The format of username in the backup is email address. e.g. <EMAIL>  * The instance already has both of following two users which has the same email address  (<EMAIL>)  in  ** {{lower_user_name}} column and   ** {{lower_email_address}} column    This issue also occurs when the target server/datacenter instance has different users with the same email address.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a space export in Cloud instance (supposing it has a space creator {{<EMAIL>}} )   # Create a vanilla Confluence Server instance    # Add following users in Server instance  ** which has username  {{<EMAIL>}} and email address {{<EMAIL>}}   **  which has username {{foo}} and email address  {{<EMAIL>}}    # Import the Cloud export in step 1 into the Server instance     h3. Expected Results  Space is imported successfully.   Also space creator are expressed as unknown user following [Import a space from Confluence Cloud|https://confluence.<USER>doc/import-a-space-from-confluence-cloud-967876449.html]     h3. Actual Results    Space import fails generating """"unq_lwr_username"""" violation error on the screen.  The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:      h3. Workaround    Resolve the duplication in the Server instance beforehand.   In the above case, remove or rename the user which has username {{<EMAIL>}} """

summary: "The Plan your Upgrade page fails to load if Synchrony is misconfigured"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The """"Plan your Upgrade"""" page will fail if Synchrony's node isn't properly configured. Even though the Collab. Editing my be OFF, the page will still fail to load.    (!) This error has been happening for instances that have been migrated from the standalone version to DC. We have reproduced the issue with that environment.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create a Confluence instance 6.9.0  # Upgrade UPM and ATST  # Turn off collaborative editing  # Install Confluence DC license  # Restart Confluence and configure DC instance, don't configure anything on synchrony side  # Visit Plan your upgrade configuration page.  # Issue is visible """"Looks like we're having trouble retrieving some information. Please refresh the page""""  # Once Synchrony is configured correctly and Confluence is restarted the error is gone.      h3. Expected Results    The page will load when Synchrony is misconfigured but the Collab. Editing is turned off      h3. Actual Results    Although the Collab. Editing is turned off, the page checks Synchrony's configuration. As the Synchrony JVM wasn't setup after moving to DC, the below error will be logged:        h3. Workaround    There are two workarounds:    * Setup Synchrony's JVM(DC) when facing the issue.  * Upgrade to Confluence 6.12(or above) and let Confluence manage Synchrony's. """

summary: "Large MP4 video failed to be previewed"
description: """h3. Summary  Large MP4 videos (>100MB) failed to be previewed where else smaller MP4 videos can be previewed without any issue. Both the File Preview and Multimedia Macro method is not working.    h3. Expected  The Video should be able to be previewed normally:  * File Preview:   !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!   * Multimedia Macro:   !screenshot-5.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual  Unable to load the file:  * File Preview:   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   * Multimedia Macro:   !screenshot-4.png|thumbnail!     h3. File Type   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Note  The unsupported File format will show the following message:   !screenshot-6.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Download and view the video file."""

summary: "Changing BaseURL breaks the Clustering configuration page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When BaseURL of Confluence is changed, the *Clustering* page breaks    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Confluence v7.3.4  # Set it up and navigate to *General Configuration* > *Clustering*  # The page will be looking fine (/)  # Add context path in *server.xml*   # Update the Base URL to include the context path    h3. Expected Results    Clustering page should work fine.    h3. Actual Results    The stylesheet it broken and we see page like follows,   !css-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround      Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available    h3. Notes    The BaseURL isn't updated for this page and URL used to fetch stylesheet is the old one,      Here {{http://localhost:6734}} is the old BaseURL, it should be {{http://localhost:6734/c734}} where *c734* is the newly added context path"""

summary: "Opening the image attached as part of resolved Comments hides the image behind Resolved comments window."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Opening the image attached as part of resolved Comments hides the image behind Resolved Comments window.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page or go to existing page   # Add Resolved comments.   # Attach a image to the Resolved comments   # Click on (...)   # Go to Resolved Comments   # click on the image to open    h3. Expected Results    The image should open and Resolved comments window should go off.  h3. Actual Results    The Resolved comments window does not go off and image is hidden behind it.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Javascript error when creating Jira issue"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Javascript error when creating Jira issue from highlighted text    h3. Steps to Reproduce  h5. In Jira  # Create Jira with 400 project  # Create a custom date field and assign the field to all screens  # Assign date field to a single issue type  # Make custom date field as required    h5. In Confluence  # Create a page with text  # Highlight text to create a Jira issue    h3. Expected Results  Popup loads without any issue and user is able to create a Jira issue   !image-2020-04-08-14-12-35-472.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  Popup loads but *Getting Required Fields* loads indefinitely    !image-2020-04-08-14-18-16-525.png|thumbnail!     Error found from *Developer Tools* console      h3. Workaround  Create a separate custom date field for each project"""

summary: "Page Title overlaps with Page Path on Chrome"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Page Title overlaps with page path when clicking on spaces post opening a page in Confluence.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open a page in Confluence   # Click on """"spaces""""   # Page Title overlaps with page path as depicted in attached screenshot    h3. Expected Results    Page Title should not overlap with Page Path  h3. Actual Results    Page Title overlaps with page path when clicking on spaces post opening a page.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Indentation of numbered list in mail notification is broken"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Indentation of numbered list is not presented properly in mail notifications    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Have a Confluence page that has mal server setup  # Create a page, and insert the following content, and mention a Confluence user   !CONFSERVER-59693-1.png|thumbnail!   # Save the page  # Open the mail notification in Outlook    h3. Expected Results  The notification should look exactly like what is being shown in the page    h3. Actual Results  The indentation is not shown properly   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  * Use other mail client other than Outlook    h3. Note  This issue is not shown in Gmail browser client   !CONFSERVER-59693-2-inGmail.png|thumbnail!     """

summary: "Inspect permissions shows incorrect results for mixed-case groups or users"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Any users or groups with uppercase characters in the username/groupname are not shown as having permissions in the inspect permissions feature. Note that such usernames and group names can only be created using external directories.    The error happens on initially loading the data at start-up, so any groups or users added during testing without a restart are ok until the cache reinitialisation.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    When we add a group (Group1) from an external directory and give it permission to view a space, it works fine. It shows up in the inspect permissions table with an upper case G, and respects permissions updates.   # When we do something that triggers a cache reinit (eg, re-ordering user directories), Group1 now shows up in the table as group1 with a lower case g and no longer respects permission updates.    # Try to give group1 permission to view a space and it doesn't show up in the table.    h3. Expected Results    Permissions can be updated as per usual.  h3. Actual Results    !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    Permission update is not reflected  h3. Workaround    None"""

summary: "After linking Confluence with Jira, myWorkClientChecker TimeoutExceptions are thrown during Confluence startup"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  After linking Confluence with Jira, myWorkClientChecker TimeoutExceptions are thrown during Confluence startup    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up application link with Jira.   # Authorize access for the application.   # Restart Confluence    h3. Expected Results  Confluence starts up without any errors    h3. Actual Results  Confluence starts up and throws timeout errors related to Workbox.    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:      h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Zooming out on a page causes panel macros with embedded links or attachments t to render inconsistent behavior."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Zooming out on a page containing panel macros with embedded links or images render with a scroll bar but others don't . This is only reproducible on Chrome.   h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Open a Chrome browser.   # Create a page.  # Add multiple panel macros.  # Insert a link or image in all the macros and update the page.   # Zoom out.     h3. Expected Results  All panels should exhibit the same behavior. Either all display with a scroll bar or not.   h3. Actual Results  Some panels render with scroll bar and others do not.    !Screen Shot 2020-04-09 at 3.39.49 PM.png|thumbnail!   h3. Workaround  Use FireFox or IE.     Tested on Link and Windows   Chrome 80.0.3987.149"""

summary: "Code block with Python syntax highlighting causes the browser page to freeze"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Inserted certain strings into code block with Python syntax highlighting causes the browser page to freeze.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create new page   # Insert Code Block   # Select """"Python"""" for syntax highlighting   # Insert below sample strings     # Save page    h3. Expected Results    Page can see same response time as other syntax highlighting types.  h3. Actual Results    Take long time to open pages.  h3. Workaround    Use other syntax highlighting language"""

summary: "Back and forward navigation doesn't work in combo filters on search UI"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Back forward Key navigation doesn;t work   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In confluence open search by clicking the input on top right corner with the label Search   # try filtering any combo like field - like spaces - by typing some text   # try navigating back and forward using back and forward keys    h3. Expected Results    Cursor should navigate around typed text  h3. Actual Results    Cursor stays on same spot  h3. Workaround    use delete key"""

summary: "Missing Edit button for Confluence admin if the admin does not have the view and edit space permission."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  By default, Confluence admin should have the permission to edit and view all pages in Confluence regardless if they have the space permission or not. However, If Confluence admin does not have the view/edit space permission, the edit button is missing for the Confluence admin.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a space and page  # Configure the space to make only {{confluence-users}} group have the edit and view space permission   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!   # Create a new admin user account and add the account to {{confluence-administrator}} group only   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    !screenshot-4.png|thumbnail!   # Login as the newly created admin account  # Access any page in the space created in step 1    h3. Expected Results  The admin account should see the edit button and edit the page    h3. Actual Results  The edit button is missing for the admin   !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  The workaround is to grant the edit permission for the Confluence admin group."""

summary: "Inserting a JIRA issue macro in a Chart Macro causes value not to be shown on the chart"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Inserting a JIRA issue macro inside a chart on a Confluence page causes the value to not appear on the chart when the page is saved. However, if the page is previewed, the values from the JIRA issue macro is showing correctly on the chart.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Environment: JIRA v8.1.3 and Confluence v6.13.4   # Insert a Chart Macro on a page   # Insert a table inside the Chart Macro with two columns and few rows populate it with random values and leave one column empty   # Create a JIRA issue macro and insert it in the empty column   # Save the page    h3. Expected Results    Values from the JIRA issue macro should be shown on the chart.  h3. Actual Results    Values do not appear on the chart and only appearing when previewed.   [^JiraIssueTest.mp4]  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "When I click on link of issue in my e-mail, it should open in app and not in browser"
description: """It would be great if I click on link of issue in my e-mail, it would open in app and not in browser, since now I read an email then I have to remember the page title and search it in app     """

summary: "Archived pages are not accessible when you import a space exported from Cloud"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Confluence Cloud is introducing the ability to archive pages. This is currently in beta.  If you import a space, exported from cloud, that contains archived pages, the pages are not visible, because there is no UI to display the archive.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In Confluence Cloud, create a space, some pages, then archive some of these pages (... > archive)   # Export the space to XML  # Import the space into Confluence Server or Confluence Data Center    h3. Expected Results    The pages are available (in the page tree, at the root of the space, in the trash,... somewhere they can be accessed and restored)    h3. Actual Results    The pages are present in the XML export, but are not accessible through the Confluence UI.     h3. Workaround    As a workaround, restore any pages you want to keep from the Archive before you export the space. """

summary: "MSI and EXE versions of Companion can be installed and run side by side"
description: """If you install both the MSI and EXE versions of Companion, you will have 2 versions installed and both can run simultaneously. You will also see both appear in Windows' add/remove programs screen.  h3. Expected behaviour    A potential solution would be to prevent installation of one version when the other is detected to already be installed.  h3. Workaround    Manually uninstall one of them."""

summary: "TDM merger takes long time to complete with multiple inline tasks"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When Collaborative editing is disabled, TDM Merger takes long time to complete when user goes to edit Confluence page that is include many inline tasks.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Disable Collaborative editing   # User A goes to edit a page that include many inline tasks (Version 1)   # Confluence auto-saves a draft based on Version 1, and user does not save the page   # User B edits the same page and saves, promoting the page to Version 2   # User A views his drafts (or edits the page again)   # Confluence tries to merge the three versions (Version 1, Version 1 draft, and Version 2), and results in Confluence slow performance    h3. Expected Results    Merge complete without any performance issues  h3. Actual Results    Single task is taking up-to 2 minutes to complete:    Thread dump shows a lot of threads consumed by TDM Merger:    h3. Workaround    Enable collaborative editing"""

summary: "RuntimeException: Cannot read resource assets/icons/confluence-icon-video.svg"
description: """h3. Summary    Uploading multimedia file results in 'java.lang.RuntimeException: Cannot read resource assets/icons/confluence-icon-video.svg stacks in atlassian-confluence.log.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new or edit existing page   # Upload mp4 file    h3. Expected Results    No stacks in logs  h3. Actual Results    File is uploaded, however the below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:     """

summary: "Protection Mechanism Failure in file downloading in Companion - CVE-2020-4020"
description: """The file downloading functionality in the Atlassian Companion App before version 1.0.0 allows remote attackers, who control a Confluence Server instance that the Companion App is connected to, execute arbitrary .exe files via a Protection Mechanism Failure.    h5. Acknowledgements  Credit for finding this vulnerability goes to <USER>Hatting (UFST)."""

summary: "Untrusted Search Path in Content - Edit Files / Companion - CVE-2020-4019"
description: """The file editing functionality in the Atlassian Companion App before version 1.0.0 allows local attackers to have the app run a different executable in place of the app's cmd.exe via a untrusted search path vulnerability.    h5. Acknowledgements  Credit for finding this vulnerability goes to <USER>Hatting (UFST).    """

summary: "Unable to upload files via WebDav in Windows 10"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  * When following the guide here to configure WebDav for Windows 10 using File Explorer or Cyberduck, we're unable to upload any files.  ** [Configuring a WebDAV client for Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-a-webdav-client-for-confluence-148044.html]  * Issues does not persist in Mac using Finder.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Follow the guide below to set up Webdav in Windows 10.  ** [Configuring a WebDAV client for Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-a-webdav-client-for-confluence-148044.html]  # After adding the WebDav directory, try to add a file into any of the sub-directories.     h3. Expected Results  # We're able to paste files in without any issues.    h3. summary: "Inline comments not saved due to link macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a link macro has been defined and the display and target names are different(or empty) it is not possible to create inline comments on the words that have been used in the link display name.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page   # Add random content and for this example make sure to add the words (Background, concepts)   # Add a link macro(insert link) in the following ways:   ** use the """"Advanced"""" option and add a link to a non-existing page. Set the link name to """"Background concepts"""".   ** link to an existing piece of content and alter the link name by adding spaces or additional words   # Publish the page   # Select the word """"background"""" in the content that was created in step 2 and try to add an inline comment    h3. Expected Results    The inline comment should be saved  h3. Actual Results    The inline comment is not saved and the following message is displayed:  {quote}We can't add your inline comment; the section you highlighted may contain a macro or user mention. Try highlighting plain text.  {quote}  The following error is shown in the browser developer tools:      {panel:title=Technical observations}  I have observed that a working link looks as follows:    whereas a link causing this issue looks like the following two examples:    The _<ac:plain-text-link-body>_ attribute is only added when the link name and actual link reference is not the same.  {panel}    h3. Workaround   * Use the unlink option on the hyperlink effectively removing the link   * Ensure the link name and page content name is the same(again you might have to edit and save the affected page for the attribute to be removed)   * Select more than just the individual words when creating an inline comment"""

summary: "Team Calendars: reminder mails are not set to all users for events created in a personal space"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When events are created in a calendar on personal spaces, the reminder mails are not sent to the other users added to the event, except the one that created the event.  The same does not occur for events created on public spaces.    h3. Environment  * Any Confluence version after 6.13  * Team Calendars installed, any version since 6.0.37    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Confluence instance with Team Calendars installed and at least 2 users (for this example *admin* and *u1u1*);   # Create personal space for each user and add a calendar to each one;   # Log-in as *admin* and create an event for the near future on his personal space's calendar   ## Add both users (*admin* and *u1u1*) to the event and set a reminder.   !adminCal1.png|thumbnail!    # Wait for the time the reminder should be sent;   # Log-in as *u1u1* and create a different event also for the near future and in his own calendar, similar to the one from Step #3;   !u1u1Cal1.png|thumbnail!    # Wait for the time the reminder for this new event should be sent.    h3. Expected Results    Both users receive a reminder mail at the time of both steps #4 and #6    h3. Actual Results    * At the time of step #4 only user *admin* receives a reminder mail;  * At the time of step #6 only user *u1u1* receives a reminder mail;    Note: both users receive a notification mail when added to the event created by the other.      h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

These are old tables that have fallen out of use and were removed from Confluence starting with version 6.10.  h3. Workaround    None"""

summary: "Clicking Ellipsis To Expand Page Hierarchy Causes Restriction Lock Icon To Disappear"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    While viewing a page that is a child of another page, if you click the ellipsis to show the full page tree hierarchy the red lock icon that you would use to modify page restrictions disappears.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Space called 'Global Tools Home'   # Create a Page called 'GTF Internal'   # Create a Child Page called 'Projects - GTF Internal'   # Create a Child Page under that called 'Test'   # Add a viewing and editing restriction on this page   # Click the ellipsis to expand the page tree   # Notice the red lock disappears    h3. Expected Results    In this case the expected behavior would be that the red lock continues to appear even after you click the ellipses next to dashboard on top to expand the page hierarchy.  h3. Actual Results    The red lock is not visible.    h3. Workaround   # *[Recommended approach]* Click the *Ellipsis* button (in the upper-right corner) and select *Restrictions* to view the same Restrictions window, essentially achieving the same outcome.   # *Refresh* the affected Page so it gets back to the initial state, as the ellipsis symbol in the breadcrumbs section and the padlock icon will get back.   # Open the *Developer Tools* and use the *Inspect* function to select the padlock area and remove the {{display: none}} property; or find the """"page-metadata-banner"""" element and remove the {{display: none}} property.   *PS:* The third option is not a viable workaround for most users, as the element will be reset every time the page gets reloaded."""

summary: "High CPU Usage (100% spikes) not released when inserting a docx file with charts to a page"
description: """h3. Problem  When importing a docx file with charts attached to a Confluence server, it will cause the CPU usage to spike to 100%. In order to get the threads released, we need to shutdown/kill the Confluence Java process.    h3. Environment  I have been able to reproduce in both 7.4.0 and 6.15.7.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Insert the docx file attached to a Confluence page in a local v7.4.0 instance and using the insert menu (please have a look at the video mp4 attached).    h3. Expected Results  The CPU usage should go down once the insert is completed.     h3. Actual Results  The CPU usage is not released and Confluence needs to be restarted.    Sample Stack Trace from a runaway RUNNABLE thread that has hit this condition:       h3. Workaround  * Few workarounds are working in 7.4.0 such as saving the file as either a DOC or as a PDF file instead of DOCX. Then the import will not cause CPU spikes.  Instead of importing the document from the insert menu. You can use the """"..."""" menu from the page to attach the .docx file there and it will not cause CPU spikes.  * Remove the charts from the file prior to upload.     h3. Notes    I have attached the recording where I show the workarounds  - import the DOCX document from the """"..."""" menu on the page, browsing the Attachments and attaching the document there (not inserting it inside the page)  - how the PDF and DOC version insertion is working fine whereas it is causing high CPU usages for the DOCX file."""
