description: """h3. Issue Summary    When attempting to reuse a variable from the template menu, a new variable is created instead.    Per [Create a Template > Template Variables|https://confluence.<USER>doc/create-a-template-296093779.html#CreateaTemplate-Templatevariables]:  {quote}You can add the same variable more than once in the same template, which is useful if you need the same information in more than one place on the page.  {quote}  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new template.   # Instead of typing $, use the template menu to create a new variable:  !image-2020-05-13-16-16-28-947.png!   # Now, try to select the same variable from that menu in order to add a 2nd copy to the page.    h3. Expected Results    The variable is reused on the page.  h3. Actual Results    An additional $ is appended to the variable, creating a brand-new variable.    !6.15.8.png|width=348,height=221!  h3. Workaround    From the documentation on Templates:  {quote}Hint: Type $ and the variable name, then press Enter to add a new variable or to select an existing variable from a list of suggestions. The suggestions dialog shows variables already defined in this template.  {quote}  Confirmed reusing a variable in this way works as expected."""

summary: "Display issue in Edit Preview mode if using custom header and footer"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Display issue in Edit Preview mode if using custom header and footer    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Add a header and footer to space   * Create a page   * Click on Edit    * Click on preview   * The page content is displayed 3 split columns corresponding to :  ** header  ** body  ** footer      h3. Expected Results  It should not split in 3 columns as header, body and footer    h3. Actual Results  * The page content is displayed 3 split columns corresponding to :  ** header  ** body  ** footer    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Export page to word will rename the page title."
description: """h3. Description  When user export a page with title """"Test Page"""" to a word file, the word file will be created with file name """"Test+Page.doc"""". If the user use this word document to import it to another Confluence, Confluence will take the file name """"Test+Page"""" as the page title instead of """"Test Page"""".    h3. Steps to reproduce  # Create a page with title """"Test Page""""  # Export the page to word  # A word file with file name """"Test+page"""" is generated  # Go to another space  # Select Import word document and select the word file we exported from step 3.  # Confluence will take the file name """"Test+page"""" as the page title.   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Result   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected result  The page is imported with the original title    h3.Workaround  The workaround will be manually change the page title before it is imported."""

summary: "Task Report listed in Table of Content"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  An empty *Task Report* macro is listed in a *Table of Content* macro    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page with a *Task Report* macro  # Configure the *Task Report* macro to not display any task  # Create a few *heading* on the page  # Add a *Table of Content* macro to the page  # Save the page    h3. Expected Results  *Task Report* is not displayed under the *Table of Content* because there isn't any heading   !image-2020-05-15-17-00-22-445.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  *Task Report* is displayed under the *Table of Content*   !image-2020-05-15-17-01-24-689.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  n/a    h5. Notes  # The Task Report isn't listed if the tasks are displayed   !image-2020-05-15-17-03-32-868.png|thumbnail!    # Preview displays a different behavior   !image-2020-05-15-17-04-26-944.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Excerpt date not displayed in Child Display"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Date in an *Excerpt* macro not displayed in Child Display *macro*  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page   # Add an *Excerpt* macro   # In the macro add some text and date in the first line   !image-2020-05-15-19-10-52-357.png!   # Create another page and add the *Child Display*macro to the page   ** Choose Excerpt Display = Simple   # Save the page    h3. Expected Results    The page with the *Excerpt* macro will display the date  h3. Actual Results    The page with the *Excerpt* macro doesn't display the date   !image-2020-05-15-19-12-57-987.png!    Comparison with an *Excerpt Include* macro   !image-2020-05-15-19-13-32-523.png!  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Attachment Size Limit Doesn't Apply to the Edit in Office feature"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The 'Edit in Office' feature doesn't restrict reupload of attachment that is bigger than the configured attachment upload size limit.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Set the attachment upload limit to 5MB  # Upload an attachment that is 4MB for example.  # Use 'Edit in Office' to edit the attachment  # Append text or images to the attachment (Deliberately increase the attachment size to more than 5MB)  # Save and reupload to Confluence    h3. Expected Results  Confluence will prevent the reupload because it exceeds the file size limit.    h3. Actual Results  Confluence bypasses the file size limit check and reuploads the file.    h3. Workaround  If we were to replicate the same behavior using Atlassian Companion app, the file size limit check *does* apply here:   !Screenshot 2020-05-18 at 12.45.16 PM.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Building Confluence 7.5.0 from source fails on ""File .browserslistrc not found"""
description: """When building Confluence 7.5.0 from source code, you get an error similar to this:    This error is caused by a missing browserslist configuration file in the distribution package.       To work around the issue, create the {{.browserslistrc}} file in source code root folder manually with the following content:  """

summary: "Inline comment author becomes Anonymous after import SPACE"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Inline comment author becomes *Anonymous* after import SPACE.  The customer is trying to migrate a SPACE which contains lots of inline comments, and all the authors were changed to *Anonymous* after import SPACE to the new server.  It has huge impacts on the business as they cannot find who resolved those inline comments.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an inline comment and <USER>it as *Resolved*.   # *Export* SPACE and remove and *import* back the same SPACE.   # Check the inline comment and confirm it the author is changed to *Anonymous*.    h3. Expected Results    The inline comment should show the author like the following:   * *Resolved by admin yesterday*    h3. Actual Results    But the author becomes *Anonymous* like the following:   * *Resolved by Anonymous yesterday*    !resolved.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Twitter feed Widget does not respect ""height"" parameter"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Twitter feed Widget does not respect """"height"""" parameter  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Insert a Twitter feed widget (e.g. a timeline [https://twitter.com/TwitterDev)]   # Set the height parameter on the macro    h3. Expected Results    Height of macro is restricted to user setting.  h3. Actual Results    Macro shows entire feed.   h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Content by Label Macro - simple display excerpt option  returns  all excerpt rows instead of only one row"
description: """  h3. Issue Summary  Content by Label Macro - simple display excerpt option  returns  all excerpt rows instead of only one row    According to official docs  [Content by Label Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/content-by-label-macro-145566.html] Display excerpts -> simple option   displays the first line of text contained in an Excerpt macro any of the returned pages. If there is not an Excerpt macro on the page, nothing will be shown. However SImple display excerpts displays all the rows from excerpt macros       h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create page A.    # Add Excerpt macro to the page.    # Add multiple lines of text into macro body   # Add label """"test"""" to the page   # Create page B.     # Add """"Content by label"""" macro to page B. Select label added to page A   # Expand options menu    # Navigate to Excerpt Display option and select """"simple""""  parameter from the picklist.    #  Click on preview (or save macro and update the page)    h3. Expected Results  First line of the excerpt macro from page A is displayed    h3. Actual Results  All lines added to excerpt macro on page A are displayed     h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: " Navigating to site home page prompts a not permitted notification.  "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a registered user navigates to the site home page, the user is notified with a not permitted warning.    However,   * The user can view the home space page.   * Anonymous user can view the Space.   * Unlicensed users can view the Space.   * Issue is also seen when using the [Smart Terms for Confluence app|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1219677/smart-terms-for-confluence-glossary?hosting=server&tab=overview]    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure the site home page to the Space's home page. [Configuring the Site Home Page|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-the-site-home-page-138140.html].   # Create a new group without *can use* global permissions.   # Enable anonymous users.   # Create user in the group.   # Configure group to have view permissions in space.   # Configure anonymous users to view space.   # Enable unlicensed users to space via Jira <USER>   # Login with the new user.   # Navigate to space and pages within space. (/)   # Navigate to site home page. (x)    h3. Expected Results    Open site home page without issue.  h3. Actual Results  Prompted Not permitted   !notperm.png|thumbnail!   h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Displaying a blog post with the Blog Posts macro shows an incorrect post date"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Blog Post Macro displays a publish date many hours in the past when compared with the actual creation date of blog posts.    Changing the server timezone does not seem to fix the behavior.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a blog post   # Insert a blog post macro   # Set 'content type to display' to titles   # Save Blog post    h3. Expected Results    Blog post macro should display the right time  h3. Actual Results    Blog Post Macro says blog was published hours ago though created or updated a minute ago.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira Issue description contains no formatting when created using Create Multiple Issues From Table in the Confluence Editor"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    While creating multiple Jira issues within Confluence editor from a table, the auto populated description does not retain any spacing.  When viewing the description in the Jira issue, all spacing and much of the formatting has been removed.  h3. Steps to Reproduce     • Create a simple table with at least three rows.     !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!      • Use the first row as the table headers.  The first one being Summary and second one Description.   • Add """"Test"""" as the summary in both empty cells in the """"Summary"""" column.   • Write two or more paragraphs with a space between or add a simple bulleted list to the empty cells in the column named Description.   • Highlight the entire table and click the """"Create Jira Issue"""" icon in the pop up   • Click """"Create Multiple Issues From Table""""     !screenshot-5.png|thumbnail!      • Select the correct headers for Summary and Description.   • Create the Jira Issues.   • Open the Jira Issues in Jira.  h3. Expected Results    The basic spacing and formatting is retained in the description in the Jira issue.  h3. Actual Results     !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!  !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!  !screenshot-4.png|thumbnail!     The Jira issue does not retain any spacing or most formatting in the Description.  This results in Descriptions which are formatted with spacing, bullets, or bold/italic/underlines being jumbled up and at times unreadable.       h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Whitelist page hangs with Wildcard Expression on Google Chrome"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When pasting or typing a long URL with a wildcard on the Whitelist page, Google Chrome turns unresponsive for a few minutes.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Access the *Whitelist* page(with Chrome).  # Paste the following URL:      h3. Expected Results  The """"type"""" option will change automatically and fastly to """"Wildcard Expression"""".    h3. Actual Results  The page will hang for a few minutes until it can be saved.    h3. Workaround  Use another browser like Safari"""

summary: "Question for Confluence draft"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Question for Confluence draft missing  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence 7.2.0 and Question for Confluence 2.7.20   # Click on *Questions* from the menu bar   # Click on *Ask a question*   # Draft a question   !image-2020-05-27-22-24-38-892.png|thumbnail!   # Do not safe. Click on *Questions* from the menu bar   # Click on *Ask a question*   !image-2020-05-27-22-26-38-099.png|thumbnail!   # Go to user's profile   # Click on *Draft*   !image-2020-05-27-22-28-52-558.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results    Draft to appear under *Draft* in user profile  h3. Actual Results    Draft doesn't appear under *Draft* in user profile  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available  h5. Note    If a user has created multiple draft questions, only the most recent draft question will appear. Also, admins are unable to purge all draft questions."""

summary: "Related Labels macro fetching content from deleted page"
description: """h3. Summary    The Related Labels macro will also bring labels that are no longer active.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create two pages.  # On one page add two labels  #* label1 and label2  # On the second page, add only the """"label2"""" and use the Related Label macro.  # Delete the first page that had the two macros.    h3. Expected Results  The Related Macro will not show any data    h3. Actual Results  The macro will show """"label1"""" and """"label2""""     h3. Notes  Apparently, this behavior is being inherited from CONFSERVER-6520 and  CONFSERVER-43549    h3.Workaround  Purge the trash to completely delete the page along with the label."""

summary: "Source Editor add blank space after bold and italic."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When the page contains text that is bold and italic, a blank space will be added after the bold and italic text.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install source editor  # Create a Confluence page  # Add following sentence in the page.  {quote}  Testing source editor bold and italic.  {quote}  # Bold and italic the last word. (Do not bold and italic the . )  # Save the page  # Edit the page  # Click the <> button  # Click Apply and save the page  # A blank space is added between the last text and the dot.  (i) For details, please refer to the following video.   [^Recording #11.mp4]     h3. Expected Results  No additional space is added after bold and italic text.    h3. Actual Results  additional blank space is added after bold and italic text.   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Table headers do not stick to the top when scrolling in Firefox"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  # For long tables in Confluence, as you scroll down the page, the header would stick to the top so that it appears even when we're viewing lower entries in the table.  # In Firefox however, this functionality seems to break intermittently.    h3. Expected Results  # Header appears even after scrolling down.  **  !Sticky.PNG|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  # In Firefox, header does not appear from time to time.  # This behavior is not seen in Google Chrome  **  !Not-sticky.PNG|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create table with header and lots of rows.  # Scroll down.   ** As issue is intermittent, if issue not reproducible, refresh the page and scroll again.  h3. Workaround  * Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available  """

summary: "Custom Span Tags Cause all Heading on Page to Change"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When creating a page and formatting specific sections that have a <span> tag seems to cause unwanted effects when trying to switch a single heading, which causes the whole page to also get changed to the same formatting. This can be a major pain point if users copy over information that introduces these span tags into their connect.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Edit a page with several different headings already on there (H1, H3, Pre-formatted).  # Introduce a <span> tag to modify anything within the line.  # Simply put the cursor in middle on line on one of the headings  # Change the heading to another heading, such as H3 to Pre-formatted    h3. Expected Results    Only a single line should change.    h3. Actual Results    The whole page is changing to the same formatting.    h3. Workaround    Currently, the workaround is to remove the <span> tags."""

summary: "Confluence Server displays same storage format different than Confluence Cloud"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  # Content imported from Confluence Cloud to Confluence Server display different.  # Issue appears to be caused by Confluence Cloud and Server displaying the same storage format differently.  **     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page in Confluence Cloud, and add some images and content with link breaks.  # Export the Site or Space out, and import into Confluence Server.  # Check on the content of the page in Confluence Server.    h3. Expected Results  * Content appears as it does in Confluence Server as it does in Confluence Cloud  *  !cloud.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  * Line breaks that appear in Confluence Cloud, separating the content, do not appear in Confluence Server.  *  !server1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  * There is no workaround other than to manually edit the pages and fix the formatting.  """

summary: "Excerpt-include macro does not display an Edit button for Office Excel macro generated content "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When an _Excerpt-include_ macro displays content rendered from an _Office Excel_ macro, the content does not include an Edit button.  h3. Environment    Confluence 7.5.0  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page that includes an _Excerpt_ macro.   ** Within this macro, insert an _Office Excel_ macro that references an Excel file that's attached to the page.   # Next, create another page with an _Excerpt-include_ macro.   ** Configure this macro instance to reference the _Excerpt_ macro created in the previous step.    h3. Expected Results    The Excel content rendered on the page that has the _Excerpt-include_ macro *should display* an *Edit* button located directly above the included Excel spreadsheet.  h3. Actual Results    The Excel content rendered on the page that has the _Excerpt-include_ macro *does not display* an *Edit* button located directly above the included Excel spreadsheet.  h3. Notes    The page that includes the _Excerpt_ macro does display the *Edit* button regardless of Confluence version.  h3. Workaround    The only known workaround for this issue would be to edit the Excel worksheet within the original source page that contains it."""

summary: "Synchrony is unable to start after a non-graceful Confluence shutdown"
description: """h3. Problem  After a non-graceful Confluence shutdown, synchrony.pid isn't removed from confluence_install/temp/ folder. The next time Confluence starts, Synchrony is unable to start.    h3. Environment  Windows/Linux  Confluence    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Reboot the server or kill Confluence process in a non-graceful way  # Start Confluence    h3. Expected Results  Confluence and Synchrony process starts    h3. Actual Results  Only Confluence starts    h3. Workaround  # Shutdown Confluence  # Manually delete *confluence_install/temp/synchrony.pid*  # Start Confluence    h3. Notes  """

summary: "Notify watcher option is checked by default when user cookies is deleted or expired"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If a user uncheck the notify watcher option when editing a page, the option will get checked back if the user cookies is delete or expired in the browser or switched to another browser where new cookies is generated.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  *Case 1*  # Access Confluence via Google Chrome  # Access a Confluence page  # Edit the page  # Uncheck the """"Notify Watcher"""" option  # Save the page  # Clear the Chrome Browser cache and cookies  # Close Chrome  # Open Chrome and access to the same page  # Edit the page  # You will notice that the """"Notify Watcher"""" option is checked  (i) This behavior affect all Chrome, FireFox, and IE 11 Browser    *Case 2*  # Access Confluence via Google Chrome  # Access a Confluence page  # Edit the page  # Uncheck the """"Notify Watcher"""" option  # Save the page  # Access Confluence via FireFox or IE 11 browser  # Access the same page  # Edit the page  # You will notice that the """"Notify Watcher"""" option is checked.  (i) For details, please review the following video   [^Recording #12.mp4]       h3. Expected Results  The Notify Watcher option will remain uncheck    h3. Actual Results  The Notify Watcher option is reset to check when cookies is deleted, expired, or recreated in other browser    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Velocity Template Injection in Custom user macros - Macros Platform - CVE-2020-4027"
description: """Atlassian Confluence Server and Data Center before version 7.5.1 allowed remote attackers with system administration permissions to bypass velocity template injection mitigations via an injection vulnerability in custom user macros.    This issue was discovered and reported by GHSL team member [@pwntester (<USER>Munoz)|https://github.com/github/securitylab_vulnerabilities/blob/master/vendor_reports/https/github.com/pwntester].    *Affected versions:*   * version < 7.5.1    *Fixed versions:*   * 7.5.1"""

summary: "Checking inline tasks will cause unpublished tasks to be set incorrectly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If inline tasks are being worked on when editing a confluence page, and these published pages have tasks that are being checked or unchecked by others, the wrong task states will be published when the draft is published.    Example video:  [^tasks-checked-incorrectly.mov]     This may be related to another issue observed in [CONFSERVER-59676|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-59676] where drafts do not show the Unpublished Changes warning message when tasks are modified.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  1. Create a page with two or three task elements and publish the page without checking any tasks  2.  Edit the page again and create a new task between the existing ones (but don't publish and stay in edit mode)  3.  Open the same page in a second tab in view mode and check all the tasks  4.  In the first tab, you will now see that the wrong tasks are checked  5.  The then published page will have incorrect task states    h3. Expected Results    When task states are changed by others in published pages the state will be updated or notified in their drafts or edited pages.    h3. Actual Results    Changing task states in published pages will incorrectly overwrite the state of any new tasks that have been added or changed in edited pages.    h3. Workaround    There is currently no workaround        """

summary: "Text Size is Inconsistent When Printing Page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a user goes to print a Confluence page from their browser, they will randomly get a page with the text rendered in a smaller size. This appears usually when adding comments to a page and then going to print the page.    Specifically, this issue happens when using the browser's own print option and not Confluence's own export options. There are several valid reasons why a user will want to use this method instead of exporting from within Confluence, as currently there is not a method to include comments on a single page export (this option is only shown for whole Space exports) and Confluence will export the page different than what is shown unless the PDF export is customized with CSS/HTML.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create a page with a few paragraphs worth of content and save.  # Add a comment to the page and then immediately press CTRL + P to print the page from the browser.  # Notice that the text is smaller than what is shown on the page.  # Refresh the page and print it again. You will see that the text is back to its normal size.    h3. Expected Results    A page with the correct text size shown on the export.    !normal.png!    h3. Actual Results    The text is rendered in a smaller size.    !small.png!    h3. Workaround    Luckily refreshing the page seems to resolve this issue, however, this can be an extra step that can be tedious in workflows that need to export single pages with comments."""

summary: "The ""Upload"" button get pushed out when page title is too long"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Tested with Atlassian Companion Version 1.1.0 for Mac. If the page title is too long (noticeable when it's 60 characters long), the upload button will get pushed out of the window:   !Screenshot 2020-06-04 at 3.34.03 PM.png|thumbnail!      !Screenshot 2020-06-04 at 3.47.15 PM.png|thumbnail!       h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page with title """"longlonglonglonglonglonglonglonglonglnglonglonglonglongtitle""""  # Add an office file in the page above  # Edit the attachment using Companion App    h3. Expected Results  The """"Upload"""" button will stay intact.    h3. Actual Results  The """"Upload"""" button is hidden out from Companion App pop up window.    h3. Workaround  Try keeping the page title under 60 characters long."""

summary: "PDFs generated from ""https://miro.com/signup/"" doesn't displays properly using pdf macro"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The pdf files generated from https://miro.com/signup/ is not displayed properly in Confluence using pdf macro  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page in Confluence   # Attach the pdf file """"Test-PDF-Miro-Confluence.pdf"""" which has been generated from https://miro.com/signup/  # Insert pdf macro in the page and view the pdf using pdf macro  # Issue is reproducible in google chrome / fire fox / IE / Safari    h3. Expected Results  * The complete pdf file is not shown and only some part of the pdf gets displayed.     h3. Actual Results  * The complete pdf file should get displayed while viewing the pdf using pdf macro    h3. Workaround  * Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Reorder in page tree not working as described. "
description: """h3. Problem    While users are trying to reorder pages using the page tree (drag and drop in the tree), they are not allowed to do it. There is a gray sign which indicates that users can't put the page to the intended place.     Users have Edit permission to the page to be reordered and also view access to its parent page as mentioned in the document.  [Move and Reorder Pages.|https://confluence.<USER>doc/move-and-reorder-pages-146407727.html]  h3. Environment  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page ex: parent page   # Create three child page ex: child 1, child 2, child 3.   !image-2020-06-10-10-49-44-828.png|width=175,height=156!   # According to documentation [Move and Reorder Pages,|https://confluence.<USER>doc/move-and-reorder-pages-146407727.html] Assign view restriction on the parent page to the users:   !image-2020-06-10-10-53-35-918.png|width=707,height=194!   # Assign edit restrictions on-page being reordered (assign edit restriction on the child pages for user so for example user named 'abcd' can edit it)   !image-2020-06-10-10-55-01-061.png|width=707,height=194!   # log in with the user 'abcd' and try to reorder the child page from space tools.   ** Space tools >> Reorder Pages >> Pages greyed out with the above permission define.    ** It does not work.    !image-2020-06-10-10-50-46-497.png|width=408,height=255!   (i) When the user tries an alternative way with Moving the page (Move -> select new parent -> check Reorder -> put the page to the right place) it works correctly.  h3. Expected Results    _User should able to reorder pages from Space tools > Reorder pages with the permission defined in [Move and Reorder Pages|https://confluence.<USER>doc/move-and-reorder-pages-146407727.html]._    _It is working with ellipses button (...) > Move (as per permission defined in the KB__)_   h3. Actual Results    With the Space tools > Reorder pages user can't reorder pages.  h3. Workaround     Try moving page alternative way:    Head over to the page to be move > Ellipse button (...) > Move > Select new parent -> check reorder -> Move.    It works correctly with the edit permission for the child, view for the parent"""

summary: "Deleting text inside noformat macros is not working properly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When delete text from the start inside the noformat content, noformat macro doesn't work properly in FireFox, Edge.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open Confluence with FireFox   # Create a page   # Put noformat macro   # Add a few text   # Use the delete key from the start of any text of content   ** Lose formatting   # Add wiki markup inside the noformat macro      *Affected browser*   - Windows 10   -- FireFox(76.0.1、77.0.1)   -- Edge(44.17763.831.0)   - macOS Catalina 10.15.5) / macOS Mojave(10.14.6)   -- FireFox(77.0.1)   -- Edge(83.0.478.45)    h3. Expected Results    Noformat macro not lose formatting and insert wiki markup as a simple text  h3. Actual Results    Noformat macro lose formatting in editor and wiki markup text is not display as a simple text  h3. Workaround    After published page, text display as a simple text but there are no workaround for edit screen."""

summary: "Clicking on a table in edit mode causes the page to scroll to the top in Firefox"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When editing a page in *Firefox* and clicking on a table that got ordered list, the page scrolls all the way to the top, but clicking again doesn't cause the same behaviour, seems to be only the first click after editing the page. a storage format of an example page is enclosed.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page with a table   # Insert ordered list (3 levels) with stylised text    # Publish the page   # Edit the page, scroll down to the table and click anywhere on the table    h3. Expected Results    The page shouldn't scroll to the top when clicking on the table  h3. Actual Results    The page scrolls to the top on first mouse click  h3. Workaround    This behaviour seems to be affecting Firefox browser only, use another browser as a workaround."""

summary: "Notification emails sometimes contain broken images when the images are attached to the blog/page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a Confluence user receives a notification email about a blog post/page, the images attached and contained in the notification email sometimes display as broken icons.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Integrate Confluence with a Mail Server  # Create a test user and ensure that you are watching a space. Additionally, edit the user profile settings to ensure that your mail subscriptions/email settings will ensure that you will be notified for updates.  # Log in as an admin or another test user and create a blog post OR page with no restrictions in the space that your other test user watches so that they will receive an email notification.    h3. Expected Results  You will be able to see the content and images of the notification email    h3. Actual Results  You are able to see the content (text) of the notification email but the images are broken    !blog.png|thumbnail!   !page.png|thumbnail!       h3. Workaround  Do not attach the image to the blog post and instead embed the image as an absolute URL to the image asset.     h3. Additional Testing Notes  * This issue is always reproducible with Gmail web client and Office Outlook desktop client, even while _Anonyous Access_ is enabled.  * Having a valid session with Confluence open at the same time does not have an effect on whether Gmail is able to render the image.   * Issue is not reproducible with Apple iMail desktop client"""

summary: "Newly created Jira issue by highlighting text fails to update the page when multiple users create them on the same page."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When multiple users are creating Jira issues by highlighting text, the newly created Jira issue is not updated in the page.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a table in the page and add some text   # User1 highlight a text and right mouse click and open the Jira issue window and fill out the form,  but do not save it  # User2 login and edit the same page, and create a Jira issue.  # User1 save the Jira issue.   # You may see the following error:    We can confirm the new Jira issue is created in Jira, but it's not updated in the page.  The error message on the screen may not happening always.    h3. Expected Results  Newly created issue by User1 should be updated in the page.    h3. Actual Results  The new Jira issue was created by User1 was not updated in the page.     h3. Workaround  The new Jira issue can be added using the Jira issue macro add-on.  Instead, Jira Issue maco add-on can be used to avoid this issue."""

summary: "Instructional text mode in template keeps when input multibyte character (Japanese or Chinese)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When typing a letter or a word in instructional text (or hint) of template, the Instructional text mode will change to normal text mode automatically. The background color of the input place will change from grey to white.    However, if the input is a multibyte character like Japanese or Chinese, the Instructional text mode will be kept, the background will keep as grey.    The reproducing of above issue depends on browser.  ||Browser||Reproduced or not||  |Chrome|yes|  |Firefox|no|  |Edge|yes|  |Safri|yes|  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a *product requirements* template   # Put mouse pointer on any instructional text(hint) area   # Shift input mode to Hiragana (Japanese) mode   # Type 'あいう' in Japanese mode   # All new input will be treated as instructional text.    h3. Expected Results    The new input will be treated as normal text input without grey background.  h3. Actual Results    The new input will be treated as instructional text with grey background.  h3. Workaround    Input in English mode first, make the grey area of instructional text disappear, and then input in Japanese/Chinese mode."""

summary: "Companion triggers view on save after user clicks view"
description: """*Steps to reproduce*   # Edit a file with Companion, upload it, then click the view button   # Save the same file again    *Expected behaviour*    Companion transitions to file saved state.     *Actual behaviour*    Preview link is opened in browser, companion transitions to file saved state.     """

summary: "Sharing an image as an anonymous user triggers an Unknown Server error message"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  * When an anonymous user tries to share an image in Confluence it gets an *Unknown Server* error message    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # As an anonymous user, in a page that has an image, click on the image  # On the top-right of the page, click on the *Share* button   !Screen Shot 2020-06-16 at 11.34.15.png|thumbnail!   # On the *Add people* field, type in a valid email address and click on the *Share* option    h3. Expected Results  * An email is sent to the email address provided with the image shared    h3. Actual Results  * The following error message is shown:   !Screen Shot 2020-06-16 at 11.31.35.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  * Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Attachments macro does not work when Spam Prevention is enabled"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  * The Attachment macro is not able to display the Captcha box when Spam prevention is enabled in Confluence.  * This allows us the macro to select an attachment, but does not upload it as the security token from the captcha is missing.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Enable Spam Prevention in Confluence.  ** [Spam Prevention|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-captcha-for-spam-prevention-169970.html]  # Create a new page, and add the *Attachments macro*  # Try to add an attachment using the macro.  ** You will either get a message on the UI saying that the security token is missing, or in some cases the page just refreshes and the attachment is not uploaded.  # If we use the *Attachments* page of the page itself, the Captcha box is shown and we're unable to upload attachments without any issues.    h3. Expected Results  # The Captcha box is shown for the Attachments macro, allowing attachments to be uploaded when Spam prevention is enabled.    h3. Actual Results  # Attachments macro becomes unusable when Spam prevention is enabled.    h3. Workaround  # Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Submitting the Form for Companion App Feedback isn't working"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Customers are reporting that the form in the context menu of Companion app that is used to provide feedback is returning an error.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # The Companion app has a link in its context menu for submitting feedback.   # Clicking """"Submit"""" in this form returns an error    h3. Expected Results    The form is submitted sucessfully    h3. Actual Results    The following message is displayed to the user:        The below error is shown in the developer tools:    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available  """

summary: "Session timeout in Confluence Server Mobile App - Android "
description: """h3. Problem Description    Customers that logging in with their Confluence Server Mobile App in Android, able to login, but after a session timeout, the user will be logged out again.    The login page does not remember the login information when checking """"Remember Me"""". This is not happening in IOS Mobile App.    h3. Workaround  Use Web browser in the Mobile."""

summary: "Granular text selection not appearing on iOS app for Confluence Server"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  # When selecting a text in View mode, the option to select the text we want to highlight is not shown.  # We will need to edit the page first to be able to see the specific text we want to select.  # Issue does not persist on the Android app, or even on iOS Safari.  **  !Droid_text_selection.jpg|thumbnail!   **  !iTextSelect.jpg|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page in Confluence with some text.  # Save the page.  # Try to select a text by long pressing on it.    h3. Expected Results  # The option to select the text we want is shown.    h3. Actual Results  # The option to select the text we want is clear\invisible.    h3. Workaround  # Edit the page to copy specific text  # Alternatively, use the Safari browser."""

summary: "Custom Jira fields with type ""Number Field"" do not appear on Jira Charts macro"
description: """h4. Issue summary   # In Jira, we can create custom fields for issues with different types, like """"List"""", """"Number Field"""", and """"Label"""", each with their own format.  # In Confluence, with the *Jira Chart* macro, when using the *Two Dimensional* chart's *Display Option*, custom *Number Field* from Jira do not appear as selection for the *XAxis* and *YAxis*.  # Other custom fields with type """"List"""" and type """"Label"""" appear fine.  # There is a difference between the text and label however, where for the text custom fields, I could only filter them between null or not null, and could not use the equals sign *=* in my query, but I could do so with labels, querying the exact value like * = 2.4.3* for example.    h4. Expected behavior  # All custom fields should be able to appear in the Jira Charts macro    h4. Current Behavior   # Custom field with type """"Number Field"""" do not appear.    h4. Workaround  # Use type """"Label"""" instead."""

summary: "Sharing page to a group show error in logs"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Error is shown in the logs when sharing a page to a group.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a group in an internal Directory, or allow it to Sync from an external one   # Make sure to have users with valid e-mail addresses in the group   # Access any page in Confluence   # Click Share link on the top of the page   # On the dialog, type a group name   # Click Share    h3. Expected Results   * E-mail is sent to the users   * No error on the logs expect if one actually occurs    h3. Actual Results   * E-mail is sent to the users   * SQL error is shown on the logs:   ** With Postgres:       ** With Oracle:      h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available    Issue was not reproducible in 7.4.0"""

summary: "Copy and paste page likes in a comment overwrites the complete comment"
description:
"""h3. Issue Summary When copying the string ‘and XX others like this’ at the bottom of the page, pasting it into your comment and then liking your new comment, the comment changes from the original text to {quote}You like this {quote} It changes every line of text to the one on the quote above. h3. Steps to Reproduce # Start writing a comment # Select the text from the likes footer at the bottom of the page, e.g the String ‘and 12 others like this’ and paste it into your comment # Add some more text to the comment, so it reads approx. {quote} and 12 others like this -> We already have a lot of agreement!! {quote} # Save the comment # Reload the page (so you are able to like the new comment) # Like your new comment h3. Expected Results The comment in regular ‘read’ view is displayed as before. h3. Actual Results The comments content is displayed as {quote} You like this You like this {quote} h3. Workaround Currently, there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available. h3. Notes: * This was not reproduced on Confluence Cloud * When editing the comment, the 'correct' content is displayed. This bug is a display-only defect * When looking at the initial page request, the page source delivered by the server contains the correct comment text * When looking at the comment's storage format, the with classes is included, when copy & pasting from the page This, together with some over-zealous .js / css selectors probably cause the replacing of content in every

tag of the comment."""

summary: "Doing a find and replace in the page editor on firefox 6X.X versions cause the browser to crash"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Certain versions of Firefox specifically in the 60.X version range (confirmed 68.0 and 64.0 versions) have a bug when implementing a find a replace for words on the page editor that cause the browser to crash    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Use a firefox browser version of 60.X for example 68.0    Attempt to do a find and replace      h3. Expected Results  Find a replace should occur without issues  h3. Actual Results  The browser crashes.    h3. Workaround    Upgrade Firefox to a newer version 70.X versions appear to not have the issue."""

summary: "Edit files does not work on some instances with Companion 1.2.0"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Edit files does not work on some instances with Companion 1.2.0  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Attempt to edit a file from Confluence    h3. Expected Results    File opens in Companion.   h3. Actual Results    Download fails from Companion with 401 error code.   h3. Workaround    N/A"""

summary: "Error message shows incorrect value of queued tasks"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Even when reducing the total queue size for Jira Issues Macro, the error message will say that """"there are more than 1000 tasks queued""""    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # To reduce the pool and queue size, edit {{setenv.sh}} and add the below parameters  {code:borderStyle=solid| title= }  CATALINA_OPTS=""""-Dconfluence.jira.issues.executor.queuesize=1 ${CATALINA_OPTS}""""  CATALINA_OPTS=""""-Dconfluence.jira.issues.executor.poolsize=1 ${CATALINA_OPTS}""""        h3. Expected Results    The error message should indicate the correct value of tasks queued.    h3. Actual Results  The error message in the application logs says *JIM Marshaller rejected task because there are more than 1000 tasks queued*, however, we can also see *queued tasks = 1* in the same error.    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Unusual log entries when deleting remote links of JIRA issues"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  when deleting remote links for JIRA issues from a page gets below Warning message:    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Step 1 : Create One Jira Issue in Confluence Page   # Step 2 : Save the Confluence Page after adding the Jira Issue.   # Step 3 : Now Remove the Jira Issue Link and save the Page.   # Step 4 : The issue gets removed from the Confluence page but it generate below warning in the Confluence Logs,    h3. Expected Results  There should be no Warning message as the Issue has been removed properly from the Confluence Page.  h3. Actual Results  Jira Issue is getting removed but it logs the below warning message,      h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Display error in ""People who can view""-Window when Language is changed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Display error in """"People who can view""""-Window when Language is changed.    The translated text for People who can view overflows and is hidden under the search box. PFB difference between English and Spanish.  ||Heading 1||Heading 2||  |!Screenshot 2020-06-24 at 7.08.04 PM.png|width=251,height=141!|!Screenshot 2020-06-24 at 7.08.41 PM.png|width=214,height=128!|       h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open People who can view from the Page tools, the text title will be fine   # Change the user language to Any different language   # People who can view when translated to the different language will overflow as shown in the screenshot.    h3. Expected Results    Title should be coming properly even after changing language.  h3. Actual Results    Title is getting overflowed and hidden under search box.  h3. Workaround       Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Excel Macro edit button only available on the page excel file is attached to"
description: """h3. Problem    When using the Excel Macro, the *Edit* button is only available on the page that the excel sheet is attached to. Other pages using an Excel Macro that is attached to another page no longer include the *Edit* button above the macro.   h3. Environment    Confluence 7.5.0  h3. Steps to Reproduce    * Add an Excel file as an attachment to a page  * Add an Excel Macro to this page using the Excel file.  * Publish the page  * Create a new page   * Add an Excel macro with the *Page Name* set to the previously created page that we added the Excel file to, and set the File Name to the Excel file.  * Publish the page    h3. Expected Results    We expect that when published the Excel Macro on the second page will include an *Edit* button in the upper right corner.   h3. Actual Results    There is no *Edit* button on the second page. However, the *Edit* button can be seen when viewing the Excel Macro on the original page.   h3. Workaround    Enabling the Edit in Office dark feature will restore the missing *Edit* links to the page.   h3. Notes"""

summary: "Adding a task macro to the system dashboard does not allow checking of tasks"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When you add a task macro within the welcome section template of the system dashboard you cannot check or uncheck tasks.  According to our [documentation|https://confluence.<USER>doc/editing-the-site-welcome-message-138561.html] it should be possible to include macros within this section.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Edit the welcome message displayed on the default dashboard   # Inlcude a task macro   # The tasks are displayed but cannot be checked or unchecked.    h3. Expected Results  Our documentation states you can add macros to this sewction as mentioned on [Editing the site welcome message|https://confluence.<USER>doc/editing-the-site-welcome-message-138561.html]  * The tasks macro works - as it does on standard page and you can check/complete tasks    h3. Actual Results  There is no response when you click on a task checkbox.    h3. Workaround  If you set your homepage to standard page within General Configuration the macro will work, but you lose the _Discover_ sidebar with Recent Updates/Popular <USER>as on the system dashboard."""

summary: "Duplicated content in pages after running Synchrony Hard Data Removal"
description: """h3. Issue summary  With Collaborative Editing On and Synchrony Hard Data Removal Enabled in the Scheduled Jobs, the editor sometimes will randomly repeat the content and causing the content to be duplicated after saving the page.    h3. Steps to replicate the issue  h4. Creating a two pages  # Create a page with title *Page 1*.   # Create a child page with title *Page 2*. This page must contain some text.  # Save *Page 2*    h4. Removing Synchrony Data (Hard)  # Go to http://<base url>/admin/scheduledjobs/viewscheduledjobs.action  # Find on the page *Synchrony Data Removal (Hard)*  # Click *Run* button to trigger the *Hard Data Removal* job.    h4. Reproduce the bug  1. Return to the *Page 1* created earlier  2. (the next step need to be done very quickly)  3. On the left side, in the Page Tree, click *Page 2* link, and press *E* keyboard shortcut repeatedly at the same time until the page opens for editing. This steps to trigger the editor to load slowly.    h3. Expected behavior  Exact content loaded in the Editor.    h3. Actual behavior  Page load for editing, and after a while (few seconds/milliseconds) a duplicate content (text) appears.     h3. Notes  Issue happen randomly for normal editing. Only when the editor took sometime (few seconds) to load. Users who experience slow network connection while loading the editor usually hit into the issue.    h3. Workaround  Issue does not persist after turning *Collaborative Editing mode* to *Off* and disabling *Synchrony Data Removal (Hard)*."""

summary: "Team Calendars cut off long issues name on the legend"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When viewing a calendar using Team Calendars, the name in the legend is cut off if it's too long.    Team Calendars version: 6.0.49  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add an event > Event Type = Jira Issue Dates   # Add a long name   # Click Ok    h3. Expected Results    See the full name on the legend.  h3. Actual Results    Part of the name is cut off.  !TeamCalendarsBug.jpg|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Impossible to turn the page when searching User name with ""&"" "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When searching the user name contain """"&"""", the turning page results in the error message """"You must specify a search term.""""    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create 40 users with the pattern """"user&"""".  # In the page """"users"""" add """"&"""" in the """"Find user"""" text box  # Try to get to the second page    h3. Expected Results  We should be able to show the second-page result.    h3. Actual Results  The following error shows on the UI:   !Screenshot 2020-06-26 at 12.10.00.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround  It needs to search the same via SQL query,"""

summary: "Creating new page while editing the initial draft of a new page causes orphaned pages"
description: """h3. Problem  After selecting the 'Create' option to create a new page, if the Create button is selected again, while editing the draft of the first new page, the second new page will be created as a child of the first page- however, the first page was never published in the first place.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Select the 'Create' option to create a new page  # While editing the draft of this new page, click the 'Create' button again (don't publish the first page)  # Publish this second page    h3. Expected Results  Either Confluence shouldn't let this second, child page be created if its parent page wasn't created in the first place, or it should create the new child page on a valid path, disregarding the invalid parent page.    h3. Actual Results  This results in the second, child page being orphaned. It will not show on the page tree, but the breadcrumbs at the top of the page will display the correct path, even though the parent page does not exist and can't be reached.  """

summary: "Chart macro's background color turns red when selecting JPG image format"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Selecting *""""JPG""""* in image format for *Chart macro* show the background of the macro in red rather than white.   * Confluence 7.5.1   * Oracle Corporation Java 1.8.0_241-b07    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add Chart macro in a page   # Edit the properties of the macro and select """"JPG"""" under """"image format""""   # Publish the page    h3. Expected Results    The background of the macro should be in *white*   h3. Actual Results    The background of the macro shows in *red* making it difficult to view  h3. Workaround    Change the *image format* in the properties of the macro from JPG to *PNG*"""

summary: "Pages set to ""Viewing and editing restricted"" show twice under Space Tools -> Restricted pages and pagination is not working when more than 15 page restricted"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Pages that are restricted with *""""Viewing and editing restricted""""* appear twice under Space Tools >> Permissions >> Restricted pages  h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create 16 page in a space.   # Grant *""""Viewing and editing restricted""""* restriction to all 16 page   # Go to Space tools >> permissions >> restricted pages   # The page is listed twice and the pagination is not working and only showing 15 page restriction.    h3. Expected Results    The pages with """"Viewing and editing restricted"""" shouldn't be listed twice under """"Restricted Pages"""" and the pagination is working when more than 30 page restricted.  h3. Actual Results    The pages with """"Viewing and editing restricted"""" are listed twice under """"Restricted pages"""" and pagination is not working. Below video shows that page is duplicated in page restriction and pagination is not working when more than 15 page restriction in a space.   [^Recording #17.mp4]   h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Restricted pages that are deleted still show up in ""Restricted Pages"" tab"
description: """h3. Problem    If a page has restrictions applied to it and is deleted, the page will still be listed in the Space Tools > Permissions > Restricted Pages tab  h3. Environment    Confluence Server and Data Center  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add restrictions to a page   # Delete the page   # Go to Space Tools > Permissions > Restricted Pages   # The deleted page will still be listed here    h3. Expected Results    The deleted page should not be listed under _Restricted Pages_  h3. Actual Results    The deleted page is still listed under _Restricted Pages_  h3. Workaround    Restore the page, then remove restrictions and delete the page again. The page will no longer be listed in Restricted Pages.  h3. Notes    The issue is not reproducible on versions 7.5.0 and higher"""

summary: "Editor toolbar disappears when editing large page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When attempting to edit a large page having scrolled enough for the top navigation bar to disappear, the editor will be missing a text editor toolbar.  Issue reproducible on 6.12.2 and 6.13.4    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a long, content-rich page, long enough to get a scroll bar   # Scroll the page enough for the top navigation bar to disappear   # Click """"e"""" to enter page editor    h3. Expected Results   * Editor loads with a toolbar containing text editing tools.    h3. Actual Results   * Toolbar will be missing    h3. Workaround  Not reproducible on 6.13.12, 6.15.9 or 7.1.1  Exiting editor, scrolling to the top of the page and clicking edit again will bring back the toolbar.  Reloading the page should bring back the editor toolbar"""

summary: "Preview is broken when Table, section and checkboxes are used"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Preview is broken when Tables, sections, and checkboxes are used.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page with Section Macro   # Insert bullets and then some content on the paragraph.   # Insert the next bullet with table content.   # Insert the next bullet instead of bullet please use the checkbox.   # Create a new section and add Checkbox.   # Observe the second section in the preview mode.    h3. Expected Results    The checkbox appears when we preview the page with the content beside the checkbox  h3. Actual Results    The content besides the checkbox is all overwritten with multiple checkboxes    !Checkbox overwriten.png|thumbnail!  h6. The storage format of the page    [^storage format]  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available. However, when you save the page, you see the content just fine."""

summary: "Status macro is displayed with different ""width"" in View templates page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Status macro is displayed with different width in View templates page  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Move _Confluence admin > Global Templates and Blueprints_   # Click """"Add global page template"""" and create new page template   # Use status macro in template   # View created template    h3. Expected Results    Status macro should be same as page     !Screen Shot 2020-07-02 at 11.25.51.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    min-width is not applied    !Screen Shot 2020-07-02 at 11.25.25.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "It is possible to publish personal draft with collaborative editing turned on"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  It is possible to publish a personal draft with collaborative editing set to on.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Turn off Collaborative editing  # Edit a page  # In a new tab enable Collaborative editing.  # Save/publish a personal draft of that page    h3. Expected Results  Once collaborative editing is enabled, user will receive a message in tab with a personal draft that publishing is not possible.    h3. Actual Results  Saving of the page is possible and user is able to save the page and overwrite the current version of the page, effectively bypassing the shared draft functionality.    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "While using Companion app, Preview shows previously viewed Item instead of current one."
description: """h3. Problem    While using Companion app, Preview shows previously viewed Item instead of current one.  h3. Environment    Confluence Version :  7.4.1    OS :  Linux 3.10.0-1127.8.2.el7.x86_64 using  amd64  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page in Confluence. Upload couple of pdf or xlsx or txt or doc files.   # Use Confluence Companion application to edit one of these files.   # Upload the saved file into Confluence, using companion app.   # Choose a different file from the same page and click edit to view preview using companion app.    h3. Expected Results    _Confluence should show the preview of the chosen file._  h3. summary: "Code block macro disappears when editing the macro like syntax highlighting language on Firefox"

summary: "Special characters in page titles breaks excerpt macro"
description: """h3. Problem   * *Scenario 1*   If a page title contains a special character such as a colon("""":"""") and is referenced in an _excerpt include macro_ and the page is moved within the hierarchy or to a different space the macro breaks when the page is updated and shows the following error:  {quote}Error rendering macro 'excerpt-include'    No link could be created for '<page name>'.  {quote}   * *Scenario 2*   This also happens when a page title is edited and a special character(colon """":"""") is added. When the page with the _excerpt include macro_ is updated the macro breaks with the same error as above.    h3. Environment   * Confluence 7.4.0   * Confluence 7.5.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce   * *Scenario 1*   ## Create a page with an _excerpt macro_ adding random text and a page title as follows: """"Testing: Phase 1""""(*note the colon*)   ## Create a page with an _include excerpt macro_ and reference the page created in step 1 to display the excerpt   ## Move the page created in step 1 to a different place in the hierarchy or space   ## Edit the page created in step 2 and without making any changes just update the page   * *Scenario 2*   ## Create a page with an _excerpt macro_ adding random text and a page title as follows: """"Testing Phase 1""""   ## Create a page with an _include excerpt macro_ and reference the page created in step 1 to display the excerpt   ## Edit the page created in step one and change the title to """"Testing: Phase 1""""(*note the colon that was added*)   ## Edit the page created in step 2 and without making any changes just update the page    h3. Expected Results    The link to the """"excerpt macro"""" should be correctly updated and the data within displayed without an error.  h3. Actual Results   * The following error is displayed once the page is updated in both scenarios:  {quote}Error rendering macro 'excerpt-include'    No link could be created for '<page name>'.  {quote}    h3. Workaround   * Do not use any special characters in page titles containing _include macros_   * Manually fix the reference in the _excerpt include macro_ if it breaks    h3. Notes   * *Scenario 1*   ** The link is prefixed by the space key and once the page is moved the space key is removed."""

summary: "Confluence search fails with HTTP 500"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Hit the Confluence search box on the top left of the screen there is a spinning wheel and then nothing.    Checking the browser logs (F12 or Developer Tools -> Network tab), it's possible to see a HTTP 500 Error:     !Screen Shot 2020-07-17 at 18.37.36.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Try to search   # It fails    h3. Expected Results    The search works.    h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log:        The lines related to {{java.util.regex.Pattern}} go on forever for hundred of pages.    This is most likely caused by https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/PLUGWEB-422    h3. Workaround    Increase the Java parameter {{-Xss}} to a higher value.    This parameter is the responsible to address the recursive calls made on the Java code, exactly what is happening as shown in the Exception above, therefore increase it can solve the issue at some degree.    More details about this parameter can be found in this discussion:    [JVM option -Xss - What does it do exactly?|https://stackoverflow.com/questions/4967885/jvm-option-xss-what-does-it-do-exactly]"""

summary: "Upgrading from Confluence 6 to 7 can cause Synchrony schema to not update successfully"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When performing an upgrade to Confluence from 6 to 7, there is a schema change for Synchrony to support new Data Eviction standards. However, under certain circumstances this schema update can fail and the new columns and keys will not be properly created, causing post-upgrade Collaborative Editor issues.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Perform an upgrade from Confluence 6 to 7  h3. Expected Results   - Synchrony tables will be dropped and have their schema updated to reflect new table columns and keys (documented in brief here: [Editing and creating pages fails on Confluence due to content reconciliation errors - Unknown column 'snapshots0_.contentid' in 'field list'|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/editing-and-creating-pages-fails-on-confluence-due-to-content-reconciliation-errors-unknown-column-snapshots0_-contentid-in-field-list-1004943042.html]    h3. Actual Results   - Collaborative editing will fail after the upgrade   - It seems that the schema update will fail to drop the Synchrony tables (evidenced by the tables having content in them still after the upgrade). The actual rest of the upgrade completes successfully   - In Oracle, you may see the following error message after the failed update to the Synchrony table schema during the upgrade.  Here, we see the Synchrony tables cleanup scheduled job failing:      h3. Workaround   - You can manually drop the Synchrony tables and recreate them using the schema workaround noted in the [Editing and creating pages fails on Confluence due to content reconciliation errors - Unknown column 'snapshots0_.contentid' in 'field list'|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/editing-and-creating-pages-fails-on-confluence-due-to-content-reconciliation-errors-unknown-column-snapshots0_-contentid-in-field-list-1004943042.html].   - Technically, you don't have to drop the tables, but they do need to be empty, so truncating them pre-upgrade will also allow for the upgrade to complete successfully."""

Expected Results    Confluence 6.x can start on latest AdoptOpenJDK 8u262  h3. Actual Results    Confluence 6.x fails to start  h3. Workaround    Upgrade to Confluence 7.x"""

summary: "Configuring the JVM system property page-tree.partial-loading-batch-size displays an extra page (n+1)"

Steps to Reproduce     # Edit your {{setenv.sh}}   # Configure {{page-tree.partial-loading-batch-size}}   # Notice there is always one more page shown than set. It's not a major change or workaround but is counter intuitive when reading our documentation.      h3. Workaround  Just set your value to 1 lower than required, remember the counter starts at 0. Workaround    Use search parameters (with wildcards if needed) in the search box.    1. Type in the search criteria:   !Screen Shot 2020-07-24 at 11.40.48.png|thumbnail!    2. Click on """"Search for"""" to add a search parameter:   !Screen Shot 2020-07-24 at 11.40.57.png|thumbnail!    3. Also, searching for the user's ID (entire ID or parts of it) seems to produce better search results from the user picker:   !Screen Shot 2020-07-24 at 11.39.32.png|thumbnail!"""
