description: """h3. Issue Summary  There are a lot of issues rendering an excel file with Office Excel macro when the files contain charts that occupy several columns     *Reproduced with:*  * Confluence 6.13.10 and 7.4  * Browsers: Chrome, Firefox, and Safari    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new page in Confluence and attach this excel file:  [^excel_chart_text2.xlsx]   # Save the page and Edit again  # Add an *Office Excel* macro to the page  # For the Office Excel macro options make sure you see the attached excel file in the File Name field  # Insert the macro and publish the page    h3. Issues Observed    # The charts that are side by side in the original excel file overlap when rendered in the Office macro, making it impossible to see the content.  # The excel file columns width is not the same after the file is rendered in the macro, it shrinks as small as possible when there is no text content in it.  # The charts are rendered outside the macro and it goes beyond the Confluence page width.   !Screen Shot 2020-07-24 at 14.26.59.png|thumbnail!    !Screen Shot 2020-07-24 at 14.27.17.png|thumbnail!       h3. Expected Results    # Expected to see the charts side by side as we see in the excel file.  # Expected to see the columns with the same width as defined in the excel file.  # Expected to have all the content inside the macro and had the macro display a horizontal scroll bar to see the entire content inside.    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Some Confluence Server mobile app's declared device permissions are not applicable"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Some device permissions declared by the Confluence Server mobile app on Android and iOS are not applicable with current app functionality.  This was discovered through recent conversations with the Server Mobile development team and end users.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # View device permissions on your device (see [https://www.wired.com/story/how-to-check-app-permissions-ios-android-macos-windows] for instructions)    h3. Expected Results    Device permissions for each app on its respective device platform should only display permissions that are applicable / necessary for the app to operate.       h3. Actual Results    As confirmed by developers,    * Android app does not need """"READ_CALENDAR"""" – permissions should not be listed   * iOS app does not need any permissions relating to image upload since that feature is not currently supported – these permissions should not be listed:   ** NSCameraUsageDescription   ** NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription    Please review other permissions to confirm if they are also required to grant access to certain app functionality.  h3. Workaround    N/A"""

summary: "Powerpoint Attachment Preview Causes Full Garabage Collection Confluence 7.x"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Powepoint attachments with _small_ graphs fail to preview in the page attachments list and cause performance issues.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Edit a page in Confluence 7.x   # Attach [^crash-presentation.pptx]  to the page   # Navigate to (***) > Attachments  # Preview [^crash-presentation.pptx]     h3. Expected Results    # The attachment previews or provides a failed preview with no performance degradation    h3. Actual Results    # The preview spins and the heap is used up    Garbage collection logs show full garbage collection and the app can crash:        h3. Workaround    Reattach the powerpoint file with a larger graph. """

summary: "Copying a page with the Excerpt Include macro breaks it if having specific characters"
description:
"""h3. Issue Summary In Confluence Server, if the user is using the [Excerpt Include macro|https://confluence.doc/excerpt-include-macro-148067.html] in order to retrieve content from a page that has specific characters (like """":""""), the same will display the following result on the copy: *""""Error rendering macro 'excerpt-include': No link could be created for 'specific page: specific character""""* h3. Steps to Reproduce # Create a new page with a colon character in the title (eg: *Page: One*) # Edit *Page: One* and insert an *Excerpt macro* #* *!https://emoji.slack-edge.com/TE8HJA7RS/info_mark/6ac3f9befb556605.png|width=16,height=16!* The Storage Format of the page should look something like: {code:html}


INLINE

This is my excerpt macro


{code} # Create another page with any title (eg: *Page Two*) # In *Page Two*, add an *Excerpt Include* macro and point it to *Page: One* # Use the *Copy* function on *Page Two* and save it as *Page Two Clone* in any space h3. Expected Results The copy should be properly displaying the Excerpt Include content. h3. Actual Results Instead of having the content, the message below is displayed: *""""Error rendering macro 'excerpt-include': No link could be created for 'specific page: specific character""""* h3. Notes * Similar as CONFCLOUD-65349 but for Confluence Server * A video has been recorded with the steps to reproduce. The *excerpt* video can be found on the attachments of this request (steps to reproduce for Confluence v7.6.1) * This seems to be happening with copied pages because the same remove the *space key* from the macro parameter, hence, making the macro to not identify it. * Simply tapping on the excerpt include macro in order to *edit* and then, searching for the page will automatically adjust it. * Pages without specific characters are not affected once the same *does not use* a space key as a reference. h3. Workaround * Instead of using the *Copy* function, try to manually copy the content with the excerpt to a new page. * Change the title of the pages using the affected character"""

summary: "Text color breaks Page Properties report macro"
description: """h3. Problem    Text color on the Page Properties Macro is not picked up by the Page Properties Report macro.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create this *Main Page Properties Report Macro* page  #* Looks for pages with the label *reportlabel*  #* On the located pages, read the Page Properties Macro with id *maintable*  #* Show the column data: *Business entity* and *Project name*     !page_properties_report_new.png|thumbnail!   #* For now, it is expected to show no rows until we create the below page  # Create this child *Page properties macro page* and label the page with *reportlabel*     !child_page_properties.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results    *Main Page Properties Report Macro* page shows the data columns """"Business entity"""" and """"Project name"""" from the child page (/)     !page_properties_report_datafilled_column.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    *Main Page Properties Report Macro* page is not showing the data columns """"Business entity"""" and """"Project name"""" from the child page (x)     !page_properties_report_empty_column.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    # On the child page with the page properties macro  #* Select / highlight the Column names (i.e. *Business entity* and *Project name*)  #* Set the Font color to say *Orange*  #* Now, set the Font color to *Orange* again (doing this will now remove the color off the column names)  # Publish the page  # Refresh the main Page Properties Report macro page and the column data should now appear     !page_properties_report_datafilled_column.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Editor refresh itself when inserting a link"
description: """h3. Steps to replicate   # Edit a page in Confluence.   # Click on *+* in the editor tool to insert a link or use the (CRTL/CMD+K) shortcut.   # Enter or paste a link into the *search box* and click *Search for <the_link>*.    h3. Expected Behaviour    Confluence will return the search result.  h3. Actual Behaviour    The editor refresh when the user click on *Search for <the_link>* after pasting the link in the *search* box:  * !pastelink.png|thumbnail!    Hovering to the *Search for <the_link>* found that the URL matches the page editor URL in the browser thus clicking on it will trigger the editor to refresh itself.  h3. Workaround    Click on the *Search* button directly to search for the internal Confluence content. For the external link, insert the link directly using the *Web Link* tab."""

summary: "Office Excel thread hangs if xls file contains formulas"
description: """h3. Problem  Office Excel (view-xls) macro does not load and its corresponding thread gets stuck if the file contains formulas. Example:      h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Attach the file  [^Test.xls]  to a page  # Add the *Office Excel* macro and select that file  # Save the page    h3. Expected Results    The page and the macro render as expected.    h3. Actual Results    The page does not load anymore and the thread gets stuck infinitely. Only restarting the application will clear up that thread.    h3. Workaround    The file  [^Test-noformulas.xls]  is the same but the """"COUNTIF"""" formulas were removed. The bug cannot be reproduced with that file. So, the options are:    # Edit the file and remove those formulas  # Or, use the File Preview macro instead of the Office Excel one    h3. Notes  Example of hanging thread:      This can result in full unresponsiveness of the instance if the page is visited the same number of times that is defined in maxThreads (48 by default) since they all will get stuck in this operation. Only restarting will clear them up.    It also affects Data Center because the Office Excel macro is not covered by the file conversion sandboxing due to this bug:    * https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-59425"""

summary: "Warning on the logs even when the job Clean Temporary Directory runs successfully"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The Clean Temporary Directory job successfully deletes the files but the logs show a warning. This does not happen when you run the job manually.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure a Confluence instance;  # Start Confluence;  # Head to _General Configuration > Scheduled Jobs_;  # Next to Back Up Confluence, select _Run_ (this will generate a .zip file in the <confluence-home>/temp directory);  # Go to <confluence-home>/temp and note that the zip file for the back up is there;  # Wait until the time set for the job to run (4:20 am by default);  (i) Note: Because Confluence backup is set to remain on the folder for 24h, you may have to wait until the next day or the zip won't be deleted. Unless you're affected by this bug.    h3. Expected Results  # The zip file is successfully removed from the directory;  # There is no error on the logs.    h3. Actual Results  # The zip file is successfully removed from the directory;  # The logs show this warning:      h3. Workaround  Since the job is working, the only workaround, for now, is to ignore the warnings on the logs."""

summary: "Cannot add empty task list to custom template "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When creating a custom template, adding a task list and remove the text, so only the checkbox remains, upon publishing, the checkbox disappears.     (!) Exactly the same behavior as https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-58869 except it only affects custom templates and happens with collaborative editing enabled.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom template and edit to add a task list.   remove the default text: Type your task here, using """"@"""" to assign to a user and """"//"""" to select a due date so that only the checkbox remains   # Step 2  Publish this task list.  h3. Expected Results  The checkbox should remain  h3. Actual Results  The checkbox disappears.     h3. Workaround    Add a space key after removing the text allows for the checkbox to remain after publishing similar to https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-58869 """

summary: "Jira Macro column values not appearing when Custom Field (Jira) name contains comma or semicolon"
description: """h3. Summary    When JIRA Custom Fields name contains comma (,) or (;) JIRA Macro identify the them as separators and does not include the field values in the JIRA Macro table generated in Confluence         Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field that include a comma or semicolon, add value.   # Create a JIRA Macro in a Confluence page   # Include the custom field that contains a comma or semicolon   # Publish page.          [^Screenshot from 2017-10-25 18.30.04.png] [^Screenshot from 2017-10-25 18.31.26.png]  h3. Expected Results    Values are shown in Custom field column  h3. Actual Results    _Empty values are shown and the header is separated where the commas or semicolons are positioned._  h3. Workaround      Remove commas from JIRA Custom Fields"""

summary: "Cannot add custom field as columns in Jira Issue Macro (table view) due to case-sensitivity"
description: """h3. Problem    In a Confluence site version *7.4.x* or *7.6.x* connected to a Jira Software via an application link.    We cannot add 2 Jira *custom fields* as *columns* to display in a *Jira Issue Macro* (table view) due to case-sensitivity.  h3. Environment    Confluence version *7.4.x* or *7.6.x* connected to a Jira Software version *8.4.2* via an application link  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create 2 custom fields in Jira and configure them to be displayed on some default screens for the user to fill in. In our example, we called these fields *end date* and *End date* and they were date pickers.   # Create a new Jira Issue / Bug and assign a new value to these fields   # Go to Confluence and create a new Confluence page adding a *Jira Issue Macro*   # Customize the Jira Issue Macro and try to make the new custom fields displayed as a column in a Table view    h3. Expected Results    _The user can pick the 2 custom fields as *columns* to display in a *Jira Issue Macro*_  h3. Actual Results    _After picking the first custom field, the second one becomes unavailable for selection in the *Jira Issue Macro*_  h3. Workaround    _No Workaround at this stage except renaming fields on the Jira side that may not be convenient for the user needs._  h3. Notes    A video is attached with my steps to reproduce in Confluence"""

Further tests reveal that this happens when there are pages with """"content_status='draft' and creationdate=null"""".  h3. Actual Results  Changing the title of certain pages fails and it reverts to the old name. When we edit the page again, we see the old page's title.    h3. Workaround    We can add a random entry in the creationdate field for pages with content_status=draft:  """

summary: "Confluence Copy/Move Page is Not listing Destination Space Name for Some Space Names"
description: """h3. Problem    In confluence while trying to copy/move page from one space to another, if the destination space name is a subset of a list of spaces already present, the space name is not listed. Hence, the page cannot be copied or moved to the destination page.    h3. Environment  .  Confluence Version 7.2.0 or above.     h3. Steps to Reproduce    Create 20 spaces with the following names:    *Step 1:*    1. HaCon Orga TPS01  2. HaCon Orga TPS02  .  .  .  20. HaCon Orga TPS20  Also create a space named HaCon Orga TPS.     *Step 2:*    Create a test page in any one of the 20 pages initially created. Assuming the page is created under HaCon Orga TPS20.  Try to move or copy this page to the space HaCon Orga TPS.     h3. Expected Results    The destination space should be listed either through partial search or through full search of the string.     h3. Actual Results    The string is not listed through either partial search nor through full string search. Only the other numbered spaces are listed.     h3. Workaround     A semi partial workaround is when moving page from space to space, there is a browse option at the end. This allows for choosing the space that is not coming up in the search. But this workaround does not work for copying pages to new space, where the browse option is not present.    Rebuilding content index also does not fix the issue.    h3. Notes  """

description: """h3. Issue Summary    Macros with long name gets appended with extra characters … in German Language.  h3. Works fine with English.   !Screenshot 2020-08-17 at 12.02.09 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  The macro should not have extra characters appended to it.     h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Atlassian Troubleshooting & Support Tools Sends Support Zip Even if Attach Support Zip Option is Unchecked"
description: """h3. Problem    Atlassian Troubleshooting & Support Tools Sends Support Zip Even if """"*Attach Support Zip*"""" Option is Unchecked    h3. Environment  .  Confluence 7.4.0  Microsoft Edge 84.0.522.59     h3. Steps to Reproduce    1. Login to Confluence 7.4.0 using Microsoft Edge version 84.0.522.59  2. Navigate to Confluence > General Configuration > Troubleshooting & Support Tools > Get Help > Contact <USER>or Report a Bug  3. Uncheck the """"Attach Support Zip"""" option  4. Click on send.    h3. summary: "Jira Issue Date event won't show date/time range if the range is 24h"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a Jira Issue Date event is created on Team Calendars using 2 custom fields for Date Time Picker, if the event is *exactly* 24h, the event does not display the 24h range, instead, it displays only on the start date. If using TC 6.0.47 it shows only on the end date.   !image-2020-08-20-13-55-18-469.png|thumbnail!   h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Setup an applink between Confluence and Jira;  # Create two custom Date Time Picker fields on Jira;  # Set up the first field to 6/30/2020 6:30 PM and the second one to 7/1/2020 6:30 PM;  # On Team Calendars, create an event with the Jira Issue Dates type;  # Select these fields for the start and end date.    h3. Expected Results  The event range appears from 6/30/2020 6:30 PM to 7/1/2020 6:30 PM.    h3. Actual Results  The event range appears only on one of the days. The event start and end date/time changes to show only a day and the checkbox """"All day event"""" is checked.    h3. Workaround  Change one of the fields so the date will be 24h01min.  For example, instead of   {panel}starting date/time = *6/30/2020 6:30PM* and final date/time = *7/1/2020 6:30PM*{panel}   use  {panel}starting date/time = *6/30/2020 6:30PM* and final date/time = *7/1/2020 6:31PM*  {panel}"""

summary: "Page export to PDF fails if temp folder is a symlink "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If the {{<Confluence_Home>/temp}} folder is a symlink to somewhere else PDF exports lead to a Page Not Found error    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence   # symlink {{/temp}} from inside the home folder to somewhere else like follows,     # Export a page as PDF    h3. Expected Results    The page should be exported and downloaded by the browser    h3. Actual Results    Confluence shows a Page Not Found error   !image-2020-08-21-14-33-28-734.png|thumbnail!     h3. *Diagnosis*    Capturing a HAR file will show that PDF export action is redirecting to a URL      Though, this redirected URL is not accessible.      h3. Workaround    The PDF export is still available in the {{<Confluence_Home>/temp}} folder and can be fetched manually. Or, symlink should be removed """

summary: "Not able to apply background colors to row in table using Edge and IE"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Not able to apply background colors to the row in a table using Edge and IE.    For the Edge browser, the row selection is not possible. For the IE browser, it is possible to select the row, but while applying the background colors, it only applies to the last cell of the row.  h3. Environment   * IE 11   * EDGE 44    Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page   # Insert a table.   # Select any row except the header row.   # Choose the background color to fill in the row.    h3. Expected Results    The background applies to the row.  h3. Actual Results    For the IE browser, the background color is only applied to the last cell of the row.    For the Edge browser, it is not possible to select the rows.  h3. Workaround    Use the Chrome browser."""

summary: "Code Macro Throwing ""Can't Find Brush For <language> Error"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary  We are experiencing an issue between the Code Macro block and one of our custom addons after upgrading to Confluence 7.4.1. It seems to only happen when both of the macros are on one page, even when not nested and when they shouldnt be relating to each other.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  1. It happens on new pages as well as old. I created a new page and dropped in a super simple code block with no issues, but as soon as I added our custom Macro, the issue started popping up intermittently.  2. The only setting that seems important here is the Syntax Highlighting, and the error will happen when its set to one of our custom languages. The rest of the options can be blank, as seen in the screenshot.  3. At the moment I see it happening in Chrome and IE, though I dont have Firefox/Edge/Safari to test with.  4. Create a new page. Add in a Code Block using the custom language. Anywhere else on the page, add in the custom macro. This should make the error intermittently appear.    h3. Expected Results  Both of these macros should be able to exist on a page without the browser throwing """"SyntaxHighlighter Cant find brush for:"""" errors    h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:  These errors show up when both macros are on the page.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Confluence not able to render more than 2 page properties macro in a single page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When user try to add more than 2 page properties report macro in a Confluence page, Confluence is only able to render the first 2 page properties report macro and the following macro will render with the following error.    *PS: Tested in Confluence version 7.6. The feature is working fine in version 7.6.*  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Confluence page   # Add 4 page properties report macro   # Save the page   (i) For details, please refer to the following video   [^Recording #19.mp4]    h3. Expected Results    Expect to see 4 page properties report macro render correctly without any error  h3. Actual Results    Only the first 2 page properties report macro is able to render.   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround #1    Add the page properties report macro in another page. Then use the include page macro to display the page properties macro from other pages.  h3. Workaround #2    Set the confluence.masterdetails.maximum.recursion.depth [system property|https://confluence.<USER>doc/configuring-system-properties-168002854.html] to a higher number (defaults to 2) (requires a restart of Confluence)"""

summary: "Sidebar border grows with the size of the sidebar padding."
description: """h3. Summary  The sidebar has a border that grows according to the sidebar size. For instance, if you have a page with a long title, as soon as you move the sidebar so you can read the title, the border also grows.     This border also hides the text, so you need to move the sidebar until the border is no longer hiding the text.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a page with a long title.  # Go moving the sidebar. You will notice a border with the same color of the sidebar(gray).    h3. Expected Results  The border will have a fixed padding/size    h3. Actual Results  The border grows along with the sidebar     h3. Notes  * Screenshots showing the border attached.  * The sidebar also does not have a tooltip showing the title(tracked on https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-31930).Sidebar    h3.Workaround  No workaround  """

Steps to replicate  # Add attached storage format to a page:  [^test_storage_format.txt]  # Edit * n Chrome* (using 'e' shortcut) from *Certificate Management* section  # Observe the location on the page of the editor    h3. Actual results  # Entering the editor you are navigated to roughly a full-screen size above where the Certificate Management heading title should be displayed    h3. summary: "Create button becomes hidden behind search box when Confluence window is resized"

summary: "The Companions does not work correctly when the upgrade fails"
description: """h3. Problem    The Companions app does not work properly when the upgrade fails and the user running the app does not have admin permissions or limited access on the system.    The problem seems to be caused due the user limited access on the system, that causes the upgrade to fail and then the app runs in a bad way. *If change the app to not try to update, the app run just fine. It seems the failed update is causing problems on the app to run after.*    The Companion app does run, but these problems happens:   * The companion app screen is always blank with no information   * Click on the icon on the task bar does not open the pop-up with the options   * The files are downloaded correctly, but the Companion app does not show them, only a blank screen   !Screen Shot 2020-09-01 at 15.55.34.png|thumbnail!    * After saving the file, nothing happens (same blank page on the app)   * The logs shows this error on the start-up:      h3. Environment    * Companion app installed with EXE file  * Windows where the user does not have admin privileges/limited access    h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Install the Companion app   # Try to edit a file   # Save the file   # Nothing happens    h3. Expected Results    The Companion apps works and the files are saved back on Confluence.    h3. Actual Results    The Companions apps does not run correctly and do not save files back on Confluence.  h3. Workaround    Open the file:    <DISK>:\Users\<user>\AppData\Local\atlassian-desktop-companion\app-XXXX\resources\companionConfig.<USER>   Change the:    To:    Save the file and restart the Companion app  h3. Notes"""

description: """CWD-4153 improves performance for fetching memberships, but will also return invalid memberships from the underlying LDAP directory. This can result in warnings in the logs of an application synchronising with a remote Crowd directory:        As a workaround, ensure that any memberships defined in RFC 2307 directories refer to existing users."""

summary: "Significantly Slower Sync to Confluence or JIRA in Crowd 2.8 due to /rest/usermanagement/1/group/membership"
description: """Crowd 2.8's /rest/usermanagement/1/group/membership is significantly slower than earlier versions of Crowd, 2.7 and 2.6 . Here is an example between Crowd 2.6.5 and Crowd 2.8.0 with 50000 users, and 1000 groups, and 1000000+ group memberships (this is with flat group memberships only. The problem is compounded with Nested Group memberships):  # Crowd 2.6.5 took 56s to load /rest/usermanagement/1/group/membership    # Crowd 2.8.0 took around 8 mins to load /rest/usermanagement/1/group/membership:      Both queries return the exact same XML output    h3.Note  This is tested with the same exact user base (via CSV import). It is especially evident with a high user-base ."""

summary: "Crowd does not fire 'Group|UserCreated' nor 'GroupMembersipCreated' events syncing users at login"
description: """Embedded Crowd will try an sync a user from a LDAP or Crowd directory when it tries to login. This means that it is:    # Possible to login before a full directory sync.  # Possible for the right groups to be checked at login.    This can mean that we create users and/or groups or change the membership of groups. However, we don't actually fire {{GroupCreateEvent}}, {{UserCreatedEvent}}, {{GroupMembershipCreatedEvent}}, {{GroupMembershipDeletedEvent}} when we perform these changes. In JIRA we need these events so that we can clear caches that count user limits and caches for permissions.    This is not a problem in the 'Delegated LDAP' directory where {{AutoGroupCreatedEvent}}, {{AutoGroupMembershipCreatedEvent}}, {{AutoGroupMembershipDeletedEvent}} events are fired as relevant."""

Expected Results  Crowd Internal Directory would be deleted from the directories of Crowd.    h3. Actual Results  System error is seen in the browser.    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-crowd.log file:       h3. Notes  Attached pdf of System error seen in the browser.  Also tested in Crowd 2.8.2"""

summary: "Incremental sync fails when adding memberships to an LDAP server via a Remote Crowd"
description: """h3. Steps to reproduce:  * Need an LDAP directory (with some test users and groups) and two crowd servers (A and B)  * Create a Cached Directory in Crowd A pointing to the LDAP directory  * Synchronize Crowd A.  * Create an Application in Crowd A with the LDAP directory in it.  * Create a Remote Crowd directory in Crowd B pointing to the Crowd A Application  * Synchronize Crowd B. It should do a full sync as the first sync.  * Add a group membership to LDAP directory (i.e. add a user to a group).  * Synchronize Crowd A, then Synchronize Crowd B.  * Crowd B should do an incremental sync.    h3. Expected Result:  Incremental Sync for Crowd B succeeds.    h3. Actual Result:  Incremental Sync for Crowd B fails with a MembershipAlreadyExistsException."""

summary: "Unable to import users from Jira"
description: """I'm using Crowd 2.8 to import users from our legacy Jira system into a Crowd internal directory.  The users and groups from Jira are migrated, but the group memberships do not (i.e. the groups in Crowd are empty).  In Crowd's catalina.out I see:    2015-05-13 15:49:56,244 http-bio-8095-exec-4 INFO [crowd.importer.importers.BaseImporter] Importing 1019 users.  2015-05-13 15:50:02,856 http-bio-8095-exec-4 INFO [crowd.importer.importers.BaseImporter] Importing user-to-group member  ships.  2015-05-13 15:50:02,862 http-bio-8095-exec-4 ERROR [crowd.importer.importers.BaseImporter] Could not add memberships for   group 'disaster-planning-committee' as it does not exist in directory with ID: 4096002  com.atlassian.crowd.exception.GroupNotFoundException: Group <disaster-planning-committee> does not exist          at com.atlassian.crowd.dao.group.GroupDAOHibernate.findByName(GroupDAOHibernate.java:158)          at com.atlassian.crowd.dao.group.GroupDAOHibernate.findByName(GroupDAOHibernate.java:46)          at com.atlassian.crowd.directory.AbstractInternalDirectory.findGroupByName(AbstractInternalDirectory.java:783)          at com.atlassian.crowd.directory.AbstractInternalDirectory.findGroupByName(AbstractInternalDirectory.java:64)          at com.atlassian.crowd.manager.directory.DirectoryManagerGeneric.findGroupByName(DirectoryManagerGeneric.java:48  4)          at com.atlassian.crowd.manager.directory.DirectoryManagerGeneric.addAllUsersToGroup(DirectoryManagerGeneric.java  :1323)    ....    I see a """"Could not add memberships for group ..."""" line for (presumably) every group we've imported.    Of course, at the end of the import process, I would expect to have all the users and groups and their memberships represented in the Crowd directory.    Many thanks for allowing us to directly create bugs in your system!"""

summary: "Filter users by accountExpires in AD for uncached directories"
description: """CWD-3789 filters expired users during synchronisation for cached directories. However, for an uncached directory, the filter isn't used.    The same filter should be applied during operations on an uncached directory."""

summary: "Session timeout set to zero (which is invalid) if an invalid session timeout is set on the settings page"
description: """When a user sets an invalid session timeout (say `30a`) on the session configuration page and presses the Update button, the page correctly marks the fields with the message """"The supplied value is invalid."""", however it sets the field value to 0 instead back to the valid value that was present before. Zero as a session timeout is itself invalid adding to the not so nice user experience.    To replicate:  1) Install Crowd 2.8.2. and configure it with all the default values  2) Login as admin  3) Go to the Session configuration screen  4) The session timeout should be set to 30  5) Set it to something invalid, e.g. `30a` and press the Update button  6) The page will say that the value was invalid but will also set the value to 0  7) Hit the Update button again  8) The page will respond that 0 is also an invalid value    Expected behaviour:  Instead of setting the value of the session timeout to 0 after the user entered an invalid value into the field, it should be set to the original valid value (the one shown when the page is loaded)."""

summary: "When adding an application, ""Select..."" can be added as a group"
description: """To replicate:  1) Add an application  2) On the `Authorisation` screen, select `Select...` in the `Directory groups` dropdown and click `Add Group`  3) The """"group"""" gets added which is wrong because it's not a group  4) Add a second, real group  5) Finish creating the application  6) On the `Groups` tab of the application configuration you can see the """"Select..."""" group present, so it was actually added    Expected behaviour:  Cannot add """"Select..."""" as a group to new or existing applications"""

summary: "Group not visible for application configuration when two directories contain the same groupe name"
description: """When configuration the users allowed to access an application in Crowd, there is a little conflict problem.    Context :   * Two or more directories are declared in Crowd  * Two of the directories contain a group with the same name    Example :  * Directory A  ** group FOO  ** group BAR  * Directory B  ** group FOO  ** group TER    We can see that directories A and B contain the same group name FOO.    Depending on the order of directories in the Directories tab of the application configuration, only one group FOO is visible. If Directory A is first, then available group in Groups configuration will be FOO (Directory A). If Directory B is first, only FOO (Directory B) will be visible.    If we want to allow both FOO groups to access the application, Crowd does not allow us to do it.    Could you please allow Crowd to make all groups from allowed directories in Groups configuration, wether they have the same name or not, please ?    Workaround :  If Directory A is first :  * Add Group FOO from Directory A to allowed groups  * Change directories order to put Directory B first in the directories order  * Add Group FOO from Directory B to allowed groups  * Change back directories order    Groups FOO from both directories will be allowed to access the application, and directory order is respected."""

Trying to give it an email address gives an """"You do not have permission to update your profile.""""    Given it's a read only directory, there shouldn't even be an update button."""

summary: "Crowd is shipped with Postgres JDBC4 instead of JDBC41"

If you are using 1.7 or 1.8 then you should use the JDBC41 version.  {quote}  Since Crowd 2.8,  Java 7 and Java 8 is supported, but we still ship with PostgreSQL 9.0 JDBC4     h3.Workaround  Download the JDBC41 driver from http://jdbc.postgresql.org/download.html and replace the existing PostgreSQL driver in _$Crowd_Install/apache-tomcat/lib_"""

summary: "Search for users/groups without a custom attribute is inefficient due to use of NOT IN"
description: """When searching for users who do not have a custom attribute, Crowd generates a HQL query like        The {{NOT IN}} in that query results in slower than necessary execution times.    Rewriting it to use {{NOT EXIST}} is approximately 30% faster according to timings given by postgresql's EXPLAIN ANALYZE, and more than twice as fast if there's a limit of 31 applied (some limit is the case in many real world queries).    """

summary: "NPE when parsing Group entity without attributes"

summary: "Users are synced with the application even if they don't have permissions to login"
description: """Users that does not have permission to login to applications appear in their people directories.    How to replicate the issue:    h3. In Crowd side:  1) Setup a crowd 2.8.x  2) Create """"usera"""" and """"userb"""" in Crowd internal directory  3) Create """"groupa"""" and """"groupb"""" in Crowd internal directory  4) Place the """"usera"""" inside the """"groupa"""" and """"userb"""" inside """"groupb"""" in Crowd   5) Create a Confluence application in Crowd  6) Place the Crowd internal directory in the Confluence application  7) Allow just the """"groupa"""" access to Confluence in Crowd UI    h3. In Confluence side:  8) Go to the administration -> user directories and create a Crowd user directory there  9) Configure the new directory to access the Confluence application setup in Crowd previously  10) Sync Confluence with the new Crowd user directory    h3. Actual behavior  * Confluence shows *all* users in the people directory (""""usera"""" and """"userb"""") even the ones that doesn't have access to the application in Crowd UI. Crowd transfer all users from the configured directories, no matter if they have access to the application or not.    h3. Expected Behavior:  * Confluence would sync just the allowed users in Crowd (""""groupa"""" and """"usera""""). summary: "UserDao.removeAttribute does not keep InternalUser.attributes property up to date"

summary: "PostgreSQL query for Crowd wrong"

The Postgres example is wrong, as for Postgres there is no DATEDIFF function.  See: https://wiki.postgresql.org/wiki/Working_with_Dates_and_Times_in_PostgreSQL     The selection of rows is not narrowed down enough, all attributes get casted to timestamps (which leads to errors if the attribute is a literal).  And the comparision at the end of the where clause cannot work, becuase the difference of two timestamps on Postgres is always an interval.  I amended the query so that it works with Postgres (tested agains postgres 9.3 with latest Crowd):        """

summary: "Enable X-FRAME-Option in HTTP response headers in order to provide clickjacking protection"
description: """Crowd is vulnerable to [Clickjacking|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clickjacking]. That is, it is possible to frame crowd from a page hosted in a different domain and trick the user into performing an action they did not intend to perform, for example changing their display name.    This issue can be addressed by using the [{{X-Frame-Options}}|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clickjacking#X-Frame-Options] header and or through the [CSP frame-ancestors directive| https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clickjacking#Content_Security_Policy]. When fixing this issue we need to ensure that resources that need to be able to be framed are still allowed to be framed, e.g. gadget resources."""

summary: "Technically Incorrect Terminology in Support Documentation"
description: """In the Further Information section of the support documentation page at https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/CROWD/Recovering+your+Console+application+password, we claim that the password is """"encrypted"""" using SHA1. This is technically incorrect and has been pointed out by an external user. SHA1 is a hashing algorithm and is technically different from encryption/decryption. The security team recommends updating the documentation to make this clear, changing the bullet to indicate that the password is """"persisted as a SHA1 hash"""", if this is still the case. Steps to reproduce:  # Connect Bitbucket Server/JIRA/Confluence/FishEye/Crucible to and LDAP user directory  # Create a group, {{groupA}} on LDAP and sync it over  # Grant the group a Global permission  # Rename the group in LDAP.  # Re-synchronize the LDAP directory.  # Verify that the name of the group the users belong to was updated  # Check Global Permission and see the original group name is still listed, and the new group name is not listed.    h5. Workaround:  Manually add the new group name into Global Permission. However this is a problem when the number of group gets larger by time."""

summary: "Previously synchronised users that now have the same username LDAP attribute get cyclically renamed each sync"
description: """This can occur when a configuration of an existing directory is changed, so that the usernames of the users in the directory are no longer unique.    HTR:  * configure an ldap directory (tested with AD2012)  * add two users with different samAccountName ('user1', 'user2'), same mails ('mail')  * set Crowd to us samAccountName as the username attribute, do a full sync  * set Crowd to use mail as the username attribute, do a full sync    Actual results:  * during first sync both users get selected for update (getUsersToAddAndUpdate)  * during first sync 'user1' gets renamed to 'mail' (which is okay), then 'user2' tries to use 'mail' as the username, that one is busy, so it renames 'user1' to 'mail #1' ('forceRenameUser'), and names 'user2' mail  * during next sync the user who's now named 'mail #1' get selected for update (getUsersToAddAndUpdate)  * the user gets renamed to 'mail', but before that the previous user gets renamed to 'mail #2'  * the users get renamed over and over again during subsequent syncs     Expected:  * the directory ends up in a stable state after synchronization"""

summary: "Apache Struts 2 Remote Code Execution (CVE-2017-5638)"
description: """*Description*   Crowd used a version of Struts 2 that was vulnerable to [CVE-2017-5638|https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/WW/S2-045]. Attackers can use this vulnerability to execute Java code of their choice on systems that have a vulnerable version of Crowd.    *Affected versions:*   * All versions of Crowd *from 2.8.3* before *2.9.7* (the fixed version for 2.9.x), from version *2.10.1* before *2.10.3* (the fixed version for 2.10.x) and from version *2.11.0* before *2.11.1* (the fixed version for 2.11.x) are affected by this vulnerability.    *Fix:*   * *Crowd* 2.11.1 is available for download from [https://www.atlassian.com/software/crowd/download].   * *Crowd* 2.10.3 is available for download from [https://www.atlassian.com/software/crowd/download-archive].   * *Crowd* 2.9.7 is available for download from [https://www.atlassian.com/software/crowd/download-archive].     *Hotfix:*  The preferred fix is to upgrade Crowd to a version that's not vulnerable (see the *Fix* section). If you cannot schedule an upgrade immediately, and are using Crowd 2.10 or 2.11, you can replace the affected library as a temporary workaround.    To replace the library:  # Stop Crowd  # Download [struts2-core-2.3.32.jar|https://maven.atlassian.com/content/groups/public/org/apache/struts/struts2-core/2.3.32/struts2-core-2.3.32.jar]  # Remove all existing copies of struts2-core-2.3.29.jar:    # Copy the downloaded struts2-core-2.3.32.jar into each of the lib/ directories you removed the jar from  # Restart Crowd    This temporary solution is provided only for your convenience and an upgrade to an official Crowd release should be scheduled as soon as possible.    For additional details see the [full advisory|https://confluence.atlassian.com/crowd/crowd-security-advisory-2017-03-10-876857916.html]."""

summary: "Updated_date is always updated when an external user logs in"
description: """h3. Summary  Updated_date is always updated when an external user logs in, even if their attributes have not been updated    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Connect Crowd to LDAP  # Run the following with an LDAP username:    # Note the date  # Make sure no user attributes are changed in LDAP for this user  # Log out and back into Crowd  # Run:       h3. Expected Results  Date/timestamp stays the same, as user attributes have not been changed.    h3. Actual Results  The date is updated every time the user logs in. When attempting to replicate this with an internal user however, this date is not updated. So the updated_date is not useful and it will show the user was last updated when they logged in.     h3. summary: "Update Nested Groups in Crowd documentation"
description: """From the following documentation:  * https://confluence.atlassian.com/crowd/nested-groups-in-crowd-153749260.html    Under the title *Presenting Flattened Lists of Users to Integrated Applications* :  {quote}  Integrated applications may ask Crowd for a list of members in a group. Crowd will present all users who are members of the group and all users belonging its sub-groups, consolidated into one list. We call this list a 'flattened' group. This is necessary because many integrated applications do not understand the concept of nested groups. For that reason, Crowd makes the nesting transparent to integrated applications.  {quote}    It's nowhere stated that in order for nested groups to be identified and searched in Crowd side, the application itself (such as Confluence) will need to specify to """"enable nested groups"""". It's quite misleading as readers would think that Nested Group Membership is enabled automatically    It'll be great if we can add that information in that statement, for example:    """"Integrated applications may ask Crowd for a list of members in a group. Crowd will present all users who are members of the group and all users belonging its sub-groups, consolidated into one list. We call this list a 'flattened' group. This is necessary because many integrated applications do not understand the concept of nested groups. For that reason, Crowd makes the nesting transparent to integrated applications. - This is true once Nested Group has been enabled in the respective integrated applications""""  """

summary: "Changes to unrelated application configuration might cause incremental sync to fail and fallback to full synchronization"
description: """HTR:   * set up two applications, app1 with dir1, app2 with dir2   * enable aliasing for app2   * try incremental syncing using app1, all works as expected   * now add a user to dir2, and set an alias for the user    Expected:   * app1 can still perform an incremental sync   * app2 falls back to a full sync (because alias events aren't supported during incremental sync)    Actual:   * both app1 and app2 fall back to full sync, the REST request for events to app1 produces the error:      This is caused by mishandling alias events, and not filtering them when processing, even if they don't apply to the application that's making the request."""

summary: "Users without an userExpired attribute get removed when synchronising ActiveDirectory with the 'Filter out expired users' setting enabled"
description: """When using an ActiveDirectory directory and enabling the 'filter out expired users' checkbox users that are marked as expired should get filtered out, and not be present in the Crowd directory.    However, in some configurations AD might not return the {{userExpired}} attribute in LDAP queries. This will cause users who don't have the attribute to be inadvertently filtered out, and removed from Crowd.    h4. Steps to Reproduce   * Setup Crowd and add AD user directory;   ** For the port, input 3268 for the Global Catalog;   ** Ensure *Filter out expired users*;   * Save and sync;    h4. Expected behavior    Non-expired users are synchronized and appear in Crowd correctly.  h4. Actual Behavior    Crowd filters out all users from that directory, so no users are displayed.  h4. Investigation    (Reference: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-64099)   * When using INCREMENTAL sync with """"Filter out expired users"""", Crowd's ldapsearch includes a check for {{accountExpires}};   * {{accountExpires}} is not stored with Global Catalog (port 3268): [https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms675098(v=vs.85).aspx];   * This causes the search to return no results, causing Crowd to believe no users exist;       h4. Workaround   * Disable """"Filter out expired users""""; or   * Connect to port 389, instead of 3268;     (i) In Crowd versions older than 2.12, this issue only impacts Incremental sync, so disabling incremental sync is a possible workaround for those versions.  """

"Rename user in Active Directory with delegated user directory create new user with same external_id"
description: """h3. Steps to reproduce the issue  # Create a delegated user directory connected to an Active Directory and set it to add to the group confluence-user automatically.   # Assigned the new user directory to an Application (Confluence for example)  # Create a new Crowd user directory on Confluence with """"Read and Write"""" LDAP Permission.   # Login with the user """"admin"""" that existed on Active Directory on Confluence.  # Rename the user """"admin"""" on Active directory with username """"admin1""""  # Logout on Confluence then login again with the new username """"admin1""""  # Then Logout and login on Confluence again    h3. Expected behavior  The user should be able to login correctly.     h3. Actual behavior  The user login failed with the following error message:      h3. Diagnostic:   # There were 2 users with the different username and the same external_id were created on the Crowd, which can be identified by the following query:    # When Confluence is performing the user directory synchronization, the user will be bounced between 2 users account after each synchronization.   # This particular issue is reproduced with Active Directory only.    h3. Workaround solution   We can remove the newly created user via the Crowd UI and rename the old user to the new username."""

summary: "The bundled version of atlassian-rest had a weakness in its Cross-site request forgery protection"
description: """The bundled version of atlassian-rest in Atlassian Crowd before version 2.8.4 and from version 2.9.0 before version 2.9.1 was vulnerable to a Cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability in certain browsers, for example chrome, due to an assumption that non-simple content-types could not be sent cross domain. This allowed remote attackers to bypass the Cross-site request forgery protection implemented in atlassian-rest for rest resources that depended upon consuming non-simple content-types to protect them against CSRF attacks. For more information about the atlassian-rest issue see [https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/REST-339] and https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/REST-343."""

summary: "Crowd remote directory full synchronization may trigger additional ldap query which may significantly increase synch time"
description: """h1. Symptoms    When groups that are synchronized from the remote directory have members that are outside of the [user or group filter / dn|https://confluence.atlassian.com/crowd/restricting-ldap-scope-for-user-and-group-search-169118612.html], Crowd may issue additional query to the remote directory in order to fetch those objects. During full synchronization this additional query is superfluous and does not influence the correctness of membership resolution.  h1. Example    Crowd directory is configured as follows:   * pull groups from dn: ou=Groups   * pull users from dn: ou=Users    LDAP directory group contains members outside of ou=Groups and outside of ou=Users:    In such situation Crowd, during synchronisation, will issue additional query to remote directory in order to fetch cn=bot-account,ou=Automation, which was not fetched before as the group dn configured in Crowd is ou=Groups.    This additional query may become a timing culprit in situation where there are many additional member objects in groups that are outside of the scope configured and when the LDAP server is slow to respond.  h1. Workaround    None at this point"""

summary: "Improve logging when attempting to authenticate using an expired account"
description: """There are multiple suggestions related to the same issue - Remote apps connected to Crowd display an *incorrect* message when the user password has expired in a Crowd Internal Directory.        Since the credentials are expired, rather than incorrect, the application should inform it accordingly.    Even in small organizations, end-users are usually not aware of all the possible systems that manage their accounts. Thus it is time-consuming for Sys Admins to receive requests from a user trying several times to use a valid password, and then figure out what's the real issue, which it's easily resolved once the user inputs their credentials in Crowd console:        Having a better message pointing out what to do, or automatically redirecting the login page to Crowd's """"Change Expired Password"""" page would be a huge improvement.    ----    List of related requests:    https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-39656  https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-66444  https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFSERVER-28819  https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFSERVER-10455  https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CWD-1765  https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CWD-3323"""

summary: "Crowd does not use the user unique identifier attribute when synchronizing duplicate users with different case"
description: """h3. Summary  In a scenario in which a user exists multiple times in an external directory, but each time with a different case in the username - thus having a unique identifier for each instance of the user, Crowd will try to add all instances of the user to the cwd_user table, as expected.  However, due to the constraint we have in the cwd_user table, Crowd will consider only the *lower_user_name + directory_id* to make sure that the user is unique, while in fact, the user is already unique if we consider the *external_id* value and the *user_name* with case sensitive.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a user called *MyUser* in an external LDAP;  # Create a user called *myuser* in the same external LDAP;  # Synchronize the directory in Crowd using a Unique Identifier (e.g. objectGUID if using AD).    h3. Expected Results  Both users should be synchronized in Crowd since they are different users in the LDAP (Unique Identifier proves that).    h3. Actual Results  The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-crowd.log file:  """

Steps to reproduce:   # Create a new directory i.e. OpenLDAP   # Select 'Mange groups locally' while creating the directory   # Create two groups for example - 'Group 1' and 'Group 2'   # Add a remote user from the OpenLDAP into both these groups in Crowd.   # Export XML from the instance   # Try importing this XML."""

summary: "Audit Log Shows Updates to Null/Empty Fields When Using Oracle DB"
description: """h3. Environment    * Crowd 2.12.0+ with external directory  * Oracle DB  * Connected application such as Confluence or BitBucket    h3. Issue    When users from an external directory who have an empty mandatory field (such as email), log in to a connected application, Crowd will add a new entry to the Audit Log showing the email address was updated.  However, no update occurs.  The update shows as changing """" """" to """" """".    h3. Cause    Oracle DBs treat empty strings as nulls, but LDAP treats them as empty string.  So, when comparing the information received from the external directory to what is in the Crowd DB, there is a mismatch between the empty string and null, so Crowd attempts to """"update"""" the field, and adds an entry to the audit log showing it did so."""

summary: "Crowd does not time out Azure AD connection if connection from Crowd to Azure AD locks up"
description: """h2. *Summary of Issue*    When Crowd makes a connection to Azure AD and the connection locks up, the connection does not time out and various threads and the LDAP Sync thread remain permanently stuck.    h2. *Symptoms*    * User and Groups stop syncing from Azure AD into Crowd    The synchronisation status for the User Directory is frozen:     !frozen_sync_status.jpg|thumbnail!     h2. *Expected result*    Any connection failures from *Crowd* (or the *adal4j* library) to Azure AD should time out and retry the connection later.    h2. *Actual result*    The LDAP sync thread initiates the sync and just blocks on the SSL connection (to Azure AD).  Based on the Thread Dumps, it appears that all HTTP threads are also blocked on the on the SSL connection (to Azure AD).    h2. *Diagnosis*    Capture [Thread Dumps|https://confluence.atlassian.com/confkb/troubleshooting-confluence-performance-issues-with-thread-dumps-688882387.html] of the Crowd process.    The LDAP Caesium sync thread will be blocking:        Various other threads will be blocking on SSL waiting for data from Azure AD:                h2. *Work around*    The default time out when connecting to Azure AD is currently infinity so it will just permanently block waiting for data from Azure AD SSL connection.    * Restarting Crowd will allow Crowd to re-initiate connections to Azure AD  """

summary: "Crowd access logs does not log the username"
description: """h3. Summary  The current Crowd documentation for enabling access logs [How do I enable Access Logging for Crowd?|https://confluence.atlassian.com/crowdkb/how-do-i-enable-access-logging-for-crowd-753893970.html/] suggests that adding the {{%\{X-AUSERNAME\}o}} attribute in the Tomcat Valve will record the username in the access logs. However, after testing, this is not working as _documented_ because the header {{%\{X-AUSERNAME\}o}} does not exist in the Crowd requests.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a vanilla Crowd instance  # Enable access logging by following the steps described in this KB [How do I enable Access Logging for Crowd?|https://confluence.atlassian.com/crowdkb/how-do-i-enable-access-logging-for-crowd-753893970.html/]  # Restart Crowd for the changes to take effect  # Access the Crowd login page  # Check the newly created access log file    h3. Expected Results  According to the documentation, the username should be documented with the incoming requests    h3. Actual Results  The username is empty in all the requests     h3. Workaround  There is no workaround at the moment"""

If updating the whole doc is not possible at the moment, I believe we should at least remove the latest versions as it may cause a lot of confusion when using the doc as reference. """

summary: "Upgrade Struts with CVE-2018-11776 fixed"
description: """On August 22, the Apache Software Foundation [announced|https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/WW/S2-057] a remote code execution vulnerability in Struts, called CVE-2018-11776. After investigating, we have determined that Crowd is not affected by this vulnerability. While Crowd do use Struts, it is not configured in a way that would leave it susceptible to this bug. However, as an extra precaution that is in-line with industry best practices, we are updating Struts in Crowd to the latest version."""

summary: "Incremental Sync in Active Directory fails when local groups are enabled"
description: """Currently incremental sync on Active Directory will fail when local groups are enabled, this is a new bug introduced in Crowd 3.2    Steps to Reproduce:   # Have an Active Directory with incremental sync enabled   # Enable Local groups   # Add at least one local group    Full synchronization will complete successfully, but incremental syncs will fail with the following exception in the logs  """

summary: "Crowd Data Center should not allow nodes to have different Crowd versions"
description: """It is possible to have a Crowd Data Center cluster where nodes have different Crowd versions. For instance, you can have a two node cluster where one node is running Crowd 3.1.1, and the other is running Crowd 3.1.2. Our documentation states that the nodes must have the same version, but we should also have startup checks that make sure the nodes are on the same version."""

summary: "User unable to inherit automatically assigned group/permissions under specific scenario"
description: """h3. Summary    Users, when created in remote crowd and automatic assigned group on first login configured, can be locked out of applications if the auto assign group contains the application level permissions to login under certain circumstances.    h3. Environment  Reproduced with  * Crowd 3.1.2  * Fisheye/Crucible 4.6.0  * Bitbucket Server 5.15.0     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure External application (Fisheye/Crucible and Bitbucket were tested) have a group that grants global access. (Fisheye-user, or Bitbucket-user)   # Connect Crowd as remote user directory with the action of """"Automatically assigned groups"""" set to add the group mentioned above respectively   # Add a user under the respective applications   # Synchronize the External application with crowd   ** Confirm user is present in user listing in the external application   # Attempt to login with user on external application    h3. Expected Results  User authenticates to external application, inheriting the necessary group and the ability to login    h3. Actual Results  Results in """"Password incorrect or unknown user"""" error continuously  The group in question is applied to the user but the user's ability to actually login is not present as expected.    !User with group but not access..png|thumbnail!     *Note*  The group """"fisheye-users"""" existed and had global permissions set before the sync.    h3.Workaround  Triggering a Full (not incremental) Sync to Crowd will correct the user attributes, updating the """"no-access"""" to """"Fisheye User"""" or """"Bitbucket User"""" respectively. (This can be done by disabling/re-enabling the Crowd directory within the external application).  A restart of the external application (triggers a full sync on startup)    h5. Prevent the issue  During replication steps, if step 4 is skipped, the issue will not appear as the user will not be present and first time login will work."""

summary: "Inconsistent behavior of findDirectMembersOfGroup depending on primary group setting"
description: """When calling com.atlassian.crowd.directory.RFC4519Directory#findDirectMembersOfGroup with an unresolvable DN an empty list will be returned. However when this is called on a MicrosoftActiveDirectory instance and primary group support is enabled, this will throw javax.naming.NameNotFoundException. While the superclass' method checks for the group being missing, the subclass assumes it exists."""

summary: "Group are created in the directory eventhough the admin has disable the add group permission in the application directory permission page."
description: """h3. Description  When the user has multiple directories in Crowd Application and disables the add group permission for the top directory in the Crowd Application, the group are still created in the top directory when admin add the user from top directory to a group from lower position directory.    *Steps to reproduce*  # Add 2 directory in Crowd.  *Directory 1*  _user 1  user 2  basegroup1_  group2  *Directory 2*  _basegroup2  group3_  # Create a JIRA application in Crowd  # In JIRA application (In Crowd), go to the _Permission_ tab  !applicationdirectorypermission1.png|thumbnail!    !applicationdirectorypermission2.png|thumbnail!    !crowddirectorypermission1.png|thumbnail!    !crowddirectorypermission2.png|thumbnail!   # Disable the _Add Group_ permission and enable the _modify group_ permission for _Directory 1_ and _Directory 2_  # Connect Crowd to JIRA and configure it as _read/write_    !jiradirectory.png|thumbnail!   # Perform synchronization  # In JIRA, try to add the _user2_ from _Directory 1_ to _group3_ from _Directory 2_  # The user is successfully added to _group3_ in JIRA and a new group name _group3_ is created in the _Directory 1_  (i) For details, please review the following video   [^Recording #47.mp4]     Expected behavior  The _group3_ is not created in _Directory 1_    Actual Behavior  _group3_ is created in _Directory 1_"""

summary: "Password expiration reminder is sent to disabled users"
"LDAP hostname containing an underscore is empty when editing existing User Directory details"
description: """h3.Steps to reproduce issue    # Install Crowd 3.3.2  # Add an entry in /etc/hosts that has an underscore character    #* Assuming you have your AD server on 127.0.0.1 (or put the IP address of where AD server is located)  # Add in a new User Directory in Crowd pointing to *my_ad_server*  # This all adds okay (/)     *What happened...*      # When editing an existing LDAP User Directory in Crowd 3.3.2, the hostname entry is blank when the hostname contained an underscore (x)     *What should have happened...*    # The Hostname field should have displayed the Hostname of the LDAP Server even when it contains an underscore      *Root cause*    The LDAP hostname is stored as ldap://<ldap_host>:<port_number> in the single *ldap.url* field in the *cwd_directory_attribute table*.    It appears this is parsed back out into the Front End User Directory screen / *Hostname* and *Port* fields.    When the *ldap.url* contains an underscore in the LDAP hostname, it is not parsed into the front end resulting in a blank hostname field.    *Work around*    Re-enter the hostname of the AD server if you wish to make any changes in order to save the User Directory configuration changes.    *System Impact*    This issue does not appear the synchronisation of the User Directory and only affects the displaying of the User Directory details screen."""

summary: "Crowd silently ignores changes to active status of users in Azure AD."
description: """When Crowd synchronises users for the very first time from Azure AD it will recognise """"active"""" state of users correctly. Unfortunately, on next synchronisations Crowd ignores changes to these statuses, so once they are set, they will never change."""

summary: "Duplicated group names break incremental sync"
description: """In full sync mode, groups with duplicated names are dropped. However they're not dropped in incremental sync. This may cause the incremental sync to fail.    Steps leading to an error:   # Duplicated groups in AD are created.   # Directory in Crowd is created.   # Initially it'll do full sync, but the duplicated names won't be synced.   # Incremental sync will see that the GUIDs of those duplicated groups are missing.   # It'll ask for data since the last full sync. Duplicated groups were created before last sync, so they won't be returned.   # Incremental sync will fail, because it doesn't see the missing GUIDs in the delta results."""

summary: "Crowd does not correctly log abnormal terminations of password expiry mail sending"
description: """Crowd does not log the cause of abnormal password expiry notification email batch job. While this does not apply to all possible ways of abnormal termination, there are numerous ways where the job would be terminated without leaving sufficient information to troubleshoot."""

summary: "Password expiry mail notification sending fails for users with null email"
description: """If any of the users eligible for getting a password expiry notification email have a null email, the password expiry notification sending will fail. Some emails might be sent, depending on the batch in which the users with null emails have appeared, however none of the users from the batch with null users onwards will receive any notifications.    While technically users without emails are invalid data, they shouldn't cause users with correct emails to not receive email notifications about password expiry."""

summary: "Problem with & ampersand in group names"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Group is not found when the group name contains & (ampersand).      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a group name with {{testgroup3 & testgroup4}}  # Sync the group to Crowd  # Go to the {{Group}} page  # Click the group  # Click the {{Direct member}} tabe  # You will notice that the group is changed from {{testgroup3 & testgroup4}} to {{testgroup3}}  # Click to the {{Details}} tab and a error appear.   [^Recording #48.mp4]       h3. Expected Results  No error appears    h3. Actual Results  Group no found error appear    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Password expiry mail notifications are sent to inactive users"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When sending password expiry reminder emails Crowd does not take into account the """"active"""" flag of the user and sends emails to inactive users as well  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure password expiry email notifications   # Ensure there's an inactive user within the notification period    h3. Expected Results    The user shouldn't get an email because they're inactive. As inactive users cannot log in, reminding them that their password will expire does not make much sense.  h3. Actual Results    The user is not notified.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Password expiry mail notifications are sent to users with expired passwords"
description: """h4. Problem Statement  'Password will expire' notification is sent instead of the expired password with '- days'     h4. Steps to reproduce  # Go to Directories > Select the 'Directory' > *Configuration*  # Set the details as below:   !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!   # Go to a test user account >*Attributes  # Set the 'passwordLastChanged' to 24th Dec 2018 as below:   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h4. Actual Outcome:  'Password will expire' notification sent to the user eventhough the password has expired (with '-' days to expire):   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     h4. Expected Outcome  Notifications must not be sent out for expired passwords."""

summary: "Crowd does not support diacritics when using MSSQL"
description: """Users with diacritics. E.g. KRŮČEK are stored without diacritics Krucek when using MSSQL, even with SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CS_AS collation.     !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!               But, if we're using Mysql, utf-8 collation, it working just fine:     !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!       h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Some special characters in group names are preventing viewmembers.action to work properly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Special Characters such as hashtag (#), ampersand (&), or percentage (%) in group names are not encoded properly and it will break functions such as {{viewmembers.action}}.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a brand new Crowd version 3.3.3  # Create a group name {{a&b}}  # View the group  #* The URL is {{http://localhost:8095/crowd/console/secure/group/view!execute.action?name=a%26b&directoryID=163841}}, you'll see that the ampersand (&) is being encoded properly to {{%26}}  # Click on """"Direct Member""""  #* The expected URL is {{http://localhost:8095/crowd/console/secure/group/viewmembers.action?groupName=a&b&directoryID=163841}}, you'll see that the ampersand (&) is NOT being encoded properly to {{%26}}    h3. Expected Results  Crowd will display group member of group *a&b*    h3. Actual Results  Crowd display group member of group *a* instead because of the URL encoding issue.    The below exception is thrown in the {{atlassian-crowd.log}} file:    (i) If the {{groupName}} argument in the URL is not encoded properly, {{directoryID}} argument will not be able to get through which result in the error above.        h3. Workaround  Manually modify the un-encoded character in the URL.    For example, the following URL is broken because the ampersand (&) is NOT being encoded properly to {{%26}}  * http://localhost:8095/crowd/console/secure/group/viewmembers.action?groupName=a&b&directoryID=163841    Change the ampersand (&) to {{%26}} like so:  * http://localhost:8095/crowd/console/secure/group/viewmembers.action?groupName=a%26b&directoryID=163841    You should now be able see the members of *a&b* group now."""

summary: "Insufficient Session Expiration of user sessions - CVE-2018-20238"
description: """Various rest resources in Atlassian Crowd before version 3.2.7 and from version 3.3.0 before version 3.3.4 allow remote attackers to authenticate using an expired user session via an insufficient session expiration vulnerability."""

The issue can happen when JVM tries to read from OS-level sockets while it was in the closed state.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    Has not been reproduced yet.  h3. Expected Results  We could have some re-trying mechanism that prevents this.  h3. Actual Results      h3. Workaround    No workaround available. """

summary: "XML backup trims duplicate spaces from group names"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a group has 2 (or more) spaces in a row in its name, the XML Backup and automated backup will trim those extra spaces down to 1. This could result in duplicate groups as well as groups not matching downstream applications  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Crowd   # Set up two groups (group names below are using - to represent a space character for ease of reading):   ** {{test-group}}   ** {{test--group}}   # Run a Backup of Crowd   # Try to restore the backup to the same or a different Crowd instance    h3. Expected Results    The import/restore will succeed.  h3. Actual Results    The import/restore will fail due to duplicate group names, OR the import will succeed but the group name will have changed.    *If the import fails due to duplicates:*    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-crowd.log file:    Another error will also be thrown indicating a violation of a unique key constraint has happened. Eventually, we will then see:    In the XML file generated by the backup, we can see the extra space was removed:    h3. Notes    Extra spaces seem to only be possible in between words. Crowd will not allow the creation of groups with leading or trailing spaces.  h3. Workaround  h4. Option 1 - Remove The Group (if unused)  # Run the below DB query to get a list of all groups that have 2 (or more) spaces in their name:    # Check to see if any users are assigned to this group, using the {{id}} returned in the previous query:    # If no results are returned, then you can safely delete this group and re-run the export. If results are returned, then the group may be in use. Check downstream applications (Jira, Confluence, etc) to see if permissions or access is based on membership in this group. If so, proceed to Option 2    h4. Option 2 - Manually Modify the XML File   # Open the XML file and do a search for the group ID (found in the first query above). You will need to manually change the group name for every entry in a {{<group>}} or {{<membership>}} parent tag.   (!) Do not do a global find/replace, as if you have duplicate directories, it will change both, and the issue will still exist   # Save the file, and re-run the import.   # Groups (with extra spaces) will be imported now."""

summary: "Crowd dead-locks under heavy load"
description: """h3. Summary  Crowd can deadlock under heavy load when sending requests using application/xml response types. The Crowd Client is hardcoded to use application/xml response types.     h3. Notes  This is a known issue with Jersey: [Stuck threads under heavy load · Issue #3460 · jersey/jersey · GitHub|https://github.com/jersey/jersey/issues/3460]    h3. Solution  Switch from application/xml response type to application/json in the Crowd Client."""

summary: "Updating Data Center License Documentation"
description: """h2. Summary  There's no public facing documentation instructing how to update Crowd Data Center license when it expired.    h2. Why is this Needed  Suppose a Cluster of 4 nodes, where _crowd.cfg.xml_ is present with License key in both Local Home and Shared Home, does updating an expired Data Center License through Crowd UI updates all nodes?    Does one needs to restart all nodes for the changes to take effect? Is there a need of manual intervention to update _crowd.cfg.xml_ on Local Home directories of nodes or updating through the UI updates them all?    h3. Workaround  Follow [Moving to Crowd Data Center|https://confluence.atlassian.com/crowd/moving-to-crowd-data-center-935372358.html], apply the license through the UI and restart all nodes for them to pick-up the needed new license.    If by any reason this doesn't works, open _crowd.cfg.xml_ on all nodes and ensure that they all have the new license key listed under the following property:  """

summary: "Improve ""Configuring your Mail Server"" document"
description: """According to [Crowd 3.3 Release Notes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/crowd/crowd-3-3-release-notes-958762272.html]:  {quote}  *Multiple server alert addresses*  From now on, Crowd lets you configure multiple server alert email addresses, so whenever some alert occur, every of these emails receive notification.   {quote}  (which relates to [CWD-5018|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CWD-5018])    However, the document [Configuring your Mail Server|https://confluence.atlassian.com/crowd/configuring-your-mail-server-17956965.html] still mentions *Server alert email* as a field that could only receive one e-mail address. The screenshot must also be updated.    It is also unclear about how should the e-mail addresses be separated in the field (comma, semi-colon, etc)."""

Environment  Crowd 3.4.4    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure mail server, test and apply changes.  # Then configure """"Subject prefix""""  # Apply changes and reload the page.    h3. Expected Results  Same data will be visible.    h3. Actual Results  Only """"Subject prefix"""" input field is empty.    h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "crowd.polling.startdelay JVM parameter is not respected"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The is a JVM parameter in Crowd, {{-Dcrowd.polling.startdelay}}, that should delay the sync of any external directories upon startup by the number of miliseconds specified in the JVM parameter.    This was working until Crowd 2.12.0.  Since then, this parameter only effects the start of the first sync once the directory is enabled.  h3. Environment   * Crowd 2.12.0 or later   * Remote directory such as LDAP or AD    h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Start Crowd   # Add the remote directory   ## Set the sync interval to 10 minutes   ## You can see the directory will sync every 10 minutes   # Stop Crowd   # Add the JVM parameter, and set it to 900000ms (15 minutes)   # Start Crowd    h3. Expected Results    The next sync should take place at least 15 minutes after start-up.  This is the case in Crowd versions pre 2.12.0  h3. Actual Results    The sync will continue at its normal pattern, every 10 minutes, based off the last successful sync time.  h3. Notes    It appears, in 2.12.0 and later, that this parameter is only respected when adding a new directory.  For example:   * When adding a new directory in Crowd, Crowd should automatically sync it within a few minutes of the directory being added.   * If the JVM parameter is set, Crowd will respect that delay, and only sync once the delay has expired.    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available     """

summary: "Missing 2x/3x versions of empty state/error images on group direct members view"
description: """h3. Description    There are no 2x/3x versions of the empty state  and error images in the """"Direct members"""" view of a group when viewing a group in a directory with nested groups disabled. This results in a missing image being shown.    h3. Workaround    No workaround is necessary"""
