Stopping the Server instance  2019-08-16 17:35:00,235 localhost-startStop-1 INFO [com.atlassian.crowd.startup] Starting Crowd Server, Version: 3.4.5 (Build:#1070 - 2019-05-23) in Data Center mode  {code}     # After some time node is automatically removed    h3. Expected Results    There shouldn't be duplicate nodes present in the configuration and clustering page  h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:    h3. Workaround    Required, if there is no workaround please state:   A sufficient time of around 60 seconds between start and stop doesn't replicate the problem"""

summary: "Calculating licenses for users with empty external ids fails"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When calculating license usage for users that have empty external ids the calculation will fail on the application's side with a NullPointerException.    This can typically happen in directories with a nullable external id attribute by definition or when the external id attribute was misconfigured. Internal directories generally should not be affected by this issue as default external ids are generated for those directories.    While Crowd does augment external ids provided to applications, it will relay a null or empty external id as a null, so the issue can occur both when the directory is defined in Jira/Confluence/BBS as well as when it's a directory defined in the Crowd defined for that application.       h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Setup a directory which has at least one user with an empty external id in Jira/Confluence/BBS   # Setup license monitoring for that application    h3. Expected Results    The license usage is calculated.  h3. Actual Results    License calculation fails on the connected application's side with an exception like this one:      h3. Workaround    If the users are not necessary in the application, they can be removed either manually or by configuring the directory in a way that would filter them out. If they are necessary and the directory is external then check if the attribute used for external id is non-nullable and unique within that directory. While the issue generally should not occur for internal directories the external_id column in the cwd_user table could be populated manually for those users."""

summary: "Changing license to one with user limit lower than current number of users you have results with losing access to Crowd"

Expected Results    Crowd won't accept this license and show an error about that the license you're providing has a user limit lower than current number of users you have.    This is how it works when you provide an expired license. The behaviour in this case should be similar.  h3. Actual Results    License key is accepted. You lose access to Crowd.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Updating Crowd DC license doesn't reflect on the other nodes"
description: """When using Crowd Datacenter, changing the license on one node will not refresh on the other nodes automatically even after waiting several hours.    The license is updated correctly on the shared_home/crowd.cfg.xml file.    h4. Expected behavior    Refreshing the licence page on each node should indicate the updated license in the UI.    h4. summary: "Email subject prefix doesn't apply to password expiration reminder"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  While CWD-5401 is preventing the admin from adding an email subject prefix. However, if the email subject prefix is already been added to Crowd, it will not show in the password expiration reminder. Emails such as manual password reset or forgot username email *do* contain the configured subject prefix.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Configure email template for password expiration reminder (optional)  * Configure mail server  * Configure an arbitrary email subject prefix  * Set a reminder interval for password expiration reminders  * Wait for an email    h3. Expected Results  The subject prefix is showing in the password expiration reminder email    h3. Actual Results  The subject prefix is NOT showing in the password expiration reminder email    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "System properties to disable analytics and the connection to marketplace are not picked up by Crowd"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    It is not possible to disable those features through the following system properties on Crowd:        They are documented here:    * [Disabling analytics information sharing|https://confluence.atlassian.com/upm/configuring-marketplace-connectivity-306350947.html]  * [Problems Connecting to the Atlassian Marketplace|https://confluence.atlassian.com/upm/problems-connecting-to-the-atlassian-marketplace-273875718.html]    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Edit *<Crowd-Install>/apache-tomcat/bin/setenv.sh*  # Add the following system properties:    # Start Crowd    h3. Expected Results    Marketplace connection and analytics are disabled after startup.    h3. Actual Results    Both features remain enabled.    h3. Workaround    Disable those features through the UI after startup:    * Marketplace connection: *Cog Icon > Manage Apps > Settings*  * Anatytics: *Cog Icon > Analytics*"""

summary: "If the Marketplace is unreachable, UPM causes Crowd to hang"
description: """h3. Summary    Crowd administration panel can take longer to respond if the marketplace is unreachable.    h3. Environment    * Crowd 3.7  * UPM 3.0.7    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Add entry in */etc/hosts* to simulate marketplace being unreachable:    # Try to visit the Manage Apps page, notice it is trying to reach the marketplace  # Try to click on other items of the admin panel before the request to the marketplace times out    h3. Expected Results    Pages load quickly.    h3. Actual Results    If the thread is still trying to reach the marketplace, the menus will not load until the request times out:      The browser tab will hang if you click on any other item in Crowd in the time frame between the messages above (marketplace request and timeout).    h3. Notes    This bug is similar to the following one which affects Confluence:    * https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFSERVER-58927    h3. Workaround    In Crowd, disable connectivity to Marketplace:    * Cog Icon >> General Configuration >> Manage Apps >> Settings >> Uncheck the 'Connect to the Atlassian Marketplace' box, then click 'Apply'    Or, configure an outbound proxy as the Internet gateway for Crowd so the Marketplace address can be reached:    * [How to Configure Outbound HTTP and HTTPS Proxy for your Atlassian application|https://confluence.atlassian.com/kb/how-to-configure-outbound-http-and-https-proxy-for-your-atlassian-application-834000120.html]"""

summary: "Importing Users from JIRA using the Atlassian Importer to a Delegated Directory in Crowd does not import nested groups"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When admins use the Atlassian Importer feature to import users and groups from Jira to a delegated user directory in Crowd, the users and groups get imported however, only the group-user memberships are imported. Group-group memberships are not imported.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In Jira create nested groups and assign users to these groups.    # In Crowd configure a delegated directory and set the *Use nested groups* option   # In Crowd head over to *Users > Import Users > Atlassian Importer*   # Configure the import options and assign the users/groups to the delegated directory.   # Import    h3. Expected Results    All the users, and groups with all their memberships are imported.   h3. Actual Results    Users and groups are imported but only the group-user memberships are imported. Group-group memberships are not.  h3. Workaround    No workaround at the moment"""

summary: "Importing Users from JIRA using the Atlassian Importer to a Internal Directory in Crowd does not import nested groups and user memberships"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When admins use the Atlassian Importer feature to import users and groups from Jira to a internal user directory in Crowd, the users and groups get imported however, but their memberships do not. This case is seen when the ***Use nested groups* option in the internal directory configuration is set.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In Jira create nested groups and assign users to these groups.    # In Crowd configure an internal directory and set the *use nested groups* option.    # In Crowd head over to *Users > Import Users > Atlassian Importer*   # Configure the import options and assign the users/groups to the internal directory.   # Import    h3. Expected Results    All the users, and groups with all their memberships are imported.   h3. Actual Results    Users and groups are imported but their memberships are not. group-user and group-group memberships are not imported.   h3. Workaround    No workaround at the moment"""

summary: "During an active session,you can't change your password if it gets expired"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    During an active session, you can't change your password if it gets expired  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1) Login Crowd through admin and set expiry day as 1   2) Changed the local time of machine to 2 min before the expiry time   3) Login with a user in the crowd   4) Wait for 2 min to get over and try to change the password.   5) You will get an invalid password response even if the previous password you entered is correct.   6) Log and try to login again, I got the message that your password is expired and then you are able to change the password  h3. Expected Results    We should be able to change the password if we are already logged in and the password gets expired  h3. Actual Results  Invalid password    h3. Workaround   You would need to logout so that you get a password expiry window to change your password"""

summary: "Crowd Azure AD Group Filtering add non Filter Group after a successful user Login"
description: """h3. Steps to Replicate   # Configure Crowd with *Azure Active Directory*   ** [Configuring Azure Active Directory|https://confluence.atlassian.com/crowd/configuring-azure-active-directory-935372375.html]   # Choose *specific groups from Azure AD* and add the groups to the Crowd *Group Filtering*.   # *Synchronise* the directory.   # Add Crowd directory in any Atlassian Application (tested with *Confluence*)   # Allow *only specific group* to authenticate to the application.   # *Synchronise* the directory.   # Get one of the user from *Crowd (Azure Directory)* to *Login* into *Confluence*.    h3. Expected Behaviour    Only *specific groups from Azure AD* that is part of the *Group Filtering* added into Confluence and Crowd.  h3. Actual Behaviour    If the user belongs to any other group in Azure AD (*not* part of the groups in *Group Filtering*) additional Group is added into *Confluence and Crowd* after the user *Login* into Confluence (or Crowd integrated application).   * [^crowdAzure.mp4]    List of Groups in Azure AD   * !azureGroup.png|thumbnail!    (i) *Synchronising* the *Azure AD directory in Crowd* helps to remove the groups temporarily until the next user login.  h3. Workaround    In order to mitigate the issue admins can disable membership synchronisation during user log in. Adding groups during log in can be disabled. Here are the steps to apply the workaround:   # Check the directory ID of the Azure AD directory:      Please note that directory name is case sensitive. This query will result with ID you need in next step.          2. Disable membership synchronisation during user log in:    Where here_put_dir_id is the directory ID from step 1.    The workaround doesn't require restart of Crowd.  h4. Drawbacks of the workaround:   * Memberships won't be synchronised during user log in anymore. This means that when you add a user to group in Azure AD you'll have to sync the directory in Crowd. Synchronisation needs to be scheduled to run reasonably often."""

summary: "Adding a logging package to ""Logging and Profiling"" may not show the package after adding but it does still take effect"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Adding a logging package to """"Logging and Profiling"""" may or may not show the added package but it will still take effect. If it shows after adding and you navigate away from the page and back, the package will disappear but will remain in effect.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to Logging and Profiling   # Add {{com.mchange.v2.c3p0}} and set to DEBUG    h3. Expected Results    You will see {{com.mchange.v2.c3p0}} in the list of logging packages and be able to navigate away from the screen, come back and still see it.  h3. Actual Results    The {{com.mchange.v2.c3p0}} sometimes visually shows up after adding, sometimes not but the change seems to have still taken effect.    If it shows up in the list and you navigate to a different page and return to Logging and Profiling, the added package will disappear but the intended DEBUG logging will remain in effect until you click """"Revert to Default""""  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available.    Click """"Revert to Default"""" to remove the added package which cannot be seen."""

summary: "Crowd Password History Doesn't Work Correctly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Crowd Passwrd History Doesn't Work Correctly  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Crowd does not behave correctly when changing a password based on the stored password history count(13 was set for this case). The Password History Count is not used as the basis for the assessment when a user tries to use his initial password again after first change.    Here is a sample process:    1. Create a new user in crowd and enter a valid, new 15-digit pw as the initial password (Start#12345678!)  -> OK  2. Change the user's password in crowd with the same pw from point 1 (Start#12345678!)  -> does not work, which is OK  3. Change the user's password in the crowd with another valid pw (Start#12345678w)  -> OK  4. Change password again with the identical pw from point 1 (Start#12345678!)  -> password can be changed, which is NOT OK    h3. Expected Results    Crowd should not allow to use an old password based on history count.  h3. Actual Results    User is able to set old password.  h3. Workaround       Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "afw.table.Schema.getNames returns unordered python set"
description: """afw.table.Schema's getNames method returns an ordered set in C++ but an unordered set in python. It should probably return a list in python so that the ordering is consistent with iterating through the Schema."""

summary: "afw.table.Schema equality operator only compares keys"
description: """afw.table.Schema's equality operator only checks that keys are in the same type and order, which means that schemas with different field names and units are considered equal. Although this is documented, it is a rather surprising default."""

summary: "Cannot apply crosstalk in Gen 3 DECam processing"
description: """{{IsrTask}} delegates complex crosstalk corrections to {{CrosstalkTask}}. However, the Gen 3 call to {{CrosstalkTask}} is [disabled|https://github.com/lsst/ip_isr/blob/5673ca6e3699f4db46772b3e01c59e44131b93bf/python/lsst/ip/isr/isrTask.py#L870-L874], citing DM-17169. Because {{CrosstalkTask}} does not run, Gen 3 calls to {{IsrTask}} will fail unless the configuration includes {{doCrosstalk=False}}.    Please make it possible to run {{CrosstalkTask}} in Gen 3 and re-enable it."""

summary: "Cannot do linearity corrections in Gen 3 DECam processing"
description: """{{IsrTask}} has code to construct a linearizer ({{ip.isr.linearize.LinearizeBase}}) from Butler datasets. However, this code cannot be used at present in Gen 3 because:  * {{IsrTask}} does not have a {{PrerequisiteInput}} for linearizer data  * it is not clear where this data should come from in a new DECam repository. See discussions on {{#dm-middleware}} on [March 16|https://lsstc.slack.com/archives/C2JPT1KB7/p1584401758440300] and [March 23|https://lsstc.slack.com/archives/C2JPT1KB7/p1584988007111300]. (In Gen 2, the linearizer is a virtual dataset handled by the {{CameraMapper}}, but everybody agrees that in Gen 3 it should be stored in the repository proper.)    Please provide a way to make the linearizer data available in Gen 3 DECam repositories, both in from-scratch repositories (see DM-21862) and in repositories converted from Gen 2."""

summary: " Cannot load refcats in Gen 3 DECam processing"
description: """Attempting to run the Gen 3 single-frame pipeline as described in [DM-23616|https://jira.lsstcorp.org/browse/DM-23616?focusedCommentId=240326#comment-240326] leads to {{AstrometryTask}} crashing with the error """"No reference tables could be found for input region"""". Running with {{--show graph}} reports that no photometric or astrometric reference catalog shards are included in the quanta for {{CalibrateTask}}. However, they can be retrieved from the Butler using {{Registry.queryDatasets}} (only tested with no data ID constraints).    The working hypothesis from [#dm-middleware|https://lsstc.slack.com/archives/C2JPT1KB7/p1585010272157800] is that this is a problem in ingestion (i.e., in {{convert_gen2_repo_to_gen3.py}}), so I'm filing this as a bug in {{obs_base}}.    Please fix the conversion\(?) so that a repository created as described in DM-23616 can correctly match catalogs to image data IDs."""

summary: "Qserv fails to JOIN result sets of two (or many) tables which have a non-trivial overlap in column names"
description: """The latest version of Qserv is unable to handle result sets of a query representing a {{JOIN}} of two (and, presumably, many) tables. Here is an example of such query launched at NCSA agains the Qserv instance {{lsp-int}}:    This query fails due to the following complain:  {code}  ERROR 4110 (Proxy) at line 1: Query processing error: QI=569202: Failed to instantiate query: Error creating table:result_569202: Error applying sql: Error 1060: Duplicate column name 'qserv_trans_id' Unable to execute query: CREATE TABLE result_569202 (`jobId` INT(9),`qserv_trans_id` INT(11),`solution_id` BIGINT(20),`designation` TEXT,`source_id` BIGINT(20),`random_index` BIGINT(20),`ref_epoch` DOUBLE,`ra` DOUBLE,`ra_error` DOUBLE,`dec` DOUBLE,`dec_error` DOUBLE,`parallax` DOUBLE,`parallax_error` DOUBLE,`parallax_over_error` FLOAT,`pm  {code}  *NOTE* that the complain is about a special column *{{qserv_trans_id}}*. There is another column *{{source_id}}* present in both tables.  A workaround for this problem was to _explicitly_ specify columns of interest:  {code:sql}  SELECT source_id,lc.transit_id,lc.band,lc.time,lc.mag,lc.flux,lc.flux_error,    lc.flux_over_error,lc.rejected_by_photometry,lc.rejected_by_variability,    lc.other_flags,lc.solution_id  FROM gaia_dr2_01.gaia_source s,       gaia_dr2_01.light_curves lc  WHERE s.source_id=lc.source_id AND s.source_id=4038784423947097344;  {code}  This query was successful returning a desired result set:  {code:sql}  +---------------------+-------------------+------+--------------------+---------+---------+------------+-----------------+------------------------+-------------------------+-------------+--------------------+  | source_id           | transit_id        | band | time               | mag     | flux    | flux_error | flux_over_error | rejected_by_photometry | rejected_by_variability | other_flags | solution_id        |  +---------------------+-------------------+------+--------------------+---------+---------+------------+-----------------+------------------------+-------------------------+-------------+--------------------+  | 4038784423947097344 | 17493695788622223 | G    | 1713.2098570466237 | 15.2095 | 15543.3 |    44.1311 |         352.207 |                      0 |                       0 |        4097 | 369295551293819386 |  | 4038784423947097344 | 17497788065945061 | G    | 1713.2838568094814 | 15.2007 |   15670 |    31.8942 |         491.312 |                      0 |                       0 |           1 | 369295551293819386 |  | 4038784423947097344 | 19395005121172660 | G    | 1747.5907481256177 | 14.1615 | 40807.5 |    41.8696 |         974.634 |                      0 |                       0 |           1 | 369295551293819386 |  ...  {code}"""

summary: "Enable running ci_hsc_gen3 contents on AWS"
description: """Title might not summarize the end goal in the best way so: this issue collects bugs and improvements I plan on making in order to be able to execute the same list of commands that are execute when ci_hsc_gen3 is scons'd. This boils down to to making the necessary changes such that the following list of commands execute correctly, given the stated conditions, irregardless of whether the commands can be executed via a scons flag or not:    If provided a correct butler.yaml, AWS credentials, a S3 Bucket as repo root and an connectable RDS instance  {code:bash}makeButlerRepo.py --override s3://bucketname/ -c butler.yaml  registerInstrument.py s3://bucketname/  makeGen3Skymap.py s3://bucketname/ -C ~/ci_hsc_gen3/configs/skymap.py skymaps  ingestExternalData.py s3://bucketname/ ci_hsc_gen3/resources/external.yaml  pipetask run -d """"patch = 69"""" -b s3://bucketname/       -i calib/hsc,raw/hsc,masks/hsc,ref_cats,skymaps,shared/ci_hsc       -o shared/ci_hsc_output       --register-dataset-types       -p ci_hsc_gen3/pipelines/CiHsc.yaml    This stopped being the case when S3Datastore was implemented.  h2. *2. Improvements*  h3. *a. ingestExternalData.py script in ci_hsc_gen3 hard-codes """"symlink"""" transfer type*    Which is fine in context of **ci_hsc_gen3  scons but makes no sense in non-POSIX compliant cases. Add a flag to [ingestExternalData.py|https://github.com/lsst/ci_hsc_gen3/blob/master/bin.src/ingestExternalData.py#L41] that defaults to symlink but allows other transfer types to be specified.  h3. *b. makeButlerRepo.py creates a """"."""" key when a fresh S3 Bucket is targeted*    Does not break functionality but shouldn't happen. The """"."""" alias for absolute path shouldn't appear as a Key in the data repository root. Investigate and fix.  h2. *3. Extra goals*   * get a scons flag that wraps all the above instead of an external script or individual commands   * Add a way to turn checksums off. Talking to [~<USER> brought to my attention that this flag does not seem to be added to butler.yaml when DAF_BUTLER_CONFIG_PATH is set, but does if -c is explicitly provided. I suspect --override is missing/not applied in one case but is in the other. In any case if I want to get scons -aws (point directly above) then I'll have to mess with the SConfigure anyhow so I might as well investigate.   * See if timings for individual steps can be added as a flag to scons. Potentially useful but not reported by current scons is the timing breakdown of individual commands."""

summary: "HSC defects_visit table has unexpected filter column"
description: """While working on DM-24259, I performed automated Gen 2 ingest of an HSC dataset in the following order:  # Call {{obs.subaru.ingest.HscIngestTask}} on the raws, with the {{obs_subaru}} config overrides  # Call {{pipe.tasks.ingestCalibs.IngestCalibsTask}} on the calibs, with the {{obs_subaru}} config overrides  # Call {{lsst.pipe.tasks.ingestCuratedCalibs.IngestCuratedCalibsTask}} on the defects in {{obs_subaru_data}}, with the {{obs_subaru}} config overrides    This led to the following error on defects ingest:      Investigation revealed that the table had columns for date and filter, but the {{obs_subaru}} config for {{IngestCuratedCalibsTask}} expected the {{defects_visit}} table to only have a date column. [~<USER> speculates that the table was created during calib ingest (which is configured to have its {{*_visit}} tables be (date, filter)). However, if this is the case then it's not clear how ingestion passes when building {{obs_subaru}}; there are no instrument-specific config files for ingestion.    Given that defects should not be filter-dependent, this appears to be a bug in HSC ingestion. Investigate the issue to confirm the cause, and fix. One plausible option is to create the defects-related registry tables only when ingesting defects, not calibs."""

summary: "Freezing a config locks the registry(ies) of other instances of that config"
description: """Config field instances that correspond to a {{RegistryField}} have an internal member, {{_field}}, pointing back to the original field. {{_field}} points to the same object for all config instances corresponding to the same config. This causes a rare bug where calling {{ConfigChoiceField.freeze}} locks the registry for all config instances using that {{RegistryField}}, including instances that did not exist at the time of the call. See my [post on #dm|https://lsstc.slack.com/archives/C2JPL2DGD/p1586539537027000] for a specific example.    Please change the code so that independently created config instances no longer affect each other. This issue appears to be a side effect of the fix for DM-17757, and may be difficult to fix without introducing still other side effects."""

summary: "Fix non-normalized matching kernel case in the decorrelation afterburner"
description: """In DM-24371, the implemented afterburner correction implicitly assumes that the _matching kernel_ is sum == 1 which is not usually the case, especially without scaling the science and reference image values by their photometric calibration.   * The afterburner correction does not sum to 1, which changes the fluxes of the difference image (or shift its photometric scale).   * The variance plane calculation of the afterburned difference image is scaled to the level of var1+var2 which is incorrect if the matching kernel contains flux rescaling, i.e. not sum == 1.   * The same consideration is required to support a _preconvolution_ kernel, though it needs further investigation to determine the statistical meaning of an _afterburned_ _preconvolved_ image.   * Also, this causes the corrected PSF not to be automatically sum == 1, though this is corrected by the {{KernelPsf}} creation."""

summary: "Gen 2->3 conversion of DECam repositories can give duplicate defects"
description: """If I convert a DECam Gen 2 repository containing defects, the conversion succeeds, but running {{ProcessCcd}} on the result gives an error:    The immediate cause is that one copy of the defects is from the Gen 2 repository, the other copy was ingested during the conversion.    Discussion on [#dm-middleware|https://lsstc.slack.com/archives/C2JPT1KB7/p1590719387081900] suggests that this is due to conflicting responsibilities:  * {{obs.base.Instrument}} has some [hard-coded definitions|https://github.com/lsst/obs_base/blob/a51665517dd0ecacd8d72fbcead186475fcf66d3/python/lsst/obs/base/instrument.py#L38-L45] of curated files, which are processed any time {{ConvertRepoConfig.doWriteCuratedCalibrations}} is set. Defects are included.  * {{ConvertRepoConfig}} also has a [{{curatedCalibrations}} field|https://github.com/lsst/obs_base/blob/7c071951e367c4bef83840b75921f3a2f3af29cb/python/lsst/obs/base/gen2to3/convertRepo.py#L252-L263], which presumably allows other datasets to be handled using {{writeCuratedCalibrations}}. This list does not include defects by default.  * HSC [adds defects to {{ConvertRepoConfig.curatedCalibrations}}|https://github.com/lsst/obs_subaru/blob/c641360ad764cf8db63ecf1d14af6c93660160ff/config/hsc/convertRepo.py#L35-L36]. The bug described above *does not* occur.  * DECam makes no changes to {{curatedCalibrations}}, and its [config override file|https://github.com/lsst/obs_decam/blob/8567ae41add358da7b9af65fc81c39e39078b975/config/convertRepo.py] makes no mention of defects. The bug *does* occur.    The above behavior implies that DECam repositories with defects cannot be converted using the default configs, which goes against our general aim to provide working defaults for all Tasks.    Possible solutions:  * Do away with {{ConvertRepoConfig.curatedCalibrations}}, as proposed by [~<USER>. However, this does raise the question of how to handle observatory-specific curated calibrations.  * Have DECam explicitly register defects in {{curatedCalibrations}}, as HSC does. May be hard to scale to other instruments.  * Duplicate the dataset types hardcoded into {{Instrument}} in the default {{ConvertRepoConfig.curatedCalibrations}}. Won't benefit configs that overwrite this field instead of appending to it."""

summary: "Some reST documents' rendering do not appear consistent with documentation"
description: """In the course of developing DMTN-136, I observed a couple of inconsistencies between the reST-source documentation at    [https://developer.lsst.io/restructuredtext/style.html]    and the actual rendering of the document.    First, the {{:guilabel:`text`}} markup, which is described here:  https://developer.lsst.io/v/DM-5063/docs/rst_styleguide.html#other-semantic-markup  as rendering the text surrounded by a rounded rectangle with blue border and light-blue fill, appears to render as just the plain text (though the markup is, correctly, stripped off).    Also, the {{`URL`__}} syntax for rendering clickable links, described here:    https://developer.lsst.io/restructuredtext/style.html#external-links    specifically the """"second method"""", described as creating an """"anonymous link"""", did not work as expected: it produced a link, but the backticks and two underscores appeared in the rendered output.  This was able to be mitigated by simply removing the markup, since """"bare"""" URLs are also made into clickable links in the rendering."""

summary: "defaultFilter is not used if a filterName is given to loadSkyCircle"
description: """If {{defaultFilter}} is supplied without a {{filterMap}}, and the user specifies {{filter=something}} in {{loadSkyCircle()}} (as one normally does, because when you load the refcat you don't know how its filter mappings are configured), {{getRefFluxField()}} will fail:        I believe the solution is to add {{camFlux}} to the {{fluxFieldList}} in {{getRefFluxField()}}, so that if {{defaultFilter}} is specified, you'll still get a fluxField of some kind. The behavior of how {{defaultFilter}} and {{filterMap}} should interact is undefined in the docs that I can see, so although this is a behavior change, I think it is more self-consistent. We also do not use {{defaultFilter}} anywhere in the stack currently that I've found, and this change should make it useable for RFC-697."""

summary: "Investigate unusually large memory footprint in Qserv czar"
description: """When Qserv is processing many parallel _shared scan_ queries changes in the memory footprint of the {{mysql-proxy}} may indicate a possible memory leak in the application. Here and example of the snapshots taken by UNIX tool {{top}} *after* launching one batch of 80 shared scan queries at a time:    One more snapshot was taken while processing another batch of 80 queries at a level of 50% done (on average):    It looks like there is a big pile of memory allocated and held by the process. This memory is not released after finishing each batch (of 80 queries).    A goal of this ticket is to investigate a source of the memory consumption, understand why the memory is not released after completing the queries, and find a fix for the problem."""

"Remove the use of Regaussianization quantities for star-PSF comparison"
description: """The two functors in pipe_analysis namely E1ResidsHsmRegauss and E2ResidsHsmRegauss subtract the PSF model ellipticity from PSF-deconvolved ellipticity for a star catalog. This is not a well motivated operation and could lead to misuse. This ticket is do the following two things:    1) Remove Regauss from the functor names as quantities from Regaussianization procedure aren't to be used.    2) Replace HsmRegauss_e1/e2 with measured adaptive source moments"""

summary: "Inconsistency between the IDL and Control in LabVIEW"
description: """Inconsistency between the IDL and control in LabVIEW. The IDL file is generated by ts_sal v5.0.0. The following structure is from the IDL:  {code:c++}struct command_disable {   long TestID;   string<8> private_revCode; //private   double private_sndStamp; //private   double private_rcvStamp; //private   long private_seqNum; //private   string private_identity; //private   long private_origin; //private   long private_host; //private   boolean value;   };   #pragma keylist command_disable TestID{code}  The LabVIEW control is:   !disableCtrl.png|width=270,height=315!    It is clear that they are different.    In addition, if some attribute (such as *TestID*) should be given the value in the LabVIEW SAL monitor, the word of """"//private"""" should appear in the IDL file."""

Investigate this bug in a pure-{{obs_base}} context and fix the problem. Unit tests for pickling support of {{RawIngestTask}} and related classes are a good starting point."""

summary: "Jenkins stack-os-matrix fails due to leftover EUPS .lockDir"
description: """stack-os-matrix jobs occasionally fail with {{eups declare: Unable to take exclusive lock on /j/ws/stack-os-matrix/[...]: locks are held by [user=jswarm, pid=...]}}    The actual lock directory in the stack looks like {{/project/jenkins/prod/agent-ldfc-ws-1/ws/stack-os-matrix/adacff179f/lsstsw/stack/cb4e2dc/.lockDir}}.  Removing that and its contents resolves the problem but is manual (although a Jenkins pipeline, {{sqre/infra/clean-locks}} has been created to let anyone do this cleanup without requiring administrator intervention).    The problem appears to be correlated with manual termination of previous jobs.  The code in eups is supposed to clean up lock files and directories when interrupted, but perhaps something else is going on here.    Either fix the manual job termination to clean up properly, or turn off locking altogether for these jobs, if that is safe and feasible."""

summary: "obs_decam missing configs for bias and flat construction in Gen2"
description: """{{constructBias.py}} and {{constructFlat.py}} do not load the Decam-specific configs set in {{config/isr.py}} . For the bias, that in particular means that if you use the stack to construct the master bias frame, it is missing the overscanBiasJump correction, leading to a discontinuity in the final calexps. {{obs_lsst}} handles the configs correctly, with the following line added to {{bias.py}} and {{flat.py}}: {{config.isr.load(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), """"isr.py""""))}}     """

summary: "Fix minor bug in Gen2 DCR diffim template generation"
description: """{{sensorRef.datasetExists(**patchArgDict)}} fails in {{getTemplate}} of {{ip_diffim}} for DCR coadds, because it needs to have a subfilter defined in the {{patchArgDict}}. Including {{subfilter}} isn't a problem for non-DCR coadds. This only affects Gen2."""

summary: "Have cp_pipe/ptc.py continue if there is a bad flat pair"
description: """When looping through postISR flat pairs, ptc.py fails if there is a bad pair (at least one flat of the pair is bad):  {code:python}          for (v1, v2) in visitPairs:              # Get postISR exposures.              dataRef.dataId['expId'] = v1              exp1 = dataRef.get(""""postISRCCD"""", immediate=True)              dataRef.dataId['expId'] = v2              exp2 = dataRef.get(""""postISRCCD"""", immediate=True)  {code}     In cases like this, the loop over the flat pairs should continue instead of stopping the task (especially useful if we have a large number of pairs). """

summary: "Gain slope is inverted in EXPAPPROXIMATION case in plotPtc.py "
description: """When using the EXPAPPROXIMATION case, the slope of the green dotted line which represents the gain is inverted.  It should be 1/pars[1], not pars[1].  The attached plot labeled 'bad' shows the current code.  I edited line 832 to use 1/pars[1], and got the plot labeled 'good'.  note that this can't be the final fix, since pars[1] is defined differently between the EXPAPPROXIMATION and POLYNOMIAL cases.  The final fix will need a little more thought, like defining a variable 'slope' or using '1/gain' or something."""

(2) If all of the points in an amp get rejected in the _getInitialGoodPoints routine, then the program crashes.  We want it to continue and complete the other amps, even if one amp is bad.  There are two points where I've seen it crash.  One is the line """"mask = mask & goodPoints"""", which is about line 1200, and the other is the line """"mask = mask & newMask"""", which is about line 1230.  I think if either one of the inputs is all 'False', then it returns the following error:     The following input string should generate the error:  """

summary: "ap_verify import slow in Gen 3"
description: """Ingesting {{HiTS2015}} takes a very long time in Gen 3; after 30 minutes on {{lsst-devl01}}, it had not yet finished the preliminary import used to create an independent repository. This is unexpected, since of the affected datasets (calibs, crosstalk, defects, refcats, templates), only templates should scale up in size with the overall dataset size, and only defects and refcats should require file copies. For comparison, the entire import of {{CI-HiTS2015}} takes 30 seconds.    Run tests comparing the current Gen 2 and Gen 3 behavior to confirm whether the latter is much slower. If so, track down the cause."""

summary: "Not able to un-check the ""Published"" check box for localized pages"
description: """When a site is localized you can go to the page settings of any page, go to """"Localization"""" tab. Then you will be able to see the default language for that page plus the localized version of the same page.    There is a """"Published"""" checkbox which by default the first time you localize a page is is unchecked.    Then you can check once you are ready to publish the localized version.    However if you no longer want the localized version to be published, when you try to un-check the """"Published"""" checkbox and save it, it goes back to check. So we cannot unpublish a localized page once it has been published.    Not sure if this is the desired behavior or it is a bug, but I decided to report.    Steps to reproduce:    1 - Activate a second language on DNN 9.1.1  2 - Go to the home page;  3 - Go to the page settings;  4 - Go to the """"Localization"""" tab of the home page;  5 - Check if you see the default language and the second localized language;  6 - If not click """"Make Page Translatable""""  7 - Click the checkbox where it says """"Published"""" under the localized version of the page;  8 - Click """"Update Localization"""" to save;  9 - Now click again """"Published"""" under the localized version of the page to un-check it;  10 - Click """"Update Localization"""" to save;  11 - Behaviour: """"Published"""" gets checked again  12 - Expected behavior: """"Published"""" checkbox would become unchecked  """

summary: "Platform source code identified issues - OO Language Requirements"
description: """h2. CA2217: Do not mark enums with FlagsAttribute  The issue """"CA2217: Do not mark enums with FlagsAttribute"""" is a broad issue that does not pertain to a single source code file.    h3. Cause  An externally visible enumeration is marked with FlagsAttribute and it has one or more values that are not powers of two or a combination of the other defined values on the enumeration.    h3. Rule Description  An enumeration should have FlagsAttribute present only if each value defined in the enumeration is a power of two, or a combination of defined values.    h3. How to Fix Violations  To fix a violation of this rule, remove FlagsAttribute from the enumeration.    h3. When to Suppress Warnings  Do not suppress a warning from this rule.    h3. MSDN Documentation  http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms182335.aspx      h2. CA1050: Declare types in namespaces  The issue """"CA1050: Declare types in namespaces"""" is a broad issue that does not pertain to a single source code file.    h3. Cause  A public or protected type is defined outside the scope of a named namespace.    h3. Rule Description  Types are declared in namespaces to prevent name collisions, and as a way to organize related types in an object hierarchy. Types that are outside any named namespace are in a global namespace that cannot be referenced in code.    h3. How to Fix Violations  To fix a violation of this rule, place the type in a namespace.    h3. When to Suppress Warnings  Although you never have to suppress a warning from this rule, it is safe to do this when the assembly will never be used together with other assemblies.    h3. MSDN Documentation  http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms182134.aspx"""

summary: "SI: Redactor script removes ""empty"" iframe elements"
description: """There is a function in redactor.source.js which is supposed to clean up empty elements. summary: "Null reference exception happened when the page is redirected in SSLOffload environment"
description: """When a page is setup to redirect to the existing page in SSLOffload environment, null reference exception occurs when the page is typed in from the browser  In this scenario Page2 is setup to redirect to Page3. Please check the attached file for details on configuration.     To reproduce,  1. Setup SSL Settings  2. Setup Page2 to redirect to Page3  3. Request Page2 from a browser (e.g https://dnndev.me/Home/Page2)    After investigation, the exception is from the function   IsSecureConnectionOrSslOffload(HttpRequest request)  where the PortalSettings is null and it was used to get the ActiveTab information.    In our opinion for this scenario, checking the IsSecure of the Active Tab is not related to determining whethere a request is secure or not.    Our suggestion on fixing is as following.      public static bool IsSecureConnectionOrSslOffload(HttpRequest request)          {              if (request.IsSecureConnection)              {                  return true;              }              string ssloffloadheader = HostController.Instance.GetString(""""SSLOffloadHeader"""", """""""");              //if the ssloffloadheader variable has been set check to see if a request header with that type exists              if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(ssloffloadheader))              {                  string ssloffload = request.Headers[ssloffloadheader];                  if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(ssloffload))                  {                      return true;                  }              }              return false;          }  """

summary: "Performance issue with PasswordResetToken"
description: """Password reset may time out on sites with many users.  This is caused by GetUserByPasswordResetToken sproc, which has to perform a full clustered index scan.   I suggest adding a dedicated filtered index on PasswordResetToken (Not Null) and set PasswordResetToken = Null (and PasswordResetExpiration = Null) whenever it is timed out or used, to keep the filtered index small."""

summary: "PB Users: Newly authorized users not shown on Authorized list for some time"
description: """If an administrator sets an unauthorized user to authorized and then does a search for that user under the authorized list, (i.e. by changing the “SHOW:” field to “authorized”) that user is not located.  In order to see the newly authorized user the administrator must change the “SHOW:” field to “All” and then the search function will locate the newly authorized user.    To recreate the scenario do the following:  # Logon to any site as Administrator or Super-Admin  # Go to Manage > Users  # Change “SHOW:” field to UNAUTHORIZED to list all unauthorized users  # Select one and change that user to be “authorized”  # System responds with appropriate “User has been authorized” message  # Change “SHOW:” field to AUTHORIZED to list all authorized users  # Enter the authorized user’s name in the “Search Users” field  # Issue:  System will NOT locate the newly authorized user  # Change the “SHOW:” field to “all” and then Enter the authorized user’s name in the “Search Users” field  # Result: the system will locate the newly authorized user-id.    Note: it has been noticed that after a few minutes (5?), the newly authorized user does become locatable under the Authorized users list.  """

summary: "Patchable:false tasks should not be shown in configure view"
"YAML that causes TranslateProject to fail prevents later commits from building"
description: """If you break the YAML in a way that causes TranslateProject to return an error, Evergreen will not be able to build newer commits after you fix the YAML. The for loop that loops over new revisions calls *RepoTracker.GetProjectConfig , which calls *RepoTracker.GetRemoteConfig, which calls model.GetProjectFromFile , which calls *model.TranslateProject, which could error because, e.g., evaluateSelectors fails..     However, the error handling only continues in the case of project validation errors https://github.com/evergreen-ci/evergreen/blob/31348a4f683941adf7e152381a7bc10c352173b1/repotracker/repotracker.go#L236. This means that if the error comes from TranslateProject, the for loop will exit early. The problem is we assume that all project errors are validation errors."""

summary: "Artifact filename truncation can unexpectedly truncate the beginning of a filename"
description: """EVG-6327 introduced filename truncation and I believe the expectation is that [the code|https://github.com/evergreen-ci/evergreen/blob/86ebeb15ddc211f1390c5cc56af54a3712728e62/operations/fetch.go#L470-L477] would truncate the end of the file. The algorithm in words:  # Split the <filename>.<extension> on the dot.  # Throw away the end X characters from <filename>    However, if the <filename> contains a dot (e.g: {{mongodb_mongo_v4.4}}), the actual behavior of the algorithm becomes:  # Split <filename>.<extension> on _all_ dots.  # Throw away the end X characters from the first string preceding a dot.    For filenames that mongodb's evergreen yaml defines, the first string typically identifies the contents while the back of the string identifies the version.    Additionally, it seems plausible that the code as-is can error: consider the case where 30 characters need to be trimmed, but the first string only contains 20 characters. I suspect [newEndIdx|https://github.com/evergreen-ci/evergreen/blob/86ebeb15ddc211f1390c5cc56af54a3712728e62/operations/fetch.go#L476] would be negative here resulting in a panic."""

summary: "Tasks scheduled after an image deploy should not run on hosts running an older image"
description: """If a new image is deployed, it is legitimate for a developer to submit work to the queue that relies on features available only on the new image. However, evergreen does not prevent those tasks from running on older instances of the image. Tasks submitted after an image upgrade should only be eligible to run on instances at least as new as the newly deployed image.    Even if there isn't a hard dependency on new features, running tasks scheduled together (say, as part of a commit queue run) on different flavors of an image can have adverse effects, such as disabling build cache sharing between elements of parallelized build tasks. See, for instance https://evergreen.mongodb.com/build/mongodb_mongo_master_enterprise_rhel_62_64_bit_dynamic_required_patch_ffa3d0117ed0ae23fca299fc3474502e701a086b_5f3ae12f0305b9615360e007_20_08_17_21_11_17, where the first run of the {{unittests}} task ran on a different image type than the other {{compile}} related tasks ({{compile}}, {{dbtest_no_archive}}, and {{compile_all}}, leading to a very slow commit queue build."""

summary: "Scheduling an unknown task prevents all tasks from being scheduled during generate.tasks"

We have a bug that is causing the subset to contain tasks that were never originally generated.    This manifests as an error        but then none of the other tasks that were valid get scheduled.    Can we please continue-on-error here and schedule all the tasks that were valid?    For posterity the failure is [here|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/sys_perf_linux_standalone_genny_auto_tasks_e07c2d29aded5a30ff08b5ce6a436b6ef6f44014_20_08_03_22_24_44]. For reasons I can't figure out, it tries to schedule {{mixed_workloads_genny}} which wasn't a part of the tasks it originally generated [here|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/sys_perf_compile_amazon2_genny_generate_all_tasks_e07c2d29aded5a30ff08b5ce6a436b6ef6f44014_20_08_03_22_24_44]. All of the other tasks it schedules are correct, but this one extra task is causing none of them to be scheduled, and this is impacting the build barons' ability to investigate."""

summary: "Spruce displays execution tasks intended to be restarted as system failures"
description: """Spruce separates display tasks into individual execution tasks in the [patch details|https://spruce.mongodb.com/version/5f43f081d6d80a3602a3c14f/tasks?page=0&taskName=burn_in] view. In contrast, the legacy UI only exposes execution tasks through their [display task|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/mongodb_mongo_v4.4_enterprise_rhel_62_64_bit_inmem_required_display_burn_in_tests_patch_29e21d0ff597e6dd10bbb9bab4086612339443ca_5f43f081d6d80a3602a3c14f_20_08_24_16_53_31##comparehashes=29e21d0ff597e6dd10bbb9bab4086612339443ca&threads=all].    When a display task encounters a system failure due to an AWS host being terminated, Evergreen will restart it, rather than mark the task as a system failure. In Spruce, because the execution tasks are broken out, they will appear as failed with a system failure in this case. This implies that there's action required from the user to address the system failure, when in reality the task will restart and the failure should resolve on its own.    A potential solution to address this is to display these 'system failed' execution tasks as 'in progress', since for all intents and purposes the task has not yet completed. The task details page can provide further information on whether a task is truly in progress or slated to be restarted, for users interested in differentiating between those two states."""

summary: "attach.xunit_results does not handle files with nested test suites"
description: """The xUnit reporting format allows to nest test suites (e.g. using {{TestSuite.addTestSuite()}} in JUnit. However, when the {{attach.xunit_results}} command encounters such a file, it does not recurse into nested test suites, and instead reports no test results being found.    I couldn't find a schema that allows me to verify that nested test suites are indeed part of the specification, but I found an [old Jenkins issue|https://issues.jenkins-ci.org/browse/JENKINS-6545] that shows the problem doesn't just affect Evergreen. More importantly, the JUnit logger used by PHPUnit (see https://github.com/sebastianbergmann/phpunit/blob/master/src/Util/Log/JUnit.php) has been generating files with nested test suites for as long as the git history allows me to check. For reference, this is one of the result files as we are parsing them:      The evergreen output when trying to attach this file is as follows:  """

summary: "Peer should not disseminate private data for other org's implicit collection"
description: """FAB-17303 set a default dissemination policy for implicit collections of requiredPeerCount=0 and maxPeerCount=1. The intent was for this default to apply to implicit collections of the peer's own org. However it is being applied for other org's implicit collection as well.    Since implicit org-specific collections have an endorsement policy of the organization itself, the owning organization should manage the dissemination. There is no need for peers of other organizations to disseminate. That is, if org1 and org2 both endorse a write to org1 implicit collection as part of a larger transaction, only the org1 peer should disseminate the private data.    This work item will suppress the peer's private data dissemination for other organization's implicit collections."""

summary: "Endorsing peer is not honoring RequiredPeerCount for private data dissemination"
description: """If there are not enough known eligible peers to meet a private data collection RequiredPeerCount, endorsement was succeeding rather than returning an error.    This was a regression caused by FAB-15389 in v1.4.4.  FAB-15389 shifted entirely to per-peer dissemination where required peers were tagged up front before dissemination, however there was no overall check to ensure that RequiredPeerCount was met. Prior to FAB-15389, the check was handled deeper in the gossip dissemination. Gossip still checks that each selected required peer disseminates the private data.    This fix adds the overall RequiredPeerCount check for cases where there are not enough known eligible peers.    Also, improvements are made to variable names, error messages, and comments to make dissemination code easier to understand.  """

summary: "Global var for error and sync.Once"
description: """There are a couple of implementations details in BCCSP that makes it difficult to call down into BCCSP with varying configuration.    *First Issue:*    The first issue is the use of sync.Once, see: See: [https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/v1.4.6/bccsp/factory/pkcs11.go#L39]    What this means is that if BCCSP is initialized with bad config (wrong label or pin) when using PKCS11, it has entered into a bad state from which it cannot recover. All subsequent requests, will fail even if the configuration has been corrected and is being passed down to BCCSP.    *Second Issue:*    I used bad HSM config the first time, now I want to switch to using a non-hsm configuration. This use case will not work if the BCCSP library has already encountered an error, unfortunately BCCSP when it gets an error stores that error in a global variable which once sets remains sets. Here: [https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/master/bccsp/factory/factory.go#L43]*[|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/master/bccsp/factory/factory.go#L43]*    Even though you might be using a valid configuration after using a bad one, BCCSP will always continue to return that previously hit error and initialization will always fail."""

summary: "Consortium groups don't have policies"
description: """We added a helper function to support retrieving policies for a consortium, but they don't actually have policies. We should go ahead and remove any functions around retrieving policies from a consortium. Likewise the consortiums group also does not support adding/removing policies and only contains a single hardcoded AcceptAll signature policy. It's probably ok to support retrieving the policy for consortiums group since that doesn't hurt, but it would always be the same hardcoded value so maybe consider just removing it. Luckily we didn't add any functions for adding/removing policies from a consortium or consortiums group so we don't need to do anything there."""

summary: "merge ledgerstorage package to kvledger package"
description: """The [ledgerstorage|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/tree/master/core/ledger/ledgerstorage] package encapsulates both [pvtdata storage|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/tree/master/core/ledger/pvtdatastorage] and  [block storage|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/tree/master/common/ledger/blkstorage]. We needed this package as   * we had to have [2-phase commit between pvtdata storage and block storage|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/release-1.3/core/ledger/ledgerstorage/store.go#L125].   * we had a [recovery logic|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/release-1.3/core/ledger/ledgerstorage/store.go#L230] that was needed after a commit failure or peer crash between pvtdata commit and blockstore commit.   * we treated pvtdata store as a pvt blockstore and blockstore as public block store.    After introducing the support for peer rollback, we removed the 2-phase commit between requirement between the pvtdata storage and block storage. As a result, the pvtdata storage can be ahead of blockstore. Hence, the recovery logic was also remoted. Hence, the motivation for having ledgerstorage package became feeble and hence, we remove it.     Further, core/ledger, core/ledger/ledgerstore, core/ledger/ledgermgmt create confusion and provide disinformation."""

summary: "Legacy chaincode partially installs upon build failure"
description: """*1st issue*    When using legacy chaincode to install a chaincode, if the chaincode build on the peer fails, you'll get an error that says the install failed.    However, the chaincode package actually gets installed in the peer's legacy /chaincode directory.  This is both confusing, and makes it difficult to re-attempt the build once the underlying build issue is resolved.    I emulated this problem by configuring my peer to use ccenv:latest2 to build the chaincode, which does not exist.     The same trial with new lifecycle gives the expected results. The installation fails with a similar error and the chaincode does NOT get installed at all (not in the new /lifecycle/chaincodes directory).         *2nd issue*    However, there is a second issue with new lifecycle. Even when the build issue is resolved (in my case I kept the peer up, tagged ccenv:latest to ccenv:latest2, did not change core.yaml settings), the next installation attempt still fails with the same error message, even though ccenv:latest2 now exists on the peer machine:     When I restart peer and attempt the install yet again, it succeeds.  So it seems the peer caches the fact that the builder image cannot be found, and restarting the peer is required in order for peer to find the newly tagged ccenv:latest2 builder image."""

summary: "Orderer replaces TLS CA root certs with same subject"
description: """I have multiple orgs added to system channel Orderer group in genesis block I'm not able to connect to my ordering nodes (mutual tls enabled because of raft). In ordering logs I see `TLS handshake failed with error tls: failed to verify client's certificate: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority (possibly because of """"x509: ECDSA verification failure"""" while trying to verify candidate authority certificate """"fabric-ca-server"""") `.  When I start with 1 org and then add orgs with channel update - it works, but after a few mins it throws same error again.   I figured out the problem by reading orderer source code. The problem was in CRS subjects of all CAs, they have same CN + OU which means subjects are equal.   https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/2c2274d0519a1ce1eba596ff0a43636dce64d926/internal/pkg/comm/server.go#L271 it happens because of key in this map.  CaRoot certs are getting replaced when updating root CAs for channel."""

summary: "Raft config update should be validated against correct IDs"
description: """When a config update is submitted to node, it runs through [this check|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/450ba3ae2e6434bdc0a99c4f3ebf77498d05e2ba/orderer/consensus/etcdraft/chain.go#L1304], which uses *dummy node IDs* (always starting from 1). This prevents validation from making correct decision based on current IDs in consenter set. For example, when we have [2, 3, 4] in cluster, and attempts to remove 4, this part of validation logic would believe that 3 is being removed.    In most of cases we are fine, since after all we are removing correct node with the certificate. However, if 4 happens to be offline, we would get rejected with error """"config will result in quorum loss"""""""

summary: "Stop Chains upon removal, clear resources (synchronous)"
description: """Stop the chain, then remove file ledger resources.    The removal of file ledger resources is when we persist the channel.remove marker file in the file repo.    If the orderer crashes before removal is finished, then either the marker was written, so we'll recover and complete the removal, or the chain will resume normal operation after restart.    Note: the etcdraft.Chain triggers the creation of a follower.Chain when Halt() is called. In order to stop the chain before removal this must be changed."""

summary: "Export couchdb data in binary JSON format"
description: """In the current code for exporting the couchdb state data, JSON data is exported in string human readable format. However, since the snapshot data files are not meant to be human readable, we can make this efficient by exporting the data in the binary JSON format. This adds efficiency in both during the export and later during the import at the time of bootstrapping the channel."""

summary: "Cannot enable HSM for peer CLI with environment variables"
description: """According to this documentation: [https://hyperledger-fabric.readthedocs.io/en/release-2.0/hsm.html]    You should be able to enable the use of HSM by setting the following environment variables:        This works in Fabric v1.4.7 but does not work in master.    Looking at changes that might have caused this, FAB-15951 looks like a good candidate: [https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/commit/4f7e4755fa7abc0d49a479e795923b587272cba4]    It calls factory.GetDefault() very early on, before factory.InitFactories() is called. This results in the """"boot BCCSP"""" (SW) being used and then being passed into the commands. You can see this in the log messages:    (Side note, if you want to enforce call orders like that, maybe it should be a panic instead of a debug level log message?)    The end result is that the SW implementation is used instead of PKCS11, and failures occur later on as there are no private keys in the file system for the SW implementation to use.    [~<USER> [~<USER> could you take a look please?"""

summary: "Separate internal & external Halt on etcdraft.Chain"
description: """currently, when the etcdraft.Chain halts, either internally or externally, it calls an """"haltCallback"""" that generates a new chain. This is useful for internal halt, when the chain discovers it is no longer a member of the channel and needs to transfer responsibility to either a follower or an inactive chain. In order to remove a chain, we need to close it by calling Halt with an external thread. However, currently this will cause a call to haltCalback...    The task here is to separate internal halt (called by the internal go-routine) from external Halt, that should stop the chain and indicate that haltCallback should NOT be called upon termination."""

summary: "Orderer initializes & starts follower chains upon restart"
description: """Rewrite the Registrar.Initialize() flow:  - Discover and load join-blocks. If there is a join-block, there must be a ledger; if there is none, create it.  - Discover all ledgers. This should already include all channels with join blocks as well.  - Scan for and initialize the system channel, if it exists.  - Make sure there are no empty ledgers without a corresponding join-block. Note that there may be channels with empty ledgers, but always with a join block.  - Initialize application channels, by creating either a consensus.Chain or a follower.Chain.  - Start all channels. """

summary: "Determine how to handle snapshot generation error"
description: """There are 3 code paths that may generate a snapshot.   # after a block is committed   # when a snapshot request is submitted   # at peer startup to recover a missed snapshot generation (e.g., due to peer crash)    Currently, a go-routine is created to generate snapshot in each of the above cases. Any error from snapshot generation is simply logged as an error message. Need to revisit and decide what is the best way to handle this kind of error and how to let the administrator know a peer fails to generate a snapshot.     """

summary: "Consider cleaning up $INST/var/tmp on upgrades, to prevent users getting mismatched JSP files"
description: """It seems that in some cases the jsp classes in $FISHEYE_INST won't be overwritten on upgrades (potentially because their dates is newer than the date on the jsps in the release). This can cause errors when trying to render the UI.    We should probably delete the compiled JSPs from $FISHEYE_INST when an upgrade occurs, to make sure they are not left over."""

summary: "FishEye displays an incorrect committer for a user with an underscore in its username"
description: """h3. Summary  The committer information is not displayed correctly when accessing a changeset in case the user contains an underscore in its username.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  The steps below have been tested with a Git repository, but they may apply to other repository types.  # Create a user in FishEye with {{yut}} as Username, {{Yut}} as Display name and {{<EMAIL>}} as Email  # Create a user in FishEye with {{y_t}} as Username, {{Y_t}} as Display name and {{<EMAIL>}} as Email  # These are the users in FishEye after the steps above !FE6359_users.png|thumbnail!   # In a Git repository, set the Git configuration option for user.name and user.email to match the user with the underscore so that the Git config -l command returns:  --   # Commit and push to the Git repository. The Git log command returns {{Y_t}} as Author:  --   # Locate the commit in FishEye    h3. Expected Results  The {{Y_t}} display name, matching the {{y_t}} user, is displayed as committer.    h3. Actual Results  The {{Yut}} display name, matching the {{yut}} user, is displayed as committer.   !FE6359_repositoryActivity.png|thumbnail!     When clicking on the user displayed, the page containing the details of the {{yut}} user (without the underscore) is loaded (note the {{<FishEye url>/user/yut}} in the title bar), and the page shows the """"This user has not yet committed."""" message:   !FE6359_YutUser.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes  The user creation sequence ({{yut}} - user without the underscore before {{y_t}} - user with the underscore) is critical. If the users are created in the reversed sequence, then the problem does not occur.    h3.Workaround  Delete the user shown instead of the expected one, {{yut}} in this example, and recreate it.  The correct username is displayed after the user {{yut}} is deleted, and recreated:  !FE6359_correctRepositoryActivity.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Switching between files in a changeset is slow for large changesets"
description: """*Steps to reproduce:*     * create a huge changeset with 100'000 files.   * select the changeset from the activity tab - it takes > 10s to load   * expand a tree - again it takes > 10s to expand a node   * click between files - it takes > 10s to load the file    *Code analysis:*     * we collect all files (""""revisions"""") from a changeset and sort them, it takes ~5s to build a list and another ~5s to sort it; out of it we calculate currentPage and selectedIndex parameters    excerpt from com.cenqua.fisheye.web.changeset.ChangeSetPageData#ChangeSetPageData:         * the ChangeSetPageData (""""p"""") is called by LoadChangesetPageAjaxAction, which renders changesetPageAjaxResponse.jsp, which is called 3 times to render:   ** entire page activity stream page   ** directory tree   ** file content     * the changesetPageAjaxResponse.jsp uses stuff like p.repname, p.currentPage, p.selectedIndex etc     * the directory tree has nodes with page number attached to each link, e.g.:        *Possible fix:*    1) while we cannot omit collecting and sorting of all revisions while rendering the entire tree, we could omit collecting and sorting these revisions when rendering the source page:   * the page number parameter is already passed in the <a href> link clicked by a user   * the selected index is always 0 for a source page   * we don't use 'allChangesetFileRevisions' property for a source page    2) we could try to cache sorted results (as it won't change for a given changeset and we call it 3 times)    3) we could try to reduce amount of changesetFileRevisions collected when the directory tree is rendered - as we don't draw the entire tree at once - we could fetch data using extra ajax calls when user expands the dir node    """

summary: "Workaround is invalid and can lead to massive data loss"
description: """Greetings Atlassian,    I recently used a workaround posted on your site for an issue with naming in Fisheye:  FishEye users get disabled after username change in LDAP  https://confluence.atlassian.com/fishkb/fisheye-users-get-disabled-after-username-change-in-ldap-770609556.html?continue=https%3A%2F%2Fconfluence.atlassian.com%2Ffishkb%2Ffisheye-users-get-disabled-after-username-change-in-ldap-770609556.html&application=cac    I can only speak for the MySQL version of the workaround, but one of the DELETE statements has an invalid field that causes far greater data to be deleted than what is intended. The statement in question is:  DELETE FROM cru_file_read_status WHERE cru_participant IN (SELECT cru_participant FROM cru_review_participant WHERE cru_user = @old_user_id);    In the SELECT statement, cru_participant is an invalid field of cru_review_participant. That field SHOULD be cru_participant_id. Since cru_participant IS a field of cru_file_read_status, the DELETE will affect far greater records than it should.     Example:  Bad query  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------      Good query  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------     Luckily, per the instructions provided(as well as standard good-practice), we took a backup before hand. However, I would hate for someone else to have to run into this issue. """

summary: "Update 3.10 -> 4.0 fails if crowd resyncGroupsList contains fisheye-users or crucible-users"
description: """h2.Problem description  On update 3.10 -> 4.0 we create two """"special"""" default groups {{fisheye-users}} and {{crucible-users}} with FishEye/Crucible global permissions respectively. This happens in {{com.cenqua.crucible.hibernate.upgrade.crowd.MigrateUserLicenses}}.    Further on, we add global permissions for all the groups in {{config.xml/security/crowd/resyncGroupsList}}. If user had {{fisheye-users}} or {{crucible-users}} defined there, we fail, because these groups have had the global permissions already added in {{#addDefaultGroups}}.    h2.If an update has failed  Once the update has failed because of {{fisheye-users}} or {{crucible-users}} groups, you first have to roll back to previous working version (3.10.3 or earlier) before continuing.    h2.Workaround for updating to 4.0.0 and 4.0.1  Before upgrading to 4.0, remove {{<group name=""""fisheye-users""""/>}} and {{<group name=""""crucible-users""""/>}} from {{config.xml/security/crowd/resyncGroupsList}}. Leave the other groups intact.    h2.Updating to 4.0.2  Provided you start from a working instance (not one that has failed to upgrade to 4.0.0/4.0.1), upgrading to 4.0.2 does not require any extra steps regarding this issue.    h2.Message on the first run      h2.Message on subsequent runs  DB is left in an inconsistent state, so subsequent attempts to start FishEye/Crucible fails with this output:  """

summary: "FishEye/Crucible should not provide an edit screen for external user accounts"
description: """External user directories are read-only in FishEye/Crucible, however if an admin clicks a user account in {{Administration >> Users}} they are provided an edit screen which implies that changes can be made to the accounts such as """"Edit Groups"""" and """"Change Password"""".  Because the external directories are read-only, any attempt to change anything results in a sometimes unfriendly and definitely misleading error message.    For external user accounts, the user account screen should be read-only with a message stating that this account is managed externally (preferably with the name of the source external directory)."""

summary: "createParentDirs shouldn't be called on branch creation changeset"
description: """Currently there's logic in insertFileRevision which creates parent dirs for path if those don't exists.       It makes no sense especially for branch creation commit. We store Paths in HashMap, which make cause during iterating over it some path with a created file comes before path with parent dir path causing premature dir creation. Later on when Fisheye is processing Path connected with that particular dir it checks if it already exists and ignore it if it does.       Note affect only configuration with Start Revision and No Import settings. """

summary: "Smart Commits don't work In FishEye when Day or Week time values are present"
description: """h3. Summary    When performing a Smart commit in FishEye (in 3.9 or later) if the value of days or weeks is present then the Smart Commit will fail    h3. Environment  - FishEye 3.9+  - Connected to A JIRA instance    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Perform a Smart Commit that contains {{d}} or {{w}} values for the time to input    h3. Expected Results    The Time referenced is added to the JIRA ticket    h3. Actual Results    No Time is added to the JIRA ticket if days or weeks are referenced in the commit, even if hours is also present as an input time.     The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-fisheye-xxxx-xx-xx.log.log file:    *Hours and Days mixed*:             *Just Days*:            h3. Workarounds    # In JIRA, 1 day of work has 8 hours, so specifying the number of days * 8 as hours should work. For example, supposing someone spent one full day and additional 3 hours the next day working on something, this person could use '11h' when logging time.  # If Days or Weeks really need to be specified, manually add this directly to the JIRA ticket."""

summary: "Thread Locking issues on Crucible"
description: """Our Crucible server is running horribly slow. It's a beefy server that has 4 cores and 10G RAM and the DB is hosted on a separate SQL Server. Our repo is Git but we have one very large one - 12G and 137K non-linear changesets. After 3 days, it has only indexed ~50K changesets and according to Fisheye documentation, performance should be 100-200KB/s on an avg PC. I wonder if these lock errors are causing an issue. Also, note: initial index is set to 3 threads but we're unsure if repos are actually single threaded in which case having 3 threads assigned will not help.     Please review our logs and let me know if there's an actual defect you need to resolve or if something else is going on. """

summary: "Ineffective Groups on the Global Permissions page are removed in some scenarios"
description: """Steps to reproduce:   # Open Global Permissions page, fisheye-users group is shown as """"FishEye user"""" and crucible-users is shown as """"FishEye & Crucible user"""".   # Remove FishEye license key, on the Global Permissions page it shows fisheye-users as """"None"""" and crucible-users as """"Crucible user"""".   # Click on fisheye-users """"None"""" drop-down and select """"None"""" again. (Probably an update is performed under the hood, despite value is the same).   # Restore FishEye license key.   # Open Global Permissions page, the fisheye-users group has been deleted.         Another way to achieve a similar result without toggling licences:   # Open Global Permissions page, change fisheye-users group to """"None"""". Group is still visible on the list.   # Reload Global Permission page, the fisheye-users group disappears.         It seems that whenever any group is """"None"""" and has not """"Can add repository"""" permission enabled, then such row is being deleted automatically (as it has no effect). However this is not reflected in the UI immediately.   Note this affects UX only, no impact on effective permissions so no security risk identified.    Possible fixes:    1. remove such row immediately (although then user would have no chance to follow the flow """"change to None, enable can add repo toggle"""")    2. grey out the entire row indicating that it has no effect, give a hint that it will be removed    3. keep the row. user would have to click the delete icon (x) explicitly.     also regarding the problem with switching license keys:    4. If user selects the same value from a drop-down, then do not update the value under the hood (as the """"None"""" may mean either """"None"""" for FECRU or """"Fisheye"""" for CRU instance; similarly """"FishEye"""" may mean """"FishEye"""" for FECRU or """"FishEye and Crucible"""" for FE instance).  """

summary: "After upgrading to 4.0 user avatar can be lost"
description: """When having FeCru with < 4.0 configured with remote user directory (Crowd/Jira) with forced lowercase username and then upgrading to FeCru 4.0 users with mixed case user names will loose their avatars.    In details:    Let's take “John"""" example.    Pre 4.0    We have Crowd directory with forced lower case turned on and a user with “John” username.  In cru_user we have “john” user and his avatar is saved under hash(“john”).    In 4.0    “john” is still present in cru_user with the user name lowercased, in cwd_user we keep both username “John” / lowercase userame “john”    The avatar is expected to be found in hash(fecruUser.getUsername) where the username is taken from cwd_user so it is hash(“John”) and can’t be found.    """

summary: "Source view does not update the changeset diffs properly"
description: """h3. Summary  When selecting a file in the Source view, the changesets chosen for the diff are based on the previous first revision instead of the latest.    h3. Steps to Reproduce.  # Add 2 files (A and B) to a repository indexed by FishEye.  # Update and re-commit to both files.  # Make additional changes to A and recommit.  # Make another change to A and recommit.  # Allow FishEye to index all of the commits (total 2 commits for B and 4 commits for A).  # Go to the Source view for B.  # Set the """"From"""" commit of B to the first commit of this file.  # Leave the """"To"""" commit at the second commit.  # Click on file A.    h3. Expected Results  * When clicking on A, the """"To"""" commit is the latest commit for this file in the repository/branch  * The """"From"""" commit is latest -1 commit for this file to show the last change to the file.    h3. Actual Results  * The """"From"""" commit for A is either same commit as selected for B, or the """"From"""" commit that last appeared for A if there is not matching commit to B.  * The """"To"""" commit is the next commit for the file.      h3.Workaround  Manually change the """"To"""" and """"From"""" commits for each file where the issue occurs."""
