Workaround    Right now, the only known workaround is to restore the archived project, delete it (moving to trash should also work) or change the filter specifying the projects that you need."""

summary: "Can’t set decimal value for story points field on Jira for Mac app"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Can’t set decimal value for story points field.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Start creating issue (using plus button)  # Add a decimal number to story points field (e.g. 2.5)  # Click """"Create”    h3. Expected Results  The issue should have story points value user entered.    h3. Actual Results  Story points field is empty.    App version: 110    h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Import status changed history through JSON does not display as status changed in issue history"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Import status changed history through JSON does not display as status changed in issue history. It displays the action but it displays as a plain text. The HistoryItems were transformed into a generic map  the statements are different when performing it through the UI and when importing using the JSON file  through the UI, it shows user changed the status while the JSON shows updated    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a JSON file to import issue history of status being changed    h3. Expected Results  At the issue history, it is expected that the status is displayed with colored status   !image-2020-08-13-11-01-27-165.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  The HistoryItems were transformed into a generic map   !image-2020-08-13-11-01-09-613.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Deactivated Slack users are being invited to Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Deactivated Slack users are being invited to Jira  h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Be a Slack admin  + Jira <USER> # Go to a channel with >1 members  # Deactivate one of the members of the channel whose email is not a user of Jira  # Send a Jira link  # Get prompted to invite people  # Decide to invite everyone  # Invitations will be sent to the Deactivated members of Slack    h3. Expected Results    Send invite-only to Active users  h3. Actual Results  All users get the invite even if they were deactivated from Slack  h3. Workaround    Do not use the *Add all users* who don’t have access, use the *Choose users* so you can select users that are not deactivated from Slack"""

summary: "Number of Comments Custom Fields are not displayed on the New Issue View"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, if you add a Custom Field of *Number of comments* type, it is not displayed on the New Issue View.    Old Issue View:    !image-2020-08-13-12-24-10-540.png|width=713,height=270!    New Issue View:    !image-2020-08-13-12-27-15-057.png!  h3. Suggested Solution    Add the ability of the New Issue View to display the Custom Fields of *Number of comments* type  h3. Why this is important    This should enhance greatly the user experience.  h3. Workaround     Use the Old Issue View     """

summary: "Opening a dialog in the issue view results in the glance panel rendering out of place"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When the issue view is opened in a dialog (e.g. above a board), the dialogs such as the feedback dialog causes any open glance panel to be rendered out of place. This issue was created in response to [https://community.developer.atlassian.com/t/problems-displaying-dialog-over-issue-dialog/40727].  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open a board.   # View an issue such that it’s opened as a dialog above the board.   # Open a glance panel that is high enough to be scrollable.   # Open any dialog, e.g. “Give feedback”.   # The glance panel goes outside of the issue dialog.    h3. Expected Results    The bounds of the glance web panel is unaffected.  h3. Actual Results    The bounds of the glance web panel is pushed down. See the attachment for more details.  h3. Workaround    None"""

summary: "Customize issue layout by roles/user"
description: """It'd be nice to customize the Issue Layout field ordering and display on a per-user basis or by next-gen permissions/roles.    For some organizations, certain roles in our org rely on some fields more than others and it can be hard to get a layout that satisfies all users."""

summary: "PDF Files with 'Comments' do not render correctly to the viewer"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  PDF Files with 'Comments' do not render correctly to the viewer  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Attach a PDF file that contains a Comment inside    h3. Expected Results  Hover the mouse and show the comment from the PDF file viewer  h3. Actual Results  No comment is shown:     !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "When an issue on Next-Gen has group restrictions, automation fails to execute for lacking permissions for the Actor."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When the user changes restriction roles to any ticket, automation fails to trigger transition rules. due missing permissions from actor (addon_com.codebarrel.addons.automation    addon_com.codebarrel.addons.automation user is not able to read issues that are restricted to other roles where this user is not assigned (by default is assigned to *atlassian-addons-project-access* role), you cannot add roles to this user, because security groups or the user itself is not assignable.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an automation rule that is triggered in any transition.   # Create an issue.   # Change restrictions to the *Administrator* role only.   # Transition this issue.   # Automation rule must fail due to a lack of permissions on Actor.    h3. Expected Results  Rule Execution    h3. Actual Results  Actor does not have permission to view one or more issues, or the issue was deleted (please check permissions and issue security levels):  Comment (id:10134) for issue NGS-1 (id:10163)    h3. Workaround  We can add this user to any role using email instead of username and this solves the issue(<EMAIL>). But this is not what is expected from this solution, fact that we are able to find this user with just email is another bug that in this case is helping."""

summary: "Scroll bar does not allow the user to access all issues in issues search"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    For some customers, when using Chrome, even though all the extensions are disabled and/or using incognito windows, when clicking on """"All Issues"""" (/<USER>software/c/projects/<projectKey>/issues?filter=allissues), the issue search does not show all the issues that are part of the project or that are a result from the filter you do.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    This does not happen to all the users that use Chrome browser, so it was not possible to reproduce this. On this cases, the issue needs to be verified during a call meeting with the customer.  h3. Expected Results    You should be able to use the scroll feature to access all the issues:    !image-2020-08-18-18-43-56-429.png|width=296,height=524!  h3. Actual Results    At first, the scroll bar is correctly shown. However, after some seconds (around 1-2 seconds max) it gets broken, not allowing the user to acccess all the issues:    !image-2020-08-18-18-44-35-431.png|width=222,height=393!  h3. Workaround    The only workaround by now is to use another browser. We've confirmed that this works fine when using Edge."""

summary: "Jira Administrators without site access still receive emails about projects being archived or moved to trash"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira Administrators (users that are part of any group with the *Jira Administration* permission) that had their site access revoked, but weren't removed from those groups still receive emails about projects being archived or moved to trash.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Toggle off the *Has access on site* button for a user that is <USER>(keep them on those groups)   ** !image-2020-08-19-14-19-03-920.png|width=314,height=351!   # Archive or move a project to the trash    h3. Expected Results    Jira Administrators that had their site access revoked shouldn't receive any emails about projects that were archived or moved to trash.    h3. Actual Results    The users that were Jira Administrators keep receiving emails when a project is archived or moved to the trash. These users will get information like:   * The project name   * Who performed that action   * When that was performed   * And the link for that instance    h3. Workaround    The only thing that can be done to fix this is to remove that user from all the Jira Administration groups.     !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "The CSV file importer allows users to add issues to an active and a future sprint at the same time"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Using the UI, you can add an issue only to one sprint at the time: an active sprint or a future sprint.    Using the CSV importer under the External System Import feature, Jira allows issues to be added to an active sprint and a future sprint at the same time, causing several side effects in the reports and boards    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Jira classic project  # Add issues to the project  # Create 2 sprints in this project  # Add some issues to one of the sprints and start it  # Export the issues of this project to a CSV file  # Edit the CSV file including one more """"Sprint"""" column and add a reference to the future sprint in the issues added to the started sprint. Save the file  # Import the CSV file  # Verify the issues    h3. Expected Results    The import CSV fails and an error message says that the file isn't valid    h3. Actual Results    The import succeed and the issues are added to both sprints      h3. Workaround    Edit the """"Sprint"""" field of the affected issues via UI, adding them only to the active sprint"""

summary: "Users cannot clone issues if the field required workflow validator includes the field Attachment."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Users cannot clone issues if the *field required validator* includes the field *Attachment*.   - If the field required validator which checks for the field Attachment is added to the initial create transition in the workflow, then the creation of tickets with attachments work as expected. However, cloning the same issue (with attachments) results in the error defined in the validator.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a project.   # Add the field required validator to the initial create transition in the workflow that checks for the field Attachment.   # Create a new issue in the project with an attachment (_Issue is successfully created as expected_)   # Clone the newly created issue with attachments (_Clone operation fails with the error defined in the validator._)    h3. Expected Results    Clone operation should succeed as the validated field """"Attachment"""" is present in the issue.  h3. Actual Results    Clone operation fails with the error defined in the validator.  h3. Workaround    The workaround to successfully clone the issue is to remove the field """"Attachment"""" from the field required validator in the project workflow."""

summary: "Improve attachment upload speed in new issue view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Uploading any attachment in new issue view takes longer than old view.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open any ticket   # Drag and drop any attachment     h3. Expected Results    Attachment should be uploaded faster in new issue view (similar to old issue view)    h3. Actual Results    Attachment takes longer time to upload in new issue view    h3. Notes    Did some testing by attaching 9Mb csv file and here are the results:  * Old issue view: 4-7 secs (/)   * New issue view: 2-5 min (x)    h3. Workaround    * Disable new issue view (Jira Labs) from profile settings (It won't work for next-gen projects)  ** https://URL.atlassian.net/secure/ViewPersonalSettings.jspa  * Add *?oldIssueView=true* at the end of the URL  ** https://URL.atlassian.net/browse/<issue key>*?oldIssueView=true*"""

summary: "Development field not visible in the ""New issue view"" if it is hidden alone in the ""HIDE WHEN EMPTY"" section of the Issue layout. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The “development” field is not visible in the new issue view when it is alone set to be “hidden when empty” from the Issue layout. If other fields are included to be hidden when empty with the “Development” field, then on clicking the “show more fields” not show the correct count of fields hidden and the tip tool does not mention the “development” field.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Drag only the Development field to the """"HIDE WHEN EMPTY"""" section in the Issue layout and save changes.   # Next, Along with the Development field, add another field that qualifies as empty in the """"HIDE WHEN EMPTY"""" section and save changes.    h3. Expected Results   # On hiding the Development field alone, the """"show 1 more field"""" link should be visible in the new issue view.   # If there are more fields hidden with the Development field, the show <NUM> field(s) should reflect the correct number of fields that are hidden and the tip should mention the Development field name along with the other fields that are hidden.    h3. Actual Results   # In the New issue view, the Development field and the """"show 1 more field"""" link is not visible.   # It will say """"show 1 more field"""",(total number of fields hidden -1) but there are 2 fields actually hidden! And the tip will not reflect the """"Development"""" field name!    h3. Workaround    Move the development field out of the show more section."""

summary: "Ability to hide third-party app fields"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, the new JIRA issue view does not offer a way of hiding fields from third party apps.    h3. Suggested Solution  Add the ability to hide these fields."""

summary: "No site access - Notification received for moving project to trash."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The users who are part of the <USER>administrators group tend to receive notifications for the below, even though their site access is revoked (not removed from site) and part of the <USER>administrator group.   * Project moved to trash (Not necessarily the user need be a """"project lead"""" of the project)   * Project deleted from the trash   * Project will be deleted in """"XX"""" days message.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a user to <USER>administrator group   # Can give them a """"Trusted"""" role   # Revoke site access   # Move the project to trash   # The user will receive a notification that the """"project is moved to trash by xyz""""    h3. Expected Results    Notifications should not be sent to the users who do not have site access.  h3. Actual Results    !image-2020-08-22-15-33-17-837.png|width=390,height=179!  h3. Workaround    Remove user from <USER>administrator group."""

summary: "Attaching a file to Description fails in New Issue View"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When trying to attach a file to the case in New Issue View through Attach Button, it fails for few files. Adding same files to the comment is successful  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Open the issue in New Issue View   * Click on Attach tab and select the File to be attached    !penyu_bug.gif|width=243,height=175!    h3. Expected Results    File should successfully attach to the case  h3. Actual Results    File fails to attach to the issue in new issue view  h3. Workaround   * Can be attached to the same issue from Old Issue view     """

summary: "No error when quick creating an Epic child when the project only have Epic Issues created"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a project only contains Epics, quick creating an Epic Child is not displaying any error.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a project with only Epic at the Issue Types   # Add an Epic child from another project   # Try to quick create a new Epic Child    # There is no error displayed to the end-user    h3. Expected Results    A pop-up message explaining that en Epic can't have another Epic as Child or that there are no Issue Types to create child Issues on the Epic  h3. Actual Results    Nothing, please see attached video:  [^screencast-starless.atlassian.net-2020.08.25-10_43_00.webm]  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Issue with adding attachments ( .doc, .txt ) on Log work comments"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Adding attachments with comments using the log work functionality may cause the comment the be lost or the attachment to now be adde.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an issue and go to the *...* > *Log work*    # Add a value to *Time spent*, """"5m"""" as example   # Then at the comment section write a comment, then add a .doc or .txt below this first comment, then add a comment below the attachment    h3. Expected Results    The comments above and below the attachment are added, as well as the attachment.     !expected.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    Testing on JSD classic projects what happens is that only the comment above is added, and the comment below as well as the attachment are lost.      !JSD.png|thumbnail!     Testing with JSW classic projects, sometimes the attachment is the only information added, other times the comments are added without the attachment.     !Jira software.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Public issues not loading in the same issue view page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    While navigating anonymously in the all issues filter page from a public project, selecting different issues than the first one makes the page to load again showing only the details from the first one.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Make a project public   # In incognito window access the All issues filter from that project, for example, https://<site name>.atlassian.net/<USER>software/c/projects/<project key>/issues?filter=allissues  # Try to select different issues from the results  https://<USER>getcloudapp.com/Blu5oP8E    h3. Expected Results  The selected issue details should be displayed in the same page    h3. Actual Results  The page is reloaded automatically selecting again the first issue    h3. Workaround    Open the issues in different tabs by right-clicking and selecting open in a new tab"""

summary: "%%CURRENT_DATETIME%% in post function doesn't set the correct date on Date Picker fields "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When you create a post-function on the workflow to add %%CURRENT_DATETIME%% on a custom field Date Picker field, it comers one day after date. It depends on what time you trigger this post-function.   Here we have an example:   !Screen Shot 2020-08-26 at 17.03.02.png|thumbnail!        h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a post-function on the workflow to edit Date Picker field with %%CURRENT_DATETIME%%;  # Perform the steps to trigger the post-function;  # Normally it happens at the end of the day;    h3. Expected Results    Date in Date Picker field is filled with the *wrong* date.    h3. Actual Results    Date in Date Picker field is filled with the *correct* date.    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no workaround for this matter.  """

summary: "Ability to disable the multiple sections with two separate scroll bars in new issue view"
description: """Currently, when viewing an issue in the new issue view, the main body of the issue has its own section (and scrollbar) as does the contextual fields section.    Some users find it annoying that there are multiple sections with their own scrollbars and it would be nice if there was an option for users to configure that would affectively combine those two sections into a single section with a single scroll bar."""

summary: "Not able to select an Epic with the keyboard in the creation screen"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When creating a ticket if you start typing the Epic in the Epic link field and then with the keyboard arrows select the Epic and hit enter/return, an error is displayed.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Start the creation process   # In the creation screen start typing in the Epic link field   # Select one of the epics with the keyboard and hit enter/return   [https://<USER>getcloudapp.com/xQuLzR0g]    h3. Expected Results    The Epic highlighted will be select, this happens if nothing is typed in the field  h3. Actual Results    An error is displayed """"Epic with key 'epic' does not exist.""""   [https://<USER>getcloudapp.com/xQuLzR0g]  h3. Workaround    Select the Epic with the space bar instead of the enter key"""

summary: "Jira notifications don't render GIFs added using GIPHY"
description: """h3. Summary  Jira email notifications triggered by adding GIFs in issue comments don't render GIFs in the notifications and shows an error message    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # In a Jira issue, add a GIF from GIPHY in issue comments  # Check the Jira email notification received for the above action   # Notification shows below error message  Unable to embed resource: <GIF name> of type application/octet-stream    h3. Expected Result  GIFs should be rendered in Jira notifications     h3. Actual Result  GIFs fails to render and shows an error message    Note: The email notification format is set to HTML in Jira system default user preferences and user personal settings    h3. Workaround  There is no workaround at the moment."""

summary: "Confluence link showing an error message after the site rename"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After the site rename, all the issues that have a confluence page linked to it were affected. When I check the issue, all the confluence links are showing an error message: """"Confluence site not linked"""". However, If I click on the confluence link, I'm able to access it. The problem is that it is only showing an error message instead of the information of the confluence link.    I've also linked a confluence page after the site rename and the error was not shown, the problem is only for the links previously linked with an issue.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an issue.   # Link a confluence page to the issue.   # Rename the instance.   # Check the confluence link on the issue.    h3. Expected Results    I'm able to see the confluence link without the error message.  h3. Actual Results    I'm not able to see the confluence link title, only if I open it I will see what is inside of this link.       h3. Workaround    No workaround."""

summary: "When using the 'Enter' key after selecting an option from any dropdown filed, the system gives an error message"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When using the 'Enter' key after selecting an option from any dropdown filed, the system gives an error message  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Select any dropdown field   # Start typing the letters which will help you find the correct option you need from it   # When you find it, select the option with the arrow and hit Enter    h3. Expected Results  When you hit 'Enter' the field should finish populating the selected option  h3. Actual Results    You will get an error message with the letters of your search saying:  """"FIELD with key 'Test' does not exist.""""    This is due to the system interpreters that you finish the issue creation  h3. Workaround    Once you find the correct option you would like to use on that field, go down with the arrow and hit *TAB* from your keyboard"""

summary: "When issue is cloned, existing resolution date is copied over"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When cloning an issue, if the _Resolution_ field is already set, its value is copied into the new issue that was just created. This happens both for Classic and Next-Gen project.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # When accessing any issue, clicks on """"..."""" and then on Clone   # The status of the just created issue will be set as the very first of the workflow, but its Resolution will be copied from the clone issue    h3. Expected Results    The Resolution value should be cleared  h3. Actual Results    The Resolution value is copied from the clone issue. This problem happens both in Next-Gen project AND Classic projects.    [https://<USER>getcloudapp.com/Qwu0nPD9]  h3. Workaround    +Both Next-Gen and Classic projects+    Access the board and transition the issue to another status that is not Done/Resolved/Closed. Then, move the issue back to the very first status of the applied workflow:    [https://<USER>getcloudapp.com/6quezd5z]    +For Classic projects+    It's possible to add a post-function in the Create transition (*right before the _Create the Issues Originally_ post-function*) in order to clear the Resolution field, as shown below:    !image-2020-08-31-12-20-38-148.png|width=650,height=254!"""

summary: "Epic dropdown field in Create Issue modal is not keyboard accessible"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Epic dropdown field in Create Issue modal is not keyboard accessible  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Use a keyboard   # Try to pick an epic    h3. Expected Results    it is accessible  h3. Actual Results    its not    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:    h3. Workaround    use a mouse"""

summary: "While changing project in Connect in SPA, when the app tries to do transition, the sidebar is hidden."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The bug was raised by vendor that in case when changing project in Connect in SPA, when app try to do transition the sidebar is hidden. After doing reload everything is working fine again.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to App section > Select the Application     2. Change the Project, within the App, and then the sidebar will get hidden.    h3. Expected Results    The Sidebar should not get hidden in changing the project with the application.    h3. Actual Results  The Sidebar is getting hidden upon changing the project.    h3. Workaround    On refreshing the browser, the sidebar again starts appearing.  """

summary: "Upload of Excel files (CSV, XLSX) with over 7MB get stuck at the very end when using the new issue view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Upload of Excel files (CSV, XLSX) with over 7MB get stuck at the very end when using the new issue view.    Note: we only tested with small files (less than 1MB) that this did not occur and with files over 7MB. We can't tell yet if this occurs for files between 1MB and -7MB.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open any issue detail in the new issue view   # Upload a XLSX file that is over 7MB   # Wait as usual to finish   # You'll notice that it gets stuck at the very end like the example below.   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  # File should upload as usual    h3. Actual Results  # Upload is stuck    h3. Workaround  Disable Jira Labs by clicking in your user avatar, heading to personal settings and disabling it through the toggle button"""

summary: "Quick/Email Created Issues on Next-Gen projects are not respecting configured Restrictions"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When creating Issues on a Next-Gen project using the *+ Create Issue* button or the *Incoming Mail* feature the configured *Restrictions* are not respected.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set a Role *Restriction* on a certain Issue Type of a Next-Gen project   !image-2020-08-28-12-43-48-586.png|width=518,height=218!   # Create a new Issue using the + Create Issue button:   !image-2020-08-28-12-46-54-678.png|width=250,height=100!    h3. Expected Results    The *Restriction* should be set to the newly created Issues  h3. Actual Results    The *Restriction* is not set to the newly created Issues  h3. Workaround    Create Issues using the *Create* button from the top bar"""

summary: "Web items at jira.navigator.pluggable.items are not well supported"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The icons for webitems displayed at <USER>navigator.pluggable.items can easily be chopped off or missing completely. Also, the name of the web item at this location is not displayed.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an app that declares four web items at <USER>navigator.pluggable.items with the following icons; (A) 32x32 PNG, (B) 16x16 PNG, (c) 16x16 SVG, (d) 32x32 SVG.   # Navigate to the issue search view and observe the web items.    h3. Expected Results    The icons for all web items are displayed in full and a tooltip is provided displaying the name of the web item.  h3. Actual Results    None of the web items have tooltips and only the icon for web item (b) is displayed in full. The upper left quadrant of icon (A) is displayed and the icons for web items (C) and (D) are not displayed.  h3. Workaround    Only web items with 16x16 PNG icons work at this location."""

summary: "Getting ""Something's gone wrong"" when we access the issue’s URL directly."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Getting """"Something's gone wrong"""" when we access the issue’s URL directly.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access Jira Core Classic Business Project    # Create an issue    # Click this issue via Board   # Copy the issue’s URL   # Open a new tab in the browser   # Past the URL and access it    h3. Expected Results    The issue is shown.    h3. Actual Results     We get """"Something's gone wrong"""" error   !image1.png|width=445,height=368!      Error on Console:     Splunk logs:    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Grant ""Browse Project"" permission to ""User Custom Field Value"" makes project visible to all users"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}  *NOTE:* This bug report is for *JIRA Cloud*. Using *JIRA Server*? [See the corresponding bug report|http://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-37117].  {panel}     h3. Issue Summary  Grant """"Browse Project"""" permission to """"User custom field value (XXXX)"""" makes the project is visible to all users. Regardless of whether that field is filled or not.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Go to the permission scheme > grant """"Browse Project"""" permission to """"User custom field value (XXXX)""""     h3. Expected Results  Project should not visible to all users here: https://<sitename>.atlassian.net/projects    h3. Actual Results  Project is visible to all users here: https://<sitename>.atlassian.net/projects    h3. Workaround  Remove the  """"User custom field value (XXXX)"""" in the Borwse project permission """

summary: "Custom user picker field is unable to suggest users in basic search"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Custom user picker field is unable to suggest users in basic search    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open a basic searc   # Add a user picker field   # Search for a user.     h3. Expected Results    The field should give suggestions on users that can be used for the search    h3. Actual Results    The field is unable to give suggestions.      !error.png|thumbnail!       h3. Workaround    Switch to advance search and perform the user search there. The custom field can give suggestions.      !workaround.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Activity Stream gadget not working on a single user filter"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Activity Stream gadget not working on a single user filter  h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Select any Dashboard  # Edit Dashboard  # Add gadget  # select """"Activity Stream"""" gadget  # Edit the gadget and filter by """"username"""" and select the user you wish to filter by    h3. Expected Results  Activity Stream to show the related user activity    h3. Actual Results  Message showing: """"No activity was found""""    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Exporting issue via CSV with checkbox without value makes the checkbox not be exported on the CSV"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If you create a Jira search with all the issues have no value for a checkbox custom field, if you export this search or filter as a CSV file, the CSV file will not create a column for this checkbox custom field.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a checkbox custom field, add some optional values on it and add it to a screen  # Create a filter and search for some issues that *does not have any value* on this checkbox custom field  # Export these issues as a CSV file using the option *All fields*    h3. Expected Results  The checkbox custom field should create a column on the CSV file    h3. Actual Results  No column is created for this custom field. It's like it does not exists    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Field ""Teams"" from Advanced Roadmap is not displayed in Jira Mobile app (Android/iOS)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Field """"Teams"""" from Advanced Roadmap is not displayed in Jira Mobile app    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add the field Teams from Advanced Roadmap to the issue screen    h3. Expected Results  At the UI, the field is displayed with or without a value. The expected is that in the Jira Mobile app the field should be available too.    h3. Actual Results  The field is not displayed at all.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "CSV import fails to set user picker fields (e.g assignee, reporter, custom field)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  CSV import (through issue navigator) fails to map the user for *User Picker* fields are set if using email address. The following error appears for validation:   !User import.png|thumbnail!     Even though the user is valid and email address is correct.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Set a user picker field/column in CSV file with email address. For example    # Assuming *developer* as the custom field for the user picker  # Perform CSV import through *Issue navigator* (search issue page) > *Import issues from CSV*    h3. Expected Results  Issue is created    h3. Actual Results  * Following error appears for validation:   !User import.png|thumbnail!       h3. Workaround  # Import through *Jira settings* > *System* > *External System Import* > *CSV Import*  # Or, Use AAID in the CSV file"""

summary: "When accessing the issue type Story under Epic in old issue view lands directly on Epic. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When accessing the issue type Story under Epic in old issue view lands directly on Epic.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Story under Epic.   # Access the Story in old issue view and you will be directly landed to the Epic instead of Story.     h3. Expected Results    Accessing the Story in old issue view should be landing on Story and not on Epic.       h3. Actual Results    Story in old issue view Lands on Epic.    h3. Workaround    Access the Story in new issue view """

summary: "Using issue navigator's basic search on a custom field with a colon character in the name returns the wrong results"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Using issue navigator's basic search on a custom field with a colon character in the name returns the wrong results.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field with a colon character (i.e. {{:}}) in the name e.g. """"My example field:""""   # Modify an issue to have a known value for this field e.g. 42   # Navigate to the issue navigator (click """"Filters"""", then """"Advanced issue search"""")   # Use the basic search to add a filter of your custom field = known value e.g. """"My example field:""""=42    !Screenshot_2020-09-04 Issue navigator - Jira.png|thumbnail!  h3. !Screenshot_2020-09-04 Issue navigator - Jira(1).png|thumbnail!  h3. !Screenshot_2020-09-04 Issue navigator - Jira(2).png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results   * The issue list only has issues with the given custom field value   * The refinement bar shows that your custom field is set to the value you specified    !Screenshot_2020-09-04 Issue navigator - Jira(4).png|thumbnail!    h3. Actual Results   * The issue list shows all issues, regardless of the custom field value   * The refinement bar shows that your custom field is set to """"All""""    !Screenshot_2020-09-04 Issue navigator - Jira(3).png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround   # Remove the colon character (i.e. {{:}}) from your custom field name   # Click """"Switch to JQL"""" and build your search using JQL"""

summary: "Unlicensed Atlassian Accounts receives mention notifications when granted browse projects permission from the permission scheme"
description: """h3. Summary  Unlicensed Atlassian Accounts (customers for <USER> are receiving Jira mention notifications (when they shouldn't) if they are granted browse projects permission by a group. It happens even if the issue does not have a valid request type on it.    h3. Steps to reproduce  # Create a user with no application accesses;  # Create a group and add the user above;  # Go to a <USER>project and add that group to the browse projects permission;  # Create an issue, with or without request type, and set the user as the reporter;  # Mention that user on an internal or public comment.    h3. Expected results  Since the user does not have any application access, they don't have access to the issue and shouldn't receive Jira notifications.    h3. Actual result  The unlicensed user (customer) will receive mention notifications for internal and public notifications.    h3. Notes  If the unlicensed user clicks in the *View issue* button from the notification, they will be redirected to the portal since they don't have access to Jira.    h3. Workaround  # Remove the group from the browse projects permission;  OR  # Remove the user from the group."""

summary: "Can not create subtasks from Epic when there is validator on New Issue View. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When creating a ticket under EPIC with validators on New Issue View, Jira shows a connection error message from the ticket. Unable to create Task under Epic, limited to creating subtasks directly under Epic which eliminates a third level. Also cannot see which Epic a task belongs to on the new issue view.    Reference: https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/FEEDBACK-179351    h3. Steps to Reproduce    * From the new issue view select 'Create issue in epic' button or  """"+"""" button above the issues.  * The connection error """"We couldn't create the child issue"""" shows from the ticket and couldn't create a subtask.    h3. Expected Results  Subtasks created successfully on the new issue view.    h3. Actual Results  The connection error shows up from the ticket.      h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available  """

summary: "Importing issues via issue search > CSV import is adding the user who performed the import as a watcher of all imported issues."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Importing issues via issue search > CSV import is adding the user who performed the import as a watcher of all imported issues.    The same does not apply to when we use the """"External System Import"""" CSV import option, the user who performed the import is not added as a watcher of all imported issues.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a CSV file with an issue to import in Jira   # Open the issue search and select the option to import issues via CSV.    h3. Expected Results    The issues are imported and the user who performed the CSV import will not be added as a watcher of any imported issue.  h3. Actual Results    The user who performed the import is being added as a watcher of all imported issues.  h3. Workaround    Use the """"External System Import"""" CSV import option instead of the one available at the issue search page.   # Choose !https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/files/945104532/953138831/1/1530296096612/cog.png|height=18! > *System*.   # In the Import and Export section, click *External System Import* and then click *CSV*.   # Choose your CSV source file. """

summary: "Performance issues when opening too many options of Multi select custom fields"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Multi select custom fields load all options at once when inserting selecting a value, causing performance issues when there is big number of options.    We had a use case in which there were over 10,000 options to select and this would either make it very slow to load the options or even crash the browser depending on the user's environment set up.    This seems to be related to how Jira Labs (new issue view) handles the field search as when using the old issue view (Jira Labs Off) the search is almost instant.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a multi select custom field   # Add over a thousand options   # Create an issue in which the field is visible and editable   # Try to select an option using the new issue view    h3. Expected Results  Search of options should load fast    h3. Actual Results  Search is slow potentially crashing the browser    h3. Workaround  *Option 1:* disable Jira Labs by accessing your user's personal settings (by clicking in your avatar) and toggling it off.    *Option 2:*   # Create a new custom field with the *Labels* type to use instead, which has a more optimized search  # Export all the issues with the current custom field value filled in  # Import it back mapping the original column of the custom field to the new custom field of the type labels; this should copy the value of one custom field to another"""

summary: "JSON importer unable to handle a custom field with multiple issue type scopes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The importer fails when  trying to import against a custom field that has multiple issue type scope.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field (ie. Custom Summary)   # Add a context and restrict it to issue type Bugs   # Add a second context and restrict it to issue type Task   # Import a new *Story* issue via JSON and populate the Custom Summary field.     !Config.png|thumbnail!       h3. Expected Results    The JSON importer should be able to handle the data or error out gracefully specifying which custom field cannot be handled.     ie. Error : Custom Summary is currently not available for issue Type Story.    h3. Actual Results    The importer fails with a vague error and there is no indication which custom field was causing the error      !error import.png|thumbnail!         This gets problematic when there is a huge number of custom fields being imported.     h3. Workaround    Check the custom fields configurations one by one to see which one could be causing the failure. """

summary: "Blank screen when editing or creating actions"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When attempting to edit an action from an existing rule or creating a new one in Automation for Jira, you get a blank screen.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new rule using the *Assign issue* action    h3. Expected Results    The rule is created and saved as expected.    h3. Actual Results    You get a blank screen   !Automation_rules_-_Jira.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "JQL Related Issues Conditions does not work fine for some JQL queries"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using the following query into a Related Issues Condition, Automation is not able to look for existing issues in the instance.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a rule using Issue Created   # Add a Related Issue Condition with the following query:      The rule will inform in the audit log that there was a match with the just created issue, ignoring all the existing issues from the instance.  h3. Expected Results    The rule should be able to look for issues which summary contains the same summary of the trigger issue and which key is different from the trigger issue key.  h3. Actual Results    The condition matches the just created issues even though the query specifies the key needs to be different from the trigger issue:    !image-2020-09-07-12-25-22-806.png|width=821,height=208!    The same problem happens when using Manual trigger and even Issue transitioned trigger.  h3. Workaround    It's possible to use a Branch to look for issues which summary is similar from the just created issue summary:    !image-2020-09-07-12-32-32-583.png|width=770,height=284!"""

summary: "URLs pasted from Invision into comment or description won't open with left click"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When pasting URLs from other webapps such as Invision, Jira attempts to create a smart link.    Currently, if you paste a URL from Invision directly in a comment or in the description, you won't be able to open it by left clicking on it.      h3. Steps to Reproduce    * Copy the URL from your Invision app (e.g. https://<your site>.invisionapp.com/<USER><file code>#/screens);  * Paste into a comment.    h3. Expected Results    Left clicking on that link will open the target URL.    h3. Actual Results    Nothing happens when clicking on the link with the left button.    h3. Workaround    Click with the right button -> open in a new tab.     Or, use use *Ctrl + Z* or *Cmd + Z* to prevent the link from rendering prior to saving it."""

summary: "Custom field with an accent does not appear on ""Columns to display"" in the Dashboard - Gadget"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Custom fields with an accent like """"ó"""" or """"ã"""" does not appear on the dashboard.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field with the name similar to """"órgão"""".   # Open a Dashboard.   # Add a new gadget, for example, Filter results.   # Add the field """"órgão"""" to """"Columns to display"""".    h3. Expected Results    Fields with an accent like """"órgão"""" should appear in the Columns.  h3. Actual Results   * The field will be not saved and this field with an accent will not appear.   * I've recorded a video with the current behavior: [Click here to access the video.|https://<USER>getcloudapp.com/yAubB7Qr]    h3. Workaround    Remove the accent from the field."""

summary: "Opening Misc under Text Formatting Notation Help leads to an error on the page when using Polish language."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Misc option in the Text Formatting Notation returns an error on the UI.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Change your language to Polish from account language preferences in [https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/account-preferences].   # Click on the Create issue button to start creating an issue.   # Click on the question mark you see under the Description page:   !Screenshot 2020-09-09 at 3.34.09 PM.png|thumbnail!   # On this page, browse to {{Różne}} and you will see an error as seen below:   !Screenshot 2020-09-09 at 3.35.34 PM.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results    The Misc option should display as it does in the English language:   !Screenshot 2020-09-09 at 3.36.22 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    An error is returned:   !Screenshot 2020-09-09 at 3.35.34 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior when using the Polish language on your account. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Cascading Select-List field type is not working in Basic Search"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    There is a cascading select list type field that is not working in Basic Search but it working in Advance mode. I have replicated the same in my test instance. I noticed that if I do a basic search for a cascading type filed, the option is not getting selected but if I use advance mode, it is working perfectly fine. I am attaching a screen-recording for your reference.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open Global Issue Navigator in Basic Search   # Click More> select the cascading select type field and choose a select option.   # In Basic Search, the field option is not getting selected   # In Advance JQL, the search is working perfectly fine.    h3. Expected Results    We should get the result for Cascading Select List in Basic Search  h3. Actual Results    Not getting the result in the Basic Serch but with Advance JQL, results are proper.  h3. Workaround   # Please use Advance JQL to search for the Cascading Select List field.  """

summary: "Can't unset a select field added by Connect app in the new issue view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If a Connect App, for example [Tempo Timesheets: Time Tracking & Report|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/6572/tempo-timesheets-time-tracking-report?hosting=cloud&tab=overview] creates a select custom field, e.g. Account in case of Tempo, this custom field cannot be cleared in new issue view. If the current value is removed, after reloading the issue the previous value is there again. In old issue view there is no problem, if value of Account custom field is cleared, it stays empty.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Tempo Timesheets   # Create test accounts in its settings   # Add Account custom field to the screens of a project   # Open an issue in new issue view   # Choose an account in Account custom field and save it   # Reload the issue in Browser   # Clear Account custom field   # Reload the issue in browser    h3. Expected Results  Account custom field should remain empty after clearing its content    h3. Actual Results  If Account custom field is cleared using new issue view, it gets back the previous value.  If Account custom field is cleared using old issue view, it remains empty.    h3. Workaround  Using the old issue view it is possible to clear the content of a select custom field."""

summary: "When trying to add a ""Log Work"" action and set to copy the value from a field  to Automation rules, the rule can't be published"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When trying to publish a rule where there is a """"Log work"""" action and set to copy the value from a field (created, updated, etc) on """"Date started"""" it does not allow to publish and appear the following error message:     !Screen Shot 2020-09-14 at 18.01.29.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new rule with any trigger;  # Add the """"Log Work"""" action and set to copy a value from a date/time field (as the screenshot below);  # Click to publish.    h3. Expected Results  Publish the rule without any errors    h3. Actual Results  Errors when publishing the rule. Found only an error 500 in a POST.      h3. Workaround    Use smart value, for example:     """

summary: "Accessing anonymously the issue navigator keep refreshing all the time"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If the permission scheme allows anonymous access to the project, when accessing the project anonymously the issue navigator keep refreshing all the time if we select a different issue.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Edit the permission scheme of the project adding the group *Public* to the *Browse Project* project permission.   # Open an incognito window and access <name_of_instace>.atlassian.net/browse/<pkey>   # Select a different issue on the issue navigator    h3. Expected Results  The issue should be loaded    h3. Actual Results  The page loads very slow and it keeps refreshing. See the video below:    *  [^bugAnonimous.mp4]       h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "The page goes blank and loads again at least twice when you try to open an issue from the search filter (*List view*) due to refresh by Connect Addons"
description: """h3. Summary    The page goes blank and loads again at least twice when you try to open an issue from the search filter (*List view*) due to refresh by Connect Addons  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open """"Advanced issue search"""" page (in menu """"Filters > """"Advanced issue search"""")   # Switch to """"List View"""" via the control on the top right;   # Filter a list of issues using a JQL   # Open any issue from the rendered table    h3. Expected Results    The issue view should be opened, fully loaded, and that's nearly it, no visual blinks of the issue view should happen  h3. Actual Results    The issue view opens, loads fully, and (in case of an addon) in a few seconds refresh itself by rendering nothing in place of issue, and in a moment renders issue again.  h3. Notes    This is only happening in """"Search Filters"""" when viewing results in a List view, when you click to view any of the issues.   The issue does not happen in the Detail view of the search filter or when you browse the issue directly like https://<yourinstnace>.atlassian.net/browse/<issue-key>  h3. Workaround    Load the search result in the *Detail view*    Or browse the issue directly https://<yourinstnace>.atlassian.net/browse/<issue-key>"""

summary: "Create Branch link is pointing to the previous Bitbucket server link"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Create Branch link is pointing to the previous Bitbucket link  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1) Create Bitbucket Server on AWS using Atlassian Bitbucket AMI, enable public IP   2) Create application link [http://ec2-1234.ap-southeast-1.compute.amazonaws.com|http://ec2-13-212-72-206.ap-southeast-1.compute.amazonaws.com/] with Jira Software Cloud using public DNS name for Bitbucket server    3) Shutdown Bitbucket Server and start up again. A new public IP address gets assigned and as a result, the public DNS name is changed   4) Delete and recreate application link  [http://ec2-5678-southeast-1.compute.amazonaws.com|http://ec2-13-229-151-164.ap-southeast-1.compute.amazonaws.com/] with Jira Software Cloud and Bitbucket Server   5) Try to create a new branch from any issue in Jira Software, the link points back to the original public DNS name [http://ec2-1234.ap-southeast-1.compute.amazonaws.com|http://ec2-13-212-72-206.ap-southeast-1.compute.amazonaws.com/]  h3. Expected Results    Create branch link gets updated   h3. Actual Results    Create branch link is pointing to the previous link  h3. Workaround    No workaround """

summary: "Multibyte characters in workflows displayed as question mark characters"
description: """Due to the incident [Jira Core Status - Incorrectly displayed multibyte characters|https://<USER>core.status.atlassian.com/incidents/kv3d616x8cyl] happened on 02.09.2020 multibyte characters - for languages like Chinese, Japanese, Russian etc - might be incorrectly displayed in workflows as question mark characters.    If the problem is experienced, please contact Atlassian Support and refer to the incident above.     """

summary: "Using <enter> key in JIRA issue label just cancels the list instead of saving the selected item in the new issue view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Using the new issue view, when pressing """"L"""", for editing the label, select a label and click the enter button, the label is not being saved. Instead, the selection is canceled and the value is not saved.    When making the same procedure in the old issue view, the label is being saved correctly.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open an issue using the kanban board.   # Press """"L"""" and select a label.   # Click enter.    h3. Expected Results    The label is saved correctly.  h3. Actual Results    The label is not being saved, and the selection is canceled.  h3. Workaround    Switch to the old issue view."""

summary: "Changing issue type scheme using the keyboard gives an error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When we try to change the issue type scheme and we press the *Enter* button to move to the next page, there is a 500 error.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access a project   # Go to the *Project Settings > Issue types*   # Click on *Actions > Use a different scheme*    # Select a different issue type scheme and press *Enter*   # Move to the next page pressing Enter    See below a video showing that:   * [^bugIssueTypeScheme.mp4]     h3. Expected Results  The issue type scheme should be changed.    h3. Actual Results  There is a 500 error after mapping the fields      h3. Workaround   Currently, using the mouse we do not face the same issue."""

summary: "Clicking on Parent issue while moving a sub-task to a different parent has no effect"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When trying to move a sub-task to a different parent , we get a list of suggestion of Parent issues to move the sub-task to. However , nothing happens if we click the issue.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Select any sub-task from Jira to move the issue.   # In the confirmation page , start entering the Issue key of the Parent. A list of suggestions are shown.   # Click the parent issue .    h3. Expected Results  Parent issue should get selected and should be populated in the text field.    h3. Actual Results  Nothing happens. The issues are not clickable. Refer screen shot.        h3. Workaround  Type the Issue key in the search box and click on """"Change Parent"""" button. Issue gets moved."""

summary: "Trying to set story point estimate in next-gen project via automation is choosing the wrong field"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using a next-gen project in Jira Cloud, and attempting to setup an automation rule in order to create a new issue based on any trigger, if you attempt to set the Story Point Estimate field on that new issue, it will fail.  The issue is still created, but that value cannot be set by the expected means.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In a next-gen project, Create an automation rule with any trigger (manual for example)   # For the action, select 'create new issue' and choose inside same project (by project key)   # For this example, I selected to create a new Task type issue   # Attempt to use the drop down box to select which fields to set, and choose """"Story Points""""   # Choose a numeric value for that field to set (example 57)   # Publish/save run   # Trigger the automation to run    h3. Expected Results    We expect that a new issue is created and the story point value is 57  h3. Actual Results    Issue is created, but story point estimate value is not set. Automation audit log shows an error like so:    However customfield_10044 is not the Story Point Estimate custom context field in this Next-gen project, but rather the global custom field used for classic projects.  Instead in this case, I found that in my instance the custom field is actually customfield_10088 in this project  h3. Workaround   # First in the Choose fields to set, uncheck Story points (this is technically the wrong field)   # Then at the bottom of the page, scroll down to More options,   # In more options, manually set the field using json like so:     # Save/publish run"""

summary: "If a project workflow contains field related condition/validator, then the available transitions for the issue are not visible even if the condition/validator is met until the page is manually refreshed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a project workflow contains field related condition/validator, then the available transitions for the issue are not visible when the field value is updated to meet the condition/validator until the webpage is manually refreshed  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1) Create a Jira project   2) In the project workflow, add a """"Value field"""" workflow condition:    !Screenshot 2020-09-21 at 7.34.11 PM.png|width=579,height=25!    3) Add the field details as per below example:    !Screenshot 2020-09-21 at 7.33.59 PM.png|width=466,height=182!    4) Save the transition and publish the changes to the workflow   5) Open an issue and confirm that the issue cannot be transitioned as the condition is not met (In this case, story point field is empty)    !Screenshot 2020-09-21 at 7.45.02 PM.png|width=265,height=382!    6) Populate the story point field. The available status transition for the issue is not visible even though the condition is met    !Screenshot 2020-09-21 at 7.45.23 PM.png|width=278,height=421!  h3. Expected Results    The available transitions should be visible in the Jira issue once the field is updated to meet the workflow condition.  h3. Actual Results    The available status transitions are not visible.  h3. Workaround    Refresh the page once the field value is updated to meet the workflow condition to see the available status transitions for the issue."""

summary: "Bulk delete operation fails when trying to delete tasks with subtasks"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Bulk delete operation fails when trying to delete tasks with subtasks  * The subtasks are within the scope of the search scope    h3. Steps to Reproduce    1. Create a Jira project   2. Create 3 Standard tasks with one sub-task each   3. Open Advanced issue search and search for all issues in the project  _Filters > Advanced issue search_   4. Perform bulk delete operation.  h3. Expected Results    All issues under the project are deleted.  h3. Actual Results    Bulk delete operation fails. No error message seen:    !Screenshot 2020-09-21 at 8.38.26 PM.png|width=252,height=269!  h3. Workaround    Delete all sub-tasks first and then delete the remaining issues in the project"""

summary: "Jira issue description shows incorrectly on parent issue when a subtask description is updated"
description: """h3. Summary  When description is added to a subtask, the same description is showed in the parent issue description    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create an issue (Story, Task, Bug)  # Create a subtask under the parent issue created in Step 1  # Add some text in the *Description* field to the sub-task issue > Save  # Close the sub-task and reopen the parent tasks. The description added in the subtask shows in the parent task  # Close the parent task and reopen again, the original description of the task will be shown as expected    h3. Expected Result  Subtasks description should not show in the parent task description field    h3. Actual Result  The subtask description is shown in the parent issue description    h3. Workaround  Closing and reopening the parent task shows the original description    Note: The behavior is noticed only in the modal view of the issue. If the issues are opened in a new tab the behavior is not noticed."""

summary: "CSV import to user picker fields favors portal customers"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    CSV imports to user picker fields favors portal customer accounts over Jira user accounts which means that if 1 email has both <USER>account and a portal customer account, the import will automatically map to the portal customer account rather than the Jira user account.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create <USER>account and portal customer account using the same email  # Perform a CSV import to a user picker field (eg: assignee) using the email as the value    h3. Expected Results    The expected outcome would be that the *Assignee* field would be updated and mapped to the Jira user linked to the email value in the CSV file.    h3. Actual Results    Instead, the portal customer is mapped to the *Assignee* field.    h3. Workaround    The workaround is to use the accountId in your CSV file for user mapping instead of the email address. Alternatively, you could also delete the portal customer account so that the Jira user is the only one that exists with the email."""

summary: "Users are able to populate value to the Text field (read only) from the issue view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Users are able to populate value to the Text field (read only) from the issue view    As per the [documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/custom-fields-types-in-classic-projects-972327807.html]:  {quote}A read-only text label. Only possible to create values programmatically (Used internally for imports from Mantis). Maximum of 255 characters.  {quote}  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Text field (read only) custom field   # Add the field to project screens - create, edit and view screen.   # Attempt to populate value into this field from the issue view    h3. Expected Results    The readonly text field should accept values programatically. So the field should not be editable from the issue view.  h3. Actual Results    The readonly text field is editable from the issue view  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Filter Results gadget doesn't show custom field with special characters/accent"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When you try to add a custom field with special characters or accents in its name to show in the """"Filter Results"""" gadget on a dashboard, it doesn't consider the added custom field.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Add a """"Filter Results"""" gadget to a dashboard;  # Select to show a custom field with special characters:   !Screen Shot 2020-09-23 at 10.37.11.png|thumbnail!   # Save it;  # The custom field doesn't show in the gadget:  !image-2020-09-23-10-38-50-253.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  The gadget should show the custom field selected.      h3. Actual Results  There is no error showing in the requests that the browser is doing and the custom field is not showing in the gadget results.    h3. Workaround    For now, it would be necessary to remove the special character or accents from the custom field."""

summary: "Uploading a file via the JSON/CSV importer fails when the duration reaches 60s. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Uploading a file via the JSON/CSV importer fails when the upload duration reaches 60s.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Use the External System Importer   # Choose CSV or JSON   # Upload a large file.      h3. Expected Results    The large file should be uploaded.    h3. Actual Results    When the upload duration exceeds 60 seconds, the upload will fail with a permission error.          !bug.png|thumbnail!       h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "JQL to find issues that have exactly only one Sprint"
description: """Today, when you filter to return issues on a specific Sprint, it also returns all the issues which were added to previous nested Sprints before:    {{Sprint = Sprint 1}}    We should have a way to search for issues firstly added to that specific Sprint, with no previous Sprints nested to it. You can filter by closedSprints() if the sprint in the JQL is currently active, however, if the current search Sprint is also closed, it will not work. """

summary: "Up/down keys in the issue view don't work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The keys to navigate (Up/Down/Home/PgUp/etc/) don't work when you open the issue from the results of a JQL.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Search for some issues in the Search Issue view.    # Select one of the results and click around the description area to gain the focus.   # Press the down/up keys, it won't work.    # Reload the page, click around the description area to gain the focus.   # Press the down/up keys, this time, it works.    [^bug_navigation.mp4]    h3. Actual Results  the Scroll container doesn't gain the focus when you click on it, unable to use the Keys to navigate.    h3. Expected Results  We should able to use the keys to navigate    h3. Workaround  Reload the page."""

summary: "Changing field value  on a Text field ( Single line ) custom field with ""Parent"" name does not work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If there is a custom field with Text field ( single line) we cannot seem to edit the value if the field name is """"Parent"""".  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field of type Text field ( single line ) called """"Parent-new""""   #  Add this to relevant screens in your project.  # Set some value to it in a Jira issue.  # Now change the custom field name to """"Parent"""".  # Go back to the issue and try to edit this field.    h3. Expected Results  We should be able to edit this field.    h3. Actual Results  We get an error """"We couldnt save your changes , copy the content and then try again""""         h3. Workaround  Rename the custom field """"Parent"""" to something else. """

summary: "Error Message when using URL of an issue from Classic Business Project"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Error Message when using URL of an issue from Classic Business Project  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open a Classic Business Project board and select an issue   # Select the URL from the browser and hit *Enter* in your keyboard    h3. Expected Results    Should reopen the issue again  h3. Actual Results    You will get an error message and a blank page landing in the Board from the Project    !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    [^JRACLOUD-75171.webm]    From the console logs somehow it looks like it is not finding the JSON file from the path:    h3. Workaround    Currently, the only workaround for this is loading the full issue view and copying the link from there to <USER>it"""

summary: "Display custom field ""description"" text in the new issue view"
description: """h3. Summary    Custom fields created by admins have an optional description. In the old issue view edit modal these descriptions are shown so that end users know what to enter in a particular field. The new issue view does not display these descriptions.  h3. Current behaviour    The new issue view does not display custom field descriptions.  h3. Expected behaviour    The new issue view displays custom field descriptions."""

summary: "Expand transitions window instead of scrooling"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Users unable to see all transitions and status for an issue until scrolling on the transitions window on the new issue view. The scrolling bar is displayed only when the user starts scrolling. Users might be unaware there are more options available.    Example: https://<USER>getcloudapp.com/p9uwpNAw  h3. Suggested Solution    Option of expand window as in the old issue view.  h3. Workaround    None."""

summary: "Confluence links not appearing on Issues for old and new view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary:    Confluence links are not visible in the new issue view and an error *wiki page failed to load* shows up instead of the confluence link in the old issue view.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1. Open an issue  2.Link a confluence page in old or new issue view  h3. Expected Results:    Ideally, the confluence links should have been visible as expected in the old issue view and new issue view   !screenshot-4.png|thumbnail!    !screenshot-5.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    In the new issue view, the confluence links are not visible.  In the old issue view, *wiki page failed to load* error pops up.   !screenshot-6.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "'Add People' button is not working as expected from the People screen"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  'Add People' button is not working as expected from the People screen  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to the *People* screen   # Select top-right option *Add People*   # Add an email address and hit *Send*  h3. Expected Results  Should get a message that the invite was sent   h3. Actual Results  You will get an error message:   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   h3. Workaround    Send invites from the User Management screen or even the Drop Down option of the People screen"""

summary: "Field selection not working in the Column configuration of issue navigation"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The user won't be able to choose a specific field from the column configuration in the issue navigator page. Although the custom filed is available for selection, it does not add in the column once opted.    !JQL 1.png|thumbnail!     The problem is observed if the field name and field type is similar in the global custom field and any of the Nextgen project.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  The issue pattern is not consistent.   # Create a custom field as a global customer  # Create a similar custom field and type in any Nextgen project.     h3. Expected Results  The field should add up in the column list in the issue navigator page.    h3. Actual Results    Not able to choose the field.    h3. Workaround    * Renaming either of the customer fields should resolve the problem.  * Create a new field and delete the existing one."""

summary: "Issue history records the text field updates of an issue (Description and custom text fields) with additional ari characters in the old issue view."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Issue history records the text field updates of an issue (Description and custom text fields) with additional characters in the old issue view.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  1) Create a Jira issue  2) Update description or a custom text field in the issue  3) Open the issue in old issue view and check the history tab for the update made in step 2    h3. Expected Results  The old issue view history section should record the changes made to the text field and should not contain additional <USER>entries.    h3. Actual Results  The old issue view history section records the update made to the text field along with addition <USER>information    h3. Workaround  Use the Jira new issue view"""

summary: "Improve adding user error message"
description: """When you disable to send invitations to new users, or you have reached the 10 user free licenses, or you have pre-approved a domain, you got an error when you add an unsubscribed email to a project:    The error says: """"Check your network connection, then give it another try""""    It should be more descriptive: """"Unable to add a user that is not registered to your site"""""""

summary: "Extra scrollbar appearing on the global issue navigator"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When viewing the issue detail view in the global issue navigator there appears to be an extra scrollbar on the right side that scrolls the whole view. This only occurs on the global issue navigator and doesn't occur on any other issue views in Jira.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open any issue in the global issue navigator in the detail view.    h3. Expected Results    A scrollbar will appear on both the description/content side and the context side of the issue view if there is more content available than can be displayed in the browser window.  h3. Actual Results    An extra scroll bar appears that allows users to scroll the page just about 100px up and down.  h3. Workaround    No workaround available to hide the extra scroll-bar in the global issue navigator.     """

summary: "Basic search resets when search results are sorted"
description: """h3. Summary  In Jira search, the basic search criteria is reset when search results are sorted    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # In Jira search, switch to basic search and select a few search criteria. For example, select a project and assignee from the list.  # Search for the results  # Try to sort one of the fields/columns  # The search criteria selected resets    h3. Expected Result  The search criteria selected should be intact after sorting    h3. Actual Result  Search criteria is reset after sorting    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no workaround for this behavior.    Note: Even after the search criteria is reset, the results shown would be still intact and matches the search."""

summary: "Blank screen on A4J rule while trying to add an ""Issue fields condition"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Blank screen on A4J rule while trying to add an """"Issue fields condition""""     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new custom field on your system  # Set the name as: *1. Test*  # Access A4J configuration    # Add an """"Issue fields condition""""  # Select the field created on step one.    h3. Expected Results  * You should be able to configure your conditions.    h3. Actual Results  The page goes completely blank and the below exception is thrown at the console:      *Notes:*  * It does not affect JSD automation rules.     h3. Workaround  You can rename the custom field to something like:    """

summary: "Advanced search shows options for a select list single choice custom field whereas basic search shows no options"
description: """A select list single choice custom field would not work for a user in Basic search while it works in an advanced search.     """

summary: "Filter search in gadget doesn't find all filters"
description: """h3. Summary  In the *Filter Results* gadget, the filter search doesn't show all the filters. If the filter the user is searching for is the 100th filter, typing the filter name doesn't return any results    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create multiple filters (more than 100)  # Try to search for a filter and from the browser call (as shown in the attached screenshot1) make a note of the 100th filter  # Now try to search for the 100th filter and the search doesn't return any results    h3. Expected Result  Users should be able to find all the filters in the gadget filter search    h3. Actual Result  Gadget filter search doesn't show all the filters    h3. Workaround  Use the *Advanced search* option in the gadget and choose the appropriate filter  """

summary: """ORDER BY"" created date and duedate not working from quick filters"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  """"ORDER BY"""" created date and duedate not working from quick filters  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a quick filter using the *ORDER BY* created or duedate ASC or DESC  h3. Expected Results  Show the issues based on the filter  h3. Actual Results  It will not respect the rule from the filter, and they will still show the issues like you did not select any quick filter    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be to use the JQL query in the Advanced Search screen"""

summary: "Accessing an issue from a filter makes scroll bar not working on the Old Issue View"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  By accessing a Jira filter, if you click on an issue and open it on the same page, the scroll bar will no longer work if you're using the Old Issue View.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  (!) To reproduce it, you have to have Jira Labs disables on your Personal Settings.    # Access a Jira filter (the URL will be like: *instance_name.atlassian.net/issues/?filter=<filter_id>*  # Click to open an issue at the same page (do not open it on a different page)  # The URL for this ticket will be like this: *instance_name.atlassian.net/browse/<ISSUE_KEY>?filter=<filter_id>*    h3. Expected Results  The scroll bar should work as expected    h3. Actual Results  The issue gets stuck and doesn't scroll up or down    h3. Workaround  * Use the New Issue View  * Or open the ticket on a new tab"""

summary: "No longer possible to use the arrow keys to select among existing EpicLinks using up/down arrowkey"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  It is no longer possible to use the arrow keys to select among existing EpicLinks using the up/down arrow key    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new issue   # select the epic link using the up/down arrow key from the dropdown.    h3. Expected Results  Possibility to use arrow keys (or other keyboard shortcuts) to select an existing epic link from a dropdown list.    h3.
