Actual Results  Not possible to use arrow keys or any other keyboard shortcut to select an existing epic link from a dropdown list    h3. Workaround  a) Once you find the correct option you would like to use on that field, go down with the arrow and hit TAB from your keyboard  b) Use the mouse"""

summary: "Changing the status of a subtask issue is not updated in the parent issue view until the parent issue is re-opened/refreshed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Changing the status of a subtask issue is not updated in the parent issue view until the parent issue is re-opened  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Under a Jira project, create an issue of standard issue type (Story, bug, etc)   * In the newly created issue view, create a subtask issue and open it.   * In the subtask issue view, change the status of the issue (Example: To Do -> In Progress)   * Now click on the parent issue seen on the subtask view to navigate back to the parent issue view.    Here is a screen recording of the issue: [^Screen Recording 2020-10-05 at 3.56.03 PM.mov]  h3. Expected Results    The subtask status should be updated in """"In Progress""""  h3. Actual Results    The subtask status is not updated and shows the previous status.  h3. Workaround    Re-open the parent issue to see the updated subtask status."""

summary: "Transitioning a issue with Automation for Jira doesn't re-index the transitions for new status in the new view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Transitioning an issue with Automation for Jira doesn't re-index the transitions for new status in the new view.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new rule in *Project Automation* that makes a transition when the issue is assigned   # Assign the issue using the new view    h3. Expected Results    Both the status and transitions will update without refreshing the page.  h3. Actual Results    The status is updated, but the transitions that appear are from the previous status.  h3. Workaround    Refreshing the page will update the transitions.  h4. Demonstration   !bug.gif! """

summary: "Issue keys on comments made in the old issue view are rendered with a ""#icft=KEY"" breadcrumb breaking the link for customers"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When commenting on the issue key in the Old Issue View, the issue key is converted to a link. However, when seeing this link in the New Issue View we can see that an unwanted *#icft=<issue_key>* URL parameter was added to the issue.    This parameter causes e-mail and Slack notification to break as the example below:    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Comment in an issue in the Old Issue View either using bracket notation [ISSUEKEY-123] or simply typing ISSUEKEY-123. This can also be done using Automation by adding a comment with the Smart Value \{triggerIssue};   # Access the New Issue View and mouse over the issue URL.   # It is possible to see the *#icft=<issue_key>* breadcrumb added to the issue URL.    h3. Expected Results    The issue URL won't contain the *#icft=<issue_key>* breadcrumb.  h3. Actual Results    The issue URL looks as below:  !Image 2020-10-05 at 10.09.48 AM.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround    Use the new issue view as the conversion won't happen."""

summary: "Add-ons can change an issue's ""updated"" timestamp even if they don't actually update anything in the issue"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Some add-ons send empty """"PUT"""" requests towards an issue's URL. That PUT request changes the issue's """"Updated"""" field to the timestamp of the request, even if nothing has actually been changed in the issue. This breaks the """"order by Updated DESC"""" sorting in JQL    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Have a premium subscription with Automation 4 Jira   # Use Jira normally   # Perform a JQL with """"order by updated DESC""""    h3. Expected Results  The first issues are the ones that have been edited / changed most recent    h3. Actual Results  Sometimes, it is possible to have issues with the """"updated"""" timestamp very recent, even if they weren't updated for a very long time.     The screenshot below is taken in October 2020 and the issue is seen as updated 5 hours ago. Investigation shows that Automation for Jira sent an empty PUT request   !image__14_.png|thumbnail!         h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Custom Text fields not getting updating with the inline edit in the old issue view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Text fields (Multiple lines) are not properly updating in the old issue view of Jira when using the inline editing.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a text custom field (E.g: Acceptance Criteria) and add it to your project screens   # Create a new issue and add any value to the text field created (e.g: test 1)   # In the old issue view, navigate to the issue created and try to inline edit the field    h3. Expected Results    The field is properly updated with a value   h3. Actual Results    The field is not updated, however, sometimes it gets updated after refreshing the page or adding a new value to the field. It seems to be only a displaying issue.     [Vídeo with the detailed steps|https://www.loom.com/<USER>fbe80c3123ff4af89d137d7277e86a37]  h3. Workaround   # Use the Edit button to properly edit your field:  !Screen Shot 2020-10-05 at 13.14.54.png|width=272,height=148!   # Use the detail view of your board (Kanban or Scrum):  !Screen Shot 2020-10-05 at 13.25.27.png|width=324,height=102!     """

summary: "Decimal values aren't accepted by Time tracking"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Even though the documentation [Configuring time tracking|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/configuring-time-tracking-818578858.html] mentions that decimal values are accepted in the number of hours in your working day, this doesn't seem to work.    ||Field|| Description||  |Working hours per day| The number of hours in your working day (decimal values are accepted).|    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to you *Time tracking* page: [https://YOUR_INSTANCE.atlassian.net/secure/admin/TimeTrackingAdmin.jspa]  # Click on the *Edit global settings* button  # Set a decimal value for the *Working hours per day*  # Refresh the page    h3. Expected Results  The decimal value is accepted as +Working hours per day+.    h3. Actual Results  Despite none error being shown, the decimal value isn't accepted. [Click here|https://<USER>getcloudapp.com/llu2wO48] to check a video of this behavior.      h3. Workaround     Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Editing custom multi-line text field in the old issue view has inconsistent behavior"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In the old issue view, editing custom multi-line text fields are sometimes not saved  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a custom multi-line text field to an issue   # Add values to the field, save and edit again.    h3. Expected Results  The edited value would be saved.  h3. Actual Results  The edited value are not saved and requires a second edit    h3. Workaround    # Use the new issue view.  # If old issue view is still required, edit the field a second time before saving again."""

summary: "The option ""Link Confluence page"" is not loading into the child issue after creating"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  After creating an issue inside of a """"*parent*"""" issue we'll miss the option """"*Link Confluence page*"""" into the child issue.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an issue to be the """"*parent*"""";   # Create another issue to be the child issue;   # I would like to emphasize that we need to create inside of the """"*parent issue*"""";   !image-2020-10-06-17-29-54-468.png|width=445,height=260!   # Then, access the child issue.   !image-2020-10-06-17-30-22-691.png|width=401,height=181!     * I've recorded this [video|https://<USER>getcloudapp.com/jkuDn0JA] with the steps.    h3. Expected Results   * This option """"*Link Confluence page*"""" should appear.    h3. Actual Results   * The option """"*Link Confluence page*"""" did not appear.    h3. Workaround  We need to refresh the page to load the option """"*Link Confluence page*""""."""

summary: "when using backticks to convert text to code format text is decomposed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    when using backticks to convert text to code format text is decomposed  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an issue and write something on wiki-style description   # Use backticks to convert to code this text   # The text will be decomposed with a separation of a single quote.    h3. Expected Results    To show the text in code mode only as it is  h3. Actual Results    !2020-10-03_17-33-58.gif!  h3. Workaround    Start with a backtick and then write the text and end with a backtick, or select the line and type control + shift + m"""

summary: "Permanently deleting project fails"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When trying to permanently delete a project the deletion will fail without sharing in-depth details of the issue.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Move a project to JIRA's trash can.   # Access the trash and try to permanently delete it.    h3. Expected Results    After some time the project should vanish from the trash can, meaning it was completely deleted.    h3. Actual Results    The user that triggered the deletion should receive an email with the below message:   !image.png|thumbnail!      h3. Workaround    No workaround for the issue at the moment.  Please engage Atlassian Support."""

summary: "Add options menu for child issues in the new issue view and mobile app"
description: """h3. Summary    In the old issue view, it was possible to perform some actions in child issues from inside the parent, such as Rank it in the child issues lists, assign, transition, and so on. This is an example:  !Image 2020-10-08 at 9.14.37 AM.png|thumbnail!     This option is not available in the new issue view or in the mobile app. Some possible approaches would be adding the buttons or even allowing drag and drop to reorder issues inside Epics or Stories with Sub-Tasks."""

summary: "Restricting custom Parent link type fields affects existing ones"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When creating custom _Parent link_ type custom fields, restricting the field by applying a field context or hiding it in the field configuration affects the existing fields of this type, notably the original *Parent link* field from Advanced Roadmaps.    This means that any restrictions you've applied to the custom field would also be applied to the original system field as well.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new custom field using the _Parent link_ field type (eg: Parent feature)   # Apply a field context that restricts this field to only the Story issue type OR change the field behavior to hidden in the field configuration    h3. Expected Results    That specific *Parent feature* custom field should have the applicable field context applied and be restricted to only Story issue types OR be hidden.    h3. Actual Results    The original *Parent link* system field is also affected by this restriction and parent Issue types like *Initiative* do not display any *Child issues*.     !_FAT-2__FATHER_-_Jira.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    You have to remove any restrictions done on the custom field in order for the *Parent link* field to work as expected and have Issues display the *Child issues*."""

summary: "Share with customer checkbox won't show up for Project Type scoped rules"
description: """h3. Summary  When creating a project type scoped rule to add comments on JSD projects, the """"Share with customer"""" checkbox will not show up on the comment settings.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a global """"Add comment"""" rule.  # On the """"Rule details"""" select """"Project type"""" as *Scope* and """"<USER>""" as the *Project type*    h3. Expected Results    You'll be able to set the automation comment to be customer-facing.   !image-2020-10-08-18-19-40-037.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  The """"Share with customer"""" checkbox will not show up.      !image-2020-10-08-18-20-12-542.png|thumbnail!     h3.Workaround  Create a multi-project rule and add all your SDs projects to the scope."""

summary: "Deleting a project in Jira Mobile doesn't send it to the trash, it deletes it permanently"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Deleting a project in Jira Mobile doesn't send it to the projects trash. It actually deletes it permanently.  Also no documentation states that the feature works differently or it's not supported in the mobile version.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Access Jira's app in the mobile  # Delete a project through project details    h3. Expected Results  Project should be sent to projects trash.    h3. Actual Results  Project is deleted permanently straight away.    h3. Workaround  No workarounds were found on top of deleting the project through a the web version of Jira."""

summary: "Jira Cloud App gets stuck when scrolling on an issue with a Table added"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira Cloud App gets stuck when scrolling on an issue with a Table added  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a table to the Description field   # Try to scroll using your mobile device  h3. Expected Results  Scroll all the issue  h3. Actual Results  The app gets stuck and you are not able to scroll  h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "In IOS, doing an issue transition when there is no fields in transition screen and there is a comment validation during transition, message popup will come instead of going to comment screen directly."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    For IOS users, whenever there is no field on the transition screen and there is a validation for comments during the transition, a pop up with the comment validation message will be shown on the mobile screen.      For Android users, the popup message is not shown. Instead, it goes directly to the comment section during the transition.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a transition screen with no fields and attach it to a workflow transition. In the screenshots, I have put this in the All - > Done transition.   # Create a validation for comment validation during the transition. For example -If a value for field Comment is not provided during the transition, show the following error: No comments.   # Log in to an IOS device and execute the transition. A pop up will be shown with """"No comments"""" instead of directly going to the comment section.    h3. Expected Results    During the transition, the screen should directly go to the comment section as in Android.  h3. Actual Results    A popup will be shown with the message configured in the comment validation instead of going to the comment section.    h3. Workaround    NA"""

summary: "Manually entering a date on a custom date picker does not work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When entering a date manually in a date picker field, the date does get highlighted on the calendar dropdown. It however does not get saved.  It is required that you click on the date in the calendar dropdown for the field to show that value.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open a date picker field, such as Due Date   # Enter the date *manually*, example 2020/10/14.   !Screenshot 2020-10-12 at 11.41.00 AM.png|thumbnail!    # The date does get selected but when you hit enter the date is lost    h3. Expected Results  The selected date would get updated in the field.    h3. Actual Results  The date does not get selected and the field stays empty until you click on the date.    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "No valid error message on the 'field required' to update displayed when issue transition in Jira cloud app."
description: """h3. Issue Summary         When using """"Field required validator"""" in the workflow and if the field is not updated, Jira cloud app do not display the valid error message stating """"Field <fieldname> is required"""" instead displays as """"Bad request retry""""  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Ensure to have a workflow with a """"Field required validator"""" on a transition.   # Open your Jira cloud app in android phone.    # Transition the issue from status 1 to status 2 where you have the field required validator present.   # if the field is not updated the transition will not take place and there will no error message. (User will also not know that it is a required field because there is no * or (required) is displayed on the screen.    I have recorded the behaviour below :      !Mandatory_field_Validator.gif|width=146,height=317!  h3. Expected Results    !image-2020-10-13-18-04-15-388.png|width=247,height=76!  h3. Actual Results    Bad request Retry  h3. Workaround    Make the field required from the field configuration and not in the workflow."""

summary: "Jira does not bounce back emails that are not processed by incoming mail handlers which uses Default mail server"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When Jira does not process an incoming email it does not bounce the email back!  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure the incoming mail handler to create/add a comment to issue   ** Do not set a default reporter.   ** Set create user to False     # Send an email to the mail handler's email address from a user(not in the cloud instance) with an issue-key in the subject    h3. Expected Results    The email should be bounced back, possibly with an appropriate error/failure message!  h3. Actual Results    The incoming email is not processed and silently ignored(not bounced back)  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira Admins are unable to view the navigation bar on the Project Settings for Private next-gen projects"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Users with *Administer Jira* global permission are unable to view the navigation bar on the Project Settings for Private next-gen projects that they are not a part of.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    #Step 1: As an *Administer Jira* try to access the project settings for a private next-gen project.   #Step 2: Admins can see the project details page but cannot see the navigation bar to navigate to other pages.  h3. Expected Results    A Site admin should be able to access the project settings for private next-gen projects to manage the project and users.  h3. Actual Results    <USER>cannot see the navigation bar to navigate to different settings   h3. Workaround    Add yourself as an Administrator to the private next-gen project   Or Access the pages for the project directly by accessing the URLs:  ||Project Setting||URL||  |*Project Details*|https://*<InstanceName.atlassian.net>*/<USER>software/projects/*<ProjectKey>*/settings/details|  |*Access*|https://*<InstanceName.atlassian.net>*/<USER>software/projects/*<ProjectKey>*/settings/access|  |*Issue Types*|https://*<InstanceName.atlassian.net>*/<USER>software/projects/*<ProjectKey>*/settings/issuetypes|  |*Notifications*|https://*<InstanceName.atlassian.net>*/<USER>software/projects/*<ProjectKey>*/settings/notifications|  |*Features*|https://*<InstanceName.atlassian.net>*/<USER>software/projects/*<ProjectKey>*/settings/features|  |*Apps*|https://*<InstanceName.atlassian.net>*/<USER>software/projects/*<ProjectKey>*/settings/app-fields|    *Replace:*    *<InstanceName.atlassian.net>* with your instance URL.   *<ProjectKey>* with the project key for the project, you want to access."""

summary: "Jira app for Mac does not resolve the login process in macOS Big Sur"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira app for Mac does not resolve the login process in macOS BiG Sur    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Have macOS upgraded to Big Sur and try access the Jira app for Mac    h3. Expected Results  The app should login without issues    h3. Actual Results  the login screen is not reachable, it goes into a loop to the main screen when hit the button """"LogIn,...    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available. But avoid upgrading macOS to Big Sur while a fix is not deployed."""

summary: "Inserting a Table in a Numbered List Breaks Up Numbering"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Inserting a table in a numbered list in an issue description breaks up the continuity of numbering    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Begin a numbered list in a Jira issue description   # Insert a table within an item   # Attempt to continue the numbered list    h3. Expected Results    The numbering continues after the table.    h3. Actual Results    The numbering restarts at 1.    h3. Workaround    A possible workaround would be to manually override the formatting, but this isn't ideal."""

summary: "Double space on issue comments"
description: """For some use cases, it is better to not double space the paragraph when hitting """"enter"""":   !Screen Shot 2020-10-14 at 16.27.55.png|thumbnail!   """

summary: "More clicks to add watchers comparing to the old issue view"
description: """When you add a watcher in the old issue view, it is only necessary *one* click to find the user:    !Old issue view.gif|width=716,height=386!    Different from the new issue view, that it is necessary *two* clicks:    *!image-2020-10-14-16-43-10-086.png|width=844,height=377!*    *Suggestion:* when clicking on the watcher icon the cursor should already be available to look for a user (not necessary to click """"+add watchers"""")          """

summary: "Shortcuts no longer work in the Issue navigator for list view in the new view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Most Issue actions keyboard shortcuts do not work with the new Issue view in the Issue navigator in the list view.    This includes the essentials:  * *l*: Label issue  * *i*: Assign to me  * *a*: Assign issue  * *m*: Comment on issue    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Enable *New Jira issue view* in your Personal settings  # In the Issue navigator, switch to a list view (keyboard shortcut """"t"""")  # Click on an issue to highlight it and hit """"l"""" on your keyboard    h3. Expected Results    This should trigger the *Label issue* function   !2020-10-15_16-15-26.gif|width=400&height=200!    h3. Actual Results    Nothing happens    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior other than disabling the *New Jira issue view*. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Using %%CURRENT_DATETIME%% in post function doesn't set the correct date on Date Picker fields"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Using %%CURRENT_DATETIME%% in post function doesn't set the correct date on Date Picker fields    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # create a customer field Auto-Due-Date as a Date Picker   # add post function in the workflow as follows:  On the Create transition add the following Post functions:  * The Auto-Due-Date of the issue will be set to %%CURRENT_DATETIME%%.  * The field Due date will take the value from Auto-Due-Date. Source and destination issue are the same.    h3. Expected Results  The date on field Due Date and Auto-Due-Date should show the current correct date.     Similarly, if you   # create a custom field Auto-Date-Time as a Date Time Picker  On the Create transition add the following Post functions:  * The Auto-Date-Time of the issue will be set to %%CURRENT_DATETIME%%.  * The field Due date will take the value from Auto-Date-Time. Source and destination issue are the same.    h3. Actual Results  It is showing time in GMT for Due Date. This can be verified if you are on AEST time as an example and you create an issue prior to 11:00AM, then check the Auto-Date-Time - you will notice that the Auto-Date-Time (Date Time Picker) is actually showing correct date/time value, however when copied over to Due Date - the date is reflected in GMT    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "View usage report of the Project role fails to report workflow condition"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Project role usage feature does not show the utilization for workflow _User in any project role_ condition.   !Role.png|thumbnail!   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Project role   # Navigate to any workflow and click on Edit   # Add condition in any of the transition as a _user is in any project role_   # Choose the required project role/roles   # Go to the Project role -> View Usage   # It won't report this condition   !User_Project_Role.png|thumbnail!  h3. Expected Results  Usage report should show the Workflow and the transition name where this project role is used.  h3. Actual Results    The usage report is blank.    h3. Workaround    None as of now.   However, if there is only 1 project to be used, then the condition _User in a project role_ could be used. This is correctly reported on the usage page."""

summary: "JQL search in Epic Picker times out"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Users are unable to search for epics using the epic picker field.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to any screen with Epic field picker   # Search for an epic on the Jira system and you will get the below error {quote} !epic (1).jpg|thumbnail! {quote}    h3. Expected Results    Should be able to search for the epic in the drop down and select them.  h3. Actual Results    We get an error stating that the epic with the key is not found    h3. Workaround    As of now, even if the search throws an error, we are able to save the page with the epic key mentioned.   # Type in the epic key.   # Even if the error shows up, try to save the screen or move to the next screen and the epic key will be saved."""

summary: "Searching for sub-tasks using JQL search parent clause returns incorrect results"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Following JQL returns not only sub-tasks: _parent IN (ISSUE-1) OR parent IS NOT EMPTY_  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Run following JQL: _parent IN (ISSUE-1) OR parent IS NOT EMPTY_    h3. Expected Results    Only sub-tasks are returned.  h3. Actual Results    Search returns also issues that are not sub-tasks.  h3. Workaround    Change JQL to : _parent IN (ISSUE-1) OR parent NOT IN (EMPTY)_"""

summary: "Multiline text field gets wiped out when moving back from next-gen project "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Multiline text field gets wiped out when moving back from next-gen project     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Multiline Text Custom field (ie. Acceptance Criteria)   # Set the renderer for that field to Wiki-Syle   # Create an issue on a classic project and populate the field   # Move the issue to a next gen project    # Move the issue from next gen back to the original project. Use the Move option from the full issue view.   # Choose to *retain* the field value.      !retain.png|thumbnail!  !review.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results    The multi line text field value should be retained.     h3. Actual Results    The value is wiped out.    !results.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    - On the classic project, set the renderer from Wiki Style to default.   - Perform the move and the value will be retained  - Set the renderer back to Wiki Style.  """

summary: "When trying to delete projects roles that have default users, a 500 error is displayed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    A 500 error page is displayed when a user tries to delete a project role which has default users assigned.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a project role in https://<INSTANCENAME>.atlassian.net/secure/project/ViewProjectRoles.jspa  # Click on *Manage Default Members* next to the name of the newly created project role  # Click on *Edit* under *Default Users*  # Search for a user under *Add user(s) to project role:* and click *Add*  # Return to the page https://<INSTANCENAME>.atlassian.net/secure/project/ViewProjectRoles.jspa  # Click on *Delete* next to the project role name  # Click on the *Delete* button    h3. Expected Results    The project role is deleted    h3. Actual Results    An error page """"Something went wrong"""" showing a 500 appears     !Screen Shot 2020-10-16 at 11.27.08 AM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Remove all the default users before deleting the project role"""

summary: "History tab (Issue View) shows incorrect date"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  History tab (Issue View) shows an incorrect date for Custom Date Picker or Date Fields.    When you have a different Time Zone configure to your Personal Settings screen than the one from the General Configuration screen, it reflects the date depending on the time zone that the user that performed the change but the time from the General Configuration screen.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set User *A* personal time zone to America/Mexico City   # Set User *B* personal time zone to Europe/Warsaw   # With user *B* modify a date field to an issue e.g. from 2020/10/14 -> 2020/10/15 and then change it vice-versa from 2020/10/15 -> 2020/10/14   # With user *A* verify the History Tab of the issue and it will show the date based on the time zone from user *B* but it shows the correct time and dates the change was made    *User A's view*   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   *User B's view*   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!   h3. Expected Results  The date from the field must reflect the correct time zone for both users  h3. Actual Results  It will change the date from the time zone of the last user that made the change    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "The ""Activity"" column under ""Worked on"" in the ""People"" tab has a limit of 20 characters."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The *""""Activity""""* column under *""""Worked on""""* in the *""""People""""* tab is showing a limited number of characters.   It looks like the *""""Activity""""* column has a limit to display 20 characters, if we an activity with more than 20 characters, the other characters are cut off.    !bug.png|thumbnail!  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a comment to an issue, this comment must happen in September since this month has a large number of characters.   # Open the worked on-page.   ** [https://*domain*.atlassian.net/<USER>people/*useraccounthash*/work|https://%2Adomain%2A.atlassian.net/<USER>people/*useraccounthash*/work]    h3. Expected Results    We would see the full activity, including the days.  h3. Actual Results    The activity information is limited to the first 20 characters.  h3. Workaround    None."""

summary: "Creating subtask in a Business project board task is causing the parent task description to show incorrectly."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a subtask is created for a task created from a Business project board and navigated back to the parent task the description of the subtask is copied to the parent task.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Take any Business project board.   # Create a task from the board using the + symbol. Fill the summary and description.   # Create a subtask for this task and then close the subtask.   # Click the parent task. You will see that the description of the subtask is copied to the parent task.    h3. Expected Results    The parent task description should show correctly.  h3. Actual Results    The parent task is showing the description of the subtask.  h3. Workaround    Do a refresh of the browser."""

summary: "Table content in issue renders incorrectly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Table content in issue renders incorrectly    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create an issue with the Description having the following wiki info      In the old issue view, it is rendered as:    !wiki.render1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  * Like the above, a table with 1 column    h3. Actual Results  * A table with two columns (as shown below)    !wiki.render2.png|thumbnail!   * Sometimes when editing the table in the new issue view, the table would 'oscillate' making it difficult to edit.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "New issue view editor does not honour markdown format for blockquotes with the other elements"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In the new issue view editor (e.g Description or comment field) markdown format for the blockquote with other elements does not work as expected. The bullet/number listing is shown outside the quote.    Also, the editor disables the listing icon in the menu option.   !Disabled inside quote.png|thumbnail!  h3. Steps to Reproduce    *Scenario 1*   # Open any issue key in the new issue view.   # Choose Comment/Description or any other fields with the new editor.   # Go to the editor and click on (+) -> choose _Quote_. Or use """">"""" in the editor for the blockquote.   # Use other elements inside the blockquote.      *Scenario 2*   # Create a new issue.   # Use below in the description      h3. Expected Results    !Expected result.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    *Scenario 1*   Elements are not processed by the editor.    !Element not working.png|thumbnail!    *Scenario 2*   In the new issue view screen, other elements are shown outside the quote.   !Element outsite blockquote.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Annotations in PDF is not visible when previewed in JIRA"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JIRA attachment preview doesn't the annotations added in a PDF file.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Attach a PDF with an annotation(comment) to an issue   # Click on the PDF to open it in preview    h3. Expected Results    You see the pdf contents and the comments inside the PDF  h3. Actual Results    You see the PDF content and broken rendering of annotations  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available  h4.  """

summary: "Missing Assignee in the quick panel view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Assignee icon disappears from the parent of sub-task on Next-gen.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an issue on Next-gen   # Create sub-tasks below this issue   # Assign the sub-tasks to someone   # Go to the parent   # Refresh the page    h3. Expected Results     !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!   h3. Actual Results     !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail! !screenshot-1.png|height=328!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Custom field text multi-line clear value post function recording strange data in the issue history"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When you use a custom field text multi-line clear value post function, the history record from the issue shows strange values like the clod ID and the custom field ID in the old issue view.    This data should not be displayed on the history content.   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!    !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a text multi-line custom field   # Add a post-function to clear the value in a transition   # See the history record in the old issue view     h3. Expected Results  The History record should not have any value just the clear event    h3. Actual Results  It is adding some IDs to history when seeing it in the old issue view      h3. Workaround  Use the new issue view"""

summary: "Validator set as comment require won't allow to change the assignee via transition screen if the comment will be empty for the first time "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Validator set as comment require won't allow to change the assignee via transition screen if the comment will be empty for the first time.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a screen with assignee and comment.   # Set this screen into a transition.  # Add a validator """"*If a value for field Comment is not provided during the transition, show the following error: Comment is empty.*""""  # Then, transition a ticket to this status.  # Into the transition screen, change the assignee, stay the comment empty and click to finish the transition. This action should trigger the validator message to fill out the comment.  # Keep the """"new"""" assignee and fill out the comment.  # Finish the transition.    h3. Expected Results  The assignee should be set via the transition screen after the validation message.    h3. Actual Results  Even filling out the comment, the new assignee won't be set via transition screen.  * [Video with bug.|https://<USER>getcloudapp.com/2NujlPbl]    h3. Workaround  Fill out the comment into the transition screen, and then change the assignee."""

summary: "Example date present in the date picker field in the new issue view is in the locale specific format"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In a date picker field, the date presented to the end user as an example, in the new issue view is based on the locale as per the end user's account and language settings.     This however introduces ambiguity, since the new issue view can only receive the date inputs in the format: yyyy/MM/dd    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure your account to use a non-English language such as Polish/French.   # In a date picker field such as Due Date, the example date in the UI is based on the locale only.    h3. Expected Results  The date picker should have an example in line with the format that the field can accept -- like we see in the English locale:   !EnglishLanguage.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  * Polish language:    !PolishLanguage.png|thumbnail!     * French language:   !FrenchLanguage.png|thumbnail!       h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Permanently deleting project throws timeout errors"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When trying to permanently delete a project the deletion will fail without sharing in-depth details of the issue.    Some cases may be related to issues with a great number of work logs and/or comments.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Move a project to JIRA's trash can.  # Access the trash and try to permanently delete it.    h3. Expected Results  After some time the project should vanish from the trash can, meaning it was completely deleted.    h3. Actual Results  No error messages or e-maisl are sent out.    h3. Workaround  No workaround for the issue at the moment.  Please engage Atlassian Support."""

summary: "Saving JQL filter fails when the left-side panel is expanded"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When you are an Owner of a Filter and try to make changes to it, and save it, if the left-side panel is expanded it will not show the *Save* option, only *Save as*.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open a Filter you are the Owner   # Expand the left-side panel   # Make a change to the JQL query and try to save it    h3. Expected Results  You should get the option to *Save* the changes  h3. Actual Results  You will only get the Filter show *edited* and the option to *Save as*   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   h3. Workaround    Hide the left side panel from the Filter screen and make the changes to the JQL Query."""

summary: "Export (CSV, Current Fields) for Next Gen project issue will display multiple column for custom field"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Export (CSV, Current Fields) for Next Gen project issue will display multiple columns for custom field when there's another next gen project using the same custom field *Name*.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open a next gen project   # Add """"short text"""" field to the issue type/request type. Use *Test Field* name.   # Then , create a test ticket and add the value.  # Then , open another next gen project.  # Add """"short text"""" field to the issue type/request type. Use *Test Field* name.  # Then , go to """"Issue Navigator"""" page and filter issue for one next gen project.  # Then , export the issue using """"Export (CSV, Current Fields)""""    h3. Expected Results  The *Test Field* column only display one    h3. Actual Results    The *Test Field* column display twice.    h3. Workaround  * Use *Export (CSV, All Fields)*  * Change the other field name to a different name."""

summary: "Removing jira-software-users group from under product access(Jira Software), still allows its members to access Jira even though they are not part of any other group."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Removing *<USER>software-users* group from under product access(Jira Software), still allows its members to access Jira even though they are not part of any other group.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a user to the *<USER>software-users* group, ensure this user is not part of any other group that would grant access to the product.   # Remove *<USER>software-users* group under product access.     h3. Expected Results    The user should not be able to access Jira because *<USER>software-users* group has been removed under product access.  h3. Actual Results    Even though the group *<USER>software-users* has been removed from product access and the user is not a member of any other group that would otherwise given product access, the user can still access Jira.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Show required field indicator in new issue view"
description: """Currently, the new issue view doesn't indicate which fields are required (e.g., using a red asterisk like {color:red}\*{color} or have {color:red}*(Required)*{color}).    It would be nice if this was added to the new issue view so that users can easily see when a field is required so that they can populate the field with data before trying to proceed with the ticket."""

summary: "Create issue form doesn't refresh when the selected project is changed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary   * Create issue form doesn't refresh when the selected project is changed   * Fields with predefined values don't update during the issue creation process - this can lead to incorrect values for fields in newly created issues   * Jira <USER>request types affected, but the problem is with the create issue form in Jira    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # On one project, create a new Jira <USER>request type with hidden fields that have predefined values.   !Screenshot 2020-10-21 at 19.05.15.png|thumbnail!   # On another project, create a new Jira <USER>request type without any hidden fields   !Screenshot 2020-10-21 at 19.03.26.png|thumbnail!   # Create a new issue in Jira and ensure that the project/request type containing the hidden fields is selected !Screenshot 2020-10-21 at 19.11.14.png|thumbnail!   # Change the project/request type to the one that _doesn't_ have the hidden fields    h3. Expected Results   * The form refreshes and the fields are displayed in the form without any values set or the correct values set    h3. Actual Results   * The predefined values from the other project(CLAS in this example) remain in the hidden issue fields when creating an issue. The form doesn't refresh.   !Screenshot 2020-10-21 at 19.11.30.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround   * Change the project to the desired project, change the Type to another value, then revert back to the intended Type/request type"""

summary: "Double scroll bars in Watchers list"

Steps to Reproduce   # Create a project with issues   # Browse an issue    # Add 8 watchers to the issue    Issue is not reproducible in Jira 8.5.0/8.5.1.  Issue is reproducible with Jira 8.5.2 and above.    h3. Expected Results  One scroll bar per list    h3. Actual Results  Double scroll bars within the watchers list   !Watchers.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results    Column can be moved ( drag and drop)  h3. Actual Results    Not possible to move some columns     h3. Workaround    Reduce the number of columns to be displayed to 20 or less"""

summary: "Development Tools permission is displayed as ""Application Access (Anyone on the web)"""

It should be *""""Application access: Any logged in user""""*  h3. summary: "Drag and drop attachments between issues fails"
description: """h3. Issue Summary   * Drag and drop attachments between issues fail OR Copy + Paste in the Visible rendering mode     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Upload an image to any issue   # Drag and drop the image's thumbnail to another issue   # The image appears correctly after copying by the drag and drop feature   # After saving the image doesn't get uploaded successfully and results in a broken link     h3. Expected Results    The image to be uploaded and presented as a thumbnail.  h3. Actual Results    A broken image link and no error in the logs as the attachment wasn't uploaded.  h3. Workaround    1- Copy the image or the attachment using (CTRL + C) OR (Right Click + Copy)    2- Move to the other issue in the *Text* mode NOT the visible mode    3- Choose (CTRL + V) OR (Right Click + Paste)    4- An upload prompt window will show up and the attachment should be uploaded successfully."""

summary: "Setting issuelinks-issues parameter on direct HTML links doesn't map valid issue link on screen"
description: """h3. Issue Summary Setting _issuelinks-issues_ parameter on [direct HTML links|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-create-issues-using-direct-html-links-in-jira-server-159474.html] doesn't map valid issue link on the issue creation screen, though _issuelinks-issues_ itself looks like a right one as a parameter because it is trying to map the value to the expected field. h3. Steps to Reproduce # Install a vanilla Jira instance and create _TEST_ project with _admin_ user. # Add an issue _TEST-1_ # Outside Jira, create a valid.html file in any directory and add following link {code:html|title=valid link} /secure/CreateIssueDetails!init.jspa?pid=10000&issuetype=10004&summary=test&reporter=admin&issuelinks-issues=TEST-1&issuelinks-linktype=duplicates"""">Test (valid link) h3. Expected Results With valid link, issue link is properly mapped to _TEST-1_ on issue creation screen. With invalid link, issue link is not set with error. h3. Actual Results (x) With valid link, issue link is NOT mapped to _TEST-1_ on issue creation screen. !Screen Shot 2020-02-26 at 17.55.54.png|thumbnail! (/) With invalid link, issue link is not set with error. !Screen Shot 2020-02-26 at 17.56.44.png|thumbnail! h3. Workaround Just enter the issue link manually."""

summary: "Jira Data Center clustering features might be only partially enabled if non-breaking spaces are present in cluster.properties"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira Data Center clustering features might be only partially enabled if there are [non-breaking spaces|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Non-breaking_space] in {{cluster.properties}}.    Using the tool {{hexdump}} it is possible to confirm these characters are present; look for the value {{c2 a0}}:   !hexdump.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a Jira Data Center license.  # Place a {{cluster.properties}} file containing special characters such as a [Non-breaking space|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Non-breaking_space] in {{JIRA_LOCAL_HOME}}. Here's an example file: [^cluster.properties-with-non-breaking-spaces.txt].  # Restart Jira.    h3. Expected Results  Jira Data Center clustering features are enabled normally, including cache and index replication.    h3. Actual Results  {{JIRA_SHARED_HOME}} will be enabled and used, but cache and index replication won't:  {noformat:none|title=Startup logs}      ___ File Paths ____________________________                 JIRA Home                                     : \\some_location\share\Automation\Jira_Dev\sharedhome           JIRA Local Home                               : C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Application Data\JIRA           Location of atlassian-jira.log                : C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Application Data\JIRA\log\atlassian-jira.log           Index Path                                    : C:\Program Files\Atlassian\Application Data\JIRA\caches\indexesV1           Attachment Path                               : \\some_location\share\Automation\Jira_Dev\sharedhome\data\attachments    {code:none|title=Health checks}  Name: Cluster Index Replication  Is healthy: true  Failure reason: The Jira instance is not clustered.  Severity: UNDEFINED    Name: Cluster Locks  Is healthy: true  Failure reason: The Jira instance is not clustered.  Severity: UNDEFINED    Name: Cluster Scheduler  Is healthy: true  Failure reason: The Jira instance is not clustered.  Severity: UNDEFINED    Name: Cluster Cache Replication  Is healthy: true  Failure reason: The Jira instance is not clustered.  Severity: UNDEFINED    Name: Cluster Time Synchronisation  Is healthy: true  Failure reason: The Jira instance is not clustered.  Severity: UNDEFINED    Name: Shared Home  Is healthy: true  Failure reason: The Jira instance is not clustered.  Severity: UNDEFINED  {code}    h3. Workaround  Remove all [non-breaking spaces|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Non-breaking_space] from {{cluster.properties}} and restart Jira."""

summary: "Trying to boost a term using a caret per the search syntax documentation does not appear to work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    [Jira Server search syntax for text fields documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/search-syntax-for-text-fields-939938747.html] indicates that users can boost a term in order to make Jira return more relevant search results. Details in [Search syntax for text fields: Boosting a term: ^|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/search-syntax-for-text-fields-939938747.html#Searchsyntaxfortextfields-Boostingaterm:^]    The problem with this section is that this does not appear to be true in the current versions of Jira Server today.  Boosting a term does not at all change the order in which the issues are returned in Jira, nor does it affect the number of issues returned by any JQL query.  Hence it appears to be doing nothing today.  In addition to this, it appears that Jira Cloud documentation has already acknowledge that 'boosting a term' using a caret \^ does nothing anymore and is unsupported, see [https://support.atlassian.com/jira-core-cloud/docs/search-syntax-for-text-fields/].    In turn I suspect that Jira did once behave in this manner, but it does not appear to be doing so in any of the recent versions where we have documented this (at least as far back as Jira 7.3 we have this documented).    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Try to follow the examples in [Boosting a term|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/search-syntax-for-text-fields-939938747.html#Searchsyntaxfortextfields-Boostingaterm:^]    h3. Expected Results  I would expect that either the number of results will vary by boosting a term OR the order in which those results are returned would be in some way affected by boosting one term over another.    h3. Actual Results  There appears to be no difference whatsoever in the search results when boosting any term in a text field search in JQL (either advanced or basic JQL searching)    h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Converting a Standard Issue Type to a Sub-Task using Bulk Update will cause the Sub-Task to appear in the 'Issues in Epic' section"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If you update the issue type to sub-task during a Bulk Update (even single issues), and this issue has an Epic Link, the link will be preserved even though a sub-task should not be allowed to be Epic Linked.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Story and assign it to an Epic >   # From the issue search screen, search for the project the Story exists in >   # Perform a 'Bulk Move', select only the Story, and choose the 'Move' option >   # While moving the issue in this way, the system allows you to choose a new issue type: sub-task, and give the issue a new parent. In this test, I moved the Story to a new Project, and chose another Story there to be the new Parent issue.   # Once this move was complete, and the Story attached to an Epic became a Sub-task - the Sub-task was shown in the 'Issues in Epic' section of the original Epic.    h3. Expected Results    Sub-Task would not be included as 'Issue in Epic'  h3. Actual Results    Converted Sub-Task shows as 'Issue in Epic'  h3. Workaround    Convert the issue to a issue type like Story, and convert back to a sub-task using a non Bulk Update method."""

summary: "Searching for a Confluence page with non-ASCII Characters doesn't work"
