summary: "Creating a ticket from a Scrum board gets auto populated the label field with a new incorrect value"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When creating a ticket from a Scrum board based on a JQL filter, Jira auto-populates the field with a new label. Thus populating my environment with a new, duplicate label but with lower case.  h3. Steps to Reproduce  The steps are below, I was able to reproduce it on Jira 8.7.1                                                                                            # Create a label called """"Test-Label""""  # Create a filter with the below JQL  #*   # Create a Scrum board with the filter above  # At the bottom of the backlog, click on """"+ Create issue"""", the issue creation screen pops up with a pre-populated label """"test-label"""".        h3. Expected Results  The correct label mentioned in the JQL filter (""""Test-Label"""") should appear when creating an issue from the board.    h3. Actual Results    The issue creation screen pops up with a pre-populated label """"test-label"""". This label does not exist, but if you proceeded to create the issue, it will create the new label.     Thus populating my environment with a new, duplicate label but with lower case.    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available  """

summary: "Mail Handler might go into a loop when processing messages"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Mail handlers will process messages in a loop if the following conditions are met:  * The mail handler has been configured to forward errors to a mailbox through the *Forward* field on its configuration.  * The e-mail has an attachment that is larger than the maximum size allowed by the instance (10 MB by default).    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a mail server connection and Mail Handler.  # Configure an SMTP server in the *Outgoing Mail* section of Jira.  # When configuring the Mail Handler, enter an e-mail address on the *Forward* field.  # Send an e-mail to the mailbox used by this Mail Handler, ensuring that it has a file attached to it that surpasses the maximum attachment size configured in Jira.    h3. Expected Results  The Mail Handler will process the message, the file will not be attached to the corresponding issue, and then the Mail Handler will proceed to the next message.    h3. Actual Results  The Mail Handler will read the attached file until its size is larger than the maximum allowed - once this happens, an error will be thrown indicating that the file will not be attached to the issue, which will be forwarded to the e-mail address configured in the mail handler. Once this happens, the Mail Handler will try to process the same attachment again, entering a loop.        (!) One thing to note is that this will only be replicated if Jira is configured with a valid outgoing mail server.    h3. Workaround    Disable forwarding error messages to a mailbox by removing the value from the *Forward* field in the Mail Handler configuration."""

summary: "Accessing other user profile changes current user profile avatar in Jira navigation panel"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  As logged in user I have my avatar displayed on Jira top navigation panel (right hand corner). When Gravatar is enabled (by default) it changes to the avatar of another user when you view his/her user profile in Jira.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Check that _Allow Gravatars_ is set to ON (see [Configuring JIRA application options|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver072/configuring-jira-application-options-828788218.html])   # Click on a user mention to visit their profile in Jira  # Look at the avatar displayed on the Jira blue navigation panel (top right hand corner).     h3. Expected Results    Jira should display current user avatar.  h3. Actual Results    Instead of displaying current use avatar, Jira displays the avatar of the user whose profile you visit.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Nested Groups Membership Missing when ""Update group memberships when logging in"" used"
description: """h3.    {panel:title=Atlassian Update – 22 September 2020|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#ebf2f9|titleBGColor=#ebf2f9|bgColor=#ffffff}  Hi everyone,    After reviewing the overall customer interest and impact of this bug report we have decided to close this issue down. Our analysis has shown that over time this issue hasn't collected a significant number of votes, watchers, comments, or support cases from customers and therefore has remained very low on our priority list. Given these findings we can conclude it will not be fixed in the foreseeable future and wish to be transparent about our priorities by closing it as *Timed Out*.    We understand that hearing a decision like this can be disappointing, but we hope you'll appreciate our transparent approach to product priorities and communications. We will continue to watch this issue for further updates, so please feel free to share any thoughts in the comments.    Thank you,    Mateusz Ostaszewski,    Software Engineer in Jira Server  {panel}  h3. Issue Summary    In case that """"Update group memberships when logging in"""" is set while """"Nested Group"""" is enabled, the user will somehow be lost its nested membership after log in.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Integrate JIRA with AD using """"Nested Groups""""   # Create 3 Levels of group membership in AD such as:       # Sync the directory in JIRA and confirm that the users have """"First"""" and """"Parents"""" groups.   # Rename one of the Group level e.g. """"First"""" to """"One""""   # Sync the directory in JIRA and the user should have """"One"""" as an additional group.   # Let one of the users log in.    h3. Expected Results    None of the membership will be lost  h3. Actual Results    The user will only have the direct group membership (Seconds) and lost the nested groups  h3. Workaround    Setting the """"Update group memberships when logging in"""" to """"Never"""" appears to not cause the user to lose the membership.    The following can be found when the user somehow lost the memberships  """

summary: "Accessibility: Create Issue dialog does not work in Safari when using VoiceOver"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Using VoiceOver while logged into Jira in Safari does not allow you to access the *Create Issue* dialog.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # On macOS 10.15.3 and Safari 13.0.5 (15608.5.11), enable VoiceOver via CMD-F5, and access Jira in Safari.  # Use the C (keyboard shortcut) to create new issue  # Tab to the different fields in Create new issue.    h3. Expected Results    Spoken feedback to allow access to the Create Issue dialog.    h3. Actual Results    Silence in VoiceOver + Safari.    h3. Workaround    The issue is not reproducible using VoiceOver + Chrome or using NVDA + Firefox. The issue is isolated to Safari alone."""

summary: "When inserting a text into Rich Text Editor with a space near to some notation and swithiching between Visual and Text tab, the format is lost"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When inputting a text into Rich Text Editor, on the Text tab the *Dollar ($)* symbol next to some notation (see the attached example), it lost the format when changed to the Visual bar.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new comment;  # Create a NoFormat, quote or code box;  # Click on the *Text* tab;  # Write the text with *$*;  # Change to *Visual* tab to check it.     [^sup_demo.mp4]     h3. Expected Results  Formatting will be kept when going to *Visual* tab.    h3. Actual Results  Formatting parameters are being lost when writing a *Dollar ($)* Symbol next a the notation.     h3. Workaround  # Create a new comment;  # Create a NoFormat, quote or code box;  # Click on the *Text* tab;  # Write the text with *$*;  ** put a space between *$* and end of notation;  ** do a breakline after the *$*;  # Change to *Visual* tab to check it."""

summary: "Sprint Health Gadget Uses Unmapped Statuses in Search"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When the Sprint Health Gadget reports on an Agile board and Sprint, it will consider all statuses, included any existing unmapped statuses.  This results in an complicating the search filter and may mislead users.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a board with unmapped statuses  ** Create 2 projects that have different workflow statuses. ProjectA and ProjectB  ** Create a new board for ProjectA, which will contain issues from ProjectA and statuses from ProjectA  ** Modify this board's filter to only include ProjectB.  Refresh the configuration page and check columns  ** We'll see that statuses that existed from ProjectA are not marked as unmapped   # On a dashboard, add the Sprint Health Gadget  ** Configure this to use the new board and sprint  # Next click on the """"Sprint Progress Bar"""" and choose to """"View in Issue Navigator""""    h3. Expected Results  The resulting filter will only show relevant statuses that are used by the Sprint/Board    h3. Actual Results  The resulting filter will list out all statuses, including the unmapped ones.    h3. Workaround  Go into each board that the Sprint is part of and ensure that unmapped statuses are removed"""

summary: "Special Character in Status Names can cause Invalid JQL Filters from Sprint Health Gadget"
description: """h3. Problem  When using the Sprint Health Gadget, users can click on the progress bar and choose to """"View in Issue Navigator"""".  If the Sprint has any statuses that contain """"&"""", this will result in a truncated JQL query and be invalid.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # On a scrum board, add a new status with """"&"""".  For example: """"Test & Approve""""  # Add the new status to a column, like """"In Progress""""  # On a dashboard, add the Sprint Health Gadget, referencing the Agile board we configured above.  # On the progress bar, click on the yellow """"In Progress"""" section  # Choose to """"View in Issue Navigator""""    h3. Expected Results  The resulting filter is correct and returns the issues that are part of the sprint    h3. Actual Results  JQL error is thrown showing:      The JQL is also truncated right after the word """"Test"""", losing the """"& Approve"""" portion.    h3. Workaround  * Rename statuses and remove the """"&"""""""

summary: "Field drop down options are not read by voice assistants"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using any sort of assistive technologies that generate announcements when scrolling through options (should as VoiceOver on Mac OS) on a drop-down menu, such as projects and issue types in the Quick Create menu, the options will not be read as expected.    *This prevents (or at least makes it very hard) creating issues by visually impaired people*, as they can't reliably change issue types, projects, priorities, etc.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Enable Voice Over (or any other assistive technologies that provide announcements).   # Click *Create* to create a new issue.   # Tab to the *Projects* or *Issue Types* drop-down menu.   # Use the up and down keys on the keyboard to cycle through the options on the menu.    h3. Expected Results    Every option will be read out by the voice-over assistant.  h3. Actual Results    Options are not read when you cycle through them.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Zooming an issue using Safari app on iPhone causes issue view to have less visible area"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Using Safari app on iPhone when accessing a JAC ticket, and performing a pinch-to-zoom to zoom in the issue view, causes the issue to have less visible area.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access a JAC ticket in Safari;   # Zoom in and out on the issue view;    h3. Expected Results  The issue will render to show the portion you've zoomed in;    h3. Actual Results  The issue shows less visible area as it should;    Video:  *  [^RPReplay_Final1584479699.MP4]     h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "User Picker does not return any new user if any other users have been picked"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    User Picker custom field returns No Matches for the users. This happens when the user picker has the project context, not a global context. It's happened because the REST request does not contain *projectId.*    h3. Environment  * The user picker Custom Fields has the project context    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create the project - *P1,*   # Create two users with similar user name - *cos*, *cos2,*   # Create the User Picker custom field - *daj* - with *P1* project context,   # Open a simple search,   # Choose the project *P1,*   # Add *daj* custom field,   # Open *daj* user picker dialog and search for the user *cos*  and pick him,   # Close *daj* user picker dialog for instance by clicking somewhere else on the page,   # Open *daj* user picker dialog one more time and search for the user *cos2.*    h3. Expected Results    !image-2020-03-18-15-40-00-902.png!  h3. Actual Results    !image-2020-03-18-15-39-07-477.png!  h3. Workaround    Change custom field context to global"""

summary: """More"" dropdown button stay on the page when going from issue detail page to issue navigator page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  """"More"""" dropdown button stay on the page when going from issue detail page to issue navigator page.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Open Issue navigator > Search for any issue or Click on any filter > Change the view to *List View*.  *Search issue example*   ![All issues] Issue Navigator - Jira 8.8.0 - Google Chrome 2020-03-19 14.28.09.png|thumbnail!   *Filter example*   ![All issues] Issue Navigator - Jira 8.8.0 - Google Chrome 2020-03-19 14.31.23.png|thumbnail!    # Click on the issue key to view the issue in issue detail page.  # Click on More button.  # Click Back button from the browser.   ![BSC-2] Test jira decluttering - Jira 8.8.0 - Google Chrome 2020-03-19 14.33.08.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  When back button is click the More dropdown list is missing and issue navigator page is shown.    h3. Actual Results  When back button is click, the More dropdown list remain on top of issue navigator page.   ![All issues] Issue Navigator - Jira 8.8.0 - Google Chrome 2020-03-19 14.38.04.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Extra click on the issue navigator page will remove the More dropdown list from the page."""

summary: "Characters of Options for Select List (Multiple Choice) limited based on Show Fields."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Characters of Options for Select List (Multiple Choice) limited based on Show Fields (All or Custom).  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add Select List (Multiple Choice) to a screen   # Add a few options with characters above 70   # Navigate to Create Issue Screen   # Click on Configure Fields and select """"All"""" for show fields.   # There will be 69 characters displayed only.   # Click on Configure Fields and select """"Custom"""" for show fields.   # There will be 66 characters displayed only.    h3. Expected Results    The number of characters displayed should be the same.  h3. Actual Results   !ALL.PNG|thumbnail!    !Custom.PNG|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Exporting a workflow with commas in the workflow name as XML throws error"
description: """h3. Summary    In JIRA when exporting a workflow that contains commas in the workflow name, the error ERR_RESPONSE_HEADERS_MULTIPLE_CONTENT_DISPOSITION is thrown and export is not completed.  h3. Environment    Chrome Browser  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access one of your workflows   # Choose Export > As XML    h3. Expected Results    An XML file is created and downloaded with the workflow in format XML  h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the browser:    h3. Workaround    Rename the workflow to remove the commas from the workflow name."""

summary: "Voice over assistants do not read out options picked in drop down menus"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When selecting an option through any sort of dropdown menu (combo boxes, date pickers), the option that is chosen by the user is not read out by a voice assistant utility if enabled.    *This prevents (or at least makes it very hard) creating issues by visually impaired people*, as they can't reliably change issue types, projects, priorities, etc.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Enable Voice Over (or any other assistive technologies that provide announcements).   # Click *Create* to create a new issue.   # Tab to the *Projects* or *Issue Types* drop-down menu.   # Select an option using the UP and DOWN arrows, then click enter to confirm the choice.    h3. Expected Results  The option chosen will be read by the VA utility, confirming the choice to the user.    h3. Actual Results  No feedback is given to the user through the VA utility.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Stored XSS via malicious file upload - CVE-2020-14173"
description: """The file upload feature in Atlassian Jira Server and Data Center in affected versions allows remote attackers to inject arbitrary HTML or JavaScript via a cross site scripting (XSS) vulnerability.    *Affected versions*   * version < 8.5.4   * 8.6.0 ≤ version ≤ 8.7.0   * 8.7.0 ≤ version < 8.7.1    *Fixed versions*   * 8.5.4   * 8.7.1   * 8.8.0"""

summary: "Jira create issue by mail if folder is not Inbox cc is ignored."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When mail is read it's ignoring cc when the folder to process is not Inbox.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create mail handler for """"Create a new issue or add a comment to an existing issue""""   # Specify Folder other than Inbox   # Send an email to the Jira instance with cc for the assignee.    h3. Expected Results    The cc user that does exist in Jira and has access to the project gets assigned to the issue.  h3. Actual Results    The mail is processed and the issue is created and the cc is ignored and not assigned the issue.  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "When Creating Issue/Editing Issue, Batching Notification Triggered Notification Email Did Not Render Cascading Select List Correctly"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When Creating Issue/Editing Issue, Batching Notification Triggered Notification Email Did Not Render Cascading Select List Correctly    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Enable Batching Notification  # Setup a custom field cascading select list and configure with the screen   # Create a new ticket and fill in the cascade select list, both fields    h3. Expected Results  Batching notification renders it correctly.    h3. Actual Results   !image-2020-03-25-13-56-25-496.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  No workaround currently.  """

summary: "Custom field name which contains apostrophe are shown as HTML in bulk edit issue"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Custom field name which contains apostrophe are shown as HTML codes in bulk edit confirmation screen.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create custom field """"Object of Senior Manager's review""""   # Move search screen and operate bulk change   # Select Edit Issues   # Change """"Senior Manager's review"""" field   # Click Next    h3. Expected Results    In confirmation screen, field name should display """"Object of Senior Manager's review""""  h3. Actual Results    Field name shown as """"Object of Senior Manager&\#39;s review""""    !Screen Shot 2020-03-26 at 14.41.35.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "When adding attachments (in JSD queue view), Jira automatically increments + appends a number suffix even if the file does not exists"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When adding attachments in JSD, JSD automatically increments + appends a number suffix even if the file does not exists    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Jira 8.6.0/JSD 4.6.0   # Browse > select files, in the """"Add attachment"""" pop up, cancel the the upload   # Do the same thing again, observe that the number suffix is added, it is also incremented if this is operation is done repeatedly.   # Try creating a new issue, perform the same steps above and observe the number suffix is added, even if the attachments do not exist on the new issue.    Video clip demonstration: [^Recording #4.mp4]     Additional note:  * Once the above happens, try attaching the same files in another Business or Software type project, the number suffix will also be automatically added, even on a different project type/issue.    h3. Expected Results  Jira should not append the number suffix, since the attachment with the same name does not exists.  h3. Actual Results  Jira appends a number suffix, even on a new issue without any attachments    h3. Workaround  Disable {{jira.attachments.generate.unique.suffix.enabled}} feature flag, however doing this will cause the instance will be affected this bug: https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-2169"""

summary: "Cannot perform basic search towards a field with even though the search criteria matches its scope"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When performing basic search using custom field used in multiple projects, no values shows for selection. It shows """"No Matches"""" in the drop down.    !Screenshot 2020-03-30 at 2.14.55 PM.png|width=548,height=205!    In that case, I was able to perform an advanced search and select the value, but when switching back to basic search, the value was not supported.    !Screenshot 2020-03-30 at 2.13.05 PM.png|width=640,height=171!    !Screenshot 2020-03-30 at 2.03.27 PM.png|width=636,height=157!  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Create a custom field, use it in different context.   * Try to perform a basic search, using it as criteria along with the correct issue type.    h3. Expected Results    You should be able to to select that field's options.  h3. Actual Results    You cannot perform a basic search using the custom field even though the criteria matches with its scope.  h3. Workaround    Use [advanced search|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/advanced-searching-939938733.html] instead."""

summary: "Attaching an image using the ""What you see what you get"" panel in the comment section does not update the file name correctly in the comment"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Attaching a image using the """"What you see what you get"""" panel in the comment section does not update the file name attached.   Example:  Upload image001.png then check out the text mode in the comment, it points to the correct image which is image001.png, but when uploading another image with the same name image001.png the image uploads correctly with the new name image001-1.png but the comment does not get updated and remains with the original name of the image which is image001.png which is an incorrect behaviour as the comment should be updated to include the new name of the picture which is image001-1.png.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Attach picture attachment in the """"What you see what you get"""" panel in the comment section (Example image001.png)   # Post the comment    # Check the comment in """"Text"""" Mode, it should be      # Now create a new comment and upload another picture with the same name (image001.png) from the """"What you see what you get"""" panel in the comment section.    # The image is uploaded with the name (image001-1.png), which is the correct behaviour as this was fixed in Jira 8.6 and above (https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-2169)   # Check out the comment in """"Text"""" Mode, it shows  which is incorrect.    h3. Expected Results  When checking out the comment in text mode after uploading the second image, the comment should be  to show the correct picture attached.  h3. Actual Results  When checking out the comment in text mode after uploading the second image, the comment shows  which is incorrect because it shows the first picture attached not the second picture.    h3. Workaround    Currently, the workaround for this issue is to use the """"Drag and Drop"""" feature to add the image then *choose* the image from the """"What you see what you get"""" panel in the comment section.    OR     To use the """"Browse"""" feature from the attachment Section in the ticket then *choose* the image from the """"What you see what you get"""" panel in the comment section.    (+) You could also manually edit the image name from the comment in text mode.    Note that you can find both those features from the section shown in the picture below:    *  !Attachment.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Shared filters are not working when the JQL contains decimal numbers and user profile set other than English"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When there are shared filters created when the user profile is set to """"English"""" if the others are using it whose profile language other than """"English"""" the """"JQL"""" is getting not getting translated when there are decimal numbers present in JQL and the """"shared filter"""" is not working as the decimals in JQL is not getting translated.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field with a name in the """"French"""" language of the """"number"""" type.   !Screenshot 2020-03-30 at 4.18.46 PM.png|thumbnail!   # Give the custom field some """"Decimal"""" values.   # Create a filter with some """"JQL"""" like below, save the filter when the user profile is set to """"English"""" and share it with a group.   # Log in as a different user whose profile is set to a different language other than """"English"""" and try to access the """"shared filter"""".    h3. Expected Results    The """"JQL"""" saved in the """"shared filter"""" should be translated to the user's profile language and results should be displayed like below:   !Screenshot 2020-03-31 at 7.03.30 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    The below error is thrown if we use the same filter when the user profile is set to """"French"""", but the user is expecting it to be working despite the user's profile language.   !Screenshot 2020-03-31 at 7.07.04 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    As a workaround manually edit the decimal numbers in double-quotes with a comma(,) separator instead of (.) which will give results.    !Screenshot 2020-03-31 at 7.48.30 PM.png|thumbnail!"""

summary: "CSRF via Logging and Profiling feature - CVE-2019-20415"
description: """Atlassian Jira Server and Data Center in affected versions allows remote attackers to modify logging and profiling settings via a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability.    *Affected versions:*   * version < 7.13.3   * 8.0.0 ≤ version < 8.1.0    *Fixed versions:*   * 7.13.3   * 8.1.0"""

summary: "XSS via project configuration - CVE-2019-20416 "
description: """Affected versions of Atlassian Jira Server and Data Center allow remote attackers to inject arbitrary HTML or JavaScript via a cross site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the project configuration feature.    *Affected versions:*   * version < 8.3.0    *Fixed versions:*   * 8.3.0"""

summary: "When a project is imported, the ""Start date"" of Versions in the ""Releases"" page, does not get imported"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a project is imported, the """"Start date"""" of Versions in the """"Releases"""" page, does not get imported. Only the """"Release date"""" will get imported.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Version in a Software project.   # Set a """"Start date"""" & a """"Release date"""".   #* The status of the version does not matter here """"UNRELEASED"""" OR """"RELEASED""""   # Create an XML backup   # Delete the project from Jira   # Create a new empty project with the same Project key   # Import the project from the XML back using the """"Project Import"""" tool.   # Once the import is complete check the version in the """"Releases"""" page of the project.    h3. Expected Results    *Both* the """"Start date"""" & """"Release date"""" should be imported with the project.    h3. Actual Results    *Only* the """"Release date"""" is imported with the project.  The """"Start date"""" isn't getting imported with the project.      *Before the import*    *  !Before.png|thumbnail!     *After the import*    *  !after.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    You can add the """"Start date"""" manually for each version after the import, but you will have to save/take a screenshot *before* the import in order to have the data in hand."""

summary: "Project dropdown menu in basic issue search does not respond on Internet Explorer / Edge browsers"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Project dropdown menu in basic issue search does not respond on Internet Explorer / Edge browsers.    This is reproducible only on instance with larger number of projects (1000 in reproduction scenario).    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Make sure to have many projects on the instance.  #* On a test instance, [Jira Data Generator|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1210725/data-generator-for-jira?hosting=server&tab=overview|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1210725/data-generator-for-jira?hosting=server&tab=overview] can be used to create projects data automatically.  # Using IE / Edge browser, go to Issues > Search for issues > make sure the Basic search is enabled  # Select """"Project: All"""" dropdown button    h3. Expected Results  The list of projects opens right after clicking the dropdown field.    h3. Actual Results  The list of projects does not open for a long time (or doesn't open at all). After some time, the following warning appears in the browser:   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   The browser becomes unresponsive. Closing and reopening the browser helps with this.    h3. Workaround  * Use Advanced search mode (JQL) and specify the search query  * Use other browser (e.g. Safari, Chrome, Firefox)    h3. Notes  The issue is not reproducible if the instance does not have many projects."""

summary: "Tables in plain text are not formatted properly if comment is added by a mail handler"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira does a good job formatting plain text tables when they're added as comments. For example, a SQL result set will look nicely formatted the same way it look in the console.     However, when exact same plain text in the comment is added via an email handler, it looks misformatted.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a SQL output as a comment to a Jira issue between noformat tags. For example:     # Verify the table is displayed nicely:   !comment_ui.png|thumbnail!   # Post the same comment via an email handler.     h3. Expected Results  The comment should show as if it was added through the UI.    h3. Actual Results  The plain text table doesn't display nicely. Instead, rows are split across two lines and there are extra spaces added that make the output misformatted:    !comment_email.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  None"""

summary:
"Adding a description for the Attachments field from the field configuration, shows

tags in the create issue screen."
description:
"""h3. Issue Summary When adding a description for the Attachments field from the field configuration, shows

tags in the create issue screen with the text you added in between the

tags. h3. Steps to Reproduce # Navigate to Administration --> Issues --> Field configurations --> Choose any field configuration and click on """" Configure"""". # Search for the Attachments field, click on """"Edit"""" #* !fields.png|thumbnail! # Add any text (e.g Test 2) #* !attachment field text.png|thumbnail! # Save and reindex in order for the changes to show. # Click on the Create button from the top panel in Jira # Check under the attachment field h3. Expected Results The text added in the description shows the correct text added without the

tags h3. Actual Results The text added in the description shows the text in between

tags * !issue.png|thumbnail! h3. Workaround Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available h3. Note I have tested this on another system field like Assignee Field and the issue does not appear for that field, it seems to be only affecting the Attachments field. * !Assignee field text.png|thumbnail! """

description: """Affected versions of Atlassian Jira Server and Data Center allow remote attackers to inject arbitrary HTML or JavaScript via a cross site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in Issue Navigator Basic Search.    *Affected versions:*   * version < 7.13.9   * 8.0.0 ≤ version < 8.4.2    *Fixed versions:*   * 7.13.9   * 8.4.2   * 8.5.0"""

summary: "Configure Fields form on Create Issue ordering is different on IE than Chrome"
description: """The issue is that when you look at Configure Fields dialog, the ordering is different in IE 11 than in Chrome.    h6. Steps to reproduce:  1. Go to issue > Edit > configure fields  2. Look at the fields ordering, in Chrome it is alphabetic Column A, then alphabetic Column B     h6. Actual behavior  In IE 11 it is alphabetic by Row then by Column     !ie_11.png|thumbnail!     h6. Expected Behavior  Alphabetic by Column then by Row.     !chrome.png|thumbnail!       """

summary: "Field values automatically cleared due to unfilled required fields when creating issues from Admin mode"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Some field values automatically clear when leaving a required field empty when creating issues from Admin mode, but when using the Create Issue pop-up window the behaviour does not occur.     h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Creating a new issue out of Admin mode pops up a Create Issue window, where unfilled required fields will be notified:  #* !createpopup.png|thumbnail!  # Creating a new issue from Admin mode (e.g. from Administration -> Issues) will not pop up a Create Issue window, instead, the process will go through 2 phases:  #* !createadmin.png|thumbnail!  #* !createerror.png|thumbnail!    # Step 1: Click """"Create Issue"""" so that you are navigated to a separate browser tab (not pop-up).  # Step 2: Fill the section with """"Linked Issues"""". At the same time, leave any required field as empty.  # Step 3: Click """"create"""".  # Step 4: Jira shows that some required field must be filled.      h3. Expected Results    The point is, other field values will be remained if already filled, including Epic Link, Sprint, and Linked Issues:  !createremain.png|thumbnail!    h3. Actual Results  JIRA clears field with linked issues. The point is, if required fields are unfilled (and the page refreshes), then specific fields will lose their filled value or even go missing. In the following screenshot, Epic Link, Sprint, and Linked Issues are losing their filled value (automatically cleared) while Remaining Estimate is missing:  !createcleared.png|thumbnail!    h3. Note    This problem is not reproduced while trying to create an issue from pop-up – in this case, JIRA saves all filled linked issues as expected.    h3. Workaround  * Make sure required fields are filled before clicking on Create to create a new issue  * Use the Create Issue pop-up window out of Admin mode"""

summary: "Opening a JQL search link that is from ""Detail view"", in a ""List view"" instance, open the first Issue of the search"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Opening a JQL query that was created in """"Detail View"""", into an instance that is in the """"List view"""", opens just the first issue o the query results, instead of the query results.    This is issue is not happening from """"List View"""" to """"Detail View"""".  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to Issue search.   # Change the view to *""""Detail View""""*   # Create an advanced/basic JQL query (for example): *order by lastViewed DESC*   # Search the query.   # Copy and save the search link (for example): [http://localhost:8080/browse/TDC-7?jql=order%20by%20lastViewed%20DESC]   (i) Note that the *TDC-7* in the query is the first result of the JQL query.   # Change the view to *""""List View""""*.   # Paste the saved link in the URL and search.   # The first issue *(TDC-7)* view will open, instead of the """"*Detail View*"""".    h3. Expected Results    Open the *""""Detail View""""* instead of the First issue view.  h3. Actual Results    The first issue of the JQL query returns, instead of the """"*Detail View*"""". This issue is not happening if have more than   h3. Workaround    Click on the *""""Return to search""""* link (or type *'u'*) and switch back to *""""Detail View""""*."""

summary: "Dashboard Gadgets showing characters bleeding into the gadget"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When viewing the activity stream gadget, there are characters seen in the left margin  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Dashboard   # Add the activity stream gadget    h3. Expected Results    The gadget shows as expected  h3. Actual Results    See attachment    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Manually deleting HTTP Access Logs cause them to not be recreated before an application restart."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Manually deleting Jira HTTP Access Logs cause them to not be recreated before an application restart.  (!) Note that enabling Jira HTTP Access Logs is not recommended and Tomcat access logs preferably should be used since they are enabled by default.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Enable HTTP Access Logs;  # Manually delete the HTTP Access Logs files;  # Disable HTTP Access Logs;  # Re-enable HTTP Access Logs;    h3. Expected Results  A new log file is created/rotated    h3. Actual Results  The file is not created     h3. Workaround  Restart Jira and re-enable HTTP Access Logs"""

summary: "Disabling HTML renderer in custom field description breaks the system field description"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    With 8.7, the default behavior of the *Enable HTML in custom field descriptions* and list values items option will now be switched to OFF for new Jira installations and the upgraded ones that have never used it. Please check the link below for details :   *    ** [Upgrade notes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-8-7-x-upgrade-notes-]    With this option set to off by default, many systems filed description that contains HTML characters are represented with HTML code there than plain text       !Screenshot 2020-04-15 at 7.34.26 PM.png|thumbnail!   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to field configuration tab>time tracking   # You will see under description the HTML code appears    h3. Expected Results    If """"Enable HTML in custom field descriptions"""" is set to off by default then the system filed description should be adjusted to display as plain text rather then HTML code  h3. Actual Results    Right now when we check the system field description then it is showing the HTML code in the description  h3. Workaround    User can turn on the """"Enable HTML in custom field descriptions"""" option which resolves the issue"""

summary: "XSS in XML export view - CVE-2020-4021"
description: """Affected versions of Atlassian Jira Server and Data Center allow remote attackers to inject arbitrary HTML or JavaScript via a cross site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the XML export view.    *Affected versions:*   * version < 7.13.16   * 8.0.0 ≤ version < 8.5.5   * 8.6.0 ≤ version < 8.8.1     *Fixed versions:*   * 7.13.16   * 8.5.5   * 8.8.1   * 8.9.0"""

summary: "If the text in a table has color, Rich Text Editor creates a new column instead of a new line when pressing enter"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If the text in a table has color, Rich Text Editor creates a new column instead of a new line when pressing enter key.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a table   # Change text color to red   # Edit the text in the column and press enter key (In Visual mode)    h3. Expected Results  A new line should be created    h3. Actual Results  A new column is created    Video clip:  [^Recording #5.mp4]     h3. Workaround  Switch to text mode or hit """"Shift + Enter"""" instead"""

summary: " issue type will be moved to the new issue type and status will be set to ""closed"" but : the resolution field is not set."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Resolution is empty after moving an issue to status closed using the move feature.         Without a resolution set, the resolution date also is missing. In that case, the issue data is inconsistent and you are not able to run reports based on it.    From our point of view that allows unwanted behavior. Is there any way to prevent this or to force to set the resolution on moving issues, too?  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1. open a ticket  2. select more -> move  3. you see the following screen and set """"new issue type"""" to an issue type that uses another workflow (see firstscreen.jpg)  4. click """"next""""  5. since the target workflow does not contain current status """"Einspielbereit (Prod)"""", Jira asks for the status to be set. If the user selects """"closed"""" as new status, the ticket is closed (see secondscreen.jpg)  6. click """"next""""  7. click """"next"""" again  => the issue type will be moved to the new issue type and status will be set to """"closed"""" but: the resolution field is not set. Expected Results  h3. Expected Results    The issue type will be moved to the new issue type and status will be set to """"closed""""  with the correct resolution state.  h3. Actual Results    Setting the resolution field is triggered in all our workflow transitions to target status """"closed"""" (see workflow1.jpg & resolveissuescreen.jpg). Obviously with """"move"""", the issue type and the status are set, but the workflow rules of the target issue type are ignored.  h3. Workaround    No workaround """

summary: "Sharing dashboard with multiple groups caused users to lost ability to edit the dashboard"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Users are unable to edit a dashboard if the dashboard is shared with multiple groups.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Have the following configuration:   * group1 - member is user1   * group2 - member is user2    How to reproduce:   # Create new dashboard   # Grant editor permissions to group1   # User1 is able to edit dashboard (expected)   # Grant editor permissions to group2 OR to user2 individually   # User2 is unable to edit dashboard (not expected). User2 is able to view the dashboard but the """"Edit dashboard"""", """"Add Gadget"""" and """"Edit layout"""" options are missing.    !Edit Dashboard missing.png|thumbnail!    It seems that only the group permissions takes precedence while any subsequent group/users are ignored.  h3. Expected Results    User 2 should be able to edit the dashboard, as the user is a member of """"group2"""" which has edit permissions.  h3. Actual Results    Members of the """"group2"""" group cannot edit the dashboard  h3. Workaround   # Grant edit permissions to a single group and make sure all the relevant users that need to edit the dashboard are part of this group   # Grant permissions to individual users manually."""

summary: "Template injection in Web Resources Manager - CVE-2020-14172"
description: """This issue exists to document that a security improvement in the way that Jira Server and Data Center use velocity templates has been implemented.    The way in which velocity templates were used in Atlassian Jira Server and Data Center prior to version 8.8.1 allowed remote attackers to achieve remote code execution via insecure deserialization, if they were able to exploit a server side template injection vulnerability.    *Affected versions:*   * version < 7.13.0   * 8.0.0 ≤ version < 8.5.0   * 8.6.0 ≤ version < 8.8.1    *Fixed versions:*   * 8.8.1   * 8.9.0     *Workaround for previous versions:*  You use this workaround at your own discretion.  {quote}  1. Download [Jira 8.12|https://product-downloads.atlassian.com/software/jira/downloads/atlassian-jira-software-8.12.2.zip] as zip and extract it’s content  2. [Localize your Jira instance installation directory|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/jira-application-installation-directory-938847745.html]  3. Shutdown your Jira instance  4. Go to the _<jira_installation_dir>/atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/lib/_ directory  5. Remove files: _velocity-htmlsafe-3.0.0.jar_ and _velocity-1.6.4-atlassian-7.jar_  6. Copy files _velocity-htmlsafe-3.1.0.jar_ and _velocity-1.6.4-atlassian-21.jar_ from downloaded Jira 8.12 to current directory. They are found in the _<Jira_8_12_dir>/atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/lib/_ directory  7. Go to the _<jira_installation_dir>/atlassian-jira/WEB-INF/classes_ directory  8. Replace the velocity.properties and velocity-static.properties files with their counterparts from the downloaded Jira 8.12  9. Start your Jira instance again  {quote}"""

summary: "While editing a worklog, the seconds are being ignored by Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  User is trying to edit a worklog entry. The dialog box which opens when clicking on the edit button is ignoring the value in seconds and hence even if the dialog box is saved without editing anything, the time logged is changing.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Add a time spent value - 0.2525h to a ticket.  Screenshot1, Screenshot2  * Now click on the edit option from the worklog. The dialog box contains 'Time spent' as 15m. 15sec gets ignored by Jira.  Screenshot3  * Click on 'log' without changing anything. The logged time gets changed to 0.25h i.e, 15m.  Screenshot4    h3. Expected Results  Time spent value should have been pre-filled by 15.15m when clicked on edit worklog.  h3. Actual Results  Time spent value is ignoring the seconds and just has 15m while trying to edit the worklog.    h3. Workaround  Manually edit the Time spent to the correct value."""

summary: "Error 500 viewing saved filter that searches for EMPTY in a Labels type field"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When accessing a saved filter with a search like """"mylabels in(EMPTY)"""" where """"mylabels"""" is a Labels type custom field, although the search works and the user is able to save the filter, returning to it afterward will return a 500 error.    If this filter is used by an Agile Board, the filter can't be edited.    This can be an intermittent behavior.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new custom field of """"Labels"""" type and name it """"mylabels""""   # Do a search for """"mylabels in(EMPTY)""""   # Save it as a new filter   # Navigate to """"Manage Filters""""   # Click on the saved filter    h3. Expected Results    The filter and results are shown  h3. Actual Results    500 Error page with the following error is shown:   """"Sorry, we had some technical problems during your last operation.""""    The below exception is thrown in the """"atlassian-jira.log"""" file:       (!) It also happens when adding other values to the search """"mylabels in (test, otherlabel, EMPTY)""""   The problem does not happen when searching for """"mylabels is EMPTY""""   It also does not happen with the default """"labels"""" field  h3. Workaround    The filter can be accessed when it is displayed on the Favourites bar on the left.   To fix the filter do:   # Go to Manage Filter   # Search for the filter that can be viewed   # Add it to your favorites by adding a star to it   # Click on any other filter that can be viewed correctly.   # Click the name of the filter now showing on the left panel as a favorite.   # Remove the """"EMPTY"""" search and hit Enter to search.   # Save the filter"""

summary: "About Jira page can be accessed anonymously"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  """"About Jira"""" page can be accessed anonymously. This can expose the Jira application versions. Some customers might want to prevent this information from being available as it could be used to target other vulnerabilities specific to the version.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Access <JIRA BASE URL>/secure/AboutPage.jspa anonymously    h3. Expected Results  The user gets redirected to log in    h3. Actual Results  The page is shown:   !image-2020-04-29-16-59-12-723.png|thumbnail!     Notes  This happens even if the public access is blocked using a flag in the dark features     h3. Workaround  Create a block the URL from being accessed from the proxy side. Another option is doing it from Apache Tomcat: [How to block access to a specific URL at Tomcat|https://confluence.atlassian.com/kb/how-to-block-access-to-a-specific-url-at-tomcat-966668691.html]"""

summary: "Two Dimensional Filter with Epic Link and filter on issuetype generates large JQL query"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When *Two Dimensional Filter* is configured with *Epic Link* on an axis and using a filter that has *issuetype* the generated JQL includes an additional *issuetype* check.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create filter to include {{issuetype != Epic}}   # Add *Two Dimensional Filter* gadget and select *Epic Link* as one of the axis.   # Select the hyperlink for any *Epic Link* entry    h3. Expected Results    Filter created for test {{project = BST AND status not in (Resolved, Closed, Done) AND issuetype != Epic}}    The expected JQL created is expected to use the *filter* and add the additional search criteria of the second filter. In the case of selecting all issue with *Epic Link* BST-501 we expect the search query to be  {code:java}  project = BST AND status not in (Resolved, Closed, Done) AND issuetype != Epic AND """"Epic Link"""" = BST-501 {code}  h3. Actual Results    The JQL query generated repeats the *project* check and *issuetype* but using an *in* against the already *not in*.    The result of adding in the duplicated search criteria results in customer query exceeding 10,000 characters when they have a large number of issuetypes which fails their maximum URL length.  h3. Workaround    The only workaround is to not use *issuetype* in the filter if *Epic Link* is to be used as an axis in the gadget display.    In the case of the customer they wish to exclude *Epics* from the returned data so the workaround is invalid."""

summary: "Cluster monitoring does not work when node name has parenthesis  ""()"" character"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The clustering monitoring page does not work in case the node name has parenthesis / bracket """":"""" character hence not providing CPU/Memory/Uptime information.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Deploy JIRA Datacenter 8.6.1  # In the cluster.properties file, add the node name (jira.node.id) as """"node1(1)""""    h3. Expected Results  * CPU/Memory/Uptime information to be displayed:    h3. Actual Results  * CPU/Memory/Uptime information *is not displayed*:   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  * Remove the parenthesis / bracket """":"""" from the node name (jira.node.id in cluster.properties file).     !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "The Message-ID of new and update notifications for an issue is different with MySQL 5.6 and later"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The Message-ID of new and update notifications for an issue is different with MySQL 5.6 and later  h5. A message-id of the new notification email:    h5. A message-id of the update notification email:      *1563869260000* becomes *1563869261000* .  it is a different 1000.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an issue. and check the message-id of the notification email   # Update the issue. and check the message-id of the notification email.    h3. Expected Results  The message-id is same    h3. Actual Results  The message-id is not the same    This issue happens with MySQL 5.6 and later.  MySQL 5.6 has fractional seconds support from MySQL 5.6  https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.6/en/fractional-seconds.html    Check the DB recode      The corresponding data is *1563869261* on the DB at the time it was registered to the DB.  We believe this is because the created column has no decimals and has been rounded to the nearest decimal point and carried forward.  When creating a message-ID, milliseconds are truncated, but when storing the original time in the DB, milliseconds are rounded up and registered in the DB.  At the time of update, because the date and time referenced from the DB are used, it becomes the one that is carried forward, so it is seen that this phenomenon occurs as a result of the difference of 1000.  The milliseconds are truncated to *499ms or less* , so it just so happens to work as before.    h3. Workaround  no workaround at this moment"""

summary: "Wiki style renderer: Elements inserted outside visible range of field"
description: """*Issue Summary:*    In a field using the Wiki style renderer, if a user adds a table, code block, preformatted block or panel at the bottom of the page, the field will not display or jump to the bottom, but add the element below its visible bottom.    *To reproduce:*   # Create a new issue or go to an existing issue that has a field with Wiki style renderer.   # Insert text until the scroll bar appears   # Add any of the following:   ## Table   ## Code   ## Preformatted   ## Panel    *Expected results:*    The field will automatically scroll down to where the cursor is; specifically, the bottom of the field.    *Actual results:*    The inserted element is inserted _below_ the bottom of the *visible* field. The cursor is hidden, and the field does not scroll down.    *Workaround:*    The user must scroll down manually after adding any of the mentioned elements to see what they have most recently added to the field."""

summary: "Timezone changes in Jira is updating issues with 1 hour forwarded"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When the Default time zone changes from IST (GMT+5:30(Kolkata)) to the time zone (GMT+4(Baku)) the issues' timestamps are getting updated with 1 hour forwarded. (We have also verified if there are any Day list saving changes and also by updating system time zone and Jira's time zone to the same one but still, the issue persists.)    (on) This issue is reproducible only when the timezone is set to GMT+4(Baku)).  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Check an issue's """"updated time """" when the time zone is set to (GMT+5:30(Kolkata))   # Change the time zone from (GMT+5:30(Kolkata)) to the time zone (GMT+4(Baku)) by navigating to (Jira administration > System > """"Edit settings"""" under General configuration and internationalization update the timezone)    h3. Expected Results    The issue """"updated time"""" should move backward by 1hour 30 minutes as per the difference in time zone but it's moving only by 30minutes.  h3. Actual Results    The """"updated time"""" is getting updated with timestamp 1 hour forward.  h3. Workaround   * Change the timezone to (GMT+04:00)Dubai, Muscat, Tbilisi, Yerevan from the time zone (GMT+4(Baku), the timezone changes will work normally."""

Symptoms   * A custom field called """"myCustomField"""" is created, and is assigned globally to all projects/issue types   * Some issues have a value for this field, some do not   * Searching for {{myCustomField IS EMPTY}} using JQL returns no results, despite there being known issues with an empty value for that custom field    """

summary: "Mobile app does not check start date value when enter due date value"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When using the jira server mobile app, the date value entered into the Due Date field does not appear to be checking what the start date was set to. As a result it will let you pick any value, even if this is before the start date.   This was reproduced at least when the language pack was set to Russian, but might also affect other language/region settings.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   #Create a new issue in the mobile app and set the start date to some value   # on the same issue try to set the due date before the start date    h3. Expected Results  Mobile app should prevent end users from entering invalid dates for this field, just as the web front end does.     h3. Actual Results  It does not prevent this.  The problem comes for the next person that edits the Jira issue (not in the mobile app) that person cannot publish other edits because there exists a check to prevent invalid due dates based on the start date.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Text Not Properly Handled in Multi-Select Field"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The text is being cut when its length exceeds the field's width in the multi-select field. This was tested in the create issue screen.    Also, in JSD, the text is being shortened in the customer portal instead of being wrapped, which is not the ideal way to display the options.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Multi-select custom field and add options with long text.   # Add the custom field to the create issue screen.   # Create a new issue and try to select the options in the multi-select field.    h3. Expected Results    The text should be properly displayed. Ideally, the text would be wrapped to display the entire content.  h3. Actual Results    The text that is longer than the field's width is being cut (attaching screenshot).    !multi-select-options.png|width=252,height=131!    Also, in JSD, the text is being shortened in the customer portal instead of being wrapped, which is not the ideal way to display the options.    !CustomerPortal.png|width=261,height=192!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Wrong translation for Edit Sprint in German"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Translation for the word """"edit sprint"""" in German is incorrect in the Jira platform. In German, it is translated as End sprint  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Change the language to Germany from profile > language settings   # Create a software project Using """"Scrum Software development"""" template   # Add an issue to the backlog and create an active sprint   # Here if you try to click on Edit sprint option, the Edit sprint show as """"Sprint beenden"""" which means """"End sprint"""" in German.   # Here the german translation for Edit sprint is showing incorrectly on UI   # It should show Edit sprint (Sprint bearbeiten)    h3. Actual Results    The translation show that it means End sprint (Sprint beenden)         *EXPECTED RESULT*    The translation should show Edit sprint (Sprint bearbeiten)          """

summary: "While editing an issue in two browser tabs the first committed changes are being overwritten with the least recent updates"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    While editing an issue in two browser tabs the first committed changes are being overwritten with the least recent updates.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open the same issue in two tabs in the same browser.   # In the first tab click in """"Edit"""" to edit the issue and update the """"Description"""" and click on update   # In the second tab click """"Edit"""" butting of the same issue and change the """"Summary"""" and click on update.   # Now the changes that we have made to the description in the first tab, will be removed as in the 2nd tab we have updated only the summary but not Description.          Attached screen recording of the issue that has been reported:       h3. Expected Results    It should update both Description and Summary as by clicking on the """"update"""" we are committing changes.  h3. Actual Results    Only the least recently made updates(in this case updating summary)are being considered despite that we have updated the description in the first tab  h3. Workaround    When you observed the changes are not being reflected in the second tab ( in this case it's description) try to update the description as well along with the Summary."""

summary: "JQL filter from JIRA 7.13 does not work on JIRA 8.5"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JQL filter from JIRA 7.13 does not work on JIRA 8.5  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In Jira 7.13 a search like """"customfield ~ """"PRROJECT-*"""" showed all issues, with values like """"PROJECT-1234"""" in customfield.  In Jira 8.5 the same JQL search term delivers an empty result. This is a CHANGE of behavior or functionality.   # In Jira 8.5 a search like """"customfield ~ """"PRROJECT-"""" showed all issues, with values like """"PROJECT-1234"""" in customfield.  In Jira 7.13 the same JQL search term delivers an empty result. This is a CHANGE of behavior or functionality.    h3. Expected Results    JQL query should give constant results, where as result differ   h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:    h3. Workaround       Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Canned Response (request.details) replace double quotes with '$quote' in Summary"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When we create a canned response with variable request.details. If the Summary field has double quotes, it get replaced by    . This behavior is seen only for Summary(Title). For other fields like Description, the issue is not seen.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1. Create a ticket with Title containing double quotes. For example: Error found """"Testing error"""" and update the same in description as well   2. Create a canned response and select variable request.details   3. In comment, section for Summary it shows as Error found """"Testing error""""   4. However, the Description shows correct fonts: Error found """"Testing error""""  h3. Expected Results    Summary should all show double quotes  h3. Actual Results    Summary it shows as Error found """"Testing error""""  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Gadget wrong translation in Swedish language pack"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In the gadget 'Assigned to me' from the Dashboard, Jira shows wrong in translation. It should be 'inga' instead of 'inge'  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Under Administration>Settings>Internationalization: Change the default language to """"svenska (Sverige)"""".   # Go to the dashboard that has 'Assigned to Me' gadget.    h3. Expected Results    It should be  {quote}'Du har för närvarande *inga* ärenden tilldelade till dig. Njut av dagen!'  {quote}  h3. Actual Results   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    In the actual result shows,  {quote}Du har för närvarande *inge* ärenden tilldelade till dig. Njut av dagen!  {quote}  h3. Workaround  no workaround at the moment"""

summary: "Selecting both board types in View All Boards page result in not displaying any board"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Selecting both board types in View All Boards page result in not displaying any board    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a board of type Kanban or Scrum;  # Navigate to Boards > View All Boards;  # In the filter section, expand *Board type*;  # Select _both_ Scrum and Kanban;    h3. Note that the selection is mutually exclusive, however the filter string says that """"all"""" boards are filtered.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. summary: "JQL basic query does not work as expected if the script is used in customfield"

summary: "Jira Mail Handler Configuration can't be edited when using an incorrect project / issue type combination"
description: """h3. Issue description    Jira Mail Handler of types *Create a new issue or add a comment to an existing issue* and *Create a new issue from each email message* can't be edited if they have been created with an incorrect Project / Issue Type combination.  h3. Steps to reproduce   - Create 2 Jira projects using different Issue Type Schemes. For example:   -- 1 project only has 1 issue type (Bug) -> let's call this project """"Awesome""""   -- 1 project only has 1 issue type (Story) -> let's call this project """"Awesomeness""""   - Go to the page *⚙ > System > Incoming Mails*   - Configure a Mail Server (POP or IMAP)   - Click on the *Add incoming mail handler* button to create a new mail handler   - Select the type *Create a new issue or add a comment to an existing issue*, and click on the *Next* button   - Select the project """"Awesome"""" and the issue type """"Bug""""   !Step1.png|thumbnail!   - Change the project from """"Awesome"""" to """"Awesomeness""""   -- (!) Notice that the issue type field is now empty, because the """"Bug"""" issue type does not exist in the project """"Awesomeness""""   !Step2.png|thumbnail!   - Click on the *Add* button to save this mail handler (with an empty issue type)   -- (!) Notice that the mail handler was created with the Project """"Awesomeness"""" and the issue type """"Bug"""" *which does not exist in this project   !Step3.png|thumbnail!   - Click on the *Edit* button next to this mail handler   - Click on the *Next* button to edit the configuration of this mail handler    h3. Expected behavior    We should be redirected to the page that allows us to configure various settings such as Project, Issue Type, Strip Quotes, Catch Email Address, etc...  h3. Actual behavior   - We are redirected to the page *⚙ > System > Incoming Mail* and the screen is frozen/greyed   - The only way to get out of this page is to hit the *Esc* key   - The following error is displayed in the Browse Console logs    !Step4.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround    The only know workaround is to delete this corrupted mail handler, and to re-create it with a valid combination of project and issue type."""

summary: "Long description string expands column outside of filter results gadget"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When an issue containing a long string in the description field is displayed in a filter results gadget, the description column is expanded beyond the fixed size of the gadget. This pushes any columns following the description out of view, causing confusion for end users. Likewise, if the gadget is paginating results, one or some pages will resize the description field as above, while others display """"normally"""" with all of the field columns being displayed    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an issue and add an extremely long string to the description field.   # Create a search filter which includes this issue along with some other issues.   # Create a dashboard and add the Filter Results gadget; configured to use the filter above.   # Move the description field to the first position in the column order.   # View the dashboard and notice how the fields after the description column are not visible. (You can scroll to the right to find them)    h3. Expected Results    All fields/columns should be visible in the gadget at all times without having to scroll to the right to see them. A single column should not be auto-resized to take up the entire gadget.  h3. Actual Results    The description column is being resized ad infinitum based on the length of the longest string.  h3. Workaround    Break up the long strings with manual carriage returns so that the column is resized appropriately, and so that other columns are visible.  """

summary: "Projects sharing the same scheme are listed as hidden"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Whenever you access a scheme's page, Jira allows you to display a list of the projects currently using. However, if there are more than 15 projects sharing this scheme (permission, priority, field configuration), the application will display the first 15 projects and then list the others as hidden.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Step 1  Assign more than 15 projects to the same scheme, let's use Y for this value.   # Step 2  Access this scheme's page on Jira's administrative panel.    h3. Expected Results    The entire list of projects using the scheme will be displayed.    h3. Actual Results    Only the first 15 projects are displayed and then Jira displays the following message:     {quote}  and X hidden projects.  {quote}    where 15 + X = Y. As we can see in the screenshot below:     !Screen Shot 2020-05-22 at 17.54.54.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Pasting into prefotmatted box removes line breaks"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When copying some texts into a preformatted box, in some situations it removes the line breaks.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    I found 2 distinct situations in which the line breaks vanish in the preformatted box:   # HTML content. That means when you copy some content and it goes as HTML content to the clipboard. It happens on Word, Visual Studio, Web browsers... anything that keeps the formatting over the clipboard.   # When there is *more than one line break with carriage return in a row*, it removes all of them instead of at least leaving one behind. This applies to Windows notepad, putty, and so on.  On Mac, for example, it's common to have only line breaks without the carriage returns, and it removes _only_ the several in a row, leaving one behind.    h3. Expected Results    In all cases, the text paste should behave in the same way.  h3. Actual Results    Depending on the text line breaks form, it behaves differently.    For example, let's take the text:    If it has only line feeds, and it's copied to the preformatted box, we get the expected result:    If it uses line feed+ carriage return, we get an extra line break removed:    If copied from Visual Studio Code, or any browser that does it in HTML in the clipboard:    h3. Workaround    To use the """"code"""" format box, or paste into the text mode."""

summary: "Table format breaks after highlighting the text and goes to + >> Preformatted"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Table format breaks after highlighting the text and goes to + >> Preformatted.   This is happening with both Text and Visual mode. Steps to Reproduce  # Start to edit the Description field in a ticket in Text mode  # Highlight text in a cell in a table (the Lorem Ipsum text in my example below)  # Use the + text editor menu to apply the Preformatted option to the highlighted text    h3. Expected results:  ||#||Strings||Comment||Context||  |#1|| | |  h3. Actual results:  ||#||Strings||Comment||Context||  |#1|    | | |"""

summary: "sprint health gadget"" shows data from old sprint"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Sometimes sprint health gadget"""" shows data from old sprint or closed sprint rather than showing data from recent active sprints """"    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # I created a sprint test3 and start working on it.  # After that, I create a sprint health gadget in my dashboard and selected the sprint test3.  # After completing/closing that sprint, I created another sprint named sprint 4 which is active  # After that, I checked the dashboard (sprint health gadget) and it was still showing sprint 3 rather than sprint 4.  # The refreshing browser did not help as well.      h3. Actual Results  The gadget did not refresh and shows data for closed sprint       h3. Workaround  We can edit the dashboard and change the sprint manually and save the dashboard again to view data for the active sprint.      """

summary: "Page alignment and status change issue after editing Issue list through filters/JQL query"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Focus is shifting to top of the page when issues are edited from the list after getting the list through filters/JQL  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to any existing filter or write a new JQL query to get a list of issues.   # Scroll down to the latter half of the page, go to “...” > Edit > (Any changes in the Issues’ Edit Screen) > click Update. Now you see the control/focus is taken to the top of the page. You have to scroll down to see the same issue.   # Scroll down to the later half of the page, go to “...” > Edit > Change the status of the Issue (InProgress/Done/any other). The control/focus is taken to the top of the page. When you scroll down to the issue where status was changed, it is not reflected. The status change will be reflected after ‘refreshing’ the page. (Refresh and scroll down where the issue was listed)    h3. Expected Results   * The focus of the page should remain on the Issue which was edited.   * If Issues’ status is changed, it should be reflected right away, and the focus of the page should stay on the row where the issue was edited.    h3. Actual Results   * After editing the issue, the focus of the page is shifted and taken to the top of the page. Need to scroll down to the original issue’s row.   * After changing the status of the issue, change is not reflected right away. We need to refresh the page and scroll back to the row in which issue was present    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

Expected Results    The issue is created, and there is a success message after the form submission.  h3. Actual Results    The issue is created, but the form just goes blank after the form submission.    The following error is displayed n the bowser console:    h3. Workaround    Use different browsers(Firefox, Safari,etc) other than Chrome.    The issue seems to be fixed in Chrome 83.0.4103.97."""

summary: "Misleading error message when linking issue without application access"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When trying to link issue between instance A and B where user doesnt have application access in instance B, it will throw:  {quote}  The Jira server was contacted but has returned an error response. We are unsure of the result of this operation.  {quote}  This is misleading for system admin. No error is thrown in the log either.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create application link between instance A and instance B.  # Grant user X the application access of instance A, but not instance B.  # Link issue in instance A with issue in instance B.    h3. Expected Results  Error message will be thrown stating user doesnt have application access in another instance.    h3. Actual Results  It throws the following error as soon as user tries to type the issue key:  {quote}  The Jira server was contacted but has returned an error response. We are unsure of the result of this operation.  {quote}  without any error in the log either.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Unable to change dashboard/filter ownership if shared to public but Public Sharing is turned off"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a dashboard/filter is shared to Public(""""Anyone on the web""""), but _Sharing with anyone_   _on the web_ is turned +OFF+, when attempting to +Change Owner+, the admin receives the following error:  {panel:bgColor=#f8d7da}  (x) Dashboards and filters in this Jira instance cannot be shared with anyone on the web anymore. If you want to modify this item, you must change the Shares so that it is not shared with anyone on the web.  {panel}  It may be impossible to change the ownership of that filter/dashboard if the filter/dashboard is owned by someone else (potentially who is no longer contactable), and the admin does not have +Edit+ permissions, because the admin is not able to edit existing permissions unless they are the owner, or have (EDIT) permissions (JRASERVER-41269)    Users fixing filters/dashboards that need to be remediated is harder due to JRASERVER-68523  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Jira Admin -> System -> General Configuration -> Sharing with anyone on the web: *ON*   # Create a dashboard. You are the owner of this dashboard.   # Share the dashboard to Anyone on the web (VIEW)   # Jira Admin -> System -> General Configuration -> Sharing with anyone on the web: *OFF*   # Jira Admin -> System -> Shared Dashboards   # Find your dashboard and click *Change Owner*   # Attempt to change the owner to someone else   ## (!) The error is shown    h3. Expected Results    The dashboard ownership is changed. The admin can then remove the _Anyone on the web_ share setting.  h3. Actual Results    The admin receives the error above and is unable to remove the share.  h3. Workaround   # Temporarily enable public sharing via Jira Admin -> System -> General Configuration -> Sharing with anyone on the web: *OFF*   # Remove the _Anyone on the web_ share from the affected filter(s)/dashboard(s)   # Disable public sharing via Jira Admin -> System -> General Configuration -> Sharing with anyone on the web: *OFF*"""

summary: "Two dimensional filter statistics gadget showing wrong values for 'None' on X axix"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The two dimensional statistics gadget shows wrong value for 'None' when there is multi-select-list custom field in the axis. Also, redirects to a wrong filter when clicked on the value.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  1. Create custom field with multiple choices (eg. custom field named Browsers, can have internet browser as options - IE, Edge, Firefox, Chrome etc.)  2. Create a simple filter like """"project = TEST and issuetype = Bug""""  Add Two dimensional filter statistics gadget to your dashboard specifying the filter from Step 1  3. Set XAxis = some multi-select-list custom fields  4. Set YAxis = epic link  5. Save the filter.    h3. Expected Results  The 'None' value will show the empty fields and clicking on the value will take to the JQL correctly.  h3. Actual Results  The 'None' values are shown wrong data and when clicking on the value, the previously clicked filter result is displayed.  h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Date Picker in Business Projects is displayed in English while using Jira in Turkish, displaying Date format error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Although Jira is in the Turkish language, Date Picker is displayed in English. When creating an issue and picking a date (e.g. Due Date), Jira shows the following error:   !Jira error.png!    This problem was just found in Business Projects.  h3. Configure the Pie Chart gadget to use Team as the Statistic Type  3. Click the link to a Team on the Pie Chart gadget, ON board Team in the example below:    !link.png|thumbnail!  h3.
