summary: "Batched Notification emails have an empty content when triggered by a custom event using the Issue created template"
description: """h3. Summary    Batched Notification emails have an empty content when triggered by a custom event using the *Issue created* template.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   - Go to *⚙ > System > Batching email notifications* and enable batched notifications   - Go to *⚙ > System > Events*   -- Create a custom event using the *Issue Created* event as a template   !Screenshot1.png|thumbnail!   - Edit any workflow as follows:   -- Click on the transition triggered when an issue is created   -- Click on the *Post functions* link from this transition   -- Change the event fired in the post functions to the custom event   !Screenshot2.png|thumbnail!   -- Publish the new workflow   - Open a new ticket using this workflow   - Wait for the batched notifications to be received   - Check the content of the batched notification email    h3. Expected Results    The batched notification contains:   - an sentence saying """"This issue has been CREATED""""   - the details about this tickets fields (Summary, Issue Type, Priority, etc...)   !Screenshot3.png|thumbnail!    h3. Actual Results    The batched notification is almost empty. It contains the issue key and summary and a link to it, but it's missing the rest of the information:   !Screenshot4.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround   - Fire the default *Issue Created* event during the *Create* transition   - Or disable the batched notifications feature"""

summary: "Users synced from an LDAP directory can't be added to a Jira local group from the Jira UI under some conditions"
description: """h3. Summary    Users synced from an LDAP directory can't be added to a Jira local group from the Jira UI if the 2 following conditions are met:  - this group exists in both the Jira internal directory and in the LDAP server  - the LDAP directory is configured in Jira as *Read Only, with Local Groups*    h3. Steps to Reproduce    - Configure a new LDAP directory in Jira via *⚙ > User Management > User directories*  - On the LDAP server side, create:  -- a new group that already exists in Jira. For example """"jira-administrators""""  -- a new user. For example """"sa"""". Make sure that this user is not part of the group """"jira-administrators"""" on the LDAP server side  - Sync the LDAP directory with Jira via *⚙ > User Management > User directories*  - Go to *⚙ > User Management > Users*, and verify that the user was synced in Jira:   !Screenshot1.png|thumbnail!   - Go to the page *⚙ > User Management > Groups*  - Click on the *Edit members* link next to the group """"jira-administrators""""  - Try to add the LDAP user """"sa"""" to the group """"jira-administrators""""    h3. Expected Results    This user should be added to the group, since:  - the LDAP directory is configured in Jira as *Read Only, with Local Groups*  - the group """"jira-administrators"""" exists in Jira's internal directory    h3. Actual Results    The following error is thrown in the logs:       !Screenshot2.png|thumbnail!     When running the following SQL query, we can see that the group 'jira-administrators' exist twice in the cwd_group table, once in the internal directory (id = 1), and one in the LDAP directory (id = 10100).       (!) Note that the {{local}} column is set to 1 for the group coming from the LDAP directory (id=10100). Normally, local = 1 should mean that this group membership should be managed locally in Jira (since the LDAP group is configured as *Read Only, with Local Groups*). However, this setting seems to be ignored and instead Jira tries to add this user to the group 'jira-administrators' directly on the LDAP server. This operation fails, since Jira does not have the write permission in the LDAP directory.    h3. Other symptom    When trying to add the user to the group 'jira-administrators' from the page *⚙ > User Management > Users* instead, nothing happens:  - no error is thrown in the UI  - the group is not added to the user    h3. Workaround    h4. Workaround 1    The workaround consists in temporarily configuring the LDAP directory as *Read/Write* in Jira, and then add the user to the group 'jira-administators'.  Note that:  (!) With such configuration, when the user is added to the group 'jira-administators' from the Jira UI, this user *will also be added to this group on the LDAP server side*  (!) If you set the LDAP directory back to *Read Only, with Local Groups*, and then try to remove the user from the group 'jira-administators' from the page *⚙ > User Management > Groups*, you'll get the error below. Basically, you will need to set this LDAP directory back to Read/Write, whenever you want to update the members of the group 'jira-administators'.      h4. Workaround 2    - Remove the group 'jira-administators' from the LDAP server. This way, the members of this group will be entirely managed locally on the Jira side.  - Sync the LDAP directory in Jira via *⚙ > User Management > User directories*  - Add the user to the group 'jira-administrators' from the Jira UI"""

summary: "Usernames not fully anonymized in issue history"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When anonymizing a user who has an an anonymous userKey (already anonymized or created in Jira version >= 8.4) the full names in the issue history remain unchanged.   !image-2020-06-08-14-33-43-702.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an user with Jira version >= 8.9,   # Create a custom field with type """"User Picker (multiple users)"""" and assign it to project screens   # Create a new issue and fill the User Picker field value with the username from step 1.   # Anonymize the user    h3. Expected Results  Username in the issue history is changed to the user's anonymous username    h3. Actual Results  Username in the issue history remains unchanged    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Information disclosure in Login - CVE-2020-4028"
description: """Users without session information should be pushed to the login page.   Affected versions of Atlassian Jira Server and Data Center allow remote attackers to view sensitive information via an Information Disclosure vulnerability in Login.    *Affected versions:*   * version < 8.9.1    *Fixed versions:*   * 8.9.1   * 8.10.0   * 8.11.0    *Notes:*    If the fix is causing problems it can be disabled by [adding to Jira a dark feature flag|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/enable-dark-feature-in-jira-959286331.html]    The fix is available in LTS versions - 7.13.15+ and 8.5.6+ but will be disabled. The fix can be enabled by [adding to Jira a dark feature flag|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/enable-dark-feature-in-jira-959286331.html]    Both feature flags can be [added by admin via site `<jira_directory>/secure/SiteDarkFeatures!default.jspa`|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/enable-dark-feature-in-jira-959286331.html]"""

summary: "JQL does not filter issues properly with Due Date with time zone of Baku (+4)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JQL does not filter issues properly with Due Date with the time zone of Baku (+4)  h3. Environment    Jira 8.5.1  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Please create a project and add a due date field on the issue screen.   # Set the due date in a few of the issues.   # Please verify and set the default user time zone to system field   # Now especially set a due date of 31st May in of the few issues.   # Please run the following JQL query """"project = TEST AND due is not EMPTY AND due >2020-05-25 AND due < 2020-06-03""""   # The JQL will filter the desired results    # Now  go-> system -> advanced setting and change the time Region = Asia, Time Zone = (GMT+04:00)Baku   # The same JQL project = TEST AND due is not EMPTY AND due >2020-05-25 AND due < 2020-06-03  seems to be skipping May 31 results     h3. Expected Results   * Please run the following JQL query project = TEST AND due is not EMPTY AND due >2020-05-25 AND due < 2020-06-03   * The JQL will filter the desired results     h3. Actual Results   * The same JQL project = TEST AND due is not EMPTY AND due >2020-05-25 AND due < 2020-06-03  seems to be skipping May 31 results     Notes   JIRA Build: 8.5.1   JIRA Installation Type: Standalone   Java Version : 1.8.0_202 - OpenJdk  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "XSS in WYSIWYG editor via pasted code - CVE-2020-14164"
description: """Affected versions of Atlassian Jira Server and Data Center allow remote attackers to inject arbitrary HTML or JavaScript via a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the WYSIWYG editor.    The affected versions are before 8.5.9, and from version 8.6.0 before 8.8.2.         *_Affected versions:_*   * version < 8.5.9   * 8.6.0 ≤ version < 8.8.2    *_Fixed versions:_*   * 8.5.9   * 8.8.2   * 8.9.0       """

summary: "Show username instead of user key in the audit logs when a user is added to a project role"
description: """h3. Description    When a user is added to a project role, when checking the Audit logs, this user is referred to by its """"user key"""" instead of its username:   !AuditLog_ProjectRole.png|thumbnail!     With the recent [GDPR changes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracore/gdpr-changes-in-jira-975041009.html#GDPRchangesinJira-userkeys] introduced in Jira 8.4, the user keys or newly created users have the format JIRAUSERXXXXX, which makes it impossible to know which users were added to a specific role from the Audit Logs. Instead, the Jira admin user needs to have access to the DB and query the {{app_user}} table to find the relationship between the user key and the user name.    h3. Step to reproduce    # Install Jira 8.4 or any higher version  # Create a new user  # Add this user to any project role  # Go to the page ⚙ > System > Audit Logs  # Look for the entry corresponding to the addition of the user to a project role, and verify that the user key is displayed instead of the username    h3. Suggestion    Instead of using user_key, the Audit Logs should show the username as it's more relevant to the users. Note that it's already the case when a user is added to a group (as shown in the screenshot below), so the Audit Logs should be consistent and show the username whenever some modifications is made to a user.   !AuditLog_UserManagement.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Dashboard not editable if the user does not belongs to all the groups in the Viewers field"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If a user is granted the Edit permission of a dashboard but is not in all of the groups added to the View permission, the user is not able to edit the dashboard.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create 2 groups (GroupA and GroupB)  # Create 2 users (User1 and User2)  # Add User1 into both groups  # Add User2 into GroupB only  # Grant GroupA and GroupB as _Viewers_ in a dashboard  # Grant single user User1 and User2 as _Editors_ in the same dashboard  # Login as User1 and edit the dashboard   # In another browser, login as User2 and edit the dashboard    h3. Expected Results  Both User1 and User2 are able to edit the dashboard    h3. Actual Results  Only User1 can edit the dashboard. The Edit, Share and Delete dashboard functionality is hidden from User2    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "BulkEdit Progression screen showing more than the limit of 1000 issues"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira is respecting the limit of 1000 when using BulkEdit. Running a JQL  search on the edited issue returns 1000.   !BulkEdit2.png|thumbnail!    But during the BullEdit the progression screen is wrongly  showing more than 1000 issues in this case 1438 see attached screenshot   (i) The BulkEdit functionality is not affected but it is confusing for the users    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Use BulkEdit and select 1000 issues   # Follow the BulkEdit steps   !BulkEdit1.pmg.png|thumbnail!    # The progression screen show more than 1000 issues being edited     h3. Expected Results  The BulkEdit progression screen displays  the exact number of the edited issues    h3. Actual Results  The BulkEdit progression screen display a number higher than the limit   !Bulk-Edit-progression-screen.JPG|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Man-in-the-middle in Jira email client - CVE-2020-14168"
description: """The email client in Jira Server and Data Center allows remote attackers to access outgoing emails between a Jira instance and the SMTP server via man-in-the-middle (MITM) vulnerability    *Affected versions:*   * version < 7.13.14   * 8.5.0 ≤ version < 8.5.5   * 8.8.0 ≤ version < 8.8.2   * 8.9.0 ≤ version < 8.9.1    *Fixed versions:*   * 7.13.16   * 8.5.7   * 8.8.2   * 8.9.1   * 8.10.0"""

summary: "Issue web link with underscore is not considered valid"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The underscore is a valid and unreserved character as per the [RFC 3986|http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986], section 2.3, but Jira shows an error instead.    !image-2020-06-19-16-18-16-910.png|width=525,height=242!    Ideally, Jira should accept any valid characters.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to an issue;   # Click More > Link;   # Select Web Link;   # Enter a URL containing an underscore and any Link Text;   # Click Create.    h3. Expected Results    The URL should be linked successfully.  h3. Actual Results    The error message is shown:         h3. Workaround    Utilize the IP address instead, or add a DNS entry utilizing only alphanumeric and hyphens."""

summary: "Scrum board showing move to done button when filtering by fix version"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Story with status """"done"""" and with all subtasks also """"done"""" is showing the """"move to done"""" button when the fix version is part of the filter used.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a project area SCRUM  # Create a story and a subtask.  # Define 2 releases: release1 already released, release2 unreleased  # Create a new board """"unreleased"""" for project area SCRUM using the filter:    # Set both story and subtask to release2  # Open the default board and observe the results: Story and subtask listed, no """"move to done"""" button presented.  # Open the board """"unreleased"""" and observe the results: Story and subtask listed, no """"move to done"""" button presented.  # Now, change the story to release1   # Refresh the default board and observe the results: Story and subtask listed, no """"move to done"""" button presented.  # Refresh the board """"unreleased"""" and observe the results    h3. Expected Results  Story and subtask listed, no """"move to done"""" button presented.    h3. Actual Results  Story and subtask listed but the button """"move to done"""" is presented.    h3. Workaround  * Remove the fixversion from the filter  or  * Set fixversion to a released version, thus both story and subtask will not more be presented at the board  """

summary: "Upgrade Apache Tomcat 8.5.50 - version affected by CVE-2020-9484"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The recently [disclosed vulnerability regarding Tomcat|https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2020-9484] affects the following versions:        Apache Tomcat 7x <7.0.103      Apache Tomcat 8x <8.5.54      Apache Tomcat 9x <9.0.34      Apache Tomcat 10x < 10.0.0-M4    We should bundle a more recent version of Tomcat, so that Jira is not affected by this in the future.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    * Check the CVE [report|https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2020-9484].      h3. Expected Results    * Not applicable.    h3. Actual Results    * Not applicable.    h3. Workaround    * Manually upgrade Tomcat according to our [documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-upgrade-apache-tomcat-version-used-by-jira-879957866.html]."""

summary: """Watchers"" field(or the number of watchers) is not being updated properly in jira dashboards"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    """"Watchers"""" field(or the number of watchers) is not being updated properly in Jira dashboards  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new dashboard and add a gadget """"Assigned to me"""" or """"watched issues"""".   # Now add the """"watchers"""" field and sort it based in watchers   # the dashboard should display the exact number of watchers.    h3. Expected Results    The dashboard should display the exact number of watchers.  h3. Actual Results    But the number is not being updated properly even after 15 minutes of refreshing the dashboard.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Clarify documentation for using self signed SSL certificates on Jira Server Mobile App"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The documentation for [Jira Server Mobile App|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/jira-server-mobile-app-966063511.html] mentions the following:  {quote}  If your Jira instance is configured to use SSL, you will not be able to log in on the app if:  * your certificate is self signed  {quote}   And then provides a linked KB to install the certificate on your device.  h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Review the [Jira Server Mobile App|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/jira-server-mobile-app-966063511.html] documentation.    h3. Expected Results  The documentation would be more clear -- for example:  {quote}  you will not be able to log in on the app if:  * your certificate is self signed and hasn't been installed on the device.  {quote}     h3. Actual Results  The section is not quite clear on what conditions a self signed certificate will work.    h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Update documentation to include edit permissions for shared dashboards"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In the [Managing shared dashboards|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/managing-shared-dashboards-938847879.html] documentation, it indicates that shared dashboards can only be edited by the owner:  {quote}  A shared dashboard can only be edited by the shared dashboard's owner. The owner of a shared dashboard can only modify that dashboard's shares and gadgets too.  {quote}     However, it appears we released the feature under [JRASERVER-17783|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-17783]:  {quote}  We’re glad to announce that starting with Jira Server 7.12 (released today) you can grant edit rights for filters and dashboards so that teams can keep them up-to-date without unnecessary delays.  {quote}  Can the description be updated to indicate permissions can be edited in the filter and dashboard edit screens respectively, where you can choose view or edit rights for your team members.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Review the [Managing shared dashboards|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/managing-shared-dashboards-938847879.html] documentation.    h3. Expected Results  The documentation indicates this new feature.  h3. Actual Results  The documentation does not currently outline the ability to grant edit permissions.    h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Checking users against license tier affects product performance"
description: """h3. Summary    JIRA may experience performance problems caused by a slow population of *ActiveUsersCountForLicenseCache*, see related JRASERVER-64384 .  Also, check KB for more details:  [performance problems by slow population of ActiveUsersCountForLicenseCache|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/performance-problems-by-slow-population-of-activeuserscountforlicensecache-900795144.html]"""

summary: "Mobile browser is missing certain elements from login page."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When using the mobile browser, links to dashboard are directed to <baseURL>/login.jsp. However, elements could be missing depending on the screen resolution of the mobile device (phone). This results in users unable to proceed further to view public projects (if available)    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Select a lower resolution for mobile device. Eg: 1480 x 720.  # Browse using mobile browser to dashboard page.    h3. Expected Results  * Elements such as (More -> Projects) are visible.    !2960x1440px.jpeg|thumbnail!   (i) Above screenshot is using 2960 x 1440 pixel    h3. Actual Results  * Elements such as (More -> Projects) are not visible.   !1480x720px.jpeg|thumbnail!   (i) Above screenshot is using 1480 x 720 pixel    h3. Workaround  Temporarily switch between desktop view on the browser.    h3. Others  * Tested on Samsung S9+ (Chrome and Samsung browser)."""

summary: "Documentation Fix: Description for modifying jira.search.views.max.limit setting is incorrect"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In *Administration >> System >> General Configuration >> Advanced Settings*, the *jira.search.views.max.limit* setting contains the following description:  {quote}This number sets the absolute limit on the number of issues that can be exported from search results. Users trying to request more get a 403 (Forbidden) HTTP error code. To disable, set to '-1'. After changing it, update the value of 'jira.search.views.default.max' to a value that is lower than or equal to this one.{quote}    Per [JRASERVER-67839 : Unable to disable the maximum limit of number of exported issues from GUI|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-67839], this is no longer the case as we explicitly do not allow for disabling this setting by design.    However, the description is still outdated, as attempting to set this value to -1 will instead cause the following error which immediately contradicts the description:  {quote}Value must be at least 0{quote}    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to *Administration >> System >> General Configuration >> Advanced Settings*    h3. Expected Results    The description for *jira.search.views.max.limit* does not contain the following sentence:  {quote}To disable, set to '-1'.{quote}    h3. Actual Results    The description contains an incorrect instruction.    h3. Workaround    N/A"""

summary: "Workload Pie Chart shows a warning about no work logged on issues even when the work is logged and chart is displayed."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Workload Pie Chart shows a warning about no work logged on issues even when the work is logged and chart is displayed.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a filter with some issues that do not have work logged.   # Now try adding this filter to Workload Pie Chart and try to save the filter.   # There will be a warning shown about no work logged on issues which is expected.   # Now try logging work on the issues in the filter and save the Workload Pie Chart again.   # The Workload Pie Chart is correctly displayed but the warning is still shown.    h3. Expected Results  The Workload Pie Chart should be displayed without any warning when the work is logged on issues in the filter.    h3. Actual Results  The Workload Pie Chart shows warning even when the work is logged on issues in the filter.      h3. Workaround  Click on the edit again for Workload Pie Chart gadget and save the settings which certainly refreshes the data and the warning is gone automatically."""

summary: "Deleting a custom field fails with ""No operator matches the given name and argument type"" when JIRA is connected to Postgres"

summary: "Configuring an external gadget using a local gadget URL breaks the new gadget"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Configuring an external gadget using a local gadget URL breaks the new gadget  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to a dashboard you can edit;   # Select *Add gadget* and click to load all gadgets;   # Select any *Local* gadgets and click to """"Show XML link"""" to any of them;   # Copy the XML Link;   # Click to *Manage gadgets*;   # Include the XML Link (from the same instance);   # Navigate back to the dashboard and add the external version of the gadget we created;    h3. Expected Results    The gadget works as if it was a local one;  h3. Actual Results   * Fields are not editable;   * Reording fields do not work;   * Adding fields work but removing doesn't    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Click Comment In issue view- it doesn’t scroll up automatically"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When we click on Comment button In Jira projects. It doesn’t scroll up and focus on entire comment box. However If the comment button is already at the bottom of screen, It scrolls up automatically and work as expected. We can reproduce it in almost all versions. I have attached a screen recording for better understanding.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create any issue and add comment so that comment button positions little above than extreme bottom   # Click on 'comment' screen will not scroll up to show full box.    h3. Expected Results    Screen should scroll up automatically as we click on comment button.  h3. Actual Results    h3. Workaround    As of now we can scroll up manually"""

summary: "keyboard shortcut CMD+ENTER doesn't work anymore when creating an Epic in Issues view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    keyboard shortcut CMD+ENTER doesn't work anymore when creating an Epic in Issues view   As per [Keyboard shortcut|https://jira.atlassian.com/secure/ViewKeyboardShortcuts!default.jspa] the combination CMD+ENTER should submit a description or comment.   In previous version of Jira while creating an Epic from the Project Issues view and pressing CMD+ENTER after entering the issue summary triggered the Epic creation screen.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to the project Issues section   # Clic on the bottom on *+Create* Issue   !Screenshot 2020-07-08 at 13.05.56.png|thumbnail!   # Select *Epic* as issue type and after typing the summary, press *CMD+ENTER*   !Screenshot_2020-07-08_at_13_00_16.jpg|thumbnail!   # The page refresh and nothing happen while the Epic creation screen should appear    Observe that the issue is instead created *if any other issue type* is selected.  h3. Expected Results    After a refresh the create epic screen should appear  h3. Actual Results    The page refresh and the Create Epic screen doesn't appear  h3. Workaround    Pressing the *ENTER* key instead trigger the Create Epic Screen"""

Expected Results    The Jira UI should show the results with and the area where we enter JQL should expand but it's not rendering properly.      h3. summary: "The word Issues is not translated properly in Kanban board"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The word *+Issues+* is not translated properly in Kanban board    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Change your user language to Deutsch (Deutschland) or German.    # Navigate to any of your Kanban board configurations.     h3. Expected Results    The word *+Issues+* is translated to *+Vorgänge+* like it is on the JIRA header.     h3. Actual Results    The word is not translated but instead, shows as Issues:    !Screenshot 2020-07-13 at 11.50.55 PM.png|thumbnail!    !Screenshot 2020-07-13 at 11.50.40 PM.png|thumbnail!     || English || German ||   | We're only showing recently modified issues. | Es werden nur kürzlich geänderte Issues angezeigt. |   | Hide completed issues older than | Abgeschlossene Issues ausblenden, die älter sind als |     h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Outdated error message that isn't doing its job"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Outdated error message doesn't tell me what happened or state the consequences of the proposed solution.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Was adding a comment to https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/CONFCLOUD-65975; left the tab unattended for a few hours.   # Finished my comment, and selected Add.   # Got this message.    !image (52).png!  h3. Expected Results    Expected the error message to explain what happened (apparent browser timeout), why I'm not authorised (It's because I'm not signed in?), what would happen to the comment I was writing if I refreshed (refreshing the page wipes out the comment you wrote).  h3. Actual Results    A message that doesn't tell me what happened or the consequences of the proposed solution.    h3. Workaround    There is no work around."""

summary: "Unclear Captcha images on login page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  On a server instance login page, some users may be presented with unclear CAPTCHA's that are unclear/impossible to decipher.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Refresh the captcha box/reload the login page    h3. Expected Results  CAPTCHA code is clear enough for a human to read    h3. Actual Results  Some codes can result in letters with significant overlap, delaying the login process    h3. Workaround    Refreshing the CAPTCHA box"""

summary: "Searching for groups with special characters ""#"" or ""&"" on ""Add users to a role"" makes search stop advancing after typing the first special character"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In case of looking for groups with the special characters *&* or *#* on name by using the """"Add users to a role"""" feature, it stops working at the first special character that is typed.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a group with the special characters *&* or *#* (E.g.: """"#TestingGroup 2020"""");   # Try to add that group into your project role by typing """"#TestingGroup"""".    h3. Expected Results  JIRA shows the specific JIRA group;    h3. Actual Results  JIRA shows every group with """"#"""" on its name.   !image-2020-07-15-17-11-58-162.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Upgrading JSW 7.13.4 to JSW 8.11 RC01 displays error that JIRA failed to start on Windows"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After the upgrade on windows from JSW 7.13.4 to JSW 8.11 RC01, the """"Jira failed to start"""" error appears on the screen. However no negative impact to the instance is recognised.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    Using instenv:   # Deploy instenv Windows box with postrgreSQL rds, no application installed   # Once deployed, install JIRA 7.13 connect to PostgreSQL if needed but I think h2 should be good enough   # Modify xmx value to 2048mb cause it's the minimum recommended value for 8.x Jira   # Upgrade to JIRA 8.11, start Jira   # As soon JIRA starts 'starting up' after the upgrade, open JIRA in the browser and monitor the progress.    h3. Expected Results    Jira starts up normally after upgrade (with a log in screen).  h3. Actual Results    """"Jira has failed to start"""" screen appears after the upgrade.    !_thumb_438800.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available    *Note:*    After clicking """"Take me to my Jira home"""", the instance works properly."""

summary: "Issue with strikethrough and underline markdown"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When doing a strikethrough/underline in an Issue description or Comment, the formatting is not shown in the activity stream of the issue  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a description/comment to an issue with strikethrough/underline    h3. Expected Results    The formatting will be visible in the Activity stream.  h3. Actual Results    The formatting is not visible in the Activity stream. This happens for Strikethrough as well as Underlining.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Upgrade the bundled version of Apache Tomcat to 8.5.57"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The recently disclosed vulnerability regarding Apache Tomcat    * [CVE-2020-13934|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2020-13934]    affects the following versions:              Apache Tomcat 8.x from 8.5.1 to 8.5.56      Apache Tomcat 9.x from 9.0.0.M5 to 9.0.36      Apache Tomcat 10.x from 10.0.0-M1 to 10.0.0-M6    Additionally, the following disclosed vulnerability regarding Tomcat:    * [CVE-2020-13935|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2020-13935]    affects the following versions:        Apache Tomcat 7.x from 7.0.27 to 7.0.104      Apache Tomcat 8.x from 8.5.1 to 8.5.56      Apache Tomcat 9.x from 9.0.0.M5 to 9.0.36      Apache Tomcat 10.x from 10.0.0-M1 to 10.0.0-M6    We should bundle a more recent version of Tomcat so that Jira is not affected by this in the future.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    * Check the CVE reports:  ** [CVE-2020-13934|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2020-13934]  ** [CVE-2020-13935|https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2020-13935]    h3. Expected Results    * Not applicable.    h3. Actual Results    * Not applicable.    h3. Workaround    * Manually upgrade Tomcat according to our [documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-upgrade-apache-tomcat-version-used-by-jira-879957866.html]."""

summary: "Top menu dropdown does not scroll down to show all options  if the screen is small."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If you have many options in the """"Issue"""" dropdown(in top menu), and if you have a small screen, some options are out of screen. However, you cannot scroll down to see these options.         It is a similar issue to JRASERVER-69709: More button dropdown not shown properly if the screen is small. (fixed in Jira Server and Data Center 8.6.0), but happened in different places.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Step 1   Change resolution of display to a small screen mode.   # Step 2   Click Issue dropdown from top menu, some options are out of screen.    h3. Expected Results    scroll down works to show the options out of screen.  h3. Actual Results    scroll down does not work well, the options out of screen cannot be seen.  h3. Workaround    No."""

summary: "Batched email notifications format looks different when default language is set to French,German in v8.10"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When the default language in the instance is set as French or German, the batched email notifications looks different. The bug is not reproducible in older versions.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set the default language in the instance as French (or German)   # Set up outgoing email in your instance.   # Enable batched notifications.   # Make some updates in an issue which triggers batched notification.    h3. Expected Results    The notification email should like 'There are 2 Updates' followed by the update  h3. Actual Results    The email looks like 'There is' as in the below screenshot.   !Screenshot 2020-07-21 at 6.58.24 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Download and install Jira language pack with updated/fixed translation keys.    Basically download language pack for Jira (in 8.14.0.v20200826125100 version - translations should be backwards compatible so 8.14 should work on lower versions) and install it via UPM.    Basically [https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/install-a-custom-language-pack-for-jira-server-295305642.html] but without unpacking and updating translations. The lang pack to download should be named as """"jira-core-language-pack-[lang]/8.14.0.v20200826125100/""""    Eg. to fix German language download proper """"jira-core-language-pack"""" jar from [https://packages.atlassian.com/maven/com/atlassian/translations/] eg. [https://packages.atlassian.com/maven/com/atlassian/translations/jira-core-language-pack-de_DE/8.14.0.v20200826125100/] and install via UPM.  h3. Note    Not reproducible in older Jira versions like 8.8.1, 8.7.1 or 8.4.2"""

summary: "Upgrading Jira 8 between minor versions will always indicate that setenv.sh has customizations that are not applied"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When upgrading Jira 8 between minor versions (8.6 > 8.8 for example, or 8.10 > 8.11 as another upgrade example), after the upgrade is complete, the admin will see a warning page that explains that some configuration/settings files that contained customizations could not be copied over. This is expected for any startup/installation file where the admin might have made changes. However in this case, the [$JIRAINSTALL|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/jira-application-installation-directory-938847745.html]/bin/setenv.sh file will always appear on this list, even when the admin has made no changes at all to that file.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # install Jira Software/Core 8.10.0 (other 8 versions likely affected)   # Complete the setup and make sure Jira works   # Make no changes to setenv.sh   # upgrade that existing installation directory to a newer version, such as 8.11.0   # start Jira after upgrade    h3. Expected Results    Expecting that there are no actual changes to that setenv.sh file and the initial startup would not indicate there are when you first start Jira after the upgrade.  h3. Actual Results    Admins will always see that the setenv.sh file as having had customized changes to it that have not been applied to the upgraded install    h3. Workaround    The warning page does not prevent upgrades to Jira in this case. This is just a warning message that appears to be a false positive in most cases. You can still dismiss this and complete the upgrade."""

summary: "There is no permission setting to block editing Time Tracking fields in bulk edit"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    There is no permission settings that blocks editing of Time Tracking fields in bulk edit, although all other fields are blocked.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create a user with only browse projects permission.  # Do a bulk edit of any issue.    h3. Expected Results  Only the Comment field is editable.  h3. Actual Results  The Time Tracking fields (Original Estimate and Remaining Estimate) are available to edit, and values of those fields can be changed on the issue.    h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Two dimensional filter statistics gadget does not show empty values"
description: """h3. Summary    Two dimensional filter statistics gadget's behavior has changed again in Jira 8.5 since it was fixed in Jira 8.0 JRASERVER_66318. If any issues have None value for some custom field, these tickets aren't counted in the gadget and there are no empty values (None) entries shown in the gadget (at least for multi-select-list custom fields).  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create custom field with multiple choices (eg. custom field named Browsers, can have internet browser as options - IE, Edge, Firefox, Chrome etc.)   # Create a simple filter like """"project = TEST and issuetype = Bug""""   # Add Two dimensional filter statistics gadget to your dashboard specifying the filter from Step 2   # Set XAxis = Issue Type   # Set YAxis = some multi-select-list custom fields (created in Step 1), e.g. Browser(s) field that has Internet browsers as options; Sort By, Sort Direction, Number of Results and Auto refresh settings - seems it does not matter what you put there   # Save the filter.    h3. Expected Results    Newly created gadget should show complete breakdown, including number that counts issues having None as a YAxis custom field's value   h3. Actual Results    Newly created gadget doesn't include issues having None as a YAxis custom field's value. There is no row which displays None as a field value followed by issue count (screenshot attached).       h3. Workaround    No"""

summary: "Calendar popup not working in Estonian and Icelandic"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When Jira is set to Estonian or Icelandic, the calendar popup used to select dates in the create/edit issue views does not work  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set Jira to one of the two languages   # Create a new issue and try to set its due date via the calendar popup    Note: the bug was found on 8.12.0, but probably affects previous versions as well.  h3. Expected Results    The calendar popup appears, allowing you to pick a date which is accepted when the form is submitted.  h3. Actual Results    No popup appears, the browser notifies the user that """"The JIRA Calendar does not currently support your language""""  h3. Workaround    Type date in manually"""

summary: "Some Jira Server mobile app's declared device permissions are not applicable"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Some device permissions declared by the Jira Server mobile app on Android and iOS are not applicable with current app functionality.  This was discovered through recent conversations with the Server Mobile development team and end users.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # View device permissions on your device (see [https://www.wired.com/story/how-to-check-app-permissions-ios-android-macos-windows] for instructions)    h3. Expected Results    Device permissions for each app on its respective device platform should only display permissions that are applicable / necessary for the app to operate.  h3. Actual Results   * iOS app does not need """"NSSiriUsageDescription"""" – permissions should not be listed    Please review other permissions to confirm if they are also required to grant access to certain app functionality.  h3. Workaround    N/A"""

summary: "Batched notifications job for the mentions is executed not frequently enough"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The recent changes in the batched notifications contain bug that prevents the emails containing mentions to be sent immediately.   This may also create a performance problems on bigger instances.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set longer interval, e.g. 10 minutes on /secure/admin/ConfigureBatching!default.jspa   # @mention somebody    h3. Expected Results  The batched email containing mentions should be sent almost immediately (approx 1 minute).  h3. Actual Results  The mention mail is sent after at most the configured interval period (at most 10 minutes).    Debug mail logging may be enabled to trace the batching mechanism in atlassian-jira-outgoing-mail.log  h3. Workaround  Set smaller interval. """

summary: "User name appears in Change password URL"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Personal information shared in GET request : user name appearing in the GET request  {{http://localhost:8854/j854/secure/ChangePassword!default.jspa?username=USERNAME&inline=true&decorator=dialog&_=1595499363660}}    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Login with Jira  # Navigate to user profile  # Change password  The same user name is not appeared while performing Forget password from login screen    h3. Expected Results  Username should not appear in the URL. Rather user name should be passed as a POST function rather than GET request.     h3. Actual Results  Username appears in the URL and violets the GDPR compliance.     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Search Filters with a wildcard(*) and upper case letter does not work"
description: """h3. Summary  Search Filters with a wildcard(*) and upper case letters do not work.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create 2 filters (JQL conditions in the filter do not matter):  ## with the name: XYZ_123 Test (important: Upper case for XYZ)  ## with the name XYZ-123 Test (important: Upper case for XYZ)  # Go to search filters and search by XYZ*.     h3. Expected Behaviour  Filter search with XYZ* should work and return both filters.    h3. Actual Result  Filter search with XYZ* is not giving any results though the filters exist.   !Screenshot 2020-07-31 at 5.47.31 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes:  * This works as expected in Jira 7.13.11   !Screenshot 2020-07-31 at 5.49.09 PM.png|thumbnail!         """

summary: "Jira upgrade landing page will throw an Application Link error if there is a non Atlassian Application Link"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After an upgrade Jira updates the Upgrade Report page:   <base-url>/secure/PostUpgradeLandingPage.jspa    If there is any non-Atlassian application link, this page will point out an application link error:   !image-2020-07-31-17-59-51-991.png|width=399,height=208!    There is no actual connection error or failure in the Application Link health check. This seems to be a false positive as this page cannot account for the status of non-Atlassian application links.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up any non-Atlassian application link   # [Upgrade Jira|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/upgrading-jira-installer-938846937.html]   # Go to Administration - Latest upgrade Report   # Click on Application links failing: 1 > Show Details   # Test all Applications links to make sure they're working by navigating through the links   # If all links are working and the Upgrade report error persists - Remove the Non-Atlassian application link as a test (Make sure to take note of all credentials and link before deleting)   # Go to Administration - Latest upgrade Report again   # The error should be cleared out   # Add the Non-Atlassian application link back    h3. Expected Results    If all applications links are healthy, do not show an error  h3. Actual Results    An error is thrown even though all application links are connected  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Changing dashboard layout truncates recent week's data in Created vs. Resolved gadget  "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In certain situations, when *Created vs Resolved Chart* gadget is included in narrower dashboard layouts or monitor/browser sizes are reduced, data points for last week """"Created Issues"""" show the week before rather than the present week.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a *Created vs Resolved Chart* in Jira Dashboard, with the following configuration:   ** Saved filter containing multiple issues (both created and resolved in the past few months).   ** Period: weekly.   ** Days Previously: 300.   ** Collection Operation: Cumulative.   ** Display the Trend of Unresolved: Yes.   ** Display Versions: None.   ** Auto Refresh box: Checked.     # Change dashboard layout to either a 3 columns or a 50% and narrower layout column layouts.   ** This behavior is also experienced when using the full-width layout, both when changing the scale of the monitor screen to a smaller one or when the browser window is not maximized.    h3. Expected Results   * Users should be able to see present week data when hovering the mouse over the last data points for *Created Issues*.    h3. Actual Results   * Instead, users see the week before data for *Created Issues*.   ** Attached screenshot shows a test conducted on the week of 25th July.   ** When clicking in the last data point, dates and issue counts are correct. It's only when hovering the mouse over where the behavior appears.    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Application access page not accessible if the number of groups exceeds 1500"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If there are huge number of groups (more than 1500) added to the """"Application access"""" permission, the page *Application Access* becomes unresponsive  h3. Steps to Reproduce    *Pre-requisite:* There should be more than 1500 groups in the Jira instance   # Add  1500 groups to the """"Jira Software"""" Application Access   # Click on *Cog Icon* > Applications > Application Access   # The page will keep on loading and will give multiple errors (Attached herewith)    There are no traces of error in the logs related to the behavior    Heap size : 2GB  h3. Expected Results    The page should load without any errors  h3. Actual Results    The page never loads due to which new projects cannot be configured and application access can't be granted  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Radio Buttons do not render when accessing Jira via IE11"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Radio Button custom field options are not showing when accessing Jira via Internet Explorer 11. This issue only seems to occur when embedded search JQL is in the URL. Such as:  http://example.domain.com:8080/browse/TEST-123?jql=project%20%3D%20TEST    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Radio Button custom field and add it to relevant screens.  # Go to the Issue Navigator and switch to the list view of issues.  # When accessing any issues from the list, note that the JQL parameter is still on the URL (expected behavior).  # Try to edit the value of the field (either in-line or via the screen) and see that the radio buttons are not selectable.    (i) The issue is reproducible on _Internet Explorer only_, which is no longer supported as of 8.6.x.    h3. Expected Results  The field values are selectable and the user is able to edit it.    h3. Actual Results  The radio buttons do not show up. There are no exceptions on the browser console nor on a HAR file.    h3. Workaround  Remove the JQL parameters on the URL and reload the page."""

summary: "Keyboard shortcut for submitting a comment does not work correctly"
description: """-Similar to [JRASERVER-63438|https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-63438]-  h1. Problem    The 'Share with Customer' button in the form to enter a comment has a keyboard shortcut for submitting the form, which on Mac+Chrome is *Ctrl+Alt+e*. With the new Visual mode editor for comments, the keyboard shortcut does nothing.    Note: -Tested on Mac using Chrome.-   Customer's Note: -This key combination does not work in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge for Mac, and we have also found it not to work in Chrome for Windows (not sure about others).-  h1. Steps to reproduce   # Create/view an issue, press the Comment button   # Enter a comment and use the keyboard shortcut to submit the form while having the Visual tab selected.    h2. Expected    A comment is added as a result of step one and another comment is added as a result of step 2.  h2. Actual    Only using the Comment button works. The shortcut does not seem to work at all (neither Alt+e for Internal comment nor Alt+s for regular comment in SD projects)  h4. Some other tests made to check:    *Visual Mode*   * CTRL+ALT+E (x)   * COMMAND+ALT+E (x)   * CTRL+ALT+S (/)    *Text Mode*   * CTRL+ALT+E (x)   * CTRL+ALT+S (/)    Notes: The above 'Visual' and 'Text' mode results were tested with Jira Software 8.5.4 and 8.11.0. The customer who noticed this problem could use Ctrl-Alt-e if he switches to """"text"""" mode instead of """"Visual""""."""

summary: "Jira cannot differentiate when the two attachments with the same name are inserted in two separate comments."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira cannot differentiate when the two attachments with the same name are inserted in two separate comments using the browse option in WYSIWYG editor    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Create sample JSW project  Create a JSW issue  update the issue comment with image B (image.png)  update a new comment with image C (image.png)    h3. Expected Results    Jira should be able to differentiate between two different attachments with same name when they are added in different comments    h3. Actual Results  Jira is unable differentiate between the attachments.  When the attachment is an image and another image with the same name is attached, the previous comments with inline image/attachments will be pointed to the latest updated image (including the thumbnails)    h3. Workaround    After adding the attachment in the comment and then saving it.You need to update the reference of the attachment in the comment.  """

summary: "When a node is recovering an index from snapshot it does not return 503"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a node recovers an index from a snapshot it does not return {{503 Maintenance}} as it does when you do a full reindex, instead it returns a 200 and it still allows user traffic.     This means that users can access a node without a working index, which results in incidents.    Jira should block access to a node that does not have a working index.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Perform and index recovery on a node  # Confirm you can still access that node while recovery is underway    h3. Expected Results  A node should block while it does not have a working index, the same as it does it you perform a full reindex.    h3. Actual Results  The node is available and users can access it, but without an index they cannot view issues, reports, etc.    h3. Workaround  # Manually remove the node from your load balancer while it is recovering the index  # Watch the logs for progress  # Once complete, manually re-add the node into the load balancer    NB: This affects all versions of Jira"""

summary: "collation error at startup when using Postgresql in Azure templates "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When spinning up Jira for the first time utilizing the """"quick start"""" templates in Azure the collation is set to English_United States:1252 this causes Jira to failed the startup collation test erroneously. This is the correct collation for Windows Postgres servers.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Spin up a new Jira DC instance in the Azure portal using these [instructions|https://confluence.atlassian.com/enterprise/running-jira-on-an-azure-cluster-969535564.html]   # Attempt to start JIRA    h3. Expected Results  Jira health check does not give the false warning when using .1252 collations    h3. Actual Results  The collation check fails on startup   !image.png|thumbnail!   This is a false warning and can be ignored, provided the client encoding is set correctly.    h3. Suggested Solution  We should update the health check to accept the Windows equivalent of UTF-8.    h3. Notes  According to [Postgres|https://www.postgresql.org/docs/9.3/static/multibyte.html]:  {quote}UTF-8 encoding can be used with any locale.  {quote}  In addition, we've reviewed this [blog post|http://www.g-loaded.eu/2011/02/27/locale-windows/], Locale in Windows, and it appears that you should be able to use English_United States.1252.    h3. Workaround  You can click ignore and continue for now, until the health check is fixed."""

summary: "Adding 'Single Email Address' to the notification scheme stops 'Batching email notifications' for that specific event"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Adding 'Single Email Address' to the notification scheme (any event) stops 'Batching email notifications' for that specific event  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Enable ⚙️> System > Batching email notifications   # Configure the _Browse Projects_ [Permission Scheme|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/managing-project-permissions-776636362.html] of a project to _Group (Anyone, including non-registered and anonymous users)_.   # Set one of the events (i.e. *Issue Created*) of the [Notification Scheme|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/creating-a-notification-scheme-776636401.html] of that same project to _Single Email Address_ and enter a valid one. Save the changes.   # Create a new ticket   # None of the email recipients (reporter, groups, watchers, etc.) receive the batched email   # Only the Single Email Address gets an email with a non-batched notification template   # Other events (like Issue updated) works as expected with batched template    h3. Expected Results    All the recipients should receive batched email notifications including the event where Single Email Address is added  h3. Actual Results    All the recipients are not receiving batched email notifications for the event where is added Single Email Address    Only the Single Email Address gets an email with a non-batched notification template  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Accessing a non-ascii attachment prints incorrect data due to lack of correct encoding"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Accessing a non-ascii attachement prints incorrect data due to lack of correct encoding  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Attach a file containing unicode characters   # Access the issue and open the attachment in a new browser window    h3. Expected Results    The attachment is printed correctly  h3. Actual Results    The printed content is incorrect and the whole file shows as garbage data    The below warning shows when using FireFox as the browser:    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Struts 2 CVE-2019-0230 and CVE-2019-0233 impact on Jira "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Recently, Apache released the following report regarding two different vulnerabilities in Struts 2:    [|https://struts.apache.org/announce.html#a20200813]    Is Jira affected by these CVEs?    Jira is not affected.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   Not applicable.    h3. Expected Results   Not applicable    h3. Actual Results   Not applicable    h3. Workaround   Not applicable  """

summary: "Enabling the debugging package com.atlassian.jira.security (or any sub-package) might cause JIRA to stall"
description: """h3. Summary    Enabling {{com.atlassian.jira.security}} (at [ *Administration* > *System* > *Logging & Profiling* ] ) might cause JIRA to stall.    Incoming thread will be fighting for {{org.apache.log4j.Logger}} lock in {{workflowPermissionCheck}}    Those logs will be written into {{atlassian-jira.log}} file in <JIRA_HOME>/logs/ folder.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to ⚙ > System > Log and profiling   ## Click on """"Configure logging level for another package.""""   ## Use """"com.atlassian.jira.security"""" as the package name, and select """"DEBUG"""" for the """"Logging Level""""   # Generate a high load of activity in Jira related to issues (create issues, edit issue, etc...)    h3. Expected Results    Jira works fine    h3. Actual Results    Jira might eventually become unresponsive, as almost all incoming http threads are *blocked waiting* to obtain the logger lock to log debugging information into the file atlassian-jira.log    h5. Thread dump details    Looking into thread dumps, we can see that the same exact pattern keeps repeating:   - more than 140 threads are waiting for the same lock and try to write into the Jira log files:     - while at the same time 1 thread is holding the lock, and also trying to write into the log files     - logs will be flooded with this message (thousand times per second)      h3. Workaround    Disable the debugging package {{com.atlassian.jira.security}} (or any sub-debugging packages of it (such as {{com.atlassian.jira.security.DefaultPermissionManager}} or {{com.atlassian.jira.security.WorkflowBasedPermissionManager}})      h3. Note    This type of issue is more likely to occur if Jira is writing log files on a slow hard disk, so one way to mitigate this issue would be to change the location of Jira's home folder (where the Jira application logs are stored) to a faster disk."""

Workaround    * Manually upgrade Tomcat using the process described on: [How to upgrade Apache Tomcat version|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-upgrade-apache-tomcat-version-in-jira-7-x-879957866.html]."""

summary: "Sync on Login does not work with Nested Groups for existing users from a delegated directory"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  From Jira 8.11 forward, Sync on Login feature from a delegated LDAP directory isn't working properly when having nested groups. It works perfectly on previous versions.    On Jira 8.11 there was an important upgrade for the Embedded Crowd from version 2.0 to 4.0, adding a lot of new features and fixing bugs as per [the release notes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/jira-software-8-11-x-release-notes-1013852753.html#JiraSoftware8.11.xreleasenotes-crowd].    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Have a delegated LDAP directory with Nested Groups enabled and configured. Jira will be filtering for a group, and within that group other groups where users will be.   # This group will have {{Sync on Login}} enabled as well so user's membership is update upon their login.  # On version 8.11, remove {{Group1}} from {{User1}} , and add another recently created {{Group2}} to his account.    h3. Expected Results    The user will login and his membership will be updated accordingly. Messages in the logs will be as below:        h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:      (!) The above happens for every existing user account membership update upon login and the membership *is not updated* properly, causing critical issues to customers who relies on this configuration.  (!)(!) For newly created accounts, the error above appears in the logs still, but followed by a successful message afterwards and the membership is updated correctly.  (!)(!)(!) The exact same setup ad configuration works on previous versions as 8.9 or 8.10 for example.    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior."""

summary: "Issue Statistics Gadget fails with ""an option provided by the function cascadeOption for the field xxx does not exist"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When selecting any option from the """"Issue Statistics"""" gadget, the JQL search fails with the error message *""""an option provided by the function cascadeOption for the field xxx does not exist""""* when there is a Configuration Context created without any project selected.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1. Create 2 projects """"ProjectA"""" and """"ProjectB""""   2. Create a Custom Field """"My Cascade"""" of type Select List (cascading)   3. Add these fields to the screens for """"ProjectA"""" and """"ProjectB""""   4. Add some options to the Cascade fields, for instance:  |aaa|111|  | |222|  |bbb|111|  | |222|    5. Create a filter """"AllProjectA"""" with the JQL project = """"ProjectA""""   6. Add some issues on """"ProjectA"""", selecting values for the """"My Cascade"""" custom field   7. Create a new dashboard, adding the Gadget """"Issue Statistics""""   8. Edit the gadget configuration, selecting the filter """"AllProjectA"""" and at the Statistic Type, the field """"My Cascade""""   9. Open the Dashboard to see the Gadget. When selecting any Cascade Value, another window is opened with the JQL search filled with the option selected, like    !Screen Shot 2020-08-26 at 16.17.58.png|width=685,height=189!    !Screen Shot 2020-08-26 at 17.04.37.png|width=902,height=198!    10. Return to the Cog Admin > Issues > Custom Fields and at the """"My Cascade"""" field, select """"Configure""""   11. Click at the """"Add a new Context""""  and  define a new Context for """"any issue type"""", """"ProjectB""""    !Screen Shot 2020-08-26 at 16.21.24.png|width=660,height=434!   12. Configure some values  |aaa|111|  |bbb|111|    13. Add an issue on """"ProjectB"""", filling the """"My Cascade"""" fields   14. Remove the project """"ProjectB"""" at the Admin> Projects - Actions """"Delete""""   15. Open the Dashboard to see the Gadget   16. Click one of the options  h3. Expected Results    Open another window with the JQL search filled with the option selected and the list of issues returned by the search:    h3. Actual Results   * Open another window with the JQL search filled with the option selected but without executing the JQL search and with the error message """"An option provided by the function 'cascadeOption' for the field 'My Cascade' does not exist"""":      !Screen Shot 2020-08-26 at 16.58.34.png|width=853,height=298!     * Custom Field """"My Cascade"""" still has the second configuration created, but now without any project selected at the Context   !Screen Shot 2020-08-26 at 16.57.23.png|width=726,height=423!       h3. Workaround     * Select any other Project Area for the Context   !Screen Shot 2020-08-26 at 17.00.26.png|width=543,height=445!"""

summary: "Issue Navigator's List View does not properly contain large amounts of columns"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Issue Navigator's List View does not properly contain large amounts of columns. In some JIRA versions you need to horizontally scroll the whole page instead of the columns themselves.   Issue was observed in JIRA 8.5.X family. Versions 8.5.0 - 8.5.3 work correctly. Versions 8.5.4 - 8.5.7 are affected. Additionally, version 8.11.0 was tested and it is working correctly.   * JIRA 8.5.1   ** !jira_8.5.1.png|width=809,height=330!   * JIRA 8.5.5   ** !jira_8.5.5.png|width=789,height=330!   * JIRA 8.5.7   ** !jira_8.5.7.png|width=778,height=330!   * JIRA 8.11.0   ** !jira_8.11.0.png|width=783,height=330!    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to the Issue Navigator   # Change the view to """"List View""""   # Add so many columns, that they don't all fit on the page   # Try to horizontally scroll to see the last columns    h3. Expected Results    You horizontally scroll the columns.  h3. Actual Results    There is no horizontal scroll bar in the results window, so you need to horizontally scroll the whole page.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Creating a project with shared configuration is not allowed for Jira admins and System admin if they are not part of the existing project"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira admins and System admins not able to create a project with shared configuration if they do not have Administrator or Browse permissions in the project whose configuration they would like to use to create a new project. This was working fine till 8.5.4 but from 8.5.5 this issue is seen. There is a related bug JRASERVER-70926 which was fixed in 8.5.5 which seems to be affecting this behavior.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a user with System administrator rights or Jira Administrator rights in Jira 8.5.5   # Go to Projects>Try to create a new project with a shared configuration of an existing project where the Jira Admins/System Admins do not have project-specific permissions.    h3. Expected Results    Jira Admins/System Admins should be able to view all projects in the lists to create a project with shared configuration and ultimately they should be successfully able to create a new project using the configuration of an existing project  h3. Actual Results    Jira Admins/System Admins are not able to view the list of projects existing in Jira if they are not part of the project.  h3. Workaround   * Go to the specific *project settings>permissions provide """"Browse permissions""""* to the Jira admins/SysAdmins so that they can only view the Projects in the list but cannot do administrative changes in the Project.   * Then Jira Admins/System Admins can view the list of projects in the shared configuration drop-down and they can create the projects with shared configuration.    """

summary: "When attaching a file with the same name in a comment, Jira renames the file, but not the reference to it"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  There is a mechanism within Jira Server that when you upload an attachment, if that issue already has another file attached to it by the exact same name, Jira will rename that new file.  If abc.txt already exists, then second such file by that name Jira renames to abc-1.txt as it is accepted from the browser and before attachment is complete.   The problem here is that when doing this when adding a new comment, the mark down does see in the new unsaved comment does not reference the correct file that the user selected, but rather the original file on the issue.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Attach a file to a Jira issue, example filename such as abc.txt  # Make some change to the file in question (abc.txt), but then save it locally with the same filename  # On the same Jira issue, now create a comment, In that comment, use the toolbar to click the paperclip icon to attach a file, but instead of choosing the existing attachment, choose Browse and then on your local system select the new file by the same name  # Note that Jira will rename the newly attached file with the postfix of -x (where x is the number of other attachments by the same name plus one).  For example, abc-1.txt    h3. Expected Results  We expect that the still unsaved comment will be referencing abc-1.txt, because that is the same logical file that the end user selected      h3. Actual Results  Instead, the unsaved comment is making reference back to the first attachment (abc.txt).  This in turn is very unexpected to the end user that may have never even looked to see what that original file was.  Since Jira is renaming the attachment automatically, then it should also automatically update the selected file reference before the user saves the comment.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Really long descriptions are not saved if using the Visual Tab"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If a really long description (>20 thousand characters) is added at the Visual tab of the field, hitting save won't save the content edited or added properly. Doing the same through the Text tab will work as expected.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a ticket   # Edit the description field through the Visual tab   # Add >20 thousand characters    h3. Expected Results    Hitting save will save the content edited into the field    h3. Actual Results    The content is not saved as expected.    h3. Workaround    Switch to the Text tab and hit save, this will save the content edited to the field as expected."""

summary: "JQL search for 2 wildcards(*) characters not working"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JQL search for 2 wildcards (\*) characters not working. it's throwing a warning saying {{We only allow 1 wildcard per value. Limit the number of wildcards in '""""*2020.03.00*""""' to run the query.}}  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create issues with affected versions (Example:{{*2020.03.00,{{2020.03.0001,*}}*{{2020.03.002}}{{}}}}*)   # Try to do Advanced search using JQL {{affectedVersion ~ """"*2020.03.00*""""}}    We tested it in our local instance and here are our findings:   * It works fine in the version 8.9.1   !Screenshot 2020-08-31 at 8.41.11 PM.png|thumbnail!    * It also works fine in the version 8.11.0   !Screenshot 2020-08-31 at 8.41.20 PM.png|thumbnail!    * However, it's throwing an error while trying the same JQL in Jira version 8.11.1.   !Screenshot 2020-09-01 at 8.21.16 AM.png|thumbnail!    * This bug is reproducible in Jira version 8.12 as well   !Screenshot 2020-09-01 at 8.30.18 AM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results    It should return issues  h3. Actual Results  it's throwing a warning {{We only allow 1 wildcard per value. Limit the number of wildcards in '""""2020.03.00""""' to run the query.}}  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Dashboard Editors permission not honored."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    User added to the. dashboard Editors list is not able to edit the dashboard.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1- Logging to Jira as administrator   2- On the user administration create a user """"user1"""" and two user groups : """"usergrroupA"""" and """"usergroupB""""   3- Add the """"user1"""" to the """"usergroupA"""" and NOT to the """"usergroupB""""   4- Jira administrator should be member of both user groups : """"usergroupA"""". And """"usergroupB""""   5- As Jira administrator create a dashboard with the following Configuration   - Viewers = """"Shared with Anyone on the Web""""   - Editors = User1   Check that the user1 is able to edit the dashboard.    6- Edit the dashboard and add the """"usergroupB"""" to """"Editors"""".   Check that the """"user1"""" is not able to edit the dashboard even though he is directly listed in the """"Editors"""" list   7- Add the user group """"usergroupA"""" to the """"Editor"""" list .   Check that the user1 is still not able to edit the dashboard even he is added directly to """"Editors"""" and he is member of the group """"usergroupA"""" ( and not member of """"usergroupB"""")  h3. Expected Results     A user in the dashboard list or in any user groups in that list should be able to edit the dashboard.    h3. Actual Results    User not able to edit dashboard      h3. Workaround    To be able to Edit a dashboard one of the following Conditions need to be met:  - Add the user to the Editors  list and no user groups are added  - The user should be member of all user groups added  to the Editors list."""

description: """h3. Issue Summary    XML export file is empty when filter results are exported in Chrome or Edge. It works fine in Mozilla.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1. Open Issue navigator and search for issues with any query.  2. Click on export and select XML  3. once XML is opened in other tab, right click and Save as.  4. open the saved XML file. Find the attached file for reference  5. The content in the file is different from what it is saved after opening.    h3. Expected Results  It should save the XML in the way it shows in the browser tab.    h3. Actual Results  The XML doesn't contain data. Even the headers are different to the one exported by Firefox:       h3. Notes  * The bug doesn't occur with Firefox.   * The bug doesn't occur with Jira 7.13.x.     h3. summary: "Parent link greyed out while searching for issues using Basic search"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  *Parent link* field not available in *Basic search*.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to Search for issues  # Click on the Basic search  # Click on *More* drop-down   # Search for *Parent Link*    h3. summary: "Batched Notification emails have an empty content when triggered by a custom event using the Issue deleted template"
description: """h3. Summary    Batched Notification emails have an empty content when triggered by a custom event using the *Issue deleted* template.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   - Go to *⚙ > System > Batching email notifications* and enable batched notifications   - Go to *⚙ > System > Events*   -- Create a custom event using the *Issue Deleted* event as a template   - Edit any workflow as follows:   -- Click on the transition triggered when an issue is updated   -- Click on the *Post functions* link from this transition   -- Change the event fired in the post functions to the custom event   -- Publish the new workflow   - Open a new ticket using this workflow   - Wait for the batched notifications to be received   - Check the content of the batched notification email    h3. Expected Results    The batched notification contains:   - an sentence saying """"This issue has been DELETED""""   - The """"deleted"""" section is present   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    The batched notification is almost empty. It contains the issue key and summary and a link to it, but it's missing the rest of the information:  h3. Workaround   - disable the batched notifications feature"""
