summary: "Remote JIRA Issue Link should update the issue key and URL when remote JIRA issue is moved"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Remote JIRA Issue Link should update the issue key and URL when remote JIRA issue is moved    h3. Steps to Reproduce  1. Create project A1 and A2 in Jira Server A  2. Create issue A1-1 in Jira Server A  3. Create project B1 in Jira Cloud B  4. Create issue B1-1 in Jira Cloud B  5. Create a remote link to link A1-1 with B1-1   6. Now, in Jira Server A, issue A1-1 is moved to project A2, which becomes issue A2-1.    h3. Expected Results  We expected that B1-1 should now point to A2-1 instead of A1-1.    h3. Actual Results  We can see clearly that at step 6, Jira Server does not make the call to the cloud one to update the link.    h3. Workaround  none."""

summary: "Ordering issue is not working in Next-Gen backlog"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When to reorder issues it returns back to the original place    h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Try to reorder an issue in the Next-Gen board.    h3. Expected Results    The issue is added to the Sprint or is placed in the desired position in the Backlog.    h3. Actual Results    The issue back to original place.    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Actions button in Backlog view doesn't open if it contains more options"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian Update - 02 Apr 2020|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#3C78B5|titleBGColor=#3C78B5|titleColor=#FFFFFF|bgColor=#E7F4FA}    After some analysis we've found that this issue has been duplicated by the issue at [(JSWCLOUD-18806) Actions button doesn't show the list when the menu has a lot of options|https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/JSWCLOUD-18806]. This is a current and verified bug in Jira Software, please watch that issue for further updates and more detailed workarounds.    {panel}    h3. Issue Summary    Actions button in Backlog view doesn't open if it contains more options in new issue view. The more add-ons Cloud instance has, it would populate more options in *Actions* button in Backlog view and Jira can't open it.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Installed a few add-ons which populate more options in _Actions_ button   # Create some issues in a board   # Open Backlog view    h3. Expected Results    The Actions button responds and shows all the options.   * !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    h3. Actual Results    Nothing happens  h3. Workaround   * Disable new view from [https://URL.atlassian.net/secure/ViewPersonalSettings.jspa|https://url.atlassian.net/secure/ViewPersonalSettings.jspa]   * Or, zoom out the browser until it displays all the options."""

summary: "Limiting issue visibility to certain users (security levels)"
description: """h3. Problem    Currently, it's not possible limiting issue visibility to certain users on next-gen the same as it can be done on a Classic project using security levels.  h3. Suggestion    Add a similar option to security levels as it can be done into Classics projects."""

summary: "Changing the case of column names on a next-gen board has no effect on the underlying mixed-case version"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    An edit that changes the case of a column names on a next-gen board isn't persisted.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to the board   # Click on a column name (""""Kick Off"""") to rename it   # Enter a new name that differs by case (""""Kick off""""), and hit enter   # Reload the page    h3. Expected Results    The new name has persisted.  h3. Actual Results    The previous case remains.  h3. Workaround    Go via a different name, with two separate edits.     """

summary: "Not possible to link pages to sprint after UI update"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    As per title, it is no longer possible to link a page to a sprint after the UI update. Previously, you can click on the 'linked pages' hyperlink to link Confluence pages to a sprint as shown in https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwarecloud/linking-a-confluence-page-to-a-sprint-764478134.html/ .     With the UI update, the 'linked pages' is changed to 'Plan Sprint' instead. Clicking on the plan sprint shows a drop down with a few options. Clicking on any of the options just redirects you to Confluence and does not link any pages.    !image-2020-04-02-15-55-42-606.png|thumbnail!     This behavior also does not change after you add a Confluence space to 'Pages'.     !image-2020-04-02-15-57-07-215.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Activate Confluence on your Cloud instance  # Navigate to a sprint and click on 'Plan Sprint' to link a Confluence page     h3. Expected Results    There should be a window or pop up that allows you to choose which page to link to a sprint.    h3. Actual Results    Redirection to Confluence without any indication on what to do next.    !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    No workaround for this currently.  """

summary: "Ability to restrict who can ""Browse Projects"""
description: """In Classic projects, there is a """"Browse Projects"""" permission. This makes it possible to give <USER>users read access to one project, without allowing them to see all the other Jira projects.    This is not possible with Next-Gen. If a <USER>user has any permission in a Next-Gen project, they can automatically see the full Jira project list. This is an issue if the <USER>users include clients, for example, who have privacy concerns."""

summary: "Story Point Estimate field cannot be set to zero"
description: """h3. Summary    """"Story Point Estimate"""" field in next-gen project cannot be set to zero.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create an issue in next-gen project  # Type """"0"""" in """"Story Point Estimate"""" field    h3. Expected Results    Value zero will be entered.    h3. Actual Results    Nothing appears in the field.    h3. Workaround    There is no workaround."""

summary: "Unable to start sprint in Japanese UI"
description: """h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set the UI language to Japanese   # Access a backlog   # Select End Date from the date picker   # Click """"Start(開始)""""    h3. Expected Results    The sprint will start  h3. Actual Results    An error is showing saying an invalid format is entered.   Checking the network console, POST rest/greenhopper/1.0/sprint/<N>/start is returning 400 error   !スクリーンショット 2019-03-04 16.03.30.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround   * Change the UI to English at https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/account-preferences, or   * After selecting the date, change the """"AM"""" to """"午前"""" or """"PM"""" to """"午後"""" manually"""

summary: "Next-gen board - Group by Subtasks does not respect the rule to do not show completed issues with more than 14 days."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Next-gen board - """"Group by Subtasks"""" does not respect the rule to do not show completed issues with more than 14 days, it shows issues completed with more than 14 days, we don't see the same behavior when we use the other group by options.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Jira Software Next-Gen project.   # Open the board tab.   # Create tasks and sub-tasks on this board.   # Leave the sub-task closed for more than 14 days.   # Change the group by to Subtasks.   # The completed Subtask will be displayed on your board.   # Change the group by to None.   # All completed issues with more than 14 days won't show up on this board.    h3. Expected Results  All completed issues with more than 14 days without updates should be removed from the board view.    h3. Actual Results  When we use the Group by Subtasks filter, completed Subtasks with more than 14 days without updates will keep showing up on the board.    h3. Workaround    None."""

summary: "Ticket is ""locked"" when dragging ticket to another column with multiple status"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When drag and drop a ticket to another status with multiple transitions, the ticket is locked at fixed position and would not allow us to move up or down to choose the right transition.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a board and choose *Based Swimlane on* to Story.   # Create at least 3 transitions to the next staus  # Tried to drag and drop the ticket to the next status and move it up/down to choose the transition.   h3. Expected Results  The ticket should be able to move up or down to select transition,   h3. Actual Results  The ticket is fixed in one position while dragging to the next status.     h3. Workaround    Put more tickets on the board to widen the swimlane. Or choose *None* for the swimlane settings. """

summary: "Users are not able to open unassigned parent issues in board view "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In the active sprint board, when issues are listed per user (by selecting the user profile), the user is not able to open unassigned parent issues.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # In Board settings > Swimlanes > Set *Base Swimlanes on* to *Stories*  # Create a Story and create a subtask in the story issue  # Assign the subtask to yourself/a user  # Navigate to the board, select/click on your profile icon so that issues assigned to you will be listed.  # Try to open the parent story by clicking on the issue key      h3. Expected Results  Users should be able to open unassigned parent issues in board view    h3. Actual Results  Users are not able to open unassigned parent issues    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Card Layout excludes valid custom fields when board filter contains Issue type"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When adding a custom Field To a Card as covered in [Customizing cards |https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/customizing-cards-938845307.html], valid Custom Field options can be excluded if using an Issue Type in the filter  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create Custom Field with *applicable issue types* set to any restricted value (EXE set this to Story only)   # Create a board with a Filter that has an OR statement including *issuetype = <any other issue type>* EXE:       # Navigate to the Boards *Card Layout* and attempt to add in your custom Field    h3. Expected Results    Custom Field is available  h3. Actual Results    Custom Field is missing from potions. The Custom Field should be available as it applies to some but not all of the issues present in the filter results.  h3. Workaround   * Temporarily set the *applicable issue types* under the Custom fields *scheme context* to *Any Issue Type* see [Specifying field behavior: Modifying field behavior |https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/specifying-field-behavior-938847255.html#Specifyingfieldbehavior-changingfieldsModifyingfieldbehavior]   * [Apply desired card layout |https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/customizing-cards-938845307.html]   * Reset *applicable issue types* on field configurations back to the desired settings    Once you have the Field added to the card layout you can remove it from the field context to return the setting to the original state and the field will remain on the card without issue."""

summary: "Multiple grey bars on board's backlog page."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Multiple grey bars on board's backlog page.    !Screen Shot 2020-04-17 at 18.50.44.png!  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Scrum board.   # Create a large number of issues inside the Scrum board backlog.   ** Not sure how many are necessary to reproduce this behavior.   # Grey bars can show everywhere, but it is hard to reproduce.    The gray bar has the following div:    Video demonstrating the grey bars: [^Screen Recording 2020-04-07 at 10.26 AM.mov]    The video only shows small grey bars, but some customers are seeing much bigger grey bars and on different parts on the board's backlog.  h3. Expected Results    No random grey bars showing up on board's backlog screen.  h3. Actual Results    Random grey bars are showing up on board's backlog where there is a considerable number of issues on this backlog.  h3. Workaround    None."""

summary: "Property jira.permission.create.clone.denied is no longer working"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The user reported that the property <USER>permission.create.clone.denied is no longer working.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Edit a workflow and add <USER>permission.create.clone.denied = true in a status or all statuses   # Transition an issue to the status with the property and clone it     [^<USER>permission.create.clone.denied.mp4]     h3. Expected Results    The property should not allow the issue to be cloned    h3. Actual Results    The issue is cloned      h3. Workaround    The only current workaround is to restrict the create issue permission but with this users also will not be able to create new issues    h3. Note    This property is the workaround for feature request https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-8497"""

summary: "As a Jira Software user, I want to be able to see the Epic information from next-gen projects in the search view"
description: """As a Jira Software user, I want to be able to see the Epic information (Epic name, Epic color, Epic link, Epic status ) from next-gen projects in the search view.     !Screen Shot 2020-04-22 at 11.19.21 AM.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Time tracking field is not showing when creating an issue in next-gen projects"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After added the time tracking field in the issue type story (used as a test) on the next-gen project, when I create a story, it does not show the option to configure the field. The filed is showing when I open the issue created but is not showing when I'm creating an issue.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a next-gen project.   # Go to *project settings >> issue type*.   # Add the *Time tracking* field to the issue type that you want.   # *Save* it.   # Create an issue using the issue type that you configured the *time tracking* field.    h3. Expected Results    When you are creating an issue, the time tracking field is showing as an option to configure.  h3. Actual Results    When you are creating an issue, the time tracking field is *not* showing as an option to configure. However, the field is showing when I view the issue created.  h3. Workaround    There is no workaround."""

summary: "As a Jira Next-gen user, I want to be able to create issue links using the Roadmap"
description: """Currently, users can create links between Epics using the Roadmap feature but it isn't possible to link stories and tasks using this.    As a Jira Next-gen user, I want to be able to create issue links in stories and tasks using the Roadmap feature."""

summary: "Next-gen People custom field "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When it creates a custom field type """"people"""", if you select the """"Restrict to a single person"""" option it will generate the following error while *creating* the issue: """"Could not find usernames: <user_name>.   !Screen Shot 2020-04-27 at 18.55.29.png|thumbnail!    !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     If the issue is created without any value in the custom field type """"people"""", it is possible to edit the issue and add the single user with no error.   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Users should uncheck the """"Restrict to a single person"""" option."""

summary: "Changing the status of issues after removing from the sprint causes sprint reports to become inaccurate"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Changing the status of an issue after removing it from the sprint can cause changes to 'story points completed' to be applied twice, making burnup/burndown reports out of sync with the sprint.    NOTE: There is a related issue which we have not been able to replicate that we believe is linked to this bug, described here: [https://getsupport.atlassian.com/browse/PCS-6025]  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Complete an item(issue 1) in the active sprint   # move issue 1 from the sprint to the backlog   # transition issue 1 from done → ready   # open burnup report    h3. Expected Results    In the sprint reports:    - Completed story points should increase by the amount of story points on the issue when the issue is completed.    - Completed story points should decrease by the same amount when the issue is removed from the sprint.  h3. Actual Results    In the sprint reports:    - Completed story points increases when the issue is completed    - Completed story points  decreases when the issue is removed from the sprint    - Completed story points decreases *again* when the issue is reopened in the backlog  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Cannot pick Confluence pages from the suggestion list in new issue view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When using the Link Confluence Pages feature on JIRA issues, it usually allows us to start typing the page name and suggests a list of pages that match with what we are typing. Though, if we are in the new issue view and try to pick one of the suggested pages, JIRA throws us an error saying to check our connection.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Make sure to get the new issue view enabled;  # Go to a JIRA issue and use the feature to link a Confluence page;  # Click over one of the suggested pages;  # Click over the *Link* button*    h3. Expected Results  The Confluence page is linked to the issue.    h3. Actual Results  JIRA throws us an error saying *_We're having trouble. Check your connection and try again._*    *Notes*:  * After clicking in the *Link* button, a 400 error is generated with response *{""""errorMessages"""":[],""""errors"""":{""""pageUrl"""":""""addconfluencelink.error.url.invalid""""}}*    h3. Workaround  You can either disable the new issue view or paste the Confluence page URL directly in the field."""

summary: "Deleting the furthest right column in Next-gen projects will not log an entry in the issues’ history"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a the furthest right column is deleted in Next-gen projects, the issues are automatically moved to the closest column. This would indicate a transition from one status to another, however, this transition is not logged in the Issue History. In fact, nothing is logged.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Next-gen board   # Add a new column to the furthest right side   # Add issues to the furthest right column   # Delete the column    h3. Expected Results  Issues would be moved to the closest status under the same category and their respective issue histories should log the transition.    h3. Actual Results  Nothing is logged in the issues' history.    h3. Workaround  Before deleting the column, bulk transition the issues to a column that won't be deleted."""

summary: "Sprint Health Gadget is not respecting changes made in global Time tracking"
description: """h3. Summary  The Sprint Health Gadget is not respecting the Global Time Tracking of JIRA.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Sprint Health Gadget based on a board using the Original Time Estimate.  # Create issues in a Sprint in the board and estimate them.  # Change the Global Time Tracking to be   #* Working hours per day: 10  #* Working days per week: 7  # Check the Gadget    h3. Expected Results  The values of the issues should be reflected in the Sprint Health Gadget.    h3. Actual Results  The values in the gadget will remain unchanged      h3.Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Permission error when accessing issue from Bitbucket using Safari."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Permission error when accessing issue from Bitbucket using *Safari*.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # User should have the necessary permissions to view the issue in Jira.  # Access an issue in Bitbucket from *Safari*.    # It will through error “You don't have access to this issue. Make sure the issue exists in this project. If it does, ask a project admin for permission to see the project's issues.”   However the issue can be accessed from Jira without any permission error.     h3. Expected Results  Issue should be viewable from Bitbucket in *Safari*.    h3. Actual Results  Permission error.     h3. Workaround  Issue can be viewed from Bitbucket when access in *Safari Private mode* or using different browser.   """

summary: "When Watchers field is updated on the issue, the card on Jira board is not also updated"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When watchers are updated on the issue, what is shown on the Jira Software card board (both Kanban and Scrum) doesn't reflect this change.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access the active sprint;   # Open any issue;   # Add or exclude a watcher;   # The number of watchers will stay the same, not reflecting the change. Not even refreshing the page updated this information.    h3. Expected Results    The number of watchers should be automatically updated on the card, reflecting what is the issue view is showing.  h3. Actual Results    !image-2020-05-07-15-22-45-297.png|width=584,height=137!    [https://share.getcloudapp.com/DOuQrXKY]    This seems to only happen with Watchers field. I've tried to reproduce the same problem with other fields (as Assignee, Updated) and the problem did not happen.  h3. Workaround    If you move the issue to another column, the number of watchers is then correctly updated."""

summary: "Have parent task icon reflect progress of sub-tasks"
description: """When sorting a board by subtask, it’d be great if a parent task, in its collapsed state, could give a clue about the aggregate status of its children. Now they represent cognitive load and no peace of mind whether they’re closed or open.    A progress bar would be awesome, if not on the icon itself, then somewhere on a parent task’s always-visible header row."""

summary: "Increase Checkbox/Drop down list Limit "
description: """Currently, the custom field *checkbox* or the *Drop down list* in Nex gen projects is only limited to 55 options.     A suggestion would be to increase it to allow users to have more options to be selected"""

summary: "Inconsistency in the drag and drop behaviour from Backlog to To do section in software project"
description: """*Issue*. - In Kanban projects, when ranking is disabled, if we try to drop issues from Backlog to Todo, it gives an error saying Ranking is disabled however if we zoom out the screen in a way that all the issues are visible on screen, drag and drop works fine.    *Expected behavior* - If ranking is disabled, the issue movement should not be allowed even when we zoom out the screen."""

summary: "Adding ""Issue Resolved"" event to the notification feature in Next-Gen Project leads to ""Error 500 - Internal server error"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary   * When we try to add the """"*Issue Resolved*"""" event to the Next-Gen project's notification feature (Simplified Notification Scheme) for any recipient (For example: """"All watchers""""), we face an error 500 Internal server error.   * The issue is reported to have surfaced since the week of 11th May 2020.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    1) Create a next-gen project and create an issue.   2) Open notification helper:    _Jira settings > System > Notification helper [Shortcut key: """"."""" ]_    3) Choose a user   4) Choose the issue from Next-gen project   5) Choose the notification event: """"*Issue Resolved*""""   6) Click on submit   * We see that the Issue resolved notification is not a part of the Next Gen Project's Simplified notification scheme.   * In the details section, we see the following: """"_No one currently receives notifications for the 'Issue Resolved' action. Add new recipient._""""    7) Click on _Add new recipient_   * We are redirected to a page to add a notification event to the Next-gen project's Simplified notification scheme.    8) Choose the event """"*Issue Resolved*""""   9) Choose an audience to receive the notification. For example: """"_All watchers_""""   10) Click on Add   * We face -  Error 500 Internal server error    Note: The notification scheme of Next-gen Projects was updated recently. Kindly refer to the following announcement published on 11th May: [Announcement: Project Level Email notifications|https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-articles/Announcement-Project-Level-Email-Notifications-for-next-gen/ba-p/1374538]. Unsure if this behavior could be due to this change.  h3. Expected Results    1. Either we successfully add the recipient to receive """"_*Issue Resolved*_"""" notifications.    2. Or, since we do not see the option to choose the """"*Issue Resolved*"""" notification event in the Notifications tab in NextGen Projects, we should probably prompt a message stating this action is not allowed.    !Screenshot 2020-05-18 at 11.12.06 AM.png|width=628,height=318!  h3. Actual Results   * Error 500 - internal server error    !Screenshot 2020-05-18 at 11.17.17 AM.png|width=833,height=285!    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:      h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Having only 1 column, and sprints enabled in next-gen hides issues on the backlog"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If you have 1 column and sprints enabled you will not be able to see any issues on the backlog even though they were there when you had 2 columns on the board.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to a next-gen project   # Have sprints enabled   # Create some issues in the backlog    # Delete all columns except 1 on the board   # Go back to backlog and notice that issues created earlier are missing    h3. Expected Results    Should still be able to see issues in the backlog even if you have only 1 column  h3. Actual Results    Unable to see any issues in the backlog.  h3. Workaround   # Create a new column on the board   # Or disable sprints     """

summary: "When trying to link a done issue to another open issue, done issue doesn't appear when searching"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  While trying to link issues, Done issues don't appear when searching and the link is only performed when typing the exact issue key and confirming.  h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a ticket and move it to Done  # Create another ticket and left it open  # Go in link issues and search for the Done issue name or key    h3. Expected Results  The done issue with its name should appear as all other issues appear while typing their names.  h3. Actual Results  Done issues aren't displayed and it seems that have no issues.  h3. Workaround  Currently, the only workaround available is to type the exact issue key from the done issue and confirm the link. The issue will be recognized and will accomplish the link."""

summary: "Report's legend bar covers the reports"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Report's legend bar covers the *Classic* projects reports    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open your Agile project and then browse to Reports section.   # Try to open reports such as *Cumulative flow diagram, velocity chart, sprint report* or *burndown chart.*    h3. Expected Results  The legends bar on the top of the report does not cover the report data    h3. Actual Results  The tabular bar for the report legends, is covering the data. This is inconvenient since the data behind the legends cannot be read.   !BurndownReport.png|thumbnail!  !SprintReport.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "The graph legend in Scrum Reports is broken"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Scrum Reports like Velocity Chart, Sprint Report, Burndown Chart or Cumulative Flow Diagram are broken due their graph legend appearing in the entire graph.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Open Velocity Chart, Sprint Report, Burndown Chart or Cumulative Flow Diagram from a Scrum board.     h3. Expected Results   !Screen Shot 2020-05-20 at 15.38.44.png|thumbnail!   h3. Actual Results   !Screen Shot 2020-05-20 at 15.50.23.png|thumbnail!    !Screen Shot 2020-05-20 at 15.51.18.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Issues created from board is created on the wrong project after switching project"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  On the backlog view, clicking on the *+ Create Issue* button underneath Sprint/Backlog will allow users to create the issue inline.    Switching the project by clicking on the Project Name at the top left and creating the issue from backlog view will create the issue on the wrong project.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to Project A's board. Click on the *+ Create Issue* to create an issue inline from the backlog   # Click on the Project Name on the top left and switch to another classic software project   # Create the issue on another project's board using the same method as the 1st step - Click on the *+ Create Issue* and create the issue inline.   # The issue is created but it's located on another board.   #* Video:  [^Issue created on the wrong project.webm]       h3. Expected Results  * The issue should be created on the project B's board.    h3. Actual Results  * The issue is created on the wrong project, hence it's not visible on the location where the user is creating it from.      h3. Workaround  * Don't switch project by clicking on the Project Name on the top left, as shown in the video.   * Opening project B in a new tab will not cause this issue."""

summary: "Advance/Basic Search results will not show header from any results when selected"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Advance/Basic Search results will not show header from any results when selected.    Not using the New Navigation bar shows the header cut-off and using the New Nav Bar does not show.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to the Search option   # Create a Basic or Advance search   # Select any of the result issues    [^JSWCLOUD-18968.webm]    h3. Expected Results    The issue selected should show the complete header of the ticket:     !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    The issue selected does not show the complete header:     !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Open the issue directly from the Projects or you can delete all the URL after the ticket number:     !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!     """

summary: "Legend of Cumulative Flow Diagram legend hides information"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Legend of Cumulative Flow Diagram legend hides information    h3. Environment  Jira Software cloud    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Scrum project  # Add issues to the project  # Transition them among statuses  # Access the Cumulative Flow Diagram report    h3. Expected Results  The chart and legend display properly    h3. Actual Results  The chart legend hides information   !AFTER BUG.png|thumbnail!    !Screenshot 2020-05-25 at 15.52.03.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  No workaround"""

summary: "The option to access board settings truncated when no columns on the board"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When creating a board without columns, the option from the board settings truncated, and if you not using English as your default language for Jira, there is not link from the board.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a board without columns   # Access the board    h3. Expected Results  The board shows the option to access the board settings.  h3. Actual Results  The left side truncated.    h3. Workaround    Change your Jira to English  Append *&config=columns* at the back of the board URL"""

summary: "The progress bar is not rendering correctly on board cards"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The progress bar is not rendering correctly on board cards.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Add the 'progress' field to the card layout.  # Create a new issue and add the original time estimate and remaining time estimate.  # Log time.    !image-2020-05-27-18-33-52-854.png|thumbnail!   # Check the card on the board.    h3. Remaining time estimate is not visible.     !image-2020-05-27-18-34-19-342.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "The shortcut 'a' for assigning issues may not work in the board view if using the old issue view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The keyboard shortcut 'a' may not work *for some customers* while *in a board*  using *the old issue view*.     h3. Expected Results  The assigning modal should open   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  Nothing happens (no errors on both logs and JS consoles)  h3. Workaround  Turn the new issue view 'On'. In the case this is heavily impacting you, contact our support."""

summary: "The 'Delete' option (right-mouse button menu) in the board may not work for some customers if the new issue view is on"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  *Some customers* may not be able to use the 'Delete' option from the right-mouse button menu in the *Board* when the *new issue view* is on.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Turn-on the new issue view  # Go to a Kanban board  # Press the right-mouse button -> 'Delete'   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  The modal to confirm the issue delete appears    h3. Actual Results  Nothing happens (no errors in the JS console or in the logs).    h3. Workaround  Turn-off the new issue view. In the case this is heavily impacting you, contact Atlassian support."""

summary: "Ability to show the count of the issues in an epics in the Epic panel "
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In next-gen projects, the ability to see the count of issues in an epic panel is not present.   !Screenshot 2020-05-28 at 20.18.17.png|thumbnail!     h3. Suggested Solution  Provide the ability to see the count of issues in an epic panel is not present.    h3. Why this is important  Although the epics and progress bar is present. The count should be present as it is in a classic project.  """

description: """h3. Summary    Sum Progress (Σ Progress) is not showing correctly in the card layout of Kanban and Sprint boards.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create an issue and add value for Original Estimate  # Log some work on the issue  # Add """"Σ Progress"""" field in Card Layout  # View Kanban or Sprint board    h3. Expected Results    """"Σ Progress"""" should appear like this on the card:   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    """"Σ Progress"""" appears like this on the card:   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    There is no workaround."""

summary: "Next-gen/Classic backlog - When adding a value for the story points estimate in the right-side panel, it does not update the story points estimate on the card view."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When adding value for the story points estimates in the right-side panel, it does not update the story points estimate on the card view.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a next-gen/Classic project.   # Enable feature backlog.   # Enable feature estimation.   # Create a new issue.   # Select the issue in the backlog tab.   # In the right-side panel, add value for the story points estimate field.   # The card will not update.   # Change the issue's assignee at the right-side panel.   # The card is updated with the new assignee and the new value for the story points estimate field.    !Screen Shot 2020-05-29 at 18.27.14.png|thumbnail!    According to the screenshot above, the card shows  7 story points estimate, the left-side panel show 9 story points estimate.  h3. Expected Results    The card should automatically refresh the value for the story points estimate field.  h3. Actual Results    The card is not updating the story points estimate field when we change it at the right-side panel.  h3. Workaround    Refresh the page."""

summary: "When typing ""."" as a keyboard shortcut (to access all possible operations on an issue), The ""."" appears in the dropdown"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When customer user press the shortcut """"."""", the window that opens has already a """"."""" So customer complains that he has to backspace every time..    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Select any issue from Project """"Ag & Powersports""""   # Type the '.' and the pop-up window will show a '.' already in the box.    h3. Expected Results    That the pop-up window opens without a """"."""" already there  h3. Actual Results    The """"."""" appears in the dropdown window    h3. Workaround    backspace to erase the """"."""" before type"""

summary: """Project create"" is still scrollable after picking a template"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  """"Project create"""" is still scrollable after picking a template. Comparison Screen/ Template selection stays under the project create view.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Click """"create project"""" on a software only instance     # Be shown the comparison screen, pick a template     # Scroll down and notice that the templates selection is still presented    h3. Expected Results   # Customers should not see templates selection after choosing a template.     h3. Actual Results   # Customers still see templates selection after choosing a template.     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Active Sprint view is not loaded after user visit Backlog view with Keyboard shortcut"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Active Sprint view is not loaded after user visit Backlog view with Keyboard shortcut  h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Disable new view from : https://<URL>.atlassian.net/secure/ViewPersonalSettings.jspa  #* Keyboard shortcut may not work due to https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/JRACLOUD-71717  # Open Active Sprint view  # Refresh the page and keep pressing *1* so that it goes to Backlog (don't wait until the page is fully loaded)  # Backlog view is open and loaded  # Press *2* to go back to Active Sprint view    h3. Expected Results    Active Sprint view is loaded.    h3. Actual Results    Active Sprint view is Not loaded (it keeps spinning)  *  !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Refresh the page"""

summary: "Pipe character does not work in Roadmaps"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The pipe character `|` does not work in the inline create in Roadmaps create. It is converted into a whitespace, which is then trimmed out. Works fine when updating the issue afterwards from issue side view.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to Roadmaps page.   # Create issue with pipe character.    h3. Expected Results  Issue summary contains pipe character.    h3. Actual Results  Issue summary does not have pipe character.    h3. Workaround  After creating the issue, go to issue side view and update the summary. Pipe characters should work there."""

summary: "Velocity chart's commitment shows incorrect value"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Velocity chart's commitment points are not consistent with the values observed in Sprint report or the corresponding burndown chart for the sprint.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Cannot reproduce it consistently, but please refer to the screenshots.   ## *Test Sprint 4* started off with *13* story points initially.   ## BurndownScreenshot:     !BurndownSprint4.png|thumbnail!   ## However, Velocity chart records an initial commitment of 8 points.   ## VelocityChart:    !VelocityChart.png|thumbnail!   ## BurndownChartDetails:     !BurndownDetailsSprint4.png|thumbnail!   # Similar to this, again, for sprint *Test Sprint 10*   ## Started off with commitment story points of *9*, as seen in the Burndown chart.   ## burndownSprint10:     !BurndownSprint10.png|thumbnail!   ## This time, velocity chart shows the correct commitments though.   ## VelocityChart:    !VelocityChart.png|thumbnail!   ## BurndownChartDetails:    !BurndownDetailsSprint10.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results    An expected value for the Commitment story points should have been,    Commitment= SUM of Story Points (Issues Completed during the sprint + Issues Not Completed during the sprint + Issues Removed from the Sprint after it started)    That is, it should have considered the story points for ALL the issues which were present at the *beginning* of the sprint.  h3. Actual Results    The values in the Velocity Chart are not correctly recorded, as corroborated by above screenshots.   h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Loading multiple times a board (in different tabs) may cause an error "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Some users may face an error if they open multiple times (in multiple tabs around 5 or more). The error is usually non-fatal and the board still loads    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Open the same board in new tab 5 or more times  # Wait for it to load    h3. Expected Results  Boards load with no issues      h3. Actual Results  A generic """"Oops! Something went wrong"""" error appears, although the board still loads.   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Refreshing the erroring page works"""

summary: "Provide the ability to create workflow schemes/groups and status schemes/groups within NextGen projects"
description: """  It will be better if we can create different workflows and Jira admin can select the workflow for each projects from the list of created workflows.    Example for workflow groups:-  Workflow 1:- The workflow Includes statuses such as """"To Do , In progress and Done""""  Workflow 2:-The workflow Includes statuses such as """"To Do , In progress , In review and Done""""    Also it will be better if we have the same feature for setting the Jira status. Means , we can create different groups of status lists and we can select the needed group in each project. Or we can set one group of status for all projects.    Example for status groups.:-  Status group 1:- Includes """"To Do , In progress and Done""""  Status group 2:- Includes """"To Do , In progress , In review and Done""""    In both status and workflow , either we can set for all projects or for selected projects.  The above are needed fro next generation projects."""

summary: "A warning is displayed on issues assigned to future sprints"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  A warning is displayed on issues assigned to future sprints:   !Screen Shot 2020-05-29 at 16.26.36.png|thumbnail!   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a board with issues and multiple sprints   # Create a plan for this board   # Estimate the issues and calculate the plan  #* Issues will have a warning    h3. Expected Results  The issues don't have a warning or users have  the option to set the dates on sprint.    h3. Actual Results  The warning can't be resolved.    h3. Workaround  N/A"""

summary: "Navigating through Portfolio shows constantly the pop-up message ""Leave site? Changes you made may not be saved."""
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When navigating through Portfolio screens a pop-up window showing the message """"Leave site? Changes you made may not be saved."""" is constantly displayed  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access Portfolio and click """"View all plans""""   # Access any plan    h3. Expected Results    You access the plan without problems  h3. Actual Results    A pop-up window showing the message """"Leave site? Changes you made may not be saved."""" appears and you have to click """"Leave""""    !Screen Recording 2020-03-18 at 08.11 PM.gif|width=817,height=424!  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Setting Completed issues days to 0 gives DataValidationException"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Completed issues days cannot accept 0 as its value and causes a DataValidationException error and will not save.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure your Plan and go to *Issue sources*    # Set the days of *Completed issues* to """"0"""" and click outside the field to save    h3. Expected Results    Change is saved and new value is 0.    h3. Actual Results     !JPO3_Plan_-_Advanced_Roadmaps_-_Jira.png|thumbnail!     *Request Information*        *Stack Trace*        h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "The Dependency report doesn't show all dependencies of the same type for an issue"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a user adds more than one link of the same type in an issue, the Portfolio Dependency Report will show only one entry    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure all the issue linking option in the Portfolio's *Dependency settings*   # Create a classic project and add issues to it  # Create a Portfolio plan using the board of the project created as the issue source  # Pickup one issue and add a link to 2 different issues using the type *relates to*  # Access the Portfolio plan and re-calculate it  # Click the *Reports* tab in Portfolio and selects the *Dependency Report* option    h3. Expected Results    The 2 dependencies are displayed    h3. Actual Results    Only one of the dependencies is visible in the report.     !Screen Shot 2020-05-04 at 1.05.44 PM.png|thumbnail!    !Screen Shot 2020-05-04 at 1.04.57 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Creates the dependencies using the other issue. For example:    * You have issues A, B, and C  * Add the dependency (link) between A and B from the A view  * Add the dependency (link) between A and C from the C view, not from A view"""

summary: "Removing an issue source with a pending change breaks the Plan"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Removing an Issue source with a pending change causes the Plan to break.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Plan with two different Project-based Issue sources  # Add a new scenario and create one Issue for Project A and one for Project B (do not commit the changes)  # Configure the Issue sources and remove the second Issue source (Project B)    h3. Expected Results    The source along with all Issues and changes should be removed from the Plan.    h3. Actual Results    The Plan breaks making it unusable.     !Break_this_plan_-_Portfolio_for_Jira_-_Jira.png|thumbnail!         h3. Workaround    To fix this you must re-configure the Issue sources to add back the removed issue source (Project B) then remove any changes that pertained to Project B. Once removed, you can proceed to remove the Project once more from the Issue sources."""

summary: "Plans are returning ""Something went wrong"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Plans are returning """"Something went wrong"""" when the source is a filter that doesn't specify the project.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a filter like the ones below:  #* labels = XXXXX  #* custom field = XXXX   # Create a Plan for that filter    h3. Expected Results  Plan loads without errors    h3. Actual Results  Something went wrong is displayed:      h3. Workaround  Update the filter and specify the project"""

summary: "In the plan view, sprint's end date is one day sooner than the actual date"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When setting up a sprint to end on June 25th in the board, the plan in Advance Roadmaps will show this sprint as ending on June 24th    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a sprint in a board. Set its end date and time   # Create a plan in Advanced Roadmaps to include that sprint    h3. Expected Results  The sprint's end date will be taken from the sprint data    h3. Actual Results    Due to some algorithms in the back-end, the sprint's end date in the plan is shown as one day sooner (plan's sprint end-date = board's sprint end date -1 ).  This happens even if the plan's time zone matches the user's time zone.    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "The next-gen boards aren't automatically updating either notifying users that changes were made"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    We have the following scenario: we have two users viewing the same board (backlog). If one user creates an issue in the backlog, the other user doesn't have their page automatically either the banner """"This board has been updated: Refresh"""" is being displayed.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Jira Software project  # Access its board  # Open the same project and using the browser's incognito mode  # Create an issue using one browser tab  # Look at the board in the incognito mode    h3. Expected Results    The created issue is visible on the board or the message This board has been updated: Refresh"""" is displayed    h3. Actual Results    To see the newly created issues, the user needs to manually refresh the page from times to times    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Opening an Advanced Roadmaps plan fails with the plan header shown but the roadmaps tab stuck in a loading state"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  For some customers, their Advanced Roadmaps plan page loads everything except the issues scope.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open a plan page    h3. Expected Results  # The plan header shows and after a while the loading state of the roadmaps tab content gets replaced with the actual issues of that plan    h3. Actual Results  # The loading state remains active, issue data never renders    h3. Workaround  Clearing the browser's local storage resolves the issue, check [this page|https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/32050?co=GENIE.Platform%3DDesktop&hl=en] on how to do this for Chrome."""

summary: "Add the same Description editor from the View/Edit screen to Issue type"
description: """h3. *Problem:*    Currently, the editor to add a default value to the Description has fewer options than the editor when we are editing an issue.    !Screen Shot 2020-06-17 at 16.52.49.png|width=287,height=211!  h3. *Suggestion:*    Add the same editor for both View/Edit and the Description field on the Issue type, so administrators can add a table and other information as the default value for the field.    !Screen Shot 2020-06-17 at 16.53.15.png|width=384,height=177!"""

summary: "Smart value {{issue.url.customer}} do not work "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When applying the smart value \{{issue.url.customer}}, observed that when an email is sent, it does not update the customer ticket URL.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an automation rule to send an email with smart value \{{issue.url.customer}}    h3. Expected Results    Email to be received with an URL which contains the customer view of the ticket.   h3. Actual Results    Empty email received with an URL.  h3. Workaround    No workaround available. """

summary: "Custom fields of type URL interpret any URL with a mail address in it as mailto:"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using a custom field URL and the user paste an web link that contains `*authuser= <useremail>*`on the URL body, if the user clicks on the link, it is opening a new email box with '*mail to:*' instead of opening the web page.    As an example of a URL that contains `*authuser= <useremail>*`, you can use for test the google drive link: [https://drive.google.com/.....authuser=<useremail>.../|https://drive.google.com/.....authuser].  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add the URL custom field in the issue type screen.   # Paste the following link on the URL custom field:  [https://drive.google.com/.....authuser=<useremail>.../|https://drive.google.com/.....authuser]   # Open the URL.    h3. Expected Results    It should open the link posted on the ticket  h3. Actual Results    When clicking in the link, it opens an email box.  h3. Workaround    Create a text field (multi-line) and configure the Wiki render. """

summary: "Creating a Sprint with a start date before completing the previous Sprint can cause Scope Changed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If you start a Sprint with a date *before* the completion date from the previous Sprint, the unresolved issues from the previous Sprint will be added as a Scope Changed on the started Sprint    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Complete a Sprint 1 with unresolved issues and send these issues to a Sprint 2  # Start the Sprint 2 with a date *before* the date you completed the Sprint 1    h3. Expected Results  The issues should be added to Sprint 2 without Scope Change    h3. Actual Results  The issues will be added as Scope Change on Sprint 2    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Dependency pop up doesn't have scroll bar"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Dependency pop up doesn't have scroll bar and sometimes part of the pop up is hidden    h3. Steps to Reproduce   Try clicking on a ticket with several dependencies (this is intermittent) or add a few dependencies to the issue (the pop up won't be adjusted)    h3. Expected Results  Dependency pop up has scroll bar    h3. Actual Results  Part of the pop up is hidden   !Screen Recording 2020-06-23 at 06.56 PM.gif|thumbnail!   h3. Workaround  Close the pop up and open it once again"""

summary: "Keyboard Input events interpreted inconsistently during page load"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Keyboard Input events interpreted inconsistently during page load and using the Search field    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Load any available project and select the Search field which will be available to use and begin typing    h3. Expected Results    Should conserve the words/numbers typed after the project fully loads    h3. Actual Results    Depending on the typed letters/numbers it will use and prompt you to the Shortcuts from the menu.    In this screen recording, I selected the letter """"i"""" to start typing to the search field and It assigned the issue and not wrote the letter to the Search field:    [^Bug_Loading_Project.webm]    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Grouping by release hides sub-tasks of stories assigned to that release"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When grouping by Release, sub-tasks do not appear when their parent story is assigned to a release  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan   # Create a release in the plan   # Create a story   # Create a sub-task for the story   # Assign the story to the release (created in step 2)   # Use view settings to group the plan by release    h3. Expected Results    You should see the sub-task as a child of the story in the release section  h3. Actual Results    The sub-task is not shown.  h3. Workaround    The only workarounds are to add the sub-task to the release or to not group by release."""

summary: "You can't scroll down in the hidden comment's format options list in the issue detail view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When you resize the issue detail view in the backlog, the comment options will be hidden inside the plus (*+*) sign button:   !Screen Shot 2020-06-26 at 10.18.04.png|thumbnail!     The problem is that you won't be able to scroll down on that list to select the other options.    Check this recording:  [^Comment options.mp4]     h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Access a board's backlog;  * Click on an issue to open its detail view;  * Resize the detail view;  * Click on the plus sign to open the other formatting options.    h3. Expected Results    You will be able to scroll down the list to select the other options.    h3. Actual Results    You are not able to scroll down the list.    h3. Workaround    Make sure to be on full screen, and resize the detail view to its max extension:  [^Comment options 2.mp4] """

Expected Results    Changes visible in Review Pending changes popup and can be saved  h3. Actual Results    No pending changes visible as Review Changes popup is opened.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Accessing Sprint Burndown and Sprint Health Gadgets in Confluence server Blocked by CORS"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When trying to access Jira Software Sprint health and Sprint Burndown gadgets from a Jira Server/Datacenter instance, the gadgets will fail to load with the gadget URL being blocked by CORS policy. As the CORS policy must be set on the server, this issue would require changes to the policy set for the impacted gadgets.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up Confluence Server so that it can be accessed over the internet.   # Add Application link back to Jira Software Cloud instance   # Add the gadget feed to point to the Jira instance in Confluence   # Wait long enough for the feed to pull in the gadgets   # Attempt to use the sprint burndown gadget    h3. Expected Results    The gadget loads and functions normally    h3. Actual Results    The Gadget will be blocked by CORS, you will see many errors in the Javascript console regarding functions missing. Here are some examples:        === Example HAR ===   [^confluence.jaredlongtest.com.har]   === Example Javascript Console ===   [^confluence.jaredlongtest.com-1593729482888-JS_console.log]   ===      h3. Workaround    There is currently no known workaround"""

summary: "Ability to bulk change Next-gen projects access (Private, Limited, Open)"
description: """h3. *Problem:*    Currently, as there isn't the concept of permission scheme in Next-gen projects, there's no way of bulk editing projects access at once.    h3. *Suggestion:*  Add a way for bulk changing multiple Next-gen projects' access.    This comes handy when there are way too many Next-gen projects created as it would be much easier to manage them and accomplish changes to them."""

summary: "Searching for the ""Parent Link"" field using ""IS NOT EMPTY"" returns inconsistent results"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Searching for the """"Parent Link"""" field using """"IS NOT EMPTY"""" returns inconsistent results  h3. Steps to Reproduce    We can see extra results with JQL queries related to Parent Links (Image 2020-07-06 at 11.12.07 PM.png)    The unexpected result with this query (Image 2020-07-06 at 11.11.54 PM.png)       h3. Expected Results    The search should only return issues that have a value for the Parent Link field.    h3. Actual Results    The search return issues without a value for the Parent Link field.  When using the """"IS EMPTY"""" operator the search result excludes issues that don't have a value for the Parent Link and should appear.    h3. Workaround    Only via JQL search is not possible right now.  """

summary: "Pie chart gadget sends header limit error when the filter calls around 430 issues"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Pie chart gadget sends header limit error when the filter calls around 430 issues.  When clicking on any segment of the pay chart Jira sends all the called issues through the URL, if these are more that 430 the user receives a """"header too large error""""    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a filter with a JQL like issue in (ISS-1,ISS-2,ISS3,ISS-4...........) with more than 430 issues   # Create a workload pie chart gadget and use the previously created filter   # Click on a segment of the pie chart   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  To display the information or one error indicating what is the problem    h3. Actual Results  A 400 bad request header too large error is displayed    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "The jira.permission.scheduleissue.denied property shows wrong error message when adding issue to Active Sprint"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When _jira.permission.scheduleissue.denied_ property is set to one status in the workflow, it blocks the possibility to reorder issues (“rank”) on the board and backlog as also the possibility to edit Due Date field. However, we are able to move the issue to an Active Sprint although Jira shows the following error:    !image-2020-07-07-19-30-46-968.png|width=375,height=95!    This error is the same that is shown when reordering issues in the Backlog. However, for this action, the property indeed blocks the action, making the issue return to its original place.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Apply _jira.permission.scheduleissue.denied_ (property key) / _true_ (property value) to a specific status in the workflow   # Go to the project backlog   # Drag and drop to the active sprint an issue which current status is the one to which property above was applied   # The error message will appear (_""""You do not have permission to rank...""""_) but the issue will stay in the Active Sprint   # If you refresh the page, nothing changes    h3. Expected Results    The error message should not be shown since the issue is added to the Sprint.  h3. Actual Results    An error message related to the Schedule Issue permission is shown even though the issue is added to the Active Sprint.  h3. Workaround    It's possible to build an Automation rule to, whenever Sprint field is updated, if the issue is on the specific status, clear the Sprint field. However, this action is done silently and the user may not notice the issue was not added to the Sprint since no error message will be shown."""

summary: "Log Work ""Start Date"" in the New Issue View do not reflect on Burndown Chart"
description: """h3. Summary    When logging work in the New Issue View, the *Start Date* field is automatically set to (local time - work logged). In the issue history, the logged work action is displayed as (local time - work logged) causing it to no be displayed in the Burndown Chart.    h3. Steps to reproduce    # An issue is worked and then added to the Active Sprint when the work is done;  # The work is logged in the issue;  # If using the new issue view, the logged work won't reflect in the Burndown Chart;  # On the old issue view, it will correctly reflect.    New issue view:   [^Screen Recording 2020-07-06 at 11.13 AM.mp4]   Old Issue view: [^Screen Recording 2020-07-06 at 11.14 AM.mp4]     !Image 2020-07-06 at 11.15.43 AM.png|thumbnail!   In this example, the work was logged twice, one in the new issue view and one in the old. We can see that only the second is logged.    h3. Expected Results    The logged work is displayed in the Burndown Chart.    h3. Actual Results    Logged work in the new issue view does not reflect in the Burndown Chart.    h3. Workaround    Log work in the old issue view."""

summary: " Add to the audit log when column orders change in a next-gen project"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When changing the order of columns, there is no place to identify those changes.    h3. Suggested Solution    Add logs of the changes in the audit log."""

summary: "Canceled or Won't Do option for next-gen projects"
description: """{panel:title=Atlassian status as of August 2020|borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#b2d4ff|titleBGColor=#b2d4ff|bgColor=#eaf1fc}  Hello all,    Thank you for watching, voting, and commenting on this ticket.    We understand that closing an issue as ‘Done’ can sometimes be a misnomer, e.g. the issue was a duplicate, or deprioritised as ‘won’t do', or has become obsolete.     As this ticket is a duplicate of [JSWCLOUD-18435|https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/JSWCLOUD-18435], we are closing this ticket so that we can aggregate the feedback on a single ticket.     Cheers,  <USER> Product Manager, Atlassian.  {panel}    An option to close a ticket as 'Canceled' or 'Won't Do' would be really helpful as sometimes there are tickets that have useful details and shouldn't be permanently deleted or left in the Backlog indefinitely."""

summary: "Ability to select where attachments are displayed on the Board card"
description: """Today, attachments can be displayed only in the top part of Next-gen Board cards. It would be useful if they could be placed at the bottom of the card, below the issue-key and other issue details."""

summary: "Unable to start sprint in Scrum project when the profile language is set as Russian"
description: """h3. Summary  Unable to start the sprint in Scrum project when the profile language is set as Russian as it suggests wrong End date and the end date is in English    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Set the language in your Atlassian account to Russian (русский)  # Try to Start a sprint from the backlog of the scrum project  # Click on Start Sprint  # Change the sprint duration and look at the end date    h3. Expected Results  The Sprint end date is calculated based on the 1-4 week duration you choose Or you are able to choose the date from the calendar in the Russian language   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  * The month is written in English  * The incorrect end date is calculated  * You cannot choose the month manually   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!   !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!   !screenshot-4.png|thumbnail!     h3.Workaround  Switch the language to English. Or Reach out to Atlassian support"""

description: """h3. Issue Summary    Wrong Padding on Board Issue Detail View.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to any kanban or scrum board.   # Click on an Issue to open the details on the right part of the screen.       h3. Expected Results    The text padding should be better.      h3. Actual Results   !Capture.PNG|thumbnail!       h3. Workaround      Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "After a site rename Github configuration becomes unavailable"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After doing a site rename, the Github Configuration page becomes unavailable and you're unable to manage your organizations.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Perform a site rename on an instance   # Go to *Manage apps* and open the """"get started"""" for the Github add-on    h3. Expected Results    You should be directed to the Github Configuration page where you can manage your organizations.     !GitHub_Configuration_-_tet.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    You will be directed to the Github configuration page but the page cannot load.     !GitHub_Configuration_-_Jira.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Uninstall and re-install the Github add-on from the *Find new apps*."""

summary: "Version report gadgets loads indefinitely "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The version report gadget loads indefinitely. Based on the version closed issues story points and in the story points from the open issues at the version JIRA calculates that the version will take longer than 20 years to be closed, therefore, the gadget won't load.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create and estimate some issues  # Close some of the estimated issues  # Create a release and include the closed issues  # Create a dashboard and add the version report gadget referencing the created release    (i) Make sure the closed issues have a smaller estimate than the open ones. The idea here is to make the release velocity low based on the closed issues so the open ones make the estimation bigger.    h3. Expected Results    The gadget will load as expected.    h3. Actual Results    The version report gadget loads infinitely and the following error is logged in the browser logs:      h3. Workaround    Change the Release start date to a later date or re-estimate the issues to make the release velocity higher."""

summary: "Not able to access Next-Gen project's board"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    It's not possible to access some Next-Gen project's board.    h3. Expected Results    When having the needed permissions, the user should be able to access the board.  h3. Actual Results    A """"something's going wrong message"""" is shown:    !image-2020-07-14-12-46-55-981.png|width=313,height=118!    Got a 404 response when try reloading the page:        h3. Workaround    Contact the Atlassian support."""

summary: "Misleading error message for large date values in sprint end date field"

Actual Results    This error message should be shown instead.     !image-2020-07-17-23-32-50-745.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    No workaround for this as of now."""

summary: "Group by Team while Team filter is applied shows empty list of issues"
description: """*Issue Summary*   While both filter and group-by options are applied for the *Team* field, no issues are displayed.    *Steps to Reproduce*   # Select a team in the project filter.   # Now in the view settings, select group by teams    *Expected Results*   Issues should be grouped by teams.    *Actual Results*   Shows an empty list of issues.    *Workaround*   Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "For Global Automation Rules, Conditions are not working for Next-Gen projects"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Conditions for Global Automation Rules are not working when using Next-Gen projects.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Global Automation Rule   # Use the following pattern:  Trigger: *Issue transitioned*  Condition: *Issue fields condition*  Action: *Anyone*   # Run the Rule    !image-2020-07-20-13-07-41-438.png|width=257,height=473!  h3. Expected Results    When the conditions are set, the Automation Rule should perform the action  h3. Actual Results    The Rule is not recognizing when the Condition is true:    !image-2020-07-20-13-11-10-724.png|width=751,height=166!    !image-2020-07-20-13-12-14-510.png|width=391,height=622!  h3. Workaround    Instead of using a Global Rule, the rule needs to be created on each project."""
