description: """h3. Issue Summary    Advanced Roadmaps auto-schedule does not make the calculated values of sub-tasks persistent  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an Advanced Roadmaps plan which contains sub-tasks   # Click auto-schedule    h3. Expected Results   * The auto-scheduler will work out the empty values and save the results    h3. Actual Results   * The auto-scheduler works out the empty values in the sub-tasks and displays but does not save them    h3. Workaround   * Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Creating a ticket through the roadmaps view, it automatically sets the Resolved date field."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    This behavior only occurs when we create the ticket through the roadmaps view. If we use the top bar button """"Create"""" it will not set the Resolved date field.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    1. Go to any nextgen project in our Jira cloud instance  2. Go to the Roadmap page  3. Create an issue from the roadmap view (+ create epic)  4. Notice the """"resolutiondate"""" field has been set automatically    h3. Expected Results    No Resolution Date automatically set when the issue is created  h3. Actual Results    The """"resolutiondate"""" field is set after issue creation.  h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "When using Windows, in old issue view, the DIV element that contains the issue key is overlapping the ""..."" button of issue quick view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using Windows, if the name of the project is too long and the issue key has up to 3 digit numbers, the """"..."""" button of issue quick view only work when we position the mouse cursor at a specific point/pixel. The problem only happens in the old issue view.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    1) Create a project with a long name - up to 23 characters   2) Create an issue with a issue key with up to 3 digit numbers   3) Using the old issue view, access the board   4) In issue quick view, try to click on """"..."""" button  h3. Expected Results    Hover of """"..."""" button should be shown complete.  h3. Actual Results    You need to position the mouse cursor at a specific point of the button in order to be able to click on it and access issue's details:    [https://share.getcloudapp.com/04uYlLqW]    Checking at Browser Dev Tools, it's possible to noticed that the root cause of the problem is the fact that the DIV element that contains the issue key is overlapping the button:    !image-2020-07-23-17-17-37-801.png|width=441,height=168!    The problem only happens when using Windows. When using Mac, this does not happen since the project name is shown in one line and the issue key is shown in another line.   h3. Workaround    The workaround for this would be to use the new issue view since the problem only happens with old issue view."""

summary: "The browser shortcut Ctrl + F does not find issue keys on board cards."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Ctrl + F browser shortcut does not search for issue keys on board cards, the hyphen between the project key, and the issue number breaks the search.    Here is a test Kanban board with the card KT-1.   !Screen Shot 2020-07-14 at 21.50.00.png|thumbnail!        If we press ctrl+f and search for KT, we will find the project key.   !Screen Shot 2020-07-14 at 21.51.49.png|thumbnail!    If we press ctrl+f and search for 1, we will find the issue number.   !Screen Shot 2020-07-14 at 21.52.01.png|thumbnail!    However, if we search for KT- or -1, the hyphen will break the search and we won't find the issue.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Kanban board.   # Create a card inside the Kanban board.   # Press ctrl+f.   # Search for the project key, you will find the issue.   # Search for the issue number, you will find the issue.   # Search for the project key + hyphen, the search will break.   # Search for hyphen + the issue number, the search will break.    h3. Expected Results  The browser shortcut Ctrl+F should be able to search and find an issue using the issue key information.  h3. Actual Results  The browser shortcut Ctrl+F does not support the hyphen on board cards, it breaks the search for issue keys.  h3. Workaround  Use the board search box to find the issue using the issue key.   !Screen Shot 2020-07-14 at 22.16.50.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Home button key on the keyboard does not work for the link issue text field cursor to the start"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    As per summary, in the new issue view, the home (or pos 1) key on the keyboard does not work on the link issue text field.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Attempt to link an issue in new JIRA issue view.   # Try to use the home key to return to the cursor to the start of the text field.    h3. Expected Results    The cursor returns to the start of the text field.    h3. Actual Results    The home button does not work at all.    h3. Workaround    This only affects the new JIRA issue view. If you revert back to the old view the home button works. You can disable Atlassian Labs at https://<atlassian-domain-name>.atlassian.net/secure/ViewPersonalSettings.jspa . This will revert the view back to the old issue view."""

summary: "Unestimated child issues causes incoherent dependency scheduling"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using the Auto-scheduler, unestimated issues under the parent causes it to propose incoherent dates that ignore the dependencies and creates conflict. This is also applicable when estimating with """"0"""".    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create Task1, Task2 with 2 Subtasks and Task3   # Add the dependencies with Task3 blocking Task2 and Task2 blocking Task1   # Add an *Estimate* value to Task1, Task2 and Task3 (excluding the subtasks)   # Add the start/end dates to each Task making sure that they do not clash    h3. Expected Results    When using the *Auto-schedule*, the scheduler is expected to provide new start/end dates taken into account the dependencies of the Tasks.    h3. Actual Results    The scheduler completely ignores the dependencies and schedule dates that create depedencyes conflict with lead time.     !2020-07-24_16-46-16.gif|width=400&height=200!    h3. Workaround    All child issues found under the dependency issues must be estimated."""

summary: "Right clicking on issues on backlog display ""Delete issue"" for users without permission "
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When accessing the Backlog on a Jira Software project, if you right-click to an issue, you'll be able to see the """"Delete"""" option even though you don't have such permission.     (!) If the user does not have Delete Issue Permission, Jira *will prevent* the user from deleting the issue.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Access the Backlog  # Right-click over an issue on backlog    h3. Expected Results  If the user does not have permission, it should not appear the option """"Delete""""    h3. Actual Results  The """"Delete"""" option appears even though you don't have this permission.    !image-2020-06-22-16-41-54-869.png|thumbnail!     If the user does not have Delete Issue Permission, Jira will prevent the user from deleting the issue.    !image-2020-06-22-16-43-13-728.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Linking and Epic to an issue using keyboard will display an error that the epic doesn't exist"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If you try to select an epic on the Old Issue View using only the keyboard (arrows and """"Enter"""") will display an error that the epic does not exists.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Access an issue without an epic link  # Press """".""""  # Type """"epic link"""" and press """"Enter""""  # Start typing the name of the epic  # Use Arrows' keysboard to move down to desired epic  # Press Enter to accept desired epic (+Don't user the mouse+)    h3. Expected Results  Jira should be able to identify the epic    h3. Actual Results  Jira will display an error:       h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Refine Filters Modal dialog hides buttons on Cumulative Flow report"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}  *NOTE:* This bug report is for *JIRA Software Cloud*. Using *JIRA Software Server*? [See the corresponding bug report|http://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/JSWSERVER-13334].  {panel}  h3. Summary    On the [Cumulative Flow Diagram |https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/AGILE/Viewing+the+Cumulative+Flow+Diagram] adding quick filters, Columns, and Swimlanes extends the overall length of the *Refine Reports* report filter drop down modal and will push the apply and cancel buttons beyond the window, with no option to scroll down.  h3. Environment    Verified with:   * Chrome Version 71.0.3578.98 (Official Build) (64-bit)    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create multiple [quick filters |https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/AGILE/Configuring+Quick+Filters], [Columns |https://confluence.atlassian.com/agile/<USER>agile-user-s-guide/<USER>agile-tutorials/tutorial-adding-a-column-to-a-board], and [Swimlanes |https://confluence.atlassian.com/agile/<USER>agile-user-s-guide/configuring-a-board/configuring-swimlanes] in the board configuration   # on [Cumulative Flow Diagram |https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/AGILE/Viewing+the+Cumulative+Flow+Diagram]   # Select Refine Report dropdown    h3. Expected Results    Ability to scroll if the options exceed the available Space  h3. Actual Results    The *apply* and *Cancel* buttons are cut off along with any options that extend below the window:    !Screen Shot 2019-01-29 at 9.51.32.png|thumbnail!  h3. Notes    This impacts users with smaller screens and Lower resolution settings the most, but also impacts anyone with complex boards using many columns, with multiple swimlanes and then use many quick filters to better organize the complex board.  h3. Workaround   * Zoom out in the browser (then close and reopen """"Refine Report"""" dropdown) until the apply option is visible and can be selected, or press *esc* to close the dialogue to cancel any setting changes   * Type tab key to switch the selected filter so that you can click the *apply* or *cancel* buttons"""

summary: "Open Pull Requests not showing as ""Warning"" on Releases"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Open Pull Requests not showing as a Warning on Releases.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add tickets to a Version.   # On Bitbucket, create a pull request.   # Let this pull request open.    h3. Expected Results    Even if the ticket is closed, if the pull request is opened, it will show a Warning on Releases' page as mentioned on the documentation:    https://support.atlassian.com/<USER>software-cloud/docs/use-the-release-page-to-check-the-progress-of-a-version/  h3. Actual Results    It doesn't show warnings about Pull requests.    !Screen Shot 2020-07-31 at 12.51.46.png|width=503,height=160!    !Screen Shot 2020-07-31 at 12.52.15.png|width=169,height=135!    !Screen Shot 2020-07-31 at 14.13.01.png|width=476,height=253!  h3. Workaround       Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Review changes in a plan disappeared without commits in Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    With a new plan created changes to labels via ARJ are not properly reflected in the changes popup.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # No ways to repro an issue locally were found. Customer has one test plan with 20k issues they use to learn and test ARJ. Please check the recorded support video call with the customer   # Requested access to the customer's ARJ plan (see GSAC issue)    h3. Expected Results    Changes made to labels are expected to be visible on Review Changes popup of ARJ and, when committed, should be saved on affected Jira issues.  h3. Actual Results    Even counter shows increasing amount of changes, once clicked Review Pending Changes popup is empty and no changes may be saved.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Selecting ""Hide Menu"" at any Agile Report in JSW Classic Projects hide the Report Name"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Selecting """"Hide Menu"""" at any Agile Report in JSW Classic Projects hide the Report Name    h3. Steps to Reproduce  1) Start in a Kanban or Scrum project in Jira.  2) Click """"Reports"""" in the left navbar.  3) Select a report from the """"Agile"""" category.  5) In the (topmost) 3-dots menu on the right, select """"Hide menus"""" (or press 'z').  6) Note that the report title disappears, but no vertical height change occurs.    h3. Expected Results  The report name should still be visible    h3. Actual Results  the report title disappears   !image-2020-08-03-16-05-59-947.png|thumbnail!    !image-2020-08-03-16-06-17-463.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Once a rule edits the issue type and then tries to edit a field, Jira will check if the field context scheme is applied to the original issue type instead of to the new issue type"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If the issue type of an issue is edited through an Automation rule and right after this Edit Issue fields action you have another Edit Issue field action, if the context of this field is applied only to the original issue type, Jira won't be able to update the it.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  1) Create a custom field and edit its context to Bug issue type (as an example);  2) Build an Automation rule with the structure below:  * TRIGGER: Issue created  * ACTION: Log action with {{{{issue.issuetype.name}}}}  * ACTION: Edit issue field action, setting issue type value as Bug  * ACTION: Re-fetch issue data  * ACTION: Log action with {{{{ssue.issuetype.name}}}}  * ACTION: Edit issue field action, setting any value to the just created custom field    !image-2020-08-04-16-21-24-345.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results    If the fields added to the last Edit Issue Fields are added to the Edit Screen for Bug issue type AND its context is applied to Bug issue type, all the fields should be updated.    h3. Actual Results    Even though the issue type is successfully edited (what we can confirm through the Log Actions), the following error message is shown:        h3. Workaround    To fix this, besides applying the custom field context to the new issue type (in case of the above example, Bug), you'll need to also apply this to the original issue type of the issue (in case of the above example, Task).    !image-2020-08-04-16-26-17-565.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Dragging and dropping an issue in a plan sometimes results in the issue ending up in an unexpected place"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When dragging-and-dropping issues in a plan to re-rank them there can sometimes be unexpected results and the issue does not always end up where you dropped it. It is possible that the feature is overly sensitive to exactly where the issue is dropped.    Please note: this is an intermittent problem with no definitive test case to reproduce the problem.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan with multiple issues   # Drag and drop issues to re-rank them    h3. Expected Results    The issue should *always* end up where it was dropped  h3. Actual Results    Sometime the issue ends up in a random place and it is hard to find.  h3. Workaround    Instead of using drag-and-drop to re-rank use the bulk rank operation and set the new location to be relative to another issue in the plan."""

summary: "Version start and release dates are not respecting the date format"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Version start and release dates are not respecting the date format    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # In """"General configuration"""" -> """"Advanced settings"""", set as the following.  ** <USER>date.picker.java.format: {{yyyy/MM/dd}}  ** <USER>date.picker.javascript.format: {{%y/%b/%e}}  # Navigate to a classic scrum project and click """"Releases"""" from the sidebar  # Click """"Create version""""  # Click the """"Start Date"""" box and select a random date    h3. Expected Results  The date is shown in the {{yyyy/MM/dd}} format    h3. Actual Results  The date is shown in {{MM/DD/yyyy}} format    h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Auto-scheduling on issues with dependency conflicts but no estimates are not rescheduled"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Auto-scheduling only considers issues with estimates. However, when inter-dependant issues have manually been assigned dates that conflict (i.e. the issue B depends on issue A but issue B has been scheduled to start before issue A has completed) the auto-schedule algorithm does not adjust those dates if the issues do not have estimates.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan   # Create Issue A   # Create Issue B   # Make Issue B depend on Issue A   # Set start and end dates on both issues so that Issue B is scheduled to start before Issue A is scheduled to end   # Ensure that no estimates are set on either issue   # Run auto-schedule    h3. Expected Results    The scheduling algorithm should recognise the dependency and conflicting dates and adjust them accordingly.  h3. Actual Results    The dates for the issues are not changed  h3. Workaround    Add estimates to the issues."""

summary: "Unable to create a repository shortcut using the bitbucket org name."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira is unable to resolve bitbucket org name when creating a repository shortcut in Jira software.       h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Try to create a repository shortcut with https://bitbucket.org/<org name>/<project repository>   # Even though the DVCS account is integrated, it still ask to integrate bitbucket account.  However, when creating a repository shortcut with bitbucket UUID https://bitbucket.org/<bitbucket UUID>/<project repository> the shortcut is created successfully.     h3. Expected Results    As per the instruction mentioned while creating a repository shortcut, the shortcut should be created by using https://bitbucket.org/<org name>/<project repository> which is currently not working.    h3. Actual Results    The repository shortcut is only created when using the bitbucket UUID instead of org name.     h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Sprints can appear out of order when multiple issue sources contain the same sprint"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a Plan is using more than 1 issue sources that all share the same Sprints, if the target dates are not provided, future Sprints can appear out of order and won't be scheduled in chronological order.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add multiple issue sources that all share the same Sprints   # Make sure the target dates are empty so the Plan can pull the Sprint dates instead    h3. Expected Results  The ordering and scheduling should be done in chronological order, taking into account which Sprints were created first along with the Sprint iteration length.    h3. Actual Results    The scheduling is done in random order and sometimes the next coming Sprint can be scheduled last. In the example below, the next coming Sprint 65 is scheduled after 66 and 67.     !sprint_65_.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Setting an issue in the past on a kanban plan distributes effort into the past"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using Kanban for a plan the estimates of an issue can be consumed from weeks in the past. This is different from the scrum behaviour where all capacity is consumed from the current or future sprint. This presents a problem when the issue goes from a past iteration into a future iteration.     +_Issue in past sprint does not consume capacity:_+    !Screen Shot 2020-08-14 at 10.25.35 am.png|width=529,height=127!    +_Issue in past iteration week consume capacity:_+    _!Screen Shot 2020-08-14 at 10.24.40 am.png|width=529,height=136!_    The complexity here is whether or not we are the user is reducing the estimate to account for latest estimates. When users log working hours the estimate will be reduced but the capacity is still removed from past sprints. This means that we effectively reduce the remaining capacity twice.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a plan that uses hour based estimation   # Create a Kanban team   # Group the roadmap view by team and show capacity on the timeline   # Create an issue and give it an estimate    # Set the start and end dates of the issue so that it is in a week that is in the past    h3. Expected Results    Capacity should only be taken from the current and future weeks. The issue should not consume capacity from past weeks.  h3. Actual Results    The capacity of weeks in the past is consumed.  h3. Workaround    Reduce the estimate to reflect the time assigned to past weeks (this is not an ideal solution)."""

summary: "Rule executions not working correctly on Roadmaps (Next-gen)"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When clicking on Rule executions to run a manually triggered rule through the Roadmap, the sidebar gets blank and doesn't load.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # On a next-gen software project, create a manually triggered automation rule.   # On a Roadmap, click on the first Epic and click on Rule executions.    h3. Expected Results    It shows the option to select and run the manually triggered rule.  h3. Actual Results    The sidebar shows blank.      If it loads, it's not possible to select the automation.  h3. Workaround    Open the Epic on a new tab and run the automation."""

Steps to Reproduce   # Have next-gen project type and create 250+ issues adding them to the backlog   # Open Next-gen backlog    h3. Expected Results  Next-gen backlog would *initially load* in a similar time to classic boards with 300 cards    h3. Actual Results  The backlog loads very slow.    h3. Workaround   # *Reduce number of cards on the backlog*   # *Switch to Classic projects and migrate issues over*"""

summary: "Editing a multiline text field in old issue view, shows old data unless we do explicit browser refresh."

Steps to Reproduce   # Create any issue which has a custom multiline text field.   # Navigate to the old issue view.   # Put some value into this text field and click the tick symbol. The new value should show up in the text field. Instead, it still shows the old value.   # Refresh the browser. The new value will appear in the text field.    h3. Expected Results    The data in the multiline text field should get auto refreshed once we enter the data and click the tick symbol.  h3. Actual Results    The data in the multiline text field is not getting auto refreshed.    h3. Workaround    Refresh the browser."""

summary: "Epic Link name is Truncated in New Issue view"

Hovering on the Epic Link field also does not show complete name. This makes it inconvenient to figure out which EPIC it is if the names are similar.     !image-2020-08-20-18-54-00-503.png|width=424,height=216!  h3. Workaround    As of now, the only way Epic Name could be seen completely is through old issue view.    !image-2020-08-20-18-54-11-920.png|width=259,height=122!"""

summary: "Kanban team shows sum of individual team member weekly hours"

summary: "Burnup Chart calculates completed work and work scope incorrectly for issues transitioned to final statuses before the sprint start and then added to the sprint after it"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Burnup Chart calculates the *Completed work* and *Work scope* based on the story points when issues are added to the sprint, completed (transitioned to a status mapped to the last columns), and reopened (transitioned to a status not mapped to the last column).    Currently, if an issue is already on a status mapped to the last column before the sprints starts, and it is added to the sprint, and then reopened, the report will not decrease the completed work, and will sum up the issue's story points to the work scope again.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    Here's an example I made that shows how to replicate the problem:  * Created Sprint *Moe sprint*;  * Created 3 issues;  ** *BAS-2088* with 10 story points  ** *BAS-2087* with 10 story points  ** *BAS-2089* with 10 story points  * Added BAS-2088 and BAS-2087 to the sprint;  * Configured the board columns so the status *IN REVIEW* is at the last column;  * Transitioned *BAS-2089 to* *IN REVIEW*;  * Started sprint;  ** First event logged in the report   !Screen Shot 2020-09-17 at 09.12.47.png|thumbnail!   * Added BAS-2089 to the sprint:  ** Event logged in the report. Increasing Completed work and Work scope    !Screen Shot 2020-09-17 at 09.13.59.png|thumbnail!   * Transitioned BAS-2089 to *IN PROGRESS*  ** Event logged in the report. Completed work remained the same (x), Work scope increased (x):   !Screen Shot 2020-09-17 at 09.15.43.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results    When reopening *BAS-2089*, the *Completed work* should have been decreased (in that case, to 0). And the *Work scope* should have remained the same.    h3. Actual Results    *Completed work* remained the same, and the *Work scope* was increased again, summed up by the current amount of story points in the issue.     h3. Workaround    None at the moment."""

Expected Results  Resolution field should be displayed in the issue view.    h3. Actual Results  Resolution field is not visible in the issue view    h3. Workaround  Use the Jira old issue view."""

summary: "As a Jira Software user, I want the issues to be moved back to the backlog when disabling and enabling it"
description: """If you have the backlog enabled in a next-gen board and there are issues there when you disable the backlog, all the issues are moved to the board but if you re-enable the backlog, the issues will remain on the board and you lose the information about issues you had in the backlog. There's no way to recover your backlog.    As a Jira Software user, I want the issues that used to be in the backlog when it was active to be moved back to it when re-enabling the backlog.    Also, the user should be notified that the issues will be moved to the board when disabling the backlog in next-gen projects."""

summary: "When accessing the summary view of a Software project it says the project type is Business"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The summary view of a Software project is accessible via URL, but not via the project sidebar. When accessing the Summary it says the project type is business.    (!) This only happens in some instances.    This issue brings problems to users that use the Summary as the project home page. There are links missing in the left nav panel, e.g. Boards.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access a Software project.   # Navigate to [https://domain.atlassian.net/projects/pkey/summary].   ** You will have access to the project's summary page.   #  See the project type. It is written that the project is Business.    h3. Expected Results    It should say that it is a Software Project.  h3. Actual Results    It says that it is a Business Project  h3. Workaround    There are no workarounds at the moment."""

summary: "Dependency changes don't show up in review changes dialog in Advanced Roadmaps"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Dependency changes don't show up in review changes dialog in Advanced Roadmaps  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open advanced roadmaps plan   # change an issue to have a dependency   # Open save to Jira dialog    h3. Expected Results    Dependency changes shows in the save dialog when opened  h3. Actual Results    No dependency related changes are shown  h3. Workaround  Create and Edit dependencies from the issue view in Jira"""

summary: "Status button not readable with JAWS Screen Reader"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The currently selected status option is not read to the user when using JAWS Screen Reader. When assigning requests, there is no indication if the status has been successfully changed to 'In Progress' for example, and navigating back to the button does not read this text either.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Using the screen reader go to a request;   # Try to change the status;    h3. Expected Results    The screen reader shows the statuses as expected.  h3. Actual Results    The screen reader doesn't show the statuses.  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Epic link can't be set during creation if the field is required"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If the *Epic link* field is required then this value cannot be set for Story issues while being created in the Plan.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a *Field Required Validator* in the create the transition of your Workflow for a Story issue type and publish   # Create a Story in your Plan under an Epic or while setting the *Parent* field    h3. Expected Results    The Epic link value is already provided thus the validation should pass and the issue should be created.    h3. Actual Results    The validation fails and the issue cannot be created as if there is no Epic link value provided when Advanced Roadmap is supposed to do this automatically when creating a Story under an Epic.    Presumably, this is happening because the workflow validator can't distinguish between the *Parent link* and *Epic link* fields.    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available. The Story will have to be created in JIRA and not in your Plan."""

summary: "The Epic child of an Initiative did not load the Initiative information when we click to back from Epic to the Initiative via issue key."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Epic child of an *Initiative* did not load the *Initiative* information when we click to back from Epic to the *Initiative* via issue key.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an issue type """"*Initiative*"""".   # Access the """"*Initiative*"""".   # Then add an issue type """"*Epic*"""" under the Initiative as """"*Child issue*""""   # Then click on the """"*Summary*"""" of the *Epic*, under the *Initiative*.   !image-2020-09-28-16-52-53-246.png|width=456,height=356!   # At this point, we will be on the *Initiative* > *Epic*.   # Click on the """"*Initiative*"""" key as follows below:   !screenshot-4.png|width=378,height=75!    h3. Expected Results    We should be redirected to the *Initiative*.  h3. Actual Results    The URL will be updated with """"*Initiative*"""" key, however, the main panel will continue with Epic information.   !screenshot-5.png|width=703,height=440!  h3. Workaround   * When we will open the """"*Epic*"""" if we click on the issue key instead of the summary the redirection will work."""

summary: "Scrolling on issue preview goes up and down very fast in a loop when we change zoom to 90%"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Uncontrollable scrolling on issue preview goes down and up very fast.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Decrease the zoom into your browser from 100% to 90%.  # Open the issue preview.  # Scroll down at the end of the issue preview.    * Test made on Chrome.  * I've used a screen set with resolution 1440X900.    h3. Expected Results  * The scroll should stop at the end of the page without scrolling up and down without control.    h3. Actual Results  * When we scroll at the end of the page the scroll will go up and down very fast. This behavior will keep in a loop.  * [Bug's video.|https://share.getcloudapp.com/d5upxrA7]    h3. Workaround  Change the zoom to 100%.  """

summary: "Not able to create new sprints If a board has 2 projects in the filter and one is archived"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Not able to create new sprints If a board has 2 projects in the filter and one of those is archived even if they are using the same permission scheme    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # In a board put 2 projects in the filter using the same permission scheme   # Grant the manage sprint permission to a basic user   # Archive one of the projects   # In the board with the two projects try to create a new sprint    h3. Expected Results  The sprint should be able to be created as the user has the manage sprint permission in both projects (they are using the same scheme)    h3. Actual Results    The user is not able to create sprints showing the message that the manage sprint permission is needed    h3. Workaround  Remove the archived project from the filter"""

summary: "Time tracking, working hours per day is not respected by Advanced Roadmaps"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Time tracking, working hours per day is not respected by Advanced Roadmaps    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set 7h working hours per day in time tracking   !Screenshot 2020-09-30 at 14.00.42.png|thumbnail!    # Set Remaining Time in Jira to 35h. In the Jira issue, it is shown as 1w or 35h, which is fine.   # In the plan, it should show 5d. But shows 4.375d which translates to 8h per day.   !Screenshot 2020-09-30 at 14.01.06.png|thumbnail!    !Screenshot 2020-09-30 at 14.01.15.png|thumbnail!    !Screenshot 2020-09-30 at 14.01.30.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  In the plan, it should be shown as 35h or 5d. It should follow the time tracking settings.    h3. Actual Results  In the plan, it translates to 8h per day. It is following the default time tracking settings.      h3. Workaround  No workaround  """

summary: "When opening the smart commit information from the development panel in a new tab, it loads the issue on the old issue view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When opening the smart commit information from the development panel in a new tab, it loads the issue on the old issue view    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Make sure to have an issue with smart commits  # Right click at the smart commit and select to open in a new tab    See the video reproducing the issue: https://share.getcloudapp.com/xQuApr6Z    h3. Expected Results  The new issue view must be kept while opening the development panel information in a new tab    h3. Actual Results  It opens a new tab with the old issue view    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Shortcuts G+A and G+H redirects to a 404 page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using the shortcuts G+A or G+H, instead of redirect to the recently visited agile board, it shows a 404 page or a white page with the error """"We can't find the page you're looking for"""".   [Using Keyboard Shortcuts|https://confluence.atlassian.com/agile066/<USER>agile-user-s-guide/using-keyboard-shortcuts]   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # On a dashboard, type G+A or G+H.    h3. Expected Results    It will redirect to the last viewed agile board.  h3. Actual Results    It redirects to a 404 page.    !Screen Shot 2020-10-01 at 16.41.39.png|width=503,height=306!    !Screen Shot 2020-10-01 at 16.41.17.png|width=527,height=484!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Print cards options in Jira backlog are not working. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Print cards options in the Jira backlog are not working.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Navigate to Jira Software Backlog.  # Select the print card options from the three dots.     A new page opens with the list and has options card size and select. These options don’t work and cannot be clicked. The blue *print* button doesn't work either.     h3. Expected Results    These buttons should be accessible to select the card size.     h3. Actual Results    Options are not functioning.     h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available  """

summary: "Parent - Subtask relationship is not showing on Next-gen projects when issues are imported from CSV"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When you import a parent issue like a story with its subtasks using the external system import CSV the subtasks are not showing inside the parent issue, however, when you do an advance search the subtasks show a parent relation.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Export or create a CSV file with a parent issue and its subtasks   # Use the external system import to import the CSV file into a Next-gen project  https://share.getcloudapp.com/NQu1e0wm    h3. Expected Results  The subtasks should be displayed inside the parent ticket or in the board when grouped by subtask    h3. Actual Results  No subtasks are displayed in the parent ticket just in the advanced search      h3. Workaround  Recreate the subtasks or first import all the issues into a Classic project and then move them to a Next-gen  Note: Trying to move them to reassign the parent is not working."""

summary: "Next-gen quick filters should persist"
description: """h3. Summary    Next-gen projects have quick filter that let you filter by issues assigned to yourself.     But, differently from Classic projects, currently, the filters do not persist when navigating between the Backlog and the board view.     See the recording:  [^Quick filter behaviour.mp4]     h3. Suggestion    Make the Next-gen quick filters persist so the user does not need to apply it every time they navigate back and forth."""

summary: "Using number sign/hash ""#"" in the project name makes Workload Pie Chart Report to not load"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When the project has a hash """"#"""" in the name the Workload Pie Chart Report does not open.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Put a hash """"#"""" in a project's name   # Try to open the Workload Pie Chart Report    h3. Expected Results  The report should open normally    h3. Actual Results  The page does not load      h3. Workaround  Remove the hash """"#"""" from the project name"""

summary: "Days in column"
description: """As a user, I want to be able to find out how many days a given card has been in a column in a next-gen project, so that I can identify slow-moving issues.     Currently this can be done in Classic projects (see image  !image-2020-10-07-10-54-00-481.png|thumbnail! ) under Board Settings > Card layout. I wish to have this feature in next-gen projects as well. """

summary: "After linking a page to a sprint, the count is not updated."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  After linking a page to a Sprint, the page count is not updated. It always show 0.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to Board > Backlog  # Attempt to link a Confluence page to the sprint as per [Link a Confluence page to a sprint|https://support.atlassian.com/<USER>software-cloud/docs/link-a-confluence-page-to-a-sprint/]    h3. Expected Results  The count on """"Linked page"""" should be updated.    h3. Actual Results  The count on """"Linked page"""" should be not updated. It always show 0, no matter how many pages are linked.   !image-2020-10-07-11-09-24-323.png|thumbnail!  !image-2020-10-07-11-10-04-766.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Advanced Roadmaps fields (Team, Parent Link) don't show up in Automation For Jira"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Team and Parent Link are not available anymore in the list of fields for automation rules.    Expected behavior:  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # On a project go to Project settings/Automation     # Create a rule and type “Team”    h3. Expected Results   * Team should be in the fields list    h3. Actual Results   * Team does not appear in the list as an option to choose    h3. Workaround    As a workaround, you might use the [Advanced field editing - JSON|https://support.atlassian.com/<USER>software-cloud/docs/advanced-field-editing-json/] option to define the value of the Team field. The _Edit Issue fields_ action will then look like below:        PS: this is just an example, you must see how the fields are set in your Jira cloud site and adjust it accordingly."""

summary: "Opening a jira ticket after transitioning in Kanban sidebar,fails to open with a single click"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    On a kanban board, when you are transitioning a ticket from one column to another, and then with a single click when you try to open the ticket in the sidebar, it didn’t open, however with a 3rd click, the issues get opened in the sidebar. I am attaching a quick video demonstrating the same behavior.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to a Kanban board and transition a ticket from one column to another.   # After transitioning, open the ticket in the sidebar.    h3. Expected Results  Ticket will open in the sidebar with a single click    h3. Actual Results  After transitioning the ticket, it should open in the sidebar with a single click    h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Having a board with a filter with ORDER BY Rank ASC only will cause the plan to not return any issue"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Having a board with a filter with ORDER BY Rank ASC only will cause the plan to not return any issue    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a filter with the condition    # Create a board using this filter  # Create a plan and use this board as issue source    h3. Expected Results  All the issues should be returned in the plan or at the issue selection step    h3. Actual Results  The plan doesn't identify any issue    h3. Workaround  Consider using filter conditions to specify the issues that will be returned from the filter query"""

summary: "Issue count does not appear in board container header when Sprint feature is disabled"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Issue count does not appear in the board container header when Sprint feature is disabled in next-gen projects.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a next-gen project using kanban template (or disable Sprint feature in the features page)   # Make sure backlog is enabled in features page   # Go to the backlog page   # Look at the board container header    h3. Expected Results    The issue count should be appearing on the top left part of the board container header (just like if Sprint was enabled)    Example below is for Sprint enabled project.    !image-2020-10-02-10-57-09-129.png!  h3. Actual Results    There is no issue count appearing on the top left part of the board container header.    !image-2020-10-02-10-58-40-924.png!  h3. Workaround    No workaround"""

summary: "Pending changes from an archived project breaks the Plan"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    By having a pending change, specifically, a *Create issue* event in your Plan that's linked to an archived Project, attempting to *Review changes* will cause it to break and get a """"*Something's gone wrong*"""" error.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create a Plan and 2 Projects (JPO1 and JPO2)  # Add JPO1 and JPO2 to the Issue sources  # In the Plan, create an Issue for JPO2 directly in the scope view  # Make additional random changes to issues from JPO1 in the scope view  # Archive the JPO2 Project  # Head back to the Plan and *Review changes*    h3. Expected Results    The changes in creating a new issue should either be committed or automatically discarded since the Project is archived.    h3. Actual Results    You get an error when clicking on *Review changes*   !Synology_2021_-_Advanced_Roadmaps_-_Jira.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    You must discard the pending change by first un-archiving the Project in question, manually discard the change from the *Review changes* dialogue then save changes. Once done you can archive the Project again."""

summary: "Business project Boards aren't displayed under the all Boards page"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    At the """"all"""" Boards page the Business project Boards are not displayed  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Business project   # Access the Boards page   [https://yourcloud.atlassian.net/<USER>boards?contains=]    # The Business project Board is not displayed    [^screencast-starless.atlassian.net-2020.10.07-12_01_33.webm]  h3. Expected Results    All Boards should be displayed  h3. Actual Results    Business project Boards are not displayed  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Advanced Roadmaps plan includes more issues than selected during plan creation"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Advanced Roadmaps plan includes more issues than selected during plan creation    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create 3 issues and close one  # Create a filter that returns the 3   # Create plan   # Select 1 issue  # Load the plan and the issue completed will be displayed without being selected    h3. Expected Results  Only selected issues are displayed     h3. Actual Results  Done issues are not displayed during plan creation and so they are not excluded.    h3. Workaround  Update the plan to not display done issues or update the plan's filter to exclude the same."""

summary: "Jira Mac Native app fails using macOs Big Sur"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Jira Mac Native app fails using macOs Big Sur  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Try to login using macOS Big Sur    h3. Expected Results  Login successfully   h3. Actual Results  Ending loop to login again    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Days Remaining in Sprint gadget fails to redirect to the Board when using the Sprint link"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Days Remaining in Sprint gadget fails to redirect to the Board when using the Sprint link if the *Next Sprint Due (auto)* parameter is used.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create a dashboard and add the Days Remaining in Sprint gadget;  # Select a Board and when selecting the Sprint use the *Next Sprint Due (auto)*;  # Select the Show Sprint Name option;  # Click on the Sprint name in the gadget.    Gadget set up example:  !Image 2020-10-07 at 12.38.53 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results    The Sprint will be properly loaded on the board.    h3. Actual Results    The following URL will be used and the board will open with a blank page.      h3. Workaround    Manually select the desired Sprint in the gadget configuration."""

summary: "Searching for custom fields that share name using JQL does not include all of the fields."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Customers report that searching for custom fields that share the same name using advanced searching does not show all of the fields unless they're different field types.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Have two or more custom fields named the same and share the same field type.   # Perform a JQL search with the field name. The autocomplete suggestions only show a single field and not all of the possible fields as before.    h3. Expected Results    Previously the autocomplete suggestions showed the fields with their field IDs for duplicates and all of them could be added in the JQL search. This is the expected behavior.    h3. Actual Results  The completion suggestion shows only one of the duplicated fields with no ID. This leads to JQL searches dismissing duplicate fields unless the CF ID is used.    h3. Workaround  * Perform JQL searches using the custom field ID.  ** To obtain the custom field IDs please visit your instance's Custom Field configuration page by navigating to *Jira settings* > *Issues* > *Custom Fields* and find the fields in the list. Then click on the three dots at the end of the row for each field and click Edit details. In the resulting page you'll find the Custom Field ID at the end of the browser's URL.  * Change the names of the custom fields. This will not impact the existing data in the issues that use the field."""

summary: "Next-gen boards in classic projects"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    A next-gen board appears in a classic project. The board doesn't load and an error message is shown.    !image-2020-10-15-09-33-01-858.png|width=505,height=353!  h3. Steps to Reproduce    n/a  h3. Expected Results    Next-gen boards should not be in classic projects.    And vice versa, classic boards should not be in next-gen projects.  h3. Actual Results    Next-gen boards appear in classic projects.    If you encounter this problem please contact support at https://support.atlassian.com/contact"""

summary: "Project role for Next-gen *Collaborate on issues (a set of permissions)* cannot be used for collaboration."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Project role for Next-gen *Collaborate on issues (a set of permissions)* doesn't work as mentioned in the [document|https://support.atlassian.com/<USER>software-cloud/docs/next-gen-permissions/].    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a user to site and provide only site access and *_no_* product access.    # Add this user to a custom group, and map this group to the *Collaborate on issues (a set of permissions)* project role of next-gen project.   # Tag/mention this user in one of the issue of this next-gen project so that the user is notified.   # User gets a notification email. When user click on *_View issue_* from the notification, user cannot access the site and prompts user to *request product access*.  # This way, user is unable to edit/delete own comments & attachments and cannot *view watchers* as well. User can only add comments and attachments by replying to the email notification that is received.   # However when impersonated the user, user is able to access the project and its issues, and. can also add/edit/delete own comments & attachments.    h3. Expected Results    * User without having the product access, should be able to access the cloud site and the respective project and its issues.    h3. Actual Results    * User is unable to access the site and is prompted to *request product access*    h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: """More"" menu doesn't close when item is clicked."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  """"More"""" menu doesn't close when item is clicked    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Zoomed into the browser so as to view the *More* options.  * I click on More option and selects the App.  * The drop down is still available even when navigated to the app page.  * Subsequent clicks hides the drop down list.   * When selected another app, the drop down disappeared.   |Note: This can be replicated only from App's drop down list.|    h3. Expected Results  The 'More' menu should be disappeared.     h3. Actual Results    'More' menu is still visible.    h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: " ‘Issues Without Parent Epics’ checkbox is always ticked in “Confirm what’s in scope” section"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When editing the Issue Source from any Plan, the ‘Issues Without Parent Epics’ checkbox will be always ticked, even if you uncheck it and saved this configuration.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create or Edit a Plan   # Go to the “Confirm what’s in scope” section   # Uncheck the ‘Issues Without Parent Epics’ box.  # Save  # Go back to edit the Issue Source once again and you'll see the 'Issues Without Parent Epics’ box ticked.    h3. Expected Results  'Issues Without Parent Epics’ box is unchecked    h3. Actual Results  'Issues Without Parent Epics’ box is ticked    h3. Workaround  Uncheck the checkbox and Save it again if you notice new issues not covered by epics appearing on your plan"""

summary: "Margin padding in new issue view restricts view on standard monitor sizes"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  There are various *padding-right* and *padding-left* margins in the CSS layout throughout the new issue view, that cause excess whitespace on standard monitor layouts and does not allow the user to utilize the scaling options that are integrated into the code, the scaling gets worse on wider aspect ratio monitors as well as zooming out, in the browser zoom settings, beyond 100%.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # enable New issue view  # Look at any issue from a 16:9 aspect ratio monitor    h3. Expected Results  The margin paddings scale to meet at the very minimum the industry-standard 16:9 aspect ratio 1080p monitor sizes, but 100% scaling prefered for custom monitor solutions or users that prefer to zoom out for extended screen real-estate    h3. Actual Results  As an Example, I am Looking at:      there is a padding set to scale to 100% but also restricted to a maximum of 1400px:      Looks like this:  !Screen Shot 2020-10-15 at 2.47.58 PM.png|thumbnail!    Because of this setting on an industry-standard 16:9 aspect ratio 1080p monitor, there is already an excessive amount of whitespace of 279px for the margin at padding-right (noting padding-left adds an additional 279px of whitespace), and would require scaling up to 1679px to reach the 100% scale setting for this section *noting standard monitor size with default zoom levels at 100%* so this should meet the max 100% scale at this setting    h3.Notes:  # The suggested solution here is to remove 1400px restrictions to all scalable margins  # Alternatively, scale the paddings up 300px to handle at a minimum 16:9 aspect ratio 1080p monitors by increasing all 1400px limitaions to a 1700px restriction    h3. Workaround  Disable the new issue view under your user avatar > personal settings  and deselect the checkbox for the option """"New Jira issue view"""":  !Screen Shot 2020-10-15 at 2.53.32 PM.png|thumbnail!"""

summary: "Hovering over a relative dates in the detail dialog does not show absolute date"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Hovering over a relative dates in the detail dialog does not show absolute date    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # View an issue which contains development or release information.   # Open the development dialog to view data that has a relative date (e.g. Pull requests, Builds, Deployments)    h3. Expected Results  When hovering over the relative date, an absolute date should be shown.    h3. Actual Results  There is no tooltip on hover.    h3. Workaround    Click the link to view the entity in its native tool."""

summary: "If we have only ""Development"" into ""Issue layout"" set as ""HIDE WHEN EMPTY"" the field will not appear on the New Jira Issue view if only GitHub is integrated"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If we have only """"*Development*"""" into """"*Issue layout*"""" set as """"*HIDE WHEN EMPTY*"""" the field will not appear on the New Jira Issue view, even if the field has information.  !image-2020-10-16-12-49-43-371.png|width=458,height=211!  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open the issue layout.   # Keep only """"*Development*"""" under the section """"*Hide when empty*"""".   # Access an issue that has GitHub information.    h3. Expected Results  The Development field should appear on the right panel.  h3. Actual Results    When we have the field under """"*Hide when empty*"""" the GET to retrieve the information will appear only after the """"*Show more*"""" option. So, if we don't have another field, the """"*Show more*"""" will not appear and the GET won't occur. For more information *[click here|https://share.getcloudapp.com/o0umevXk]*.   h3. Workaround    Under Issue layout configuration, move the field from *Hide when empty* to *Context fields*.   """

summary: "Resolved tickets are not shown in Scope page and are still being added to the Plan"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When setting up a plan, selecting the issue source, in the Scope pages, resolved issues are not shown in this page, but they are added to the plan. You cannot deselect them, not even using the 'deselect all' option.    If you click 'deselect all' and choose some issues, the resolved tickets are added to the plan along with the tickets you selected.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # You have resolved tickets that you don't want in your plan  # These resolved tickets never show in the 'Scope' page, where you select/deselect the issues you want/don't want  # The issues are include by default, so when you hit 'deselect all' you're not actually deselecting these ones that were not included in the Scope page  # Because they were not deselected, they show in the Plan    h3. Expected Results  Only the selected issues at the Confirm what’s in scope should be included to the plan     h3. Actual Results  Resolved tickets are still being added to the Plan    h3. Workaround  You can create a filter without these tickets and select it as issue source, rather than the project or  board."""

summary: "Commits on the Jira issue view are displayed in the wrong date format."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When viewing the Commits tab on Jira issue view, the date is formatted in mm/dd/yyyy format, regardless of the Date/time format settings    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Open an issue that contains commits in the development panel.  # Click the commits to view the dialog.    h3. Expected Results  The date is formatted using the dates formatted defined in the System -> Look and Feel settings.    h3. Actual Results  The date is formatted in mm/dd/yyyy format.      h3. Workaround  Click the link to view the entity in its native tool.  """

summary: "Velocity Chart is not an option under Personal board"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Velocity Chart is not an option under Personal board  h3. Steps to Reproduce   - Create a Scrum board with any filter and put it under Personal location instead of putting it under a project, except velocity chart all other graphs, charts will be available under the Reports section.     - Create a Kanban board with any filter and put it under Personal location instead of putting it under a project, except velocity chart all other graphs, charts will be available under the Reports section as per the Kanban board.     *    ** This is how it looks like when viewed under a project      !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    ** Velocity Chart is not an option when viewed under a personal board.     !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!   h3. Expected Results   - We should see an option called *Velocity Chart* under reports unlike all other Scrum or Kanban board when the board is located under a project    h3. Actual Results   - We do not see any *Velocity Chart* option under the Reports section    h3. Workaround       Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "FixVersion field is shown then hidden when a user without Resolve issue permission views an issue"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Using the New Issue View, when the  fields are is configured so that the *""""FixVersions""""* field is set above the """"HIDE WHEN EMPTY"""" line as a *""""Context Field""""* so that the field is forced to be shown when empty, the field will temporarily load when initially loading an issue but disappear once fully loaded    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Add the """"Fix Versions"""" field above the """"HIDE WHEN EMPTY"""" line as a *""""Context Field""""* to a project  # View an issue that does not have a value for FixVersions, as a user that does not have the Resolve Issue permissions  # you can continually refresh the page to see it occur (happens on each page load)    h3. Expected Results  FixVersions field is displayed but empty. As the field is set as a context field and should be displayed regardless of having a value or not    h3. Actual Results  The FixVersions field is temporarily displayed while the page source is loading, and disappears from the view once the screen is fully loaded,      h3. Note:  If you add a value to the """"FixVersions"""" field as a user with resolve issue permissions, the field is displayed for the alternate user as though it were still added to the """"HIDE WHEN EMPTY"""" section.    h3. Workaround  The user requires the """"Resolve issue"""" permissions for the FixVersions field to display correctly in the current state.  If permissions cannot be altered for the user, there is no known workaround for this behavior."""

summary: "Incorrect information on Advanced Roadmaps documentation"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In the [Sharing Roadmaps page for the Advanced Roadmaps product|https://confluence.atlassian.com/advancedroadmapscloud/sharing-roadmaps-998651329.html], it states that a macro called *Advanced Roadmaps for Jira* can be installed in Confluence, but, Confluence Cloud does not have the macro available through Marketplace either as a bundled one.    (i) [This macro can be downloaded for the Server edition|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1221237/advanced-roadmaps-for-<USER>in-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview] and there is also a [suggestion to implement it for Confluence|https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/CONFCLOUD-70177].  {quote}  When you share a roadmap in a Confluence page, it will inherit any filters and settings you've configured on your roadmap like issue hierarchy, filters, timeline date range, view settings, and more.    To share a roadmap on a Confluence page, you need to make sure that you have the *Advanced Roadmaps for Jira plan* macro installed in your Confluence instance. This can be downloaded from the [Atlassian Marketplace|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/search?hosting=cloud&product=confluence], provided you have the proper permissions in your instance. To learn more about adding macros to your instance, see [What are macros|https://confluence.atlassian.com/confcloud/macros-724765157.html].  {quote}    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access [Sharing Roadmaps|https://confluence.atlassian.com/advancedroadmapscloud/sharing-roadmaps-998651329.html]   # Try to follow the instructions to find the macro in the Marketplace or while editing a Confluence page.    h3. Expected Results  The macro should be available to be installed or used.    h3. Actual Results  The macro does not exist for Confluence Cloud or the documentation should be updated to remove the specific part mentioning the macro for Confluence while it is not available.    h3. Workaround  While the documentation should be updated, to embed a plan inside Confluence, the steps highlighted in the [suggestion to have it available|https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/CONFCLOUD-70177] can help."""

summary: "Clicking on Time Tracking field on Next Gen project gets a white screen if the issue has subtasks"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  A Next Gen project is nos display the information from Time Tracking when clicking on this field if the issue has a subtask.    !image-2020-10-21-11-42-53-506.png|thumbnail!   A white page appears if you click on Time Tracking on the full issue view.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Access the board from a Next Gen project  # Click on an issue +with a subtask+  # Click on Time Tracking field    h3. Expected Results   !image (7).png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results   !image-2020-10-21-11-42-53-506.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Add the Time Tracking field to the Subtask will prevent this error from happening. """

summary: "Move to Done button doesn't disappear from Kanban boards"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    +Move to Done+ button doesn't disappear from Kanban boards if the *Kanban board sub-filter* is set to not show that issue and the swimlanes are based on Stories.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Story inside a Kanban board   # Create subtasks below that Story   # Set the Kanban board sub-filter to *fixVersion is EMPTY* (just an example)   ** The important thing is to set some Kanban board sub-filter that shouldn't show the Story   # Make sure the subtasks respect the Kanban board sub-filter (in my example, the fixVersion for subtasks need to be EMPTY)   # Set some fixVersion on the Story (the Story should still be shown there since it has subtasks below it that respect the Kanban board sub-filter   # Move all subtasks to Done   # The +Move to Done+ button will appear on your board   # You can move that issue to Done using any possible way, but that button will continue there.    h3. Expected Results    The button should disappear if that Story was already moved to Done.  h3. Actual Results    The button still appears, independently if you moved that Story Done:    !image-2020-10-21-14-36-52-410.png|width=643,height=197!  h3. Workaround    Currently, the only possible workaround should be to not have that Story """"excluded"""" by the Kanban board sub-filter."""

summary: "Raise the limit options in a dropdown or checkbox fields on Next-gen projects"
description: """As a user, having only 55 possible options in a dropdown or checkbox fields on Next-gen projects is not enough:     !image-2020-09-15-10-28-43-487.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Arrow toggle in issue detail view does not work"
description: """h4. Summary  Arrow toggle in issue detail view does not work. Clicking it does not do anything.    h4. Steps to reproduce  # Load a board > click on any issue to load the issue detail view  # Refer to the arrow toggle. Click that arrow  !toggle.png|thumbnail!    h4. Expected Result  The section will be expanded or collapsed    h4. Actual Result  No changes on section. It is neither expanded nor collapsed  """

summary: "Using FireFox and viewing an issue when you resize the screen or show in high resolution either ... is displayed or one leter is displayed per line."
description: """In Firefox, using the developer tools, it looks like at a width of ~1230 the """"People"""" names appear as one would expect, at ~1240 the """"..."""" appears, and by 1450 it is back to normal (at least in my tests). This is with the left hand navbar expanded. Having it collapsed would give more horizontal whitespace. Chrome doesn't seem to have the problem.    h3. Workaround  Use Chrome or IE."""

summary: "Creating Epic in new window shows misleading validation error when epic name is not specified.  ""customfield_10104: Epic Name is required""."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When creating a new Epic opening the creation page on a new tab and not specifying the required field 'Epic Name', Jira does not create the Epic and returns with an error that does not specify it's proper name, but it informs 'customfield_10104' instead.    h3. Environment  We've replicated this issue on all versions from Jira 7.0 up to Jira 7.13.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open the """"Create"""" page on a new tab of the browser   # Select 'Epic' issue type, and click to create the issue without specifying any information    h3. Expected Results  Returns an error message specifying the field's name like any other field, for example """"Epic Name: Epic Name is required"""".    h3. Actual Results  It returns the error message """"customfield_10104: Epic Name is required"""".    h3. Notes  Any other system or custom field returns with it's proper name before the description of the error.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Issues are listed as scope change when they are added before the start of sprint"
description: """h3. Summary  Issues are listed as scope change when they are added before the start of sprint    h3. Environment  * Jira 7.8.1    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a sprint  # Create an issue  # Add the issue to sprint  # Click on 'Start Sprint'  #* The steps 3 and 4 four should happen on same minute. e.g: 12:00 PM  # Click on 'Start'  #* This should happen 1 minute after you click on 'Start Sprint'. e.g: 12:01 PM    h3. Expected Results  Issue should be listed as sprint scope one its started    h3. Actual Results  Issue is listed as scope change     h3. Notes  * The issue is not reproduced if you add the issue to the sprint then wait a minute to start it.  * The issue can't be reproduced consistently."""

summary: "JS Exception while moving issues form active sprint to backlog"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    There is JS exception while moving issue on backlog view (from active sprint to backlog)    _Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property '0' of null_  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to backlog view   # Drag & Drop issue from active sprint to backlog   # Confirm pop-up about sprint scope change   # JS error occurs on the screen (not 100% reproducible)    h3. Expected Results    No JS Exception  h3. Actual Results    JS Exception visible on the screen    h3. Notes    Not 100% reproducible     Not reproducible on 7.13.0  h3. Workaround    Dismiss error. No side effects of this error observed."""

summary: "Context Flagged custom field causes error when Flagging issue on Board"
description: """If different context is defined for the Flagged Custom Field, Jira Agile board action """"Flag"""" doesn't work.  It doesn't work for the projects inside the new context only. For projects on the default context Flagging works fine.  *Steps to reproduce:* # Go to Custom Fields: <instance>/secure/admin/ViewCustomFields.jspa # Click on configure for field Flagged # Add new Context and add it for just one agile project. # Go to that project's board # right click on an issue and select 'Add a Flag'  - Expected: Issue is flagged - Result: Error shown !flagged.jpeg!  This configuration would be useful to flag issues when created for a group of projects and easily distinguish them on the board.  h4. Workaround:   Avoid creating additional configuration context for Flagged field and reserve only one configuration context for the field for now."""

summary: """Hide Epic Labels"" /  ""Show Epic Labels"" on Kanban Backlog missing"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In Jira, the """"_Hide Epic Labels_""""/""""_Show Epic Labels_"""" option is missing from the _Board_ drop down in the kanban backlog view, even though it's available on kanban boards.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # [Enable the Kanban Backlog on a project|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver0713/configuring-columns-965542312.html#Configuringcolumns-enablingkanbanbacklog]   # Navigate to a kanban backlog view   # Open the _Board_ dropdown    h3. Expected Results    """"Hide Epic Labels"""" or """"Show Epic Labels"""" is present   !Screen Shot 2019-02-13 at 3.43.48 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    The option is missing   !Screen Shot 2019-02-13 at 3.43.19 PM.png|thumbnail!       h3. Workaround    It's possible to switch to the board view, change settings, and then toggle back to the backlog view.  The same settings will apply to both locations."""

summary: "Drag and Drop ZIP file from board to Desktop failing after first try"
description: """h3. Summary  Drag and Drop ZIP non-image file from the board to Desktop does not work on Firefox and Internet Explorer as per [JRASERVER-60728|https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-60728]. However, on Google Chrome, this feature only works on the first try, consecutive try will throw an error.    h3. Environment  * Google Chrome Version 71.0.3578.98    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Navigate to an issue with a non-image file attached (i.e a word document)  # Click document and drag to desktop    h3. Expected Results  The file should be copied to the desktop.    h3. Actual Results  The file is not allowed to be copied to the desktop. There is a pop-up:  !error.png|thumbnail!     h3.Workaround  Refresh the page to drag and drop the file."""

summary: "Some ""Project Repots"" link does not work on IE11"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Clicking on some Project Reports link in Internet Explorer will result in error 400 broken link.  h3. Environment   - Jira 7.13.0   - IE 11    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create multi-byte name project (ex. テストプロジェクト)   # Access JIRA with Internet Explorer 11   # Access _Project > Reports_   # Click following reports   -- Average Age Report   -- Created vs. Resolved Issues Report   -- Pie Chart Report   -- Recently Created Issues Report   -- Resolution Time Report   -- Single Level Group By Report   -- Time Since Issues Report   -- Time Tracking Report   -- User Workload Report   -- Version Workload Report    h3. Expected Results    Link is opened successfully  h3. Actual Results    Internet Explorer shows """"HTTP 400 bad request"""" error.  h3. Notes    The request fails due to the """"Pie Chart Report"""" link being unencoded, ie.    Other browsers able to encode the URL on their own when user click the link.    h3. Workaround   # Use other browser such as Google Chrome   # Encode multibyte text manually and access the URL"""

summary: "Inline issue create with quick filter that has AND operator for Checkbox navigate full issue dialog instead of quick issue creation "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Full issue dialog pops up at inline issue create button on Backlog if quick filter is selected and the filter includes AND operator for checkboxes.   Issue should be created inline instead of full dialog because such filter is valid to specify an issue type.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create custom field """"TestCheckBox"""" with field type CheckBox and add """"option A"""", """"option B"""" for its option.   # Add the custom field on your own issue type   # Head to Backlog screen on your project and add Quick Filter with following condition –   {{TestCheckBox = """"option A"""" AND TestCheckBox = """"option B""""}}   # Select the quick filter in the Backlog screen   !Screen Shot 2019-02-18 at 16.37.37.png|thumbnail!   # Hit """"Create Issue"""" in the Backlog screen    h3. Expected Results    Issue that has value """"option A"""" and """"option B"""" is created without showing full dialog.  h3. Actual Results    It navigates you a full dialog.   !image-2019-02-21-11-26-57-972.png|thumbnail!  h3. Notes    If you configured    {{TestCheckBox = """"option A""""}}    in your quick filter, the dialog will not pop up.  h3. Workaround    Create an issue with full dialog."""

summary: "Title & Description overlapping in backlog view"
description: """h3. Summary  Title & Descriptions are overlapping in the backlog    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Add description to Card Layout    !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   # Create issues on the board with multiple short lines of description   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  Title and Description should not overlap with each other.     h3. Actual Results  Title and Description overlaps in the backlog   !screenshot-3.png|thumbnail!      h3.Workaround  No workaround  """

summary: "Custom Date Time Picker field displays Relative and Absolute values inconsistently"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  With Relative date/time enabled (default), a custom field of type Date Time Picker:  # Displays relative values on JIRA View Issue screen  # Displays absolute values on JIRA Agile's Issue Detail View    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a custom field of type Date Time Picker  # Add the field to Create/Edit/View screen of an issue and give it a value that can be displayed relatively (yesterday, tomorrow, etc.)  # Configure an Agile board where the issue is available and add the field to Issue Detail View    h3. Expected Results  The field shows Relative values on both View Issue screen and Agile board.    h3. Actual Results  The field shows Relative value on View Issue screen but Absolute value on Agile board:  !datetime.png|thumbnail!    h3. Notes  # This doesn't affect custom field of type Date Picker. Date Picker always shows Absolute values, similar to Due Date.  # This affects both Scrum and Kanban boards.    h3. Workaround  If possible, disable Relative date/time following this KB:  * [Disable relative dates in JIRA applications|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/disable-relative-dates-in-<USER>applications-414187622.html]    All the fields will display Absolute values."""

summary: "Epic Link field does not correctly filter epic names with comma"
description: """h3. Summary    Jira allows epic names to be created with special characters such as comma ' , '  semicolon ' ; ' etc. In the epic link field, when a user enters an epic name, Jira autocomplete feature returns suggestions as the user types. However, it is noticed that the autocomplete behavior is not consistent when the epic name contains a comma. That is, Jira also includes issues that do not have comma and does not deal with the comma as a part of the name.    This can be inconvenient when there is a large number of epics created with a similar name that contains a comma as it will include unnecessary values.    Please refer to the attached screenshots to further illustrate this:    !epicLink_comma.png|thumbnail!    !epicLink_semicolon.png|thumbnail!  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a number of epics which where some has a comma and some don't   # Open an issue and search start typing the epic names which has comma.    h3. Expected Results    Jira will filter the results correctly and will respect the comma in the epic names similar to other characters.  h3. Actual Results    Jira shows epics which also does not have comma as well.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available."""

summary: "Unable to make Sprint Burndown Dashboard gadget public"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Jira allows dashboards to be made public and shared with anonymous users by setting the right permissions. This work great for many gadgets. However, when adding a Sprint Burndown gadget it does not show on the dashboard appearing as blank.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a dashboard and share it with public   # In an agile project, create a board and make sure that the filter sharing is set to public   # Create a sprint if there is none in the project.   # Make sure to add 'Anyone' group to the 'Browse Project' permission   # Add a Sprint Burndown Gadget to the Dashboard after selecting the above sprint   # Try to view the shared dashboard with an anonymous user.    h3. Expected Results    The Sprint Burndown gadget will be displayed   h3. Actual Results    The Burndown Gadet is not displayed.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Mapping more than one status in the same column breaks statuses' translation"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When more than one translated statuses are mapped into the same column, and then user tries to transition an issue to that column, the statuses will be shown in English instead of the translated language    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Have 2 statuses (or any number more than one) mapped to the same column.   #* Lets say status In Progress and Done mapped to Done column  # Change language setting to other language (lets say German)  # Drag issue from other column to Done column    h3. Expected Results  JIRA will show options of statuses in that column in the translated language (so in case of German, it should show IN ARBEIT and FERTIG)    h3. Actual Results  It shows statuses in English (in this case, In Progress and Done). Even though the column management page itself shows the status in the translated language.
