(i) It's reproducible in Scrum and Kanban boards, with several languages like German, French, etc.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Enabling Kanban backlog breaks Kanban board's Column translations"
description: """h3. Summary  By default a user will see Kanban board's column names in his/her user profile language. However, after Kanban backlog is enabled, the columns will be displayed in the user profile language of the board creator at the time when the board was created.    h3. Steps to reproduce  # Set system language to French  # As a user with English profile language, create a new Kanban board for a new Kanban project  #* This board will have 4 columns: BACKLOG, SELECTED FOR DEVELOPMENT, IN PROGRESS, DONE  # Log in as a user with German profile language and view the board  #* The columns are displayed as: BACKLOG, ZUR ENTWICKLUNG AUSGEWÄHLT, WIRD AUSGEFÜHRT, FERTIG (/)  # Configure the board and enable Kanban backlog    h3. Expected behavior  The user with German profile language should still see the other columns in German (BACKLOG is now moved to Kanban backlog).    h3. Actual behavior  The user with German profile language now sees the columns in English (-)    h3. Notes  # All user profile languages are affected  # Disabling Kanban backlog doesn't revert the translations  # Even if the board creator changes user profile language to a different one e.g. Dutch, the columns are still displayed in English for all users    h3. Workaround  None at the moment. The ticket will be updated in case one is available."""

summary: "Backlog view on board becomes distorted when adding a text field to the card layout"
description: """h3. Summary  When adding a text field (like description) with a lot of text to the card layout on backlog view, the view becomes distorted with field data overlapping each other.    h3. Environment  * JIRA 8.0.0   * Cannot reproduce in Jira 7.13    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Add Description field to Card Layout on backlog view. If the description is long, the backlog becomes distorted and it's hard to read through.    h3. Expected Results  Text is rendered properly without issue.     !Jira713.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  Text is not rendered or wrapped up properly causing everything to be mixed up.     !Jira800.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  None so far"""

summary: "Moving issues across kanban backlog throws an error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Moving issues with assigned epic across kanban backlog and divided kanban backlog area is throwing a javascript error. Problem occurs on kanplan, I couldn't reproduce it on scrum backlog. After first occurrence an error will be thrown for each drag & drop. The core functionality of dragging issues works but the usability of it is pretty low and may have side effects.  h3. Environment   * macOS Mojave 10.14   * Google Chrome 72    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a kanban project   # [Enable kanplan|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftware/<USER>software-7-4-x-release-notes-902079634.html#JIRASoftware7.4.xreleasenotes-IntroducingtheKanbanbacklog]   # Get a backlog of variety issues (with and without epic).   # Try to move an issue with assigned epic to divided kanban backlog area   # After D&D javascript error will throw    h3. Expected Results    Moving issues across kanban backlog works without errors.     h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown:      h3. Notes  It works properly on 7.13.x    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Accessing Version and licensing page return 500 error with NoClassDefFoundError: org/apache/http/impl/auth/BasicScheme"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Accessing Version and licensing page return 500 error with NoClassDefFoundError: org/apache/http/impl/auth/BasicScheme    h3. Environment   * JIRA 8.0.0, JIRA 8.0.1 with a forward proxy to access Marketplace.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a forward proxy in setenv.sh (Linux) or setenv.bat (Windows) following [How to Configure an Outbound HTTP and HTTPS Proxy for JIRA applications|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-configure-an-outbound-http-and-https-proxy-for-<USER>applications-247857187.html].    # Restart JIRA for it to take effect.  # After restart go to ⚙>Application to access Versions and Licensing page.    h3. Expected Results  Versions and Licensing page load properly.    h3. Actual Results  Versions and Licensing page return 500 error with below error:    h3. Workaround  The error happen due to the *{{-Dhttps.proxyUser=proxyuser}}* and *{{-Dhttps.proxyPassword=proxypassword}}* setting on the forward proxy. As a workaround, removing the settings will resolve the issue.  """

summary: "Velocity Chart not working if sprints are empty (without issues)"
description: """h3. Summary  * If you remove the issues from a sprint after its closure, the Velocity Chart won't display this sprint (unlike other charts that *will* show historical data, such as Sprint report, Burndown, etc)    h3. Environment  * Jira Software Cloud    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create more several sprints in order to populate the Velocity chart  # Remove all the issues from a sprint  # Check the Velocity chart    *Note*: see this video of the problem replication   [^velocitybug.mp4]     h3. Expected Results  * Velocity should show historical data    h3. Actual Results  * Velocity is not showing the sprints at all (if the sprint is empty)    h3. Notes      h3. Workaround  """

summary: "Wallboard slideshow doesn't respect the gadgets order"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Using the wallboard slideshow feature, when wallboard has more than one gadget the application doesn't respect the gadgets display order.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Create 3 dashboards as follows:  # Dashboard 1 has one gadget.  # Dashboard 2 has one gadget.  # Dashboard 3 has three gadgets.    (i) The first gadget in Dashboard 3 is a custom web page with the same settings and URL as the only gadget in Dashboard 2. Because of this, the slideshow actually works like this:    - Dashboard 1's gadget for 5 seconds.  - Dashboard 2's gadget for 5 seconds.  - Dashboard 3's second gadget for 5 seconds.  - Dashboard 1's gadget for 5 seconds.  - Dashboard 2's gadget for 5 seconds.  - Dashboard 3's third gadget for 5 seconds.  (repeating from here on)    The first gadget on Dashboard 3 seems to be skipped as it is a duplicate of Dashboard 2's gadget.     h3. Expected Results    The expected outcome would instead be a cycle of:  Dashboard 1's gadget for 5 seconds.  Dashboard 2's gadget for 5 seconds.  Dashboard 3's second gadget for 5 seconds.  Dashboard 3's third gadget for 5 seconds.    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Issue link ""Search for an issue"" does not work from Backlog and Active Sprints view."
description: """h3. Summary  Issue link """"Search for an issue"""" does not work from Backlog and Active Sprints view.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to the backlog or Active sprints view;  # At the issue details on the right frame, click on """"..."""" > Link > """"Search for an issue""""   !Screen Shot 2019-03-18 at 16.37.36.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  * Have the """"Search for an issue"""" window opened.   !Screen Shot 2019-03-18 at 16.56.44.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  * Nothing happens.    h3.Solution / Workaround  * Use the issue view (http://<USER>server/browse/ISSUE-KEY), click on """"More"""" > Link > """"Search for an issue"""".  """

summary: "Cursor inconsistency when creating issue through backlog on IE11"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When adding the name of the issue when creating issues through the backlog, the cursor moves to the end of the text. This causes text to be added at the end of the line instead of where intended. This happens on IE 11.    h3. Environment    Reproduced in both Jira 7.11.2 and Jira 7.12.3  IE 11    h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Access to the backlog and sprint.   # Create a new issue.   # Add text to the issue.   # When text is added, click where you want to add the cursor on and add more text and press space.     h3. Expected Results    When adding the text, it'll be added where clicked and where the cursor is.    h3. Actual Results    The text is added at the end of the line (See video for more details).      [^IE11_bug.mp4]     h3. Workaround    None so far. Even using keyboard arrows do not work and when editing the line character gets added but then cursor gets moved to the end of the line again."""

summary: "When creating a board using a filter that includes a '!=', all epics under All Issues in Backlog will disappear."
description: """h3. Summary    When creating a board using a filter that includes '!=', for example :     All Epics that appear under *All Issues' in Backlog will disappear.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Scrum Board with the filter :     # Head to the Backlog.    h3. Expected Results    All epics under *All Issues* will be listed out in the Backlog.  h3. Actual Results    No epics will appear under *All Issues* in the backlog.  h3. Workaround    Create a new filter with the below query and give it a name (e.g. Exclude DP-503)    Run a search with the below query;  """

summary: "Board Administrators cannot switch to a Simplified Workflow"
description: """h3. Summary  When a user is a board administrator (and not a system administrator), they cannot switch to the Simplified Workflow, despite having met the requirements for the workflow. The """"Simplified Workflow"""" button is disabled.    Granting the user administrative rights and refreshing the Column Management screen causes the """"Simplify Workflow"""" button to be enabled.    [The Documentation|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver0712/permissions-overview-959315628.html] states that a user can switch if they are a Board admin, or a System admin.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a project  # Create a new workflow, that has To Do, In Progress, Done (along with one outgoing transition from each status  # Create a new workflow scheme, and add your workflow scheme to it. Make sure to only use """"All unassigned issue types""""; rather than """"All issue types"""" as per JSWSERVER-8720.  # Change the project to the new scheme  # Assign a non admin user to the """"Administrators"""" role in the project.  # Ensure the """"Administrators"""" role has the """"Administer Project"""" permission and that """"Extended Project Permission"""" is enabled  #* This is the default permission scheme in Jira software  # As the Project Administrator (but not System Administrator) visit the board and choose """"Configure Board » Columns""""    h3. Expected Results  The """"Simplify Workflow"""" button should be enabled.    h3. Actual Results  The """"Simplify Workflow button is not enabled.    h3.Workaround  Grant the user the """"System Administrator"""" global permission temporarily, or have another user with that permission make the change instead.  """

summary: "Sprint burndown gadget asks for board twice instead of Sprint"
description: """h3. Summary  The Sprint Burndown gadget is currently asking for the board twice:   !gad.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a dashboard  # Add a Sprint Burndown gadget    h3. Expected Results    The second text should be """"Sprint""""."""

summary: "Creating an issue under the backlog with a quick filter on a reporter results in an UI error and an incorrect reporter "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When user A creates an issue in the backlog by clicking the """"Create Issue"""" at the very bottom, and the board has a quick filter of """"reporter = user B"""" enabled, the new issue will have """"userB"""" as a reporter.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Login to Jira as userA.   # Configure a board and create a quick filter as """"reporter = userB"""".   # Go back to backlog screen in the board and select the quick filter which you just created.   # Create an issue in the backlog screen under the last issue.   # Open the new issue.    h3. Expected Results    Even if the quick filter is enabled, the reporter should be userA.  h3. Actual Results    The reporter of the new issue is set to userB.  h3. Notes   * If userA is a Jira admin, the issue will be created with the wrong reporter.    * If userA is not a Jira admin, the error """"{color:#FF0000}*Please fix the fields marked below*{color}"""" will be displayed, with no fields marked to fix.    h3. Workaround    userA should unselect the quick filter and create an issue in the backlog which populates the userA as the reporter."""

summary: "Agile Wallboard gadget  truncated in Firefox"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Agile wallboard gadget when created does not render properly within Firefox. The bottom section of the gadget is truncated and you're unable to scroll or view anything outside of the standard box    h3. Environment  Jira 8.0.2    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new dashboard   # Add the Agile wallboard gadget   # Ensure you have enough issue present to cause scrolling   # View the gadget within a Firefox browser    h3. Expected Results    You will be able to see all the issues within the gadget.  h3. Actual Results    You only see a sample section of the data, scrolling is not available.    h3. Notes    (Optional - If Necessary)  h3. Workaround    This is currently only impactful for Firefox, you may use Chrome or IE11 to view the gadget."""

summary: "Agile Wallboard Gadget renders column headers and sub-task cards with incorrect background color"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a user adds Agile Wallboard Gadget and shows the dashboard in Wallboard mode, Jira will display the statuses in the gadget in white color which does not match the black background color.     !Screenshot 2019-04-17 at 11.49.41 AM.png|thumbnail!       h3. Environment    * Jira 8.1.0  * Jira 8.0.2      h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Add """"Agile Wallboard Gadget"""" and configure a Sprint with the gadget.  # Show the dashboard where the gadget was added in Wallboard mode.      h3. Expected Results    The browser renders the wallboard full black background.      h3. Actual Results    The browser renders the status background in white color.      h3. Workaround    Possible to use CSS script to alter the gadget rendering."""

summary: "Agile Wallboard Gadget does not show the full content of Sprint"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a user adds Agile Wallboard Gadget and shows the dashboard in Wallboard mode, Jira will not display all the issues in Swimlane column      From regular dashboard:     !Screenshot 2019-04-17 at 11.42.08 AM.png|thumbnail!     From wallboard mode:     !Screenshot 2019-04-17 at 11.49.41 AM.png|thumbnail!       h3. Environment    * Jira 8.1.0  * Jira 8.0.2      h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Add """"Agile Wallboard Gadget"""" and configure a Sprint with the gadget.  # Show the dashboard where the gadget was added in Wallboard mode.      h3. Expected Results    Jira renders all the issues in each column.      h3. Actual Results    Jira renders partial issues in each column.      h3. Workaround    Possible to use CSS script to alter the gadget rendering."""

summary: "JIRA Agile Simplified Workflow allows you to add duplicates for some essential post functions"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    JIRA Agile Simplified Workflow allows you to add duplicates for some essential post functions    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # JIRA Agile Simplified Workflow shows incorrectly due to the following Bug: https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/JSWSERVER-11547  # Go to the menu to add postfunctions    h3. Expected Results    You will see all available postfunctions, minus the essential postfunctions which should already be present on your workflow.     h3. Actual Results    The essential postfuctions are also present to be added to the workflow. Therefore you could add the same essential workflow postfunction twice as shown below:    !postfunction.png|thumbnail!  !postfucntion2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes  This is known to cause incorrect reporting in burndown charts where issues are not being recorded as completed inside the sprint. """

summary: "Using IE 11 not able to view PDF attachments."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Using IE 11.2848.14393 in Jira 7.12 PDF attachments don't show.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Open IE 11 navigate to any issue add attachment and click on PDF doesn't work.      h3. Expected Results  PDF should display.    h3. Actual Results  Nothing is loaded.    h3. Notes    (Optional - If Necessary)  h3. Workaround    Use different browser like Chrome it loads as expected."""

summary: "Kanban Board: Multiple statuses in one column do not all appear when transitioning to it."
description: """If you configure a Kanban Board column to use multiple statuses, they will not all appear when you have a list of issues longer than the page, and then try to transition the issue.    !8.01 Kanban.gif|width=362,height=367!    This works better/correctly in Jira 7.13.    !7.13 Kanban.gif|width=361,height=382!"""

summary: "Exception Uncaught TypeError:cannot read property 'scrollheight' of undefined when creating an Epic from Backlog"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Exception Uncaught TypeError:cannot read property 'scrollheight' of undefined when creating an Epic from Backlog.    h3. Environment    Jira 8.x    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to the backlog view   # Create Epic from the create button on top of the window.    h3. Expected results  Epic is created without issue.  h3. Actual results  There's a JavaScript exception when creating Epic.        h3. Workaround    Epic can be created from a different page e.g. the Issues page."""

summary: "Quick filtering a subtask in a  board will hide it's parent's status in the swimlane"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When selecting a quick filter in an active sprint or kanban backlog board that filters sub-tasks of a ticket that shows up in the swimlane will result in hiding the parent's status label and issue type icon.    h3. Environment    Reproducible on Jira 8.0.2  Not reproducible on Jira 7.7 and 7.13    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Login with the administrator account   # Create a Jira Software scrum or kanban project   # Create a story and assign it to User A   # Create a sub-task of that story and assign it to User B   # Add swimlane to the stories, and add two quick filters - one to look for issues assigned to User A, and another to look for issues assigned to User B   # At the active sprint view or kanban backlog, select the first quick filter to User A. The parent ticket will still have it's issue type icon and status label information in the swimlane   # Unselect the quick filter, and select the quick filter to look for ticket assigned to User B (assignee of subtask)    h3. Expected Results    The parent ticket will persist with the ticket's information of issue type icon and status label.    h3. Actual Results    The parent ticket hide the issue type icon and it's status label.    h3. Screenshots  Without filters, expected behaviour:   !Expected_behaviour.png|thumbnail!   With quick filter to the assignee of the subtask, hidden information:   !Hidden_IssueType_and_Status.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Javascript Error upon navigating through boards"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When navigating through a Kanban board, an error is thrown    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Sample kanban board  # Access to the board and open up Selected Issue sidebar  # Refresh browser  # Click on one of the tickets again, then click on another ticket to open it as Selected Issue  # Refresh browser and error triggered :    !Screen Shot 2019-05-22 at 11.19.13 AM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  No error should be triggered.    h3. Actual Results    The below exceptions are thrown on the client side:    h3. Notes  Issue above was replicated with Firefox    h3. Workaround  Besides closing the error window, no other workaround is known at the moment"""

summary: "Click "">"" under Version Panel in Backlog page throw ""Cannot read property 'doneEstimate' of undefined"" error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Click """">"""" under Version Panel in Backlog page throw """"Cannot read property 'doneEstimate' of undefined"""" error    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create new Scrum project with sample data.  # Go to Backlog > Version panel.  # Click on any *Quick Filters*, Ex: *Only My Issues*  # Click *"""">""""* button to view Version details.    !SCRUM board - Agile Board - Jira 820 - Google Chrome 2019-05-23 14.19.18.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  Version details show properly   !SOF board - Agile Board - Amirul JIRA 7123 - Google Chrome 2019-05-23 14.08.57.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  *Exception: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property 'doneEstimate' of undefined* thrown in UI:   !SCRUM board - Agile Board - Jira 820 - Google Chrome 2019-05-23 14.12.55.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes  This also happen to Kanban project where backlog is enable.  Error did not happen in JIRA 7.13.2.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior."""

summary: "Edit license button when using Internet Explorer is unresponsive"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The edit license button does not respond when using Internet Explorer in Jira 8.0+  h3. Environment   * Windows     * Internet Explorer  h3.      *Steps to Reproduce*   # Use Jira 7.13+   # Navigate to versions and licenses page using Internet Explorer   # Attempt to click edit button    h3. Expected Results    Can edit license.  h3. Actual Results    Edit button is unresponsive.  h3. Workaround    Use Chrome or Firefox"""

summary: "Story Swimlane flagged only after page reload"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a board is configured swimlane based on Story, and if you try to flag this story, the flag is not shown until a page reload.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure a board swimlane to be based on Story   # Attempt to flag a Story swimlane heading    h3. Expected Results    The flag should appear immediately    h3. Actual Results    The flag only appears after the board is reloaded    h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Browser search function does not find issues after supplying a dash."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When using the browser search function, the search does not pick up issues after adding the dash after the project key.    Ex.    Searching """"ABC"""" is fine. Picks this up on the page.    Searching """"ABC-"""" no longer picks up issues that match the criteria.  h3. Environment   * Chrome   * Firefox   * Internet Explorer    h3. Steps to reproduce   * Have a Scrum/Kanban board and some issues   * Go to Active Sprint/Kanban board view   * Press {{ctrl+f}} and key in the issue key (ie. ABC-1)    h3. Expected Results    Search finds issue named """"ABC-1""""  h3. Actual Results    Search does not find issue named """"ABC-1""""       h3. Workaround    None"""

summary: "Drag'n'drop zones in agile boards do not cover full column height"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    While drag'n'dropping an issue between columns in an agile board, blue divisions are displayed within it which correspond to the available statuses. There is a slight visual glitch visible when this happens, as the top border of the highest positioned segment is not aligned with the column's header - there is some unintentional padding left between them.  h3. Environment    IE 11    Steps to Reproduce   # Go to an agile board   # Drag an issue and hold it   # Observe the results    h3. Expected Results    Top border of the highest positioned segment (blue highlight) is aligned with the column's header, there is no unnecessary spacing between them.  h3. Actual Results    Top border of the highest positioned segment is not aligned with the column's header, there is some padding left between them:    !Screenshot 2019-05-29 at 10.23.15.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Incorrect relative date is displayed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When using relative date an incorrect value is shown. When mousing over the value the correct relative value is displayed.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Setup a fresh Jira 8.2.0 instance, ensure relative date is enabled.  # Create a new Scrum project with sample data E.G SAMPLE (SAM).  # Navigate to SAM-12 and hover over the Updated field.        h3. Expected Results  An accurate relative date is shown.    h3. Actual Results  Jira seems to be off by a day   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!    !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!           h3. Notes  I tried to reproduce this behavior in 7.12 for a feature request but was not able to.  * [JSWSERVER\-20072: Change """"date"""" value to """"date user\-tz"""" within issue history when using relative date|https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/JSWSERVER-20072]    h3. Workaround  Use absolute dates  * [Disable relative dates in JIRA applications|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/disable-relative-dates-in-<USER>applications-414187622.html]  """

summary: "When Using Greater Than Or Smaller Than (comparison operator) In JQL Search, It Return Results Like >= or <="
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When Using Greater Than Or Smaller Than (comparison operator) In JQL Search, It Return Results Like >= or <=    h3. Environment  JIRA 8.2    h3. (i) Assuming the fixed version was with only 1 fixed version.    h3. Actual Results  Results returned JIRA 8.0.0 as well    h3. Workaround  Write JQL with 1 version after the version you doesn't wish to show such as:-    """

summary: "Developer Summary custom field uses the ID as custom field name"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In Jira Software EAP 2 (maven version 8.3.0-m0003), the name for {{Developer Summary}} is shown as the ID    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to {{Administration > Issues > Custom fields}}    h3. Expected Results  The correct name and not the ID is shown.    h3. Actual Results  The ID is displayed   !Screen Shot 2019-06-10 at 8.27.38 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Notes  * Tested in Jira server and software with maven version 8.2.0-m0010 and {{Developer Summary}} is not a locked field there.    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Create board after some time data is incorrect. Copy board and copy works fine."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    I create a board and it works fine. After some time the board is incorrect.   Copy board and the copy is correct, however the original board is still wrong.   Integrity checker doesn't fix the issue.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Customer provided backup and data is incorrect.   # Copy of board is correct, can't locate the reason for the error.    h3. Expected Results    The data in the board should be correct.  h3. Actual Results    The board over time is incorrect.  h3. Notes    There is no error or any clues to why the data is wrong.  h3. Workaround    Copy the board"""

summary: "Large comments cause rendering error when showing in activity panel."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a comment is very large, say 31K or so. The activity panel will display a rendering error due to the size of the data.  h3. Expected Results    It should display the text or part of it.  h3. Actual Results    Times out and displays rendering error.       h3. Workaround    Simply add a few comments to push that one down the list then it shows comments again."""

summary: "AD group deleted before removing it from application access causes endless loop"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Deleted AD group causes application access page to loop endlessly.  h3. Environment    (Optional - If Applicable)   *   *  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an AD group   # Add this group to the Default application access.   # Open application access and remove the group.    h3. Expected Results  The group will be removed.    h3. Actual Results  The application causes and endless loop due to missing group.    The below exception is thrown in the xxxxxxx.log file:    h3. Notes    (Optional - If Necessary)  h3. Workaround    Add the group back then first remove the group from the application access then delete the AD group."""

summary: "Column Formatting of the Kanban board is gone in Subtask's Assignee view"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  The columns of the Kanban board is messed up (subtask assignee view) and the assignee is not able to drag&drop the issues to the correct column within the board.        Keywords: Kanban cards spanning, stretching, distorting through multiple columns statuses.    h3. Environment  JIRA 8.1.0  h3. Steps to Reproduce  # There are two users involved  #* User 1 - the admin user who executes the operation and is a member of Group1 security level.  #* User 2 - not a member of Group 1 security level, current assignee of the subtask  # There's one security level called Group 1 which is the default security level when the issues are created.  # The User 1 (admin) created a task (ABC-1) which automatically has a security level 1 then created a subtask (ABC-2) which the admin user assigned to User 2 (not a member of Group 1 security level)  # As User 1 (admin), I viewed the Kanban board and the columns are all intact  !admin-view.png|thumbnail!  # I then login as User 2 (assignee of the subtask) but the Kanban board's columns have no more partition and doesn't allow the assignee to drag the issues in the columns without partition  !assignee1.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results  The columns have partitions.      h3. Actual Results  Column partitions are not respected in Assignee's view (subtask).    h3. Workaround  Remove the security level on the parent issue and refresh the board (subtask assignee's view).    h3. Note  The issue is not reproducible in JIRA 7.0.10 and 7.13.4. One thing that I noticed is that since the User 2 has no visibility with the parent task (due to security level), he's not able to view it in the board unlike with 8.1.0."""

summary: "Trying to print an agile board with lots of issues limits the output to one page only"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    For Jira Software 8 versions, when looking at a Scrum backlog, say with 60 or a lot more issues, trying to print the entire list of issues a browser like chrome or firefox will only show a single page of results.  The rest appear to be getting truncated off the page, with no 2nd page to print.  h3. Environment    Confirmed behavior in 8.1.0 and 8.2.2, but unable to replicated in 7.13.1  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a board with 60 or so issues in the backlog and across planned sprints   # on the backlog page, try to print this page in your browser    h3. Expected Results    The output of the browser print pages/preview should allow the print job to scale to use multiple pages if need be to display the contents of the entire page.  h3. Actual Results  Only one page is offered to be printed by the browser.  If not all the issues on the board can be seen on the first page, the rest cannot be printed in that view      h3. Notes  Possibly a warranty bug to Jira 8 as this function works as expected in 7.13.1    (Optional - If Necessary)  h3. Workaround  There is not a work-around for this specific view yet.  A work-around will be added if found.    One possible alternative is to use the Board -> Print card option to get a list of all these issues that appear here, but this output is slightly different."""

summary: "Sprint field drop down menu is impossible to see"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When viewing the Sprint drop down on an Issue page, the text  in parenthesis is impossible to see.    !Screenshot of issue.png|thumbnail!     Specifically, in the Sprint selection list each sprint has text in parentheses following the sprint name;   - Name1 (Active Sprint)   - Name2 (Future Sprint)    When the item is not highlighted, the parenthetical text color is gray. For the highlighted item, the highlight itself is gray and the parenthetical text is white    Other selection lists that have parenthetical text, like Epic and User Picker fields, that text is black and shows clearly through the highlight.    Modifying the Look and Feel options has no effect on changing the color of the text in parenthesis.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Navigate to Issue -> Sprint and click on the dropdown    h3. Expected Results    The text would have the same color scheme as other fields as shown below.   !Intended behavior.png|thumbnail!   h3. Actual Results    The text in parenthesis is impossible to see.  h3. Workaround    The Look and Feel option, """"Menu Item Highlight Item Background Color"""" can be modified to allow the text in parenthesis to be shown easier.    !Workaround Color.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "Slow issue creation with Google Chrome in backlog"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When there more than ~60 issues in backlog, user face slowness while creating issue from the backlog.      h3. Environment    * Jira 8.X   * Google Chrome 75.X      h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create more than 60 issues in the backlog. We can use [Jira Data Generator|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1210725/data-generator-for-<USER>hosting=server&tab=overview] to generate the issues.  # Ensure that the 60 issues are displayed in a backlog of a board.  # Access the board where the issues are displayed, click on """"Create issue"""" and enter the summary of the issue.       h3. Expected Results    * Use do not see any slowness while entering the issue summary.      h3. Actual Results    * User see noticeable slowness while entering the issue summary.      h3. Workaround    * Use a browser other than Google Chrome.    +Note+    While there's a noticeable delay in adding Summary, there's no problem creating issue from backlog with Chrome. """

summary: "Create sub-task from Issues in Epics quick access menu assigns epic as parent instead of the intended story"
description: """h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Navigate to the View Issue screen of an Epic that has children Stories  # Move mouse to the right of one of the children (Stories), found within the """"Issues in Epic"""" web panel, notice that a quick access gear (dropdown) will appear  # Click the pull-down of the access gear image and select """"Create sub-task"""" (see screenshot below)    !test.png|thumbnail!     (screenshot notes: Epic is TST-24, child Story is TST-4)    h3. Expected Results    Sub-task is created as the child of the Story (TST-4, the grandchild of the parent Epic), much like if Edit was selected from the quick access menu    h3. Actual Results    Sub-task is created as the child of the Epic (TST-24)    h3. Notes    Relates to current feature request:  [JSWSERVER-15705 - Improper Message: A subtask cannot be assigned to an epic|https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/JSWSERVER-15705]     h3. Workaround  Do not use the quick access dropdown for sub-task creation.   Either:  # Navigate to the Story which is intended to be the parent and create the sub-task from there, or:  # Use a third-party add-on to automatically add sub-task children using post functions"""

summary: "Adding multiple links on an issue fails if one of the links is not valid."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If you attempt to add multiple links to an issue and one of the links is not valid, Jira will not add the other valid links.    h3. Environment    Any fresh install.  h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Step 1   Add 2 valid links between two or more issues.   # Step 2  Add an invalid link between two issues (for instance, use an inexistent issue key).    h3. Expected Results    Jira should add the valid links and disregard the invalid link.    h3. Actual Results    Jira does not add any of the links and presents an error message acknowledging that there is an invalid link.    h3. Workaround    The workaround available is to add links one by one."""

summary: "Issues' transition screen in Kanban Board will cause Description field to duplicate the values as the field's description"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When transitioning issues in the Kanban Board, should it have a Screen in the Transition, the values in the Description field will also be displayed under the field    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Software Project  # Create s Kanban Board  # Add a Screen containing 'Description' field in transition  # Go to the Kanban Board  # Transition the issue (containing values in 'Description')  # Issue will be occurring in the Transition Screen    h3. Expected Results  Transition Screen will show values normally    h3. Actual Results  Description value is also being displayed underneath the field:   !KanbanTransition823.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  * Disable '<USER>Text Editor'   !KanbanTransition823RTED.png|thumbnail!   * Change the 'Description' field renderer from 'Wiki Style Renderer' to 'Default Text Renderer'"""

summary: "Mail is not setting assignee if the inbox is not selected and using other folder."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When you configure the mail to use an alternate folder other than Inbox, Jira ignores the cc and doesn't set the assignee.  h3. Environment    (Optional - If Applicable)   *   *  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a mail handler that uses a folder other than Inbox.   # Send an mail with the cc set to Jira.   # Review the issue and note the assignee is not set.    h3. Expected Results  h3. Actual Results       h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior."""

summary: "Bitbucket is showing Jira fields in incorrect order"
description: """h3. Summary  When creating issues from Bitbucket Server side, the Create Issue screen show fields in incorrect order.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a few fields in Jira side and add them to create issue screen:  [^Jira_Bug_Create_Issue.pdf]  [^Jira_Bug_Field_Configuration.pdf]  [^Jira_Bug_Field_List.pdf]  # In Bitbucket side, open the Create Issue modal window and see that fields are displayed in incorrect order:  !BB-Create-Issue-1.png|thumbnail!   !BB-Create-Issue-2.png|thumbnail!   !BB-Create-Issue-3.png|thumbnail!      h3. Expected Results  The Create Issue modal window in Bitbucket should show the fields in the same order shown in Jira.    h3. Actual Results  The Create Issue modal window shows fields in incorrect order.     h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Dragging a closed Issue from Sprint to Backlog produces a browser error"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    **When in the Backlog view, moving a Done Issue from the current Sprint and dragging it down to the Backlog (or vice versa while it is still visible before refreshing the page) causes the browser to encounter an error.    h3. Environment    (Optional - If Applicable)   * Jira Software 8.x   * SCRUM project   * Chrome, Firefox   * Backlog view with current Sprint on top    h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Drag any complete Issue (not a Sub-task) from Sprint to Backlog and the browser will encounter an error after you acknowledge the Change in Scope.   # While the Issue is still on screen, you can drag it from Backlog to Sprint again and the same error will occur. If you refresh the screen, however, you cannot do this because the Issue is Done and it will no longer be populated in the backlog.    h3. Expected Results    The Done Issue will move from Sprint to Backlog without encountering an error.  h3. Actual Results    The browser encounters an error.    The Jira logs do not display an error. Only this is present in the atlassian-<USER>log to indicate that an action occurred:    The below exception is thrown in the browser:    !image-2019-07-24-15-43-50-053.png!    h3. Notes    This is likely tied to the fact that Backlog isn't supposed to show Done Issues by default.     h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Documentation bug: Update KB Using the Sprint Health Check Gadget"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The only Atlassian documentation covering the Sprint Health Gadget, [Using the Sprint Health Gadget|https://confluence.atlassian.com/agile064/<USER>agile-user-s-guide/using-the-<USER>agile-gadgets/using-the-sprint-health-gadget], was last revised in May 2014. The section of the document covering Scope Change is vague and leaves out multiple use cases, regarding calculating the scope change percentage.    h3. Expected Results    Since the gadget expresses scope change as a percentage (but does not provide the actual equation used), a Jira user might assume one of the simplest percentages:      h3. Actual Results  The article provides no detail and clarity specifically around how scope change is calculated with this gadget. The scope change section obliquely refers customers to another related configuration factor, and a Jira 6.4 version of documentation: [Configuring Estimation and Tracking|https://confluence.atlassian.com/agile064/<USER>agile-user-s-guide/configuring-a-board/configuring-estimation-and-tracking].     The documentation does not give much detail around the equation for the scope change percentage, i.e. the percentage calculated given different estimation statistics configured.    h3. Notes  See also bug ticket [JSWSERVER-15130 - Sprint Health gadget does not record scope change reliably|https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/JSWSERVER-15130].    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround. """

summary: "Sprint report doesn't show status for some issues"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When viewing the *Sprint Report*, issues on the *Status Report* won't display the status:  !example.png|thumbnail!    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Scrum board with four statuses and three columns:  #* Columns: Open, In Progress and Done  #* Statuses: *Open*, *In Progress*, *Done* and *Closed* (the latter two mapped to the Done column)  # Create a Sprint with three issues (e.g. *SCRUM-1*, *SCRUM-2*, *SCRUM-3*) and transition them to:  #* *SCRUM-1*: *Done*  #* *SCRUM-2*: *In Progress*  #* *SCRUM-3*: *Closed*  # Close the sprint and view the *Sprint Report*  #* At this point all statuses should be displayed correctly:  !closed_sprint.png|thumbnail!  # Transition *SCRUM-1* and *SCRUM-2* to *Closed*  # View the *Sprint Report* again  #* Now, only *SCRUM-3* should be displaying the status (*Closed*):  !after_transitioning.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results  All statuses would be correctly shown.    h3. Actual Results  Jira will only display the statuses if at least one of the issues in the report are currently on that status.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when/if available"""

summary: "If the status is unmapped in the board configuration, any issue in this status will show as removed from sprint in the sprint report"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a status is unmapped in the board config, it will not show in the sprint report.   i.e. Issues transitioned to this status will:   # show under the *Issues Removed From Sprint* category  # show as if they were in the last status before transitioning to the current one that's unmapped.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Start sprint with issue A  # Resolve issue A  # Complete the sprint  # Go to sprint report. Issue A is listed under completed issue  # Go to column management page > un-map the Done status (to unmapped column)  # Go to the report again     h3. Expected Results  The report shall not change  h3. Actual Results  Issue A is listed under issue removed from sprint and show the previous status it transitioned to """"Done"""" from.     h3. Workaround  Make all workflow statuses in the """"Complete"""" category mapped to columns so they're considered as complete in the reports.   """

summary: "Can copy Boards despite not having the 'Create Shared Objects' Global Permission"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  As a user that does not have the 'Create Shared Objects' Global Permission granted, they would see the 'Copy' option greyed out when viewing a Board:   !BoardCopy2.png|thumbnail!     However, the 'Copy' option can still be clicked and the Board would be copied:   !BoardCopy3.png|thumbnail!    !BoardCopy4.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a non-administrator user  # Create a Board which the new user has access to  # Revoke the 'Create Shared Objects' Global Permission from the new user  # Log in as the new user and access the Board  # Click 'Board' and see that the 'Copy' option is greyed out  # Click 'Copy' and the Board will be copied    h3. Expected Results  Not able to click the greyed out 'Copy' option    h3. Actual Results  Able to click the greyed out 'Copy' option and the Board will be copied   !BoardCopy3.png|thumbnail!    !BoardCopy4.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Jira doesn't display commit information if file was renamed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  It's not possible to display the commit information when the commit contains a file that was renamed.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # [Link Jira to Bitbucket Cloud|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/linking-a-bitbucket-or-github-repository-with-<USER>938846899.html].  # Create a repository in Bitbucket Cloud  ## Add a file to it;  ## commit the file, and;  ## push it to the repository.  # Rename the file with {{git mv <oldfilename.txt> <newfilename.txt>}}  ## commit the file with an issue key in the commit message;  ## and push it to the repository.  # Access the issue mentioned in the commit message  # Click on the commit link in under the *Development* panel    h3. Expected Results  The commit information would be displayed normally.    h3. Actual Results  Jira fails to display the commit with the message *Unable to retrieve commit information*:  !commit_info.png|thumbnail!    And the exception below is thrown in the {{atlassian-<USER>log}} log file:      h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Enabling ""hide completed"" functionality on a Kanban board affects issues with status category set to ""No category"""
description: """When enabling """"Hide completed"""" functionality in a Kanban board, issues mapped to statuses with no category set will also be affected along side issues with """"done"""" statuses.    Jira does not allow creating statuses without categories, but apps such as Jira <USER>can create those statuses, and if they are used on a Kanban board, this will lead to non-expected results.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Kanban board and map a status with """"No category"""" category to some column   # Move the issue to the column, and make sure it qualifies for the """"hide completed"""" crititeria (e.g. updated < -1w).  # Issue will be hidden from the board  # Assign a category to the status  # The board now shows the issue.    h3. Expected Results  """"Hide completed"""" functionality affects only issues in """"Done"""" statuses.    h3. Workaround  Assign a relevant category to the status."""

summary: "Private comments are not returning in JQL search using Participants of an Issue custom field."
description: """h3. Summary    Private comments are not returning in JQL search using Participants of an Issue custom field.    h3. Environment    * Jira Software Version 7.11.2  * Jira Toolkit Version 0.35.14    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Leave an internal comment in an Issue, your user will be listed on the Participants of an issue field automatically.  # Perform a JQL search using the field as a key and your user as an input like in the example below.  """"Participants of an issue"""" = gelb-admin     h3. Expected Results    List of the issues that have either comment internally and externally.    h3. Actual Results    List of the issues were your comments are not internal.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Project specific Shared Filter can be viewed by other Project Users"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Shared Dashboard Filter with viewer option """"Project -> All"""" can be viewed by other project users.           Environment    Linux, Windows.    Jira Software v7.12.3, v8.3.2  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create two groups with different set of users(Example: Group1[user1,user2], Group2[test1,test2])   # Create a project permission scheme with Group1,Group2 containing grant permission for all Project Permission(Browse Projects), Issue Permissions.   # Create two projects (Example: Project1, Project2) and assign the same project permission.   # Login to Jira portal as user1 and create shared filter with following permission.  Manage Filters -> Filter1 -> Add Viewer -> Project -> Project1 -> All -> Add   # Login to Jira portal as test1 user and click on Issues -> Manage Filters. Shared filter of user1 will be displayed under Popular.    h3. Expected Results    Only users within Group1 (User1, User2) should be able to view the shared filter.  h3. Actual Results    Users on both group Group1, Group2 can view the shared filter restricted to specific Project.       h3. Workaround    1.Create role and assign users to specific role.    2.Set the filter share permission limited to project role.    Manage Filters -> Filter1 -> Add Viewer -> Project -> Project1 ->Role1 -> Add"""

summary: "Benutzer: „“ is displayed on Edit Filter page instead of actual username for German Deutsch (Deutschland) language Users"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Benutzer: „“ is displayed instead of the added username in Edit Current Filter page for Deutsch language users.  h3. Environment    Linux, Windows    Steps to Reproduce   # Login to Jira software panel as Deutsch (Deutschland) language user.   # Go to """"Vorgänge""""(Issues) -> """"Filter verwalten"""" (Manage Filters) -> click """"gear icon"""" -> Click """"Bearbeiten"""" (Edit) -> Under section """"Editoren hinzufügen"""" (Add Editors) -> Select """"Benutzer"""" (user) -> search for any user -> Click +  (or)  Go to Boards -> """"Alle Boards anzeigen"""" -> click """"..."""" -> """"Konfigurieren"""" (Configure) -> click """"Freigabeberechtigungen für Filter bearbeiten"""" (Edit Filter Shares) -> Under section """"Editoren hinzufügen"""" -> Select """"Benutzer"""" (user) -> search for any user -> Click +    h3. Expected Results    Actual Username should be displayed.  h3. Actual Results    Benutzer: „“  h3. Workaround    1. Move the mouse cursor over Benutzer: „“ to view the added username.  2. username will be displayed after clicking Save."""

summary: "Triggering Card Color condition from board does not work until a page refresh"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a card color condition is triggered from the board, the card color does not appear unless the page is refreshed.    h3. Environment  Jira Software 8.2.4    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure a Kanban Board's Card Color based on JQL Query. Use the below query:  #* Flagged = Impediment  # Go back to the board, and add flag to an issue from the board.  #* Right click on the card.  #* Select """"Add flag"""".    h3. Expected Results  Card color changes immediately to selected color.    h3. Actual Results  Card color does not change, unless the page is refreshed.    h3. Workaround  Set the card color from issue view."""

summary: "Completing a sprint only moves incomplete issues to next sprint if the issue is in the board"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When completing a sprint in Jira not all incomplete issues are moved to the selected sprint.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a project with two boards  # Set one of the boards to only one issue type (e.g. Bug)  # Create two sprints and add one issue of each type to each sprint  # Start the sprint  # On the board with only one issue type, complete the sprint and select to move all issues to the second sprint.    See [^complete_sprint.mov]    h3. Expected Results  * All issues in that sprint would be migrated to the new Sprint  * Or Jira would show a message stating that X issues would not be moved as they are not part in the board.    h3. Actual Results  Jira shows:  {panel}  *X* issues were incomplete  Select where all the incomplete issues should be moved:  {panel}  but only moves the sub-set of issues that are present in the board.    h3. Workaround  * Manually bulk-edit the issues after completing the sprint.  * Or have a board with all issues in the sprint included, and complete the sprint from this board."""

summary: "View recent boards shows boards that all users have viewed instead of board your account viewed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When clicking on the Boards link in the top navigation bar is showing a section of 'Recent Boards'.  In Jira Software 8.1.2 and higher (possibly other versions), it appears that this recent board list is no longer showing unique results to each user, but instead it looks like this resource is sharing the recent boards of other JSW users (provided that your account also has access to that same board).    h3. Environment  Tested in 8.1.2, 8.2.3 and found this unexpected functionality happens.    However when trying to replicate in 7.13.1, and 8.0.2, this behavior does not happen. In these older versions each user appears to have their own recent boards values that are only generated by the boards they view.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new board (AAA project key for this example)  # Create a second Jira software licensed user  # Login as the newly created 2nd user, and then create a new board with board name (TEST)  # In a new browser (incognito) login as user 1 again, then visit the Boards -> recent boards      h3. Expected Results  If user #1 has not recently been to board TEST, they should not see it in the recent list    h3. Actual Results  User #1 sees TEST board in the recent boards list      h3. Notes  This does not appear to leak names of ALL boards exactly.  Your user account can still only see in the recent boards entries that your account actually has permissions to see.  However this recent board should be bound to the currently logged in user and that is not what appears to be happening right now.   !Screen Shot 2019-08-28 at 5.07.47 PM.png|thumbnail!   ^ shows recent boards of other users, not the current user right now      h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "XML Export does not include Log Work comments"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Issue export to XML does not include the same data as export to CSV (All fields) – specifically the _Work Log_ description is not exported.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Log work on an issue.   # Add a Work Description.   # Export issues that contain {{worklogComment}}.    h3. Expected Results    XML export includes the issue's Log Work Description.  h3. Actual Results    XML export does not contain Log Work Description.  h3. Workaround    Export → CSV (All fields) contains the Log Work Description – use this export if the field data is required."""

summary: "The Agile Wallboard Gadget flashes unexpectedly when the board utilizes ""Queries"" as card colour configuration."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  On Jira 8.2.4, the Agile Wallboard Gadget flashes unexpectedly when the board utilizes """"Queries"""" as card colour configuration.    The problem is not reproducible on Jira 7.9.0.    h3. Environment  * Jira 8.2.4    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Configure the Agile Wallboard Gadget to be based on a board.  # Go to the board configuration, and configure card color.  #* User """"Queries"""" as the card color configuration.  # Return to Agile Wallboard Gadget.    h3. Expected Results  * Agile Wallboard Gadget would show correct card color based on Queries used.    h3. Actual Results  * Agile Wallboard Gadget is flashing.  [^GadgetFlashing.webm]     h3. Notes  * Issue is not reproducible on Jira 7.9.0  [^KanbanNotFlashing.webm]       h3. Workaround  No workaround known so far."""

summary: "Reports are inconsistent for issues on unmapped statuses."
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Once issues are moved to unmapped statuses on the board, and they are assigned to a New Sprint once the current sprint ends, the reports for New Sprint do not behave consistently.    h3. Steps to Reproduce       # Add a status to the default workflow called """"Cancelled""""     # add a post function to set the resolution to """"Won't do""""     # on the scrum board make sure """"Cancelled"""" is in the """"unmapped statuses column     # Start a sprint (let's assume you have two issues in it ISSUE-1 and ISSUE-2)     # <USER>ISSUE-1 as Cancelled (obviously by a method other than drag and drop     # See how it is no longer visible on the board     # Check the sprint report for this sprint, see how it lists ISSUE-1 as """"removed from sprint""""     # Now close the sprint. It will tell you that 2 issues were incomplete (I would expect to see 1)     # Choose to move the incomplete issues to a new sprint.     # See how there is only 1 issue showing in the backlog for the sprint.     # Look at ISSUE-1, see how it says it's in the new future sprint.     # Start the new sprint.     # See how the sprint report for the new sprint does not include ISSUE-1 in the list of issues added to the sprint.     # Check the Burndown Chart for the new sprint, ISSUE-1 is showing there.    h3. Expected Results    Either the unmapped issue would be considered added/not added to the sprint by both reports.    h3. Actual Results    The issue is considered to have been added to the sprint by the Burndown Chart.     !Screen Shot 2019-09-02 at 11.20.34.png|thumbnail!   The issue is considered to not have been added to the sprint by the Sprint Report.   !Screen Shot 2019-09-02 at 12.23.36.png|thumbnail!   h3. Workaround    Mapping the statuses to a column (if the status is """"Cancelled"""", map the status to the """"Done"""" column).  """

summary: "Updating Issue on Agile Board causes contents of comment box to be erased"
description: """h6. Steps to Reproduce:    1. Open kanban board  2. Start typing a comment but don't save the comment yet.  3. Switch to a different app temporarily (just defocus click the browser)  4. Have another team member update another card on the board  5. Click anywhere in the screen to refresh the board view      h6. Expected results:  The comment should remain in the preview box    h6. Actual results:  The comment is wiped"""

summary: "Jira displays unrelated board to project"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a user open a board by following specific steps below, Jira will display board unrelated to the other board/project:    +Steps to Reproduce+    # Create 2 Software projects. Ensure that each of the projects has its own board.  # Let's assume that the project and board name are: TP1 (for Board/Project 1) and TP2 (for Board/Project 2).  # Create another board for Board/Project 2 (Let's call it TP3). Ensure when you clicked on """"BOARDS IN THIS PROJECT"""", Jira displays 2 board.  # Access Dashboard page.  # From Dashboard click on Boards (top bar) -> open TP1 & TP2 in separate tabs.  # Access TP2 board and click on """"BOARDS IN THIS PROJECT"""" and click on *TP3* board.  # Access *TP1* board now and hover over """"BOARDS IN THIS PROJECT"""". Jira will display TP1 & TP3 board.  # Clicking on TP3 board will result in Jira showing TP3 board but TP3 board will not be linked to TP1 project/board.      h3. Environment    Jira 8.1, 8.3      h3. Expected Results    * TP1 board only shows single board (TP1 board) since other boards' board filters are not retrieving issues from TP1 project.    h3. Actual Results    * TP1 board shows TP3 board under """"BOARDS IN THIS PROJECT"""".      h3. Workaround    Refresh the board page and the incorrect board link will be removed."""

summary: "Jira Software overrides PermissionScheme set in Blueprint Template Hook"
description: """h3. Summary    Bug raised in Developer <USER> https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/browse/DEVHELP-1990  {quote}  # Create a Project Blueprint/Template and ensure the type is 'Software'  # In the Hook called after the project is initially created, add code to update the 'Custom' Permission Scheme  # Inspect the project created  # This is caused by a Handler in Greenhopper code called on ProjectCreated – If it determines that the project is of type Software, then it overrides the permission scheme.    This is unexpected! You should be setting those configuration items in the Hook! Using a listener prevents us from making this work correctly!  {quote}    h3. Notes  * I've asked Jira Server which class is this """"Handler"""", is it by design, and what could be the reason for that but got no response. Putting it here for more expertise"""

summary: "DVCS sync runs indefinitely when an admin change was made in GitHub mid sync"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  If during long-running syncs to a large repository, there is an administrative change that alters the repository, i.e. the repo is locked and some data is moved since GHE doesn't send an error or status update, the repos sync will continue ad nauseam.   .   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Link a Git Hub Enterprise Account to JIRA   # Start a repository sync     # Move or lock the repo    h3. Expected Results  JIRA would eventually timeout   h3. Actual Results  JIRA continues to sync indefinitely     h3. Workaround  JIRA will need to be restarted.   A rolling restart of all nodes will need to take place for JIRA Data Center s no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Sprint Health Gadget return incorrect JQL when Status contains a ""&"" symbol"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Sprint Health Gadget return incorrect JQL when Status contains a """"&"""" symbol when clicking on *View in Issue Navigator* link    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a new status that contain """"&"""" symbol. (Example: Dev & UI)  # Add the status *Dev & UI* to the workflow.  # Go to Board configuration > Column management > Drag the status *Dev & UI* to one of the column (Example: To do).  # Go to Dashboard and add a new Sprint Health Gadget.  # Click on To do progress bar > Click on View in Issue Navigator.    h3. Expected Results  JIRA will open Issue navigator with JQL:    h3. Actual Results  JIRA will open Issue navigator with JQL below cut off after """"&"""" symbol:     !Issue Navigator - Jira 824 - Google Chrome 2019-09-26 14.25.02.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  As a workaround, you would need to remove """"&"""" symbol from the affected status name."""

summary: "After installing Jira Software 8.4.1 .obr, Software version is still listed as 8.4.0"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After installing Jira Software 8.4.1 .obr, Software version is still listed as 8.4.0.         h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Upgrade Jira Core to 8.4.1   # Download and install the Jira Software 8.4.1 package from the [Jira Software Version History|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1213607/<USER>software/version-history] page.    # The overall Jira version is shown as 8.4.1, but Software shows 8.4.0.     # Unzip the .obr file and examine the obr.xml - you will see version='8.4.0.DAILY20190821092223'>    h3. Expected Results    Jira Software will be version 8.4.1.  h3. Actual Results    Jira Software is listed at 8.4.0.       h3. Workaround       Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available.  """

summary: "Date picker from ""Release version"" action is broken with changed language"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The date picker for """"Release date"""" in the popup for """"Release version"""" action is broken. It shows row of words """"undefined"""" instead of table with days in the month. This is happening when any of the following languages are used:   * 中文 (中国)   * čeština (Česká republika)   * Dansk (Danmark)   * Nederlands (Nederland)   * suomi (Suomi)   * français (France)   * Deutsch (Deutschland)   * magyar (Magyarország)   * italiano (Italia)   * 日本語 (日本)   * 한국어 (대한민국)   * norsk (Norge)   * português (Brasil)   * română (România)   * русский (Россия)   * Slovenčina (Slovenská republika)   * svenska (Sverige)    The languages that are not affected:   * English (UK)   * English (United States)   * polski (Polska)   * español (España)    The languages for which the date picker reports unsupportability popup   * Eesti (Eesti)   * íslenska (Ísland)    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Run fresh Jira instance   # Create project with sample data   ## Use """"Scrum software development"""" project type   # Switch language to Deutsch   # Navigate to """"Releases"""" view of the create project   ## From application header click """"Projekte"""" and select created project   ## From left sidebar click """"Releases""""   # For """"Version 3"""" (with 2 unresolved issues) row click the """"..."""" button under """"Actions"""" (""""Aktionen"""")   # Select """"Release"""" (""""Veröffentlichen"""")   # Click the date picker for """"Release date"""" (""""Veröffentlichungsdatum"""")    h3. Expected Results    Date picker is rendered properly:   !Screenshot 2019-09-27 at 13.28.29.png|thumbnail!  h3. Actual Results    Date picker is broken:   !Screenshot 2019-09-27 at 13.26.54.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Importing Issues via Json are not rendered properly by the BurnDown Chart"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Import Issues via JSON, All issues have same """"issue added to sprint"""" date in BurnDown chart, even if they were added to sprint on a later date.      h3. Steps to Reproduce  Create 2 instances J1 and J2   On J1  # On 27th Sep Add 4 issues to sprint  # On 30th Sep Add 1 more issue to a sprint  # Burn Down would look like  !image-2019-09-30-22-04-49-115.png|thumbnail!   # Enable JSON Exporter [How to enable JSON export in Jira server|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-enable-json-export-in-<USER>723158427.html]  # Export all the issues from Project.    On J2  # Import the JSON file Exported from J1  # The BrunDown chart looks like  !image-2019-09-30-22-07-12-291.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  Burn Down chart must display the proper date when issue added to the Sprint  h3. Actual Results  Burn Down Chart does not display the proper date even if Issue History is completely restored by Jira Import.    h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: " Under the ""Issue Detail View"", when editing the story points, often the ""Story Points"" mouse over will block the check mark from being clickable."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When editing the Estimate field value on the """"Issue Detail View"""", the mouse over that displays """"Story Points"""" may block the checkmark created by the application to confirm the fields' new value. The only way that the user can insert the new value is by pressing the """"Enter"""" key.    h3. Environment    * Any clean instance.  * Mozilla Firefox and Microsoft Edge    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new Software Project.   # Navigate to the """"Issue Detail View"""" through the backlog (for example).   # Click on the pencil icon to edit the *Estimate* value.   # Mouse over the field.   # This will cause the description of the field and the """"Story Points"""" mouse over to be displayed.   # Attempt to change the fields' value.    h3. Expected Results    * You should be able to click on the checkmark to confirm the fields' new value.     !Screen Shot 2019-09-30 at 22.28.34.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results    * You can not click on the checkmark because it is blocked by the """"Story Points"""" mouseover.      !Screen Shot 2019-09-30 at 22.30.58.png|thumbnail!       h3. Workaround    You can insert the new value by pressing the """"Enter"""" key instead of clicking on the checkmark."""

summary: """View on board"" hyperlink does not work for issues within the collapsed view"
description: """h3. Summary  In JIRA 7.9.2, there is no collapsed view for issues in the board so after clicking on the """"View on Board"""" link, the issue is displayed immediately on the board.    It turns out that In JIRA 8.3.2, issues in the board could be collapsed so clicking on """"View on Board"""" link does not open the issue straight away if that issue is under the collapsed items.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Deploy a clean JIRA 8.3.2 instance;  # Create a JIRA Software Scrum project;  # Use [Data Generator for JIRA|https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1210725/data-generator-for-<USER>hosting=server&tab=overview] add-on to create 1K issues against the Scrum project.  # Go to the Scrum project board backlog and find the collapsed view  !Screen Shot 2019-10-07 at 16.17.11.png|thumbnail!   # Expand the collapsed view and take note of one issue key that is within the collapsed view;  # View the issue key using http://<USER>server/browse/issue-key URL  # Click on the """"View on board"""" link   !Screen Shot 2019-10-07 at 16.20.20.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results  * To get redirected to the issue on the board.    h3. Actual Results  * The board is opened without redirecting to the issue in question.    h3. Workaround  * Expand the collapsed view and use the browser search feature to locate the issue key on the board.  """

summary: "Jira Software documentation incorrectly states that Epic Link does not support auto-complete"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Per [JSWSERVER-8665 : Autocomplete for Epic Link in JQL|https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/JSWSERVER-8665], Auto-completing functionality for the """"Epic Link"""" field was introduced in Jira Agile 6.2.2, and this was formally written into the release notes for Jira Agile 6.3: [JIRA Agile 6.3 Release Notes|https://confluence.atlassian.com/agile/<USER>agile-releases/<USER>agile-release-notes/<USER>agile-6-3-release-notes]    However, documentation for [https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/advanced-searching-fields-reference-939938743.html#Advancedsearching-fieldsreference-epic_linkEpiclink] incorrectly states that Auto-complete is not possible with the Epic Link field. This can be seen as far back as 7.0 documentation through latest (8.4 at the time of writing).    Existing documentation as well as templates for future documentation releases need to be revised in order to clarify that Epic Link supports auto-completion.    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Having more than 65,000 closed sprints breaks the JQL function closedSprints()"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Having more than 65,000 closed sprints breaks the JQL function closedSprints() with an com.atlassian.<USER>issue.search.ClauseTooComplexSearchException  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # install Jira Software   # create a Scrum Development Project 'Test'   # create sample issues and 65,001 closed sprints   # test the JQL """"project = Test AND sprint in closedSprints()""""    h3. Expected Results    issues are listed without errors  h3. Actual Results    JQL fails with error      h3. Notes    Even if there is a sensible workaround as stated below, similar bugs like JSWSERVER-16401 were resolved with optimizations in the JQL function because using the workaround may impact the performance of the system.  h3. Workaround    Increase the default limit of 65000, to accommodate for the number of closed sprints.   (!) Setting the parameter too high may have a performance impact on the system.    This can be done by increasing the value for {{<USER>search.maxclauses}} in [<USER>config.properties|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/edit-the-<USER>config-properties-file-in-<USER>server-317194938.html].    You may find the number of closed sprints with the SQL statement below  """

summary: "Searching and Mentioning users may cause performance issues and high CPU load"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Certain functionalities in Jira, such as trying to mention users or otherwise typing usernames into user fields, allow Jira to return user suggestions as usernames are being typed out.     As the searching user types, Jira will use a thread to perform a search against what they've typed and return possible results back to the user. In instances with many users, directories, or nested groups, these searches may be computationally expensive and may run for several seconds in order to return results.    It is possible for the searching user to continue typing and end up making multiple user search requests before the previous requests ever complete and return. Jira will not cancel or otherwise terminate previously ongoing search threads in this case.    The instance may experience high CPU load and thread utilization if too many of these threads are working on these searches concurrently.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Have many users, groups/nested groups, and directories.  # Attempt to search for a user (i.e. by mentioning or perhaps trying to assign an issue), by typing a character out at a pace of 2-3 characters/second.    h3. Expected Results    Suggested users will be returned, and no other behaviors (high CPU/thread usage) are observed.    h3. Actual Results    Suggested users may be returned, but CPU and thread usage run high. Excessive usage may cause the system to become unresponsive.     Thread Dumps show many threads like the following, which showcase a user mention being searched against nested groups in this particular example:      Access Logs show individual HTTP requests being made for each character typed:      h3. Workaround    Reducing the complexity and dataset of users, groups, nested groups, and directories may help reduce the calculation cost and time of these searches.    h3. Suggested Solution    Allow administrators to configure the frequency at which the browser may submit search requests."""

summary: "Column header is not preserved when scrolling a board on Safari"
description: """*Problem*  When we are using a board with swim lanes the column headers are not sticky when scrolling through issues, resulting in issues scrolling behind the swimlane header.     This breaks up the visibility of the board and makes it look messy at the very least.    *Environment*  Can be replicated in Safar v13.0 and 13.0.2.     *Steps to Reproduce*  1. Create or view a board with a long list of issues or resize the browser window vertically to reduce the available screen space.  2. Depending on the OS version, you may need to change system settings to 'Show Scroll bars: Always'. In Mac OS - System Preferences > General.  3. Scroll the board.    *Expected Results*  The header of the columns remains while scrolling down.    *Actual Results*  Header does not remain static when scrolling on the board.    *Workaround*  The issue is not reproduced when using Firefox or Chrome browsers."""

summary: "error with inline creation of issue from board if multiple context with the same value are set for a custom field "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a custom field is created with 2 different context (one global and one set on a specific project) but the same value is in both context then is not possible to create issue from a board where the filter is set on the value of the custom field which is common in both context    I have been able to reproduce it in Jira 8.5.1 but not in any 7.x.x. The issue is reproducible as well for multiple contexts set on specific and different projects.      h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a custom field (Select list single choice) called *Team*   # Create *2 context* for this custom field, one *global* and one *restricted to a specific project TEST*   # Add the field to the TEST project   # For the global context add the values *Team A and Team B*   # For the project context add the values *Team B and Team C*   !Screenshot 2019-11-12 at 14.29.23.png|thumbnail!    # Create a board for the project TEST called TEAM C where the filter set for the board is:    #Try to create an issue inline from the Board TEAM C. The issue is correctly created with the value Team C in.     # Create a board for the project TEST called TEAM B where the filter set for the board is:    #Try to create an issue inline from the Board TEAM B. The issue is created but is not displayed in the board and the following message is displayed:   !Screenshot 2019-11-12 at 13.05.18.png|thumbnail!      # When opening and editing the issue we see that the value of the field Team is set on None.    This is because the in the global context and in the project restricted context the values has the same name and someway overlap  h3. Expected Results    The issue should be created and shown into the board  h3. Actual Results    The issue is created but not shown into the board  h3. Workaround    For now the workaround is to call the value in the restricted to the project context with a similar but not exact name of the other in the global context"""

description: """h3. summary: "Lucene statistics for RAMDirectory type are logged as INFO level when it is flushed."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The Lucene statistics for the type of directory {{RAMDirectory}} are written to the Jira log files as {{INFO}} level  when this type of directory is flushed, which generates additional logging for an event that is not expected to be logged at {{INFO}} level.    It was found in the logs files analyzed (please note that this can vary for each deployment) that 25% of the Lucene statistics generated belonged to the flushing of {{RAMDirectory}}.    It is important to note that for any other type of index this log provides valuable statistics which could be use to proactively identify possible index performance degradation.     h3. Steps to Reproduce    There are not steps to reproduce the issue.      h3. Expected Results    Flushing an {{IndexWriter}} of a RAMDirectory should not be logged as {{INFO}} log level, however it could be considered as {{TRACE}} log level instead.    h3. Actual Results    The below log lines appear very often in the Jira log files.         h3. Workaround    Adding and Setting the {{com.atlassian.<USER>index.MonitoringIndexWriter}} package log level to {{ERROR}}.    \\"""

summary: "Changing site logo from custom to custom with transparent background prevents color customization"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    A custom logo on a transparent background prevents the workaround in JRASERVER-45829 from working.  Further color customization does not persist.   h3. See that the header color probably changed colors again (if it was just transparent and white it will change it back to """"default"""" colors, or if you have any other color in the logo it will change it to that)   5.
