Notice how the actually visible colors haven't changed, but the color selections in the look and feel section show the colors from before correctly.   7. Attempting to reset the colors by manually typing in the color codes (the workaround in JRASERVER-45829 ) does not work.  h3. Expected Results    You should be able to modify the colors manually.   h3. Actual Results    The color selection cannot be modified.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Configure a board such that the card colors are based on a query, for example, used a query """"assignee is not EMPTY"""" (although you could use any query that would match at least one of the cards on the board)   # View the cards on the board to confirm that at least some of the cards are colored.   # Select """"Print Cards""""   # In the Print Preview, you can see that the card does not contain color.    h3. Expected Results    The card should have a color same as board.  h3. Actual Results    The card does not contain color   !image-2020-01-13-15-55-34-134.png!    !image-2020-01-13-15-56-19-240.png!  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira allows issues to be updated with Archived Version until page is refreshed"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Version Fields (AffectedVersion/FixVersion) would allow us to select Already Archived versions if the issue view page or Edit Page is loaded *before* the deletion and is *not* refreshed.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Have the {{[http://JIRA-URL/browse/Issue-key|http://<USER>url/browse/Issue-key]}} page open in one tab   # Archive an existing version:  !Version_Archived.png|thumbnail!   # You can still select the Archived version in AffectedVersion/FixVersion field  !Allowing_to_update_issue_with_Archived_version.png|thumbnail!  h3. Expected Results    Even though the page shows the Version which is now archived (Since the page is loaded *before* the version was archived), once trying to save it should prompt an error saying """"Version is already archived"""" or similar  h3. Actual Results    Jira still allows us to update the issue with an Archived version  h3. Workaround    Refresh the page is the only workaround available.   (flag) The concern is users might not be aware whether a version is already archived while having the issue view/edit screen opened and since Jira allows you to still update the issue with the archived version, this can cause trouble."""

Issue Summary  When you transition the issue from one column to the other (without clicking the issue to open the issue details on the right-side panel), the comment textbox is not Wiki style formatted       h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to any Kanban/Scrum board  # Make sure that the destination column/status in the workflow has a Transition view  # Drag the issue to the destination column/status. Don't click the issue as it will open the issue details on the right-side panel. In this way, the issue won't be replicated.  # When the transition screen pops out, notice the comment textbox    h3. Expected Results  The comment box is in Wiki style format  !wiki.png|thumbnail!    h3. Actual Results  The comment textbox is in plain/rich style format  !rich.png|thumbnail!    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Select List or any custom field type.   # Add a default value to the Select List custom field.   # Go to any Scrum board with a project with the Select List added to its screens.   # Create a Story issue. (You will notice the default value is added)  # Split the issue. (You will notice the split issue is not added with the default value)    h3. Expected Results    The default value will be added to the split issue.     h3. Actual Results    The default value is not added to the split issue.     h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Fix Version is remove from JIRA issue when you edit the issue and change version from archive to unarchive simultaneously"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Fix Version is remove from JIRA issue when you edit the issue and change version from archive to unarchive simultaneously.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create new issue in JIRA Software project which Fix Version field is visible.  # Create a new version. (Ex: *Version123*) in Project > Releases page.  # Add *Version123* to the new issue.  # On Releases page > Move *Version123* to archive.  # Open the issue in another page, click Edit issue. You would see the* Version123* is listed on Archived Fix Version/s field.  # On Releases page > Move back *Version123* to unarchive.  # Click *update* button on Issue detail page.    h3. Expected Results  JIRA will show Version123 in the Fix Version field.    h3. Actual Results  Version123 is removed from the Fix Version field.    h3. Workaround  The only workaround at the moment is to refresh the edit issue page after making any changes on Releases page in JIRA.    (flag) The concern here is that, user might not aware and accidentally remove the Fix Version while Editing the issue and changing the Version at the same time.  """

summary: "Rich text editor unable to set Polish character 'ś' in MS Edge using Visual Mode"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After upgrade JIRA from v7.13.8#713008-sha1:1606a5c to v8.6.1#806001-sha1:b7cf80b   Some user giving us feedback about using Alt+s shortcut when editing the description, adding or editing comments it saving their actions in <USER>Text Editor Visual mode.   In Poland Alt+s is using for """"ś"""" it's one of the diacritical marks.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # On Windows using Internet Explorer or Microsoft Edge   # Install keyboard layouts: Polish (Programmers)   # Choose Visual mode.   # While editing description or comment press AltGr + s to get character 'ś'.    h3. Expected Results    Character *'ś'* is showing upon pressing AltGr + s.  h3. Actual Results    It's blank and seems likely to have a refresh effect. However, it works fine in Firefox and Chrome using Visual and Text mode.  h3. Workaround    There is no workaround at the moment. User can use Firefox and Chrome that has the correct behavior."""

summary: "Logged time, using a date before the issue creation date, does not appear on Burndown Report"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    If a user logs time on an issue, and selects a date that is _before_ the issue creation date, these logged times will not appear within the Burndown Chart Report.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create Sprint, and then create and add an issue to it.   # Start sprint, log time on the issue for a date *_before_* the issue creation date, and then close the issue.   # Complete Sprint   # View Burndown Chart for created Sprint    h3. Expected Results    Time logged before issue creation date would appear within report.  h3. Actual Results    Time logged before issue creation date does not appear within report.  h3. Workaround    None"""

summary: "Unable to add a Single user to the shared dashboard to get the edit permission"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    - User added to the shared dashboard to have the Edit permission, doesn't get/have the edit permission if the user is not part of the group which has been granted Edit permission.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # User 1 has created a dashboard and giving permission to a group 1 (which the user1 is member of)   # User 1 can give edit permission only to the group that he is a member of and cannot get the list of other groups that he is not part of, there is an existing [bug|https://<USER>atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-15900] has been created to have that feature (_The user or the admin, can list other groups that they or not part of can be added to Edit permission of shared dashboard_)   # User's part of group 1 gets the Edit permission including the owner, who is user 1   # If you are giving permission to another user, user2 who is not a member of group 1 will not get edit permission. (_If there is no group given edit permission other than this user2, then the dashboard is editable for this user 2_)   # Giving edit permission to both groups and users still not possible in Jira.    h3. Expected Results  - The single user who is not a member of the group, (_which has granted to have the edit permission to the shared dashboard_) should get the Edit permission of the dashboard.    h3. Actual Results  - The single-user still has only read access and doesn't get any Edit permission.    h3. Workaround  - Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Jira throws 'issue type selected is invalid error' when moving an issue to the default Sub-Task issue type"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Attempting to move an issue from a custom sub-task type to default Sub-task issue type will fail with an """"*issue type selected is invalid*"""" error. Regardless of the issue type scheme used by the project, the new target issue type is validated against the *Default Issue Type Scheme*, which _does_ _not_ include Sub-Task in Jira Software.    This behavior looks to be an offshoot of JRASERVER-46227 — which was fixed for parent tasks — but the issue persists for sub-task issue types.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Jira software (reproduced in 7.x and 8.x)   # Create a new Software project   # Create a new sub-task issue type, e.g. """"Test Sub-Task""""   # Assign the new sub-task issue type to the project's issue type scheme   !Screen Shot 2020-03-05 at 10.50.24 AM.png|thumbnail!    # Create a new issue in the project of the new sub-task issue type   # Attempt to move the issue _within the same project_ and change the issue type to """"Sub-Task""""   !Screen Shot 2020-03-05 at 10.46.11 AM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results    The issue is moved to the new issue type.    h3. Actual Results  We'll receive an """"issue type selected is invalid"""" error:  !Screen Shot 2020-03-05 at 10.46.03 AM.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround  You can change the issue type using the bulk edit feature on the issue search page. Follow the instructions for moving issues in [our knowledge-base article on editing multiple issues at the same time|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jiracoreserver085/editing-multiple-issues-at-the-same-time-981157689.html].    If bulk edit is not an acceptable workaround, you can follow the advice in [this KB|https://confluence.atlassian.com/display/JIRAKB/Unable+to+move+issue+to+another+Issue+Type+due+to+%27The+issue+type+selected+is+invalid%27+error] to add the default Sub-Task issue type to the Default Issue Type scheme."""

summary: "Deleting the Card colour Sorted by Assignee does not take place after Refresh"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Deleting the users under Card Colours sort by Assignee does not work after refreshing the page.       h3. Steps to Reproduce  # In the board, go to Board > Configure > Card Colors   # Configure it to _Assignee_  # There are about 6 assignees in the board  !1.png|thumbnail!  # I deleted _Apple_ and _Test_ user from the Card colors  !2.png|thumbnail!  # Refresh the page    h3. Expected Results  The users that I deleted will vanish even after refresh.  h3. Actual Results  # After the refresh, the two users that I deleted are back.  !3.png|thumbnail!    h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

Actual Results    No such action to be found.  h3. Workaround    A mouse. No workaround for a keyboard only users."""

summary: "Integrity Checker for Filter Subscription returns filters as non existent although the filters are present in DB and the UI"
description: """h3. Summary  The integrity checker returns errors on filter subscriptions when the Active Directory is disabled although the subscriptions are still present in the DB and UI.     h3. Steps to reproduce,  * With an AD or any LDAP user create a filter subscription  * Disable the user or AD itself and run the integrity checker for Invalid Filer Subscription. Errors, as attached in the screenshot, are observed.    !Screenshot 2020-03-13 at 12.20.11 AM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  When checked in the DB and UI these filters are present and the user has to manually resubscribe to them.      h3. Expected Results  There was a feature request, JRASERVER-18919 which was turned down a long time back, but it should be revisited again.       """

summary: "Incorrect reporting for custom fields (Sprint) in JQL search after a change from linked application fisheye/crucible"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Incorrect reporting for custom fields (Sprint) in JQL search after a change from linked application fisheye/crucible    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Set system property _jira.devstatus.max.applink.data.provider.threads=4_ . This step is required to easily reproduce the issue but issue can also happen without this step.   # Create a Jira ticket and assign some value to custom field e.g. sprint.   # Run a JQL to search based on a custom field so that it returns newly created ticket.   # The ticket is shown as expected   # Make some changes in fisheye for this ticket. Try few times - open in 5-6 windows in parallel to reproduce the issue easily.   # Update sprint for the ticket to something else.   # Make some change in fisheye for this ticket.   # Run another JQL search same to one ran earlier.    h3. Expected Results  * Ticket should not be shown as it was moved to different sprint.    h3. Actual Results  * Ticket is shown in the incorrect sprint.    h3. Workaround  * Perform a full reindex."""

summary: "Closing a sprint generates different versions of the same sprint report. "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Different versions of the same sprint report are generated based on the boards' filter configuration.    h3. Steps to Reproduce     # Create two boards that share a similar filter.   # Set the filter for Bug Board P1  as    # Set the filter for Board P1 as    # Insert the *Resolved* status into the default workflow, mark the *Allow all statuses to transition to this one* checkbox. Save the new workflow.   # Map the *Resolved* status into the *Done* column of the board.   # Create a Sprint into one of the boards and add issues.   # Move issues around on the columns and then leave them at the *Done* column, but leave a couple of issues in the Resolved status.   # Complete the sprint in one of the boards.   # The *Sprint Report* will be generated.   # Complete this sprint and check the reports.      h3. Expected Results     * The sprint report information displayed should be the same for each of the boards.    h3. Actual Results     The screenshots outline the different versions of the reports.     !Screen Shot 2020-03-18 at 06.51.28.png|thumbnail!      !Screen Shot 2020-03-18 at 06.52.42.png|thumbnail!   h3. Workaround     Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

description: """h3. Issue Summary    A very long (~2 year, planned Feb 2018 - Feb 2020, but opened and closed on the same day in Feb 2020) sprint looks like on the screenshot   !Screenshot 2020-03-17 at 8.53.28 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce       # Create starting from an old date such as Feb 2018 and start it   # Close the sprint   # Go to burnup chart    h3. Expected Results         X axis shows proper dates and Lines are displays properly  h3. Actual Results         Only recent dates is shown in X axis and Guide line goes outside the chart (screenshot attached)      h3. Workaround       Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available     """

summary: "Closing the sprint on different board than it was started can cause that some issue will land in the backlog."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Some of the incomplete issues can be moved to a backlog, not to a new sprint, if we close the sprint from a board with a different filter than sprint was started, even if the complete sprint dialog said that all incomplete issue will be moved to a new sprint.    It is because complete sprint dialog shows the number of incomplete issues based on the sprint custom field. However, Jira moves issues based on the board filter.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create the new scrum project. Project name - *Da*,   # Create 6 issues. Issue names - *1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6*,   # For the issues *2,4,6* set the fix version to *1.0*,   # Create new sprint,   # Move *all* the issues to a new sprint,   # Start new sprint,   # Move issues *3* and *4* to """"*In-progress*"""" state,   # Move issues *5* and *6* to """"*Done*"""" state.   !image-2020-03-25-10-01-16-409.png!   # Change the board filter from    to    !image-2020-03-25-10-12-22-126.png!     # Complete the sprint and move the incomplete issues to New sprint   !image-2020-03-25-10-05-07-633.png!    h3. Expected Results    There are two potential fixes.    First one:   * Complete sprint dialog shows a number of the issues in the sprint - works like now     * Jira moves all incomplete issues to destiny sprint not only those on the current board - works differently    Second one:   * Complete sprit dialog shows a number of the issues based on the current board - works differently     * Jira moves incomplete issues from the current board a to destiny sprint and remains issues to a backlog - works like now    h3. Actual Results    Complete sprint dialog shows a number of the issues in the sprint   !image-2020-03-25-10-05-07-633.png!    Issues *2,4* are moved to a new sprint and issues *1,3* get back to backlog. To see 1,3 issues you need to get back to old filter or create a new board.   !image-2020-03-25-10-27-39-248.png!  h3. Workaround    Do not change the filter rather create the new board with a new filter and complete the sprint from the old board where the filter returns all the issues from the sprint."""

summary: "Hyperlinking PDF document in Jira Introduction breaks in Jira 8.x"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In the Jira Introduction page, when a PDF attachment is hyperlinked within the HTML code, the page breaks when the user clicks the hyperlink. The pdf file is hosted within the same Jira server. This is mainly observed in Jira 8.x release  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Place a pdf file in the Jira Web server.   # Hyperlink this file in an HTML code and place it in the Jira Introduction page.   # When the user clicks this link, the page breaks. Please find the screenshots attached.    h3. Expected Results    When the user clicks the link, the pdf should open in a new browser window without any problems.  h3. Actual Results    Page breaks as shown in the screenshot attached.  h3. Workaround    The workaround is to host the pdf file outside the Jira web server and then use that location in the HTML code."""

summary: "Sprint Burndown gadget is empty in Jira 8.8.0"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    After upgrading to Jira 8.8.0 (even fresh install), the Sprint burndown gadget is blank.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add the Sprint Burndown gadget in the dashboard   # Select any existing board in the instance and then save.    h3. Expected Results    The gadget will show the graphical sprint burndown chart data  h3. Actual Results    The gadget is empty. Notice the browser console (on Chrome) as it shows:      Firefox's browser console can display a different message:      h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for the gadget to work. However, the same content/functionality is present on the Burndown Chart Report, which can be used as a replacement until the gadget will be fixed.    *Tested in Jira 8.6.1 and the gadget works as expected*."""

summary: "The context menu will not stay open when CTRL-clicking Sprints in Safari web browser"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  In Jira 8.8.0, when using Safari web browser, the context menu will not stay open after CTRL-clicking the Sprints.    The problem does not occur when two-finger tapping or right-clicking via mouse or using other web Browsers.    It also has been tested to not occur in Jira 8.7.1.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Log into Jira 8.8.0 via Safari  # Go to any Sprints  # CTRL-click any Sprints  # Notice the context menu will open and almost instantly closes, despite no additional clicks made    h3. Expected Results  The context menu will open and stay    h3. Actual Results  The context menu will open and almost instantly disappears    Video of replicating the problem:  [^ReplicationVideo.MOV]     h3. Workaround  * Right-click via mouse  * Two-finger tap  * Use other web browsers"""

summary: "Dashboard not editable on dashboard page if owner is removed from group"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Dashboard owner will lose the ability to edit their dashboard if they are removed from a group that the dashboard was shared with.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Add dashboard owner to a group  # Share their dashboard with this group  # Remove the dashboard owner from the group      h3. Expected Results  Dashboard owner should still be able to edit their dashboard within the dashboard page.   !while in group.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  Dashboard owner no longer able to edit their dashboard within the dashboard page.   !removed from group.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Add the dashboard owner back to the group or go to the *manage dashboard* page and remove the group from the share.    h3. Suggestion  # Let the dashboard owner know via warning message that group needs to be removed from the share as it no longer exists or they no longer are part of that group before they can change the layout and add gadgets on the dashboard page.  # Or silently remove the group from the dashboard share as the owner is no longer part of the group or the group no longer exists."""

Actual Results    As per the screenshot 2   !Screenshot2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Watch function in Boards causes Error 500 when using multiple Quick Filters"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When using Boards with more than 1 Quick Filters applied, using the Watch function in the Quick view (not Issue directly) of the selected Issue would cause Error 500. This seems to be due to the URL changing to an incorrect form when the page refreshes.  (i) Affects both Watch and Unwatch actions  (i) Affects both Scrum and Kanban Boards    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Access a Board  # Apply 2 Quick Filters  ** Take note of the URL  # Click 1 Issue to prompt the Quick View  # Click the ellipsis icon (three dots) in the Quick View then click 'Watch Issue' or 'Stop watching'  # The page will refresh and the Error 500 occurs  ** Notice the URL has changed to an incorrect form    h3. Expected Results  The URL maintains the correct form while the page refreshes    Expected URL would look like this  (i) Note this is not the full URL. Also the IDs would be dependent on each use case      h3. Actual Results  The URL changes to an incorrect form while the page refreshes, leading to Error 500    The incorrect URL would look like this:    Notice the '%2C' part followed by the ID of the subsequent filter(s)    h3. Workaround  * Watch/Unwatch the Issue in the direct Issue Details view (not from the Quick view in the Board)  * Upgrade to Jira 8.8.0 (this version has been tested not facing this Bug)"""

summary: "Tables are not rendered in Jira Server Mobile App for iOS"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Tables are not rendered in Jira Server Mobile App for iOS    h3. Steps to Reproduce  Create tables in any text fields with Wiki renderer   !Screenshot 2020-04-30 at 9.44.23 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  Tables are shown properly    h3. Actual Results  Tables are not rendered at all   !Screenshot 2020-04-30 at 9.43.52 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround     Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Users are still able to access the Board despite not having permission"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When a user does not have permission to view a Board, should they use the Board's URL, they will still be brought to the Board. Though not able to see any Issues and the error message stating that the user is not able to view the Board, they are still able to see the Board's name and Quick Filters' name. This leads to a form of information leak. As the Board's ID is sequential, the user could exploit this further by manually modifying the ID in the URL to view other Boards.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Board  # Configure the Board's Share (Viewers & Modifiers) to only 1 Group  # Login as a User that is not part of that Group, access the Board via the URL  (i) The Jira instance may need to be re-indexed first  # Notice that the user is able to see the Board and Quick Filters' name despite not having permission to view the Board:   !BoardError.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results  The Board is not accessible at all    h3. Actual Results  Board is accessible. Though the Issues are not viewable, the Board and Quick Filters' names are viewable.   !BoardError.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Link to profiles in ""Users & Roles"" screen doesn't work"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When user tries to access Profile via """"User and roles"""" screen it always takes /<USER>as context path not matter which context path is set in server.xml file  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set a context path /pm in server.xml file       # Restart Jira server and login to Jira UI: http://<ip address>:<port>/pm   # After logging in, navigate to Project Setting --> User and Roles --> <Select a User profile>    h3. Expected Results    It will open the User profile with link: http://<ip address>:port/pm/secure/ViewProfile.jspa?name=admin  h3. Actual Results    It gives the error: 404 page not found since the link is redirected to http://<ip address>:<port>/<USER>secure/ViewProfile.jspa?name=admin  h3. Workaround    Need to manually change the context path from /java to context path set in server.xml file (here /pm) and then it works. However, this is not a perfect workaround."""

summary: "Unable to edit Sprint when Remaining Estimate and Time Spent is enabled."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Unable to edit Sprint when *Remaining Estimate and Time Spent* is enabled.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Create a new Scrum board.  # Enable Remaining Estimate and Time Spent.  # Create a new Sprint in the board.  # Try to edit it.      h3. Expected Results    * User is able to edit the Sprint    h3. Actual Results    * Jira throws *clauseValues is empty* Javascript error and following exception is thrown in the atlassian-<USER>log file:        h3. Workaround    * Disable *Remaining Estimate and Time Spent* from board's estimate configuration page and users will be able to edit Sprint again."""

summary: "When language is set to Chinese, the development panel is not translated, and branches is incorrectly showing as commits"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When Jira Software Server is connected to a repository in github (or possibly bitbucket), and the end user's preferred language is set to Chinese (zh_CN), the name 'branches' is incorrectly displaying as an additional 'commits' entry.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Link Jira Software Server to a github repo  # create a branch, commit, and pull request each against a single issue (example issue key SSP-23)  # Convert the system language to Chinese OR convert your user profile language preference to Chinese  # Load SSP-23 issue and look at development info    h3. Expected Results  Expecting that these terms would be correctly translated into Chinese OR that if not translated, at least correctly display the terms in English.    h3. Actual Results  No only is the term 'branches' not translated, it is made to appear as the term 'commits' which leaves the end user very confused to see two entries there for commits.    See screenshots below:  When language is in English:   !inEnglish.png|thumbnail!   When language is in Chinese:   !failedtranslationtoChinese.png|thumbnail!       h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Delete Custom field options times out due to very large customfieldvalue table "
description: """h3. Summary  Custom field option deletion becomes extremely sluggish for large instances where {{customfieldvalue}} table is massive.  {{/<USER>secure/admin/DeleteCustomField!default.jspa}} or {{/secure/admin/EditCustomFieldOptions!remove.jspa}} calls take way too long to finish on the DB SELECT queries causing proxy timeout.    Example:          h3. Environment  * 8.5.x with very big {{customfieldvalue}} table      h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Step 1: Create a lot of issues using many """"Select List"""" type custom fields  # Step 2: Try to delete/modify the customfield options    h3. Expected Results  Query is returned in time and custom field options are deleted/modified    h3. Actual Results  * Access Logs:  {code}  127.0.0.1 625x11238113x5 xxxxxx [08/May/2020:10:52:55 +0200] """"GET /<USER>secure/admin/EditCustomFieldOptions!remove.jspa?fieldConfigId=19083&atl_token=xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx HTTP/1.1"""" 200 17399 1670121 """"https://<USER>example.com/<USER>secure/admin/EditCustomFieldOptions!default.jspa?atl_token=xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx_lin&fieldConfigSchemeId=19083&fieldConfigId=19083&customFieldId=16616&returnUrl=ConfigureCustomField%21default.jspa%3FcustomFieldId%3D16616"""" """"Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:76.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/76.0"""" """"1hoakcc""""  {code}    * atlassian-<USER>log:  {code}  2020-05-08 10:52:55,404+0200 http-nio-8080-exec-1519 url:/<USER>secure/ad...ptions!remove.jspa username:xxxxxxxxx url:/<USER>secure/ad...tomfieldoption.jsp username:xxxxxx ERROR xxxxxx 623x11234240x3 1hoakcc 1.1.1.1,2.2.2.2 /secure/admin/EditCustomFieldOptions!remove.jspa [webwork.util.ValueStack] query=""""affectedIssues/size"""" {[id=""""affectedIssues"""" type=""""8"""" values=""""""""]} {[id=""""size"""" type=""""8"""" values=""""""""]}  ...skipping...  Caused by: com.atlassian.<USER>exception.DataAccessException: org.ofbiz.core.entity.GenericDataSourceException: SQL Exception while executing the following:SELECT ID, ISSUE, CUSTOMFIELD, UPDATED, PARENTKEY, STRINGVALUE, NUMBERVALUE, TEXTVALUE, DATEVALUE, VALUETYPE FROM public.customfieldvalue WHERE CUSTOMFIELD=? AND STRINGVALUE=? AND VALUETYPE IS NULL (An I/O error occurred while sending to the backend.)          at com.atlassian.<USER>ofbiz.DefaultOfBizDelegator.findByAnd(DefaultOfBizDelegator.java:85)          at com.atlassian.<USER>ofbiz.WrappingOfBizDelegator.findByAnd(WrappingOfBizDelegator.java:62)          at com.atlassian.<USER>issue.customfields.persistence.OfBizCustomFieldValuePersister.getIssueIdsWithValue(OfBizCustomFieldValuePersister.java:83)          at com.atlassian.<USER>issue.customfields.impl.CascadingSelectCFType.getIssueIdsWithValue(CascadingSelectCFType.java:128)          at com.atlassian.<USER>web.action.admin.customfields.EditCustomFieldOptions.getAffectedIssues(EditCustomFieldOptions.java:299)  {code}    * execute command directly on db    {code:sql}  # SELECT ID, ISSUE, CUSTOMFIELD, UPDATED, PARENTKEY, STRINGVALUE, NUMBERVALUE, TEXTVALUE, DATEVALUE, VAL UETYPE FROM public.customfieldvalue WHERE CUSTOMFIELD=16616 AND STRINGVALUE='21797' AND VALUETYPE IS NULL""""   id | issue | customfield | updated | parentkey | stringvalue | numbervalue | textvalue | datevalue | valuetype  ----+-------+-------------+---------+-----------+-------------+-------------+-----------+-----------+-----------  (0 rows)      real    11m34.465s  user    0m0.007s  sys     0m0.004s  {code}    h3. Notes  This is the happy flow with very small {{customfieldvalue}} in a test instance:  1. Jira runs this SELECT query to find usage of the custom field option:  {code}  2020-05-10 12:34:19,300+0530 http-nio-8080-exec-5 admin 754x861x1 1jdq6ii /secure/admin/EditCustomFieldOptions!remove.jspa 4ms """"SELECT ID, ISSUE, CUSTOMFIELD, UPDATED, PARENTKEY, STRINGVALUE, NUMBERVALUE, TEXTVALUE, DATEVALUE, VALUETYPE FROM public.customfieldvalue WHERE CUSTOMFIELD='11300' AND STRINGVALUE='10603' AND VALUETYPE IS NULL""""  {code}  2. The returned query result populates the confirmation page:  !2020-05-14_15-14-17.png|thumbnail!  3. Once you hit delete, it iterates through each of those issues with individual SELECT queries followed by the DELETE query to delete the options from each issue keys:  {code}  2020-05-10 12:34:19,607+0530 http-nio-8080-exec-5 admin 754x861x1 1jdq6ii /secure/admin/EditCustomFieldOptions!remove.jspa 1ms """"SELECT ID, ISSUE, CUSTOMFIELD, UPDATED, PARENTKEY, STRINGVALUE, NUMBERVALUE, TEXTVALUE, DATEVALUE, VALUETYPE FROM public.customfieldvalue WHERE ISSUE='14848' AND CUSTOMFIELD='11300' AND STRINGVALUE='10603'""""  2020-05-10 12:34:19,609+0530 http-nio-8080-exec-5 admin 754x861x1 1jdq6ii /secure/admin/EditCustomFieldOptions!remove.jspa 1ms """"DELETE FROM public.customfieldvalue WHERE ID='23337'""""  --  2020-05-10 12:34:19,613+0530 http-nio-8080-exec-5 admin 754x861x1 1jdq6ii /secure/admin/EditCustomFieldOptions!remove.jspa 1ms """"SELECT ID, ISSUE, CUSTOMFIELD, UPDATED, PARENTKEY, STRINGVALUE, NUMBERVALUE, TEXTVALUE, DATEVALUE, VALUETYPE FROM public.customfieldvalue WHERE ISSUE='14849' AND CUSTOMFIELD='11300' AND STRINGVALUE='10603'""""  2020-05-10 12:34:19,614+0530 http-nio-8080-exec-5 admin 754x861x1 1jdq6ii /secure/admin/EditCustomFieldOptions!remove.jspa 1ms """"DELETE FROM public.customfieldvalue WHERE ID='23652'""""  --  {code}  etc.    If the first SELECT query takes a very long time, then UI times out even before getting to the confirm delete page and hence becomes very difficult to delete customfield option.    We could potentially bulk update all issues using this value to be deleted before running the delete option call which would reduce the number of times the SELECT query iterations. However, this become cumbersome any ways for large environments.    h3.Workaround  * (/) The workaround would be to add explicit index on the *{{customfield}}* column:  {code:sql}CREATE INDEX customfieldvalue_customfield_explicit_index on customfieldvalue (customfield);{code}  * Increasing the Reverse Proxy timeout value to let the call finish could be one workaround"""

summary: "Synchronizing DVCS accounts on Jira Data Center can result in a NullPointerException"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When using Jira Data Center to synchronize DVCS accounts, it's sometimes possible that a {{NullPointerExcepiton}} occurs and synchronization for some repositories is interrupted. Synchronization is only picked up again after a certain timeout.    Note that this does not occur on Jira Server.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up a DVCS account with Jira Data Center, running at least 2 nodes   # Synchronize repositories    h3. Expected Results  All repositories become synchronized.    h3. Actual Results  Some repositories fail to become synchronized immediately and only become up to date after a certain time.    The below exception is thrown in the {{atlassian-<USER>log}} file:      h3. Workaround  Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Moving back completely the Sprint slider causes all issues to be moved out of Sprint"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When adjusting the Sprint slider, it is possible to set the number of issues that will be moved out of the Sprint. However, if the Sprint slider is moved, and then dragged back completely, it will remove all the issues in the Sprint.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Scrum Board  # In the Scrum Board, create a Sprint  # Create several Issues in the created Sprint  # Move the Sprint slider up. Notice as you drag the slider up, the issues beneath the slider will be removed when the Slider is released.  # With the Sprint filled with multiple issues, move the Sprint slider above 1 issue, then move it back without releasing the slider. Notice how the number of issues is set to 0, regardless of the actual number of issues in the Sprint  ** Should the slider be released, all the issues in the Sprint will be moved out to the below Sprint or Backlog    (i) Screen recording of replicating the problem:  [^SprintSlider.mp4]     h3. Expected Results  When the slider is moved back completely, it will not remove any issues in the Sprint and leave it as is.    h3. Actual Results  All the issues in the Sprint will be moved out to the below Sprint or Backlog    h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Wiki Markdown in custom field does not display on iOS Jira Mobile App"
description: """h3. Problem    A custom field using the Wiki Style renderer does not display the markdown correctly on IOS.  h3. Environment    Jira Mobile V0.11.2   Jira Server: 8.7    iOS: 13.5.1  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Custom field   # Set Custom field to use the Wiki Style Render   # Input some Wiki Markdown into the custom field    h3. Expected Results    _The Wiki Markdown should show correctly as it does on Android:_    _!Wiki Markdown on Android.jpg|thumbnail!_  h3. Actual Results    _The Wiki Markdown does not show correctly on iOS:_    _!Wiki Mardown on IOS.png|thumbnail!_       h3. Notes    Wiki Markdown does show correctly for the description field on both iOS and Android:   *iOS*    *!IOS Decscription field.png|thumbnail!*    *Android*    *!Android Description field.jpg|thumbnail!*     """

summary: "On Worklog window Calendar populates with incorrect month on using date time picker, When Jira is used with Russian localization "
description: """h3. Issue Summary    The calendar control gets populated for month based on hour of the day.   For example: If its 10:02 then October calendar will come, If its 09:31 then September calendar will appear.    The behavior is observed only for Month of 'May'  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # *(pre-requisite)* Set profile language to Russian and month 'May'   # Open an existing ticket   # Click on More > Log Work   # Under Date Control, select date picker   # The calendar control will be showing the month based on hour of the day    h3. Expected Results   * Current month should be displayed irrespective of time     h3. Actual Results   * Incorrect month is shown on the control based on the time (Refer attachment for more details)    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Select List (Single Choice) Default option ""None""should be removed when it's made as REQUIRED field"
description: """h3. Issue Summary   - Select List (Single Choice) Default option """"None"""" has not removed even its made to Mandatory Field in Field Configuration and It should behave same like Select List (Multiple choices).     - Select List (Multiple choices) has removed the *None* from the options when its made as REQUIRED field.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   - Create 2 custom fields of *Select List (Single Choice)* and *Select List (Multiple Choice)*   !1.png|thumbnail!   - Make them REQUIRED in field configuration   !2.png|thumbnail!   - Select List (Single Choice) still shows """"None"""" as option in Issue screen and the other not.   !3.png|thumbnail!    h3. Expected Results     - Behaviour of the Select List should be same for both Single choice and Multiple choices    h3. Actual Results     - Multiple choices select list field removes the None option when its made REQUIRED and Single choice select list still shows it in the configuration.     !Set Custom Field Defaults.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Having a Default value selected removes the *None* option from the Select List (Single Choice)     !Configure Custom Field Select List (Single) Test.png|thumbnail!      !Set Custom Field Defaults 2.png|thumbnail!      !Select List (Single) V No-1.png|thumbnail! """

summary: "In Issue basic search widget for ""Due date"" placeholder date format in light grey shows up its old/original format"
description: """h3. Issue Summary   - In Issue basic search widget for """"Due date"""" shows its old/original date format as *dd-MMM-yyyy* always.     - It doesn't consider the format configured in *Look & Feel* (Which allows to customize the way dates/times are presented to users throughout the JIRA UI)     - Using the date picker, shows the correct format and also able to enter the date manually as per the format configured in *Advanced Setting*.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Change the format of *Complete Date/Time Format* & *Day/Month/Year Format* under {{Look and Feel >> Date/Time Formats}}   !Screenshot 2020-06-16 at 4.31.24 PM.png|thumbnail!   # Configure the same format under Advanced Setting,   !Screenshot 2020-06-16 at 4.30.25 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Expected Results     - placeholder input date format should show the same format as LookandFeel.    h3. Actual Results   - It presents misleading info to enduser (placeholder text is there to inform user about the format, but it does not show the correct format, when format is customised)    !Screenshot 2020-06-16 at 4.27.34 PM.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    (x) Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Page will not scroll to the created issue in Kanban Boards"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When creating issues in Kanban Board, the page would not scroll down to the created issue. Thus users would need to manually scroll to the bottom to see the created issue.  (i) Reported to be affecting Chrome, Firefox and Edge    This problem is not affecting Scrum Board's Backlog.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Kanban board  # In the Kanban board, create several Issues  ** Ensure the list of issues in the column is long enough to require scrolling  # In the Kanban Board view, scroll to the top of the page  # Then create a new Issue in the Kanban Board via 'Create' header  # Once created, notice how the page is not scrolled to the created Issue  ** If you look at the URL, it has selected the Issue Key of the created Issue    h3. Expected Results  The page would be scrolled to the created Issue   [^scrollJira7_13_2.mp4]   (i) This is a recording took in Jira 7.13.2, where the problem is not occurring    h3. Actual Results  The page would not be scrolled to the created Issue   [^scrollJira855.mp4]     h3. Workaround  * Manually scroll to the bottom of the page  * Use the Browser's search function to search for the created Issue's Key  ** As a reference, the Issue Key can be identified in the URL after creation. Look for *selectedIssue=*.  * Use Internet Explorer which is not affected by the bug"""

summary: "Pressing ALT+CTRL+C keyboard command on Mozilla Firefox opens up a new Window for ""Create Issue"""
description: """h3. Issue Summary    In Windows OS for Jira version 8.8.1 if you tap 'ALT+CTRL+C' on the keyboard it opens up a new window for """"Create Issue"""".    This issue is not specific to a particular user/project but consistent across all the users and projects.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Login to Jira 8.8.1 instance in Mozilla Firefox in your Windows Machine.   # Type """"ALT+CTRL+C"""" command   # A new window opens up for """"Create Issue""""    h3. Expected Results    Nothing should happen. As per the [Keyboard Shortcuts|https://<USER>atlassian.com/secure/ViewKeyboardShortcuts!default.jspa] ''C'' is the shortcut used for """"Create an Issue""""  h3. Actual Results    A new window for """"Create Issue"""" comes up on tap of 'ALT+CTRL+C'  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Comment button visible to users without permission on boards"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When a project's permissions are set to *allow viewing* by any logged in user, but *commenting is limited* to specific project roles, if a user attempts to comment from a board, the button is available to them and they see the following error message:  {panel:bgColor=#eeeeee}  *Communications Breakdown*   The Jira Server Could not be contacted. This may be a temporary glitch or the server may be down. Close this dialog and press refresh in your browser.  {panel}  This message is misleading, as it makes it look like a server availability problem.    If the user navigates to the issue page or the detail view in the issue navigator, no option to comment is presented.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a project with permission that allows logged in users to view the project and issues within the project, but limit the commenting permission to users with assigned roles.   # Have another user who is not assigned a role attempt to comment on an issue within the project, from a board view.    h3. Expected Results    Either:   * Hide the comment button from the user (behavior consistent with the issue navigator);  or   * Gracefully error with an indication that the user does not have the correct permissions to comment and refer them to the project admins.    h3. Actual Results    When we open an issue on a board, the comment button is visible to users that don't have """"Add Comment"""" permission and opens the text editor. When the user clicks the Add button, the message is shown:    !image-2020-06-17-12-33-04-784.png!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "Status becomes blank in Sprint Report if the issues are no longer in the same Status as during Sprint completion"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When Sprint is completed, the Sprint Report will be generated with the information at that point of time. However, should the Issues in the Sprint Report is currently in a different Status, this would render the generated Sprint Report to display the Issues with blank Status.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a Project with Scrum Board  # Create a Sprint with Issues  # Complete the Sprint  # In the Sprint Report, take note of the Issue Status  # Transition the Issues to different Status  # Refresh the Status Report. Should the Issues are now in different Status, it will be shown as blank.    (!) It seems the Bug would only occur if none of the Issues in the Sprint Report is currently in any of the Status recorded in the report. So let's say 2 issues were in 'Status 1' when the Sprint is completed, hence shown in the Sprint Report. Should only 1 of the issue is transitioned to a different Status, the Bug would not occur. However, if both Issues are no longer in Status 1, the Sprint Report would display the Status as blank.    h3. Expected Results  The Sprint Report would show the Sprint Report according to the recorded data at the point of the Sprint completion   !sprintReportFine.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  The Sprint Report would display blank Statuses if the Issues are no longer in the same Status when the report was generated   !sprintReportBug.png|thumbnail!   (i) In the screenshot, I transitioned QQQ-17 to a different Status to show that the Bug also affects Completed Issues    h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Epic Burndown report does not display the chart depending on how the Epic Link was created."
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Epic Burndown report does not display the chart depending on how the Epic Link was created.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    The Epic Burndown report does not display the chart when using sequence 1 and 2 below to create the Epic Link:    *Sequence 1:*   * New Scrum Project Area   * Create button, new issue Epic; Epic Name 1, unassigned, SCRUM7-1   * Create button, new issue Story 1, Assigned to """"user1"""", Epic link to """"Epic Name 1 - (SCRUM7-1)""""   * Create button, new issue Story 2, Assigned to """"user1"""", Epic link to """"Epic Name 1 - (SCRUM7-1)""""   * Open Epic Burndown report: It shows:       * Click the button """"Estimate issues in the Backlog""""   * Click each story and assign an Estimative at the quick edit: Story 1, Estimate 5 pts; Story 2, Estimate 3pts;   * Open Epic Burndown report: same results.    *Sequence 2*   * New Scrum Project Area   * Create button, new issue Epic; Epic Name 1, unassigned, SCRUM8-1   * Open backlog, Epic panel and expand the Epic information   * Use the link """"Create an issue in Epic"""" to create a new issue   * Create Story 1, Assigned to """"user1""""   * Create Story 2, Assigned to """"user1""""   * Open Epic Burndown report: It shows       * Click the """"Estimate issues in the Backlog""""   * Click each story and assign an Estimative, like Story 1, Estimate 5 pts   * Open Epic Burndown report: same results.    *Sequence 3*   * New Scrum Project Area   * Create button, new issue Epic; Epic Name 1, unassigned, SCRUM9-1   * Create Story 1   * Create Story 2   * Edit Story 1 created, search for the field """"Epic Link""""   * At the Epic link field - type """"SCRUM9..."""" - It will show the """"Epic Name 1 - (SCRUM9-1)"""" at the suggestions - Select it and confirm   * Edit Story 2 created, search for the field """"Epic Link""""   * At the Epic link field - type """"SCRUM9..."""" - It will show the """"Epic Name 1 - (SCRUM9-1)"""" at the suggestions - Select it and confirm   * Open the backlog   * Click each story and assign an Estimative, like Story 1, Estimate 5 pts; Story 2, Estimate 3pts;   * Open Epic Burndown report: it will show correctly the graph, as expected.    h3. Expected Results  The graph is presented for all three sequences of steps.    h3. Actual Results  The graph is not presented for sequence 1 and 2:    The team can not keep track of the work that was done or to be done at the Epic.     h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround to fix the already incorrect Epic/Issues.    *The overall recommendation is to create the issue first, and then, edit the issue adding the Epic Link.*      Note: Issue was not reproduced using Jira 7.13.8"""

summary: "Epic Report does not show the correct values at the graph depending on how the epic link was created"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    While the Summary of the issues does show the correct values for Total, completed, unestimated issues, and the total of Story Points and done points, the graph does not show the represent the same values.    Note: Issue was not reproduced using Jira 7.13.8      h3. Steps to Reproduce    a) Create a new project SCRUM   b) Create a new Epic, Epic8393   c) Create a new Story using the Create button, called Story 8395 and add a link to the Epic8393;   d) repeat for Story8396, Story8397, Story8398, Story8399, Story8507, Story8508   e) Create a new Story 5200 using the Create button, but do not add a link to the Epic8393   f) Open the Backlog board - you will see the Epic8393 with 7 stories   g) Click over each story to open the quick edition at the right side and estimate the stories:   story5200 - 3   story8395 - 8   story8396 - 5   story8397 - 8   story8398 - 13   story8399 - 8   story8507 - 8   j) Edit story5200, search for the field """"Epic Link"""", select the field, add the link to Epic8393, transition the issue to """"done""""   k) Edit story8396 and transition the issue to """"done""""   l) Open the Epic Report.  h3. Expected Results    !Screen Shot 2020-05-15 at 15.09.45.png|width=579,height=188!  !Screen Shot 2020-05-15 at 15.43.35.png|width=557,height=188!  h3. Actual Results    !Screen Shot 2020-07-02 at 19.33.26.png|width=579,height=311!    !Screen Shot 2020-07-02 at 19.33.38.png|width=573,height=308!  h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"""

summary: "copy/paste of image into comment in a transition screen is broken"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  Copy/paste of image into comment in a transition screen is broken  While commenting an issue in the issue view it is possible to copy/paste by using CTRL+Z an image in both Text and Visual Mode, to get it automatically added to the comment. In Text Mode the image is converted in thumbnail while in Visual Mode the image is uploaded with his real size.  While copy/pasting the image in a comment in a transition screen, the functionality is broken. In Text Mode pressing CTRL+Z does nothing while in Visual Mode the image seems to get inserted but after triggering/confirming the transition, the image is not available and appear broken.   !Screenshot 2020-07-08 at 11.15.49.png|thumbnail!   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Transition an issue that has a screen set for that transition   # In the transition screen comment field copy paste an image by pressing CTRL+Z in Text Mode   # In Text Mode observe that pressing CTRL+Z does nothing   # In Visual Mode observe that pressing CTRL+Z actually attach the image, but then after transitioning/confirming the screen, the image is not actually attached and show as broken:   !Screenshot 2020-07-08 at 11.15.49.png|thumbnail!      h3. Expected Results  Image should be inserted by pressing CTRL+Z  h3. Actual Results  Image is not attached  h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. Steps to Reproduce   # Have a Jira with a Software project.   # Go to Project board   # Open Backlog tab and select an issue   # Observe the detailed view being open on the right side (if no detailed view will show then click Board button on the upper right and select """"Show detail view"""")   # Go to another tab on that board (other than Active Sprint)   # Go back to Backlog tab    h3. summary: "Remaining estimate update is not reflected in history when updated via transition screen and when there's a post function present to update 'Remaining estimate' field in the workflow'"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Remaining estimate update is not reflected in history when updated via transition screen and when there's a post function present to update 'Original estimate in the workflow'  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a transition screen with 'Log work' and 'Time tracking' and assign that transition screen to a transition in a workflow   # Add a post function to that same transition (for the purpose of this case we will use 'Done' transition) to update 'Remaining estimate' or 'Original Estimate' field to 0   # Publish the workflow   # Add 'time tracking' field to the issue screen   # Create or modify an issue 'remaining estimate' value to any value and notice the change get's reflected properly in the history   # Perform the 'Done' transition and enter 'Remaining estimate' value as 0    We use out of the box post function to set the remaining estimate value to 0 when the issue is moved to completed.  h3. Expected Results    The 'remaining estimate' change should be reflected in the history of the issue  h3. Actual Results    The 'remaining estimate' value on the case does get updated to '0' but the change is not reflected in the case history    Another impact due to this bug, is that the burndown chart for remaining estimate is not working as expected  h3. Workaround    Don't use both the transition screen and a post function on the same transition to update time tracking fields"""

summary: "Sprint field not populating until you access the board's backlog"

Steps to Reproduce   # Install Jira 8.11.0 (problem is not reproduced in 8.10.1)   # Create some projects, boards, and sprints   # With another username (that didn't access the boards but have access), press the Create button to create an issue and try to use the sprint field   # You won't see any sprints    h4. Expected Results   # By clicking in the Sprint field it should show the available sprints to select it to add to the issue    h4. Actual Results   # You won't be able to enter a sprint in the field as it will show you 'No Matches', or you will be able to see only the sprints from boards there you previously accessed (backlog):   !image-2020-08-31-15-52-06-259.png|width=586,height=482!    h4. summary: "When filling a Custom Field on a Tab, the Tab is not represented when viewing the issue"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    When filling a Custom Field on a Tab, the Tab is not represented when viewing the issue.   The Tab is only represented if you do fill other Fields on another Tab (except System Fields as Summary, Description, Labels, Priority, Issue link).   * Per the Documentation: [Defining a screen|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/defining-a-screen-938847288.html] - There is no mention that it is required to have more content filled on other tabs to show the tabs:  {quote}If none of the fields on a tab contain data then the tab is not shown. To make a tab show up, make sure *it has a custom field with a type such as Text or Select and that the field has a value.*  {quote}    So, or the documentation is incomplete, or the behavior of the Tags is not working as expected.         The Knowledge Base [Tabs Don't Show up on View Issue Screen in JIRA|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/tabs-don-t-show-up-on-view-issue-screen-in-<USER>873947916.html] has a """"Design Justification"""" on it:  {quote}The logic behind the functionality comes down to the distinction between what should be visible when viewing (only relevant information) and editing (all editable fields) an issue. We’re hiding empty custom fields and tabs on the “View issue” screen to improve readability and performance (by rendering a lower number of fields). Displaying all custom fields would make the issue unclear especially when a large number of custom fields are defined.  {quote}  However, *per Customer perception*:   This is a poor design choice (if I put the fields on a tab, I expect it to show on a tab).  h3. Steps to Reproduce   * Create a New Project Area TESTTAB   * Create a Custom Field """"mytextA"""" using the format """"Text Field (single line)"""", another Custom Field """"mytextB"""" also """"'Text Field (single line)' Field"""" and a Custom Field Date """"mydate"""", as date picker.   * Edit the """"TESTTAB: Scrum Default Issue Screen"""" screen, adding:   ** rename the """"Field Tab"""" with all the default fields to """"Tab1""""   ** Add a new tab """"Tab2"""" with the Custom Field """"mydate""""   ** Add a new tab """"Tab3"""" with the Custom Field """"mytextA""""   ** Add a new tab """"Tab4"""" with the Custom Field """"mytextB""""   * Create a new Story:   ** Fills the field Summary, on """"Tab1""""   ** Switch to tab """"Tab3"""" and fills the field """"mytextA""""   * View the issue created    h3. Expected Results    See """"Tab3"""" with the """"mytextA"""" content  h3. Actual Results   * """"Tab3"""" is not showed   * """"mytextA"""" is showed on Tab1.    h3. Workaround   * Filling any content at the """"mytextB"""" on """"Tab4"""" will show both """"Tab3"""" and """"Tab4""""   * Filling """"mydate"""" at the """"Tab2"""" does not show """"Tab2"""" and """"Tab3"""". The date is listed on """"Tab1"""" at the Date Section.   * Filling """"Epic Link"""" at the """"Tab1"""" will show """"Tab3""""   * Filling """"Description"""", """"Priority"""" or """"Labels"""" will not show Tab3"""

summary: "JSW Server not vulnerable to an Insecure Deserialization issue in Jackson Databind - CVE-2018-14720"
description: """Scanners may falsely flag some versions of Jira Software Server before 8.5.5 as vulnerable to an Insecure Deserialization issue in Jackson Databind (CVE-2018-14720). This vulnerability in a transitive dependency was being flagged because Jira Software assumed the version of applinks provided by Jira Core was an earlier version of applinks but Jira Core was actually providing a newer version that was not vulnerable to CVE-2018-14720. Jira Software Server has been updated to assume that Jira Core is providing the newer version of applinks so that scanners should not flag this issue in versions after 8.5.5."""

summary: "Consider about start warning to user about the end of IE11 support"
description: """In MDLSITE-6109 it was decided that Moodle 3.9.x will be the last branch actively supporting  IE11, with Moodle 3.10.x explicitly (actively) not supporting it.    One of the measures commented in that issue was the possibility of start warning users still using that browser. There are various aspects to decide:    - Make it opt-in (default off) or opt-out (default on).  - Audience (every user vs only admins).  - Store results (to avoid repeated messages, to account # of users...).  - Branches to implement it (only 3.9 or also 3.10 and up...)    So this issue is about to decide if it's worth creating such warning system, and if decided to proceed, implement it.    Ciao :-)"""

summary: "BeforeScenario hook failure leads to skipped test and rerun (take #2)"
description: """This is a followup of MDL-69278 where it was detected that, sometimes, problem in BeforeScenario stuff leads to skipped tests (not failures), but the run ends with non-zero exit code.    That causes reruns to happen and, as far as there isn't any rerun list (with failures) the whole job is rerun.    In MDL-69278 it was fixed for JS scenarios, but it seems that some """"goutte"""" BeforeScenario stuff also can lead to the very same problem, increasing execution a lot.    {quote}  Just got this case:    https://ci.moodle.org/job/S37.07.01%20-%20Behat%20-%20Firefox%20+%20Postgres%20+%20Classic/177/    - Normal (javascript) postgres run.  - 2 of the 3 parallel runs end with:    - That leads to 1 skipped, 0 failed. And exit error.  - Rerun reruns everything (waste of resources, 10h total run time).    So, pretty much the original issue, but happening on the {{before_goutte_scenarios()}}, for runs that aren't goutte.    Surely they deserve a similar/parallel fix (or understand why that is being called).    Ciao :-)  {quote}"""

summary: "Autocomplete widget: Improve keyboard accessibility"
description: """The tags widget that users can use to enter and remove tags (e.g. under Interests on the 'Edit profile' screen  https://sandbox.moodledemo.net/user/editadvanced.php?id=2&course=1&returnto=profile ) has keyboard support but it is not working perfectly at the moment. Specifically:    * When you tab to the list of tags (so as to delete one or cycle through them), there is no indication of focus.  * When you tab to the list of tags, no tag is currently selected which seems weird - you have to press Up or Down to select one.  * If you press Down it selects the first one, but if you press Up it selects the one before last (i.e. if there were tags 1,2,3,4,5 it will select 4, which is frankly weird.)  * The keyboard controls are Up/Down whereas tags are normally shown left to right.    I propose the following changes:    * There should be a focus highlight on the tag list (even if there are no tags in it) so you can tell what you just tabbed to.  * When the user tabs to the row, it should immediately highlight the first item, to indicate that they are selectable, instead of having nothing selected by default. For example, say the tags are 1,2,3,4,5 then when you tab to the row, '1' should immediately be selected. Pressing Down would then take you to 2, or Up would take you to 5.  * When user tabs away from the row it should remove the highlight so that no tags are highlighted.  * The Up key should take you to the correct item - initially, to the last item not the one before last.  * Add support for the Left and Right arrow keys while keeping Up/Down as well (Left should do the same as Up, Right the same as Down).    This list of tags has the Aria role 'list', which I think is OK, but it doesn't imply any particular navigation controls (lists are not usually active controls), so as far as I know we are free to change it.    We plan to develop a patch for this."""

On the New Category page, type  """"*Category 2*"""" as the Category Name and click save changes   # Scroll to the bottom of the gradebook setup page and click """"Add Category"""" button   ## On the New Category page, type  """"*Category 3*"""" as the Category Name and click save changes   # Scroll to the bottom of the gradebook setup page and click """"Add Category"""" button   ## On the New Category page, type  """"*Category 4*"""" as the Category Name and click save changes   # Scroll to the bottom of the gradebook setup page and click """"Add Category"""" button   # The above establishes 4 main categories: now, we will add 2 sub categories into each: scroll to the bottom of the gradebook setup page and click """"Add Category"""" button   ## On the New Category page, type  """"*Sub Category A*"""" as the Category Name and click save changes   ## Toggle open the Parent category section   ## Open the Parent category dropdown menu   ## Notice the four categories ARE in creation order   ## Select """"*Category 1*"""" then click Save changes   # Scroll to the bottom of the gradebook setup page and click """"Add Category"""" button   ## On the New Category page, type  """"*Sub Category B*"""" as the Category Name and click save changes   ## Toggle open the Parent category section   ## Open the Parent category dropdown menu   ## Notice the give categories ARE in creation order   ## Select """"*Category 2*"""" then click Save changes   # Scroll to the bottom of the gradebook setup page and click """"Add Category"""" button   ## On the New Category page, type  """"*A New Category*"""" as the Category Name and click save changes   ## Toggle open the Parent category section   ## Open the Parent category dropdown menu   ## Notice the six categories ARE in creation order   ## Select """"*Category 3*"""" then click Save changes   # Scroll to the bottom of the gradebook setup page and click """"Add Category"""" button   ## On the New Category page, type  """"*Final Category*"""" as the Category Name and click save changes   ## Toggle open the Parent category section   ## Open the Parent category dropdown menu   ## Notice the seven categories ARE in creation order   ## Select """"*Category 4*"""" then click Save changes   # Back up and restore the course in its entirety into a new location   ## In the newly restored course, click Gradebook setup   ## Scroll to the bottom of the gradebook setup page and click """"Add Category"""" button   ## Toggle open the Parent category section   ## Open the Parent category dropdown menu   ## Notice the seven categories ARE NOT in ABC order or in the original creation order   ## Select """"*Category 4*"""" then click Save changes   # Create a brand new course   ## Click Import   ## Import the original GBTEST course in its entirety    ## Upon completion of import, click on Gradebook Setup in this new course   ## Scroll to the bottom of the gradebook setup page and click """"Add Category"""" button   ## Toggle open the Parent category section   ## summary: "Change all the 4.0 upgrade.txt notes to 3.10"
description: """All the upgrade.txt notes (and other embed comments) must be changed from 4.0 to 3.10.    Both in 3.10 and master.    RULE:    Under parallel development, when something is worth being commented in upgrade.txt notes, it will be applied to all the development branches using the """"earliest"""" one to be released. So, if something changes in 3.10 (and also is applied to master), the upgrade.txt notes will show 3.10 in both branches.    Of course, if something only goes to one dev branch (say master), then the master version will be used in the notes (4.0 in this case).    As of 21th August 2020, these are the found cases:        I would recommend the integrator to run the regexp again just in case there are new occurrences when this is integrated."""

summary: "No 'fontsize' setting in Boost causes unit conflict"
description: """In 'theme_boost_get_pre_scss' there is the code:    $scss .= '$font-size-base: ' . (1 / 100 * $theme->settings->fontsize) . """"rem !default;\n"""";    which assumes that the 'fontsize' setting is in 'rem' units.  However:   # If a theme declares a setting of the same name (in effect to override the setting) with a non-numeric value then the error 'A non well formed numeric value encountered' happens when 'Purge all caches'.  This is because of the way that 'get_pre_scss_code' works in 'outputlib.php' and the child theme theme_config instance (referenced by $theme) is used with the Boost code 'theme_boost_get_pre_scss'.   # Boost has no 'fontsize' setting delaired in its 'settings.php'!    Therefore this code is redundant and I propose it should be removed = its a bug!    Code's been there since MDL-56511 with the string:    $string['fontsize_desc'] = 'Enter a fontsize in %';    stating percent and yet the code is 'rem'!  Thus a bug in MDL-56511.          """

summary: "Filtering in grader fails if user does not have moodle/user:editownprofile capability"

If the ‘moodle/user:editownprofile’ capability is not set or not allowed for teachers, this results in a “nopermission” error. Since the filtering of student submissions depends on the user preference value being set, the filtering fails and instead it returns all (unfiltered) student submissions. The editownprofile capability should not prevent a teacher from being able to filter while grading."""

summary: "LTI Grade Passback Failing"
description: """We are trying to use an external LTI tool - GoReact - and they are saying the lis_result_sourcedid is coming back as JSON and it shouldn't be.  It was working in previous versions of Moodle (thought they won't tell me what versions), but they confirm it doesn't work now.    Here is their response:    We're seeing the same thing in our Moodle environment as well where it worked correctly before, so Moodle has changed something that is breaking grade passback.  We'll have our developers take a look to see if there is anything that can be done on our end to work around the issue, but generally speaking we don't make changes to accommodate breaks from LTI specifications, if that's what is happening here.  If you could also contact Moodle and let them know that LTI grade passback is failing that would be helpful.     Our initial suspicion is that it could be related to the hash value being passed, but that's just from a cursory investigation, so we don't have a high degree of certainty yet. Here's a sample payload segment with the hash if it helps Moodle when you reach out to them:         {{""""lis_result_sourcedid"""": """"\{\""""data\"""":{\""""instanceid\"""":\""""2\"""",\""""userid\"""":\""""2\"""",\""""typeid\"""":\""""2\"""",\""""launchid\"""":1193508644},\""""hash\"""":\""""e49bb5de8a51ae2a6f6597e1c8c3db6d25275e5725fe72326daf4b68fcd0a45d\""""}"""",}}"""

summary: "getremoteconfaddr cannot be set to HTTP_CLIENT_IP, HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR, REMOTE_ADDR"
description: """A change in defaults set with MDL-69074 means it is now impossible to set CFG->getremoteaddrconf to HTTP_CLIENT_IP, HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR, REMOTE_ADDR (value: 0).         MDL-69074 did this:  {code:php}    if (empty($CFG->getremoteaddrconf)) {           // This will happen, for example, before just after the upgrade, as the           // user is redirected to the admin screen.  -        $variablestoskip = 0;  +        $variablestoskip = GETREMOTEADDR_SKIP_DEFAULT;       } else {           $variablestoskip = $CFG->getremoteaddrconf;       }  {code}  The problem is """"0"""", which is used by """"HTTP_CLIENT_IP, HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR, REMOTE_ADDR"""" equates to empty - and the empty branch of the IF no longer sets the value to 0.    We should change `if (empty(...` to `if (!isset(...` - which allows the UI/config.php to set the value to '0'."""

summary: "Quiz - Numerical question : inconsistent grading depending on units"
description: """*General description*    This issue revolves around using different units in answers to numerical questions when giving a unit is mandatory. This involves giving one desired numerical answer and several possible units with a conversion factor. Giving the desired numerical answer but with the wrong unit gives a higher grade than giving a different numerical answer but with the right unit.    *Detailed steps*    • Create a Numerical question.    • Under Answers, set Answer 1 to a simple number, e.g. 25, with a grade of 100%    • Under Unit handling, set Unit handling to """"The unit must be given, and will be graded.""""    • Set Unit penalty to 0.3 """"as a fraction of the question grade"""". This will allow us to see which part of the answer was counted as right, the number or the unit.    • Set Units are input as to """"drop-down menu"""" or """"multiple choice selection""""    • Under Units, fill in the following units:    Unit 1    """"cm""""   Multiplier """"1""""    Unit 2  """"mm""""   Multiplier """"10""""    Unit 3  """"kg""""      Multiplier """"0.7777"""" (This will make it very unlikely that the numerical answer given will be equal to Answer1 * multiplier)    • Click on Save changes and continue editing, then on Preview    • Make sure that under Display options, Decimal places in grades is set to at least 1    • Fill in """"25"""" and choose """"cm"""", Click on Submit and finish, the Question state is gradedright and the <USER>1.0 out of 1.0.    • Click on Start again, fill in """"250"""" and choose """"mm"""". Click on Submit and finish, the Question state is gradedright and the <USER>1.0 out of 1.0. So far so good.    • Click on Start again, fill in """"250"""" and choose """"cm"""". Click on Submit and finish, the Question state is gradedwrong and the <USER>0.0 out of 1.0. This is already a bit fishy, since the unit in itself is not wrong, only the numerical answer. Filling in """"250"""" and """"kg"""", also gives a <USER>of 0.0 out of 1.0, while this is clearly more wrong than an answer in with """"cm"""" or """"mm"""".    Finally, here is where things really go wrong:    • Click on Start again, fill in """"25"""" and choose """"mm"""". Click on Submit and finish: the Question state is gradedpartial and the <USER>0.7 out of 1.0.    • Click on Start again, fill in """"25"""" and choose """"kg"""". Click on Submit and finish: the Question state is gradedpartial and the <USER>0.7 out of 1.0.    This shouldn't be happening. 25 mm is just as wrong as 250 cm, but it still gets a 0.7 <USER> because the number happens to be the same as Answer 1. Actually, this answer should be partially correct in the other direction, right unit but wrong number, so 0.3. The last attempt is even worse: it's the wrong unit and the wrong number, but it still scores as high as the mm answer (0.7), again because it contains the right numerical answer.    *Origin of the problem*    For me, the origin of the problem lies in the fact this question type has to deal with very different ways of handling units. The way the units are entered is clearly designed for the second option under Unit handling (""""Units are optional. If a unit is entered, it will be used to convert the response to Unit 1 before grading.""""), and this supposes more or less that units are entered using the text input element. Any unit entered that is present in the list of units will be converted to unit 1, and the result is then checked against the encoded answers. Units that are not in the list are wrong and lead to the penalty being applied.    *Possible solution*    For a question that asks for the appropriate unit and a numerical answer (so """"The unit must be given, and will be graded""""), there should be the option under Units to indicate which units (of the ones that appear in the drop-down or multiple-choice) are correct (e.g. size measures, so mm, cm, m, km) and which are wrong (e.g. kg, J, dB). Only for units that are correct should the conversion via the multiplier be performed, and only the result of that calculation should be checked against the possible answers. In the current situation, the question maker is obliged to enter a weird multiplier for any wrong unit that has to appear in the drop-down menu or multiple-choice selection. In my example, a student could have score full marks with the answer """"50 kg"""" if the multiplier for """"kg"""" had been set to 2... The penalty for a wrong unit, regardless of the numerical response, should be higher than for an answer with a wrong number but a correct unit (""""25 kg"""" is worse than """"25 mm"""" or """"250 cm"""", in my example).     """

summary: "Backup reports - ""Ad-hoc task to save pending course"""
description: """Hi there,    We seem to be encountering an issue since upgrading to Moodle 3.9.1 in that the backup report emails have stopped reporting backed up courses as """"OK"""" and all of them seem to be classed as pending. E.G:  h1. Summary    Courses: 247   OK: 0   Skipped: 194   Error: 0   Unfinished: 0   Course backup ad hoc task pending: 53   Warning: 0   Automated backup pending: 0    Backup completed successfully    Whereas 3.8 would report:    Courses: 246  OK: 38  Skipped: 208  Error: 0  Unfinished: 0  Warning: 0  Automated backup pending: 0    Backup completed successfully         I can see that the Course backup ad hoc category is new so this perhaps may be to do with a change to the way the backups run. The actual back-ups themselves when checked in the morning do report """"OK"""" in the backup reports area so it seems to be an issue particular to the email reports. It is confusing and a bit misleading for this to report as none of the back-ups being """"OK"""" however when it seems they _will_ be once they are complete and no longer pending.     Perhaps it's an issue to do with when the emails are being sent out, could they be sent out later in the back-up process if that's the issue?    Similar forum post: [https://moodle.org/<USER>forum/discuss.php?d=407376#p1644287]    Let me know if you need any more details regarding this."""

summary: "File converter extension type case sensitive"
description: """Hi,  I have just come across potential issues involving extension type names. If a file has a uppercase extension (which sometimes occur with image files) this will fail as an unrecognised format because the list of supported import file formats are in lowercase.    I tested this by changing a file extension (xxx.png) to uppercase and then back to lowercase and it worked in an assignment when grading, the pdf file was generated successfully.    We use two converters, OneDrive and LibreLambda and both will experience the same issue if the file name extension is in uppercase, the file will simply not convert, a blank document will appear. If the list of supported formats in the class contained the equivalent uppercase extensions, this would work but that's not the best solution.  So, would it be worth while to implement a strtolower() somewhere to capture the filename extension before it's checked against the supported list?     """

summary: "Moving a section with drag an drop adds unnecessary aria labels"
description: """FULL STEPS    In Moodle 3.9 or later, in topics or weekly format, with editing on, use web browser's HTML inspector on a section, noting that no aria-label property is present.  (These were removed in favour of aria-labelledby.)  Drag and drop the section to move it.  Using web browser's HTML inspector again, note that an aria-label property has been added.         THE FIX    In course/format/topics/format.js (and the weeks format) function process_sections() remove the update to aria-label.         THE CAUSE   With MDL-68189, in course/format/renderer.php function section_header() the use of aria-label was replaced with aria-labelledby, but the update to aria-label was not removed."""
